problem: Routine pruning of stale snapshot-version -> EGIT_COMMIT case entries once no ebuild references them; no generalizable content beyond keeping version maps tidy.
Single-variable bump (_LLVM_MAIN_MAJOR=23 -> 24) when upstream trunk branched; project-specific routine.
An obsolete eclass needed to be flagged so consumers stop using it, but there was no machine-readable deprecation marker.
root_cause: Eclass deprecation is communicated through the eclassdoc header; without a @DEPRECATED tag pkgcheck cannot warn ebuilds that still inherit it.
fix_pattern: Add '# @DEPRECATED: none' to the eclassdoc header block (value is the replacement eclass name, or the literal 'none' when there is no replacement).
rule: When retiring an eclass, add a '# @DEPRECATED: <replacement|none>' eclassdoc tag instead of only announcing it, so pkgcheck flags remaining consumers automatically.

problem: eclass still inherited eapi9-pipestatus after the refactor that removed its only pipeline use; pkgcheck did not flag the now-unused inherit.
root_cause: Unused-inherit detection is heuristic; helper eclasses whose functions are called conditionally or indirectly escape pkgcheck's UnusedInherits check, so dead inherits linger silently.
fix_pattern: When a refactor removes the last call into an inherited eclass, manually audit the inherit list in the same change set (grep for each inherited eclass's exported functions/vars) and drop the inherit.
rule: After removing the last use of an inherited eclass function, remove the inherit yourself - do not rely on pkgcheck to catch every unused inherit.

problem: pkgcheck reported EclassDocError because functions were marked with a bare '# @DEPRECATED' tag with no argument.
root_cause: The eclassdoc @DEPRECATED tag requires a value naming the replacement (or 'none'); a bare tag is a doc-format error.
fix_pattern: Give every @DEPRECATED tag an explicit alternative, e.g. '# @DEPRECATED: qt_get_headerdir 6', so both pkgcheck and human readers know the migration target.
rule: When deprecating an eclass function, always write '# @DEPRECATED: <replacement>' with the concrete alternative call - a bare @DEPRECATED fails eclassdoc validation.

problem: 'type -P name | head -n 1' used a pointless pipeline, which then also required eapi9-pipestatus handling for error checking.
root_cause: Misremembered bash semantics: 'type -P name' already prints only the first PATH match, so head -n 1 was dead code that turned a simple command into a pipeline needing pipestatus checks.
fix_pattern: Drop the pipe and attach '|| die' directly to the command, eliminating the pipestatus dependency.
rule: Avoid gratuitous pipelines in eclass helpers - a plain command can use '|| die' directly, while a pipeline forces pipestatus/eapi9-pipestatus machinery; verify what the tool actually outputs before adding filters.
snippet:
PATH=${BROOT}$(qt_get_libexecdir ${1}):${BROOT}$(qt_get_bindir ${1}) \
	type -P "${2}" || die "${FUNCNAME[0]}: failed trying to detect binary: ${2}"

problem: Comment update and cosmetic variable reordering after deciding NOT to migrate to qt-utils helpers (mixed definitions deemed more error-prone than duplicated paths kept in sync); no functional change.
A conditional bare 'cd' inside src_prepare left the working directory dependent on whether the branch ran, so later phase code executed in different cwds (bug 979024).
root_cause: Bare cd inside an if-block mutates global phase state; code after the conditional silently inherits whichever directory the branch left behind.
fix_pattern: Wrap the conditional directory change in pushd/popd with '>/dev/null || die' so the cwd is restored no matter which branch ran.
rule: In ebuild/eclass phase functions, wrap any conditional directory change in 'pushd ... >/dev/null || die' / 'popd >/dev/null || die' so code after the conditional always runs from a known cwd.
snippet:
if [[ -n ${BASEPOL} && ${BASEPOL} != 9999 ]]; then
	pushd "${S}" >/dev/null || die
	eapply -p0 -- "${WORKDIR}/0001-full-patch.patch"
	popd >/dev/null || die
fi

problem: eapply_user ran from whatever directory earlier prepare steps happened to leave, so user patches in /etc/portage/patches applied against different roots for live vs release builds (bugs 794043, 979024).
root_cause: eapply_user applies patches relative to the current working directory; the eclass never pinned cwd before calling it, making user-patch behavior depend on unrelated control flow.
fix_pattern: pushd to one documented directory ("${WORKDIR}/refpolicy") around the eapply_user call so user patches always apply against the same tree root, matching sibling packages.
rule: Always pin the working directory explicitly (pushd/popd) around eapply_user so user patches apply from one stable, documented root regardless of live/non-live or conditional prepare paths.

problem: Live and non-live code paths ended src_prepare in different directories, so user patches behaved differently between -9999 and release ebuilds (bug 979024).
root_cause: The non-live branch cd'd into ${S} to apply a patchbundle and never returned to the directory the live path used, creating divergent cwd state between variants of the same package.
fix_pattern: Add the missing 'cd' back to the common directory after the branch-specific patching, making live and non-live converge on identical state (later refined into pushd/popd).
rule: Keep live (-9999) and release code paths in an eclass convergent: any branch that changes directory or layout must restore the same end state the other branch produces.

problem: EGO_SUM parsing used herestrings ('read ... <<< "$line"') in code reachable from global scope; herestrings/heredocs make bash create a temp file, which fails when metadata generation is sandboxed (bug 979141).
root_cause: bash implements heredocs/herestrings via temporary files (always in <EAPI9 bash, and still for large input in newer bash), and sandboxed metadata generation forbids temp-file writes during global-scope sourcing.
fix_pattern: Replace 'read -r a b <<< "$str"' with 'read -rd "" a b < <(printf %s "$str")' - process substitution uses a pipe, not a temp file.
rule: In any code that can run during metadata generation (global scope of eclasses/ebuilds), never use heredocs or <<< herestrings; feed strings via 'read -rd "" ... < <(printf %s ...)' instead.
snippet:
read -rd '' module version_modfile kvs < <(printf %s "${line}")
IFS=/ read -rd '' version modfile x < <(printf %s "${version_modfile}")

problem: eqmake6 invoked qmake via a path prefixed with EPREFIX; when cross-compiling, tools executed at build time must come from the build host (BROOT), not the target prefix.
root_cause: EPREFIX points at the installed-system offset (target), while executables run during src_configure/src_compile live under BROOT; conflating them breaks cross/prefix builds. EAPI 9 formalizes the distinction.
fix_pattern: Gate on EAPI: keep old EPREFIX behavior for EAPI 8 consumers, and for EAPI 9 call a helper that resolves the tool under BROOT ('qt_get_broot_binary 6 qmake').
rule: Any binary an ebuild executes during the build must be resolved under BROOT (build host); EPREFIX belongs only in paths recorded into installed files - when fixing an eclass API for this, gate the behavior change on EAPI to avoid breaking existing consumers.

problem: Needed a way to locate a build-time Qt tool that may live in either bindir or libexecdir, prefixed with BROOT for cross-compilation correctness.
root_cause: Qt installs some tools in bin and others in libexec; hardcoding one directory breaks when upstream moves a tool, and hardcoding EPREFIX breaks cross builds.
fix_pattern: Use a scoped PATH override with 'type -P' to probe multiple candidate directories in priority order, dying if not found.
rule: To find an executable that may live in one of several package directories, set PATH to the BROOT-prefixed candidates for a single 'type -P' invocation rather than testing paths one by one.

problem: Legacy qt6_* API needed retiring without instantly breaking hundreds of consumers across two EAPIs.
root_cause: Eclass APIs cannot be removed atomically; consumers migrate over time, so deprecation must be phased per-EAPI.
fix_pattern: Add a shared internal guard called from every old function: it returns silently on the old EAPI, emits an eqawarn naming the exact replacement on the new EAPI, and outright dies for functions banned in the new EAPI. Uses ${FUNCNAME[1]} introspection so one guard serves all wrappers.
rule: To phase out eclass functions, keep them silent in old EAPIs, eqawarn with the concrete replacement in the next EAPI, and die for banned ones - implement it once via a FUNCNAME-dispatching guard called at the top of each deprecated function.

problem: Trivial addition of qt_get_qmldir helper following the established pattern of sibling functions; no new lesson.
Old qt6_* path helpers inconsistently baked EPREFIX into some returned paths but not others, so callers could not tell which offset a path carried.
root_cause: Path-query helpers that pre-apply an offset (EPREFIX) lock callers into one use case; the same logical path needs different prefixes depending on context (BROOT to execute, EPREFIX in installed files, D to install into).
fix_pattern: Introduce a parallel qt_-prefixed API that consistently returns bare, unprefixed paths, plus a single argument-validating internal helper (_qt_get_check_func_call using FUNCNAME for error messages) shared by all of them.
rule: Design eclass path-query functions to return unprefixed paths and let each call site prepend EPREFIX/BROOT/D as its context requires; centralize argument validation in one internal helper.

problem: A global-scope 'eqawarn <<- EOF' both risked sandbox-unsafe temp files during metadata generation and actually printed an EMPTY QA warning (bug 979047).
root_cause: Heredocs in global scope may create temp files under sandboxed metadata generation, and eqawarn takes its message as arguments, not stdin - piping a heredoc into it silently produces an empty warning.
fix_pattern: Replace the has+heredoc construct with a case statement passing the message as a normal argument string.
rule: Never feed eqawarn/einfo/ewarn via heredoc or pipe - they read arguments, not stdin; pass the message inline (and this also removes the global-scope heredoc hazard).
snippet:
case ${NGINX_UPDATE_STREAM} in
	stable|mainline)
		eqawarn "NGINX_UPDATE_STREAM=${NGINX_UPDATE_STREAM} is deprecated and no longer has any effect."
esac

problem: Eclass rejected EAPI 9 ebuilds although nothing in it needed changes for the new EAPI.
root_cause: Eclasses gate EAPIs with an explicit allowlist; adding a new EAPI requires auditing the eclass for removed/changed functionality, then widening the gate.
fix_pattern: After verifying no EAPI-9-incompatible constructs are used, extend both the '@SUPPORTED_EAPIS' doc line and the 'case ${EAPI}' guard.
rule: To add a new EAPI to an eclass, audit it against the EAPI changelist, then update @SUPPORTED_EAPIS and the case-guard together - they must stay in sync for eclassdoc and runtime behavior.

problem: Eclass-provided SRC_URI/HOMEPAGE pointed at URLs (www.x.org/releases) that now issue redirects to xorg.freedesktop.org.
root_cause: Upstream moved its canonical archive host; fetches still work through redirects but are slower, fragile, and hide the real source.
fix_pattern: Point XORG_BASE_INDIVIDUAL_URI directly at the redirect target and refresh HOMEPAGE; also default XORG_PACKAGE_NAME to ${PN,,} (bash lowercase expansion) to match repo naming.
rule: When distfile or homepage URLs start redirecting, update SRC_URI/HOMEPAGE to the final destination host instead of relying on redirects; a HEAD-request check makes this detectable.

problem: Same minimal EAPI-9 enablement as xorg-meson.eclass (update @SUPPORTED_EAPIS plus case-guard); duplicate of the c6b9933f lesson.
Same redirect-avoiding URL update as xorg-meson.eclass (x.org -> xorg.freedesktop.org, ${PN,,}); duplicate of the 6b053425 lesson.
Global-scope 'IFS=, read -ra arr <<< str' herestring risked temp-file creation during sandboxed metadata generation (bug 978941).
root_cause: bash may back heredocs/herestrings with a temp file, forbidden when metadata generation is sandboxed.
fix_pattern: For splitting a delimited string into an array, use mapfile with a delimiter reading from process substitution instead of a herestring.
rule: To split a delimited string into a bash array in metadata-safe code, use "mapfile -td ',' arr < <(printf %s \"$str\")" rather than 'IFS=, read -ra arr <<< "$str"'.

problem: Global-scope herestring splitting comma-delimited module deps risked sandbox-unsafe temp files during metadata generation (bug 978940); same class as sec-keys/cargo fixes.
root_cause: Herestrings may create temp files in bash; global-scope eclass code runs during sandboxed metadata generation.
fix_pattern: Replace 'IFS=, read -ra dep_list <<< str' with 'mapfile -td "," dep_list < <(printf %s str)'.
rule: Audit eclass/ebuild global scope for <<< and << and convert them to process-substitution reads - part of a tree-wide idiom, grep-detectable.

problem: GIT_CRATES parsing in cargo.eclass used 'IFS=";" read ... <<< str' in code executed at global scope (via _cargo_set_crate_uris), which can fail under sandboxed metadata generation (bug 978857).
root_cause: bash implements heredocs and <<< herestrings via a temporary file (always before EAPI 9's bash baseline, and still for large inputs after); Portage sandboxing of metadata generation forbids that write, so any global-scope heredoc/herestring in an eclass or ebuild can break every consumer's metadata cache generation.
fix_pattern: Swap the herestring for process substitution which uses a pipe: 'IFS=";" read -rd "" a b c d < <(printf %s "$str")'; note -d "" replaces the newline the herestring used to supply. Same tree-wide campaign fixed sec-keys, nginx, and go-module the same week.
rule: Never use heredocs or <<< herestrings in code reachable from ebuild/eclass global scope (anything that runs during metadata generation); use 'read -rd "" ... < <(printf %s ...)' or 'mapfile -td X ... < <(printf %s ...)' instead - grep for '<<<' outside phase functions to find offenders.
snippet: IFS=';' read -rd '' crate_uri commit crate_dir host < <(printf %s "${GIT_CRATES[${crate}]}")

problem: Whitespace-only fix of an over-indented inherit line; no reusable content.

Portage started throwing sandbox violations for eclass code executed in global scope that used bash here-strings (<<<), because <<< creates a temporary file and global-scope metadata sourcing must not write to disk.
root_cause: bash implements <<< (and heredocs) via a temp file; when used at eclass/ebuild global scope (metadata phase) this violates the sandbox that newer portage enforces during cache regeneration.
fix_pattern: Replace 'IFS="." read -r -a ARR <<<"${VAR}"' with temp-file-free word splitting: set IFS locally, expand unquoted into an array, then unset IFS.
rule: Never use <<< / heredocs in global-scope eclass or ebuild code; split strings into arrays with a scoped IFS and unquoted expansion instead.
snippet:
-IFS="." read -r -a OKV_ARRAY <<<"${OKV}"
+local IFS=.; OKV_ARRAY=(${OKV}); unset IFS

problem: When 'read ... <<<' failed under sandbox (heredoc temp file could not be created), cargo.eclass GIT_CRATES parsing silently produced empty variables and confusing downstream errors.
root_cause: <<< here-strings need a temporary file; under sandbox restrictions creation can fail, and read's nonzero exit status was being ignored.
fix_pattern: Append an explicit '|| die' to the read-from-herestring so the failure surfaces with a clear message at the point of the problem.
rule: Any 'read ... <<<' (or other command whose failure would corrupt later logic) in an eclass/ebuild should carry '|| die' so sandbox or IO failures produce an immediate, attributable error.
snippet: IFS=';' read -r crate_uri commit crate_dir host <<< "${GIT_CRATES[${crate}]}" || die "${ECLASS}: could not create heredoc"

problem: Routine llvm.org.eclass snapshot addition (23.0.0_pre20260707 -> EGIT_COMMIT); same idiom as a8ace129 in this batch.
Adding a new Rust version (1.96.1) requires more than a new ebuild: consumers resolve Rust/LLVM slots through centralized maps in rust.eclass, which would not know the new version.
root_cause: rust.eclass keeps authoritative registries (_RUST_LLVM_MAP mapping each Rust slot to its LLVM slot, and _RUST_SLOTS_ORDERED for iteration order); a version absent from these maps is invisible to rust_pkg_setup/llvm dependency generation.
fix_pattern: In the same commit as the new ebuild, add the version to both _RUST_LLVM_MAP (with the correct bundled-LLVM slot) and _RUST_SLOTS_ORDERED (newest-first position), plus any needed package.use.force entries.
rule: When an eclass maintains a version-to-dependency registry, every version bump must update the eclass registry in lockstep with the ebuild, keeping ordering conventions (newest first).

problem: tree-sitter-grammar eclass declared PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{11..14} ), missing new python3_15 and still carrying soon-dead 3.11.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT brace ranges hardcode the supported interpreter window and rot as Gentoo adds/removes python targets.
fix_pattern: Roll the brace range forward: bump both ends of PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} ) when a new CPython is enabled tree-wide and the oldest is being retired.
rule: Treat PYTHON_COMPAT ranges as a rolling window: when a new python3_NN becomes available in the tree, extend the upper bound (and drop the lower bound only when that interpreter is actually being removed).

problem: optfeature.eclass rejected EAPI 9 ebuilds with 'EAPI not supported' even though its code needed no functional change.
root_cause: Eclasses whitelist EAPIs in a case statement and must be explicitly audited and extended for each new EAPI.
fix_pattern: Canonical minimal EAPI port: add the new EAPI to the case pattern, update @SUPPORTED_EAPIS, and (current style) move the inclusion guard 'if [[ -z ${_X_ECLASS} ]]' above the EAPI case so the check sits inside the guard.
rule: To port an eclass to a new EAPI, audit its functions for EAPI-dependent behavior, then extend both the @SUPPORTED_EAPIS doc line and the case-die whitelist together.

problem: qmake-utils.eclass needed EAPI 9 support, but its legacy qt5_*/eqmake5 functions should not be available to new-EAPI consumers.
root_cause: New EAPI adoption is the natural point to prune deprecated eclass API, but simply deleting functions would break existing EAPI-8 consumers.
fix_pattern: Add the new EAPI to the whitelist, then wrap the deprecated function definitions in 'if [[ ${EAPI} == 8 ]]; then ... fi' so they exist only for the old EAPI.
rule: When porting an eclass to a new EAPI, gate deprecated functions behind an EAPI conditional instead of deleting them, so old consumers keep working while new-EAPI ebuilds are forced off the legacy API.
snippet:
case ${EAPI} in
	8|9) ;;
	*) die "${ECLASS}: EAPI ${EAPI:-0} not supported" ;;
esac

if [[ ${EAPI} == 8 ]]; then
# qt5_get_bindir ... eqmake5 definitions
fi

problem: qt6_* helper functions lived in qmake-utils.eclass but were needed by non-qmake consumers, forcing an eclass split without breaking existing inheritors.
root_cause: Helper functions outgrow their original eclass; moving them naively would break every ebuild that inherits the old eclass for those functions.
fix_pattern: Move the functions to a new eclass (qt-utils), make the old eclass 'inherit qt-utils' unconditionally, and declare '# @PROVIDES: qt-utils' so pkgcheck knows the old eclass legitimately re-exports the new API.
rule: When relocating functions between eclasses, keep the old eclass inheriting the new one and document it with @PROVIDES so existing consumers stay source-compatible.

problem: Creation of qt-utils.eclass as the new home for qt6_* path helpers (companion to 42d72c44); also quotes all echoed paths that the old copies left unquoted.
The deprecated linux-mod.eclass was removed from the tree after last-riting; any overlay ebuild still inheriting it breaks with an unknown-eclass error.
root_cause: Gentoo replaces superseded eclasses (linux-mod -> linux-mod-r1) and removes the old one after a deprecation window; out-of-tree consumers are not migrated automatically.
fix_pattern: Migrate consumers to the -r1 successor before removal: watch for @DEPRECATED/@DEAD markers and tracker bugs (here #908692), and port inherit lines plus API differences ahead of the removal date.
rule: Audit overlay ebuilds for inherits of eclasses marked @DEPRECATED or @DEAD in ::gentoo and migrate them to the named successor before the eclass is deleted.

problem: nginx used SLOT=stable/mainline/live as abstract 'update stream' tags, causing recurring dependency-resolution and world-file bugs.
root_cause: SLOT was abused as a channel selector instead of expressing parallel-installability; slots that all block each other are just a worse version of a single slot.
fix_pattern: Collapse to SLOT="0/${PV}" (subslot still tracks the unstable module ABI), delete the mutual-blocker generator, add slotmove entries in profiles/updates/ for every old slot, and revbump every live ebuild so the metadata change propagates.
rule: Use SLOT only for genuinely co-installable variants (subslot for ABI); if you must retire bogus slots, pair the eclass/ebuild change with profiles/updates slotmove lines and a revbump of all affected ebuilds.
snippet:
-SLOT="${NGINX_UPDATE_STREAM}/${PV}"
+: "${SLOT=0/${PV}}"
# profiles/updates/2Q-2026:
slotmove www-servers/nginx mainline 0
slotmove www-servers/nginx stable 0
slotmove www-servers/nginx live 0

problem: qt5-build.eclass had no remaining in-tree consumers but was still formally alive, blocking cleanup.
root_cause: Eclass removal has a documented lifecycle: consumers removed first, then the eclass is annotated, then deleted.
fix_pattern: Add the single-line '# @DEAD' marker to the eclass doc header; pkgcheck then flags any new inherit and the eclass can be removed after the customary waiting period.
rule: Mark consumer-less eclasses with @DEAD (or @DEPRECATED with a successor) in the doc header before deleting them, so tooling warns downstream users during the grace period.

problem: Doc-only cleanup: removed a stale sentence claiming shell-completion.eclass still inherits bash-completion-r1 after the dependency was inverted.
Weekly LLVM snapshot versions (_preYYYYMMDD) need reproducible sources even though upstream publishes no tarball for arbitrary git states.
root_cause: Snapshot 'versions' are just dates; without pinning, a git checkout of main is unreproducible and un-verifiable.
fix_pattern: Centralize a case-on-PV table in the eclass that maps each _preYYYYMMDD version to an exact EGIT_COMMIT, so all llvm.org consumers fetch the identical pinned commit.
rule: For date-stamped _pre snapshot versions of VCS-only upstreams, pin the exact commit hash in one shared location (eclass or ebuild) keyed on ${PV} instead of fetching a moving branch.

problem: Bash completion install dir was queried from the build host's bash-completion.pc via pkg-config, so where files landed depended on what happened to be installed, producing inconsistent installations.
root_cause: Install paths derived from host pkg-config output are not deterministic (and wrong for cross/prefix corner cases); EAPI 9 was chosen as the boundary to stop honoring them.
fix_pattern: Gate the pkg-config lookup on EAPI: '[[ ${EAPI} != 9 ]] && $(tc-getPKG_CONFIG) --exists ...' and otherwise return the fixed well-known default directory; also gate the now-conditional 'inherit toolchain-funcs' the same way.
rule: Prefer fixed, policy-defined install directories over build-host pkg-config discovery; when tightening such behavior in a shared eclass, key the change to a new EAPI so existing ebuilds are untouched.
snippet:
if [[ ${EAPI} != 9 ]] && $(tc-getPKG_CONFIG) --exists bash-completion &>/dev/null; then
	path=$($(tc-getPKG_CONFIG) --variable="${1}" bash-completion) || die
	echo "${path#"${EPREFIX}"}"
	return
fi
echo "${2}"

problem: bash-completion-r1.eclass and shell-completion.eclass carried ~110 lines of duplicated function definitions after the newer eclass absorbed the old one's API.
root_cause: Phasing out an old eclass in favor of a superset successor leaves two copies of every function unless the old one becomes a shim.
fix_pattern: Reduce the deprecated eclass to a guard plus a single 'inherit shell-completion', and declare '# @PROVIDES: shell-completion' so consumers of the old name transparently receive the successor's implementation.
rule: Deprecate an eclass by turning it into a thin wrapper (inherit successor + @PROVIDES) after moving all functions into the successor; consumers need no immediate changes and code exists in exactly one place.
snippet:
# @SUPPORTED_EAPIS: 7 8
# @PROVIDES: shell-completion
if [[ -z ${_BASH_COMPLETION_R1_ECLASS} ]]; then
_BASH_COMPLETION_R1_ECLASS=1

inherit shell-completion

fi

problem: shell-completion.eclass supported only EAPI 8/9, but it was about to be inherited by bash-completion-r1.eclass which supports EAPI 7, so EAPI 7 ebuilds would die inside the inner eclass.
root_cause: An eclass's EAPI whitelist must cover the union of EAPIs of every eclass that inherits it, not just its direct ebuild consumers.
fix_pattern: Extend the case whitelist and @SUPPORTED_EAPIS to include EAPI 7 before making the old eclass a wrapper.
rule: Before making eclass A inherit eclass B, ensure B's supported-EAPI set is a superset of A's, or the inherit chain will die on older-EAPI consumers.

problem: Doc-only change: added a @DESCRIPTION note steering EAPI 9+ ebuilds to shell-completion.eclass instead of bash-completion-r1.
Minimal EAPI 9 enablement for shell-completion.eclass (case whitelist + @SUPPORTED_EAPIS); same idiom captured for 601b1d1b in this batch.
To retire bash-completion-r1.eclass, its functions had to move into shell-completion.eclass without any window where either eclass's consumers break.
root_cause: Eclass consolidation must be ordered so both names keep providing the full API at every commit; the successor cannot keep inheriting the eclass it is replacing.
fix_pattern: Step 1 of the two-step consolidation: copy all public/internal functions verbatim from the old eclass into the successor, drop the successor's 'inherit bash-completion-r1' and its '@PROVIDES: bash-completion-r1', keeping function names (including _bash-completion-r1_* internals) unchanged for compatibility.
rule: When merging eclass A into successor B, first inline A's functions into B (removing B's inherit of A), and only then convert A into a wrapper that inherits B.
snippet:
-# @PROVIDES: bash-completion-r1
...
--# Extend bash-completion-r1
--inherit bash-completion-r1
+inherit toolchain-funcs
+# (verbatim copies of get_bashcompdir, dobashcomp, newbashcomp, bashcomp_alias ...)

problem: greadme.eclass auto-formatted README.gentoo text through 'fmt' by default, mangling deliberate here-doc formatting; the default needed to flip to off without changing behavior for existing EAPI 8 ebuilds.
root_cause: Changing an eclass default silently alters every consumer; the safe boundary for a behavior flip is a new EAPI, since each ebuild opts into an EAPI explicitly.
fix_pattern: Keep the old default (auto-format, opt-out via GREADME_DISABLE_AUTOFORMAT) for EAPI 8 and introduce the new default (no auto-format, opt-in via GREADME_AUTOFORMAT) for EAPI 9; also inherit the eapi9-pipestatus compat eclass only for EAPI 8 because EAPI 9 provides pipestatus natively.
rule: Flip eclass behavior defaults at an EAPI boundary with paired opt-in/opt-out variables, and drop eapi9-* compat-shim inherits for ebuilds already on EAPI 9.
snippet:
case ${EAPI} in
	8) inherit eapi9-pipestatus ;;
	9) ;;
esac
...
if [[ ${EAPI} == 8 ]]; then
	autoformat=1; [[ ${GREADME_DISABLE_AUTOFORMAT} ]] && autoformat=
else
	autoformat=; [[ ${GREADME_AUTOFORMAT} ]] && autoformat=1
fi

problem: acct-user packages whose home directory is owned by a different user/group (ACCT_USER_HOME_OWNER=foo:bar) failed at installation time when that user or group was not yet installed, because most ebuilds forgot the manual acct-user/acct-group RDEPEND.
root_cause: fowners-style ownership at pkg_preinst requires the referenced user/group to exist, but nothing enforced the corresponding acct-* dependency; humans forgot it more often than not.
fix_pattern: Extend acct-user_add_deps to parse ACCT_USER_HOME_OWNER (user[:group]), auto-appending 'acct-user/<user>' when it differs from ACCT_USER_NAME and 'acct-group/<group>' when not already in ACCT_USER_GROUPS.
rule: Every user or group name an ebuild assigns ownership with must be backed by an acct-user/acct-group RDEPEND; encode such dependency derivation in the eclass instead of trusting each ebuild to remember it.

problem: make_desktop_entry/make_session_desktop offered a transitional --eapi9 flag to opt into new behavior early, but in actual EAPI 9 the new behavior was only active if the (now obsolete, dying) flag was passed - i.e. never.
root_cause: The flag-parsing logic conflated 'flag present' with 'new behavior wanted'; once the new EAPI arrived, the behavior had to become unconditional while the flag turned into an error, and the original if-structure could not express that.
fix_pattern: Restructure to case-on-EAPI first: for EAPI 7|8 the flag opts in (eapi9=1 + shift); for newer EAPIs set eapi9=1 unconditionally and die if the obsolete flag is still passed.
rule: For transitional --eapiN option flags in eclass functions, make the new behavior the unconditional default once EAPI N is real, and turn the flag itself into a die - test both branches, since the opt-in path stops being exercised.
snippet:
case ${EAPI} in
	7|8)
		if [[ ${1} == --eapi9 ]]; then eapi9=1; shift; fi ;;
	*)
		eapi9=1
		[[ ${1} == --eapi9 ]] && die "--eapi9 arg is obsolete in EAPI-${EAPI}!" ;;
esac

problem: java-utils-2.eclass appended BDEPEND on dev-java/javatoolkit and java-dep-check inside 'if is-java-strict; then ... fi', making the dependency set change with the JAVA_PKG_STRICT environment variable; this had to be reverted.
root_cause: Ebuild metadata (DEPEND/BDEPEND/etc.) is cached and must be a pure function of the ebuild+eclass text; is-java-strict reads an environment variable, so deps silently differed between metadata generation and build time - invalid for ::gentoo.
fix_pattern: Revert the conditional dependency block and its escape-hatch variable; if a QA tool is needed for an optional strict mode, it must be handled outside metadata (e.g. unconditional dep, USE flag, or documented manual install).
rule: Never make dependency variables conditional on environment variables or any state outside the ebuild source (USE flags are the only sanctioned conditional); metadata must be deterministic and cacheable.
snippet:
# INVALID pattern that was reverted:
if is-java-strict; then  # reads JAVA_PKG_STRICT from env
	BDEPEND="${BDEPEND} dev-java/javatoolkit"
	BDEPEND="${BDEPEND} dev-java/java-dep-check:0"
fi

problem: junit5.eclass post-test QA ran jdeps over every *.class file found; test resources that merely have a .class extension (not real JVM classfiles) made jdeps fail and die the build.
root_cause: File-extension matching is not file-type validation; jdeps aborts on inputs lacking the JVM classfile magic number.
fix_pattern: Filter by magic bytes before invoking the tool: read the first 4 bytes with od, keep only files beginning cafebabe, eqawarn and skip the rest, and pipe the survivors NUL-delimited into xargs -r -0 jdeps.
rule: When feeding globbed files to a strict binary-format tool in an ebuild check, validate each file's magic number first and skip mismatches with a QA warning instead of letting the tool die.
snippet:
find "${classes}" -type f -name '*.class' -print0 |
while IFS= read -r -d '' classfile; do
	magic=$(od -An -tx1 -N4 "${classfile}" | tr -d ' \n')
	if [[ ${magic} == cafebabe ]]; then printf '%s\0' "${classfile}"
	else eqawarn "Skipping invalid .class file: ${classfile#${classes}/}"; fi
done | xargs -r -0 "${jdeps_bin}" > "${jdeps_output}" || die "jdeps failed"

problem: Dependency on sys-kernel/linux-firmware[redistributable,-unknown-license] became unsatisfiable when upstream dropped the unknown-license USE flag
root_cause: A USE-conditional dependency referencing a flag the target package no longer declares in IUSE cannot be matched; plain [flag]/[-flag] deps require the flag to exist in every matched version
fix_pattern: Append the USE-default '(-)' to the flag in the dependency atom so versions lacking the flag are treated as having it disabled
rule: When a dependency's USE flag may be absent in some versions (flag being added or removed over time), suffix it with (+) or (-) USE-defaults so the atom stays satisfiable across the transition.

problem: Routine bump of the _LLVM_NEWEST_STABLE gating variable to 22 (controls which LLVM slots get USE defaults); bug 977824
Same one-line _LLVM_NEWEST_STABLE=22 bump as in llvm-r2.eclass; bug 977824
mount-boot.eclass touched a /boot/.keep file in pkg_prerm, leaving a stray file and keeping an empty /boot directory alive after the last boot-related package was unmerged
root_cause: The .keep hack was redundant: if /boot is a mountpoint the eclass ensures it is mounted so Portage cannot remove it anyway, and if it is an empty plain directory there is no reason to preserve it
fix_pattern: Delete the touch-.keep code from pkg_prerm entirely and rely on mount state to protect the directory
rule: Only create .keep files (keepdir) for directories that genuinely must survive unmerge; a mounted filesystem cannot be removed by Portage, so mountpoints need no .keep protection.

problem: unipatch died claiming genpatches is missing the point-release patch when building X.Y.0 kernels, where no incremental patch exists by definition
root_cause: The sanity check assumed every kernel release ships a matching version patch inside genpatches, which is false for the initial .0 release
fix_pattern: Introduce a documented opt-out ECLASS_VARIABLE (K_NO_VERSION_CHECK) and skip only the die when it is set, keeping the check for everyone else
rule: When an eclass sanity check has legitimate edge cases, add a documented opt-out variable for those cases instead of weakening or removing the check globally.
snippet:
if [[ -z ${K_NO_VERSION_CHECK} && -z ${KV_PATCH_FOUND} ]]; then
	die "GENPATCHES appears to be missing Linux patch ${OKV}"
fi

problem: /usr/src/linux symlink was not updated when upgrading gentoo-kernel-bin because its localversion suffix changed from -gentoo-dist to -gentoo-dist-bin, so the eclass treated the old install as a foreign kernel and refused to touch the symlink
root_cause: Symlink-migration logic only matches the current package's KV_LOCALVERSION; renaming the suffix makes previously-installed versions look unrelated
fix_pattern: Add a temporary migration branch: match the exact package, use absence of the formerly mutually-exclusive package as a heuristic (! has_version sys-kernel/gentoo-kernel), and re-run the eligibility check with the old suffix via a one-shot env override on the function call
rule: When renaming a package or changing its version/localversion suffix, add a temporary migration path keyed on a has_version heuristic so upgrades from the old naming are treated as in-place updates instead of foreign installs.
snippet:
elif [[ ${CATEGORY}/${PN} == sys-kernel/gentoo-kernel-bin ]] &&
	! has_version sys-kernel/gentoo-kernel &&
	KV_LOCALVERSION=${KV_LOCALVERSION%-bin} \
	dist-kernel_can_update_src_symlink "${target}"
then
	ln -f -n -s "${target##*/}-${version}" "${target}"

problem: Pure arch-list extension: alpha, m68k, mips added to the existing hppa|ppc|... case that maps arches to the vmlinux image path
DISTUTILS_SINGLE_IMPL packages got a broken PEP517 backend BDEPEND after a refactor: PYTHON_USEDEP was expanded immediately at eclass load instead of being substituted later by python-single-r1
root_cause: The dependency template must contain the literal text ${PYTHON_USEDEP} for deferred substitution, but the refactor put it in a double-quoted string (needed to expand ${DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517} immediately), so both expanded at once
fix_pattern: Escape the deferred placeholder inside the double-quoted string so only the intended variable expands now: dev-python/${DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517}[\${PYTHON_USEDEP}]
rule: In dependency template strings that are post-processed (PYTHON_USEDEP substitution in single-impl python), backslash-escape the placeholder when the same double-quoted string must expand other variables immediately; mixing immediate and deferred expansion is a classic eclass bug.

problem: epytest's has_version check for dev-python/pytest-jobserver used [${PYTHON_USEDEP}], which broke in contexts where PYTHON_USEDEP is not declared (python-any-r1 ebuilds) and was too restrictive; bugs 975685, 977387
root_cause: Helper functions must not depend on optional caller context; PYTHON_USEDEP only exists when the consuming eclass exports it
fix_pattern: Construct the USE-dep from the running interpreter instead of caller-provided variables, with a (-) default for robustness
rule: To test at runtime whether an installed python package supports the current interpreter, derive the python_targets_* flag from EPYTHON with a (-) USE-default instead of relying on PYTHON_USEDEP being set.
snippet:
local usedep="python_targets_${EPYTHON/./_}(-)"
if has_version "dev-python/pytest-jobserver[${usedep}]"; then
	args+=( -p jobserver )
fi

problem: DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 accepted values (flit, flit_scm, jupyter, poetry) that did not match the dev-python package names of the backends, blocking a data-driven implementation and confusing maintainers
root_cause: Historical value naming drifted from the actual backend package names
fix_pattern: Rename values to the package names (flit-core, flit-scm, jupyter-packaging, poetry-core); keep an EAPI-gated compatibility shim that rewrites old spellings only for EAPI 7/8; collapse per-backend BDEPEND cases into one generic dev-python/${DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517} branch using case fallthrough ;& and drop obsolete version constraints
rule: From EAPI 9, DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 must use the backend package name (flit-core, flit-scm, jupyter-packaging, poetry-core, not flit/flit_scm/jupyter/poetry) - fix the value when bumping a python ebuild's EAPI; when deprecating eclass variable values, gate the old spellings on old EAPIs rather than removing them outright.

problem: Python packages installing compiled extension modules (*.so) without DISTUTILS_EXT=1 only produced a QA warning, so packages missing the correct build flags/handling went unnoticed
root_cause: eqawarn-only enforcement lets maintainers ignore the misconfiguration indefinitely; DISTUTILS_EXT controls extension-specific behavior that matters for correctness
fix_pattern: Keep the eqawarn but add an EAPI-gated die so the QA notice becomes fatal in EAPI 9: [[ ${EAPI} != [78] ]] && die "DISTUTILS_EXT not set"
rule: Set DISTUTILS_EXT=1 in any distutils-r1 ebuild that installs compiled extension modules; from EAPI 9 the missing setting is a fatal error, not just a QA notice - check this when bumping EAPI.
snippet:
eqawarn "QA Notice: Python extension modules (*$(get_modname)) found installed. Please set:"
eqawarn "  DISTUTILS_EXT=1"
[[ ${EAPI} != [78] ]] && die "DISTUTILS_EXT not set"

problem: Obsolete interpreters left in PYTHON_COMPAT (python3_11 and older, pypy3, jython, python3_13t...) and stale version patterns in python_gen_cond_dep were silently ignored, accumulating cruft in ebuilds
root_cause: Silently skipping obsolete implementations gives maintainers no incentive to clean up; the eclass distinguishes impls deprecated before vs after EAPI 9 to phase enforcement in
fix_pattern: Make implementations deprecated before EAPI 9 fatal in EAPI 9 (die with an explicit cleanup message) while keeping later deprecations non-fatal via case fallthrough; apply the same EAPI gate to old version patterns in _python_impl_matches
rule: Prune dead implementations from PYTHON_COMPAT and stale version patterns from python_gen_cond_dep calls before bumping a python ebuild to EAPI 9 - they become fatal errors there.
snippet:
jython2_7|pypy|...|python3_1[01]|python3_13t)
	[[ ${EAPI} != [78] ]] &&
		die "Please remove old implementations from PYTHON_COMPAT for EAPI ${EAPI}"
	;&

problem: Routine removal of python3_11 from kernel-build.eclass PYTHON_COMPAT brace range (python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..14})
Plumbing change: pass the new SECUREBOOT_SIGN_TOOL variable through to ukify via --signtool and add --no-sign-kernel because systemd-sbsign lacks --sign-kernel and the kernel image is already signed
Dist-kernels built from a Debian-derived base config kept CONFIG_MODULE_SIG enabled even with USE=-modules-sign, because module signing is on by default in that config
root_cause: The config merge only added an 'enable signing' fragment when the USE flag was on; with the flag off nothing overrode the base config - absence of an enable fragment does not disable an inherited kconfig default
fix_pattern: Always merge a fragment for the feature: write an explicit disable fragment using '# CONFIG_FOO is not set' lines in the else branch and merge it unconditionally; also hoist the default assignment (: "${MODULES_SIGN_HASH:=sha512}") out of the USE-conditional up to the variable declaration
rule: When merging kernel config fragments driven by a USE flag, emit an explicit '# CONFIG_X is not set' fragment for the off case - never assume the base defconfig has the feature disabled.
snippet:
else
	cat <<-EOF > "${WORKDIR}/modules-sign.config" || die
		# CONFIG_MODULE_SIG is not set
		# CONFIG_MODULE_SIG_ALL is not set
		# CONFIG_MODULE_SIG_FORCE is not set
	EOF
fi
merge_configs+=( "${WORKDIR}/modules-sign.config" )

problem: pkg_preinst died on rm of merged-usr module symlinks when installing from a quickpkg binary package; bug 977187
root_cause: quickpkg can cause the pkg_preinst phase to run twice; the phase unconditionally removed and recreated symlinks that only exist on the first run
fix_pattern: Guard every destructive operation with an existence test before acting: if [[ -L path ]]; then rm ... || die; dosym ...; fi - making the phase a no-op on re-run
rule: Write phase functions idempotently: pkg_preinst (and friends) may run more than once (e.g. via quickpkg), so check that a file or symlink still exists before removing or replacing it.
snippet:
for file in build source; do
	if [[ -L "${ED}/lib/modules/${version}/${file}" ]]; then
		rm "${ED}/lib/modules/${version}/${file}" || die
		dosym "../../../src/linux-${version}" "/usr/lib/modules/${version}/${file}"
	fi
done

problem: Feature addition: SECUREBOOT_SIGN_TOOL user variable selecting sbsign vs systemd-sbsign (different OpenSSL provider support), with a case statement dying on invalid values; sbverify has no systemd equivalent so sbsigntools stays a hard dep

UKI measured-boot (PCR policy) signing was forced to reuse the Secure Boot key, which fails when the TPM supports different algorithms than shim, and bad key/cert paths were only discovered deep into src_install; bug 973344
root_cause: One key served two mechanisms with different algorithm constraints, and no early validation of user-supplied key material existed
fix_pattern: Add parallel MEASUREDBOOT_SIGN_KEY/CERT/HASH user variables that default to the secureboot ones via : "${NEW:=${OLD}}" for backward compat, and add a pkg_setup sanity check running openssl x509 against the configured key/cert that dies early with a clear message (handling pkcs11: URIs separately)
rule: When splitting one user-configured credential into two, default the new variables to the old ones for backward compatibility, and validate user-supplied keys/paths in pkg_setup so misconfiguration fails before the expensive build.
snippet:
: "${MEASUREDBOOT_SIGN_KEY:=${SECUREBOOT_SIGN_KEY}}"
: "${MEASUREDBOOT_SIGN_CERT:=${SECUREBOOT_SIGN_CERT}}"
openssl x509 "${openssl_args[@]}" ||
	die "Measured Boot signing certificate or key not found or not PEM format."

problem: QA notice about a dangling symlink in installed kernel sources: scripts/ ships a symlink into tools/, but only a stripped-down tools/ is installed, orphaning the link
root_cause: Installing a pruned subset of an upstream source tree can leave symlinks pointing into paths that were not installed
fix_pattern: After copying files into the image, delete all broken symlinks in one pass: find -L dir -type l -delete (with -L, only dangling symlinks still test as type l)
rule: When installing a pruned copy of an upstream tree, run 'find -L "${ED}<dir>/" -type l -delete || die' after copying to remove dead symlinks and silence the broken-symlink QA check.
snippet:
# Clean up dead symlinks
find -L "${ED}${kernel_dir}/" -type l -delete || die

problem: Documentation hygiene: underscore-prefixed eclass function tagged @INTERNAL in its doc block
Documentation hygiene: two underscore-prefixed helper functions tagged @INTERNAL
Cleanup: removed !kde-plasma/${PN}:5 blocker exclusions for packages that only ever existed in one SLOT, so the blockers were no-ops
Dead-code removal: dropped the _PSLOT Plasma 5/6 switch variable after the last Plasma 5 packages were removed from the tree
Eclass only allowed EAPI 8, blocking consumers from moving to EAPI 9
root_cause: EAPI allow-list in the case ${EAPI} guard and @SUPPORTED_EAPIS doc were pinned to 8
fix_pattern: Add the new EAPI to both the @SUPPORTED_EAPIS doc header and the case guard (8|9), bump the copyright year, and audit EAPI-conditional code paths; the commit also repositioned the inclusion guard relative to the EAPI check
rule: To add a new EAPI to an eclass, update both the @SUPPORTED_EAPIS documentation and the case ${EAPI} guard together, then audit every EAPI-conditional branch in the eclass for behavior changes in the new EAPI.

problem: Cleanup of :5 blockers for last-rited packages; noteworthy only for the 'TODO: drop on 2026-09-01' comment idiom annotating temporary blockers with an expiry date
Eclass only allowed EAPI 8; ebuilds moving to EAPI 9 would die at inherit time.
root_cause: EAPI allowlist in the eclass is a hard case statement; new EAPIs must be opted in explicitly per Gentoo policy.
fix_pattern: Add 9 to the EAPI case, update @SUPPORTED_EAPIS, and move the inclusion guard (if [[ -z ${_FOO_ECLASS} ]]) ABOVE the EAPI check so the check runs once inside the guard - this is the current devmanual skeleton ordering used in all EAPI-9 eclass migrations.
rule: When adding a new EAPI to an eclass, update @SUPPORTED_EAPIS and the case statement together, and adopt the guard-first skeleton (inclusion guard wraps the EAPI check).

problem: Routine removal of dead KF5 SLOT=5 ver_test branch after last KF5 consumers left the tree; no generalizable content.
Same EAPI-9 enablement pattern as 59722c2 (add 9 to case, guard-first skeleton); duplicate of that lesson.
Removal of unused Qt5PatchCollection dev-qt handling from kde.org.eclass; dead-code cleanup with no generalizable content.

kde.org_src_unpack called cargo_src_unpack whenever cargo.eclass was inherited, breaking packages that inherit cargo with CARGO_OPTIONAL=1 and USE-conditional rust support (cargo phases must not run when rust was not set up).
root_cause: 'has cargo ${INHERITED}' only proves the eclass was sourced, not that the optional feature is active; with CARGO_OPTIONAL=1 cargo functions are only valid after rust_pkg_setup exported CARGO.
fix_pattern: Gate cross-eclass phase calls on the eclass's activation marker variable in addition to inheritance: has cargo ${INHERITED} && [[ -n ${CARGO} ]]. This mirrors what cargo.eclass itself does internally.
rule: When an eclass conditionally invokes another eclass's phase functions, check both that the eclass is inherited AND that its OPTIONAL-mode activation marker (e.g. ${CARGO}) is set.
snippet:
local isrusty
if has cargo ${INHERITED} && [[ -n "${CARGO}" ]]; then
	isrusty=true
fi
[[ ${isrusty} ]] && cargo_src_unpack || default

problem: Packages mixing kde.org sources with Rust (cargo) needed manual src_unpack overrides in every ebuild: releases must use cargo_src_unpack (to unpack crates), live ebuilds need git-r3 plus Corrosion crate handling.
root_cause: A single exported src_unpack must dispatch differently depending on which build-system eclasses the consumer inherited and whether the build is live or release.
fix_pattern: Dispatch inside the eclass phase on ${INHERITED}: release + cargo -> cargo_src_unpack; live -> git-r3_src_unpack + default, then if both cmake and cargo are inherited run the Corrosion crate unpacker.
rule: When an eclass phase must cooperate with optional companion eclasses, branch on 'has <eclass> ${INHERITED}' inside the phase instead of requiring every ebuild to override the phase.
snippet:
case ${KDE_BUILD_TYPE} in
	live)
		git-r3_src_unpack; default
		has cmake ${INHERITED} && has cargo ${INHERITED} && \
			_kde.org_live_corrosion_unpack ;;
	*)
		has cargo ${INHERITED} && cargo_src_unpack || default ;;
esac

problem: Live (9999) CMake+Rust packages using Corrosion have multiple independent crate roots; cargo_live_src_unpack only handles one ${S}, so crates were not fetched for every corrosion_import_crate project.
root_cause: Corrosion imports crates per-CMakeLists.txt, so each directory containing corrosion_import_crate is a separate cargo project needing its own live unpack; also errors inside a process substitution pipeline are silently ignored by default.
fix_pattern: Find every CMakeLists.txt mentioning corrosion_import_crate and run cargo_live_src_unpack once per directory with S overridden per-call; check the pipeline inside the process substitution with pipestatus || die, and for EAPI 8 inherit the eapi9-pipestatus compat eclass via a fallthrough case (;&).
rule: When iterating a pipeline via process substitution in an eclass, append 'pipestatus || die' inside the substitution to catch failures, and use 'case ${EAPI} in 8) inherit eapi9-pipestatus ;& *) inherit ...' to pull compat shims only for old EAPIs.
snippet:
case ${EAPI} in
	8) inherit eapi9-pipestatus ;&
	*) inherit git-r3 ;;
esac
...
while IFS= read -r -d '' path ; do
	S="${path%/*}" cargo_live_src_unpack
done < <(find "${S}" -type f -iname CMakeLists.txt -print0 | \
	xargs -0 grep -z -Z -l -i corrosion_import_crate; pipestatus || die)

problem: Same EAPI-9 enablement pattern as 59722c2 (add 9 to case, guard-first skeleton); duplicate of that lesson.

After dropping KF5 dual-slot support, ebuilds still setting KFMIN<5.240 would silently get wrong (KF6-only) dependencies; also the exported pkg_setup became a no-op but existing EAPI-8 consumers still expect it.
root_cause: Removing a legacy code path from an eclass changes behavior for consumers that still pass the legacy trigger value; silently ignoring it produces broken depgraphs instead of an actionable error.
fix_pattern: Add a fail-fast guard 'ver_test ${KFMIN} -lt 5.240 && die "KF5 is unsupported!"' at eclass load, keep a documented no-op phase and export it only for the old EAPI: if [[ ${EAPI} == 8 ]]; then EXPORT_FUNCTIONS pkg_setup; fi.
rule: When dropping legacy support from an eclass, die loudly on the legacy trigger value and keep no-op exported phases only for EAPIs that previously had them.
snippet:
ver_test ${KFMIN} -lt 5.240 && die "KF5 is unsupported!"
...
if [[ ${EAPI} == 8 ]]; then
	EXPORT_FUNCTIONS pkg_setup
fi
EXPORT_FUNCTIONS src_prepare src_configure

problem: Swapped CMake FeatureSummary include for the ECM-flavored ECMFeatureSummary/ecm_feature_summary in generated CMakeLists; KDE-specific API preference, no generalizable content.
Defaulting KFMIN from ${PV} for kde-frameworks category made live/9999 versions demand unreleased dependency versions; dropped the quirk in favor of a fixed baseline. Narrow KDE-specific fix.
Eclass detected sibling eclasses by testing their internal include-guard variables ([[ -n ${_KDE_ORG_ECLASS} ]]), which is private API and breaks if the guard is renamed or the skeleton changes.
root_cause: Include-guard variables are @INTERNAL implementation details; PMS provides ${INHERITED} as the supported record of sourced eclasses.
fix_pattern: Replace every [[ -n ${_FOO_ECLASS} ]] cross-eclass check with 'has foo ${INHERITED}'.
rule: To test whether another eclass is active, use 'has <eclass-name> ${INHERITED}', never its private _FOO_ECLASS guard variable.

problem: Upstream CMake options like ENABLE_PCH and WARNINGS_AS_ERRORS default to ON in some packages, breaking or polluting distro builds; but passing -D...=OFF to every package triggers 'Manually-specified variables were not used' warnings where the option does not exist.
root_cause: CMake warns about -D variables the project never reads, so blanket-disabling options across many packages is noisy; the option must be disabled only where defined.
fix_pattern: Grep the root CMakeLists.txt for the option declaration and emit the -D flag only on match; collect flags from a helper function via command substitution into cmakeargs.
rule: When force-disabling an upstream CMake option across many packages, grep CMakeLists.txt for 'option.*NAME' first and pass -DNAME=OFF only if present.
snippet:
_ecm_disable_unwanted() {
	if grep -Eq "option.*ENABLE_PCH" CMakeLists.txt; then
		echo "-DENABLE_PCH=OFF"
	fi
	if grep -Eq "option.*WARNINGS_AS_ERRORS" CMakeLists.txt; then
		echo "-DWARNINGS_AS_ERRORS=OFF"
	fi
}
cmakeargs+=( $(_ecm_disable_unwanted) )

problem: Unconditionally passing -DBUILD_QCH=$(usex doc) produced unused-variable CMake warnings on packages that dropped the BUILD_QCH option (ported to ECMGenerateQDoc).
root_cause: The eclass passed a -D flag for an option some consumers no longer define; CMake warns on manually-specified unused variables.
fix_pattern: Asymmetric gating: always pass =ON when the feature is requested (USE=doc, missing option should then be a visible failure), but pass =OFF only if grep finds the option in CMakeLists.txt.
rule: For USE-conditional CMake options that upstreams are phasing out, pass ON unconditionally when enabled but grep-guard the OFF case to avoid unused-variable warnings.
snippet:
_ecm_buildqch_optional() {
	if use doc; then
		echo "-DBUILD_QCH=ON"
	elif grep -Eq "option.*BUILD_QCH" CMakeLists.txt; then
		echo "-DBUILD_QCH=OFF"
	fi
}

problem: ecm.eclass selected Qt6 by passing -DQT_MAJOR_VERSION=6, a mechanism upstream KDE has discouraged; risk of build systems preferring Qt5 if Qt5Core is found before QtVersionOption.cmake is included.
root_cause: ECM's QtVersionOption.cmake defines BUILD_WITH_QT6 as the supported switch; QT_MAJOR_VERSION is legacy and not honored everywhere.
fix_pattern: Set the supported switch as a preloaded cache variable in the common config file instead of a -D arg: set(BUILD_WITH_QT6 ON CACHE BOOL "").
rule: For ECM/KDE-style CMake projects, hard-select Qt6 with BUILD_WITH_QT6=ON (cache preload) rather than QT_MAJOR_VERSION=6.
snippet: set(BUILD_WITH_QT6 ON CACHE BOOL "") # QtVersionOption.cmake: Hard-require Qt6

problem: Live (git) builds of KDE packages ran upstream CMake logic that detects git and installs commit hooks into the checkout (program detection, sandbox-unfriendly side effects).
root_cause: Upstream CMakeLists guards its git-hook setup with a GIT_SOURCE_TARBALL flag that is TRUE only in git checkouts; distro builds from git should behave like tarball builds.
fix_pattern: In src_prepare, when building from a git checkout ([[ -d .git ]]), sed the GIT_SOURCE_TARBALL flag from TRUE to FALSE in CMakeLists.txt, guarded by a grep so untouched packages stay untouched.
rule: For -9999 ebuilds, neutralize upstream build-system logic that keys on 'is this a git checkout' (hook installation, git describe versioning) so live builds behave like release builds.
snippet:
if [[ -d .git ]]; then
	if grep -Eq "\s*set.*GIT_SOURCE_TARBALL TRUE" CMakeLists.txt; then
		sed -e "/\s*set.*GIT_SOURCE_TARBALL/s/TRUE/FALSE/" -i CMakeLists.txt || die
	fi
fi

problem: Large KF5 support removal from ecm.eclass (drop _KFSLOT machinery, ban legacy ECM_TEST values with explicit die); same die-on-legacy-trigger idiom already captured for 5afc1d0.
Under 'nonfatal cmake_src_compile', build failures were reported as success: ebuilds checking the return value of cmake_build never saw the failure.
root_cause: cmake_build ended with 'popd > /dev/null || die' after the build command; under nonfatal, die returns instead of aborting and the function's return value became popd's exit status (0), clobbering the build tool's $?.
fix_pattern: Capture $? immediately after the build command, do the cleanup, then return the saved code explicitly.
rule: In any helper meant to work under nonfatal, save $? right after the real command and 'return $rc' after cleanup - never let a trailing popd/cleanup command define the function's exit status.
snippet:
local rc=$? # save build tool's exit code in case of running under nonfatal
	popd > /dev/null || die
	return $rc

problem: CMAKE_SYSTEM_NAME was only written into the generated toolchain file when cross-compiling, causing inconsistent behavior for packages that expect it always set (bug 975603).
root_cause: CMake treats a manually set CMAKE_SYSTEM_NAME as 'this is a cross build' and flips CMAKE_CROSSCOMPILING to true; so setting it unconditionally is only safe if CMAKE_CROSSCOMPILING is also set explicitly to TRUE or FALSE.
fix_pattern: Always emit CMAKE_SYSTEM_NAME (mapped from ${KERNEL}) plus an explicit CMAKE_CROSSCOMPILING computed from tc-is-cross-compiler; keep Windows RC compiler setup conditional inside the CMake if().
rule: If a CMake toolchain file sets CMAKE_SYSTEM_NAME unconditionally, it must also set CMAKE_CROSSCOMPILING explicitly, otherwise native builds are misdetected as cross builds.
snippet:
set(CMAKE_SYSTEM_NAME "${sysname}")
set(CMAKE_CROSSCOMPILING $(tc-is-cross-compiler && echo TRUE || echo FALSE))
if(CMAKE_CROSSCOMPILING AND CMAKE_SYSTEM_NAME STREQUAL "Windows")
	set(CMAKE_RC_COMPILER $(tc-getRC))
endif()

problem: Pure refactor: consolidated multiple conditional heredoc appends into one heredoc, moving shell conditionals into CMake-side if($(shell-test && echo 1 || echo 0)) expressions; no behavior change.
Kernel team wanted to retire separate vanilla-sources packages but still offer unpatched kernels; gentoo-sources always applied the full genpatches set.
root_cause: genpatches uses a numeric NNNN_ filename convention where <1500 are upstream incremental patches and >=1500 are distro patches, so a USE flag can filter by prefix.
fix_pattern: Add IUSE=vanilla; in unipatch(), when USE=vanilla, iterate the patch dir and delete every patch whose 4-digit numeric prefix is >1499 before applying, keeping only upstream incremental patches.
rule: When a patched-sources package needs an unpatched variant, encode patch provenance in a numeric filename convention and filter by prefix at prepare time behind a USE flag instead of maintaining a separate package.
snippet:
if in_iuse vanilla && use vanilla; then
	for patch in ${KPATCH_DIR}/*; do
		patchname="${patch##*/}"
		numericprefix="${patchname:0:4}"
		if [[ $numericprefix =~ ^[0-9]{4}$ ]] && (( numericprefix > 1499 )); then
			rm ${patch} 2>/dev/null
		fi
	done
fi

problem: Building gcc:15[ada,d] with gcc:11[d] plus ada-bootstrap-14 installed failed: Ada's bootstrap CC (needed for the 'configure -x ada' GNAT test) was clobbered by the D bootstrap setup which exports CC/CXX afterwards.
root_cause: Two setup helpers (toolchain_setup_ada, toolchain_setup_d) both export CC/CXX; last writer wins, and Ada strictly needs a CC matching its gnat1, while D tolerates mismatch because the GDC envvar covers what configure uses.
fix_pattern: Reorder the calls so the helper with the strict CC requirement (Ada) runs last: '_need_d_bootstrap && toolchain_setup_d' before '_need_ada_bootstrap_mangling && toolchain_setup_ada', with a comment explaining the ordering constraint.
rule: When multiple setup functions export the same environment variables (CC/CXX), run the one whose value is load-bearing last, and prefer per-language override variables (GDC) where they exist.
snippet:
# D goes first ... Ada fails a configure test w/o matching CC.
_need_d_bootstrap && toolchain_setup_d
_need_ada_bootstrap_mangling && toolchain_setup_ada

problem: toolchain_setup_ada exported only CC with the ada bootstrap -specs override, leaving CXX pointing at a different (possibly mismatched) compiler set up by the D bootstrap path.
root_cause: Compiler override via spec file was applied to CC but not CXX, so mixed-language configure/build steps used inconsistent toolchains.
fix_pattern: Export CXX with the same -specs=${T}/ada.spec override alongside CC.
rule: When overriding a compiler via spec files or wrappers for bootstrap, export CC and CXX as a consistent pair, never just one.

problem: Routine llvm.org.eclass snapshot addition: maps PV 23.0.0_preYYYYMMDD to a pinned EGIT_COMMIT in the eclass version table; no generalizable content beyond the existing pattern.

python3_11, python3_13t and pypy3_11 were removed from _PYTHON_ALL_IMPLS; ebuilds still listing them in PYTHON_COMPAT do not fail - the values are moved to _PYTHON_HISTORICAL_IMPLS and silently ignored as 'obsolete', so packages quietly lose those targets.
root_cause: python-utils-r1 treats historical implementations in PYTHON_COMPAT as ignorable rather than fatal, to allow gradual tree-wide removal; overlays that never clean PYTHON_COMPAT accumulate dead entries and may end up with an empty effective implementation set.
fix_pattern: Track main-tree impl lifecycle: removed impls move from _PYTHON_ALL_IMPLS to _PYTHON_HISTORICAL_IMPLS and from the 'supported' to the 'obsolete' branch of the _python_set_impls case; overlay maintainers should prune PYTHON_COMPAT and verify at least one supported impl remains.
rule: After the main tree drops a Python implementation, audit overlay PYTHON_COMPAT lists - obsolete values are silently ignored, not errors, so breakage only appears when no supported implementation is left.

problem: Routine update of eclass self-test to match currently supported CPython/PyPy versions after old impls were dropped.
wine.eclass pkg_postinst called 'use opengl || use vulkan' but wine-proton does not have those flags in IUSE, so the shared postinst broke (use on a non-IUSE flag) once wine-proton started using it.
root_cause: Eclass phase code assumed all consumers declare the same USE flags; 'use foo' on a flag absent from IUSE is invalid and behaves inconsistently.
fix_pattern: Guard every 'use foo' in shared eclass/phase code with in_iuse, treating an absent flag as enabled for warning purposes: '! in_iuse foo || use foo' means 'flag missing OR turned on'.
rule: When eclass or shared phase code tests a USE flag that not all consumers declare, wrap it as '! in_iuse flag || use flag' instead of a bare 'use flag'.

problem: Merged-usr /lib symlink fixup for kernel modules lived as inline code in kernel-install.eclass pkg_preinst and could not be reused by other kernel packaging.
root_cause: Useful logic was welded into one eclass's phase function instead of a parameterized helper.
fix_pattern: Extract into dist-kernel_update_lib_symlinks with optional args defaulting to KV_FULL/image path; detect merged-usr with '[[ -L ${EROOT}/lib && ${EROOT}/lib -ef ${EROOT}/usr/lib ]]' then rm the /lib symlinks in ${ED} and dosym /usr/lib/modules paths instead.
rule: When a package installs symlinks under /lib on a merged-usr system, detect it via '[[ -L ${EROOT}/lib && ${EROOT}/lib -ef ${EROOT}/usr/lib ]]' in pkg_preinst and recreate the links under /usr/lib to avoid collisions.
snippet:
if [[ -L ${EROOT}/lib && ${EROOT}/lib -ef ${EROOT}/usr/lib ]]; then
	rm "${ED}/lib/modules/${version}"/{build,source} || die
	dosym "../../../src/linux-${version}" "/usr/lib/modules/${version}/build"
fi

problem: kernel source symlink update helpers (can_update_symlink/update_symlink) were locked inside kernel-install.eclass, which exports phases, so packages not wanting those phases could not reuse them.
root_cause: Helpers placed in a phase-exporting eclass are unusable by ebuilds that must not inherit its phase functions.
fix_pattern: Move pure helper functions into the companion *-utils eclass (dist-kernel-utils) and have the phase eclass call them; keep names prefixed by the utils eclass. Version-detection logic strips revisions and checks REPLACING_VERSIONS/has_version -r before touching a symlink owned by another package.
rule: Put reusable helper functions in a non-phase-exporting utils eclass so both phase eclasses and standalone ebuilds can call them; before rewriting a shared symlink, verify it points at a version of this same package (REPLACING_VERSIONS or has_version -r), otherwise leave it alone.
snippet:
has "${symlink_ver}" ${REPLACING_VERSIONS} && return 0
has_version -r "~${symlink_pkg}" && return 0
# otherwise another kernel package owns it, leave alone
return 1

problem: linux-mod.eclass (superseded by linux-mod-r1) was last-rited.
root_cause: Deprecated eclass reached end of removal cycle (bug 908692).
fix_pattern: Add '# @DEAD' above the existing '# @DEPRECATED: linux-mod-r1.eclass' marker so pkgcheck flags any remaining consumers; the eclass is then removed after the grace period.
rule: Track @DEPRECATED/@DEAD markers on main-tree eclasses your overlay inherits and migrate consumers (e.g. linux-mod to linux-mod-r1) before the eclass is deleted; a deterministic grep for @DEAD/@DEPRECATED in inherited eclasses catches this early.

problem: Compiler flag probe piping preprocessor macros to '$(tc-getCXX) -E -P ... -' misbehaved for some users (bug 976305) because the compiler could not tell what language the stdin input was.
root_cause: When source is fed via stdin ('-'), the compiler driver cannot deduce the language from a file extension, and user CXXFLAGS with unusual options made the default guess wrong.
fix_pattern: Always pass an explicit '-x c++' (or '-x c') language flag when preprocessing/compiling from stdin in configure-style probes.
rule: When probing the toolchain by piping code into the compiler via '-', always add '-x c'/'-x c++' so the language is explicit regardless of user flags.

problem: Crate downloads via https://crates.io/api/v1/crates/<name>/<ver>/download started failing: the API endpoint bans download agents without a User-Agent (plain curl/wget), is officially 'slow and expensive', and required '-> name-ver.crate' arrow renames bloating SRC_URI.
root_cause: The API URL is just a redirect to the CDN and upstream now requires third-party build systems to fetch from the CDN directly.
fix_pattern: Point crate URIs straight at the static CDN, which also yields the correct filename so no arrow rename is needed: https://static.crates.io/crates/${name}/${name}-${version}.crate
rule: When generating crates.io SRC_URI (hand-rolled or in an old cargo.eclass copy), use https://static.crates.io/crates/<name>/<name>-<version>.crate instead of the crates.io/api download endpoint.

problem: Maintainer email address update across metadata.xml, profiles and eclass comments; no packaging content.
Workaround dependency 'dev-qt/qtbase:=' lacked an explicit slot, so the binding operator would bind to whatever slot is installed - wrong once a Qt7 slot appears.
root_cause: Bare ':=' on a multi-slot package means 'any slot, rebind on change'; without naming the slot the dep can resolve to an unintended slot in the future.
fix_pattern: Write the slot explicitly together with the binding operator: 'dev-qt/qtbase:6='.
rule: For dependencies on packages that have (or will have) multiple slots, always spell ':<slot>=' rather than a bare ':=' so the dep cannot silently bind to a different slot later.

problem: During Qt major/minor upgrades portage could schedule dependent Qt module rebuilds too late relative to qtbase (portage ordering bug 921333), breaking builds mid-upgrade.
root_cause: Exact-version pins alone do not make portage order the qtbase update before its consumers; a binding subslot operator gives the resolver a rebuild edge.
fix_pattern: Add a subslot-binding RDEPEND on the core package from the eclass so every consumer gets the ordering hint, and keep the workaround in one place for easy removal: '[[ ${PN} != @(qtbase|qttranslations) ]] && RDEPEND="dev-qt/qtbase:="'.
rule: When a family of packages must be rebuilt in lockstep with a core package and portage misorders the upgrade, add a ':=' binding dep on the core package - and centralize such temporary workarounds in the eclass, commented with the bug number, so cleanup is one deletion.

problem: Building gcc trunk with D enabled failed at link with 'undefined reference to std::__cxx11::basic_string...::_M_create_plus' - a libstdc++ symbol that only exists in the newer libstdc++.
root_cause: Mixed-version bootstrap: objects compiled by host g++ (trunk) used trunk libstdc++ headers, but the gdc-14 bootstrap compiler linked against libstdc++.so from gcc 14, so new symbols were unresolved.
fix_pattern: When selecting a specific older GCC to bootstrap a frontend (GDC for D), also export CC and CXX from the same bootstrap GCC bin dir so all objects share one libstdc++ version.
rule: When bootstrapping with a pinned older compiler, pin CC/CXX (and friends) to the same toolchain version - never let some translation units be built by a newer compiler whose C++ runtime headers outpace the runtime library you will link.
snippet:
export CC=${bootstrap_gcc_bin_dir}/${CHOST}-gcc
export CXX=${bootstrap_gcc_bin_dir}/${CHOST}-g++
export GDC=${bootstrap_gcc_bin_dir}/${CHOST}-gdc

problem: SELinux policy module cross-compilation failed (bug 964031): the build looked for interface files in the build host's /usr/share/selinux instead of the target sysroot.
root_cause: SHAREDIR was set from "${EPREFIX}"/usr/share/selinux; EPREFIX points at the build prefix, but files consumed at build time from installed dependencies live under ESYSROOT when cross-compiling.
fix_pattern: Change the path prefix from EPREFIX to ESYSROOT for build-time input locations: emake SHAREDIR="${ESYSROOT}"/usr/share/selinux.
rule: Any path an ebuild reads build-time inputs from (headers, policy interfaces, .pc dirs, module trees of DEPEND packages) must be rooted at ESYSROOT, not EPREFIX or EROOT - EROOT/ED are only for install-time destinations, EPREFIX only for the literal prefix component.

problem: Added s390 to the per-arch kernel image path case in dist-kernel_get_image_path; arch-specific table entry with no generalizable content.

Setting ZIG_SLOT to an array in an ebuild produced a baffling downstream error ('Invalid atom (dev-lang/zig:0.16,9999)') instead of a clear message, and validation only happened when a specific function ran, not at inherit.
root_cause: Eclass consumed a user-set variable in global BDEPEND without validating type or value at inherit time; bash silently flattens an array into a comma-joined scalar inside an atom.
fix_pattern: Wrap global-scope validation and BDEPEND construction in an internal _set_globals function executed at inherit time, then 'unset -f' it; check array-ness with '[[ ${VAR@a} =~ [aA] ]]' and die with a clear message; die early on missing/out-of-range values.
rule: Validate eclass input variables (set, correct type via ${VAR@a}, value range) in an inherit-time _set_globals function so misuse fails immediately with a clear message instead of surfacing as an invalid-atom or runtime error.
snippet:
_zig_utils_set_globals() {
	[[ -n "${ZIG_SLOT}" ]] || die "ZIG_SLOT must be set"
	[[ ${ZIG_SLOT@a} =~ [aA] ]] && die "ZIG_SLOT must be a string, not an array"
	ver_test "${ZIG_SLOT}" -lt "0.13" && die "ZIG_SLOT must be >= 0.13"
}
_zig_utils_set_globals
unset -f _zig_utils_set_globals

problem: Live ebuilds with Zig 0.16 failed with 'package not found': the eclass created the zig-pkg symlink as a relative path ('zig-pkg') in whatever the current directory was, while zig resolves it relative to the passed --build-file ${S}/build.zig.
root_cause: Helper relied on the current working directory when creating a symlink, but the consumer tool resolves the path relative to a different anchor (the build file location).
fix_pattern: Create the symlink with an explicit absolute destination: ln -s "${WORKDIR}/zig-pkg" "${S}/zig-pkg".
rule: In eclass/ebuild helpers, never create files or symlinks via cwd-relative paths - always anchor to ${S}/${WORKDIR}/${ED}, because phase functions give no guarantee about the current directory.

problem: GCC bootstrap unconditionally reset STAGE1_*FLAGS to -O2 for Ada/D even when the bootstrap compiler is the same version being built, needlessly dropping user flags; niche toolchain.eclass refinement.

SELinux policy releases historically encoded the upstream patch level as -rN in the version, but -rN is reserved for ebuild revisions, forcing an awkward ${PV/_p/-r} translation to keep old distfile names working.
root_cause: Upstream-ish patch levels were originally mapped onto Gentoo's revision suffix, colliding with the meaning of ebuild revisions.
fix_pattern: Use _pN in PV for patch bundles and name new distfiles after ${P} directly; keep the legacy ${PV/_p/-r} translation only for versions older than the cutover, gated with ver_test: 'elif [[ ${EAPI} = 8 ]] && ver_test -lt 2.20260101; then BASEPOL=${PV/_p/-r}; else BASEPOL=${PV}'.
rule: Never encode an upstream patch level as -rN (reserved for ebuild revisions); use _pN, and when migrating distfile naming schemes, gate old-name compatibility with a ver_test cutover instead of renaming old distfiles.

problem: Live ebuilds failed against upstream repos using the sha256 object format: git init defaults to sha1, so fetch/checkout of a sha256 remote errors with mismatched algorithm.
root_cause: git repositories have an immutable object format chosen at init; a sha1-initialized local store cannot fetch from a sha256 remote.
fix_pattern: Detect the remote format from hash length via 'git ls-remote <uri> HEAD' (40 chars = sha1, 64 = sha256), pass it to 'git init --object-format=', and on fetch failure print a hint to remove the stale local clone since in-place migration is impossible.
rule: When a live/VCS fetcher may meet sha256 git repos, probe the remote hash length with ls-remote and init with the matching --object-format; a format mismatch requires deleting the local clone, so surface that hint on fetch failure.

problem: Routine addition of a new LLVM snapshot: map PV 23.0.0_preYYYYMMDD to its EGIT_COMMIT in the eclass case table.
Routine removal of superseded LLVM snapshot entries from the eclass PV-to-commit table.
Routine bump of _LLVM_OLDEST_SLOT from 15 to 16; the eclass auto-filters out-of-range LLVM_COMPAT values so consumers need no edits.
Routine bump of _LLVM_OLDEST_SLOT from 15 to 16 mirroring llvm-r2.
Routine shrink of _LLVM_KNOWN_SLOTS brace range to {19..16} in the legacy llvm.eclass.
pytest-xdist test runs under a make jobserver (emerge --jobs coordination) oversubscribed CPUs because pytest did not participate in the jobserver.
root_cause: pytest-xdist spawns its own worker count ignoring the jobserver token protocol unless the pytest-jobserver plugin is loaded.
fix_pattern: Detect an active jobserver by pattern-matching MAKEFLAGS for --jobserver-auth=, then add '-p jobserver' if dev-python/pytest-jobserver is installed, else ewarn once (guarded by a _WARNED flag variable).
rule: To detect whether the build runs under a GNU make jobserver, test '[[ ${MAKEFLAGS} == *--jobserver-auth=* ]]' and only then enable jobserver-aware behavior in spawned tools.
snippet:
if [[ ${MAKEFLAGS} == *--jobserver-auth=* ]]; then
	if has_version "dev-python/pytest-jobserver[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]"; then
		args+=( -p jobserver )
	fi
fi

problem: distutils-r1 still called makeopts_jobs, which is banned in EAPI 9 (dies at runtime) in favor of get_makeopts_jobs.
root_cause: multiprocessing.eclass split its API: get_makeopts_jobs/get_makeopts_loadavg read all of MAKEOPTS, GNUMAKEFLAGS and MAKEFLAGS with saner defaults; the old makeopts_jobs is EAPI<=8 only.
fix_pattern: Replace $(makeopts_jobs) with $(get_makeopts_jobs) wherever a job count is passed to non-make tools.
rule: When moving ebuilds/eclasses to EAPI 9, replace makeopts_jobs/makeopts_loadavg with get_makeopts_jobs/get_makeopts_loadavg - the old names die in EAPI 9.

problem: Eclass still called the old multiprocessing.eclass helper makeopts_jobs for computing parallel job counts (python_optimize, epytest xdist).
root_cause: multiprocessing.eclass renamed/superseded makeopts_jobs with get_makeopts_jobs; old name is deprecated API.
fix_pattern: Mechanical substitution of the helper call: local jobs=$(get_makeopts_jobs) and jobs=${EPYTEST_JOBS:-$(get_makeopts_jobs)}.
rule: When an eclass helper is renamed (makeopts_jobs -> get_makeopts_jobs), grep for every call site and migrate to the new name before the old one is removed.

problem: Sanity check that genpatches contains the target kernel version failed when the patch tarball unpacked into subdirectories, because the bash glob dir/* only matched the top level (bug 974322).
root_cause: A for-loop over a non-recursive glob (for file in ${KPATCH_DIR}/*) cannot see files nested in subdirectories, and also word-splits on unquoted expansion.
fix_pattern: Replace the glob loop with a NUL-delimited find pipeline: while IFS= read -r -d '' file; do ...; done < <(find "$KPATCH_DIR" -type f -print0).
rule: When scanning a directory whose layout may include subdirectories, iterate with find -print0 | while read -d '' instead of a shell glob.
snippet:
while IFS= read -r -d '' file; do
    filename="${file##*/}"
    [[ "$filename" == *"${OKV}"* ]] && { KV_PATCH_FOUND=yes; break; }
done < <(find "$KPATCH_DIR" -type f -print0)

problem: Test suites using pytest-timeout together with pytest-rerunfailures could hang indefinitely during src_test (bugs 931020, 907289).
root_cause: Known bad interaction between the two pytest plugins (pytest-timeout#18, pytest-rerunfailures#99): the default signal-based timeout covers fixture/rerun machinery and deadlocks on rerun.
fix_pattern: When both EPYTEST_TIMEOUT and EPYTEST_RERUNS are active, epytest now adds -o timeout_func_only=true so the timeout applies only to the test function body.
rule: When pytest-timeout and pytest-rerunfailures are combined and tests hang, pass -o timeout_func_only=true to pytest.

problem: Python wheels started installing *.dist-info/sboms/ directories containing build paths (/var/tmp/portage), unreproducible timestamps, and crate hash inventories into the image.
root_cause: Newer wheel build backends (notably maturin for rust-backed wheels) emit SBOM files into dist-info; they are unreproducible junk the package manager already models.
fix_pattern: Extend the find -delete junk list in distutils_wheel_install with '*.dist-info/sboms' and '*.dist-info/sboms/*' paths, same treatment as licenses/ and license_files.
rule: When wheels ship new dist-info metadata directories that embed build paths or timestamps, add them to the wheel-install junk deletion list rather than shipping them.

problem: New CPython 3.15 (and 3.15t freethreading) had to be recognized by the python eclasses.
root_cause: python-utils-r1 hardcodes the supported implementation list and version-range case patterns in four separate places plus a test script.
fix_pattern: Update _PYTHON_ALL_IMPLS brace ranges (python3_{11..15}, python3_{13..15}t) and every case pattern 3.1[0-4] -> 3.1[0-5] in _python_verify_patterns, _python_set_impls, _python_impl_matches, and the brace ranges in eclass/tests/python-utils-r1.sh.
