rule: When adding a new interpreter version to a version-pattern-driven eclass, grep for every hardcoded range pattern (brace expansions and case globs) and update the eclass self-tests in the same commit.

problem: GStreamer 1.26 requires a newer meson than older branches, but the eclass carried one flat BDEPEND for all versions.
root_cause: Upstream raised the minimum build-tool version in a new release series; eclass dependencies must track it per version series.
fix_pattern: Gate the build-tool BDEPEND on the consumer's PV inside the eclass: if [[ ${PV} =~ 1.26.* ]]; then BDEPEND=">=dev-build/meson-1.4"; fi, citing upstream release notes.
rule: When a new upstream release series raises its build-tool minimum, add a PV-conditional BDEPEND in the shared eclass instead of editing every consumer ebuild.
snippet:
# Bump based on upstream release notes
if [[ ${PV} =~ 1.26.* ]]; then
	BDEPEND=">=dev-build/meson-1.4"
fi

problem: Every gstreamer-meson consumer was forced into multilib-minimal (32+64-bit builds), even packages where multilib makes no sense.
root_cause: The eclass unconditionally inherited multilib-minimal, exported multilib_src_* phases, and hardcoded [${MULTILIB_USEDEP}] in dependency strings.
fix_pattern: Introduce a @PRE_INHERIT switch : "${GST_PLUGINS_MULTILIB:=true}"; conditionally inherit multilib-minimal; put the usedep into a helper variable _GST_PLUGINS_MULTILIB_USEDEP="[${MULTILIB_USEDEP}]" (empty when off) used in all *DEPEND; provide _gstreamer_native_usex that dispatches multilib_native_usex vs usex; and export either multilib_src_* wrappers or plain eclassname_src_* shadow phases via EXPORT_FUNCTIONS depending on the switch.
rule: To make multilib optional in an eclass, gate inherit multilib-minimal, the [${MULTILIB_USEDEP}] suffix, and EXPORT_FUNCTIONS behind a pre-inherit true/false eclass variable, with a die on unknown values.

problem: Routine addition of a pinned EGIT_COMMIT for a dated LLVM snapshot version; no generalizable content beyond the existing snapshot mechanism.

Zig 0.16 changed how fetched packages are stored (compressed plus folder form, hardcoded local zig-pkg/ dir, needs build.zig present) breaking the eclass's offline dependency unpacking; 0.17+ changes it again via ZIG_LOCAL_PKG_DIR.
root_cause: Upstream toolchain changed its package cache layout and fetch behavior across releases, including a 0.16-only regression (fetch requires build.zig, target dir not configurable).
fix_pattern: Version-gate every behavior with ver_test on ZIG_SLOT: export ZIG_LOCAL_PKG_DIR for >=0.17; for ==0.16 touch build.zig before ezig fetch and symlink the hardcoded zig-pkg/ dir into WORKDIR; choose the --system dir per version range; make superseded helpers (zig_live_fetch) die with a pointer to the replacement (zig_live_src_unpack).
rule: When a language toolchain changes its cache/fetch layout between versions, branch eclass logic on ver_test of the toolchain slot, document per-version paths inline, and make removed code paths die with the name of the replacement function.
snippet:
if ver_test "${ZIG_SLOT}" -ge "0.17"; then
	system_dir="${ZIG_LOCAL_PKG_DIR}"
elif ver_test "${ZIG_SLOT}" -eq "0.16"; then
	touch build.zig || die  # 0.16 fetch regression
	system_dir="${WORKDIR}/zig-pkg/"
else
	system_dir="${ZBS_ECLASS_DIR}/p/"
fi

problem: Routine addition of "0.16.0" to the zig_supported_versions list; no generalizable content.
Dozens of Go packages failed to build (nearly 40 bugs closed at once) because their go.mod toolchain directive required a newer Go than the eclass minimum BDEPEND >=dev-lang/go-1.20 guaranteed.
root_cause: Modern go.mod files carry go/toolchain version directives; the go tool refuses to build with an older toolchain, so an outdated eclass-wide minimum lets emerge schedule builds doomed to fail.
fix_pattern: Raise the central BDEPEND in go-module.eclass to the current patched toolchain: BDEPEND=">=dev-lang/go-1.24.11:=" (note the := slot operator forcing rebuilds on go upgrades).
rule: When Go packages start failing with toolchain-version errors, bump the shared >=dev-lang/go minimum in one central place (eclass or overlay-wide) rather than per package, and keep the := slot operator.

problem: Kernel ebuilds with a mismatched K_GENPATCHES_VER silently applied a genpatches set built for a different kernel version, producing confusing patch failures later (bugs 972596, 970493).
root_cause: Nothing validated that the unpacked genpatches tarball actually contained the linux incremental patch for the OKV version the ebuild intends to build.
fix_pattern: In unipatch(), when K_WANT_GENPATCHES is set, scan the unpacked patch dir filenames for the OKV string and die with an actionable message naming the variable to fix (K_GENPATCHES_VER) if not found.
rule: When an ebuild combines two independently versioned sources (base tarball + patchset), add an explicit early consistency check that dies with the variable name to correct, instead of letting the mismatch surface as a cryptic patch failure.
snippet:
if [[ -z ${KV_PATCH_FOUND} ]]; then
	eerror "GENPATCHES does not contain linux patch ${OKV}"
	eerror "Please check your ebuild for the proper K_GENPATCHES_VER=N"
	die "GENPATCHES appears to be missing Linux patch ${OKV}"
fi

problem: Packages installing hwdb files each had to regenerate the binary udev hardware database themselves, and many still used the upstream-deprecated 'udevadm hwdb' command.
root_cause: Upstream systemd deprecated 'udevadm hwdb' in favor of 'systemd-hwdb update'; there was no shared eclass helper encoding the current invocation.
fix_pattern: Add udev_hwdb_update() to udev.eclass wrapping ebegin/systemd-hwdb update --root "${ROOT}"/eend, to be called from pkg_postinst AND pkg_postrm of hwdb-installing packages.
rule: When packages install hwdb files, call a shared udev_hwdb_update helper from both pkg_postinst and pkg_postrm, using 'systemd-hwdb update' not the deprecated 'udevadm hwdb'.

problem: udev_hwdb_update() returned early whenever ROOT was set, leaving the hwdb.bin stale in chroot/cross installs; it also assumed systemd-hwdb exists on the build host.
root_cause: Old habit of skipping pkg_postinst work when ROOT != / even though systemd-hwdb natively supports operating on another root via --root.
fix_pattern: Drop the [[ -n ${ROOT} ]] && return guard and instead guard on tool availability: type systemd-hwdb &>/dev/null || return 0, then always run systemd-hwdb update --root "${ROOT}".
rule: In pkg_postinst helpers, if the underlying tool supports --root, run it against ${ROOT} instead of skipping when ROOT is set, and guard on the tool's presence rather than on ROOT.

problem: Qt 6.11+ generates SBOM files during build/install; they are unwanted distro payload and were causing build problems in qtwebengine.
root_cause: Upstream enabled SBOM generation by default; unlike QT_FEATURE flags, setting it only for qtbase does not propagate to the other dev-qt/* modules, so it must be passed per package.
fix_pattern: Add -DQT_GENERATE_SBOM=OFF to the common mycmakeargs in the eclass's src_configure for all dev-qt/* rather than only the affected module.
rule: When upstream adds default-on metadata generation (SBOM, license bundles) that the package manager already tracks, turn it off centrally at configure time in the shared eclass.

problem: Cross-compiled builds using 'go run' broke for projects that unset GOOS/GOARCH to run build-host tools: the eclass exported GOFLAGS="-exec=wrapper" globally, so the CHOST wrapper got applied to native build-host executions too.
root_cause: GOFLAGS -exec is global for every 'go run', but the wrapper is only correct for the target tuple; 'go run' natively looks up a wrapper named go_${GOOS}_${GOARCH}_exec in PATH, which self-selects by tuple.
fix_pattern: Create a directory in ${T}, symlink the sysroot run wrapper to go_${GOOS}_${GOARCH}_exec inside it, and prepend that dir to PATH instead of setting GOFLAGS+=-exec.
rule: When providing a cross-execution wrapper for 'go run', install it in PATH under the name go_${GOOS}_${GOARCH}_exec instead of GOFLAGS=-exec, so native (unset GOOS/GOARCH) invocations are unaffected.
snippet:
if script=$(sysroot_make_run_prefixed); then
	go_exec="${T}/go-exec"
	PATH="${go_exec}:${PATH}"
	mkdir -p "${go_exec}" || die
	ln -snfr "${script}" "${go_exec}/go_${GOOS}_${GOARCH}_exec" || die
fi

problem: Cross-compiled Go binaries silently built with CGO_ENABLED=0 (Go's cross default), losing libc-based DNS resolution and NSS integration; behavior differed between native and cross builds.
root_cause: Upstream Go defaults CGO on only for native builds; distributions generally want cgo (libc resolver) even when cross-compiling.
fix_pattern: Export CGO_ENABLED=1 as an overridable default (CGO_ENABLED="${CGO_ENABLED:-1}"), gated to EAPI 9+ via a case on ${EAPI} so existing EAPI 7/8 ebuilds keep old behavior; ebuilds can still force 0 before inherit.
rule: Introduce behavior-changing eclass defaults only for the new EAPI (case ${EAPI} in old) : ;; *) new-default ;; esac) so existing ebuilds are unaffected, and always make the default overridable with ${VAR:-default}.

problem: go-module.eclass set up compile flags (GOFLAGS etc. via go-env_set_compile_environment) during src_unpack, so any flag filtering an ebuild did in src_configure (filter-flags, strip-flags) came too late and was ignored (bug 963401).
root_cause: Environment capture in the wrong phase: flag variables were snapshotted before the phase where ebuilds conventionally mutate CFLAGS/LDFLAGS.
fix_pattern: Add go-module_src_configure() calling go-env_set_compile_environment, EXPORT_FUNCTIONS src_configure for EAPI 9+ while EAPI 7/8 keep the src_unpack call; GO_OPTIONAL consumers must now call both go-module_src_unpack and go-module_src_configure manually.
rule: Capture toolchain flags into build-system variables in src_configure (after flag filtering), never in src_unpack; when relocating eclass phase logic, gate it on the new EAPI to preserve old behavior.

problem: Routine EAPI 9 enablement: added 9 to the supported-EAPI case and @SUPPORTED_EAPIS; no generalizable content beyond the standard pattern.
One-line tuple match extension: run Wine wrapper for *-cygwin CHOSTs like *-mingw32; untested, user-requested (bug 970795); minimal generalizable content.

sysroot run wrapper used /usr/bin/ld.so for all dynamically linked target executables, which is wrong for non-default ABIs (e.g. x86 32-bit binaries need /lib/ld-linux.so.2 while ld.so points to the x86-64 loader), breaking multilib cross execution.
root_cause: The dynamic linker path is per-ABI, but the discovery heuristic (ld.so/ldd symlink in the sysroot) only ever finds the default ABI's loader.
fix_pattern: For non-default ABIs, compile a trivial int main(){return 0;} with $(tc-getCC) ${CFLAGS} ${CPPFLAGS} ${LDFLAGS}, then extract its .interp section with $(tc-getOBJCOPY) -O binary -j .interp to learn the exact loader path the toolchain actually links against; prefix it with the sysroot and validate it is an executable file.
rule: To find the correct dynamic linker for a target/ABI, do not guess paths: build a minimal test executable with the target compiler and read its .interp section.
snippet:
echo 'int main(void){return 0;}' > "${test}.c"
$(tc-getCC) ${CFLAGS} ${CPPFLAGS} ${LDFLAGS} -o "${test}" "${test}.c" || die
read -d '' -r DLINKER < <($(tc-getOBJCOPY) -O binary -j .interp -- "${test}" /dev/stdout)
DLINKER="${DLINKER:+${MYEROOT}${DLINKER}}"
[[ -f ${DLINKER} && -x ${DLINKER} ]] || DLINKER=

problem: Routine addition of a pinned EGIT_COMMIT for a dated LLVM snapshot; no generalizable content.
Routine removal of EGIT_COMMIT entries for snapshot versions no longer in tree; no generalizable content.
Copyright header year fix only; no generalizable content.
genpatches tarballs were fetched only from developer personal webspace (dev.gentoo.org/~alicef,~mpagano), a single fragile origin.
root_cause: SRC_URI-generating code predated the patchsets being mirrored on the official Gentoo distfile mirror network.
fix_pattern: Prepend the canonical mirror URL before the devspace fallbacks in the generated URI list: https://distfiles.gentoo.org/pub/proj/kernel/genpatches/${tarball} followed by the dev.gentoo.org URLs.
rule: When a distfile exists on the official mirror network, list the mirror URL first in SRC_URI and keep developer/upstream URLs as fallbacks for resilience.

problem: Style cleanup removing readonly from WINE_USEDEP; same convention as qt6-build commit, no new content.
Eclass marked many public variables (QT6_PREFIX, QT6_LIBDIR, ...) readonly, deviating from eclass conventions and preventing any legitimate override or re-sourcing.
root_cause: readonly offers no practical protection benefit in eclasses and is inconsistent with how the rest of the tree behaves; bash readonly is process-global and can break re-inherit or ebuild-side adjustments.
fix_pattern: Drop the readonly keyword and just assign the variables normally in the _prepare_env function.
rule: Do not mark eclass variables readonly; document them as not-to-be-modified instead, since readonly is irreversible in bash and against tree convention.

problem: GCC libgo build failed when an unrelated /usr/lib64/libruntime.so (from dev-cpp/fbthrift) existed on the system (gcc PR121877, bug 972774).
root_cause: libtool configure probes link against stray libraries in the sysroot, so an unrelated library name collided with libgo's runtime detection and made libtool misdetect compiler capabilities.
fix_pattern: Pre-seed the libtool cache variables via exported environment before configure so the broken probe never runs.
rule: When configure/libtool misdetects something due to environment pollution, override the specific ac_cv_*/lt_cv_* cache variable with export in src_configure instead of patching configure.
snippet:
if is_go && [[ -f "${ESYSROOT}/usr/$(get_libdir)/libruntime.so" ]] ; then
	export lt_cv_prog_compiler_c_o_GO=yes
	export lt_cv_prog_compiler_pic_works_GO=yes
fi

problem: sec-policy ebuilds could never be revbumped: BASEPOL defaulted to ${PVR}, so -rN was baked into the patchbundle SRC_URI filename and a plain revision bump broke fetching.
root_cause: -rN is reserved exclusively for ebuild revisions, but the eclass abused it to version upstream patch-bundle tarballs, coupling fetch URLs to revision numbers.
fix_pattern: For EAPI 8+ encode the bundle version as _pN in PV and transform it (PV/_p/-r) when constructing the artifact name; introduce an internal _SELINUX_POLICY_UPSTREAM_PV=$(ver_cut 1-2) for the real upstream version so SRC_URI never references PVR; keep the old PVR behavior only for EAPI 7 compatibility.
rule: Never derive SRC_URI or any upstream artifact name from ${PVR}; encode extra upstream versioning as _pN in PV and translate it, keeping -rN free for pure ebuild revisions.

problem: Every policy package had to hand-roll a per-USE-flag RDEPEND loop to keep selinux_policy_types_* USE flags consistent across dependencies.
root_cause: No shared USE-dependency string existed, so constraints were duplicated (and drifted) across the eclass and consumers.
fix_pattern: Expose an @OUTPUT_VARIABLE SELINUX_POLICY_USEDEP containing 'flag(-)?,...' and collapse the per-flag loop into a single dep atom sec-policy/selinux-base-policy[${SELINUX_POLICY_USEDEP}], mirroring the PYTHON_USEDEP idiom.
rule: When multiple consumers must apply the same USE-flag constraints to dependencies, publish a *_USEDEP output variable from the eclass instead of having each ebuild build the string itself.

problem: rpm_unpack died with 'failed to grep rpm payload' on valid gzip/uncompressed rpms (bug 973157) because the pipeline treated grep no-match as a fatal error.
root_cause: grep exits 1 on no match, and a blanket 'pipestatus || die' cannot distinguish legitimate no-match (payload has no PayloadIs* marker) from real failure.
fix_pattern: Check only PIPESTATUS[0] (the strings command) for failure and accept empty grep output as 'none'; rely on the downstream extraction tool to die loudly if the guess was wrong.
rule: When grepping command output for optional markers, never die on the grep exit status; validate only the producer via PIPESTATUS[0] and treat no-match as a legitimate branch.
snippet:
payload=$($(tc-getSTRINGS) "${a}" | grep -o 'PayloadIs[a-zA-Z]*'; \
  if [[ ${PIPESTATUS[0]} != 0 ]]; then die "strings failed"; fi)

problem: RPM_COMPRESS_TYPE was optional for backwards compatibility, but unset values leave cases where neither rpm nor rpm2targz reliably works.
root_cause: An eclass variable introduced late cannot be made mandatory retroactively without breaking existing EAPI 7/8 consumers.
fix_pattern: Gate the hard requirement on the next EAPI: keep eqawarn for old EAPIs, die at inherit time for EAPI 9+ when the variable is unset.
rule: To make a new eclass variable mandatory without breaking existing consumers, warn (eqawarn) in current EAPIs and enforce with die only from the next EAPI onward.
snippet:
if [[ ${EAPI} != [78] && -z ${RPM_COMPRESS_TYPE} ]]; then
	die "RPM_COMPRESS_TYPE= must be defined starting with EAPI 9"
fi

problem: rpm-based ebuilds could silently pick app-arch/rpm built without the USE flag matching the rpm's payload compression, failing at unpack; legacy PayloadIsLzma rpms fail with rpm2targz entirely (bugs 973073, 971578, 321439).
root_cause: app-arch/rpm's decompressor support is USE-conditional, but the eclass BDEPEND was a static '|| ( rpm2targz rpm )' with no way to express which compression the distfile actually uses.
fix_pattern: Add a @PRE_INHERIT variable RPM_COMPRESS_TYPE set before inherit; a _rpm_set_globals function (unset -f afterwards) translates it into a USE-conditional BDEPEND like >=app-arch/rpm-4.19.0[lzma(+)]; at unpack time detect the actual payload via strings|grep -o 'PayloadIs[a-zA-Z]*' and eqawarn if the declared type mismatches; die for lzma+rpm2targz which cannot work.
rule: When an eclass's generated dependencies must vary per consumer, use a pre-inherit variable processed in a _set_globals function that is unset -f after use, and add a runtime QA check that the declared value matches reality.
snippet:
# ebuild side, before inherit:
RPM_COMPRESS_TYPE="zstd"
inherit rpm
# eclass side:
_rpm_set_globals() { ... BDEPEND=">=app-arch/rpm-4.19.0[${rpmuse%,}]" ... }
_rpm_set_globals
unset -f _rpm_set_globals

problem: Routine addition of one llvm snapshot PV-to-EGIT_COMMIT pin; no generalizable content beyond the existing snapshot mechanism.
Packages migrating off deprecated tc-ld-disable-gold were being pointed blanket at tc-ld-force-bfd, forcing bfd even where lld/mold work fine.
root_cause: The deprecation notice suggested a stronger replacement than needed; most gold-era failures were gold-specific bugs, not modern-linker incompatibilities.
fix_pattern: Amend the @DEPRECATED tag to 'tc-ld-force-bfd or drop entirely if works with everything but gold', signalling that removal is usually the right migration.
rule: When removing tc-ld-disable-gold calls, test with lld/mold first and drop the call entirely; only substitute tc-ld-force-bfd if the package genuinely needs bfd.

problem: Removal of an obsolete wine-proton version exception once its TODO condition (9999 rebased onto wine-11) was met; housekeeping only.
Backport of USE=time64 support to gcc 11.5.0: lower tc_version_is_at_least gate, bump patchset, and move the time64 profile package.mask boundary in lockstep; gcc-internal mechanics.
Same time64 backport pattern applied to gcc 12.5.0 (version-gate lowering plus profile mask update); gcc-internal mechanics.
Adjusting a wine-proton-specific version exception for wow64 IUSE defaults; package-specific branching on PN and ver_test with 9999.
Rust packages had no clean way to skip several flaky/sandbox-incompatible tests; each ebuild hand-built 'cargo test -- --skip a --skip b' argument lists.
root_cause: cargo test only accepts skip filters after the '--' separator (they go to the test harness, not cargo), which is easy to get wrong when the caller also passes its own '--' args.
fix_pattern: Eclass gained CARGO_SKIP_TESTS: declare an array of test names in src_test before calling cargo_src_test; the eclass prefixes each with --skip, verifies the variable is an array via declare -p, and splices the skip flags after the existing '--' separator if the caller supplied one.
rule: To skip individual tests in a cargo package, declare the CARGO_SKIP_TESTS array before cargo_src_test (or pass '-- --skip <name>') instead of patching the test out or disabling the whole suite.

problem: Routine rust.eclass version add: new Rust release needs entries in both _RUST_LLVM_MAP (LLVM slot pin) and _RUST_SLOTS_ORDERED; mechanical, tree-specific.
Routine llvm snapshot PV-to-EGIT_COMMIT pin addition; no generalizable content.
LLVM patchset and manpage tarballs were fetched from a developer's personal devspace (dev.gentoo.org/~mgorny/dist/llvm/), a fragile single-owner location.
root_cause: Gentoo introduced official project distfile hosting (distfiles.gentoo.org/pub/proj/), deprecating per-developer devspace URLs for project artifacts.
fix_pattern: Point SRC_URI at the mirrored project path, including the major version in the layout: https://distfiles.gentoo.org/pub/proj/llvm/patchsets/${LLVM_MAJOR}/llvm-gentoo-patchset-${LLVM_PATCHSET}.tar.xz
rule: When a dev.gentoo.org/~dev/dist URL for Gentoo patchsets stops working, look for the artifact under distfiles.gentoo.org/pub/proj/<project>/ which is the new canonical mirrored location.

problem: GCC patchset fetching relied on a gentoo_urls() helper cycling through eight developers' personal devspaces selected by TOOLCHAIN_PATCH_DEV, breaking whenever a dev's space moved or retired.
root_cause: No official hosting existed for project patch tarballs, so the eclass accumulated a fallback list of per-developer URLs.
fix_pattern: Delete gentoo_urls() and TOOLCHAIN_PATCH_DEV entirely; hardcode the official mirrored project location https://distfiles.gentoo.org/pub/proj/toolchain/gcc/patches/gcc-${PATCH_GCC_VER}-patches-${PATCH_VER}.tar.${SUFFIX} (overlay GCC forks that set TOOLCHAIN_PATCH_DEV must drop it).
rule: Fetch Gentoo toolchain patchsets from distfiles.gentoo.org/pub/proj/toolchain/gcc/patches/ instead of developer devspaces, and drop TOOLCHAIN_PATCH_DEV from ebuilds inheriting toolchain.eclass.

problem: Comment-only reword clarifying that the newest Ruby goes first in RUBY_TARGETS_PREFERENCE; no code change.
Users kept getting old Ruby 3.2 pulled in even after it was dropped from the default RUBY_TARGETS (bug 972018).
root_cause: RUBY_TARGETS_PREFERENCE feeds eclass-generated || ( ... ) dependency groups, and the package manager satisfies the first viable alternative, so listing the old target first kept selecting it.
fix_pattern: Reverse the order of the stable-targets string so the newest interpreter is the first alternative.
rule: In any eclass or ebuild that generates || ( ) dependency alternatives, list the preferred (usually newest) option first, because the resolver picks the first satisfiable entry.

problem: Boost detection failed under cross-compilation: meson looked for Boost headers/libs on the build host instead of the target sysroot.
root_cause: BOOST_INCLUDEDIR/BOOST_LIBRARYDIR were exported relative to ${EPREFIX}, which points at the build prefix, not the cross sysroot where target libraries actually live.
fix_pattern: Export the Boost hint paths relative to ${ESYSROOT} so both native (ESYSROOT==EPREFIX) and cross builds resolve correctly.
rule: When exporting search-path hints for target libraries/headers in ebuilds or eclasses, root them at ESYSROOT, not EPREFIX or /usr, so cross-compilation finds the target's files.

problem: Routine llvm snapshot PV-to-EGIT_COMMIT pin addition; no generalizable content.
Housekeeping removal of snapshot EGIT_COMMIT pins whose ebuilds were already dropped; hygiene only.
User-supplied kernel config fragments (/etc/kernel/config.d/*.config) were read from BROOT, i.e. the build host, so cross-built kernels ignored the target system's config drop-ins (bug 771324).
root_cause: BROOT points at the build host's root; configuration describing the *target* system must come from the target sysroot (ESYSROOT).
fix_pattern: Switch the glob from "${BROOT}"/etc/kernel/config.d/*.config to "${ESYSROOT}"/etc/kernel/config.d/*.config (with nullglob save/restore around the array expansion).
rule: Read target-system configuration files from ESYSROOT, not BROOT; BROOT is only correct for locating build-host executables and tools.
snippet:
local shopt_save=$(shopt -p nullglob)
shopt -s nullglob
local user_configs=( "${ESYSROOT}"/etc/kernel/config.d/*.config )
eval "${shopt_save}"

problem: Maintainer's personal debug einfo line leaked into ::gentoo inside an unrelated commit and had to be purged; only lesson is to audit staged hunks before pushing.
Revert of a Hurd multilib workaround that became unnecessary once a proper fix landed elsewhere; no reusable content beyond 'remove workarounds when the root cause is fixed'.

Hurd cross-glibc installed to wrong libdir because no profile exists yet during crossdev bootstrap, so multilib_env hardcoded MULTILIB_ABIS for x86_64-gnu; commit was reverted two days later as unneeded.
Pure refactor converting a musl if-check to a case statement in preparation for a later (reverted) Hurd branch; no behavior change.
CI broke for dune.eclass (and other consumers) because multiprocessing.eclass marked makeopts_jobs/makeopts_loadavg @DEPRECATED retroactively for existing EAPIs.
root_cause: Eclass API deprecation is treated as an error by pkgcheck CI for every consumer, so a retroactive @DEPRECATED tag instantly breaks all users of the function.
fix_pattern: Mechanical migration of every call site from makeopts_jobs/makeopts_loadavg to get_makeopts_jobs/get_makeopts_loadavg via grep -RP '(?<!get_)makeopts_jobs' plus sed; the get_ variants also read GNUMAKEFLAGS and MAKEFLAGS automatically instead of only what the caller passes.
rule: When calling multiprocessing.eclass job helpers in ebuilds or eclasses, use get_makeopts_jobs/get_makeopts_loadavg (no arguments needed, reads all MAKEOPTS-like variables); the bare makeopts_* forms are deprecated and banned in EAPI 9.

problem: Marking makeopts_jobs/makeopts_loadavg @DEPRECATED caused strange CI failures on GitHub and Codeberg for existing consumers, so the @DEPRECATED tags were removed while the EAPI 9 ban was kept.
root_cause: @DEPRECATED in eclassdoc immediately flags every consumer still on EAPI 7/8, even though the function remains fully supported there; deprecation and per-EAPI banning are separate mechanisms.
fix_pattern: Drop the @DEPRECATED eclassdoc tags but keep the runtime die-guard that bans the functions only in EAPI 9; document the ban in the @DESCRIPTION text instead.
rule: When phasing out an eclass function, ban it only from the next EAPI with a runtime guard and do not add @DEPRECATED while many current-EAPI consumers remain, or CI will flag them all at once.
snippet:
# @FUNCTION: makeopts_jobs
# @DESCRIPTION:
# This function is banned in EAPI 9, use get_makeopts_jobs instead.

problem: GOOS mapping for GNU/Hurd: the CHOST tuple for Hurd ends in plain '-gnu' (no 'linux'), mapping to GOOS=hurd; niche porting trivia.
Kernel source builds failed with missing libbfd files (bug 970027) because the toolchain no longer implicitly provided them.
root_cause: Kernel host tools link against libbfd from binutils, but sys-libs/binutils-libs was never declared as a dependency of kernel sources; it only worked by accident until binutils packaging changed.
fix_pattern: Add sys-libs/binutils-libs to the kernel-sources dependency block alongside bison/flex/libelf/pkgconfig.
rule: When a build fails on missing bfd.h or libbfd, add an explicit dependency on sys-libs/binutils-libs instead of relying on the binutils toolchain package to provide the library.

problem: Apache's generated pcre2-config wrapper invoked the wrong pkg-config when cross-compiling (bug 932162) because PKG_CONFIG was only tc-exported in src_configure, after the wrapper had been written in src_prepare.
root_cause: tc-export PKG_CONFIG happened in a later phase than the code depending on it; the wrapper script needs the CHOST-prefixed pkg-config in its environment from the moment it is created/used.
fix_pattern: Move tc-export PKG_CONFIG from src_configure into src_prepare immediately before generating the wrapper script, keeping export ac_cv_path_PKGCONFIG=${PKG_CONFIG} in src_configure.
rule: tc-export toolchain variables in (or before) the phase where the first consumer of the variable runs, especially when generating wrapper scripts that call the tool; exporting only in src_configure is too late for src_prepare-generated helpers.

problem: eclass-manpages generation failed with 'makeopts_jobs: no @DESCRIPTION found' after @DEPRECATED was inserted between @FUNCTION and @USAGE.
root_cause: Eclassdoc tags have a required order; @DEPRECATED must come after @USAGE and directly before @DESCRIPTION, otherwise the doc parser stops matching.
fix_pattern: Reorder the doc block to @FUNCTION, @USAGE, @DEPRECATED, @DESCRIPTION and verify by building app-doc/eclass-manpages.
rule: When editing eclassdoc blocks, keep tag order (@FUNCTION, @USAGE, @DEPRECATED/@INTERNAL, @DESCRIPTION) and validate with the eclass-manpages build, which acts as a lint for doc structure.

problem: sysroot.eclass tried to create qemu-user exec wrappers for targets whose kernel differs from the build host (e.g. Hurd, BSD), producing wrappers that cannot work.
root_cause: qemu-user emulation only supports Linux userland binaries; a wrapper for a non-Linux CHOST can never execute target binaries on a Linux build host.
fix_pattern: Add an early bail-out branch: if CHOST is not *-linux-*, log and return a distinct status (2) meaning 'no wrapper possible', letting callers skip -exec/wrapper logic.
rule: When generating cross-execution wrappers (qemu-user, binfmt), first check the target kernel matches Linux and degrade gracefully (skip wrapper, distinct return code) for foreign-kernel targets.
snippet:
elif [[ ${CHOST} != *-linux-* ]]; then
	einfo "Target is not Linux. Continuing without ${SCRIPT##*/} wrapper."
	return 2

problem: makeopts_jobs/makeopts_loadavg have an error-prone API (caller passes its own MAKEOPTS string, easily picking the wrong variable), and needed to be phased out when adding EAPI 9 support.
root_cause: Old API accepted arbitrary argument strings instead of reading MAKEOPTS/GNUMAKEFLAGS/MAKEFLAGS itself; simply deleting it would break EAPI 7/8 consumers.
fix_pattern: Move the implementation to an @INTERNAL _makeopts_jobs helper; the public legacy function becomes a thin wrapper that dies unless EAPI is 7/8; the new get_makeopts_jobs calls the internal helper directly. Same for loadavg. SUPPORTED_EAPIS gains 9.
rule: To ban an eclass function starting from a new EAPI while keeping old EAPIs working, split the body into an _internal helper and gate the legacy public name with: has "${EAPI}" <old-eapis> || die '...banned in EAPI ${EAPI}, use <new> instead'.

problem: Adding a new CPU architecture (Elbrus e2k) requires touching tc-ninja_magic_to_arch, tc-endian, and the eclass test list in eclass/tests/toolchain-funcs.sh together; arch-addition plumbing with little overlay relevance.
Routine EAPI 9 enablement: add 9 to the EAPI case and @SUPPORTED_EAPIS, and move the EAPI check inside the inclusion guard; no non-obvious content.
cvs.eclass needed EAPI 9 support while it relied on the eapi9-pipestatus compatibility shim, which must not be inherited in EAPI 9 where pipestatus is native.
root_cause: eapi9-* compat eclasses backport EAPI 9 features to EAPI 7/8; inheriting them under real EAPI 9 is wrong/unsupported, so the inherit must be conditional on EAPI.
fix_pattern: Use per-EAPI branches in the version check: inherit the compat shim only for 7|8 and an empty branch for 9; also keep the case inside the _ECLASS inclusion guard so the conditional inherit runs once.
rule: When bumping an eclass that uses eapi9-* compat shims (eapi9-pipestatus, eapi9-ver-rs) to EAPI 9, make the shim inherit conditional: 'case ${EAPI} in 7|8) inherit eapi9-pipestatus ;; 9) ;; *) die ;; esac'.

problem: Maintainer field handed to maintainer-needed@gentoo.org; pure metadata housekeeping.
Same EAPI 9 + conditional eapi9-pipestatus inherit pattern as cvs.eclass a0ca880; duplicate of that lesson.
Routine weekly LLVM snapshot addition: maps PV 23.0.0_preYYYYMMDD to a pinned EGIT_COMMIT in the eclass; pattern is specific to llvm.org.eclass workflow.
Cross-compiling to Hurd with cmake needs CMAKE_SYSTEM_NAME=GNU (CMake's official sysname for Hurd) added to the KERNEL-to-sysname map in cmake_src_configure; niche porting mapping.
Documentation-only commit describing GOAMD64/GOARM64/GOPPC64/GORISCV64 and BUILD_GO* user variables; no code change.

Cross-compiled Go packages fail whenever the build invokes 'go run' (common for code generation), because go builds the helper for the target architecture and then tries to execute it on the build host.
root_cause: 'go run' always compiles for GOOS/GOARCH and executes the result; under cross-compilation the target binary cannot run on the host without an emulator.
fix_pattern: Obtain a qemu-user exec wrapper from sysroot_make_run_prefixed (sysroot.eclass) and append -exec=<wrapper> to GOFLAGS so 'go run' executes target binaries through the wrapper; skip when no wrapper is available/needed.
rule: When a cross-compiled Go build uses 'go run' or otherwise executes freshly built target binaries, add -exec=<qemu wrapper script> to GOFLAGS (wrapper from sysroot_make_run_prefixed) instead of patching the build to avoid go run.
snippet:
inherit sysroot
local script
if script=$(sysroot_make_run_prefixed); then
	GOFLAGS+=" -exec=${script}"
fi

problem: sysroot.eclass created a broken exec wrapper when a package used tc-env_build to build host (CBUILD) tools, because SYSROOT/ESYSROOT still pointed at the target sysroot.
root_cause: tc-env_build swaps CC/CHOST etc. to the CBUILD toolchain but previously left SYSROOT/ESYSROOT at the cross-target values, so sysroot-aware helpers thought they were still targeting the cross sysroot.
fix_pattern: In the tc-env_build environment-prefix list, additionally set ESYSROOT=${BROOT} and SYSROOT= (empty) so build-machine tools resolve against the build host.
rule: Any 'run this for the build machine' environment wrapper must reset sysroot variables too (SYSROOT= and ESYSROOT=${BROOT}), not just CC/CHOST/flags, or sysroot-aware logic will mis-detect cross-compilation.

problem: Go helper binaries built for CBUILD could be miscompiled with CHOST-specific micro-arch settings (GOAMD64 etc.) when CHOST is a newer model of the same architecture as CBUILD.
root_cause: Go arch-tuning variables like GOAMD64/GOARM64 are per-target, but a single exported value leaks into builds for the build machine.
fix_pattern: Introduce BUILD_GO* counterparts defaulted from the CHOST values via ': "${BUILD_GOAMD64:=${GOAMD64}}"' in tc-export_build_env, and have tc-env_build override the plain GO* vars with the BUILD_ ones using 'local -x' only when go-env is inherited.
rule: For toolchain knobs that differ between CBUILD and CHOST, mirror them as BUILD_<VAR> with a CHOST-value fallback and swap them in only inside the build-env wrapper, keyed on 'has <eclass> ${INHERITED}' for optional integrations.
snippet:
if has go-env ${INHERITED}; then
	: "${BUILD_GOAMD64:=${GOAMD64}}"
fi
# in tc-env_build:
has go-env ${INHERITED} && local -x GOAMD64=${BUILD_GOAMD64}

problem: There was no way to run a single command under the Go compile environment without permanently exporting CC/GOARCH/CGO_* into the rest of the ebuild's phase.
root_cause: go-env_set_compile_environment exports globally; combining it with tc-env_build for one-off CBUILD builds needs the changes scoped to one call.
fix_pattern: Add a go-env_run wrapper that declares all affected variables with bash 'local -I' (inherit current value, restore on function return), calls go-env_set_compile_environment, then execs the given command; the environment mutation is automatically undone when the function returns.
rule: To apply an environment-mutating setup function to just one command in bash, wrap it in a function that declares the touched variables with 'local -I' before calling the setup, giving automatic save/restore semantics.

problem: Cross-compiling Go for a different operating system produced host-OS binaries because only GOARCH was derived from CHOST while GOOS was left at the host default.
root_cause: GOOS defaults to the OS of the Go toolchain host; cross-OS targets (BSD, Solaris, Windows, Hurd) need it set explicitly from the tuple just like GOARCH.
fix_pattern: Export GOOS=$(go-env_goos) alongside GOARCH=$(go-env_goarch) in go-env_set_compile_environment, where go-env_goos maps CHOST tuple substrings to Go OS names.
rule: When setting up a Go cross environment from CHOST, always derive and export both GOARCH and GOOS from the tuple, not GOARCH alone.

problem: New CPAN release 1.03 must be packaged as Gentoo version 1.30.0, which does not match the upstream tarball version.
root_cause: Gentoo normalizes CPAN float-style versions (1.03) into dotted 3-component versions (1.30.0); the perl-module eclass needs to know the real upstream version to build SRC_URI.
fix_pattern: Set DIST_VERSION to the literal upstream CPAN version and DIST_AUTHOR to the CPAN uploader before inheriting perl-module; name the ebuild with the normalized Gentoo version. Test-only deps go in BDEPEND inside test? ( ).
rule: When bumping a dev-perl package, always set DIST_VERSION to the raw CPAN version and derive the ebuild filename from the normalized version - never assume they match.
snippet:
DIST_AUTHOR=ISHIGAKI
DIST_VERSION=1.03
inherit perl-module
# ebuild file: Module-CPANTS-Analyse-1.30.0.ebuild

problem: XS module fails to build against perl-5.44 with missing type/declaration errors in .xs/.xsp files (bug 978889).
root_cause: ExtUtils::ParseXS changed its processing order of INCLUDE: directives in the perl 5.44 cycle, exposing latent bugs where XS files relied on headers transitively included by earlier-processed modules.
fix_pattern: Add the missing explicit #include to each affected XS file (e.g. #include <wx/icon.h> in Bitmap.xs, #include "cpp/overload.h" in Colour.xs) via a FILESDIR patch appended to PATCHES; patch was taken from Debian (ntyni) via rt.cpan.org rather than written from scratch.
rule: When an old XS package breaks on a new perl, suspect header-include ordering changes in ExtUtils::ParseXS and fix by making every XS file include what it uses; check Debian and rt.cpan.org first for an existing patch.

problem: Ebuild still carried append-cflags -std=gnu17 (a C23/GCC-15 build workaround) after the upstream release that fixed the underlying issue.
root_cause: The workaround had been added with a TODO comment 'Drop on next release > 1.7.1' plus a bug reference, but the drop was not executed at bump time.
fix_pattern: Remove the append-cflags call, drop the now-unused flag-o-matic inherit, and keep RESTRICT/REQUIRED_USE tidied near the metadata block. The original TODO with an explicit version condition is what made the cleanup findable.
rule: Always annotate compiler-standard or other temporary workarounds with a 'TODO: drop when > VERSION' comment and a bug URL, and grep for such TODOs on every version bump so stale hacks (and their now-unneeded inherits) get removed.
snippet:
src_configure() {
	# TODO: Drop on next release > 1.7.1
	# bug #944014
	append-cflags -std=gnu17   # <- delete on bump, plus flag-o-matic inherit

problem: Comment-only cleanup: removed a stale TODO about p7zip drop-in replacement that 7zip[symlink] already satisfies; no behavioral change.
Package hard-depended on app-arch/p7zip, which was about to be last-rited (removed from the tree).
root_cause: p7zip is an abandoned fork; the maintained app-arch/7zip installs '7zz' by default and only provides the classic '7z' command name when built with USE=symlink (added in 7zip-24.09).
fix_pattern: Replace the dep with an ||-group preferring 7zip with the symlink flag, using (+) so the flag defaults on if unknown, and revbump every ebuild since RDEPEND changed on an installed package: RDEPEND="|| ( >=app-arch/7zip-24.09[symlink(+)] app-arch/p7zip )".
rule: When a runtime tool package is being last-rited, switch consumers to '|| ( new-pkg[compat-flag(+)] old-pkg )' with the replacement listed first, and revbump because RDEPEND changed.

problem: Version bump of a game with a crusty hand-written makefile: called absent sdl-config with SDL2, redefined _FORTIFY_SOURCE=2 and duplicated hardening flags causing redefinition warnings, used -std=c99 plus manual -D*_SOURCE defines, and put the runtime 7z tool in DEPEND instead of RDEPEND.
root_cause: Upstream makefile hardcodes toolchain and feature flags that fight the distro toolchain: sdl-config no longer exists for SDL2 (pkg-config only), _FORTIFY_SOURCE and -fstack-protector are already set by Gentoo's toolchain defaults, and -std=c99 without GNU extensions forces feature-test-macro defines that clash with flags from dependencies.
fix_pattern: Patch the makefile to use $(shell ${PKG_CONFIG} --cflags/--libs sdl2 SDL2_mixer), export PKG_CONFIG via tc-export PKG_CONFIG in src_prepare, pass GENTOO_CPPFLAGS="${CPPFLAGS}" through emake, delete the makefile's own -D_FORTIFY_SOURCE/-fstack-protector/-z relro lines, and switch -std=c99 to -std=gnu99 so no -D_XOPEN_SOURCE/_BSD_SOURCE defines are needed; move the 7zip/p7zip dep to RDEPEND since it is invoked at runtime, and add virtual/libcrypt:= for the -lcrypt link in the server.
rule: When patching a hand-rolled makefile, replace sdl-config with ${PKG_CONFIG} calls, strip upstream's own hardening/-D_FORTIFY_SOURCE flags (the toolchain already provides them at possibly stronger levels), prefer -std=gnuXX over -std=cXX to avoid feature-macro redefinition, and classify tool deps by when they run (runtime tools belong in RDEPEND).
snippet:
src_prepare() { ...; tc-export PKG_CONFIG; }
emake -f makefile CC="$(tc-getCC)" CPP="$(tc-getCPP)" \
	GENTOO_CPPFLAGS="${CPPFLAGS}" ...
# makefile patch:
CFLAGS += $(shell ${PKG_CONFIG} --cflags sdl2)
-CFLAGS += -fstack-protector -D_FORTIFY_SOURCE=2 -fPIE

problem: BDEPEND allowed plain app-arch/7zip, but the build invokes the '7z' command which bare 7zip does not install (it installs '7zz').
root_cause: app-arch/7zip only provides the p7zip-compatible '7z'/'7za'/'7zr' names when built with USE=symlink, available from 7zip-24.09.
fix_pattern: Tighten the dep to >=app-arch/7zip-24.09[symlink(+)] inside the existing || group with p7zip.
rule: If a build or runtime script calls '7z' (not '7zz'), depend on >=app-arch/7zip-24.09[symlink(+)]; a bare app-arch/7zip dependency is insufficient.

problem: optfeature hint still recommended soon-to-be-removed app-arch/p7zip instead of app-arch/7zip; text-only dependency-hint update.
BDEPEND preferred (or solely allowed) p7zip, which was about to be removed; in the ||-group the deprecated package was listed before the maintained one.
root_cause: Portage/dependency resolvers install the first satisfiable alternative in an || group, so ordering encodes preference; leaving a dying package first steers new installs to it.
fix_pattern: Reorder the || group so >=app-arch/7zip-24.09[symlink(+)] comes before app-arch/p7zip, and revbump the ebuild whose sole dep changed to the || form.
rule: In || ( ) dependency groups, list the preferred/maintained provider first, because the resolver picks the first satisfiable entry; reorder when preferences change and revbump if the dep set changes materially.

problem: elog text recommending p7zip updated to 7zip ahead of p7zip last-rites; message-only change.
pkg_postinst elog text switched from app-arch/p7zip to app-arch/7zip ahead of p7zip removal; message-only change.
optfeature hint switched from p7zip to 7zip ahead of p7zip last-rites; message-only change (note: tree-wide migration must also cover optfeature/elog strings, not just *DEPEND).
pkg_postinst supported-formats optfeature loop updated p7zip to 7zip; commit note observes the loop conflates format name with package name and should use an associative array.

Version bump of a pytest-plugin package whose test suite spawns pytest subprocesses: subprocess tests failed under Gentoo's PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD regime, some tests need unpackaged tools (pyright), and python3.15 emits blocking deprecation warnings.
root_cause: Gentoo runs pytest with plugin autoloading disabled and plugins whitelisted via env; test helpers that re-exec pytest in a subprocess must forward PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD/PYTEST_PLUGINS into the child environment or the child behaves differently; new CPython versions often break test suites via new DeprecationWarnings before real support lands.
fix_pattern: Patch the upstream subprocess-env whitelist to pass through PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD and PYTEST_PLUGINS (patch submitted upstream), declare test plugins declaratively with EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( "${PN}" pytest-{freezer,mock,xdist} ) + EPYTEST_PLUGIN_LOAD_VIA_ENV=1 + EPYTEST_XDIST=1, deselect pyright-requiring tests with EPYTEST_DESELECT, exclude the broken interpreter with a comment ('# 3.15 is broken via tons of deprecation warnings'), and pin sdist provenance with PYPI_VERIFY_REPO.
rule: For python packages whose tests spawn nested pytest, ensure PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD/PYTEST_PLUGINS survive into the subprocess env, and manage plugins declaratively via EPYTEST_PLUGINS instead of hand-rolled python_test bodies; document any PYTHON_COMPAT exclusion with a reason comment.
snippet:
# 3.15 is broken via tons of deprecation warnings
PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..14} )
EPYTEST_PLUGIN_LOAD_VIA_ENV=1
EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( "${PN}" pytest-{freezer,mock,xdist} )
EPYTEST_XDIST=1
distutils_enable_tests pytest

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window slide python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15} to enable py3.15; no other changes needed.

Enabling py3.15 required touching a hand-written python_test that set PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD and manually passed -p rerunfailures --reruns=10, with a manually maintained BDEPEND on pytest-rerunfailures.
root_cause: Older ebuilds predate distutils-r1's declarative EPYTEST_* variables; manual python_test bodies duplicate logic the eclass now handles (including auto-adding the plugin deps).
fix_pattern: Delete python_test and the manual test BDEPEND; set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (no plugins autoloaded) and EPYTEST_RERUNS=10 for the flaky test before distutils_enable_tests pytest - the eclass injects pytest-rerunfailures into test deps automatically.
rule: Prefer declarative EPYTEST_PLUGINS/EPYTEST_RERUNS/EPYTEST_TIMEOUT/EPYTEST_XDIST set before distutils_enable_tests over custom python_test functions; the eclass then manages the corresponding test dependencies for you.
snippet:
# tests/test_watcher.py::TestWatcher::test_watch_multiple_dirs
# is extremely flaky
EPYTEST_PLUGINS=()
EPYTEST_RERUNS=10
distutils_enable_tests pytest

problem: Enabling py3.15 left dead conditional dependency scaffolding: python_gen_cond_dep blocks for 'python*' (vs dropped pypy3_11) and for 3.11 (dropped from PYTHON_COMPAT) no longer served any purpose.
root_cause: python_gen_cond_dep conditions are relative to PYTHON_COMPAT; when the impl set changes (pypy dropped, minimum raised), conditions become always-true or always-false and should be inlined or deleted.
fix_pattern: Move the greenlet dep to a plain RDEPEND line, delete the typing-extensions dep entirely (only needed for <=3.11), drop pypy3_11 from PYTHON_COMPAT, and add EPYTEST_DESELECT for a test asserting an interpreter error message that changed in 3.15.
rule: Whenever you change PYTHON_COMPAT, re-audit every python_gen_cond_dep block: inline conditions that became universal and delete deps only needed by removed implementations; expect error-message-asserting tests to need deselection on new CPython.

problem: openrct2-0.5.3 failed (bug 979314) because PATCHES still referenced the old openrct2-0.4.16-include-additional-paths.patch even though a rebased 0.5.3 version of the patch had been added to files/ during the bump.
root_cause: The bump was done as a pure ebuild rename (100% similarity); the new patch file was committed but the PATCHES array was never updated to point at it, so the stale patch (still present in files/) got applied and no missing-file error surfaced.
fix_pattern: One-line fix: point the PATCHES entry at "${FILESDIR}/${PN}-0.5.3-include-additional-paths.patch".
rule: When a bump adds a re-versioned patch to files/, grep the new ebuild's PATCHES for the old patch filename - a stale-but-still-existing patch silently applies wrong content instead of erroring, so filename-exists checks are not enough; verify the version prefix in each PATCHES entry matches intent.

problem: virtual/perl-IO-Compress-2.223.0 required >=virtual/perl-Compress-Raw-Zlib-${PV} (=2.223.0), but Compress-Raw-Zlib upstream only released 2.222, making the dep unsatisfiable.
root_cause: The IO-Compress family versions usually move in lockstep so ${PV} is used as the sibling dep version, but upstream sometimes skips a release for one module, breaking the ${PV} shortcut.
fix_pattern: Revbump and replace the ${PV} reference with the explicit real version of the sibling module: >=virtual/perl-Compress-Raw-Zlib-2.222.0; keep the existing comment 'must be kept in step but sometimes not .... use ${PV} when you can'.
rule: Never trust ${PV}-based cross-package version deps for 'kept in step' module families without verifying the sibling actually released that version; an unsatisfiable >= dep is detectable by a resolver check right after the bump.

problem: Routine version bump of ntfs3g; ebuild copied forward unchanged (notable existing idioms: SLOT="0/90" subslot tracking SONAME major, --with-fuse=internal required for suid, .la file cleanup).
SRC_URI host (ut2004.ut-files.com) went dead, and the distfile carried a bogus double extension 'tar.tar' (bug 979267).
root_cause: Fan-hosted mirrors for legacy game files disappear; the original mirror served the bzip2 tarball under a mangled '.tar.tar' name which got enshrined in the Manifest.
fix_pattern: Point SRC_URI at a stable archival mirror (unreal-archive S3 bucket, URL-encoded path) and rename the DIST entry to the correct .tar.bz2 extension - since the file content is identical, the Manifest line keeps the exact same size/BLAKE2B/SHA512 hashes, only the filename changes; no revbump needed as installed content is unchanged.
rule: When a distfile host dies, migrate to a durable archive mirror and take the opportunity to fix mangled distfile names; identical content can be renamed in the Manifest by editing the DIST filename while preserving the existing hashes.

problem: Defaults.pm was silently never installed (bugs 819276/914352) after a previous commit moved 'emake defaults' from src_configure to src_compile.
root_cause: ExtUtils::MakeMaker enumerates the files it will install when Makefile.PL runs (perl-module_src_configure); a .pm file generated later in src_compile is absent from the generated Makefile's manifest, so 'make install' skips it without any error.
fix_pattern: Move the generation step back before the perl Makefile is created: run 'emake defaults' in src_configure ahead of perl-module_src_configure, with a comment explaining the ordering constraint; revbump since installed files change.
rule: If a build system enumerates installable files at configure time (MakeMaker, many autotools setups), any generated files must exist before that configure step runs - generating them in src_compile silently drops them from installation.
snippet:
src_configure() {
	default
	# This must precede perl-module_src_configure for Defaults.pm to
	# be installed.
	emake defaults
	cd lib || die
	perl-module_src_configure
}

problem: Package fetched GitHub's autogenerated tag tarball (archive/${PV}.tar.gz) with no upstream authentication, while upstream publishes signed release tarballs.
root_cause: Autogenerated tag tarballs are not signed and not byte-stable guarantees from upstream; the signed release artifact under releases/download/ enables cryptographic verification.
fix_pattern: Switch SRC_URI to the release-uploaded tarball, inherit verify-sig, add the .asc detached signature guarded by 'verify-sig? ( )', set VERIFY_SIG_OPENPGP_KEY_PATH to the sec-keys-provided key, and BDEPEND on the matching sec-keys/openpgp-keys-* package under the verify-sig USE flag (IUSE gets verify-sig automatically via eclass IUSE but here added explicitly).
rule: Prefer upstream-uploaded release artifacts over autogenerated tag tarballs, and when upstream signs releases wire up verify-sig: eclass inherit + .asc in SRC_URI under verify-sig? + VERIFY_SIG_OPENPGP_KEY_PATH + BDEPEND on sec-keys/openpgp-keys-<upstream>.
snippet:
inherit ... verify-sig
SRC_URI="
	https://github.com/grke/${PN}/releases/download/${PV}/${PN}-${PV}.tar.bz2
	verify-sig? ( https://github.com/grke/${PN}/releases/download/${PV}/${PN}-${PV}.tar.bz2.asc )
"
VERIFY_SIG_OPENPGP_KEY_PATH=/usr/share/openpgp-keys/grke.asc
BDEPEND="... verify-sig? ( sec-keys/openpgp-keys-grke )"

problem: Test suite failed against zlib >= 1.3.2 (bug 974937): a test expecting a gzip read error now sees different behavior.
root_cause: zlib 1.3.2 changed corrupt-gzip handling from returning a read error to treating it as early EOF (zero bytes read), so tests asserting the old error message broke.
fix_pattern: Backport the two upstream fix commits as a FILESDIR patch that guards expectations with ZLIB_VERNUM preprocessor checks (#if ZLIB_VERNUM < 0x1320), documenting the upstream commit URLs in the patch header; also filter-lto for LTO-sensitive tests and set CK_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT=0/CK_TIMEOUT_MULTIPLIER=0 for slow check-based suites.
rule: When tests break after a system library update, look for upstream commits adapting to the new behavior and backport them with version guards (e.g. ZLIB_VERNUM) instead of skipping tests; always cite the upstream commit URLs in the patch header.

problem: Bump done as a 100%-similarity ebuild rename (0.5.2 -> 0.5.3) while adding a rebased files/ patch for 0.5.3 - but PATCHES inside the untouched ebuild still referenced the old 0.4.16 patch, causing bug 979314 fixed in a follow-up.
root_cause: A rename-only bump cannot pick up a new version-suffixed patch filename; the newly added openrct2-0.5.3-include-additional-paths.patch sat unused in files/ while the stale patch kept applying.
fix_pattern: The follow-up commit bd3a0452 edited PATCHES to reference the 0.5.3 patch. Prevention: when a bump commit both renames the ebuild and adds files/*-${PV}-*.patch, the ebuild diff must not be empty.
rule: Treat 'ebuild renamed with 100% similarity + new version-suffixed patch added to files/' as a red flag: the PATCHES array almost certainly needs updating, and an unused new patch in files/ is detectable by grepping ebuilds for each files/ entry.
snippet:
# bump commit: files/openrct2-0.5.3-include-additional-paths.patch added,
# but ebuild renamed 100% similar => PATCHES still lists 0.4.16 patch

problem: New Go release still printed the old version from --version, and generating shell completions requires executing the freshly built target binary, which is impossible when cross-compiling.
root_cause: Upstream tagged v0.10.0 but forgot to bump the hardcoded version string in cli.go; shell-completion generation runs the built binary, and the completion subcommands exit nonzero even on success so plain die-on-failure cannot be used.
fix_pattern: Carry a small files/${PN}-${PV}-fix-version.patch correcting the hardcoded version string; wrap completion generation AND its install in 'if ! tc-is-cross-compiler' (toolchain-funcs), validate generated output content with grep -q || die instead of relying on exit status, and ewarn on the cross path telling users how to generate completions manually.
rule: When a bump executes the freshly built binary during src_compile (completions, man pages), guard it with tc-is-cross-compiler and validate its output content; also check the binary's --version matches PV and patch upstream's stale hardcoded string if not.
snippet:
if ! tc-is-cross-compiler; then
	# those commands exit OK with 1, so we can't use die
	./rdap --completion-script-bash > rdap.bash
	grep -q "complete -F" rdap.bash || die "bash completion script is invalid"
fi

problem: Shared libraries and helper executables installed via 'doins -r' ended up mode 0644, breaking runtime loading/execution of the bundled llama components.
root_cause: doins installs everything as 0644 regardless of source permissions; .so files and executables copied as part of a directory tree lose their exec bit.
fix_pattern: Add fperms 0755 on the affected image paths right after doins -r (fperms accepts globs relative to ${ED}), and revbump (-r1 -> -r2) because the installed image changes for existing installs.
rule: After installing a build-output tree with doins -r, restore 0755 on shared objects and executables with fperms (or use dolib.so/dobin/exeinto instead), and revbump since installed file metadata changed.
snippet:
insinto usr
doins -r "${BUILD_DIR}"/lib
fperms 0755 /usr/lib/ollama/libllama-*-impl.so
fperms 0755 /usr/lib/ollama/lib*.so.0.0.0
fperms 0755 /usr/lib/ollama/llama-*

problem: Deleted an already-commented-out m68k unexec-dumping workaround from three ebuilds; pure dead-code housekeeping with no generalizable content.
Package not yet marked compatible with the new CPython 3.15, blocking the interpreter rollout through the dependency graph.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT is an explicit allowlist; each new interpreter slot requires per-package testing and a range bump before reverse deps can use it.
fix_pattern: After the test suite passes under the new impl, edit the brace range in place ({11..14} -> {12..15}); no revbump is done for a pure compat extension, and the EOL floor impl (python3_11) is dropped in the same commit.
rule: When a new CPython slot lands, test and extend PYTHON_COMPAT's brace range in place without a revbump, and take the opportunity to drop impls below the tree's supported floor.

problem: Same one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_15 as dev-python/utidylib (see 13d7e37f); no additional content.
Same one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_15 as dev-python/utidylib (see 13d7e37f); no additional content.
Same one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_15 as dev-python/utidylib (see 13d7e37f); no additional content.
Same one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_15 as dev-python/utidylib (see 13d7e37f), on a DISTUTILS_EXT=1 package; no additional content.
Packages lacked freethreaded (no-GIL) CPython support, blocking the python3_14t/python3_15t rollout across their reverse dependencies.
root_cause: The numeric brace range python3_{12..15} never includes the t (freethreading) ABI variants; they are separate impls that must be listed explicitly and only after the test suite passes on a no-GIL build.
fix_pattern: Append python3_{14,15}t to PYTHON_COMPAT (keeping the regular range) after verifying tests on a freethreaded interpreter; no revbump; done leaf-first in mass batches across the dependency graph.
rule: To enable no-GIL Python support, explicitly append python3_{14,15}t to PYTHON_COMPAT after tests pass on a freethreaded build, since the numeric brace range does not cover t variants.

problem: Same one-line freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition as dev-python/dependency-groups (see f36f7266); no additional content.
Same one-line freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition as dev-python/dependency-groups (see f36f7266); no additional content.
Needed to make the package installable under freethreaded Python without claiming its test suite is validated on those impls.
root_cause: The set of impls a package works on and the set where its test suite runs reliably can legitimately differ; new t variants may have flaky or hanging tests while the package itself is fine.
fix_pattern: Keep a separate PYTHON_TESTED array for impls whose tests run, and compose PYTHON_COMPAT from it plus the extra impls; python_test then skips impls not in PYTHON_TESTED.
rule: When an impl should be installable but its tests are not yet reliable there, maintain a PYTHON_TESTED array and build PYTHON_COMPAT from it plus the extra impls, skipping tests for the extras.

problem: Same one-line freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition as dev-python/dependency-groups (see f36f7266); no additional content.
Same one-line freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition as dev-python/dependency-groups (see f36f7266); no additional content.
Enabling tests on freethreaded Python risks failures from unrelated installed pytest plugins that autoload and do not support no-GIL builds.
root_cause: By default pytest autoloads every installed plugin; distutils-r1's EPYTEST_PLUGINS mechanism must be set to control this, and an unset value means legacy autoload behavior.
fix_pattern: Declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (empty allowlist, disables plugin autoloading) before the distutils_enable_tests pytest call, in the same commit that extends PYTHON_COMPAT to t variants.
rule: When enabling tests on new or fragile Python impls, set EPYTEST_PLUGINS to exactly the plugins the suite needs (empty array if none) before distutils_enable_tests so pytest runs without plugin autoloading.

problem: Same one-line freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition as dev-python/dependency-groups (see f36f7266); no additional content.
Same one-line freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition as dev-python/dependency-groups (see f36f7266); no additional content.
Same one-line freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition as dev-python/dependency-groups (see f36f7266); no additional content.
Same freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition as f36f7266, additionally dropping python3_11 and pypy3_11 in the same one-liner (routine impl housekeeping).
Same one-line freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition as dev-python/dependency-groups (see f36f7266); no additional content.
Same one-line freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition as dev-python/dependency-groups (see f36f7266); no additional content.
Same one-line freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition as dev-python/dependency-groups (see f36f7266); no additional content.
Same one-line freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition as dev-python/dependency-groups (see f36f7266); no additional content.
Same one-line freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition as dev-python/dependency-groups (see f36f7266); no additional content.
Same one-line freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition as dev-python/dependency-groups (see f36f7266); no additional content.
Package not installable/testable under CPython free-threaded (no-GIL) interpreters; PYTHON_COMPAT only listed GIL builds.
root_cause: Gentoo models free-threaded CPython as separate PYTHON_COMPAT implementations (python3_14t, python3_15t); packages must opt in explicitly after their test suite is verified to pass on the no-GIL interpreter, they do not get it automatically with python3_14/15.
fix_pattern: One-line PYTHON_COMPAT extension appending the freethreading targets, committed WITHOUT a revbump because adding implementations only widens PYTHON_TARGETS choice and does not change files installed for existing users. This exact idiom was applied verbatim across ~24 dev-python packages in one batch by mgorny (representative of the whole batch: requests-unixsocket, u-msgpack-python, pytest-expect, webencodings, soupsieve, html5lib, beautifulsoup4, olefile, validators, uc-micro-py, pytest-regressions, pytest-datadir, mdurl, linkify-it-py, markdown-it-py, littleutils, priority, gitpython, dunamai, genty).
rule: When a pure-Python package's tests pass on free-threaded CPython, add the t-suffixed implementations to PYTHON_COMPAT as 'python3_{12..15} python3_{14,15}t' in place, with no revision bump.

problem: C-extension package (DISTUTILS_EXT=1) lacked free-threaded CPython targets.
root_cause: Extension modules need explicit upstream support for the free-threaded ABI (Py_mod_gil / abi t builds); once upstream supports it and tests pass, the ebuild change is identical to pure-Python packages.
fix_pattern: Same one-line PYTHON_COMPAT += python3_{14,15}t, no revbump, even with DISTUTILS_EXT=1; distutils-r1 handles building the extension per-implementation so no extra plumbing is needed.
rule: DISTUTILS_EXT=1 is not a blocker for enabling freethreading targets, but for C extensions verify upstream free-threaded ABI support and a passing test run on python3_*t before adding the targets.

problem: PYTHON_COMPAT carried stale implementations (pypy3_11, python3_11) while lacking freethreading targets.
root_cause: Old interpreter targets linger in PYTHON_COMPAT after they stop being useful; a PYTHON_COMPAT edit is the natural moment to normalize the whole list.
fix_pattern: Single commit rewrote PYTHON_COMPAT from '( pypy3_11 python3_{11..15} )' to '( python3_{12..15} python3_{14,15}t )', dropping EOL/pypy targets and adding t targets together, still without a revbump.
rule: When touching PYTHON_COMPAT for any reason, prune end-of-life or no-longer-supported implementations in the same edit instead of leaving mixed stale lists.

problem: godot 4.7.1's platform/linuxbsd/detect.py unconditionally probes pkg-config modules 'harfbuzz-raster harfbuzz-vector' that only exist in harfbuzz >= 13, but harfbuzz-13 was not yet in the Gentoo tree, so configure failed against the in-tree harfbuzz (bug 978085).
root_cause: Upstream raised its assumed minimum dependency version ahead of the distro; the older library is actually still usable, only the build-system probe is too strict.
fix_pattern: Conditional sed in src_prepare guarded by has_version, stripping the new pkg-config module names only when the installed dep is old; harfbuzz already has ':=' in RDEPEND so a later harfbuzz upgrade triggers a rebuild that re-runs the check. Also in the same bump: distro-side rename of slotted mbedtls pkg-config modules (mbedtls -> mbedtls-3) via sed, and dropping a local patch (no-volk) that was upstreamed.
rule: When upstream's build probe demands a dependency version newer than the tree provides but the old version still works, patch the probe conditionally with has_version '<cat/pkg-N' in src_prepare instead of blocking the bump or prematurely raising the dependency floor.
snippet:
if has_version '<media-libs/harfbuzz-13'; then
	sed -e '/pkg-config/s/ harfbuzz-raster harfbuzz-vector//' \
		-i platform/linuxbsd/detect.py || die
fi

problem: SCons-built app bundles ~25 third-party libraries and its builtin_* unbundle switches are unreliable (some ignore whether the dep is used), risking silent use of bundled code.
root_cause: Godot's detect.py uses builtin_<lib> flags to decide system vs bundled linking, but flag handling is inconsistent per platform/module.
fix_pattern: Belt-and-braces unbundling: physically 'rm -r' every thirdparty/<lib> directory that has a system replacement in src_prepare, then still pass builtin_<lib>=no (or =yes for disabled optional deps whose dirs were deleted) in esconsargs, so any fallback to bundled code fails loudly at build time; symlink system headers (doctest) into thirdparty/ where the build expects a vendored path; plus tc-export AR CC CXX RANLIB, sed pkg-config to $(tc-getPKG_CONFIG), and filter-lto for known-broken LTO (bug 921017).
rule: When unbundling from a build system whose 'use system lib' switches are untrustworthy, delete the vendored source directories too so accidental bundled fallback becomes a hard build failure instead of a silent vendoring.
snippet:
local unbundle=( brotli freetype harfbuzz icu4c mbedtls pcre2 zlib zstd ... )
rm -r "${unbundle[@]/#/thirdparty/}" || die
# and in esconsargs: builtin_freetype=no builtin_zlib=no ...

Package only listed regular CPython impls in PYTHON_COMPAT, so it could not be installed for free-threaded (nogil) Python 3.14t/3.15t interpreters even though it works there.
root_cause: Free-threaded CPython builds are separate PYTHON_TARGETS (python3_14t, python3_15t) in Gentoo; a package is only available for them if the ebuild explicitly lists the t-suffixed impls in PYTHON_COMPAT.
fix_pattern: Append the t-suffixed impls after the regular range in PYTHON_COMPAT, editing the ebuild in place with NO revision bump (adding a new impl does not change what existing users have installed). Same one-line edit was applied tree-wide by mgorny to ~19 packages in this batch, including DISTUTILS_EXT=1 C-extension packages (pycares, frozenlist, httptools, time-machine), i.e. compiled extensions qualify too once they build and pass tests under the free-threaded ABI.
rule: When a dev-python package (pure or C-extension) builds and passes tests under free-threaded CPython, change PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} ) to PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} python3_{14,15}t ) in place without a revbump.
