Test suite failed (bug 976270) because an import used only by the tests (typer) was not installed when FEATURES=test ran.
root_cause: Upstream test suites often import optional integration packages that are not runtime deps; distutils_enable_tests only pulls in pytest itself, so every extra test-only import must be declared manually and gets missed on bumps when upstream adds new test deps.
fix_pattern: Add the missing package to the existing BDEPEND test? group with [${PYTHON_USEDEP}], no revbump (test deps do not affect installed image). Same-day sibling fixes: valkey +cachetools (bug 976607), plotly +pyarrow (bug 976766), authlib +flask +joserfc (bug 978076).
rule: When FEATURES=test fails with ModuleNotFoundError/ImportError for a module the package does not import at runtime, add the providing package to BDEPEND under test? ( ... [${PYTHON_USEDEP}] ) instead of deselecting the tests.

problem: Duplicate of the missing-test-dep idiom recorded at 5b16ecc0 (rich-click): added dev-python/cachetools to test? BDEPEND (bug 976607).
Duplicate of the missing-test-dep idiom recorded at 5b16ecc0 (rich-click): added dev-python/pyarrow to test? BDEPEND (bug 976766).
Tests failed for lack of jsonschema/pyyaml (bug 978028), and simply adding them would have broken the wd40 (Rust-free) profile because dev-python/jsonschema needs Rust (rpds-py).
root_cause: Adding a test dependency is not always profile-neutral: on restricted profiles like features/wd40 some deps are unavailable, so the new test? dep makes USE=test unresolvable there.
fix_pattern: Add the versioned test deps to BDEPEND test? ( ... ) as usual, and in the same commit mask the flag on the affected profile by appending 'dev-python/opentelemetry-sdk test' to profiles/features/wd40/package.use.mask under the existing 'Need dev-python/jsonschema' comment block.
rule: When adding a test (or any USE-conditional) dependency whose dep tree pulls in Rust or another mask-listed stack, also mask that USE flag for the package in the corresponding profile package.use.mask in the same commit.
snippet:
# profiles/features/wd40/package.use.mask
# Need dev-python/jsonschema (or its revdeps).
dev-python/opentelemetry-sdk test

problem: Test run via 'distutils_enable_tests unittest' was inadequate (bug 978029); the suite is pytest-based and includes a benchmark file needing the unpackaged/unwanted pytest-benchmark plugin.
root_cause: unittest discovery does not execute pytest-style tests, and blindly switching to pytest would either autoload stray plugins or fail collecting tests/test_benchmark.py which imports pytest-benchmark.
fix_pattern: Switch to the pytest runner with explicit empty plugin list (EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() disables plugin autoloading and declares no plugin deps) and skip the plugin-requiring file via EPYTEST_IGNORE rather than adding a benchmark-plugin dependency.
rule: When converting a distutils-r1 package's tests from unittest to pytest, set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest and EPYTEST_IGNORE any test files that need plugins you do not want to depend on (e.g. pytest-benchmark).

problem: Duplicate of the missing-test-dep idiom recorded at 5b16ecc0 (rich-click): added dev-python/flask and dev-python/joserfc to test? BDEPEND (bug 978076).
pytest suite failed in new upstream tests/isolated directory (subprocess-isolation tests) under the portage build environment (bug 978247).
root_cause: Upstream added a tests/isolated tree whose tests spawn isolated subprocesses and do not work reliably in sandboxed ebuild test runs; benchmarks were already excluded the same way.
fix_pattern: Add the failing directory to the EPYTEST_IGNORE array inside python_test(), next to the already-ignored benchmark files.
rule: When an upstream release adds test directories that are environment-sensitive (isolated/subprocess/benchmark tests) and they fail under sandbox, append them to EPYTEST_IGNORE instead of patching the test suite.

problem: Test suite fails with ModuleNotFoundError for pkg_resources (bug 978315).
root_cause: setuptools >= 81 no longer provides the bundled pkg_resources module implicitly; Gentoo split it out into dev-python/pkg-resources, so anything importing pkg_resources now needs an explicit dependency.
fix_pattern: Add a test-conditional BDEPEND on dev-python/pkg-resources with PYTHON_USEDEP.
rule: If a package's tests (or runtime code) import pkg_resources, add an explicit dependency on dev-python/pkg-resources[${PYTHON_USEDEP}] — never rely on setuptools to provide it.

problem: Same pkg_resources-split pattern as python-lzo (bug 978398): tests import pkg_resources, fixed by adding dev-python/pkg-resources[${PYTHON_USEDEP}] to the existing test? BDEPEND block.
Tests were run via upstream's custom runner (cd test && python run_all.py) which failed (bug 979174) and offered no deselect/parallelism control.
root_cause: Custom test-runner scripts bypass pytest tooling, so failures cannot be deselected or ignored per-test and runs are serial.
fix_pattern: Switch to distutils_enable_tests pytest with EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (no plugin autoloading, no extra deps) and EPYTEST_XDIST=1 (parallel), then call epytest on the test directory in python_test().
rule: Replace upstream custom test-runner scripts with epytest pointed at the test directory; declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() for a deterministic plugin set and EPYTEST_XDIST=1 for parallelism.
snippet:
EPYTEST_PLUGINS=()
EPYTEST_XDIST=1
distutils_enable_tests pytest

python_test() {
	local -x NO_NET=1
	epytest test
}

problem: Test suite required extra dependency packages solely because upstream's pytest configuration turns all warnings into errors (bug 979233).
root_cause: jaraco-skeleton projects ship pyproject/pytest config with filterwarnings = error, so any new DeprecationWarning from a newer Python or missing warning-suppression dep fails the whole suite.
fix_pattern: Override the ini option on the pytest command line with -o filterwarnings= (empty value clears it), allowing the extra test deps to be dropped; PYTHON_COMPAT was trimmed to supported targets in the same commit.
rule: When tests fail only because of upstream filterwarnings=error pytest config (typical for jaraco.* packages), clear it at runtime with 'epytest -o filterwarnings=' instead of adding deps or patching.

problem: Routine switch of distutils_enable_tests from unittest to pytest, adding EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() and EPYTEST_XDIST=1 (same modern pytest idiom as the netcdf4 lesson in this batch); fixed bug 979238.
Package restricted to python3_{12..14} while CPython 3.15 target already exists in Gentoo.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT is an explicit allowlist; every new interpreter slot needs per-package opt-in after its test suite passes on the new version.
fix_pattern: Extend the brace range in PYTHON_COMPAT after testing; done tree-wide in bulk once a new target is available. Companion commits in the same sweep also drop the retired oldest slot (11->12) and stale pypy3_11 in the same one-line edit.
rule: When a new CPython target lands in python-utils-r1, run the package's tests under it and widen the PYTHON_COMPAT brace range, retiring the oldest slot in the same edit.

problem: Package not installable for free-threading (no-GIL) CPython interpreters.
root_cause: Free-threading CPython builds are separate PYTHON_COMPAT targets (python3_14t, python3_15t) requiring explicit opt-in, only valid once the whole dependency chain supports them.
fix_pattern: Append the t-suffixed targets to PYTHON_COMPAT using brace expansion.
rule: Enable python3_XYt free-threading targets by adding python3_{14,15}t to PYTHON_COMPAT, but only after verifying every runtime dep already lists those targets.

problem: One-line freethreading enablement: PYTHON_COMPAT += python3_{14,15}t, same pattern as async-timeout in this batch.
One-line freethreading enablement: PYTHON_COMPAT += python3_{14,15}t, same pattern as async-timeout in this batch.
One-line freethreading enablement: PYTHON_COMPAT += python3_{14,15}t, same pattern as async-timeout in this batch.
Alongside the py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT bump, a stale global-scope 'export SETUPTOOLS_SCM_PRETEND_VERSION=${PV}' was deleted.
root_cause: PyPI sdists ship PKG-INFO, from which setuptools-scm/hatch-vcs resolve the version without git metadata, so the pretend-version export is dead weight (it is only needed for VCS snapshots/GitHub tarballs lacking sdist metadata).
fix_pattern: Remove the export during routine maintenance of a pypi.eclass-based ebuild.
rule: For packages fetched as PyPI sdists, SETUPTOOLS_SCM_PRETEND_VERSION exports are usually unnecessary — audit and drop them when touching the ebuild; keep them only for GitHub/VCS tarballs.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15} plus copyright year update; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}, dropping pypy3_11 in the same edit; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}, dropping pypy3_11; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{12..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other changes.
openjdk:8 still advertised a javafx USE flag whose PDEPEND target dev-java/openjfx:8 no longer exists in the tree (only openjfx:11 remains), leaving a dangling optional dependency.
root_cause: The dependency package's slot was removed from the tree but the consumer's optional USE-conditional PDEPEND was not cleaned up.
fix_pattern: Delete the USE flag from IUSE and the PDEPEND line, without a revision bump.
rule: When a package referenced only through a USE-conditional PDEPEND is removed from the tree, drop the flag and the PDEPEND together; no revbump is needed because the installed image is unchanged.

problem: Tests (and a runtime fallback path) fail on Python 3.15 because they string-match the hashlib.new() ValueError message.
root_cause: CPython 3.15 changed the unknown-algorithm error text from 'unsupported hash type <name>' to 'unsupported hash algorithm <name>' (cpython commit 6be49ee); prefix-matching code and test assertions broke.
fix_pattern: Backport the pending upstream fix (opendev review 986313) as a FILESDIR patch listed in PATCHES with the review URL in a comment; the patch matches both message variants via regex and checks for the algorithm name in the message instead of a fixed prefix.
rule: When enabling a new Python version, expect breakage in code/tests that string-match exception messages; backport the upstream fix as a files/ patch and cite the upstream review or commit URL in a comment above the PATCHES entry.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{12..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{13..14} -> python3_{13..15}; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{12..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{12..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{12..14} -> python3_{12..15} (pbr backend); no other changes.
Security point release 152.0.6 (bug 979264) needed in tree quickly; risk of accidentally changing build logic during a hurried bump.
root_cause: Upstream point releases change only source code, not build requirements, so the ebuild content should not change at all between 152.0.5 and 152.0.6.
fix_pattern: The new firefox-152.0.6.ebuild is a byte-identical copy of firefox-152.0.5.ebuild (git diff between the two versions is empty); only Manifest gained the new source tarball. Versioned support variables (FIREFOX_PATCHSET=firefox-152-patches-03.tar.xz, LLVM_COMPAT, RUST_MIN_VER, WASI_SDK_VER) stay untouched because the patchset still applies within the same branch.
rule: For upstream point/security releases within the same branch, add the new version as an exact copy of the previous ebuild and regenerate Manifest; verify the bump introduced no logic drift by diffing the new ebuild against the old one and expecting empty output.
snippet:
cp foo-1.2.3.ebuild foo-1.2.4.ebuild
pkgdev manifest
git diff --no-index foo-1.2.3.ebuild foo-1.2.4.ebuild  # must be empty

problem: Package not yet marked compatible with the new CPython 3.15 target, blocking users and reverse deps from switching PYTHON_TARGETS.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT is an explicit allowlist; every dev-python package must be opted in to each new interpreter after its test suite is confirmed to pass.
fix_pattern: One-line change PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..14} ) -> ( python3_{12..15} ), committed without a revision bump because installed content for existing implementations is unchanged. Note the ordering across this commit series: leaves of the OpenStack dep graph (stevedore, oslo-*) were enabled before their consumers (keystoneauth1, python-*client), since a package can only gain a target once all its PYTHON_USEDEP dependencies already have it.
rule: When enabling a new Python implementation, extend the PYTHON_COMPAT brace range bottom-up through the dependency chain (deps first, consumers after), run the test suite under the new interpreter, and do not revbump.

problem: Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT extension to python3_15; same pattern as python-ironicclient.

While enabling py3.15, the ebuild still advertised EOL implementations (python3_11, pypy3_11) and let pytest autoload whatever plugins happened to be installed on the system, making test results non-deterministic.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT lists rot as old interpreters are retired, and distutils_enable_tests pytest historically ran with plugin autoloading on, so unrelated installed pytest plugins (xdist, cov, rerunfailures...) could break or mask test failures.
fix_pattern: In the same touch: PYTHON_COMPAT trimmed from ( pypy3_11 python3_{11..14} ) to ( python3_{12..15} ), and EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() declared immediately before distutils_enable_tests pytest. An empty EPYTEST_PLUGINS array tells distutils-r1 to run pytest with plugin autoloading disabled and no extra plugin deps; list plugin package names in the array only when tests actually need them.
rule: Whenever you touch a dev-python ebuild for a target bump, normalize PYTHON_COMPAT to currently supported implementations and declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS explicitly (empty array if the test suite needs no plugins) so pytest runs are hermetic.

Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT extension to python3_15.
Package (with mypyc-compiled C extension, DISTUTILS_EXT=1) not installable for users running free-threaded (no-GIL) CPython builds.
root_cause: Free-threaded CPython is a separate ABI, so python-r1 models it as distinct opt-in targets (python3_14t, python3_15t); packages, especially those building native extensions, must be individually verified and enabled.
fix_pattern: Append the freethreading tokens after the regular range: PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} python3_{14,15}t ). The t-suffixed targets coexist with the normal ones; no revbump. Done here for a DISTUTILS_EXT=1 package, meaning the C-extension build and test suite were confirmed to work without the GIL (same commit series enabled a maturin/pyo3 package, ast-serialize, the identical way).
rule: To support free-threaded CPython, append python3_XYt tokens to PYTHON_COMPAT alongside the regular targets, but only after building and testing the package (including any C/Rust extensions) under the no-GIL interpreter.

Routine freethreading enablement (append python3_{14,15}t) for a maturin/pyo3 Rust-extension package; same idiom as mypy commit.

Package not yet enabled for the new python3_15 target after Gentoo added it tree-wide.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT is a static per-ebuild opt-in list; every new CPython target requires the maintainer to test and extend it.
fix_pattern: After verifying the test suite passes under the new implementation, extend the brace range in place (no revbump needed, adding an impl only widens availability): PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} ).
rule: When a new python3_N target lands in the tree, test the package under it and extend the PYTHON_COMPAT brace range in the existing ebuild without a revbump; drop targets that were removed from the tree in the same edit.

distutils-r1 ebuild used distutils_enable_tests pytest without declaring which pytest plugins the test suite needs, leaving pytest plugin autoloading in effect (fragile under new Python versions where unrelated installed plugins can break collection).
root_cause: Without EPYTEST_PLUGINS the eclass cannot know the plugin set, so it cannot disable plugin autoload or generate exact test BDEPEND.
fix_pattern: Declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS before distutils_enable_tests pytest; an empty array explicitly states 'no plugins needed' and makes the eclass export PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD.
rule: In distutils-r1 ebuilds running pytest, always declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS (empty array when no plugins are needed) so plugin autoloading is disabled and plugin test-deps are eclass-generated.

problem: Enabling py3.15 required fixing test failures (time.time() precision change broke expires_at assertions) and the ebuild still used the legacy test layout: manual test? BDEPEND, python_test() with local EPYTEST_DESELECT/EPYTEST_IGNORE and manual PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1.
root_cause: Upstream tests assumed float second-precision timestamps that changed behavior on newer Python; the ebuild predated the EPYTEST_PLUGINS mechanism so plugin deps and pytest env handling were hand-rolled.
fix_pattern: Backport the exact upstream test-fix commit as a FILESDIR patch, citing the upstream commit URL in a comment above PATCHES; replace the test? BDEPEND block with EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( requests-mock ); hoist EPYTEST_DESELECT/EPYTEST_IGNORE to global-scope arrays and delete python_test() entirely so the eclass default epytest run is used.
rule: When a new Python version breaks only the test suite, backport the upstream test-fix commit as a patch (with the commit URL in a comment) instead of deselecting tests, and modernize to EPYTEST_PLUGINS plus global EPYTEST_DESELECT/EPYTEST_IGNORE so a custom python_test() becomes unnecessary.
snippet:
EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( requests-mock )
distutils_enable_tests pytest
PATCHES=(
	# https://github.com/requests/requests-oauthlib/commit/b1dd93c...
	"${FILESDIR}/${P}-test.patch"
)
EPYTEST_DESELECT=(
	# Internet access
	tests/test_core.py::OAuth1Test::testCanPostBinaryData
)

problem: Plain PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{11..14} to {12..15} (new target added, removed 3.11 dropped); same pattern as e4db68c.
Plain PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{11..14} to {12..15}; same pattern as e4db68c.
Plain PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{11..14} to {12..15}; same pattern as e4db68c.
PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} that also dropped the retired pypy3_11 target in the same edit; same pattern as e4db68c.
Ebuild set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() although its tests run via distutils_enable_tests unittest, so the variable was dead code.
root_cause: EPYTEST_* variables only affect the epytest/pytest runner; with the unittest runner they do nothing and mislead readers into thinking pytest is involved.
fix_pattern: Remove EPYTEST_PLUGINS when the test runner passed to distutils_enable_tests is not pytest.
rule: Only set EPYTEST_* variables when tests actually run under pytest; delete them when distutils_enable_tests uses unittest (or another runner).

problem: Plain PYTHON_COMPAT range bump to python3_{12..15}; same pattern as e4db68c.
Plain PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{11..14} to {12..15}; same pattern as e4db68c.
Plain PYTHON_COMPAT range bump to python3_{12..15}; same pattern as e4db68c.
Test-only pytest-plugin dependencies were hand-written in a BDEPEND test? block, duplicated across the release and -9999 live ebuild.
root_cause: Ebuild predated the EPYTEST_PLUGINS mechanism; hand-rolled test deps drift and do not disable pytest plugin autoloading.
fix_pattern: Replace the BDEPEND test? block with EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-lazy-fixtures requests-mock ) before distutils_enable_tests pytest, and apply the identical change to both the versioned and the 9999 live ebuild in one commit.
rule: Move pytest-plugin test deps from a manual BDEPEND test? block into EPYTEST_PLUGINS, and keep the -9999 live ebuild in sync with the release ebuild in the same commit so it does not rot.

problem: Plain PYTHON_COMPAT range bump to python3_{12..15}; same pattern as e4db68c.
Enabling py3.15 surfaced a nondeterministically failing test, a leftover manual BDEPEND test dep alongside an existing EPYTEST_PLUGINS array, and a stale pypy3_11 target.
root_cause: requests-mock had been added to BDEPEND before EPYTEST_PLUGINS existed, and one upstream test regressed (suspected dependency-version-induced), unrelated to the interpreter itself.
fix_pattern: Consolidate all pytest plugins into one EPYTEST_PLUGINS array (brace expansion works: pytest-{httpbin,mock}), drop the manual BDEPEND block, drop retired pypy3_11, and deselect the regressing test inside python_test() with a comment stating why.
rule: Keep every pytest-plugin dep in a single EPYTEST_PLUGINS array rather than split between it and BDEPEND, and always annotate an EPYTEST_DESELECT entry with a comment explaining the failure so it can be revisited.

problem: Plain PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{11..14} to {12..15}; same pattern as e4db68c.
Plain PYTHON_COMPAT range bump to python3_{12..15}; same pattern as e4db68c.

USE=xattr had no effect on the build: the ebuild passed --enable-attr/--disable-attr, but gettext's configure switch is actually --enable-xattr, so xattr support silently followed automagic detection regardless of the flag (bug 910070).
root_cause: $(use_enable xattr attr) renames the configure option via its second argument, and the rename was wrong; autoconf configure ignores unrecognized --enable-*/--disable-* options, so the mistake produces no hard error, only the 'Unrecognized configure options' QA notice in the build log.
fix_pattern: Drop the wrong second argument so the option name matches configure: $(use_enable xattr). Sam James fixed all live ebuild versions in one commit.
rule: Whenever use_enable/use_with carries a second (option-renaming) argument, verify the exact switch name in ./configure --help, and treat any 'QA Notice: Unrecognized configure options' in a build log as a USE flag that is silently a no-op.

problem: Ebuild still called multilib_parallel_foreach_abi, an obsolete multilib-build.eclass function that has not actually run ABIs in parallel for many years (bug 979259).
root_cause: The parallel variant lost its parallelism when the underlying multiprocessing support was removed from the eclass; it survived only as a misleading alias for the serial function and is slated for removal.
fix_pattern: Mechanical rename to the regular function: multilib_parallel_foreach_abi -> multilib_foreach_abi, works identically in src_* and pkg_* phases.
rule: Replace multilib_parallel_foreach_abi with multilib_foreach_abi everywhere; more generally, when an eclass function is documented as a deprecated no-op alias, grep the overlay and migrate before the alias is removed and breaks sourcing.

problem: Same multilib_parallel_foreach_abi -> multilib_foreach_abi rename as 939e595 (applied in pkg_postinst across four ebuild versions).
Same multilib_parallel_foreach_abi -> multilib_foreach_abi rename as 939e595 (pkg_preinst/pkg_postinst in gtk+2 and gtk+3 ebuilds).
Same multilib_parallel_foreach_abi -> multilib_foreach_abi rename as 939e595 (src_configure).
Ebuild still called multilib_parallel_foreach_abi in src_configure although that function has not actually run anything in parallel for years and is deprecated (bug 979259).
root_cause: multilib-build.eclass kept multilib_parallel_foreach_abi as a backward-compat alias after parallelism was removed; old ebuilds copied the name forward through bumps without noticing the deprecation.
fix_pattern: Mechanical rename of the eclass function call: multilib_parallel_foreach_abi <fn> -> multilib_foreach_abi <fn>. No revbump needed since generated build output is identical.
rule: When an ebuild uses multilib_parallel_foreach_abi, replace it with multilib_foreach_abi; more generally, on every bump grep your inherited eclass function calls against current eclass docs for deprecated aliases.

problem: Same deprecated multilib_parallel_foreach_abi call as x11-misc/virtualgl (bug 979259); identical mechanical rename to multilib_foreach_abi.
verify-sig failed on the new openvpn release because the installed sec-keys/openpgp-keys-openvpn keyring did not yet contain the key upstream used to sign it (bug 979252).
root_cause: Unversioned dependency on the sec-keys package allowed emerge to be satisfied by an outdated keyring that predates upstream's key rotation/addition, so signature verification failed at fetch/unpack time.
fix_pattern: After bumping the keys package, pin a minimum version of the sec-keys dep inside the verify-sig conditional so the keyring containing the new key is enforced.
rule: When a verify-sig package fails signature verification after a bump because upstream signed with a new key, bump the corresponding sec-keys/openpgp-keys-* package and change the dep to >=sec-keys/...-<new-version> under verify-sig?.

problem: New pytest-plugin package: the PyPI sdist ships no tests, and the upstream hatchling pyproject.toml produced a wheel missing the actual pytest_tap package (empty wheel), so the installed plugin was unusable.
root_cause: Upstream set both sources=["src"] and packages=["pytest_tap"] in [tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel], which hatchling resolves to no matching package; and upstream excludes tests from the sdist.
fix_pattern: Use the GitHub tag tarball instead of PyPI so tests are available (SRC_URI ... -> ${P}.gh.tar.gz), backport the merged upstream pyproject fix as a files/ patch (packages=["src/pytest_tap"], drop sources), and self-test the plugin with EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( "${PN}" ) plus EPYTEST_PLUGIN_LOAD_VIA_ENV=1 so the just-built plugin is loaded via the environment during epytest.
rule: For pytest-plugin packages: fetch from the git tag when the sdist lacks tests, verify the built wheel actually contains the import package (hatchling src-layout misconfig is common), and enable the plugin under test via EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( "${PN}" ) + EPYTEST_PLUGIN_LOAD_VIA_ENV=1.

problem: libxcb build broke when the xcb-proto python dir path needed quoting: Makefile passed -p $(XCBPROTO_XCBPYTHONDIR) unquoted to c_client.py, so paths with spaces/special characters split into multiple arguments (bug 979126).
root_cause: Upstream Makefile.am (and the generated Makefile.in) left a make variable holding a filesystem path unquoted in a shell command line.
fix_pattern: Add a files/ patch that quotes the variable in BOTH Makefile.am and the pre-generated Makefile.in with identical hunks, so the fix takes effect without adding eautoreconf (no autotools regeneration, no extra BDEPENDs); wire it via PATCHES=( ... ). Same commit also pruned dead interpreters from PYTHON_COMPAT (3_{10..14} -> 3_{12..14}).
rule: When patching an autotools build system, patch the generated Makefile.in alongside Makefile.am with the same hunk to avoid pulling in eautoreconf; and always quote make variables that expand to paths.

problem: virtual/httpd-php-8.5 kept ~keywords on arches (alpha, hppa, loong, mips, s390) where its only provider dev-lang/php:8.5 had just been unkeyworded for a security issue, making the virtual unsatisfiable there (bug 977138).
root_cause: Keywords on a virtual are independent of its providers, so dropping keywords on the provider silently strands the virtual with broken deps on those arches.
fix_pattern: Edit the virtual's KEYWORDS to exactly the intersection of what its providers still carry.
rule: Whenever you add or drop keywords on a package, immediately sync any virtual (or meta-package) whose || deps point at it, keeping the virtual's KEYWORDS a subset of its providers'.

problem: Ebuild carried IUSE="+doc" only to silence the pkgcheck RequiredUseDefaults warning caused by REQUIRED_USE="qt6? ( doc )", force-enabling doc builds for every user.
root_cause: Making a flag default-on in the ebuild is a global hammer: it was chosen to satisfy REQUIRED_USE under default profile flags, not because most users want it.
fix_pattern: Drop the + from IUSE and instead set the flag default in the relevant profile (here: doc enabled for the package in the desktop profile, where qt6 is on), so REQUIRED_USE stays satisfiable per-profile.
rule: When a +flag default exists only to satisfy REQUIRED_USE under some profile's defaults, move the default into that profile's package.use instead of forcing it on all users via IUSE="+flag".
snippet:
-IUSE="+doc qt6 test unicode"  # +doc to address warning RequiredUseDefaults
+IUSE="doc qt6 test unicode"
 REQUIRED_USE="qt6? ( doc ) test? ( unicode )"

problem: Enabling python3_15 made the test suite fail: tests still used assertEquals, one of the deprecated camelCase unittest aliases that CPython finally removed.
root_cause: unittest's legacy aliases (assertEquals, failUnless, ...) were deprecated for years and removed in new CPython; stale upstream test suites break only when the new interpreter is enabled.
fix_pattern: sed the removed alias to its modern name in src_prepare rather than patching or skipping tests; simultaneously the compat range moved from python3_{11..14} pypy3_11 to python3_{12..15} (drop EOL interpreters while adding the new one).
rule: When enabling a new Python version and tests die with AttributeError on assertEquals/failUnless-style names, sed the removed unittest aliases in src_prepare (s:assertEquals:assertEqual:) instead of restricting tests.

problem: py3.15 enablement doubled as cleanup: the ebuild still carried namespace-package workarounds (deleting *.pth files in python_compile and calling distutils_write_namespace sphinxcontrib before tests) that are no longer needed.
root_cause: sphinxcontrib packages historically used pkg_resources-style namespaces requiring eclass hacks; modern releases use native/implicit namespaces so the hacks are dead weight and can mask real issues.
fix_pattern: While bumping PYTHON_COMPAT to python3_{12..15}, delete the python_compile override that removed .pth files and drop distutils_write_namespace from python_test; declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() to run pytest without plugin autoloading.
rule: When touching a sphinxcontrib-* or other former namespace package, check whether .pth-deletion/distutils_write_namespace workarounds are still required and remove them if tests pass without.

problem: Ebuild used the legacy DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit backend name and ran its own plugin tests without declaring the plugin, risking testing the source tree instead of the built package.
root_cause: distutils-r1 renamed/split the flit backend value to flit-core, and the newer EPYTEST_PLUGINS mechanism expects pytest plugins under test to be declared explicitly and loaded via the environment.
fix_pattern: Switch DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit to flit-core, bump PYTHON_COMPAT to python3_{12..15}, and add EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( "${PN}" ) with EPYTEST_PLUGIN_LOAD_VIA_ENV=1 before distutils_enable_tests pytest.
rule: Use DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit-core for flit_core-built packages, and for any pytest-plugin package declare itself in EPYTEST_PLUGINS with EPYTEST_PLUGIN_LOAD_VIA_ENV=1.

problem: py3.15 enablement needed three companion fixes: an explicit dev-python/pytest test dep duplicated what distutils_enable_tests already adds, the package (a pytest plugin) was not declared via EPYTEST_PLUGINS, and tests used the removed assertEquals alias.
root_cause: distutils_enable_tests pytest injects the pytest dep itself; new CPython removed legacy unittest aliases; plugin self-testing now goes through EPYTEST_PLUGINS.
fix_pattern: Remove dev-python/pytest from test? deps, add EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( "${PN}" ), and sed assertEquals->assertEqual in python_prepare_all while bumping PYTHON_COMPAT to python3_{12..15}.
rule: Never list dev-python/pytest manually when distutils_enable_tests pytest is used; treat a PYTHON_COMPAT bump as the moment to fix removed-alias test failures and declare plugin self-loading.
snippet:
test? (
		dev-python/fixtures[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]
-		dev-python/pytest[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]
	...
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( "${PN}" )
+	sed -i -e 's:assertEquals:assertEqual:' tests/test_*.py || die

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{12..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump; also drops pypy3_11 alongside adding python3_15.

py3.15 enablement also had to add EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() because the eclass's newer pytest handling expects an explicit plugin list; without it, autoloaded third-party plugins can break or pollute the test run.
root_cause: distutils-r1/epytest moved from implicit pytest plugin autoloading to an explicit opt-in model: EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() disables autoloading entirely, EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pkg ... ) loads only the named ones.
fix_pattern: Declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (empty array) above distutils_enable_tests pytest for packages whose tests need no pytest plugins, insulating tests from whatever plugins happen to be installed on the build host.
rule: When enabling tests via distutils_enable_tests pytest, always set EPYTEST_PLUGINS explicitly: empty array if no plugins are needed, or the exact plugin list otherwise.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() addition (same idiom as dev-python/munch 1ee8691b).
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{12..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() addition (same idiom as dev-python/munch 1ee8691b).
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{12..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other change.
Pure style commit: S="${WORKDIR}"/${MY_P} moved from after the dependency block to directly after SRC_URI, matching skel.ebuild variable ordering; no functional change.
Routine version bump 6.1.1->8.0.0; only generic content is that stable keywords (amd64 arm64 x86) were reset to ~arch for the new version, standard bump hygiene.
CMake emits deprecation warning (fatal error with cmake >=4) because a subdirectory CMakeLists.txt declares cmake_minimum_required older than 3.5.
root_cause: Upstream ships an unused example/cmake/CMakeLists.txt with an ancient cmake_minimum_required; cmake 4 removed compatibility with <3.5 so any stray sub-project file triggers the warning/error even if never built.
fix_pattern: Delete the offending unused CMakeLists.txt in src_prepare before cmake_src_prepare instead of patching it, since the examples are not built (MSGPACK_BUILD_EXAMPLES=OFF).
rule: When cmake warns/errors about compatibility with CMake < 3.5 from a file in an unbuilt subtree (examples, bundled deps), just rm that CMakeLists.txt in src_prepare rather than patching version numbers.

problem: src_install died on 'rmdir ${D}/var/log ${D}/var/run' when USE=fpm was disabled because those directories are only created by the FPM SAPI build.
root_cause: A cleanup step for files installed by an optional component was run unconditionally; with the component's USE flag off the paths do not exist and rmdir fails, killing the merge.
fix_pattern: Wrap the cleanup in the same USE conditional that gates the component that creates the files: if use fpm; then rmdir ... || die; fi.
rule: Any src_install cleanup of files created by a USE-conditional component must be guarded by that same USE flag (or made tolerant of absence), otherwise the ebuild dies for other flag combinations.
snippet:
if use fpm ; then
	rmdir "${D}/var/log" "${D}/var/run" || die
fi

problem: Multiple PHP iconv test suite failures on musl (non-glibc) systems: tests hung on //IGNORE, byte-compared stateful ISO-2022-JP output, and used glibc-only charset names like CP850.
root_cause: The tests assume GNU iconv behavior. POSIX leaves the //IGNORE suffix optional, charset names implementation-defined, and stateful encodings like ISO-2022-JP have multiple correct byte encodings; musl's iconv (ICONV_IMPL == "unknown") differs on all three, so failures are test bugs, not package bugs.
fix_pattern: Backport/write upstream-style test patches keyed on the iconv implementation: add SKIPIF blocks that skip when ICONV_IMPL is not glibc/libiconv, append //IGNORE only for GNU implementations, fall back to ASCII charsets elsewhere, and relax EXPECTF to EXPECTREGEX. Ship as FILESDIR patches added to PATCHES across all affected ebuild versions.
rule: When tests fail only on musl and involve iconv, suspect GNU-iconv assumptions (//IGNORE, charset names, stateful encodings) and skip/parameterize those tests per iconv implementation instead of patching runtime code or disabling the whole suite.

problem: Regression tests written for the bundled copy of libgd failed when the package is built against the system (external) libgd.
root_cause: Upstream regression tests gh19666/gh19739 exercise fixes present only in the bundled libgd fork; with an external libgd the behavior legitimately differs, so the tests are invalid rather than the build broken.
fix_pattern: Replaced ad-hoc 'rm ext/gd/tests/ghNNNN.phpt' lines in src_prepare with a proper backported patch adding SKIPIF guards ('if (!GD_BUNDLED) die("skip requires bundled GD library")'), which is upstreamable and self-documenting.
rule: When a test only makes sense for a bundled library variant, prefer a SKIPIF/conditional-skip patch (upstreamable) over rm-ing the test file in src_prepare; convert existing rm hacks to skip patches when one exists upstream.
snippet:
--SKIPIF--
<?php
    if (!GD_BUNDLED) die("skip requires bundled GD library\n");
?>

problem: Newly added 'rmdir ${D}/var/log ${D}/var/run' used unquoted ${D}, which word-splits and breaks if the build root path contains spaces.
root_cause: Shell quoting hygiene lapse in a hotfix; ${D}/${ED}/${S}-derived paths must always be quoted in ebuilds.
fix_pattern: Quote each path argument: rmdir "${D}/var/log" "${D}/var/run" || die.
rule: Always double-quote ${D}, ${ED}, ${S}, ${WORKDIR}-based paths in ebuild shell commands; review any pasted fix for unquoted expansions before committing.

problem: One .phpt test broke against curl 8.17+; fixed upstream but not backported to this branch, so the ebuild rm's the single test file in src_prepare with a comment stating the cause.
Ebuild needed py3.15 enabled; it also still carried a pypy3_11 entry, an obsolete !dev-python/namespace-ruamel blocker, and a python_compile hack deleting .pth files.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT ranges and workarounds accrete; enabling a new interpreter is the natural point to shed implementations being dropped and hacks whose reason has disappeared.
fix_pattern: Rewrite PYTHON_COMPAT to the new window ( python3_{12..15} python3_{14..15}t ), drop the pypy target, delete the stale blocker and the custom python_compile override in the same commit.
rule: When enabling a new Python (raising PYTHON_COMPAT), simultaneously drop targets being retired and audit the ebuild for blockers/phase hacks that only existed for those old targets.

problem: One-line PYTHON_COMPAT window shift to python3_{12..15}; same idiom as the ruamel-yaml commit.
The xwidgets USE flag had been removed (webkit off-screen rendering was broken); restoring it required expressing that it only works with the GTK toolkit build.
root_cause: An optional feature depends on a specific toolkit configuration (gtk), so its deps and configure switch must be nested under that toolkit's USE conditional, and users must be warned when the flag silently has no effect.
fix_pattern: Nest the feature deps inside the toolkit conditional: gtk? ( x11-libs/gtk+:3[X] xwidgets? ( net-libs/webkit-gtk:4.1= x11-libs/libXcomposite ) ); replace hardcoded --without-xwidgets with $(use_with xwidgets) only in toolkit branches where it can work; ewarn when the flag is set without its prerequisite instead of REQUIRED_USE; document the flag in metadata.xml.
rule: For a USE flag that only functions under another flag's configuration, nest its dependencies inside that flag's conditional group and emit an ewarn (or REQUIRED_USE) for the ineffective combination.
snippet:
gtk? (
	x11-libs/gtk+:3[X]
	xwidgets? (
		net-libs/webkit-gtk:4.1=
		x11-libs/libXcomposite
	)
)
! use gtk && use xwidgets && ewarn \
	"USE flag \"xwidgets\" has no effect if \"gtk\" is not set."

problem: Compilation happened outside src_compile (configure-phase symlink hacks plus patches to stop cmake ExternalProject from git-cloning llama.cpp and to strip -s -w Go ldflags), violating phase separation and relying on fragile source patches.
root_cause: Upstream cmake uses FetchContent/ExternalProject to download llama.cpp at build time and hardcodes Go ldflags '-s -w' (pre-stripping); the old ebuild fought this with sed-style patches and did work in the wrong phases.
fix_pattern: Pre-seed the dependency source instead of patching the download: symlink the separately-fetched llama.cpp tarball into ${BUILD_DIR}/_deps and pass -DFETCHCONTENT_SOURCE_DIR_LLAMA_CPP=... so cmake never hits the network. Use scoped 'local BUILD_DIR=... local CMAKE_USE_DIR=...' to configure/compile a sub-project with the cmake eclass, stage a component via '${CMAKE_BINARY} --install ... --component X', and build the Go binary directly with 'ego build -trimpath -ldflags "-X pkg.Version=${PV}"' (omitting upstream's -s -w) instead of patching the flags out of cmake.
rule: When upstream cmake FetchContent/ExternalProject downloads a vendored dependency, fetch it via SRC_URI and point FETCHCONTENT_SOURCE_DIR_<NAME> at the unpacked tree; and when upstream hardcodes stripping Go ldflags, bypass the wrapper and invoke ego build with your own -ldflags rather than patching.

problem: Plain version bump 7.6->7.7; new ebuild is byte-identical to the previous one, no packaging change to learn from.
Enabling py3.15 left behind scaffolding for partially-tested interpreters: a PYTHON_TESTED subset array, python_gen_cond_dep-wrapped test deps, and a python_test early-return for untested impls.
root_cause: The PYTHON_TESTED/PYTHON_COMPAT split and python_gen_cond_dep guards exist only while some targets (old pythons, pypy) lack test support; once the compat window moves past them the indirection is dead weight.
fix_pattern: Collapse PYTHON_COMPAT to the plain new range, unwrap $(python_gen_cond_dep ...) test deps into plain [${PYTHON_USEDEP}] deps, drop version-conditional runtime deps (importlib-resources for <=3.11), and delete the has-EPYTHON guard in python_test.
rule: python_gen_cond_dep and PYTHON_TESTED-style guards should be removed, not carried forward, once the interpreters they special-cased leave PYTHON_COMPAT.

problem: One-line PYTHON_COMPAT shift python3_{11..14}+pypy3_11 -> python3_{12..15}; routine py3.15 enablement.
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT shift to python3_{12..15}; routine py3.15 enablement.
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT shift to python3_{12..15}; routine py3.15 enablement.
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT shift to python3_{12..15}; routine py3.15 enablement.
py3.15 enablement replaced the pypy3.11 EPYTEST_DESELECT block with a python3.15 one; same case-${EPYTHON} deselect idiom as parso.
Test suite fails on a newly enabled Python version because a few tests assert interpreter-specific behavior (exact exception message wording; syntax that the new Python now accepts).
root_cause: New CPython releases legitimately change error messages and grammar; parser/tooling test suites that hardcode those details fail without any package bug.
fix_pattern: Override python_test and deselect the specific tests per interpreter with a case statement on ${EPYTHON}, commenting why each is deselected, then call epytest.
rule: When enabling a new Python version, deselect version-sensitive tests via 'case ${EPYTHON} in python3.XX*)' with EPYTEST_DESELECT rather than restricting tests or holding back PYTHON_COMPAT.

problem: Dropped ~m68k from a prerelease's KEYWORDS (unkeywording an arch that cannot currently build/test it); routine keyword maintenance.

A multi-locale dictionary distfile (.oxt from LibreOffice extensions) bundles en-AU/CA/GB/US/ZA variants plus shared hyphenation/thesaurus files, and the ebuild must install only the user-selected locales.
root_cause: One upstream archive serves several locales with shared components (en_US thesaurus used by all, en_GB hyphenation used by all non-US variants), so per-locale installation needs flag-driven selection and cleanup of unused READMEs.
fix_pattern: Define PLOCALES array, generate IUSE from it (IUSE="${PLOCALES[*]/#/+l10n_}"), require at least one via REQUIRED_USE="|| ( ${PLOCALES[*]/#/l10n_} )", then in src_prepare build MYSPELL_DICT/MYSPELL_HYPH/MYSPELL_THES arrays per enabled l10n_ flag for the myspell-r2 eclass, tracking shared files (add hyph_en_GB.dic once if any non-US locale is enabled).
rule: For a single upstream archive containing per-locale variants, derive l10n_ USE flags from a PLOCALES array and select the eclass file lists conditionally in src_prepare, handling components shared across locales exactly once.

problem: One-line PYTHON_COMPAT shift to python3_{12..15}; routine py3.15 enablement.
hg-git 1.2.0 broke against mercurial 7.2 but the ebuild's open-ended '>=dev-vcs/mercurial-6.6' dependency happily allowed the incompatible new version.
root_cause: Plugins/extensions tracking a fast-moving host application need an upper bound once an incompatible host release exists; a bare >= lower bound encodes only the past, not the known breakage.
fix_pattern: Change the RDEPEND to an upper-bounded '<dev-vcs/mercurial-7.2[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]' so portage holds mercurial back (or blocks the upgrade) until a fixed hg-git is released.
rule: When a new release of a runtime host application breaks an extension package, cap the extension's dependency with '<host-version' immediately instead of waiting for an upstream fix.

problem: USE=btpclient install failed: the ebuild manually ran 'dobin tools/btpclient' but upstream's build system had moved the binary to client/btpclient/btpclient.
root_cause: Files installed manually via dobin/doins (because upstream's make install skips them) hardcode build-tree paths, which silently move between upstream releases; the failure only shows with the relevant USE flag enabled.
fix_pattern: Update the hardcoded path to the new build-tree location: use btpclient && dobin client/btpclient/btpclient.
rule: On every version bump, re-verify build-tree paths used by manual dobin/doins of not-installed-by-default files, and test with the gating USE flags enabled since these paths break silently otherwise.
snippet:
-use btpclient && dobin tools/btpclient
+use btpclient && dobin client/btpclient/btpclient

problem: Test build broke with a compile error in a test .cpp file that previously built fine, after a gtest (or toolchain header) update removed a transitive include.
root_cause: The source relied on <iomanip> being pulled in transitively via gtest headers; newer gtest/libstdc++ cleaned up its includes, exposing the missing direct include.
fix_pattern: Backport the upstream one-line fix as a FILESDIR patch adding the direct '#include <iomanip>', with the Gentoo bug URL and upstream commit URL in the patch header, and add it to PATCHES.
rule: When a package suddenly fails to compile with 'no member/undeclared identifier' errors after a gcc/libstdc++/gtest bump, look for a removed transitive include and patch in the missing direct #include, citing the upstream commit in the patch header.

problem: Live ebuild broke after upstream removed the libshaderc/libglslang configure split; either library is still needed at build time and configure now autodetects one.
root_cause: Upstream merged/removed configure options; autodetection between two alternative providers creates automagic behavior where the less-tested path may be silently chosen.
fix_pattern: Track the upstream change in the -9999 ebuild: drop the removed --disable-libglslang and the libshaderc IUSE mapping, and keep a hard dep on the provider upstream prefers (shaderc, which provides glslc and pulls glslang anyway) so the alternative path is never automagically selected.
rule: When upstream turns an explicit option into autodetection between alternative providers, depend unconditionally on the provider upstream prefers instead of letting configure pick, to avoid automagic and the less-tested code path.
snippet:
-	vulkan:libshaderc,vulkan,vulkan-static
+	vulkan:vulkan,vulkan-static
-		--disable-libglslang # prefer shaderc
 vulkan? ( media-libs/shaderc[${MULTILIB_USEDEP}] media-libs/vulkan-loader[...] )

problem: boost::optional<bool> copy construction misbehaved (upstream regression) causing misleading program behavior in consumers.
root_cause: Upstream bug in the new union-based optional implementation; header-only library so the bug propagates into everything compiled against installed headers.
fix_pattern: Backport the exact upstream fix commit as a FILESDIR patch, add it to PATCHES, and revbump (-r1 -> -r2) even though it is a header-only change, so users recompile against fixed headers. The patch header documents Bug:/Patch: URLs and notes 'File paths adapted to match dist layout' because the boost release tarball layout differs from the git repo layout.
rule: When backporting an upstream git patch to a package whose release tarball layout differs from the repo (boost: include/boost -> boost, libs/X/test -> test), rewrite the patch paths to the dist layout and always revbump for behavior-changing header fixes.
snippet:
PATCHES=(
	...
	"${FILESDIR}"/${PN}-1.91.0-optional-bool-copy-ctor.patch
)
# patch header:
# Bug: https://github.com/boostorg/optional/issues/146
# Patch: https://github.com/boostorg/optional/commit/1fb60cb
# File paths adapted to match dist layout.

problem: Old Makefile build had automagic deps; new upstream CMake build wanted to install the systemd unit itself into a pkg-config-derived path, unit ran as wrong user, plugins were not installed at all, and users upgrading needed a warning about the moved plugin path.
root_cause: Upstream CMake decides init-system integration at configure time via pkg_check_modules(systemd) (automagic, wrong for Gentoo split init support); unit hardcoded User=proxy instead of the acct-user; upstream used configure-time file(GLOB) on plugin outputs that do not exist yet so install() got an empty list.
fix_pattern: Switch to cmake.eclass with explicit -D3PROXY_USE_*=$(usex ...) toggles; patch CMakeLists to only configure_file() the .service into BUILD_DIR and install it yourself with systemd_dounit "${BUILD_DIR}"/3proxy.service plus newinitd for openrc; patch the unit to User=3proxy/Group=3proxy matching acct-user/3proxy; fix the plugin install with install(DIRECTORY ... FILES_MATCHING PATTERN) which globs at install time; use eapi9-ver's ver_replacing in pkg_postinst to warn only on upgrades from older versions.
rule: Never let a build system detect the init system or install units itself: patch it to just configure the unit into BUILD_DIR, install with systemd_dounit + newinitd, align unit User/Group with the acct-user, and gate upgrade warnings with ver_replacing -lt <ver>.

problem: openmw would break when built against the upcoming mygui 3.5 which has breaking API changes.
root_cause: Consumer dep was only lower-bounded (>=dev-games/mygui-3.4.3:=), so a breaking upstream release of the library immediately applies to consumers.
fix_pattern: Replace the lower bound with an upper bound: <dev-games/mygui-3.5.0:= ; the >= part is dropped because no older versions remain in tree, keeping the dep string minimal.
rule: When a library ships a release with breaking changes, add a <next-version upper bound in consumers preemptively, and drop lower bounds that are already satisfied by every in-tree version.

problem: sed automagically linked sys-apps/attr (via a gnulib module) without any dependency declared, surfacing as bug 979209.
root_cause: >=sys-apps/acl-2.4.0 dropped its own dep on sys-apps/attr, so attr was no longer guaranteed present transitively; the hidden automagic configure check in gnulib then flips based on what happens to be installed. Sam James notes 'I suspect we're going to see a bunch of these' - any package that silently relied on acl pulling attr is affected.
fix_pattern: Revbump; add IUSE=xattr, xattr? ( sys-apps/attr ) to RDEPEND (with [static-libs(+)] variant under static?), and pass $(use_enable xattr) in src_configure to make the gnulib check deterministic.
rule: When a common dependency drops one of its own deps, audit consumers for automagic linking against the now-orphaned library and convert it to an explicit USE flag + use_enable + dependency, with a revbump.
snippet:
IUSE="acl nls selinux static test-full xattr"
RDEPEND="!static? ( ... xattr? ( sys-apps/attr ) )"
DEPEND="${RDEPEND} static? ( ... xattr? ( sys-apps/attr[static-libs(+)] ) )"
src_configure: $(use_enable xattr)

problem: Release bump of a package with many bundled git-submodule dependencies that must be reproduced without git, while unbundling everything the system can provide.
root_cause: Upstream vendors ~20 Externals as submodules; release tarballs do not include them, and keeping any bundled lib means extra licenses and hidden code.
fix_pattern: Pin each kept submodule as a COMMIT variable with a github commit-tarball SRC_URI (USE-conditional where the feature is optional, e.g. rcheevos under retro-achievements?), mv -T into Externals/<name> in src_prepare; maintain a KEEP_BUNDLED associative array mapping bundled dir -> license and append those licenses to LICENSE; rm -r every Externals dir not in the keep list so unbundling is enforced; sed out include(CCache) to stop automagic ccache; pass -DUSE_SYSTEM_*=ON for everything unbundled, -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS=OFF for private libs, and -DCMAKE_DISABLE_FIND_PACKAGE_Git=ON on non-live builds so the host git cannot affect the build.
rule: For submodule-heavy upstreams, pin each needed submodule as a commit tarball in SRC_URI, whitelist kept bundles in an associative array that both feeds LICENSE and drives deletion of everything else, and disable find_package(Git)/ccache automagic for reproducibility.
snippet:
declare -A KEEP_BUNDLED=( [imgui]=MIT [picojson]=BSD-2 ... )
for license in ${KEEP_BUNDLED[@]}; do LICENSE+=" ${license}"; done
for s in Externals/*; do
	has "${s#Externals/}" "${!KEEP_BUNDLED[@]}" || remove+=( "${s}" )
done
rm -r "${remove[@]}" || die
mycmakeargs+=( -DCMAKE_DISABLE_FIND_PACKAGE_Git=ON )

problem: Ebuild located qmake via $(qt6_get_bindir)/qmake, which resolves to the target (ROOT) Qt install and is wrong under cross/prefix setups where the build must run the tool from BROOT.
root_cause: qt6_get_bindir points at the installed-Qt bindir rather than the build-host tool; the qt eclasses gained qt_get_broot_binary as the correct helper for executable tools needed at build time.
fix_pattern: Mechanical migration: --with-qmake6="$(qt_get_broot_binary 6 qmake)" replaces "$(qt6_get_bindir)"/qmake in both release and live ebuilds.
rule: When an ebuild needs to execute a Qt host tool (qmake, qtpaths, qhelpgenerator) during build, use qt_get_broot_binary <ver> <tool> instead of qtX_get_bindir paths.

problem: Same qt_get_broot_binary migration, but the ebuild also exported QTPATHS unconditionally, which would now die with USE=-qt6.
root_cause: qt_get_broot_binary calls die when the requested binary is missing, which is expected when Qt is not installed (USE=-qt6); the old code ran the helper outside any USE guard.
fix_pattern: Move every qt_get_broot_binary call inside `if use qt6`, including turning the env-var prefix assignment into a scoped `local -x QTPATHS=$(qt_get_broot_binary 6 qtpaths)` within the guard, then call econf normally.
rule: Only call die-capable Qt path helpers behind the USE flag that guarantees the tool exists; use `local -x VAR=...` inside the guard instead of VAR=... prefixing the econf call.
snippet:
if use qt6; then
	myeconfargs+=( --with-qmake="$(qt_get_broot_binary 6 qmake)" )
	local -x QTPATHS=$(qt_get_broot_binary 6 qtpaths)
fi
econf "${myeconfargs[@]}"

problem: android-tools built and installed shared libraries that are purely internal, causing breakage reported in bug 979198.
root_cause: CMake BUILD_SHARED_LIBS defaulted/leaked to ON (cmake.eclass sets it for many projects), turning private convenience libraries into installed shared objects.
fix_pattern: Add -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS=OFF to mycmakeargs so internal libs are static and not exposed.
rule: For CMake packages whose libraries are internal implementation details (multi-tool bundles like android-tools, dolphin), explicitly pass -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS=OFF rather than relying on the project or eclass default.

problem: msgpack-c 7.0.1 renamed its library file libmsgpackc.so.2 -> libmsgpack-c.so.2 without bumping the SONAME version, so := consumers would keep linking a file that no longer exists after the bump.
root_cause: A library file/SONAME rename with unchanged version number is invisible to plain slot deps but breaks every linked consumer exactly like a SONAME bump.
fix_pattern: Encode the rename in the subslot: SLOT="0/2-c" (previously 0/2), with an in-ebuild comment that this one-off can be dropped at the next real soname bump; the subslot change makes all :=-consumers rebuild via slot-operator.
rule: Treat a library rename (even at the same version) as an ABI break: change the subslot to a synthetic value like '2-c' so slot-operator rebuilds fire, and document when it can be normalized again.
snippet:
# Need the -c as a one-off (can drop on next soname bump) as the rename
# libmsgpackc.so.2 -> libmsgpack-c.so.2 is effectively an ABI break.
SLOT="0/2-c"

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window shift to python3_{12..15}, dropping 3.11 and pypy3_11 in the same edit.
Stale test-only deps (dev-python/fields, dev-python/py) removed after upstream no longer uses them; re-audit test deps on bumps.
Distro-installer needs a large, fast-moving set of Gentoo-specific modules on top of the upstream release, only wanted on livecd builds.
root_cause: Carrying ~90KB of downstream patches in FILESDIR bloats the tree and the patches only apply to the livecd use case.
fix_pattern: Host the patch stack as a GitHub compare-view patch fetched USE-conditionally via SRC_URI (livecd? ( .../compare/<base>...<fork>:<branch>.patch -> ${LIVEPATCH}-gentoo-patches.patch )), Manifest-pinned, and eapply it from DISTDIR only under `use livecd`. Also passes explicit -DPYTHONLIBS_VERSION/-DBOOSTPYTHON_COMPONENT derived from ${EPYTHON#python} so CMake picks the python-single-r1 interpreter's Boost.Python.
rule: Large optional downstream patchsets can live in SRC_URI as a Manifest-pinned dist patch applied conditionally with eapply, instead of FILESDIR; but note GitHub compare/commit .patch URLs are not byte-stable, so mirror or expect Manifest churn.

problem: With USE=-qt6 the ebuild died in src_configure even though the qt path was supposedly disabled: qt_get_broot_binary was still executed and die'd on the missing binary (bug 979154).
root_cause: Bash evaluates all command substitutions in an argument list before the outer command runs: in $(usev doc $(usex qt6 -DQHG_LOCATION=$(qt_get_broot_binary ...) ...)) the inner $(qt_get_broot_binary) runs regardless of the doc/qt6 flags. Nested usev/usex also can't be quoted correctly (quoting turns empty results into literal "" array elements; not quoting word-splits paths).
fix_pattern: Replace the nested usev/usex one-liner with explicit if/else blocks appending to mycmakeargs, where the helper is only invoked inside the branch that guarantees it succeeds and its result can be properly quoted.
rule: Never nest a die-capable or path-emitting command substitution inside usex/usev arguments - the inner command always runs; use plain `if use X; then mycmakeargs+=( ... ); fi` so calls are conditional and quotable.
snippet:
if use doc; then
	if use qt6; then # bug #979154
		mycmakeargs+=( -DQHG_LOCATION="$(qt_get_broot_binary 6 qhelpgenerator)" )
	else
		mycmakeargs+=( -DCMAKE_DISABLE_FIND_PACKAGE_Qt5Help=ON )
	fi
fi

problem: Package not installable for free-threaded (no-GIL) CPython variants although it works there.
root_cause: Free-threaded interpreters are separate PYTHON_COMPAT targets (python3_14t, python3_15t) that must be opted into per package after its test suite passes on them.
fix_pattern: Append the t-variants to PYTHON_COMPAT once tests pass under freethreaded CPython; no other change needed for pure-Python or already-compatible extension packages (works for setuptools/poetry-core/hatchling PEP517 builds alike, including DISTUTILS_EXT=1 packages like wrapt/isal/zlib-ng in the same commit series).
rule: To enable free-threaded Python support, run the test suite on python3.14t/3.15t and, if green, add `python3_{14,15}t` alongside the regular targets in PYTHON_COMPAT.

problem: Same freethreading enable idiom as dev-python/ddt (add python3_{14,15}t to PYTHON_COMPAT).
Same freethreading enable idiom as dev-python/ddt, notable only in that it is a DISTUTILS_EXT=1 C-extension package.
Same freethreading enable idiom as dev-python/ddt.
Same freethreading enable idiom as dev-python/ddt.
Same freethreading enable idiom as dev-python/ddt.
Same freethreading enable idiom as dev-python/ddt (C-extension package).
Same freethreading enable idiom as dev-python/ddt.
Same freethreading enable idiom as dev-python/ddt (C-extension package).
Same freethreading enable idiom as dev-python/ddt (poetry-core build).
LTS-branch version add of a large Go package that builds VM guest agents for foreign arches/OSes and generates shell completions at build time.
root_cause: Go binaries trip standard QA checks, guest agents must run inside VMs of other arches (including Windows), and completion generation executes a freshly built binary which is impossible when cross-compiling.
fix_pattern: Idioms worth copying: QA_PREBUILT listing every Go binary; static agent builds with GOARCH=<target> CGO_ENABLED=0 ego build -tags agent,netgo,static (per USE=amd64/arm64 branch, incl. GOOS=windows); a helper mapping CBUILD via go-env_goarch to the right _dist/bin[/linux_GOARCH] output dir; guarding `incus completion bash/fish/zsh > ...` behind `if ! tc-is-cross-compiler` with an ewarn fallback; CONFIG_CHECK with per-symbol ERROR_/WARNING_ messages; SLOT=0/lts to separate the LTS stream.
rule: In Go ebuilds, never execute a just-built target binary (completions, docs) without a tc-is-cross-compiler guard, and set QA_PREBUILT for all installed Go executables.
snippet:
if ! tc-is-cross-compiler; then
	"${bindir}"/incus completion bash > "${D}"/usr/share/bash-completion/completions/incus || die
else
	ewarn "Shell completion files not installed!"
fi

problem: Stale HOMEPAGE URL updated after upstream restructured their website; no generalizable content beyond keeping metadata current.
Python package not installable on freethreading (no-GIL) CPython interpreters even though upstream supports them.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT only listed regular CPython targets; the freethreading builds are separate implementation targets (python3_14t, python3_15t) that must be listed explicitly, and a package can only enable them after every dependency in its [${PYTHON_USEDEP}] graph already has them.
fix_pattern: Append the t-suffixed targets to PYTHON_COMPAT once tests pass under a freethreading interpreter. Gentoo enabled them bottom-up through the dependency graph in one commit series (sniffio/outcome before trio, trio before pytest-trio, forbiddenfruit before blockbuster) because PYTHON_USEDEP on a dep fails if the dep lacks the new implementation. Works for pure-Python and C-extension (DISTUTILS_EXT=1) packages alike, e.g. dev-python/coverage and dev-python/immutables.
rule: When enabling a new Python implementation target (e.g. freethreading python3_14t/python3_15t), add it to PYTHON_COMPAT starting from the leaves of the dependency graph upward, and only after the package's own test suite passes on that interpreter.

problem: Same freethreading enablement pattern as f8096bfe (add python3_{14,15}t to PYTHON_COMPAT).
sip-build was pointed at qmake via $(qt6_get_bindir)/qmake, which resolves inside the target sysroot and breaks cross-compilation (executing a target-arch qmake on the build host).
root_cause: qmake is a build tool and must be executed on the build host, so it has to come from BROOT, not from the host/target Qt install dir that qt6_get_bindir returns.
fix_pattern: Switch to the newer qt6 eclass helper qt_get_broot_binary(), which returns the path of a Qt tool from BROOT: --qmake="$(qt_get_broot_binary 6 qmake)".
rule: When an ebuild executes a Qt tool (qmake, moc, rcc, ...) at build time, obtain its path with qt_get_broot_binary <ver> <tool> instead of composing it from qt6_get_bindir, so cross-compilation picks the build-host binary.

problem: Same qt_get_broot_binary migration for qmake as 35e16619 (pyqt6-webengine).

SELinux base policy denied pam-using domains (su, sudo) access to /usr/share/pam config files, and first-time installs hit a circular dependency because the systemd auth interface unconditionally required the dbus module.
root_cause: Upstream refpolicy fixes existed but were not yet in any release; the dbus policy module may not be installed yet when the systemd module is first built, so unconditional dbus_system_bus_client() calls create an install-order cycle.
fix_pattern: Cherry-pick the upstream commits into FILESDIR as git-format patches (keeping the '(cherry picked from commit ...)' provenance line), add them to PATCHES=(), and revbump the ebuild to -r1 since installed policy content changes. Inside the policy, wrap the optional cross-module call in optional_policy(` ... ') to break the cycle.
rule: When runtime bugs are fixed upstream but unreleased, backport the exact upstream commits as FILESDIR patches with provenance headers and revbump; for optional cross-package integrations, guard them so first-install ordering cannot deadlock.

problem: Patches listed in PATCHES=() were silently never applied: the package built and installed fine but without the fixes it claimed to carry.
root_cause: The ebuild defined a custom src_prepare() that called only eapply_user; the PATCHES array is applied by the default src_prepare implementation (default / default_src_prepare), so overriding the phase without calling `default` drops PATCHES on the floor with no error.
fix_pattern: Replace the bare eapply_user call inside the custom src_prepare with `default`, which runs eapply "${PATCHES[@]}" followed by eapply_user.
rule: Whenever an ebuild defines its own src_prepare and also sets PATCHES, the phase must call `default` (or explicitly eapply "${PATCHES[@]}"); calling only eapply_user silently skips all PATCHES.

problem: Weekly GCC 17 snapshot needed adding without hand-editing patch logic every time.
root_cause: Snapshot-driven toolchain packages churn weekly; anything version-specific hard-coded in the ebuild makes each bump error-prone.
fix_pattern: Keep the ebuild fully data-driven via toolchain.eclass variables (PATCH_GCC_VER, PATCH_VER, MUSL_GCC_VER, MUSL_VER) so a snapshot bump is a pure file copy plus Manifest entry, and maintain an upstreamed_patches array in src_prepare that rm's patchset patches already merged upstream before toolchain_src_prepare runs.
rule: For snapshot/rolling packages, factor every version-specific value into eclass variables and keep an explicit list that deletes patches which upstream has absorbed, so each bump is a copy with zero logic edits.
snippet:
local p upstreamed_patches=(
	# add them here
)
for p in "${upstreamed_patches[@]}"; do
	rm -v "${WORKDIR}/patch/${p}" || die
done

problem: src_configure hit portage sandbox violations when USE=julia was enabled (Julia runtime probing /proc/self/fd during cmake checks), aborting the build.
root_cause: Embedded language runtimes executed during configure introspect their own process via /proc/self/*, which the sandbox treats as write/predict violations even though they are harmless.
fix_pattern: Whitelist the specific pseudo-files with addpredict in the phase where the tool runs, scoped inside the USE conditional, with a bug reference comment per path.
rule: For harmless sandbox violations caused by a build-time tool touching /proc/self/* or similar pseudo-files, addpredict the exact path in the affected phase (scoped to the triggering USE flag) rather than disabling the sandbox.
snippet:
if use julia; then
	addpredict /proc/self/mem # bug 602894
	addpredict /proc/self/fd # bug 909435
fi

problem: nvc failed at build/runtime because configure's AC_CHECK_FUNCS detected fpurge and the code took the fpurge path, which misbehaves on glibc systems (bug 979033); separately USE=debug produced a broken build.
root_cause: autoconf function detection can succeed for a function whose semantics differ or are unsuitable on the target libc; the portable-on-glibc alternative __fpurge was also checked, so removing fpurge from the check list forces the working code path.
fix_pattern: Patch configure.ac to drop the offending function from AC_CHECK_FUNCS (the ebuild inherits autotools, so eautoreconf regenerates configure), add the patch to PATCHES, revbump to -r1, and drop the broken USE=debug / $(use_enable debug) plumbing. Also migrated bash-completion-r1 to the newer shell-completion eclass in the same revbump.
rule: When autoconf detects a libc function whose implementation is broken or wrong for the target, patch it out of AC_CHECK_FUNCS so the fallback path is used, rather than working around it in C flags or environment.
snippet:
-AC_CHECK_FUNCS([fpurge __fpurge strchrnul strndup gettid popen])
+AC_CHECK_FUNCS([__fpurge strchrnul strndup gettid popen])

problem: clazy-standalone crashed with "fatal error: 'stddef.h' file not found" unless installed in the same directory as the clang binary, docs under /usr/share/doc were compressed although a tool reads them, and tests ran against installed rather than built binaries.
root_cause: clang-based standalone tools locate their resource/include directories relative to their own binary path, so installing them into a generic bin dir breaks header lookup; portage compresses docdir contents by default; test suites often pick up the system-installed copy via PATH.
fix_pattern: Patch CMake to install (and stage in BUILD_DIR) the standalone tool into $(llvm-config --bindir); in src_test recreate the expected directory layout under BUILD_DIR with mkdir/symlinks and prepend BUILD_DIR bin/lib to PATH and LD_LIBRARY_PATH so tests exercise the built copy (bug 811723); in src_install use docompress -x /usr/share/doc/${PF} for files consumed programmatically (bug 965513).
rule: Tools built as clang/LLVM plugins or standalones must be installed into the LLVM bindir so relative resource lookup works, and test phases should force PATH/LD_LIBRARY_PATH at the build tree so the system copy is never tested by accident.

problem: pkgcheck BadDescription warning on the ebuild.
root_cause: DESCRIPTION ended with a trailing period, which Gentoo QA policy (and pkgcheck's BadDescription check) forbids, along with descriptions that start with the package name or exceed length limits.
fix_pattern: Strip the trailing period from DESCRIPTION; no revbump needed since metadata-only changes do not affect installed files.
rule: Keep DESCRIPTION a short sentence fragment without a trailing period and without repeating the package name; run pkgcheck scan to catch this class of issue automatically.
snippet:
-DESCRIPTION="Modern, secure, portable, multiprotocol bootloader and boot manager."
+DESCRIPTION="Modern, secure, portable, multiprotocol bootloader and boot manager"

problem: Same BadDescription fix as a2a4a1e1 (trailing period stripped from DESCRIPTION, no revbump).
Same freethreading enablement pattern as f8096bfe, applied to both the meta -0 ebuild (python-r1) and the versioned distutils-r1 ebuild.

Enabling freethreading targets while the ebuild still carried pypy3_11 support meant keeping a python_gen_cond_dep dependency wrapper and a pypy-only EPYTEST_DESELECT block for tests upstream refuses to fix.
root_cause: An implementation kept in PYTHON_COMPAT only for legacy reasons forces conditional dependency syntax and per-implementation test deselections throughout the ebuild.
fix_pattern: Drop the problematic implementation from PYTHON_COMPAT in the same commit that adds new targets, then collapse $(python_gen_cond_dep '...' 'python*') back to a plain dependency with [${PYTHON_USEDEP}] and delete the case ${EPYTHON} deselect block.
rule: When dropping a Python implementation from PYTHON_COMPAT, sweep the ebuild for python_gen_cond_dep wrappers and EPYTHON-conditional test logic that existed only for it and simplify them away.

problem: Same freethreading enablement pattern as f8096bfe.
Same freethreading enablement pattern as f8096bfe; ebuild uses a PYTHON_TESTED array to distinguish implementations whose tests actually run.
Same freethreading enablement pattern as f8096bfe (done after its dependency trio).
Same freethreading enablement pattern as f8096bfe (done after deps sniffio/outcome).
Same freethreading enablement pattern as f8096bfe.
Same freethreading enablement pattern as f8096bfe.
Same freethreading enablement pattern as f8096bfe, notable only in that it is a C extension (DISTUTILS_EXT=1) and still just needed the PYTHON_COMPAT addition.
Same freethreading enablement pattern as f8096bfe.
Same freethreading enablement pattern as f8096bfe.
Same freethreading enablement pattern as f8096bfe.
Free-threaded CPython targets (python3_14t/15t) were in PYTHON_COMPAT but excluded from PYTHON_TESTED, so pytest built for them without ever running its own test suite on them.
root_cause: Staged-enablement idiom: new interpreter targets are first added to PYTHON_COMPAT only (install allowed, tests skipped via a PYTHON_TESTED helper array); once the ecosystem matures they must be promoted into PYTHON_TESTED, which is easy to forget.
fix_pattern: Move the freethreaded brace-expansion from the PYTHON_COMPAT-only tail into the PYTHON_TESTED array; PYTHON_COMPAT stays defined as "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}" plus any untested extras. Test-skipping logic keyed on PYTHON_TESTED then automatically covers the new targets.
rule: When an ebuild maintains a PYTHON_TESTED subset of PYTHON_COMPAT, adding an interpreter to COMPAT is only step one - promote it into PYTHON_TESTED once its deps/tests work, or the package is shipped untested on that target.

problem: Pure-Python package did not declare support for free-threaded (no-GIL) CPython builds, blocking reverse deps from enabling python3_14t/15t.
root_cause: Free-threaded CPython uses separate PYTHON_TARGETS (python3_14t, python3_15t) in Gentoo; every package in a dependency chain must list them in PYTHON_COMPAT before a leaf consumer can, so enablement proceeds bottom-up through dependency trees (this batch: charset-normalizer -> requests -> httpbin/flask stack -> jsonschema family).
fix_pattern: One-line PYTHON_COMPAT edit adding the t-suffixed targets, done without a revbump because it only widens available targets. Representative of ~17 identical commits in this wave; several simultaneously dropped python3_11/pypy3_11 in the same line.
rule: To enable free-threaded CPython for a pure-Python package, append python3_{14,15}t to PYTHON_COMPAT - but only after every dep in its (R|B)DEPEND graph already has them, working leaf-first up the dependency tree; no revbump needed.

problem: C-extension package (DISTUTILS_EXT=1) lacked free-threaded CPython targets; the same edit also carried python3_11 and pypy3_11 removal.
root_cause: For native extensions the t-targets can only be added once upstream (here Cython-generated code in lxml 6.x) actually supports the free-threaded ABI; the target list edit is the same one-liner as for pure Python, but the precondition is upstream support, not just dep readiness. PyPy was dropped tree-wide in the same wave, so both changes ride one line.
fix_pattern: Single PYTHON_COMPAT rewrite combining removal of EOL/dropped implementations with addition of freethreaded ones: python3_{11..15} pypy3_11 -> python3_{12..15} python3_{14,15}t. No revbump: target-list widening plus dropping targets nothing depends on anymore.
rule: For DISTUTILS_EXT=1 packages, verify upstream declares free-threading support (cp3XXt wheels / Py_mod_gil) before adding t targets; batch impl drops and additions into one PYTHON_COMPAT edit.

problem: One-line PYTHON_COMPAT addition of python3_{14,15}t (mass freethreading wave; see 9ffd1ffb for the reusable pattern).

One-line PYTHON_COMPAT addition of python3_{14,15}t (mass freethreading wave; see 9ffd1ffb).
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT addition of python3_{14,15}t (mass freethreading wave; see 9ffd1ffb).

One-line PYTHON_COMPAT edit adding python3_{14,15}t and dropping python3_11/pypy3_11 (mass freethreading wave; see 9ffd1ffb).
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT edit adding python3_{14,15}t and dropping python3_11/pypy3_11 (mass freethreading wave; see 9ffd1ffb).
