One-line PYTHON_COMPAT edit adding python3_{14,15}t and dropping python3_11/pypy3_11 (mass freethreading wave; see 9ffd1ffb).
After dropping python3_11 from PYTHON_COMPAT, a python_gen_cond_dep guard for a stdlib-backport dep (typing-extensions) still referenced the removed version range 3.{11..12}.
root_cause: Version-conditional deps like typing-extensions exist only for older interpreters lacking a stdlib feature; when PYTHON_COMPAT sheds an old version, every python_gen_cond_dep range in the ebuild must be re-checked and shrunk, else the ebuild carries stale (or, if the range empties, dead) conditions.
fix_pattern: Narrow the version list argument of python_gen_cond_dep in the same commit that edits PYTHON_COMPAT: 3.{11..12} -> 3.12. If the range would become empty, delete the whole conditional block.
rule: Whenever you remove an interpreter version from PYTHON_COMPAT, grep the ebuild for python_gen_cond_dep and shrink or delete every version range that mentioned it.

problem: One-line PYTHON_COMPAT edit adding python3_{14,15}t and dropping python3_11/pypy3_11 (mass freethreading wave; see 9ffd1ffb).
Ebuild fetched a raw GitLab archive tarball, requiring MY_P/S mangling and an exported SETUPTOOLS_SCM_PRETEND_VERSION because VCS archives lack setuptools-scm version metadata.
root_cause: setuptools-scm derives the version from git metadata that forge-generated tarballs do not contain; the PyPI sdist instead ships PKG-INFO with the version baked in, so building from the sdist removes the need for the pretend-version hack and all the custom SRC_URI/S plumbing.
fix_pattern: Switch to the pypi eclass: drop SRC_URI, S, MY_P, and the SETUPTOOLS_SCM_PRETEND_VERSION export; set PYPI_NO_NORMALIZE=1 because the PyPI project name keeps its hyphen (uri-template) rather than the PEP 503 normalized underscore form. Manifest DIST entry changes from the .tar.bz2 forge archive to the PyPI .tar.gz.
rule: When a setuptools-scm package forces you to export SETUPTOOLS_SCM_PRETEND_VERSION, prefer switching SRC_URI to the PyPI sdist via the pypi eclass (with PYPI_NO_NORMALIZE=1 if the project name is not PEP 503-normalized) instead of keeping the forge tarball and the pretend-version workaround.
snippet:
DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=setuptools
+PYPI_NO_NORMALIZE=1
 PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} python3_{14,15}t )
-inherit distutils-r1
+inherit distutils-r1 pypi
-SRC_URI="https://gitlab.linss.com/.../${MY_P}.tar.bz2"
-S=${WORKDIR}/${MY_P}
-export SETUPTOOLS_SCM_PRETEND_VERSION=${PV}

problem: One-line PYTHON_COMPAT edit adding python3_{14,15}t and dropping python3_11/pypy3_11 (mass freethreading wave; see 9ffd1ffb).

A dependency (dev-python/regex) was wrapped in python_gen_cond_dep '...' 'python*' solely because regex does not support PyPy; once pypy3_11 was dropped from PYTHON_COMPAT the guard became a no-op that obscured the real dependency.
root_cause: The 'python*' impl-pattern in python_gen_cond_dep exists only to exclude non-CPython implementations; when PYTHON_COMPAT contains only CPython targets the condition is always true and the wrapper is dead code.
fix_pattern: Collapse the python_gen_cond_dep 'python*' wrapper into a plain [${PYTHON_USEDEP}] dependency in the same commit that removes pypy from PYTHON_COMPAT.
rule: After removing the last non-CPython implementation from PYTHON_COMPAT, grep the ebuild for python_gen_cond_dep with a 'python*' pattern and unwrap those deps to plain PYTHON_USEDEP form.

problem: One-line PYTHON_COMPAT edit adding python3_{14,15}t and dropping python3_11/pypy3_11 (mass freethreading wave; see 9ffd1ffb).

After PyPy was dropped from PYTHON_COMPAT, the ebuild still carried a python_gen_cond_dep 'python*' wrapper around the cython BDEPEND and an [[ ${EPYTHON} != python* ]] runtime check in python_compile that force-disabled the C extension for PyPy.
root_cause: PyPy-specific handling lives in two places - conditional deps (impl-pattern 'python*') and phase-function EPYTHON checks - and both silently become dead code when the implementation is removed from PYTHON_COMPAT.
fix_pattern: Unwrap the conditional dep to a plain [${PYTHON_USEDEP}] atom and reduce the compile-phase condition to the remaining USE check only; delete the explanatory pypy comment.
rule: Removing an implementation from PYTHON_COMPAT is a two-site cleanup: grep both dependency strings (python_gen_cond_dep patterns) and phase functions (EPYTHON / python_is_python3-style tests) for now-dead impl branches.
snippet:
-	if ! use native-extensions || [[ ${EPYTHON} != python* ]]; then
+	if ! use native-extensions; then
 		local -x FROZENLIST_NO_EXTENSIONS=1

problem: Test suite started failing on a new CPython patch release (bug 976743): a test that enumerates all dunder attributes of a class to verify FrozenList API parity hit new interpreter-added dunders (__annotations_cache__, __annotate_func__) that were not in its SKIP_METHODS allowlist.
root_cause: CPython minor/patch releases can add new implicit class attributes (here from PEP 649/749 deferred annotations); tests that introspect and compare full attribute sets are inherently fragile against interpreter updates, independent of any package code change.
fix_pattern: Backport the exact upstream fix as a files/ patch (two upstream commits squashed, with the upstream PR URL and bug URL recorded in the patch header and a PATCHES comment) that adds the new dunders to the test's SKIP_METHODS set - rather than deselecting the test or restricting the Python version.
rule: When tests break only on a new Python patch/minor release due to newly introduced object attributes, look for an upstream commit extending the test's skip/allow list and carry it as a FILESDIR patch with the upstream PR referenced, instead of EPYTEST_DESELECT.

problem: One-line PYTHON_COMPAT addition of python3_{14,15}t (mass freethreading wave; see 9ffd1ffb).
Package not installable for free-threaded (nogil) CPython although the code works there.
root_cause: Free-threaded targets (python3_14t/python3_15t) are opt-in per package in PYTHON_COMPAT; they are never enabled implicitly by the plain python3_XX entries.
fix_pattern: Append the t-suffixed targets to PYTHON_COMPAT. Adding implementations requires no revbump. The same tree-wide sweep also dropped stale python3_11/pypy3_11 entries in the same line edit, also without revbump.
rule: When new free-threaded CPython targets exist and the package's tests pass under them, append python3_{14,15}t to PYTHON_COMPAT in place, no revbump needed; use the touch to drop implementations being removed tree-wide.

problem: Same mechanical freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition as 9c3c823e (hpack).
Freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition; also dropped python3_11 and pypy3_11 in the same edit without revbump (pattern covered by 9c3c823e).
Same mechanical freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition as 9c3c823e (hpack).
DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit was set for a package whose pyproject.toml declares the flit_core.buildapi backend.
root_cause: distutils-r1 distinguishes the full flit tool from the minimal flit-core backend; using the 'flit' value pulls a heavier build dependency than the package actually needs and mismatches the declared PEP 517 backend.
fix_pattern: Change DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit to flit-core (done opportunistically while adding freethreading targets).
rule: Set DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 to the exact backend named in pyproject.toml's build-backend field (flit_core.buildapi -> flit-core), not a superset tool.

problem: Same mechanical freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition as 9c3c823e (hpack).
Same freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition as 9c3c823e, on a C-extension package (DISTUTILS_EXT=1) that needed no extra changes.
Same mechanical freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition as 9c3c823e (hpack).
Same mechanical freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition as 9c3c823e (hpack).
Same mechanical freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition as 9c3c823e (hpack).
Same mechanical freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition as 9c3c823e (hpack), using the python3_{14..15}t range spelling.
Freethreading enabled on a maturin/PyO3 Rust extension (DISTUTILS_EXT=1) with the same one-line PYTHON_COMPAT change; modern PyO3 extensions can support nogil with no further ebuild changes.

Freethreading targets should be installable, but the test suite is only validated on GIL builds.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT gates which implementations the package installs for, while a maintainer-defined PYTHON_TESTED array gates which implementations run the test suite; the two sets can legitimately differ.
fix_pattern: Append python3_{14..15}t only to PYTHON_COMPAT, leaving PYTHON_TESTED unchanged, so t-builds install but skip tests. Contrast poetry-core (28dcb0b4) which added them to PYTHON_TESTED because its tests do pass.
rule: Keep a PYTHON_TESTED subset array when some implementations must install but cannot run the test suite; derive PYTHON_COMPAT from it and append the untested implementations there only.

problem: Same mechanical freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition as 9c3c823e (hpack).

Patches shared by freecad-1.1.1 and freecad-9999 were named ${PN}-9999-*.patch, so future live-ebuild maintenance could change patch content underneath a released (potentially stable) ebuild (bug 979123).
root_cause: files/ content is shared across all versions of a package; naming shared patches after the live ebuild invites editing them for 9999 while releases silently pick up the modified file.
fix_pattern: git mv the patches to ${PN}-1.1.1-* (the oldest release using them); the release ebuild references them via ${P}-*, and the 9999 ebuild references the release-versioned filenames explicitly.
rule: Name files/ patches after the oldest release version that applies them, never after 9999; the live ebuild should reference the release-named files, so 9999 churn forces a new filename instead of mutating a release's inputs.

problem: Routine 4.6.7 bump (bug 979128), but the ebuild carries two non-obvious idioms worth reusing: upstream publishes a signed checksum manifest instead of a detached .sig, and since 4.6.5 CMake auto-detects the Lua version, ignoring LUA_FIND_VERSIONS.
root_cause: verify-sig supports signed-checksums files via verify-sig_verify_signed_checksums; wireshark's cmake/modules/FindLua.cmake hardcodes a version search list, which breaks lua-single slot pinning when multiple Lua versions are installed.
fix_pattern: SRC_URI fetches SIGNATURES-${PV}.txt renamed to ${P}-signatures.txt and src_unpack runs verify-sig_verify_signed_checksums with openssl-dgst; src_prepare seds FindLua.cmake to replace the search list with the eclass-selected ${ELUA}.
rule: When a build system auto-detects an interpreter version instead of honoring configure flags, sed/patch its version search list to the eclass-selected implementation (ELUA, EPYTHON) in src_prepare; and when upstream signs a checksum manifest rather than the tarball, use verify-sig_verify_signed_checksums in src_unpack.

problem: A python_gen_cond_dep guard restricting the cffi dependency to 'python*' implementations became a no-op wrapper after PyPy was dropped from PYTHON_COMPAT.
root_cause: The conditional existed only to exclude pypy3; once PYTHON_COMPAT contains only CPython (the python* glob also matches python3_14t/15t), the guard matches every implementation and only adds noise.
fix_pattern: Flatten $(python_gen_cond_dep '...' 'python*') to the plain dependency atom while enabling freethreading targets.
rule: Whenever PYTHON_COMPAT changes, re-audit python_gen_cond_dep guards and remove any whose implementation pattern now matches every listed implementation.

problem: Same mechanical freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition as 9c3c823e (hpack).
Enabling freethreading made two poetry-core tests fail: they assert wheel/ABI tags (cp314) that differ on free-threaded builds (cp314t).
root_cause: Upstream tests hardcode non-t wheel tag expectations and were not adjusted for freethreading builds.
fix_pattern: Add t-targets to PYTHON_TESTED (so the suite still runs on them) and conditionally deselect only the tag-sensitive tests using a case on ${EPYTHON} with EPYTEST_DESELECT.
rule: When enabling freethreading targets, run the test suite under the t interpreter and deselect tests that encode ABI/wheel tags via a case ${EPYTHON} in python*t) guard rather than skipping the whole suite.
snippet:
local EPYTEST_DESELECT=()
case ${EPYTHON} in
	python*t)
		EPYTEST_DESELECT+=(
			# tests not adjusted for freethreading tags
			tests/masonry/builders/test_complete.py::test_wheel_c_extension
			tests/masonry/builders/test_wheel.py::test_tag
		)
		;;
esac

problem: Freethreading enablement plus flattening a now-redundant python_gen_cond_dep 'python*' guard on the dev-python/regex test dep (same idiom as b72dd75f cryptography).
Same mechanical freethreading PYTHON_COMPAT addition as 9c3c823e (hpack).
Package did not offer free-threaded (no-GIL) CPython targets even though it is pure Python and its test suite passes on 3.14t/3.15t.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT only listed GIL builds; free-threaded CPython is a separate implementation target (python3_14t, python3_15t) that must be explicitly opted into per package after verifying test-suite compatibility.
fix_pattern: Edit PYTHON_COMPAT in place (no revbump needed for adding implementations): add python3_{14,15}t; the same sweep drops EOL python3_11 and pypy3_11. Representative of a 13-commit batch in this set.
rule: When a distutils-r1 package's tests pass under free-threaded CPython, add the t-suffixed targets to PYTHON_COMPAT in place (PYTHON_COMPAT changes need no revbump), and prune EOL implementations in the same edit.

problem: asgiref could not be enabled for free-threaded CPython because its Local class leaked contextvar data into unrelated threads on 3.14t.
root_cause: Python 3.14 added sys.flags.thread_inherit_context (default-on for free-threaded builds): new threads inherit the spawning thread's context, so contextvar-backed thread-local storage becomes visible in child threads, breaking documented semantics.
fix_pattern: Backport the not-yet-released upstream fix (django/asgiref PR #564) as a FILESDIR patch, add it in a new -r1 revision (source change => revbump), enable python3_{14..15}t, and cite the upstream PR URL in a comment above PATCHES so the patch can be dropped on next bump.
rule: When a new Python target fails tests, backport the merged-but-unreleased upstream fix as a FILESDIR patch with the PR URL commented above PATCHES, and revbump; enabling the target and the patch go in the same revision.

problem: Enabling free-threaded targets without parallel test execution would make an already slow, thread-heavy test suite slower and less representative.
root_cause: distutils-r1 runs pytest serially unless EPYTEST_XDIST is set; free-threading enablement is a good moment to opt into xdist for suites known to be parallel-safe.
fix_pattern: Set EPYTEST_XDIST=1 next to EPYTEST_PLUGINS before distutils_enable_tests pytest; the eclass then adds pytest-xdist to test deps and passes -p xdist -n auto automatically.
rule: When a pytest suite is parallel-safe, set EPYTEST_XDIST=1 instead of hand-adding pytest-xdist deps and -n flags; the eclass wires everything.

problem: Ebuild declared DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit although upstream builds with the flit_core backend only, pulling the full dev-python/flit tool as an unnecessary build dep.
root_cause: flit (the CLI tool) and flit-core (the minimal PEP 517 backend) are distinct; pyproject.toml build-backend = "flit_core.buildapi" only needs flit-core.
fix_pattern: Change DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit to flit-core (done here while touching the ebuild for freethreading); check pyproject.toml [build-system] to pick the exact backend.
rule: Set DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 to the exact backend named in pyproject.toml's build-backend; use flit-core, not flit, when the backend is flit_core.buildapi.

problem: Routine free-threading enablement: PYTHON_COMPAT gains python3_{14,15}t, drops py3.11/pypy3_11; same pattern as 35650abb.
Routine free-threading enablement in PYTHON_COMPAT; same pattern as 35650abb.
Routine free-threading enablement in PYTHON_COMPAT; same pattern as 35650abb.
Routine free-threading enablement in PYTHON_COMPAT; same pattern as 35650abb.
Routine free-threading enablement in PYTHON_COMPAT; same pattern as 35650abb.
Routine free-threading enablement in PYTHON_COMPAT; same pattern as 35650abb.
Routine free-threading enablement in PYTHON_COMPAT; same pattern as 35650abb.
Routine free-threading enablement (python3_{14..15}t range spelling); same pattern as 35650abb.
Routine free-threading enablement in PYTHON_COMPAT; same pattern as 35650abb.
Build passed wrong paths for Qt6 host tools (moc/rcc) to CMake: qt6_get_bindir was hardcoded even though Qt moved these binaries from bindir to libexecdir (bug 969111).
root_cause: qmake-utils' qt6_get_bindir/qt6_get_libexecdir force the ebuild author to know which directory each Qt tool lives in, and the location changes between Qt versions; the paths are also EPREFIX-based, not BROOT-based, which is wrong for host tools when cross-compiling.
fix_pattern: Migrate inherit from qmake-utils to qt-utils and use qt_get_broot_binary <qtver> <tool>, which resolves the tool's real location (bindir vs libexecdir) and prefixes BROOT. Applied identically across a 12-commit sweep in this batch.
rule: When an ebuild needs the path to a Qt host tool (moc, rcc, uic, qmake, lrelease, qhelpgenerator, qdoc), inherit qt-utils and call qt_get_broot_binary N tool instead of concatenating qt6_get_bindir/qt6_get_libexecdir with a tool name.

problem: CMake QHG_LOCATION pointed at $(qt6_get_libexecdir)/qhelpgenerator via deprecated qmake-utils functions.
root_cause: Same as freecad 578718fb: per-tool directory guessing with EPREFIX-based qt6_get_* functions is fragile and cross-incorrect.
fix_pattern: Switch inherit qmake-utils to qt-utils and replace the concatenated path with $(qt_get_broot_binary 6 qhelpgenerator); the surrounding usev/usex wrapper stays untouched.
rule: Eclass migrations of this kind can be a pure two-line mechanical change: swap the inherit and rewrite each $(qt6_get_*dir)/tool call to $(qt_get_broot_binary 6 tool).

problem: Doxygen config sed injected the qhelpgenerator path via deprecated qt6_get_libexecdir; ebuild also sat on EAPI 8.
root_cause: Deprecated qmake-utils path helpers; EAPI 9 available.
fix_pattern: Replace the sed replacement text with $(qt_get_broot_binary 6 qhelpgenerator) and bump EAPI 8->9 in place on the existing -r2 without a new revision, because both are build-time-only changes that do not alter installed files.
rule: EAPI bumps and build-tool path fixes that cannot change the installed image may be committed in place without a revbump.

problem: Ebuild hand-built a Qt tool path as $(qt6_get_libdir)/qt6/bin/qdoc, encoding Qt's internal directory layout directly in the ebuild.
root_cause: No eclass helper existed for locating arbitrary Qt host binaries, so authors reconstructed paths from libdir; any upstream layout change silently breaks the call.
fix_pattern: Replace the entire hand-assembled path with $(qt_get_broot_binary 6 qdoc) and bump EAPI 8->9 in place.
rule: Never hand-concatenate Qt internal directory layout (libdir/qt6/bin/...) in ebuilds; always resolve host tools through the eclass helper so layout changes are absorbed centrally.

problem: Qt6 bindir was prepended to PATH using the implicit-EPREFIX qt6_get_bindir, and an obsolete QTLIB export lingered.
root_cause: Host tools invoked during build live under BROOT, not EPREFIX; the new-style qt_get_bindir returns an unprefixed path so the caller must (and can) choose the correct prefix explicitly.
fix_pattern: Prepend ${BROOT}$(qt_get_bindir 6) to PATH for tool lookup, use qt_get_broot_binary for single tools like moc, and delete the dead QTLIB export while touching the block.
rule: New-style qt-utils dir functions return unprefixed paths: prefix BROOT yourself for build-host tool lookup (and EPREFIX for install locations); use eclass-switch commits to drop obsolete exported variables.
snippet:
-		export PATH="$(qt6_get_bindir):${PATH}"
-		export QTLIB="$(qt6_get_libdir):${QTLIB}"
-		export MOC="$(qt6_get_libexecdir)/moc"
+		export PATH="${BROOT}$(qt_get_bindir 6):${PATH}"
+		export MOC="$(qt_get_broot_binary 6 moc)"

problem: Install-location dirs (mkspecs, plugindir) were fetched via deprecated version-suffixed qt6_get_* functions.
root_cause: qt-utils replaces the per-version function family with version-as-argument functions returning unprefixed paths.
fix_pattern: Rename qt6_get_mkspecsdir/qt6_get_plugindir to qt_get_mkspecsdir 6 / qt_get_plugindir 6, keeping the explicit ${EPREFIX} prefix because these are target install locations, unlike BROOT-prefixed host tools.
rule: In the qt-utils migration, install-destination dirs keep ${EPREFIX} while build-host tool paths take BROOT; do not blindly convert every qt6_get_* call to qt_get_broot_binary.

problem: Mechanical swap of deprecated $(qt6_get_bindir)/qmake for $(qt_get_broot_binary 6 qmake); same lesson as 578718fb.
Mechanical qhelpgenerator path swap to qt_get_broot_binary; same lesson as 578718fb.
Mechanical qhelpgenerator path swap to qt_get_broot_binary in release and 9999 ebuilds; same lesson as 578718fb.
Mechanical qhelpgenerator path swap inside a Doxyfile sed; same lesson as 578718fb.
Mechanical swap of five exported autoconf tool variables (MOC/UIC/RCC/LRELEASE/QHELPGENERATOR) to qt_get_broot_binary; same lesson as 578718fb.
Mechanical swap of usex-guarded LRELEASE/MOC/RCC/UIC CMake args to qt_get_broot_binary across three ebuilds; same lesson as 578718fb.
Ebuild used old versioned qt-utils helper qt6_get_mkspecsdir after the eclass introduced new-style version-agnostic functions.
root_cause: qt-utils.eclass replaced per-major-version helpers (qt5_get_*/qt6_get_*) with unified qt_get_* functions taking the Qt major version as an argument.
fix_pattern: Replace $(qt6_get_mkspecsdir) with $(qt_get_mkspecsdir 6) (same for plugindir/libdir etc.); no revbump needed since the installed result is identical.
rule: When an eclass deprecates versioned helper functions in favor of a parameterized new-style API, mechanically rewrite calls to the new form (function name + version argument) during routine maintenance.

problem: Same qt-utils migration as qtkeychain: qt6_get_plugindir/qt6_get_mkspecsdir rewritten to qt_get_plugindir 6 / qt_get_mkspecsdir 6.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT extension python3_{12..14} -> python3_{12..15} in release and 9999 ebuilds (plus copyright year touch-up).
Build failed with USE=nvidia (bug 979116): the intended CMake cache setting BUILD_NVIDIA_NVML=no never took effect.
root_cause: Typo in mycmakeargs: the entry was written as '-BUILD_NVIDIA_NVML=no' (missing the D of the -D flag), so CMake did not receive the cache definition and NVML support was misdetected, breaking the nvidia build.
fix_pattern: Fix the flag to '-DBUILD_NVIDIA_NVML=no'. Every literal cache assignment in mycmakeargs must start with -D; a malformed flag is swallowed without an obvious error at configure time.
rule: When a CMake option mysteriously has no effect, check the mycmakeargs entry starts with '-D'; lint all mycmakeargs literals to match ^-D (or an eclass helper like $(cmake_use_find_package ...)/$(usex ...)).

problem: Major bump to kea 3.2.0 (meson build) needed sandbox-safe test running and control of installed-file permissions under meson.
root_cause: The full meson test suite includes suites needing network interfaces, running MySQL/PgSQL, Kerberos, and suites broken on 32-bit; meson < 1.9 also offers no easy permission control at install time.
fix_pattern: Enumerate suites via 'meson test --list' into an associative array, unset a per-USE/per-arch SKIP_TESTS list (using tc-get-ptr-size for 32-bit), then run 'meson_src_test ${!TEST_SUITES[@]}'. Pass --install-umask=0o023 in emesonargs, then re-tighten sensitive paths with fperms/chmod in src_install; use eapi9-ver's ver_replacing in pkg_postinst for upgrade-only warnings.
rule: For meson packages with partially sandbox-hostile tests, whitelist suites dynamically from 'meson test --list' minus a skip array instead of RESTRICTing all tests; use --install-umask plus fperms to normalize meson-installed permissions.
snippet:
local -A TEST_SUITES
while IFS="/: " read -a words ; do
  [[ "${words[0]}" != shell-tests ]] && TEST_SUITES["${words[-1]}"]=1
done < <(meson test --list || die)
for SKIP in ${SKIP_TESTS[@]}; do unset TEST_SUITES["${SKIP}"]; done
meson_src_test ${!TEST_SUITES[@]}

problem: evolution-ews 3.60.2 declared >=evolution-data-server-3.54.3 but actually requires the lockstep 3.60.2 e-d-s, so users with older e-d-s got breakage (bug 978822).
root_cause: GNOME components released in lockstep (evolution / e-d-s / -ews) use each other's new private API each cycle; the copied-forward minimum dep understated the real requirement.
fix_pattern: Raise the dependency to the matching lockstep version (>=gnome-extra/evolution-data-server-3.60.2:0=) and rename the ebuild to -r1 so the dependency fix propagates to already-installed users.
rule: When bumping one member of a lockstep-released suite, bump its intra-suite minimum deps to the same version, and revbump if you fix the dep after the ebuild shipped.

problem: Gem failed against bigdecimal-4 in tree because its gemspec pinned 'bigdecimal ~> 3.1' (bug 976106).
root_cause: Ruby gemspec pessimistic version constraint (~>) is stricter than the code's real compatibility; Gentoo moved to a newer dep major version before upstream relaxed the pin.
fix_pattern: Backport the upstream PR that widens the gemspec constraint ('>= 3.1', '< 5') as a files/ patch, add it to PATCHES, and revbump to -r1 so installed copies get the regenerated gemspec.
rule: When a Ruby package breaks only because of an over-strict gemspec dependency pin, patch (or sed) the gemspec constraint referencing the upstream PR and revbump, rather than holding back the dependency.

problem: Test suite needed aiohttp but it was missing from the test dependencies, causing test failures/collection errors (bugs 918003, 976572).
root_cause: Upstream test suite grew an import that was never mirrored into the ebuild's BDEPEND test? group.
fix_pattern: Add dev-python/aiohttp[${PYTHON_USEDEP}] inside BDEPEND='test? ( ... )' across all live ebuild versions in one commit; no revbump needed since only test-time behavior changes.
rule: When tests error on a missing import, add the module to the test? ( ) BDEPEND group in every affected ebuild version; test-dep-only changes do not need a revbump.

problem: Pre-release 2.9.94 promoted to final 3.0.0 as a pure 'git mv' rename (100% similarity) plus Manifest update; no ebuild content change needed.
Build failed on riscv (bug 979046): configure aborted with 'Unrecognized CPU architecture' when probing cacheline size.
root_cause: Upstream autoconf macro TORRENT_CHECK_CACHELINE in scripts/common.m4 hard-codes a case list of $host_cpu values and calls AC_MSG_FAILURE for unknown arches; riscv32/riscv64 were missing.
fix_pattern: Backport the merged upstream PR as files/${P}-fix_riscv.patch adding a riscv32*|riscv64* case defining LT_SMP_CACHE_BYTES 64, append to PATCHES with the bug number as a comment; patching .m4 requires the ebuild to run eautoreconf (it already did).
rule: When configure fails on a new arch due to an exhaustive case statement, backport the upstream arch-case patch into files/ and remember that .m4/configure.ac patches only take effect with eautoreconf.

problem: Ebuild declared DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit, pulling the full flit tool as a build dep when only the build backend is needed; fixed while enabling py3.15.
root_cause: flit (and poetry) ship their PEP517 backend in a separate minimal -core package; declaring the full tool bloats BDEPEND and can block new-python enablement on the heavy package's own compatibility.
fix_pattern: Set DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit-core (analogously poetry-core, hatchling instead of hatch) and extend PYTHON_COMPAT in the same maintenance commit.
rule: In distutils-r1 ebuilds always prefer the -core PEP517 backend value (flit-core/poetry-core) unless the build genuinely needs the full tool.

problem: Same -core backend idiom as weasyprint: DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 poetry -> poetry-core while enabling py3.15 and raising the floor to python3_12.
python_check_deps() could return a misleading exit status when the last conditional block (use man) was skipped; also tab/space whitespace inconsistency.
root_cause: python_check_deps' return value is whatever the last executed command returns; ending the function with a USE-conditional 'if' makes the result depend on flag state instead of an explicit verdict.
fix_pattern: Terminate python_check_deps with an explicit 'return 0' after all 'python_has_version ... || return 1' probes, so success is stated deliberately.
rule: Always end python_check_deps (and similar boolean shell hooks) with an explicit return 0; rely on '|| return 1' per probe rather than the incidental status of the last statement.
snippet:
python_check_deps() {
	...
	if use man; then
		python_has_version "dev-python/argparse-manpage[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]" || return 1
	fi
+
+	return 0
}

problem: dbus-based tests started failing after a revbump merely because '-r1' made ${PF} longer: the portage tmpdir path exceeded the unix socket path length limit (~100 bytes) used by dbus test sockets.
root_cause: AF_UNIX socket paths have a hard kernel length limit (108 bytes, dbus effectively ~99); portage's deeply nested ${T}/${WORKDIR} paths plus package name can cross it, so test failures appear or vanish with package-name length.
fix_pattern: In src_test export a short TMPDIR created with mktemp under /tmp, run the suite via 'nonfatal meson_src_test', capture $?, remove the tmpdir, then die on failure - preserving cleanup while keeping fatal semantics.
rule: When a test suite creates unix sockets and fails with path/bind errors (or breaks only after a rename/revbump), point TMPDIR at a short /tmp path for src_test.
snippet:
src_test() {
	local -x TMPDIR="$(mktemp -d --tmpdir=/tmp ${PF}-XXX || die)"
	MAKEOPTS="-j1" nonfatal meson_src_test
	local ret="${?}"
	rm -r "${TMPDIR}" || die
	[[ "${ret}" != 0 ]] && die "tests failed"
}

problem: upower installed its test suite into the image because upstream's new installed-tests meson option defaults on.
root_cause: Upstream made installation of tests optional (installed_tests meson option); leaving it enabled ships test binaries/data users don't need and adds hidden deps.
fix_pattern: Add -Dinstalled_tests=false to emesonargs; no revbump discussion here, just stop installing them. Maintainers deliberately chose not to add a USE flag for installed tests to avoid complexity.
rule: When bumping a meson package, diff meson_options.txt for new options like installed_tests and disable installing tests rather than shipping them or wrapping them in a USE flag.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT extension to python3_15, one-line change.
Enabling py3.15 broke fonttools: code still called glob.glob1(), a private stdlib function removed in Python 3.15.
root_cause: Python 3.13 deprecated and 3.15 removed the undocumented glob.glob1(); packages using private stdlib APIs break on new CPython even when their public-API usage is fine.
fix_pattern: Write/backport an upstream patch replacing glob.glob1(dir, pat) with glob.glob(pat, root_dir=dir) (works back to 3.10), submit it upstream (PR referenced in PATCHES comment), carry it in files/, and create a new -r1 ebuild with python3_15 added instead of editing the existing one.
rule: When enabling a new Python target fails on a removed/private stdlib API, patch to the documented equivalent (checking the whatsnew deprecation list), upstream the fix, and add the new impl in a revbumped ebuild carrying the patch.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT extension python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15} (floor raised alongside new target).
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT extension to python3_15 with floor raised to 3.12.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT extension to python3_15 with floor raised to 3.12.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT extension to python3_15 with floor raised to 3.12.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT extension python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT extension python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}.
py3.15 enablement in openpyxl required modernizing test setup and pruning: pypy3_11 dropped, one test newly failing due to changed exceptions, and manual pytest plugin-autoload disabling replaced.
root_cause: Old ebuild manually exported PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 in python_test; the distutils-r1/epytest machinery now supports declaring EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests to run with no plugins autoloaded, and Python security fixes changed exception types breaking one xml test.
fix_pattern: Set 'EPYTEST_PLUGINS=()' above distutils_enable_tests pytest and remove the manual PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD export; adjust EPYTEST_DESELECT for the test broken by changed stdlib exceptions with a comment explaining why; drop dead <bugs-to> metadata for a vanished upstream tracker.
rule: Use EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( ... ) (empty for none) to control pytest plugin autoloading instead of exporting PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD manually, and document each EPYTEST_DESELECT entry with its cause.

problem: New openvswitch 3.3.9 ebuild shows the idioms needed for a C daemon with lockstep python bindings, static libs under LTO, and autoconf feature misdetection.
root_cause: openvswitch's python bindings (dev-python/ovs) are released in lockstep and must match exactly; the package installs static archives which break with LTO bytecode; configure auto-detects valgrind/unwind/flake8/dot causing nondeterministic builds.
fix_pattern: Pin bindings with '~dev-python/ovs-${PV}' via python_gen_cond_dep; use dot-a eclass: lto-guarantee-fat in src_configure and strip-lto-bytecode in src_install; force deterministic configure by exporting ovs_cv_flake8=no, ovs_cv_dot=no and ac_cv_header/ac_cv_lib cache variables from usex.
rule: For autoconf packages, export ac_cv_*/project _cv_ cache variables tied to USE flags to prevent automagic dependencies, pin same-source language bindings with the ~ operator, and pair lto-guarantee-fat/strip-lto-bytecode whenever static .a files are installed.

problem: Package needs build-time-only Python tooling (pyyaml/jinja2 for docs) that is only required under certain USE flags; naive DEPEND would force Python deps on everyone.
root_cause: Doc/extras generation scripts run under any Python at build time, so they belong in BDEPEND via python-any-r1, gated on the USE flags that need them.
fix_pattern: Use python-any-r1 with python_gen_any_dep inside USE-conditional BDEPEND, and make python_check_deps() short-circuit success when no relevant flag is set; call python-any-r1_pkg_setup only when needed and pass -DPython3_EXECUTABLE=${PYTHON} to CMake.
rule: When Python is only a conditional build-time tool, use python-any-r1 + python_gen_any_dep in USE-gated BDEPEND and have python_check_deps() 'use foo || return 0' so unaffected builds skip the check.
snippet:
BDEPEND="doc? ( $(python_gen_any_dep '
  dev-python/pyyaml[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]
  dev-python/jinja2[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]') )"
python_check_deps() {
  use doc || use extras || return 0
  python_has_version -b "dev-python/pyyaml[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]" &&
  python_has_version -b "dev-python/jinja2[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]"
}

problem: Go package bump: previous version shipped a 259 MB GOMODCACHE '-deps.tar.xz'; new version needed a maintainable dependency story.
root_cause: Full module-cache tarballs are huge; a 'go mod vendor' tree is far smaller (14 MB here) and go-module.eclass supports it transparently when a vendor/ dir is present.
fix_pattern: Publish a ${P}-vendor.tar.xz (vendored deps) as a release asset on a dist repo (gentoo-golang-dist style) and add it as a second SRC_URI entry; in src_compile 'export -n GOCACHE XDG_CACHE_HOME' then plain 'go build'.
rule: For Go packages, prefer a vendored-deps tarball hosted as a release asset over a GOMODCACHE deps tarball -- it is ~20x smaller and go-module.eclass picks vendor/ up automatically.

problem: A single upstream-broken CTest (mobileproviderstest) fails and blocks FEATURES=test builds.
root_cause: Test is broken upstream (KDE bug), not a packaging error; running it wastes everyone's time until upstream fixes it.
fix_pattern: cmake.eclass supports a CMAKE_SKIP_TESTS array of CTest name regexes; add the test name with a comment linking the upstream bug instead of RESTRICTing all tests.
rule: When one CMake/CTest test is known-broken, list it in CMAKE_SKIP_TESTS with a bug-URL comment rather than disabling the whole test suite.

problem: Flaky TestPageManager test disabled via CMAKE_SKIP_TESTS; same idiom as plasma-nm commit d0c2fda in this batch.
cmake.eclass emitted an 'unused CMake variable' QA warning: -DQTC_QT6_ENABLE_KDE was passed even when the CMake code never read it (only read when QT_MAJOR_VERSION=6 / plasma path active).
root_cause: cmake.eclass warns about -D variables the configure run never consumes; passing an option unconditionally when the consuming code path is USE-conditional triggers it. Also find_package(Git) silently picked up dev-vcs/git, an undeclared dep.
fix_pattern: Move the -D switch inside the same 'use plasma &&' conditional that enables the code path that reads it, and pass -DCMAKE_DISABLE_FIND_PACKAGE_Git=ON to stop the stray find_package.
rule: When cmake.eclass warns about an unused variable, only pass that -D option in the branch where CMake actually reads it; and neutralize automagic find_package(X) lookups with -DCMAKE_DISABLE_FIND_PACKAGE_X=ON.

problem: Prebuilt binary package only supported amd64 although upstream publishes an arm64 artifact too.
root_cause: SRC_URI pointed at a single arch-specific zip; adding another arch needs per-arch conditional fetch plus keyword masking on everything else.
fix_pattern: Split SRC_URI into 'amd64? ( url )  arm64? ( arm64-url )' blocks (each artifact gets its own Manifest entry) and set KEYWORDS="-* ~amd64 ~arm64" so unsupported arches are explicitly excluded for the binary package.
rule: For -bin packages with per-arch upstream artifacts, use USE(arch)-conditional SRC_URI entries and prefix KEYWORDS with -* to whitelist only the arches you actually ship.

problem: Ebuild manually set ECARGO_VENDOR and shuffled cargo_home around in src_unpack, duplicating what cargo.eclass already does.
root_cause: Older workaround kept after cargo.eclass grew proper defaults for crate tarballs from gentoo-crate-dist; the override became dead weight and a source of breakage.
fix_pattern: Delete the ECARGO_VENDOR assignment and the 'mv cargo_home' hack, rely on cargo_src_unpack defaults, and pass --offline to cargo_src_compile; keep RUST_MIN_VER updated with upstream's MSRV.
rule: Do not override cargo.eclass internals (ECARGO_VENDOR, cargo_home layout) unless the default demonstrably fails -- stale overrides break when the eclass evolves.
snippet:
-ECARGO_VENDOR="${WORKDIR}/vendor"
 src_unpack() { ... cargo_src_unpack
-	mv cargo_home ${P}/ || die
 }
-	cargo_src_compile
+	cargo_src_compile --offline

problem: Upstream moves older release tarballs from files/ to files/old/ after a while, breaking fetch for ebuilds that stay in tree; configure also probes htonll causing a -Wimplicit-function-declaration QA false positive.
root_cause: SRC_URI with a single path goes stale when upstream archives old releases; QA_CONFIG_IMPL_DECL_SKIP exists for known-benign configure implicit-decl probes.
fix_pattern: List both the current and the archive URL in SRC_URI (Portage tries each until one works) and declare QA_CONFIG_IMPL_DECL_SKIP=( htonll ) for the harmless probe; also -UNDEBUG is force-appended only for the testapp/debug targets then filtered again.
rule: When an upstream relocates old tarballs to an archive path, put both URLs in SRC_URI so the ebuild keeps fetching regardless of the release's age.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT expansion python3_{12..14} -> {12..15} after testing under py3.15; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT expansion to python3_15; nothing else changed.
Ebuild used DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit, pulling the full dev-python/flit tool although the pyproject backend is flit_core.buildapi.
root_cause: distutils-r1 distinguishes 'flit' (full flit) from 'flit-core' (only flit_core); most packages only need the latter, so 'flit' adds an unnecessary dependency chain.
fix_pattern: Change DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit to flit-core (done here together with the py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT bump).
rule: If pyproject.toml declares build-backend flit_core.buildapi, set DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit-core, not flit, to avoid pulling the full flit tool as BDEPEND.

problem: Package with USE=test and a custom python_test() failed in clean chroots because dev-python/pytest was missing from BDEPEND (bug 979004).
root_cause: A previous edit dropped the distutils_enable_tests call; defining python_test() yourself does NOT add the pytest BDEPEND -- only distutils_enable_tests wires up 'test? ( dev-python/pytest )' and IUSE/RESTRICT.
fix_pattern: Re-add 'distutils_enable_tests pytest' before the custom python_test() definition (a later python_test overrides the eclass-generated one but keeps the deps), plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() and EPYTEST_XDIST=1 for plugin isolation and parallel tests.
rule: Even when you write a custom python_test(), keep the distutils_enable_tests pytest call -- it supplies the pytest BDEPEND and test USE plumbing; your later function definition simply overrides the body.
snippet:
EPYTEST_PLUGINS=()
EPYTEST_XDIST=1
distutils_enable_tests pytest

python_test() {
	local EPYTEST_DESELECT=( ... )
	...
}

problem: SRC_URI pointed at a developer's personal devspace (dev.gentoo.org/~sam/...); moved to the permanent distfiles.gentoo.org/pub/proj/ location. Reminder to host generated tarballs at stable, non-personal URLs.

libxcrypt 4.5.2 bump: build fails with newer GCC (-Werror=discarded-qualifiers), a test breaks on ppc64el (varargs open() wrapper ABI), LTO builds are broken, and configure needs perl creating a bootstrap cycle (libxcrypt -> perl -> libxcrypt).
root_cause: Multiple: stricter const-checking in new GCC; __wrap_open mocks declared with fixed args while real open() is variadic (C ABI mismatch); upstream LTO bug; unconditional build-time perl dependency in configure.
fix_pattern: Cherry-pick the two already-merged upstream commits as files/*.patch with the upstream commit URL as the first line of each patch; break the perl cycle by shipping a maintainer-pregenerated 'make dist'-style ${P}-autotools.tar.xz selected by a NEED_BOOTSTRAP toggle in the ebuild; filter-lto with a bug reference; gate old-GCC breakage in pkg_pretend.
rule: For toolchain-adjacent packages with circular build deps, publish a pre-bootstrapped (autoreconf'd) tarball and switch SRC_URI on a NEED_BOOTSTRAP variable; always head cherry-picked patches with the upstream commit URL so the next bumper can check if they landed.
snippet:
NEED_BOOTSTRAP="no"
if [[ ${NEED_BOOTSTRAP} == "yes" ]] ; then
	inherit autotools
	SRC_URI="https://github.com/besser82/libxcrypt/releases/download/v${PV}/${P}.tar.xz"
else
	SRC_URI=".../${P}-autotools.tar.xz"
fi
# Doesn't work with LTO: bug #852917
filter-lto

problem: Qt C++ objects in gpsd were compiled with the user's CFLAGS instead of CXXFLAGS -- the SCons build copied qt_env['CFLAGS'] for C++ sources.
root_cause: Build-system bug: wrong flags variable used for C++ compilation, so user CXXFLAGS were silently ignored and C-only flags could hit the C++ compiler.
fix_pattern: Take the already-merged upstream MR as files/${P}-cxxflags.patch (one-line s/CFLAGS/CXXFLAGS/ in SConscript), note 'merged' in the PATCHES comment so it can be dropped on the next bump.
rule: If C++ objects are built with CFLAGS (check build logs for missing CXXFLAGS), patch the build system to use CXXFLAGS and carry the upstream-merged patch until the next release.
snippet:
-    qt_flags = qt_env['CFLAGS']
+    qt_flags = qt_env['CXXFLAGS']
PATCHES+=( "${FILESDIR}/${P}-cxxflags.patch" ) # merged

problem: Live ebuild cleanup fixing several classic errors: 'static' USE that would break reverse-deps, automagic libcap dependency undeclared, ntp daemons listed in DEPEND though only needed at runtime, X USE flag gating pure-Python tools, udev rules installed without reload on uninstall.
root_cause: Accumulated cruft: misnamed options, automagic detection, DEPEND/RDEPEND conflation, and hard deps for what are really optional runtime tools.
fix_pattern: Drop the static USE (force shared=True), add sys-libs/libcap to DEPEND to make automagic detection deterministic, move runtime-only daemon alternatives (ntpsec/chrony/ntp ||-block) to RDEPEND only, replace the X flag with unconditional script install plus optfeature hints in pkg_postinst, and call udev_reload in both pkg_postinst and pkg_postrm.
rule: Runtime-only helpers belong in RDEPEND (not DEPEND), optional tool dependencies become optfeature messages instead of USE flags, and any package installing udev rules must udev_reload in both postinst and postrm.

problem: New neard release: parallel make failed (tools objects raced against generated headers), a systemd USE flag existed only to install a unit file, and the NFC daemon silently did nothing without kernel NFC support.
root_cause: Makefile.am lacked a $(local_headers) prerequisite for the new tools objects; unit-file installation costs nothing so a USE flag for it is pointless; kernel config was never checked.
fix_pattern: Patch Makefile.am to add the missing objects to the $(local_headers) dependency list (upstream PR referenced in patch header) instead of forcing -j1; drop the systemd USE flag and always --enable-systemd with --with-systemdsystemunitdir=$(systemd_get_systemunitdir); add CONFIG_CHECK="~NFC" via linux-info and a start_pre initd hook that modprobes nfc.
rule: Fix parallel-make races by adding the missing prerequisite in Makefile.am (never MAKEOPTS=-j1), and never gate mere systemd-unit installation behind a USE flag -- install units unconditionally to systemd_get_systemunitdir.

problem: Font build pulled imagemagick just to convert one bitmap to PNG, restricted parallel make, and hard-depended on perl modules only used by optional runtime scripts.
root_cause: Upstream Makefile regenerates artifacts that are already shipped precompiled in the tarball; missing stamp prerequisites caused the parallel race; runtime-script deps were treated as build deps.
fix_pattern: Patch the Makefile to install the precompiled .png instead of regenerating it (dropping the imagemagick BDEPEND), add bdf-stamp prerequisites to ttf-stamp targets to fix parallel make, and move perl-module deps for optional scripts to optfeature calls in pkg_postinst.
rule: When the source tarball already ships a precompiled artifact identical to what a heavy tool would regenerate, patch the build to use it and drop the tool from BDEPEND.
snippet:
-ttf-stamp: hex-stamp
+ttf-stamp: hex-stamp bdf-stamp
---
use utils && optfeature "unipng2hex" dev-perl/GD virtual/perl-Getopt-Long

problem: X.org app bump where upstream switched from autotools to meson; old ebuild inherited xorg-3 and carried XORG_CONFIGURE_OPTIONS that no longer exist.
root_cause: X.org modules are migrating to meson; Gentoo provides a parallel xorg-meson eclass mirroring xorg-3, and autotools-era configure options must be dropped.
fix_pattern: Change 'inherit xorg-3' to 'inherit xorg-meson' and delete the XORG_CONFIGURE_OPTIONS array; deps stay the same.
rule: When an X.org package's new release builds with meson, swap the xorg-3 inherit for xorg-meson and remove autotools-only variables like XORG_CONFIGURE_OPTIONS.

problem: Upstream renamed its GitLab repo (mesa/drm -> mesa/libdrm); HOMEPAGE and metadata.xml remote-id updated to avoid redirects and keep version-check tooling pointed at the right repo.
HOMEPAGE URL refresh (x.org/wiki -> x.org) to avoid an HTTP redirect; no packaging content.

A big-endian-only test failure (test_pointer_array_struct_with_guint8) blocked tests; ebuild runs pytest through meson_src_test so EPYTEST_DESELECT is unavailable and skipping goes through PYTEST_ADDOPTS.
root_cause: Upstream test is acknowledged as not testing anything useful and broken on BE; when pytest is driven by meson, the only lever is the PYTEST_ADDOPTS environment variable.
fix_pattern: Extend PYTEST_ADDOPTS with another -k exclusion and a per-test comment naming the bug. CAVEAT: the commit passes two separate -k options, but pytest treats -k as last-one-wins, so "-k 'not a' -k 'not b'" only applies 'not b'; the correct form is a single "-k 'not a and not b'".
rule: When skipping pytest tests via PYTEST_ADDOPTS, combine all exclusions into ONE -k expression joined with 'and' -- repeated -k flags silently override each other.
snippet:
# correct:
local -x PYTEST_ADDOPTS="-k 'not test_python_calls_sync and not test_pointer_array_struct_with_guint8'"
# what the commit did (first -k is discarded by pytest):
# PYTEST_ADDOPTS="-k 'not a' -k 'not b'"

problem: gjs extension tests fail under Portage usersandbox; skipped by patching meson.build to not compile the gjs test target (no runtime skip knob existed). Idiom: comment out the test source in meson.build with a warning() naming the Gentoo bug.
libpeas 2.2.1 restored Gentoo lua handling and fixed a missing vala dependency: vapi generation used vala without dev-lang/vala in BDEPEND, and vala setup ran in the wrong phase.
root_cause: vala.eclass now provides vala_setup which must run in src_configure (it exports VALAC etc. for the configure run); forgetting the BDEPEND makes it work only by dependency accident. Gentoo's unversioned lua.pc needs a carried meson patch, with lua vs luajit selected off LUA_SINGLE_TARGET.
fix_pattern: Add 'vala? ( dev-lang/vala )' to BDEPEND and call 'use vala && vala_setup' at the top of src_configure; map LUA_SINGLE_TARGET onto two meson feature options via usex over lua_single_target_luajit.
rule: Packages generating .vapi need dev-lang/vala in BDEPEND and 'use vala && vala_setup' in src_configure; select lua vs luajit meson features from LUA_SINGLE_TARGET with usex.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT expansion to python3_15 on scipy; no other change.
Enabling py3.15 in PYTHON_COMPAT exposed one test failure only on the new interpreter (array typecodes changed)
root_cause: New CPython versions change stdlib behavior (array typecodes) that individual tests assert on; failure is interpreter-specific, not package-wide
fix_pattern: Bump PYTHON_COMPAT to python3_{12..15} and add an interpreter-conditional deselect inside python_test() using a case on ${EPYTHON}, so the test still runs on older interpreters
rule: When enabling a new Python version breaks only some tests on that interpreter, deselect them per-EPYTHON with a case statement in python_test() instead of deselecting globally

problem: Added two EPYTEST_DESELECT entries for tests broken by a numpy behavior change, with a one-line comment naming the cause; routine deselect maintenance
OpenRC service crashed at boot while the systemd unit worked fine
root_cause: The systemd unit had ordering (After=display-manager equivalent) that the hand-written OpenRC initd file lacked, so the daemon started too early
fix_pattern: Ship a new files/<name>.initd-r1 with a depend() block mirroring the systemd unit ordering ('after display-manager') plus checkpath for its state dir, install it via newinitd, and revbump the ebuild so existing installs pick up the new init script
rule: When a daemon misbehaves under OpenRC but not systemd, port the systemd unit's ordering/setup into the initd depend()/start_pre(), rename the initd file (-r1) and revbump so the changed file actually reaches users

problem: CONFIG_CHECK/check_extra_config failed or gave wrong results when building with tests inside a container: an on-disk kernel config existed but did not match the actual running host kernel
root_cause: linux-info.eclass prefers an on-disk kernel source/config over the running kernel's config; in containers the on-disk config is unrelated to the kernel the tests will actually run on
fix_pattern: Before check_extra_config, point KERNEL_DIR at a path that cannot exist so linux-info finds no sources and falls back to runtime-only checks (/proc/config.gz of the running kernel)
rule: When kernel-feature checks must reflect the RUNNING kernel (e.g. only for src_test), set KERNEL_DIR to a nonexistent dummy path before check_extra_config to force linux-info into runtime-only mode
snippet:
pkg_pretend() {
	CONFIG_CHECK="~BPF ~BPF_SYSCALL ..."
	if use test; then
		# force linux-info to check only the running kernel (bug 977516)
		KERNEL_DIR="linux-info-runtime-checks-only"
	fi
	check_extra_config
}

problem: MY_P=${P/_rc2/~rc2} in global scope: the unquoted ~ in the parameter-expansion replacement string is subject to tilde expansion, corrupting the value (and thus SRC_URI/S) under bash versions that expand it
root_cause: In bash, the replacement text of ${var/pat/repl} undergoes tilde expansion when the ~ is unescaped; ebuild global scope has no quoting around the assignment RHS expansion to prevent it
fix_pattern: Escape the tilde in the replacement: MY_P=${P/_rc2/\~rc2} (same for _rc -> ~RC upstream naming)
rule: When translating Gentoo _rc/_beta suffixes to upstream '~'-style version strings via ${P/x/~y} in global scope, always backslash-escape the tilde in the replacement

problem: Same tilde-expansion bug as hamlib commit a4105cc: unescaped ~ in ${P/_rc/~RC} used in SRC_URI and S in global scope; fixed by escaping to ${P/_rc/\~RC}
Herestring (<<<) used in a global-scope loop to build SRC_URI failed under sandboxed metadata generation
root_cause: bash implements heredocs/herestrings with a temporary file (always in < EAPI 9, and still for large input in >= EAPI 9); metadata generation may run under a sandbox that forbids the temp-file write, so global-scope code must not use them
fix_pattern: Replace 'read A B C <<< "$str"' with process substitution: 'read -rd '' A B C < <(printf %s "$str")' which needs no temp file; note read -d '' returns nonzero at EOF so do not append || die
rule: Never use heredocs or herestrings in ebuild global scope (including helper functions called from global scope); feed read via < <(printf %s ...) process substitution instead
snippet:
for DATASET in ${!DATASETS[@]}; do
	read -rd '' FILENAME VERSION ENVVAR < <(printf %s "${DATASETS[$DATASET]}")
	SRC_URI+="${SRC_DATA}/${FILENAME}.${VERSION}.tar.gz "
done
unset DATASET FILENAME VERSION ENVVAR

problem: Same global-scope herestring issue as geant-data (690c4a1c): a helper function called from global scope to build submodule SRC_URIs used 'read <<<'
root_cause: Herestrings create temp files, which breaks sandboxed metadata generation; functions executed during metadata phase count as global scope
fix_pattern: Switched to 'read -rd '' ... < <(printf %s "${line}")' and dropped the trailing '|| die' because read -d '' exits nonzero when it hits end-of-input, which would false-positive the die
rule: When converting 'read <<<' to 'read -rd '' < <(printf ...)', remove any '|| die' on the read since a nonzero exit is the normal EOF outcome
snippet: read -rd '' name hoster dep url commit < <(printf %s "${line}")

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{12..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other change needed
Typo LICENCES vs LICENSES in a dodoc call meant the license/permission-notice files required by bindist redistribution were not installed in the live ebuild
New Emacs package needed the standard packaging scaffolding (site file, autoloads, ERT tests)
root_cause: app-emacs packages have a fixed idiom set that is easy to get wrong when packaging from scratch
fix_pattern: EAPI=9 + inherit elisp; SITEFILE=50${PN}-gentoo.el with a two-line files/ site file adding load-path and loading autoloads; src_compile calls elisp_src_compile then elisp-make-autoload-file; src_test uses elisp-test-ert with explicit -l test files; live/release split with git-r3 and GitHub tag tarball renamed to ${P}.gh.tar.gz; metadata sets stabilize-allarches
rule: For a new app-emacs package, follow the elisp eclass template: 50PN-gentoo.el sitefile, elisp-make-autoload-file in src_compile, elisp-test-ert for tests, and mirror elisp RDEPEND into BDEPEND for byte-compilation

problem: Emacs package byte-compilation could fail or silently miscompile because its elisp library dependencies were only in RDEPEND, not available at build time
root_cause: Byte-compiling elisp requires the required libraries present when the compiler runs, i.e. they are build-time deps too; app-emacs ebuilds commonly forget BDEPEND
fix_pattern: Add BDEPEND="${RDEPEND}" mirroring the elisp deps, and revbump (-r1) so the corrected dependency metadata applies to already-installed users
rule: In app-emacs ebuilds, always mirror elisp RDEPEND into BDEPEND (byte-compilation needs them), and revbump when fixing dependency metadata

problem: Random, job-count-dependent test failures on Python 3.14+ in a branch (django 5.2.x) that upstream does not support on 3.14
root_cause: Python 3.14 changed the default multiprocessing start method on Linux from fork to forkserver; test suites written around fork semantics (inherited state) fail intermittently under forkserver
fix_pattern: Extend the local backport patch to call multiprocessing.set_start_method("fork") at the top of the test runner (tests/runtests.py), forcing the old behavior only for the test run; no revbump needed since only test behavior changes
rule: When a package's tests become flaky on Python 3.14+, suspect the fork->forkserver default change and pin multiprocessing.set_start_method('fork') in the test entry point (test-only patches need no revbump)

problem: Routine py3.15 enablement that also dropped pypy3_11 and python3_11 from PYTHON_COMPAT in the same edit
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} (package uses DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=uv-build); no other change
EPYTEST_RERUNS=10 was set AFTER the distutils_enable_tests pytest call, so the eclass never added the dev-python/pytest-rerunfailures test dependency it implies
root_cause: distutils_enable_tests reads EPYTEST_PLUGINS/EPYTEST_RERUNS/EPYTEST_TIMEOUT at call time to generate BDEPEND; variables assigned later affect epytest at runtime but produce no dependency, giving unreliable/failing test envs
fix_pattern: Move the EPYTEST_RERUNS assignment above distutils_enable_tests (done here together with the py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT bump)
rule: Always set all EPYTEST_* knobs (PLUGINS, RERUNS, TIMEOUT) before calling distutils_enable_tests, because the eclass converts them into test dependencies at call time
snippet:
EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( anyio pytest-timeout )
EPYTEST_RERUNS=10
distutils_enable_tests pytest

problem: Test suite implicitly required dev-python/coverage even though the ebuild does not depend on it, breaking tests in a clean environment
root_cause: Upstream's pytest ini filterwarnings configuration references warning categories from optional tooling (coverage); pytest imports the module named in a filterwarnings entry, so a missing optional package aborts the run
fix_pattern: Neutralize the ini-file warning filters on the epytest command line with '-o filterwarnings=' instead of adding an unnecessary test dep or patching the config
rule: When pytest fails because ini filterwarnings pull in optional modules (coverage etc.), append '-o filterwarnings=' to the epytest call to clear the inherited filters

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; no other change
Spelling fix in an ewarn message (superseeds -> supersedes); message-only edits do not need a revbump
Package failed to build/run against the gmmlib version the ebuild claimed sufficient (>=22.9.0)
root_cause: Upstream actually requires a newer gmmlib API (22.10.0); the declared minimum bound was stale, so the dep graph allowed a broken combination
fix_pattern: Raise the lower bound (>=media-libs/gmmlib-22.10.0:=) keeping the := slot operator, and revbump to -r1 so the corrected constraint is re-evaluated on users' systems
rule: When a bug reveals the real minimum version of a dependency, fix the >= bound in the ebuild AND revbump; a metadata-only edit without revbump never reaches installed users

problem: Ebuild installs udev rules via udev_dorules but never triggered a rules reload, so the new device rules were inert until reboot
root_cause: udev.eclass installs rules but reloading udev is the ebuild's responsibility in pkg_postinst AND pkg_postrm; the calls were simply missing
fix_pattern: Add udev_reload to pkg_postinst and add a pkg_postrm() with udev_reload; revbump to -r1
rule: Any ebuild that calls udev_dorules/udev_newrules must also call udev_reload in both pkg_postinst and pkg_postrm (grep-able QA check), and the fix warrants a revbump

problem: Upstream ships proprietary tools as commandlinetools-linux-<buildid>_latest.zip with no semantic version, making normal PV-based SRC_URI impossible
root_cause: Google publishes only build-number-stamped 'latest' archives; the archive contents change per build id, not per marketing version
fix_pattern: Encode the build id in the Gentoo version as 0_p<buildid> and derive the URL with $(ver_cut 3); set RESTRICT="bindist mirror" for the proprietary blob and install under /opt with group-based permissions (fowners/fperms + acct-group)
rule: For versionless upstream binary drops identified only by a build number, use PV=0_p<buildid> and reconstruct the URL with ver_cut, so plain version bumps stay mechanical

problem: While enabling py3.15, DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 was corrected from 'flit' to 'flit-core'
root_cause: distutils-r1 distinguishes the flit backend values: the value must match the actual build-backend in pyproject.toml (flit_core.buildapi -> flit-core), otherwise the wrong build dependency is pulled in
fix_pattern: Check pyproject.toml [build-system] build-backend and set DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 to the exact matching eclass value; audit this on every version bump since eclass values get split/renamed
rule: On python package bumps, re-verify DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 against pyproject.toml's build-backend rather than copying the old value forward
snippet:
DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit-core
PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} )
inherit distutils-r1 pypi

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; no other change
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} (pdm-backend package); no other change
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{12..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_15; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_15; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_15; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_15 plus copyright-year refresh.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_15; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_15; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_15; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_15 on a DISTUTILS_EXT=1 package; no other change.

PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_15 that also deleted the stale '9.6.0: fails to compile with py3.15' comment once the newer version compiled; minor hygiene: remove blocker comments when re-enabling an impl.
PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_15 that simultaneously dropped python3_11 and pypy3_11 from the older ebuild, keeping the supported window aligned with the eclass default.
Version bump of a multilib + optional-python autotools package needed a full 143-line ebuild; keeping it correct by hand is error-prone.
root_cause: Complex packages drift between the release ebuild and the -9999 live ebuild if they are not kept in sync at every bump.
fix_pattern: The new 2.6.1 ebuild is byte-identical to the updated 9999 ebuild (only the release branch of the [[ ${PV} == *9999 ]] conditional differs in effect); the same commit also bumps PYTHON_COMPAT in the 9999 ebuild. Release-only bits are gated in one if-block: verify-sig inherit, conditional .asc in SRC_URI, KEYWORDS, sec-keys BDEPEND, and src_unpack calls verify-sig_verify_detached before default.
rule: Maintain the live/9999 ebuild as the single template and make every version bump a copy of it, updating the live ebuild in the same commit so they never drift.

problem: Cross-compiling (llvm-to-llvm cross) failed at link time with: ld.lld: error: /usr/lib/*.so is incompatible with elf_${ARCH}.
root_cause: Upstream's shipped libtool relinks against -rpath /usr/lib, pulling host libraries into a cross link; Gentoo's patched libtool avoids this, but only if elibtoolize is run to apply Gentoo's libtool fixes to the shipped ltmain.sh.
fix_pattern: inherit libtool and call elibtoolize in src_prepare (after default) in every ebuild version, without a full eautoreconf. Bug #978993.
rule: When an autotools package linked with libtool picks up host /usr/lib libraries during cross-compilation (elf_ARCH incompatibility from the linker), inherit libtool and add elibtoolize to src_prepare instead of patching or re-autoreconfing.

problem: Linking with ld.lld failed: "version script assignment of 'local' to symbol '_fini' failed: symbol not defined" (same for _init). Bug #973026.
root_cause: The upstream libtool .sym version scripts explicitly mark _init/_fini as local, but lld (unlike bfd) errors out when a version script names symbols that do not exist in the output; modern toolchains may not emit _init/_fini at all.
fix_pattern: FILESDIR patch removing the 'local: _fini; _init;' stanza from every *.sym version script; the patch header documents the Gentoo bug, the upstream PR, and the termux patch it was borrowed from.
rule: When ld.lld rejects a version script over local/_init/_fini symbols that are not defined, patch the version script to drop those symbol names; always record bug URL and upstream PR provenance in the patch header.

problem: Build failed on new compiler warnings because upstream hardcodes -Werror in Makefile.am (bug #976265).
root_cause: Upstream forces -Werror in COMMON_CFLAGS, so any new GCC/Clang warning breaks the build; Gentoo policy is that released packages must not build with -Werror.
fix_pattern: Small FILESDIR patch dropping -Werror from Makefile.am (package already runs eautoreconf via its other build patches), added to PATCHES; no revbump needed because installed files are unchanged for users who built successfully.
rule: When a package hardcodes -Werror in its build system, strip it with a minimal patch (or sed) rather than filtering warnings, since any future compiler upgrade can break the build.

problem: The detached .sig file was downloaded unconditionally for all users, even with USE=-verify-sig; and the sec-keys dependency was unversioned so an outdated installed keyring could fail verification of new releases.
root_cause: SRC_URI listed the .sig outside a verify-sig? USE-conditional group, and BDEPEND on sec-keys/openpgp-keys-emersion had no minimum version guaranteeing the currently-used signing key is present.
fix_pattern: Wrap the signature URL in verify-sig? ( ... ) inside SRC_URI and add a date-based lower bound on the keyring package; also sync stable and live ebuilds in one commit.
rule: Always put detached signature URLs inside verify-sig? ( ... ) in SRC_URI and version-bound the sec-keys keyring dependency to a snapshot that contains the key actually used to sign the release.

problem: Package broke on python-3.14 because it still used distutils.core setup.py and an old-style bin/ script; upstream is unresponsive (bug #974016).
root_cause: Upstream never migrated off distutils (removed from modern Python) and its fix sat unmerged in a GitHub PR.
fix_pattern: Vendor the unmerged upstream PR as files/github-pr-2.patch, with a header explaining it is 'Most of PR#2, without the version bump or README changes'; new -r5 revbump uses DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=setuptools against the PR-added pyproject.toml with a [project.scripts] entry point, plus a relative dosym from the nagios plugins dir to /usr/bin so the python-exec wrapper machinery stays intact.
rule: When a new Python release breaks a dead/slow upstream, adopt its unmerged upstream PR as a FILESDIR patch (documenting exactly what was included/excluded) and revbump, rather than writing a divergent local fix.
snippet:
PATCHES=( "${FILESDIR}/github-pr-2.patch" )
src_install() {
	distutils-r1_src_install
	local nagiosplugindir="/usr/$(get_libdir)/nagios/plugins"
	dodir "${nagiosplugindir}"
	dosym "../../../bin/${MY_PN}" "${nagiosplugindir}/${MY_PN}"
}

problem: New Emacs Lisp package; shows the current canonical app-emacs skeleton (EAPI=9, elisp eclass).
root_cause: N/A - new package, template value only.
fix_pattern: EAPI=9 + inherit elisp, NEED_EMACS minimum, SITEFILE=50${PN}-gentoo.el with a 2-line autoload site file, GitHub tag SRC_URI renamed to ${P}.gh.tar.gz, identical release and 9999 ebuilds differing only via the PV conditional, elisp-make-autoload-file in src_compile, and per-file ERT test loop in src_test; elisp deps expressed as >=app-emacs/* RDEPEND.
rule: For Emacs packages, use the elisp eclass template: NEED_EMACS + SITEFILE + elisp-make-autoload-file, and run tests by looping elisp-test-ert over tests/*-tests.el.

problem: Package broke against >=dev-python/cryptography-47 (bug #978394): cryptography's abstract key classes now require deepcopy support; one test also failed against newer tpm2-tss.
root_cause: A dependency's major version bump changed API contracts (abstract classes require __deepcopy__), and the system TPM stack implements a newer spec revision than the test expects.
fix_pattern: Backport the upstream compat commit as a FILESDIR patch, revbump to -r3, raise the dependency to >=dev-python/cryptography-47 so the patched behavior matches the dep bound, and deselect the spec-revision-sensitive test via EPYTEST_DESELECT with a comment.
rule: When a dependency's major bump breaks a reverse dependency, backport the upstream fix, revbump, and move the dependency lower-bound up to the version the patch targets; skip individually-broken tests with EPYTEST_DESELECT plus a reason comment instead of RESTRICTing all tests.

problem: Enabling python3_15 required actual code fixes, not just a PYTHON_COMPAT edit: py3.15 removed/changed inspect lazy_annotations behavior django relied on.
root_cause: New CPython minor versions change stdlib APIs; upstream fixes existed only as individual git commits not yet in any release.
fix_pattern: Combine the relevant upstream commits (plus maintainer's own fixes) into one FILESDIR patch and list every upstream commit hash in a comment directly above the PATCHES entry; reuse the same django-6.0.7-py315.patch across both the 5.2.x and 6.0.x ebuilds since it applies to both.
rule: When enabling a new Python implementation needs upstream code changes, backport them as a single FILESDIR patch whose provenance (each upstream commit hash) is recorded in a comment next to the PATCHES entry, and share one patch file across ebuild versions when it applies cleanly.
snippet:
PATCHES=(
	# based on upstream commits:
	# b1a65eac7c09250d36e12464fc8fff2a401246b6
	# ed13a58bf63df94508c8a0fe779da0b6a2bc26bb
	# e7f539f813bd56a71ca3c1fbf379f47691002086
	# plus my own fixes
	"${FILESDIR}"/django-6.0.7-py315.patch
)

problem: pkgcheck was unable to evaluate the dependency atom >=sci-libs/hipBLAS-${ROCM_VERSION}[${ROCM_USEDEP}], blocking QA checks for the whole package.
root_cause: ROCM_USEDEP expands to a USE_EXPAND-dependent flag set that varies across the version range matched by the >= atom, which pkgcheck cannot elaborate.
fix_pattern: Weaken the atom minimally (drop the USE dependency, keep the version bound) and document the intended stricter dependency in a comment above it.
rule: When a QA tool cannot process a dependency expression, simplify the atom just enough to make the tool work and leave a comment stating the constraint you actually intended.

problem: USE=nvenc silently also enabled nvdec, which newly requires clang with the NVPTX LLVM target as a build dep - an awkward, heavy toolchain requirement forced onto all nvenc users (bugs #974512, #978224).
root_cause: Two upstream features were folded into one USE flag ($(use_enable nvenc nvdec)); when one of them grew an expensive BDEPEND the coupling became unacceptable.
fix_pattern: Split nvdec into its own USE flag: add it to IUSE and metadata.xml, express the feature dependency as REQUIRED_USE="nvdec? ( nvenc )", gate the clang[llvm_targets_NVPTX] BDEPEND behind nvdec, switch to $(use_enable nvdec), and revbump every affected version since dependencies changed.
rule: When a sub-feature hidden behind an existing USE flag acquires a heavy or awkward dependency, split it into its own flag with REQUIRED_USE expressing the feature coupling, and revbump because the dependency graph changed.
snippet:
IUSE="... nvdec nvenc ..."
REQUIRED_USE="
	nvdec? ( nvenc )
"
BDEPEND+=" nvdec? ( llvm-core/clang:*[llvm_targets_NVPTX] )"
# src_configure: $(use_enable nvdec)  # was $(use_enable nvenc nvdec)

problem: Ebuild used a bash herestring (read -r ... <<< ...) in a function executed at global scope to build SRC_URI, breaking metadata generation (bug #978942).
root_cause: bash implements <<< via a temporary file; global-scope ebuild code runs during metadata cache regeneration in restricted environments where writing temp files (and forking) is not permitted, so herestrings/process substitution/command substitution of externals must not run at global scope.
fix_pattern: Replace the herestring split with pure parameter-expansion word splitting: save IFS, set IFS=';', expand into an array, restore IFS; also declare the loop variable local to stop it leaking into global scope.
rule: Never use <<<, <(), pipes, or anything that forks or writes files in code that runs at ebuild global scope (including helper functions called there); split strings with IFS + array expansion instead.

problem: GHC build failed because the bundled terminfo library's generated ./configure uses 'test ... == ...' (bug #978912).
root_cause: == inside test(1) is a bashism; when configure runs under a strict POSIX /bin/sh the comparison fails with an 'unexpected operator' style error, taking the wrong branch or aborting.
fix_pattern: One-line FILESDIR patch changing == to = directly in the shipped configure script (no autoreconf of the bundled lib), applied via eapply in src_prepare with the bug URL as a comment.
rule: When a configure script fails under POSIX sh on a 'test x == y' comparison, patch the generated configure directly to use a single =, since regenerating a bundled sub-library's configure is riskier than the one-character fix.

problem: musl's riscv32 syscall table wrongly defined __NR_nanosleep (a time32 call absent on natively-time64 riscv32) and omitted close_range/quotactl_fd, breaking applications doing raw syscalls on riscv32.
root_cause: Arch-specific header bug in upstream musl, already fixed by an upstream commit but not in any release.
fix_pattern: Backport the upstream commit verbatim (with full commit message) as a FILESDIR patch and create a -r2 revbump whose KEYWORDS are restricted to only the affected arch (KEYWORDS="-* ~riscv" vs the -r1 full keyword set), so unaffected stable arches are not forced to rebuild libc for a riscv32-only fix.
rule: For a fix that only affects one architecture in a critical system package, add the revbump with KEYWORDS limited to that arch (-* ~arch) and leave the previous revision's full keywords intact.
snippet:
# musl-1.2.6-r2.ebuild (riscv32 fix only):
KEYWORDS="-* ~riscv"
PATCHES=(
	...
	"${FILESDIR}"/${P}-riscv32-syscalls.patch
)
# musl-1.2.6-r1 keeps: KEYWORDS="-* amd64 arm arm64 ~m68k ~mips ppc ppc64 ~riscv x86"

problem: Enabling py3.15 exposed a few async tests failing only on that implementation, and benchmark tests were being disabled via fragile sed edits of the test file.
root_cause: New CPython minor versions routinely break a handful of tests; sed-mangling test sources breaks silently when upstream reshuffles tests, and blanket skips lose coverage on impls where tests pass.
fix_pattern: Bump PYTHON_COMPAT to python3_{12..15} plus freethreading variants python3_{14..15}t, and replace the sed hack with a python_test() that uses EPYTEST_DESELECT, appending py3.15-only deselects inside a 'case ${EPYTHON}' block; pass 'epytest -o strict_markers=False' to tolerate upstream benchmark markers.
rule: When a new Python implementation breaks only some tests, add a python_test() with a case-${EPYTHON} block appending impl-specific EPYTEST_DESELECT entries instead of RESTRICTing tests or sed-editing test files.

problem: A test dependency (pydantic) was not available for every PYTHON_COMPAT implementation, handled via a PYTHON_FULLY_TESTED array duplicated in python_gen_cond_dep and in python_test.
root_cause: Hardcoded 'fully tested' implementation lists go stale on every new Python version bump and must be edited in two places.
fix_pattern: Drop PYTHON_FULLY_TESTED and python_gen_cond_dep; list the test dep unconditionally in test? BDEPEND, and in python_test gate the dep-requiring tests on an at-runtime has_version check with PYTHON_USEDEP so the skip tracks reality automatically.
rule: Gate implementation-dependent test skips on has_version 'dep[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]' inside python_test instead of maintaining a hardcoded list of fully-tested implementations.

problem: ggml lacked a ROCm/HIP GPU backend option that users requested (bug 978853).
root_cause: The cmake HIP backend was never wired to a USE flag, and ROCm packaging needs eclass support for AMDGPU target flags and version-matched HIP libraries.
fix_pattern: Set ROCM_VERSION before 'inherit rocm'; add rocm to IUSE with REQUIRED_USE="rocm? ( ${ROCM_REQUIRED_USE} )"; depend on >=dev-util/hip-${ROCM_VERSION} and hipBLAS[${ROCM_USEDEP}]; pass -DGGML_HIP=$(usex rocm) (plus backend-specific tweaks like -DGGML_HIP_MMQ_MFMA=OFF); revbump -r1 to -r2 because USE flags changed; document the new flag in metadata.xml.
rule: When adding a ROCm backend USE flag, use rocm.eclass with ROCM_VERSION set before inherit, mirror ROCM_REQUIRED_USE/ROCM_USEDEP into REQUIRED_USE and deps, and revbump since the installed image changes for existing users.
snippet:
ROCM_VERSION=7.2
inherit cmake rocm
IUSE="... rocm"
REQUIRED_USE="rocm? ( ${ROCM_REQUIRED_USE} )"
RDEPEND="rocm? (
	>=dev-util/hip-${ROCM_VERSION}
	>=sci-libs/hipBLAS-${ROCM_VERSION}[${ROCM_USEDEP}]
)"

problem: kmscon-10.0.1 failed to build on systems with dev-libs/libtsm-4.5.0 installed (bug 978975) even though the ebuild's dependency bound was satisfied.
root_cause: Upstream started using API only present in libtsm 4.6.0, but the ebuild kept the stale >=4.5.0 minimum from the previous release, so the dep graph let users build against a too-old library.
fix_pattern: Raise the version bound in COMMON_DEPEND from >=dev-libs/libtsm-4.5.0:= to >=dev-libs/libtsm-4.6.0:= (keeping the := slot operator).
rule: When a build failure is caused by a too-old version of a declared dependency, fix it by raising the >= bound to the real minimum instead of documenting 'upgrade first'; re-check minimum dep versions on every bump.

problem: alertmanager failed to build with an older installed Go toolchain (bug 978973) because nothing in the ebuild required a new-enough dev-lang/go.
root_cause: go.mod's 'go 1.25' toolchain directive is not automatically translated into a Gentoo dependency; BDEPEND only listed dev-util/promu, so users with older go hit compile errors.
fix_pattern: Add an explicit >=dev-lang/go-1.25.0 to BDEPEND alongside the build tool.
