rule: When bumping a Go package, read the 'go' directive in go.mod and mirror it as BDEPEND ">=dev-lang/go-X.Y.0" so the toolchain requirement is enforced by the package manager.

problem: The virtual's || dependency used =dev-lang/perl-5.44.0_rc1, which stops matching as soon as perl is revbumped to -r1.
root_cause: The '=' operator without a trailing '*' matches exactly that version with zero revision, so any perl revision bump breaks resolution of the virtual.
fix_pattern: Replace '=dev-lang/perl-5.44.0_rc1' with '~dev-lang/perl-5.44.0_rc1'; the '~' operator matches the given version with any -rN revision.
rule: To pin a dependency to one upstream version while still accepting ebuild revisions, use ~cat/pkg-ver instead of =cat/pkg-ver.

problem: Building with setuptools >75.6.0 emitted a deprecation warning about the 'License :: OSI Approved ...' trove classifier in setup.py.
root_cause: setuptools deprecated license trove classifiers in favor of SPDX license expressions in the license field, so old-style setup.py metadata now warns (and will eventually error).
fix_pattern: Backport the upstream commit as FILESDIR patch: delete the License classifier and add license='LGPL-3.0-or-later' (SPDX id) to setup(); reference the upstream issue/commit URLs at the top of the patch. Done together with the PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}.
rule: When new setuptools complains about license classifiers, patch the build metadata to an SPDX license expression (ideally backporting the upstream fix) rather than pinning old setuptools or ignoring the warning.
snippet:
-        'License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Lesser General Public License v3 (LGPLv3)',
...
+    license='LGPL-3.0-or-later',

problem: fastapi's test suite imports pydantic-settings, which was missing from the test dependencies, so tests failed with collection/import errors.
root_cause: Upstream added a test-only dependency during a version bump and the ebuild's test? BDEPEND block was not updated to match.
fix_pattern: Add dev-python/pydantic-settings[${PYTHON_USEDEP}] to the test? ( ... ) group in BDEPEND.
rule: After any bump (or enabling a new implementation), actually run the test suite and add every newly-imported module to the test? ( ) block in BDEPEND with [${PYTHON_USEDEP}].

problem: The extension package (DISTUTILS_EXT=1) was not enabled for freethreading (no-GIL) CPython builds.
root_cause: Freethreading CPython variants are separate PYTHON_COMPAT targets with a 't' suffix and require explicit opt-in per package once the extension is known to build and pass tests without the GIL.
fix_pattern: Append the freethreading targets to PYTHON_COMPAT using the brace-range 't' syntax: PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} python3_{14..15}t ).
rule: To enable freethreading CPython support, add python3_XYt entries to PYTHON_COMPAT after verifying the build and tests pass; this matters most for DISTUTILS_EXT=1 packages where GIL-free operation is not guaranteed.

problem: Same go-toolchain lesson as 115004dd (alertmanager): added BDEPEND >=dev-lang/go-1.25.0 to match go.mod's toolchain requirement.
Same freethreading enablement idiom as d6837f23 (librt): added python3_{14..15}t to PYTHON_COMPAT of a pure-python package.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT range bump python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} (also dropped pypy3_11) across two ebuilds; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{12..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} (dropped pypy3_11); no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{12..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_14 -> python3_{14..15}; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; no other changes.
Routine security/version bump of c-ares to 1.34.8; new ebuild is a copy of the previous one (verify-sig, QA_CONFIG_IMPL_DECL_SKIP, gtest network-test filtering all pre-existing).
New Emacs package hosted on Codeberg; naive SRC_URI/S would fail because Gitea/Forgejo archive tarballs are unversioned and unpack into a directory named after the repo, not ${P}.
root_cause: Codeberg (Gitea/Forgejo) /archive/${PV}.tar.gz downloads have a generic filename and extract to ${PN}/ (repo name only, no version), unlike GitHub archives which extract to ${PN}-${PV}/.
fix_pattern: Rename the fetched tarball with '-> ${P}.tar.gz' to avoid distfile collisions across versions, and set S="${WORKDIR}/${PN}". For elisp packages also: SITEFILE="50${PN}-gentoo.el" with a matching files/ site-init file, elisp-enable-tests ert ./test/, and call elisp-make-autoload-file in src_compile.
rule: When SRC_URI points at a Codeberg/Gitea archive, always rename it to ${P}.tar.gz and set S to ${WORKDIR}/${PN} because the archive extracts to the bare repo name.

problem: formattertest fails intermittently (flaky) during src_test, breaking otherwise-good builds and blocking stabilization.
root_cause: Upstream test is nondeterministic; failure is not a packaging regression, so failing the merge on it is noise.
fix_pattern: Append the test name to the existing skip-list array in src_test with a comment citing the bug number, and apply the same edit to every live ebuild version so the fix doesn't regress on the next bump. Note the same commit also stabilized (~amd64 -> amd64) once tests were reliable.
rule: When a test is confirmed flaky (fails intermittently, not tied to your change), add it to a named skip array in src_test with a '# bug NNNNNN: flaky test' comment and mirror the change into all in-tree versions including 9999.

problem: Whitespace cleanup: removed a double blank line left behind by a previous edit.
openssh failed to build/configure against >=openssl-4 because the ebuild unconditionally passed --with-ssl-engine, but the OpenSSL ENGINE API was removed in OpenSSL 4.
root_cause: A configure option hard-wired in the ebuild targets a feature that exists only in older versions of a library dependency; once the dep is upgraded the option becomes invalid.
fix_pattern: Gate the configure flag on the installed dependency version using has_version inside src_configure, keeping the old behaviour for older OpenSSL (the option is default-off so it cannot be left to autodetection).
rule: When a configure flag depends on a feature removed in a newer version of a dependency, wrap it in a has_version version check in src_configure instead of dropping it outright, so both old and new dep versions keep working.
snippet:
if has_version "<dev-libs/openssl-4" ; then
	# bug #973058: ENGINE support removed in openssl-4
	myconf+=( $(use_with ssl ssl-engine) )
fi

problem: Prometheus-family Go package built via promu produced statically linked binaries with useless git-hash/builduser embedding, and needed a dependency tarball for offline vendoring.
root_cause: Upstream .promu.yml defaults to cgo:false (fully static Go binaries) and embeds Branch/BuildUser/BuildDate ldflags that are meaningless in a distro build; Gentoo policy prefers dynamic linking and reproducible builds.
fix_pattern: Ship a small FILESDIR patch to .promu.yml adding 'cgo: true' under go: and 'static: false' under build:, build with 'promu build -v --prefix bin', and fetch vendored deps from a gentoo-golang-dist vendor tarball as a second SRC_URI entry alongside the upstream source tarball.
rule: For promu-built Prometheus exporters, patch .promu.yml (cgo: true, static: false) to enforce dynamic linking and use a prebuilt -vendor.tar.xz dependency tarball instead of network fetching.

problem: alertmanager could not be built from upstream's published source tarball because building the bundled web UI from source is broken/impractical; also same static-linking and git-hash-embedding issues as other promu packages.
root_cause: Upstream expects a Node/Elm toolchain to regenerate web UI assets; the release process publishes those assets as a separate prebuilt tarball instead of including them in the source archive.
fix_pattern: Add the upstream prebuilt web-ui tarball as an extra SRC_URI entry and copy it into place in src_prepare (cp -a "${WORKDIR}"/dist ui/app), then build only the Go side with promu; patch .promu.yml for cgo:true/static:false as with other exporters.
rule: When an upstream ships generated frontend assets as a separate release artifact, fetch that artifact via SRC_URI and copy it into the expected source path in src_prepare rather than trying to rebuild the JS/Elm toolchain.

problem: genshi broke on Python 3.15: creating ast nodes without required fields (e.g. _ast.Expression()) was a DeprecationWarning since 3.13 and became a hard error in 3.15.
root_cause: CPython PR #105880 turned field-less AST-node construction into an error in 3.15; old metaprogramming code that instantiates node classes empty and sets attributes afterwards stops working.
fix_pattern: Revbump to -r1 adding a FILESDIR patch backported from the open upstream PR (referenced by URL in a comment above PATCHES), extend PYTHON_COMPAT to python3_15; the patch constructs AST nodes with required kwargs derived from cls.__annotations__.
rule: When enabling a new Python target breaks a package on a known CPython behaviour change, backport the upstream PR as a FILESDIR patch with the PR URL in a comment, and revbump since installed files change.

problem: Enabling py3.15 required more than a PYTHON_COMPAT edit: the audioop-lts conditional dep had to be extended to 3.15, and DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit had to become flit-core.
root_cause: audioop was removed from the stdlib in Python 3.13, so the dev-python/audioop-lts backport is needed for every 3.13+ version and its python_gen_cond_dep version list must track new targets; 'flit' is a deprecated alias for the flit-core backend.
fix_pattern: Extend the python_gen_cond_dep version list to include the new target and rename the PEP517 backend value in the same edit.
rule: When adding a new Python target to PYTHON_COMPAT, grep the ebuild for python_gen_cond_dep version lists (stdlib-removal backports like audioop-lts) and append the new version, or the dep will silently be missing on that target.

problem: Routine version bump of solaar to 1.1.20; udev rules handling, CONFIG_CHECK and sandbox-test deselects all carried over from previous ebuild.
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}, dropping pypy3_11 and the 3.11 floor.
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}.
libcst (Rust extension via PyO3) could not build for Python 3.15 because the bundled PyO3 version does not yet declare support for that interpreter version and aborts the build.
root_cause: PyO3 hard-caps the maximum supported CPython version at compile time; a new CPython released after the vendored PyO3 crates makes the build fail even though the abi3 interface is forward-compatible.
fix_pattern: Export PYO3_USE_ABI3_FORWARD_COMPATIBILITY=1 at ebuild top level so PyO3 builds against the newer interpreter using the stable ABI, instead of waiting for a crate bump.
rule: When a PyO3-based Rust/Python package fails with 'the configured Python interpreter version is newer than PyO3's maximum supported version', export PYO3_USE_ABI3_FORWARD_COMPATIBILITY=1 in the ebuild.
snippet:
# allow PyO3 to build against Python newer than its declared max
export PYO3_USE_ABI3_FORWARD_COMPATIBILITY=1

problem: A single test (test_config_env) fails only on Python 3.15 due to deprecation warnings, blocking the py3.15 enablement.
root_cause: New interpreter version emits DeprecationWarnings that the test suite treats as errors; failure is interpreter-specific, not package-wide.
fix_pattern: Deselect the failing test only for the affected interpreter using a case statement on ${EPYTHON} appending to EPYTEST_DESELECT inside python_test; also simplify python_gen_cond_dep wrappers that only existed for pypy once pypy is dropped from PYTHON_COMPAT.
rule: When a test fails only on one Python implementation/version, deselect it per-interpreter with 'case ${EPYTHON} in' + EPYTEST_DESELECT instead of skipping it everywhere.

problem: py3.15 enablement using the same per-${EPYTHON} EPYTEST_DESELECT case idiom as inline-snapshot, plus removal of pypy-only workarounds (cloudpickle cond-dep, cargo_src_test guard) after dropping pypy3_11.
Package works on Python 3.15 but its test suite cannot run there yet; also the GitHub tarball needed SETUPTOOLS_SCM_PRETEND_VERSION to build.
root_cause: Enabling a target and gating tests are separate concerns: PYTHON_COMPAT controls install targets while a PYTHON_TESTED helper array can restrict which impls run tests. GitHub archive tarballs lack the PKG-INFO/version metadata that pypi sdists carry, forcing SETUPTOOLS_SCM_PRETEND_VERSION hacks.
fix_pattern: Keep a PYTHON_TESTED array of impls whose tests pass and define PYTHON_COMPAT=( "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}" python3_15 ); switch SRC_URI from the GitHub archive to the pypi sdist (inherit pypi, delete SRC_URI) which lets the SETUPTOOLS_SCM_PRETEND_VERSION export be dropped.
rule: To enable a new Python target whose test deps are not ready, split PYTHON_TESTED from PYTHON_COMPAT; and prefer pypi sdists over GitHub archives for setuptools-scm projects so no pretend-version export is needed.
snippet:
PYTHON_TESTED=( python3_{12..14} )
PYTHON_COMPAT=( "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}" python3_15 )
inherit distutils-r1 pypi
# (drop SRC_URI + SETUPTOOLS_SCM_PRETEND_VERSION)

problem: USE=doc was broken (doxygen doc build fails, bug 961617) and nobody was going to fix the dead-upstream doc machinery.
root_cause: Optional doxygen documentation build bit-rotted upstream; keeping the IUSE flag ships a permanently broken feature.
fix_pattern: Remove doc from IUSE, drop the BDEPEND on doxygen, and replace $(use_with doc docs) with a hard --without-docs in the configure args; done in-place without revbump since installed files for existing users (doc disabled) do not change. Also tightened the live-ebuild guard from [[ ${PV} == *9999 ]] to *9999* .
rule: When an optional USE-controlled feature is broken upstream with no fix in sight, delete the flag and hard-disable the feature (--without-X) instead of leaving a known-broken IUSE; no revbump needed if default-off.
snippet:
-IUSE="debug doc test tools"
+IUSE="debug test tools"
-BDEPEND="doc? ( app-text/doxygen )"
	local myeconfargs=(
+		--without-docs
-		$(use_with doc docs)
	)

problem: Same broken-IUSE-doc removal as libzmf (bug 961616): drop doc flag, hard --without-docs, doxygen BDEPEND removed, *9999* glob fix.
HOMEPAGE updated from openssh.com to openssh.org; pure metadata churn.
Routine version bump to openssh 10.4_p1; the 437-line ebuild is a copy of the previous version (EOL-USE-flag pkg_pretend guard, musl utmp workaround, verify-sig all pre-existing).
py3.15 enablement plus the same GitHub-archive-to-pypi-sdist switch as dev-python/executing (inherit pypi, drop SRC_URI and SETUPTOOLS_SCM_PRETEND_VERSION).
py3.15 enablement needed supply-chain and test-environment care: switching to pypi sdists while keeping authenticity, and tests that depend on whether an optional package is installed on the host.
root_cause: GitHub archives were previously used partly for provenance; pypi now offers attestations, verified via PYPI_VERIFY_REPO which records a .provenance file in the Manifest. Some tests exercise pkg_resources and only pass if dev-python/pkg-resources happens to be installed.
fix_pattern: Set PYPI_VERIFY_REPO=<upstream github repo> with 'inherit pypi' to verify sdist provenance; guard environment-dependent test deselects with has_version, e.g. deselect the pkg_resources test when dev-python/pkg-resources is NOT installed; per-3.15 failures deselected via case ${EPYTHON}.
rule: When moving a dev-python package from GitHub tarballs to pypi sdists, add PYPI_VERIFY_REPO for provenance verification; and gate deselects of environment-sensitive tests with has_version checks rather than deselecting unconditionally.
snippet:
PYPI_VERIFY_REPO=https://github.com/pylint-dev/astroid
inherit distutils-r1 pypi
...
if ! has_version "dev-python/pkg-resources[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]"; then
	EPYTEST_DESELECT+=( tests/test_manager.py::AstroidManagerTest::test_identify_old_namespace_package_protocol )
fi

problem: The ebuild passed MOC/RCC/UIC paths from qt6_get_bindir to CMake, but Qt6 installs moc/rcc/uic under the libexec directory, so the paths pointed at binaries that are not really there.
root_cause: Qt6 moved developer host tools (moc, rcc, uic) from the bin dir to /usr/lib64/qt6/libexec; only user-facing tools like lrelease stay in the bin dir. The old qmake-utils eclass had no libexec getter.
fix_pattern: Migrate inherit from qmake-utils to qt-utils and use qt6_get_libexecdir for moc/rcc/uic while keeping qt6_get_bindir for lrelease.
rule: When passing Qt6 host tool paths to a build system, use qt6_get_libexecdir (qt-utils eclass) for moc/rcc/uic; qt6_get_bindir is only correct for user-facing tools like lrelease.
snippet:
inherit qt-utils # was: qmake-utils
$(usex gui LRELEASE=$(qt6_get_bindir)/lrelease '')
$(usex gui MOC=$(qt6_get_libexecdir)/moc '')
$(usex gui RCC=$(qt6_get_libexecdir)/rcc '')
$(usex gui UIC=$(qt6_get_libexecdir)/uic '')

problem: Ebuild hardcoded Qt6 install paths like /usr/$(get_libdir)/qt6/plugins and .../mkspecs in generated qmake config, duplicating knowledge of Qt's layout.
root_cause: Hardcoded Qt directory layout breaks silently if Qt changes its install layout and cannot follow prefix/libdir variations; the qt-utils eclass provides canonical getters.
fix_pattern: Replace hardcoded paths with qt-utils eclass helpers qt6_get_plugindir and qt6_get_mkspecsdir (prefixed with ${EPREFIX}).
rule: Never hardcode Qt6 subdirectory paths in ebuilds; use the qt-utils getters (qt6_get_plugindir, qt6_get_mkspecsdir, qt6_get_libexecdir, qt6_get_bindir) instead.

problem: One-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}, dropping pypy3_11.

Test suite fails to collect under pytest-9: 'Duplicate parametrization IDs detected, but strict_parametrization_ids is set' because parameter sets ("None", None) and (None, None) both stringify to id 'None-None'.
root_cause: pytest-9 turned duplicate auto-generated parametrize IDs from a silent tolerance into a hard collection error; the string "None" and the object None produce identical auto-IDs.
fix_pattern: Patch the test to give one colliding parameter set an explicit unique id via pytest.param(..., id=...); ship as FILESDIR patch with the upstream PR URL in a comment above PATCHES, alongside the PYTHON_COMPAT bump.
rule: When pytest-9 aborts collection with 'Duplicate parametrization IDs', wrap one of the colliding parameter sets in pytest.param(..., id="unique-id") and upstream the patch, referencing the PR URL in the ebuild.

problem: Test phase used the manual pytest plugin-isolation idiom: PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 plus explicit '-p aiohttp -p asyncio' flags, and duplicated the plugin (pytest-aiohttp) in test BDEPEND by hand.
root_cause: Ebuild predates the distutils-r1/epytest EPYTEST_PLUGINS variable; the manual idiom is verbose and lets declared BDEPEND drift out of sync with the -p flags actually passed.
fix_pattern: Set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-{aiohttp,asyncio} ) before distutils_enable_tests pytest; the eclass then disables plugin autoload, passes the -p flags, and injects the plugin test deps automatically, so the explicit plugin entry is removed from BDEPEND and python_test shrinks to just extra options.
rule: When a python ebuild sets PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 and -p flags manually, replace both (and the hand-written plugin BDEPEND) with EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( plugin... ) declared before distutils_enable_tests.
snippet:
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-{aiohttp,asyncio} )
 distutils_enable_tests pytest
 
 python_test() {
-	local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1
-	epytest -o addopts= -p aiohttp -p asyncio --asyncio-mode=auto
+	epytest -o addopts= --asyncio-mode=auto
 }

problem: python_test manually exported PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 to isolate the test run from installed pytest plugins.
root_cause: Manual plugin-autoload disabling predates the eclass-native mechanism; keeping it hand-rolled is redundant now that epytest understands EPYTEST_PLUGINS.
fix_pattern: Declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (empty array) before distutils_enable_tests: this tells epytest to disable plugin autoloading with no plugins loaded, replacing the manual export while keeping other python_test contents (env vars, -o addopts=).
rule: For test suites that need zero pytest plugins, set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() instead of exporting PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 yourself; an empty array still disables autoload.
snippet:
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=()
 distutils_enable_tests pytest
 
 python_test() {
 	local -x TZ=UTC
-	local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1
 	epytest -o addopts=
 }

problem: The hypothesis CLI supported fewer Python implementations than the library, so enabling py3.15 required moving it between two compat sets rather than editing one PYTHON_COMPAT line.
root_cause: Package has impl-dependent optional functionality (CLI entry point) whose dependencies lag behind the core library on new interpreters; a single PYTHON_COMPAT cannot express the split.
fix_pattern: Maintain a named sub-array (CLI_COMPAT) and compose PYTHON_COMPAT from it plus the extra impls; enabling the new impl for the CLI means moving python3_15 from the extras into the sub-array. Same idiom as selenium's PYTHON_TESTED array.
rule: When part of a package (CLI, tests, optional feature) supports only a subset of implementations, declare that subset as its own bash array and build PYTHON_COMPAT from it, so later bumps are a one-array edit and USE-conditional deps can reference the subset.

problem: A brand-new snapshot ebuild (0.5_p20260701) was committed with a stable 'x86' keyword while every other arch was ~testing; committer had to follow up demoting it to ~x86.
root_cause: Stable keyword copied over from the previous ebuild during the version bump instead of being demoted to testing; new versions must always start as ~arch.
fix_pattern: One-line KEYWORDS edit changing 'x86' to '~x86'; no revbump needed since the fix only widens testing status.
rule: After copying an ebuild for a version bump, verify KEYWORDS contains no stable entries (every token should start with ~); a stable keyword on a freshly added version is always a mistake unless it went through stabilization.

problem: Ebuild declared DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit although the project only needs the flit_core build backend, pulling the full flit tool as a build dep.
root_cause: flit and flit-core are distinct DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 values mapping to different BDEPENDs; upstream pyproject.toml uses flit_core.buildapi, so the heavier value was wrong/wasteful.
fix_pattern: Change DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit to flit-core (done here opportunistically while bumping PYTHON_COMPAT to python3_{12..15}).
rule: When pyproject.toml declares build-backend = 'flit_core.buildapi', set DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit-core, not flit, to avoid depending on the full flit frontend.

problem: Enabling py3.15 was combined with silently dropping python3_11 and pypy3_11 from PYTHON_COMPAT in the same one-line change.
root_cause: Gentoo's annual Python cycle retires old implementations (3.11, pypy3_11) at the same time new ones (3.15) are enabled; carrying dead impls in PYTHON_COMPAT blocks the tree-wide removal.
fix_pattern: Rewrite the compat range in one edit: PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{11..14} pypy3_11 ) becomes PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} ); nearly every commit in this sweep does the same, plus a copyright-year touch-up.
rule: When enabling a new Python implementation in PYTHON_COMPAT, drop implementations scheduled for tree removal in the same edit rather than leaving stale entries for a later pass.

problem: py3.15 enablement done via the PYTHON_TESTED sub-array feeding PYTHON_COMPAT (same subset-array idiom as hypothesis CLI_COMPAT).
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}, dropping pypy3_11; no package-specific insight.
Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}.
Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}.
Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}, dropping pypy3_11.
Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}.
Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}.
Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}.
PYTHON_COMPAT bump plus adding EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() to disable pytest plugin autoload (idiom covered by python-lsp-jsonrpc lesson).
PYTHON_COMPAT bump plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() addition (same empty-plugins idiom as python-lsp-jsonrpc/curtsies).
Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}, dropping pypy3_11.
Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}.
Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}.
Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}.
Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}.
Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}.
Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}.
Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}.
PYTHON_COMPAT bump to py3.15 left stale build backend and dead pypy-only test workarounds
root_cause: Ebuild used DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry (full poetry) where poetry-core suffices, and carried a python_test() with pypy3-only EPYTEST_DESELECT after pypy3_11 was dropped from PYTHON_COMPAT
fix_pattern: While enabling py3.15: switch DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry to poetry-core, drop pypy3_11 from PYTHON_COMPAT, and delete the whole python_test() whose only content was a pypy3* case deselect
rule: When adjusting PYTHON_COMPAT, audit the ebuild for now-dead per-implementation workarounds (pypy deselects, conditional deps) and prefer the -core PEP517 backend (poetry-core, flit-core) over the full tool

problem: After dropping pypy3 from PYTHON_COMPAT, python_gen_cond_dep wrappers targeting 'python*' were redundant
root_cause: $(python_gen_cond_dep '...dep...' 'python*') exists only to exclude pypy; once PYTHON_COMPAT contains only CPython the condition always matches
fix_pattern: Collapse the python_gen_cond_dep wrapper into a plain dependency with [${PYTHON_USEDEP}], and switch DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit to flit-core
rule: When pypy is removed from PYTHON_COMPAT, replace every python_gen_cond_dep block conditioned on 'python*' with the bare dependency it wraps

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to py3.15, drop pypy3_11; no other changes
Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{12..14} to {12..15}
pytest test suites can break or behave nondeterministically when random installed pytest plugins autoload during epytest
root_cause: distutils_enable_tests pytest historically ran pytest with all installed plugins autoloading; unrelated plugins (e.g. pytest-asyncio, coverage plugins) alter behavior or fail collection
fix_pattern: Declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (empty array) before distutils_enable_tests pytest so distutils-r1 disables pytest plugin autoloading; list required plugin packages in the array when tests actually need them
rule: In distutils-r1 ebuilds using pytest, always set EPYTEST_PLUGINS explicitly (empty if no plugins needed) so only intended plugins load during tests

problem: Ebuild referenced a ${FILESDIR} patch that was never committed, breaking src_prepare for every user until a follow-up commit added it
root_cause: Developer tested with a local files/ patch and pushed the ebuild change without git-adding the new patch file
fix_pattern: Follow-up commit adding the missing files/libpeas-1.38.1-disable-lua-test.patch; the real fix is process: run pkgcheck scan / check git status before pushing
rule: Before committing an ebuild that adds a PATCHES entry, verify the referenced files/ patch is staged in the same commit (pkgcheck flags missing FILESDIR files)
snippet: pkgcheck scan dev-libs/libpeas  # catches MissingFile/UnknownFile in files/

problem: Routine removal of python3_11 from PYTHON_COMPAT in two ebuilds
Lua extension tests failed because the lua LGI library has no girepository-2.0 support yet
root_cause: Test suite depends on an ecosystem component (LGI) that has not been ported to the new GNOME introspection ABI; failure is not fixable inside the package
fix_pattern: Apply the targeted upstream patch that comments out only the lua test sources in tests/meson.build, keeping the rest of the test suite active, and cite the upstream issue URL in a PATCHES comment
rule: When tests fail due to an unported third-party ecosystem dependency, backport/borrow the upstream patch disabling only those tests instead of RESTRICT=test for the whole suite, and link the upstream tracking issue

problem: Backported patch lacked provenance and used ${P} in its FILESDIR reference; PYTHON_COMPAT still listed python3_11 which deps no longer support
root_cause: Patch added during version bump without noting which upstream commit it comes from, making future bumps hard to audit
fix_pattern: Rename patch file to explicit ${PN}-<version>-name.patch, reference it with the literal version, and add a comment above the PATCHES entry with the upstream commit URL; drop obsolete PYTHON_COMPAT entry
rule: Annotate every backported patch with its upstream commit URL in a comment so the next bumper can tell if it is already merged and can be dropped

problem: Routine version bump 1.16.12 carrying a one-line upstream assertion-fix patch; package-specific
Test deselections applied only to python3.14 also needed for python3.15 when enabling the new target
root_cause: Per-implementation EPYTEST_DESELECT used an exact-version case pattern, so a new CPython version with the same failures was not covered
fix_pattern: Widen the case glob in python_test() from python3.14*) to python3.1[45]*) so the same deselect list covers both versions
rule: When the same test failures affect consecutive CPython versions, use a bracket glob in the python_test case statement instead of duplicating the deselect block

problem: Package with vendored Rust crates failed to build for users with an older Rust toolchain (bug 977862) because RUST_MIN_VER was stale
root_cause: Upstream (or a vendored crate) raised its MSRV during a version bump but the ebuild's RUST_MIN_VER was not updated, so the rust eclass accepted a too-old compiler
fix_pattern: Bump RUST_MIN_VER to the real minimum supported Rust version so the eclass dependency resolution requires a new-enough virtual/rust
rule: On every bump of a Rust or maturin package, re-check the MSRV (rust-version in Cargo.toml and crate build errors) and keep RUST_MIN_VER in sync

problem: CMake install dir for Qt mkspecs was wrong on Gentoo Prefix because $(qt6_get_mkspecsdir) returns a path without EPREFIX
root_cause: Eclass path helper outputs the ROOT-relative path; passing it verbatim as an absolute -D install dir loses the prefix offset
fix_pattern: Prepend "${EPREFIX}" to the helper output when passing it to mycmakeargs
rule: When feeding eclass *_get_*dir helper output to a build system as an absolute install path, check whether the helper includes EPREFIX and prepend it if not

problem: clang 23.x build (docs) started referencing files under mlir/utils/pygments which were not in the fetched monorepo subset
root_cause: Packages that fetch a sparse subset of a monorepo (LLVM_COMPONENTS) break when a new version's build system reaches into a previously-unfetched subdirectory
fix_pattern: Add the newly required subdirectory to the LLVM_COMPONENTS array so the component tarball/sparse checkout includes it
rule: For monorepo-subset packages, treat missing-file configure/build errors after a bump as a signal to extend the component/sparse-checkout list, not to patch the build system
snippet: LLVM_COMPONENTS=( clang clang-tools-extra cmake mlir/utils/pygments utils/docs )

problem: Snapshot ebuild (GitHub archive tarball, no git-r3 inherit) used EGIT_COMMIT to hold the pinned commit hash
root_cause: EGIT_COMMIT is a git-r3 eclass-reserved variable; setting it in an ebuild that does not use git-r3 violates the eclass API namespace and trips pkgcheck
fix_pattern: Revbump renaming the variable to a plain COMMIT (non-reserved) used in SRC_URI and S
rule: Never set EGIT_*/other eclass-reserved variables in ebuilds that do not inherit that eclass; use a neutral name like COMMIT or MY_COMMIT for pinned snapshot hashes

problem: An old version needed by a reverse-dep pin (<qhexedit2-0.9.0 for ponyprog) had been removed, but the remaining revdep could not move to 0.9.0
root_cause: Treecleaning old versions without checking reverse-dependency version pins; the old version also lacked PyQt6/newer-python support
fix_pattern: Partially revert the removal by re-adding the old version as a new revision (-r2) carrying an upstream PyQt6-compat backport patch, and drop stable keywords on the re-added ebuild
rule: Before dropping an old version, grep the tree for reverse-dep pins on it; if one exists, keep or re-add the version (as a fresh revision with needed compat patches) rather than leaving the revdep unsatisfiable

problem: Flaky ctest (kfiltertest) intermittently failing on test infrastructure
root_cause: Nondeterministic upstream test; not fixable at packaging level
fix_pattern: Add the test name to the cmake.eclass CMAKE_SKIP_TESTS array with the Gentoo bug number as an inline comment per entry
rule: For flaky or known-broken ctest cases in cmake-based packages, list them in CMAKE_SKIP_TESTS with one '# bug NNNNNN' comment per test instead of restricting all tests

problem: Build failure with Perl 5.44: 'Error: unrecognised line: #include ... (possible start of a truncated XSUB definition?)' when compiling .xs files
root_cause: ExtUtils::ParseXS >=3.58 (shipped with Perl 5.44) parses XS syntax more strictly and rejects a preprocessor directive placed directly after a MODULE line without a blank line between them
fix_pattern: Backport the upstream one-line patch inserting an empty line between the MODULE = ... line and the following #include in the .xs file; revbump; also rename carried patches to the ${P}- prefix convention
rule: When a package with XS code breaks on Perl 5.44+ with 'unrecognised line ... truncated XSUB definition', insert a blank line between MODULE/PACKAGE declarations and any following preprocessor directive in the .xs source

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump {11..14} to {12..15}
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump {11..14} to {12..15}
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump {12..14} to {12..15}
Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to py3.15
PYTHON_COMPAT bump with flit-to-flit-core backend switch and pypy3_11 drop; same pattern as cleo/cairocffi records
Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to py3.15
Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to py3.15
Package not enabled for new CPython 3.15; old PYTHON_COMPAT still listed py3.11, pypy3_11 and old freethreading variants.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT is a static list; each new CPython release requires per-package opt-in after testing, and impls falling out of the supported window should be dropped in the same pass.
fix_pattern: Single-commit compat rotation: python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}, freethreading python3_{13,14}t -> python3_{14..15}t, drop pypy3_11 when it lags; also add EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests to run tests with plugin autoloading disabled.
rule: When enabling a new CPython version in PYTHON_COMPAT, rotate the whole window in one commit: raise the floor, drop stale pypy/freethreaded variants, and update the t-variant range to match.
snippet:
-PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{11..14} python3_{13,14}t pypy3_11 )
+PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} python3_{14..15}t )
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=()
 distutils_enable_tests pytest

problem: Dropping old version 6.6.5-r2 would silently de-keyword ~loong, which only the old ebuild carried.
root_cause: Newer ebuild had been added before the ~loong keywording of the older one, so KEYWORDS diverged; removing the only version keyworded for an arch removes that arch's coverage entirely.
fix_pattern: In the same commit that deletes the old ebuild, diff KEYWORDS between old and new versions and copy any missing keywords (~loong here) onto the surviving ebuild; also drop the now-unused patchset DIST from Manifest.
rule: Before deleting an old ebuild version, diff its KEYWORDS against the newest version and carry forward any keyword the newer ebuild lacks, otherwise an arch silently loses the package.

problem: Known-broken tests (locale-dependent output, outdated comparison files, segfault-on-exit, hangs) failed again in the new 26.04.3 release.
root_cause: Upstream test breakage persists across releases; test skips applied to one version were not ported to the newer ebuild.
fix_pattern: Maintain a CMAKE_SKIP_TESTS array in src_test with one comment line per group explaining WHY each test is skipped and citing bug numbers; extend the same list in the new version's ebuild when failures recur (bugs 967519, 977513).
rule: For cmake-based packages, skip broken tests via a commented CMAKE_SKIP_TESTS array citing bug numbers, and port that list forward when bumping if the failures persist.

problem: Sphinx doc build dependency was wrong: docs no longer build with dev-python/recommonmark (bug 978300).
root_cause: recommonmark is dead upstream and its Sphinx markdown role was taken over by myst-parser; upstream docs config switched but the ebuild's distutils_enable_sphinx dep list was stale.
fix_pattern: Swap the extra dependency argument of distutils_enable_sphinx from dev-python/recommonmark to dev-python/myst-parser to match what upstream's docs/conf.py actually imports.
rule: When a Sphinx doc build fails on a markdown extension, check upstream conf.py extensions and update the distutils_enable_sphinx dep list; recommonmark usages migrate to myst-parser.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT rotation python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT rotation to python3_{12..15}; one-line change.
While enabling py3.15, the ebuild still applied patches by overriding python_prepare_all with a local PATCHES array, and tests ran serially.
root_cause: Old distutils-r1 style predates global PATCHES support and the eclass xdist knob; a python_prepare_all override just to set PATCHES is needless boilerplate.
fix_pattern: Replace the python_prepare_all override with a top-level PATCHES=( ... ) array and add EPYTEST_XDIST=1 next to EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() so the eclass runs pytest under pytest-xdist.
rule: In distutils-r1 ebuilds, declare patches in a global PATCHES array instead of overriding python_prepare_all, and set EPYTEST_XDIST=1 to parallelize test suites that tolerate it.

problem: Test-only pytest plugin (pytest-trio) was hand-listed in BDEPEND under test?, while EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() told the eclass no plugins were needed, so the plugin was installed but never loaded.
root_cause: distutils-r1/epytest grew the EPYTEST_PLUGINS mechanism: naming plugins there both generates the test? () BDEPEND automatically and loads exactly those plugins with PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD; duplicating deps manually is now an anti-pattern and can desync from what actually loads.
fix_pattern: Delete the manual test? ( dev-python/pytest-trio ) BDEPEND block and declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-trio ) before distutils_enable_tests pytest; the eclass adds the dependency and the -p load flag itself.
rule: In distutils-r1 ebuilds, declare required pytest plugins in EPYTEST_PLUGINS (before distutils_enable_tests pytest) instead of hand-writing test? BDEPEND entries; use EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() when no plugins are needed.

problem: RDEPEND used python_gen_cond_dep to add pyasynchat/pyasyncore only for 3.12/3.13/3.14, which after the compat rotation covered every remaining implementation.
root_cause: asynchat/asyncore were removed from CPython stdlib in 3.12, so the deps were conditional while 3.11 was supported; raising the PYTHON_COMPAT floor to 3.12 made the condition universally true.
fix_pattern: Flatten the python_gen_cond_dep wrapper into plain [${PYTHON_USEDEP}] dependencies once the version condition matches the whole PYTHON_COMPAT set; also drop pypy3_11 from compat in the same commit.
rule: After raising the PYTHON_COMPAT floor, audit python_gen_cond_dep blocks and inline any whose version list now covers all supported implementations.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT rotation to python3_{12..15}; one-line change.
Ebuild overrode python_test just to export PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 before calling epytest.
root_cause: Before EPYTEST_PLUGINS existed, disabling pytest plugin autoloading required a manual python_test override; the eclass now handles it declaratively.
fix_pattern: Delete the python_test override and set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest; the empty array means run with autoload disabled and no extra plugins.
rule: Replace python_test overrides that only set PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 with a declarative EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() line.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT rotation to python3_{12..15} for a scikit-build-core extension; one-line change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT rotation to python3_{12..15}; one-line change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT rotation to python3_{12..15}; one-line change.
New Go release bundles libsass via cgo with hardcoded -O2 and builds against vendored libsass sources; tests need network; large disk use during test builds.
root_cause: Go vendoring ships C library sources inside vendor/ with cgo directives that hardcode optimizer flags and include paths, ignoring system libs and user CFLAGS; Go test suites commonly hit the network (module fetches, npm registry).
fix_pattern: Ship a dedicated vendor tarball from the gentoo-golang-dist GitHub org in SRC_URI; unbundle the C lib by patching the vendored cgo stanza to drop -O2/-I../libsass_src and add -lsass, with RDEPEND on dev-libs/libsass:= under the USE that enables it; export CGO_CFLAGS/CGO_LDFLAGS from user flags in src_configure; rm network-dependent *_test.go files in src_prepare; gate disk needs with check-reqs (larger CHECKREQS_DISK_BUILD when FEATURES=test).
rule: To unbundle a C library from a Go package, patch the vendored package's #cgo directives to link the system lib (-lname) and remove hardcoded optimization/include flags, then propagate user flags via CGO_* exports.
snippet:
-// #cgo CFLAGS: -O2 -fPIC
-// #cgo CPPFLAGS: -I../../libsass_src/include
+// #cgo CFLAGS: -fPIC
+// #cgo CPPFLAGS: -DUSE_LIBSASS_SRC
-// #cgo LDFLAGS: -lstdc++ -lm
+// #cgo LDFLAGS: -lstdc++ -lm -lsass

problem: The kmscon man page was only installed with USE=doc, so default installs shipped no man page.
root_cause: The meson 'docs' option (which builds the man page via docbook-xsl/xsltproc) had been conflated with API documentation and wrongly gated behind a doc USE flag with a bogus gtk-doc BDEPEND.
fix_pattern: Drop the doc USE flag, force -Ddocs=enabled in src_configure, and move the real toolchain deps (app-text/docbook-xsl-stylesheets, dev-libs/libxslt) into DEPEND; remove the incorrect gtk-doc BDEPEND.
rule: Man pages are essential documentation: install them unconditionally rather than behind a doc USE flag, and depend on the actual doc toolchain (docbook-xsl + libxslt for xsltproc-generated pages), not a guessed one.

problem: Trio-based tests need pytest-trio, whose dep chain pulls in a Rust toolchain — too heavy to force on every test run, but silently skipping means losing coverage.
root_cause: Optional heavy test-only dependencies create a tradeoff between mandatory expensive BDEPEND and lost test coverage; needs an explicit user-visible knob.
fix_pattern: Add a dedicated USE flag (test-rust) guarding the heavy plugin inside test? ( ... ) BDEPEND, declare the always-needed plugin via EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-asyncio ), then in python_test probe with has_version: if the plugin is present, extend a local EPYTEST_PLUGINS copy; otherwise add the affected test file to EPYTEST_IGNORE.
rule: Gate test-only dependencies with heavy toolchains (rust etc.) behind a dedicated test-* USE flag, and in python_test branch on has_version to either load the plugin or EPYTEST_IGNORE the tests that need it.
snippet:
IUSE="test-rust"
BDEPEND="test? ( test-rust? ( dev-python/pytest-trio[${PYTHON_USEDEP}] ) )"
python_test() {
	local EPYTEST_IGNORE=()
	if has_version "dev-python/pytest-trio[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]"; then
		local EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( "${EPYTEST_PLUGINS[@]}" pytest-trio )
	else
		EPYTEST_IGNORE+=( jeepney/io/tests/test_trio.py )
	fi
	epytest
}

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT rotation to python3_{12..15}; one-line change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT rotation to python3_{12..15}; one-line change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT rotation incl. freethreading variants python3_{13,14}t -> python3_{14..15}t; same idiom as pyxattr.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT rotation to python3_{12..15}; copyright year touch-up only.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT rotation to python3_{12..15}; one-line change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT rotation to python3_{12..15}; one-line change.
Ebuild declared DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry while the actual build backend is the lightweight poetry-core.
root_cause: distutils-r1 distinguishes the poetry-core backend value from full poetry; using the heavier/legacy value pulls the wrong build dependency for packages whose pyproject build-backend is poetry.core.masonry.api.
fix_pattern: Change DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry to DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry-core during the compat bump so BDEPEND matches the declared backend.
rule: Set DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 to the exact backend named in pyproject.toml's build-system table; for poetry.core.masonry.api that is poetry-core, not poetry.

problem: python_test manually exported PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 and passed -p pytest_mock, and pytest-mock was hand-listed in test? BDEPEND.
root_cause: Pre-EPYTEST_PLUGINS boilerplate: plugin dependency, autoload disabling and -p loading were maintained in three separate places.
fix_pattern: Declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-mock ) before distutils_enable_tests pytest, drop the BDEPEND entry, and reduce python_test to just epytest with the package-specific -o option; the eclass injects the dep and -p flags.
rule: When python_test passes -p <plugin> plus PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD manually, migrate the plugin names into EPYTEST_PLUGINS and delete the duplicated BDEPEND and env handling.
snippet:
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-mock )
 distutils_enable_tests pytest
 python_test() {
-	local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1
-	epytest -p pytest_mock -o tmp_path_retention_policy=all
+	epytest -o tmp_path_retention_policy=all
 }

problem: llvm-23.0.0.9999 build started failing because the sparse checkout driven by LLVM_COMPONENTS no longer contained files the build newly references (mlir/utils/pygments).
root_cause: llvm.org.eclass fetches only the monorepo subdirectories listed in LLVM_COMPONENTS; upstream added a build-time reference into a subtree not in the list, so the file was simply absent from ${S}.
fix_pattern: Add the missing subdirectory to the LLVM_COMPONENTS array: LLVM_COMPONENTS=( llvm cmake libc mlir/utils/pygments third-party utils/docs ).
rule: When a monorepo package fetched via a sparse component list (LLVM_COMPONENTS or similar) errors on a missing in-tree file, add that subtree path to the component list instead of patching the reference out.
snippet: LLVM_COMPONENTS=( llvm cmake libc mlir/utils/pygments third-party utils/docs )

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT extension from python3_{12..14} to python3_{12..15}; no workarounds needed.
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT bump; also dropped an obsolete python_compile override that deleted stray *.pth files from BUILD_DIR (package-specific cleanup).

Enabling python3.15 hit new stdlib-behaviour test failures (exception message differences) on top of the existing python3.14 failures, and the ebuild still used the manual PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD export.
root_cause: CPython minor releases change ElementTree/exception message details, so tests that assert exact messages fail per-interpreter; the manual plugin-autoload export was superseded by the eclass EPYTEST_PLUGINS mechanism.
fix_pattern: Use a case on ${EPYTHON} with the ;& fall-through operator so the 3.15 branch accumulates its own EPYTEST_DESELECT entries and then falls into the 3.14 branch, and declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests instead of exporting PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 in python_test.
rule: For per-interpreter test failures that stack across Python versions, use case ${EPYTHON} with ;& fall-through to accumulate EPYTEST_DESELECT, and prefer EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() over manually exporting PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD.

problem: LLVM release builds with the default LLVM_UNREACHABLE_OPTIMIZE=ON turn llvm_unreachable() into undefined behavior, producing garbage crashes instead of deterministic aborts (bug 978856).
root_cause: Upstream CMake option LLVM_UNREACHABLE_OPTIMIZE defaults to optimizing unreachable paths in release mode; distro builds want internal errors to trap reliably for debuggability and safety.
fix_pattern: Add -DLLVM_UNREACHABLE_OPTIMIZE=OFF to mycmakeargs in multilib_src_configure.
rule: When packaging LLVM or LLVM forks, pass -DLLVM_UNREACHABLE_OPTIMIZE=OFF so llvm_unreachable aborts deterministically instead of becoming UB in release builds.

problem: cffi test suite failed in the Portage sandbox because test_meson_python_build downloads build deps from the network (bug 978877).
root_cause: Portage builds run with network-sandbox; any test that fetches from the Internet (here a meson-python build pulling wheels) can never pass.
fix_pattern: Add the test to EPYTEST_DESELECT with an '# Internet' comment and a link to the upstream issue asking for an offline marker; keep the pip-calling tests in EPYTEST_IGNORE separately.
rule: Deselect any test that needs network access via EPYTEST_DESELECT, annotate why (Internet) and link the upstream issue so the deselect can be dropped later.

problem: The -9999 live ebuild had drifted from the release ebuilds (old EAPI, dead-end SRC_URI logic that hardcoded one COMMIT and died for any other PV) and used a fragile gitweb dynamic snapshot URL.
root_cause: Upstream has no stable release tarball hosting; the old ebuild generated snapshots from gitweb (index.cgi?...;a=snapshot;h=COMMIT;sf=tgz), which produces unstable archives and requires per-version commit pinning.
fix_pattern: Sync the 9999 ebuild to the current template: bump EAPI, and for releases switch SRC_URI to a maintainer-mirrored fixed tarball (https://dev.gentoo.org/~dev/distfiles/mirrored/${P}.tar.xz) instead of gitweb snapshot generation.
rule: Avoid dynamic gitweb/cgit snapshot SRC_URIs (checksums are not reproducible); mirror a fixed tarball yourself, and periodically re-sync -9999 ebuilds with the newest release ebuild.

problem: New dropbear 2026.92 test suite exercises dropbearconvert against OpenSSH's ssh-keygen, failing without openssh installed, and one parametrized test can never pass (ssh-keygen has no PEM output for ed25519).
root_cause: Upstream tests shell out to external tools not declared as dependencies, and one test asserts a capability the external tool lacks (upstream issue filed).
fix_pattern: Add the external tool to test-conditional BDEPEND respecting USE interactions: test? ( !minimal? ( net-misc/openssh ) ); for the unfixable test add a small FILESDIR patch inserting pytest.skip with the reason, rather than deleting the whole test file.
rule: When a test suite invokes external programs, declare them under test? ( ) BDEPEND; for genuinely unpassable individual tests, patch in a pytest.skip with a reason and report upstream instead of removing the file.
snippet:
BDEPEND="test? ( !minimal? ( net-misc/openssh ) )"
# files/${P}-skip-failing-convert-test.patch:
+if keytype == "ed25519" and keyformat == "PEM":
+	pytest.skip("ssh-keygen does not support PEM format for type ed25519")

problem: webkit-gtk was broken on ppc64 by the same vendored-skcms clang musttail miscompile already worked around on riscv (bug 970556, webkit bug 305745).
root_cause: The bundled skcms library enables [[clang::musttail]] based on compile-time detection that is wrong on some architectures; the failure signature on ppc64 matched the known riscv one.
fix_pattern: Extend the existing arch guard rather than duplicating logic: if use riscv || use ppc64; then append-cppflags -DSKCMS_HAS_MUSTTAIL=0; fi, applied consistently across all live revisions, citing both bug URLs.
rule: When a vendored library exposes a preprocessor feature knob, prefer append-cppflags -DKNOB=0 per affected arch over patching, and when a second arch shows the same failure signature, widen the existing use-conditional instead of adding a parallel block.
snippet:
if use riscv || use ppc64; then
	# https://bugs.gentoo.org/970556
	# https://bugs.webkit.org/show_bug.cgi?id=305745
	append-cppflags -DSKCMS_HAS_MUSTTAIL=0
fi

problem: PostgreSQL 14-16 shipped an upstream regression that can self-deadlock replicas when replaying WAL from an older minor version.
root_cause: Upstream backpatch (commit 2bb60eb4fe et al.) landed after the packaged minor releases; waiting for the next minor release would leave users exposed to a serious runtime bug.
fix_pattern: Fetch the official upstream patch per branch, store as files/${P}-replicadeadlock.patch with the full upstream commit header/URL preserved for provenance, add PATCHES=( "${FILESDIR}/${P}-replicadeadlock.patch" ), and rename each ebuild to -r1.
rule: When cherry-picking an upstream fix that changes runtime behavior, keep the upstream commit header in the patch file for provenance and always revbump (-rN) so installed systems rebuild.

problem: libssh2 1.11.1 was affected by three CVEs (CVE-2026-7598, CVE-2026-15661, CVE-2026-55200) with fixes only in upstream git, not in any release.
root_cause: Upstream security fixes land as individual commits well before a release; the tree must not wait for the next tarball.
fix_pattern: Create a -r1 revision whose PATCHES array adds one patch per CVE, each file named ${PN}-${PV}-CVE-XXXX-*.patch and headed by the upstream commit URL plus the Gentoo bug comment link; the new revision starts life with ~arch keywords for the normal stabilization cycle.
rule: For CVE backports, ship one patch file per CVE named after it, put the upstream commit URL and bug reference as the first lines of each patch, and revbump the ebuild.

problem: A ~loong keyword drop applied to nuitka-4.1.2 had to be re-applied to 4.1.3, which was added in flight with the old KEYWORDS line; keyword changes must be propagated to every live version.
cpp-httplib 0.38.0 no longer works on 32-bit systems, so its ~arm/arm, ~ppc, x86 keywords had to be removed (bug 973312), stranding every reverse dependency on those arches.
root_cause: Upstream dropped 32-bit support in a new version; keywords must reflect actual arch support, and the dependency tree must stay keyword-consistent.
fix_pattern: Remove the affected keywords from the new version's KEYWORDS, then dekeyword the whole reverse-dependency chain (indilib, kstars, openrgb, nuitka-via-ccache) in the same PR, each commit referencing the same tracker bug 973312.
rule: When a library loses an arch keyword, sweep all reverse dependencies on that arch in the same change set and reference one tracker bug, otherwise the tree has unsatisfiable deps that pkgcheck/CI will flag.
snippet:
-KEYWORDS="~alpha amd64 ~arm arm64 ~hppa ~loong ~mips ~ppc ppc64 ~riscv ~s390 ~sparc x86"
+KEYWORDS="~alpha amd64 arm64 ~hppa ~loong ~mips ppc64 ~riscv ~s390 ~sparc"

problem: Transitive dekeyword of ppc/x86 across all indilib versions because dependency cpp-httplib dropped 32-bit support (bug 973312 cluster).
Transitive ~x86 dekeyword (including the 9999 ebuild) as part of the cpp-httplib 32-bit drop cluster, bug 973312.
Second-level transitive dekeyword: kstars lost x86 because indilib lost it because cpp-httplib dropped 32-bit support (bug 973312 cluster).
nuitka lost its ~loong keyword even though nuitka itself works there, because its test-only dependency chain (ccache, which depends unconditionally on cpp-httplib) became unkeywordable on loong.
root_cause: Keywording requires all dependencies to be resolvable on the arch, including USE=test-conditional BDEPEND; a keyword loss deep in a test-dep chain therefore propagates up to consumers.
fix_pattern: Remove ~loong from KEYWORDS with a commit message explaining the full dep chain (nuitka -> ccache -> cpp-httplib) and referencing both bugs, so the keyword can be restored when the chain is fixed.
rule: Test-only dependencies gate KEYWORDS too: if a test BDEPEND cannot be keyworded on an arch, either dekeyword the package or restructure the test deps, and document the chain in the commit message for future re-keywording.

problem: restic 0.19.0 failed to build on systems without a new-enough Go toolchain because the ebuild's BDEPEND lacked an explicit dev-lang/go entry matching what the sources require (bug 977617).
root_cause: The Go version floor implied by the eclass (or by having go merely present) was below the toolchain version the release's go.mod requires, so builds broke on hosts with older go.
fix_pattern: Add an explicit versioned compiler dependency: BDEPEND=">=dev-lang/go-1.25.8 ..." alongside the existing verify-sig entry.
rule: For Go packages, set BDEPEND on >=dev-lang/go-X.Y.Z matching the go/toolchain directive in the release's go.mod rather than relying on the eclass default floor.

problem: The amule 9999 ebuild carried two downstream patches (disable version check, xdg-open preview) that no longer applied or were superseded, and hard-depended on sys-libs/binutils-libs which is only needed for BFD backtraces.
root_cause: Upstream's new CMake build added first-class options (-DDEFAULT_VERSION_CHECK, -DENABLE_BFD) covering what the downstream patches and unconditional dependency used to handle.
fix_pattern: Drop the PATCHES array in favor of -DDEFAULT_VERSION_CHECK=NO in mycmakeargs, add IUSE=bfd, move sys-libs/binutils-libs behind bfd? ( ), and wire -DENABLE_BFD="$(usex bfd)".
rule: On live-ebuild syncs and bumps, re-check whether carried patches have become upstream configure options, and convert niche unconditional dependencies into USE-conditional ones wired to the new options.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} for a python-single-r1 consumer; no workarounds needed.
zbar's GTK widget uses X11-specific gdk_x11 API, but x11-libs/gtk+:3 can be built with USE=-X where that API surface is absent, causing build/ABI breakage against non-X gtk (bug 957827).
root_cause: A dependency whose USE flag changes its exposed API/ABI (gtk+ X backend) was declared without forwarding the consumer's matching USE flag, so gtk+[-X] satisfied the dep while missing the symbols zbar uses.
fix_pattern: Forward the flag in the dependency atom with a conditional USE dep, gate the X11 code path at compile time when X is off, and revbump: x11-libs/gtk+:3[${MULTILIB_USEDEP},introspection?,X?] plus 'use X || append-cppflags -DGENTOO_GTK_HIDE_X11' (macro provided by Gentoo's gtk+ patching).
rule: When your package uses backend-specific API of a toolkit (gdk_x11_*, wayland-specific, etc.), forward that backend USE flag in the dependency atom ([X?]) and compile the code path out when the flag is off; a dep-behavior change like this needs a revbump.
snippet:
IUSE="X ..."
COMMON_DEPEND="gtk? ( x11-libs/gtk+:3[${MULTILIB_USEDEP},introspection?,X?] )"
multilib_src_configure() {
	# bug #957827
	use X || append-cppflags -DGENTOO_GTK_HIDE_X11
	...
}

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; no new workarounds needed.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; no new workarounds needed.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; no new workarounds needed.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} for python-any-r1 doc tooling; no new workarounds needed.
Boilerplate test-only BDEPEND block using python_gen_cond_dep for a single pytest plugin (pytest-mock).
root_cause: Before distutils-r1 grew EPYTEST_PLUGINS, every pytest plugin a test suite needed had to be spelled out manually in BDEPEND under test? with python_gen_cond_dep (extra noise in DISTUTILS_SINGLE_IMPL packages).
fix_pattern: Delete the manual test BDEPEND block and declare the plugin list in the EPYTEST_PLUGINS array before calling distutils_enable_tests pytest; the eclass then generates the test dependencies (and enables strict plugin autoloading control).
rule: When a dev-python ebuild lists pytest plugins in a test? BDEPEND block, migrate them into EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( plugin-names-without-dev-python-prefix ) placed before distutils_enable_tests pytest.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window bump python3_{11..14}+pypy3_11 to python3_{12..15}; note the old floor and pypy3_11 were dropped in the same edit.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} for a python-any-r1 build-time consumer; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} plus copyright year; nothing package-transferable.
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{12..14} to python3_{12..15}.
EPYTEST_IGNORE/EPYTEST_DESELECT arrays set inside python_test without local, leaking into the environment across implementations/phases.
root_cause: EPYTEST_* variables read by epytest are ordinary shell variables; assigning them non-local inside python_test pollutes global scope and can accumulate or leak between per-impl test runs.
fix_pattern: Prefix the array assignments inside python_test with local; also note the documented reason for skips (scp/sftp/channel tests need a login-capable user to ssh into, but portage-created users are nologin).
rule: When setting EPYTEST_IGNORE/EPYTEST_DESELECT (or any EPYTEST_* array) inside python_test, declare them local so they do not leak outside the function.

problem: One-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} on a poetry PEP517 package.
Test suite broke under newer pytest (pytest 9): conftest.py defined pytest_report_header(startdir), a hook signature pytest removed.
root_cause: pytest majors periodically drop deprecated hook arguments; suites with old-style conftest hooks fail collection even when the package code itself is fine, blocking new-Python enablement.
fix_pattern: Backport the already-merged/open upstream fix as a FILESDIR patch, citing the upstream PR URL in a comment above the PATCHES entry; also set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() to explicitly declare the suite needs no plugins (disabling plugin autoloading).
rule: When a test suite fails after a pytest major bump, check conftest.py for removed hook signatures, backport the upstream one-liner as a FILESDIR patch with the PR URL in a comment, and declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS (empty array if none needed).

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}, dropping pypy3_11.
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{12..14} to python3_{12..15}.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} plus copyright year.
A few individual ctest cases fail or hang (locale-dependent output, segfault on exit, stuck spawned processes) while the rest of the large KDE test suite is fine.
root_cause: Big Qt/KDE suites routinely contain environment-sensitive tests (locale, D-Bus, spawned helpers); restricting all tests would lose coverage.
fix_pattern: Maintain a CMAKE_SKIP_TESTS array local to src_test listing only the broken test names, with a one-line comment per group explaining why and citing bug numbers; combine with a ctest timeout for tests that hang after passing.
rule: For cmake/KDE packages with a handful of broken tests, skip them individually via a commented local CMAKE_SKIP_TESTS array in src_test (with bug references) instead of RESTRICT=test.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump on a package where PYTHON_COMPAT exists only for the test suite (commented as such); no reusable content beyond the bump.
sec-policy/selinux-docker was listed in BDEPEND, so SELinux systems could end up without the policy module installed at runtime (and binpkg consumers never pull it).
root_cause: SELinux policy packages are needed on the running system where the daemon runs, not on the build host; dependency class was misassigned.
fix_pattern: Move selinux? ( sec-policy/selinux-docker ) from BDEPEND to RDEPEND and rename the ebuilds to -r1 (revbump), because a runtime-dependency change must reach already-installed users.
rule: SELinux policy (sec-policy/*) and anything the installed program needs at runtime belongs in RDEPEND, and moving a dep into RDEPEND requires a revbump so existing installs re-resolve it.
snippet:
RDEPEND="${DEPEND}
	>=app-containers/runc-1.3.6[apparmor?,seccomp?]
+	selinux? ( sec-policy/selinux-docker )
"
BDEPEND="
-	selinux? ( sec-policy/selinux-docker )
	dev-go/go-md2man
"

problem: Flaky network-dependent tests fail intermittently, and a new Python implementation had to be installable before its test run was trusted.
root_cause: Some suites are timing/network sensitive; and during a new-CPython rollout you often want PYTHON_COMPAT to include the new impl while tests only run on vetted ones.
fix_pattern: Use the PYTHON_TESTED/PYTHON_COMPAT split (PYTHON_COMPAT=( "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}" python3_15 ) while unvetted, later folding the new impl into PYTHON_TESTED), and set local EPYTEST_RERUNS=5 together with the pytest-rerunfailures plugin in EPYTEST_PLUGINS to auto-retry flaky tests.
rule: To ship a package on a new Python before its test suite is vetted, keep a PYTHON_TESTED array separate from PYTHON_COMPAT and gate python_test on it; for known-flaky tests set EPYTEST_RERUNS with the pytest-rerunfailures plugin instead of deselecting them.
snippet:
PYTHON_TESTED=( python3_{12..15} )
PYTHON_COMPAT=( "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}" )
...
python_test() {
	local EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-{asyncio,httpserver,rerunfailures} )
	local EPYTEST_RERUNS=5
	epytest
}

problem: Folded python3_15 from COMPAT-only into PYTHON_TESTED once its test run was vetted (same PYTHON_TESTED idiom as truststore); dropped py3.11/pypy3_11.
Same PYTHON_TESTED fold for py3.15; freethreading variants python3_{14..15}t kept in PYTHON_COMPAT only (installable but untested).
Ebuild used the legacy DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit value even though the actual PEP517 backend is flit_core.
root_cause: distutils-r1 historically accepted 'flit' as the name for the flit_core backend; the eclass now prefers the accurate 'flit-core' value and 'flit' is a legacy alias slated for removal.
fix_pattern: Rename DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit to flit-core while touching the ebuild for the py3.15 PYTHON_TESTED fold.
rule: Use DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit-core (the real backend package) rather than the legacy 'flit' alias when the pyproject build-backend is flit_core.buildapi.

problem: Routine PYTHON_TESTED fold of py3.15 for setuptools; header comment reminds to bump dev-python/ensurepip-setuptools in lockstep.

zig-9999 live ebuild stopped building: upstream replaced the zig build --zig-lib-dir flag with the ZIG_LIB_DIR env var, and a --prefix regression made relative --prefix-lib-dir resolve against CWD instead of the install prefix.
root_cause: Live (9999) ebuilds track upstream build-system churn; upstream superseded a CLI flag with an environment variable and separately introduced an install-path regression (relative dirs no longer resolved against prefix, diverging from CMake/Autotools/Meson convention).
fix_pattern: Replace the removed flag with 'export ZIG_LIB_DIR="${S}/lib/"' in src_configure, and carry a FILESDIR patch for the prefix regression whose header cites the pending upstream PR so it can be dropped when merged.
rule: When a 9999 ebuild breaks on upstream build-tool changes, switch to the new interface (flag to env var) in the ebuild and carry regressions as FILESDIR patches headed by the upstream PR URL, submitting the fix upstream first.

problem: python_test still carried a case ${EPYTHON} in pypy3*) block of EPYTEST_DESELECT entries although pypy3 had been dropped from PYTHON_COMPAT.
root_cause: Dropping an implementation from PYTHON_COMPAT does not automatically remove its per-implementation conditionals; dead workaround code lingers and misleads future maintainers.
fix_pattern: Delete the now-unreachable case ${EPYTHON} in pypy3*) deselect block in the same series that removed pypy3 support.
rule: When removing an implementation from PYTHON_COMPAT, grep the ebuild for EPYTHON/impl-conditional blocks (pypy3*, python3_X) and delete the dead branches.

problem: pip's BuildEnvironment and test venv helper broke on Python 3.15: they computed system site paths by diffing sys.path after site.addsitedir(), which returns nothing new since 3.15 no longer re-executes .pth files for known paths.
root_cause: Python 3.15 changed site/.pth semantics (addsitedir skips already-known paths), so code inferring site dirs from sys.path growth silently gets an empty set.
fix_pattern: Backport the upstream fix as a FILESDIR patch (clear sys.path=[] first so addsitedir rebuilds only system paths, then subtract), citing both the upstream PR and follow-up commit URLs in comments above the PATCHES entry; enable py3.15 in PYTHON_TESTED in the same commit.
rule: When enabling a new CPython on packages that manipulate sys.path/site (installers, venv tools), expect site/.pth semantic changes and backport upstream compatibility patches with PR/commit URLs recorded next to the patch.

problem: Two overlapping downstream patches (test-wheel removal and dependency unbundling) left inconsistent leftovers: a .pth file was still created for a stubbed-out editable install, producing warnings about nonexistent directories under Python 3.15.
root_cause: Stacked downstream patches that each partially disable the same upstream machinery drift out of sync; one patch stubbed the wheel-install function while the other still expected its side effects.
fix_pattern: Merge the overlapping patches into one consolidated -r1 unbundling patch, remove obsolete logic entirely, and deliberately keep the patch minimal (leave unused fixtures in place rather than deleting them) to ease future rebases; revbump the ebuild.
rule: When carrying multiple downstream patches that touch the same subsystem, consolidate them into one minimal patch that removes only what is necessary, so version bumps rebase cleanly.

problem: containerd 2.2.5 fails to build with older Go toolchains; ebuild had no minimum dev-lang/go version, so users with an old go got cryptic compile errors.
root_cause: Go projects declare a minimum toolchain in go.mod (go/toolchain directives), but go-module.eclass does not translate that into a dependency; the ebuild must pin it explicitly.
fix_pattern: Add >=dev-lang/go-1.25.0 to BDEPEND matching the go.mod requirement of the release.
rule: On every bump of a go-module package, read the go.mod 'go' directive and mirror it as >=dev-lang/go-X.Y.Z in BDEPEND.

problem: SRC_URI used ${PN} for the upstream GitHub repo name and the ebuild lacked a minimum Go BDEPEND.
root_cause: ${PN} in SRC_URI hides the real upstream URL (breaks greppability and silently breaks if the package is ever renamed while upstream is not); go.mod minimum toolchain was not reflected as a dependency.
fix_pattern: Spell the upstream repo name literally in SRC_URI (https://github.com/hashicorp/packer/...) keeping ${PV}/${P} only for version parts, and add BDEPEND=">=dev-lang/go-1.25.11".
rule: Write upstream project/org names in SRC_URI as literal text (only version components via variables), and pin the Go toolchain floor from go.mod in BDEPEND.

problem: Go package version bump (1.15.4, bug 977136): upstream tarball has no vendored modules, so the build would need network access which the sandbox forbids.
root_cause: go-module eclass builds offline; every bump of a non-vendoring Go project needs a freshly generated dependency tarball matching the new go.mod, hosted somewhere fetchable (here the maintainer's own space, gentoo.neutroniak.com).
fix_pattern: New ebuild inherits go-module + shell-completion, adds a second SRC_URI entry for the regenerated ${P}-deps.tar.xz (grew 300MB->724MB across versions), keeps RESTRICT+=" test", builds with ego and installs zsh completion via dozshcomp.
rule: When bumping a Go package that ships no vendor/ dir, regenerate and rehost the ${P}-deps.tar.xz dependency tarball for the exact new version and add it as a second SRC_URI entry; never reuse the old deps tarball.

problem: nnn links against libpcre2 but its dependency 'pcre? ( dev-libs/libpcre2 )' had no slot operator, so a libpcre2 SONAME/subslot bump would not trigger a rebuild and leave nnn with a dangling shared-library reference.
root_cause: dev-libs/libpcre2 declares a subslot tracking its library ABI; consumers that link against it must bind to that subslot with := or the package manager cannot schedule rebuilds on ABI change (ncurses/readline in the same ebuild already had :=, pcre2 was simply missed).
fix_pattern: One-character fix: change the dep atom to dev-libs/libpcre2:= inside the USE conditional. No revbump was done since it only affects future resolutions.
rule: Every DEPEND/RDEPEND on a shared library whose package defines a subslot must carry the := slot operator, including atoms hidden inside USE conditionals; audit all linked deps, not just the obvious ones.

problem: rust-bin 1.96.1 was committed before the Gentoo-built bootstrap binaries for niche arches (mips*, sparc64, ppc64-musl, riscv64-musl) existed, so their SRC_URI entries had to stay inactive until the artifacts were uploaded.
root_cause: Binary artifacts for less common arches are produced asynchronously (projg2/rust-bootstrap releases) and lag the main release; listing their SRC_URI before the files exist breaks Manifest generation and fetching.
fix_pattern: The ebuild keeps the niche-arch SRC_URI block in a separate conditional deliberately written as 'if false && [[ ${PV} != *9999* ... ]]' with a comment 'Keep this separate to allow easy commenting out if not yet built'. Once binaries were uploaded, the commit just deletes 'false &&' and adds the new DIST entries to the Manifest.
rule: For prebuilt packages whose per-arch artifacts trail the release, isolate those SRC_URI additions in a dedicated conditional gated by a trivially removable 'false &&' toggle, and flip it in a follow-up commit once the files are published.
snippet:
# Keep this separate to allow easy commenting out if not yet built
if [[ ${PV} != *9999* && ${PV} != *beta* ]] ; then  # was: if false && [[ ...
	SRC_URI+=" sparc? ( ${GENTOO_BIN_BASEURI}/rust-${PVR}-sparc64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.xz ) "
fi

problem: IUSE default flipped from +doc to doc to stop building Rust documentation by default; package-specific default-USE policy tweak with no generalizable fix pattern.

When cross-compiling Rust, the per-target environment overrides for the pkg-config and openssl-sys build crates were keyed on ${CHOST}, but those crates look the variables up under the Rust target triple, which differs from the Gentoo CHOST (e.g. i686-pc-linux-gnu vs i686-unknown-linux-gnu), so the overrides were silently ignored.
root_cause: Rust target triples are normalized differently from autoconf CHOST triples; crate-specific env vars like PKG_CONFIG_PATH_<target> and <TARGET>_OPENSSL_DIR must use the rust triple spelling to be picked up.
fix_pattern: Derive the triple once with rust_abi from rust.eclass and build the variable names from it: local rust_host_triple="$(rust_abi "${CHOST}")" then pcvar="${pcvar}_${rust_host_triple//-/_}" and osslvar="${rust_host_triple}_${osslvar}"; osslvar="${osslvar^^}"; osslvar="${osslvar//-/_}". Same commit also shows live-ebuild upkeep: raise RUST_MIN_VER to the latest stable and tighten PYTHON_COMPAT when syncing 9999.
rule: When exporting per-target env vars consumed by Rust build-script crates (pkg-config, openssl-sys), derive the key from the Rust target triple via rust_abi "${CHOST}", never from raw CHOST.
snippet:
local rust_host_triple="$(rust_abi "${CHOST}")"
for pcvar in PKG_CONFIG_{PATH,LIBDIR} ; do
	pcvar="${pcvar}_${rust_host_triple//-/_}"
	...
done
osslvar="${rust_host_triple}_${osslvar}"; osslvar="${osslvar^^}"; osslvar="${osslvar//-/_}"

problem: Package not yet enabled for Python 3.15 during the tree-wide rollout; old range also still carried soon-to-be-removed 3.11.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT is a static per-ebuild list, so each new CPython release requires touching every ebuild; the rollout is combined with pruning implementations being phased out.
fix_pattern: Representative of the batch: change PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{11..14} ) to python3_{12..15} in one edit (add newest, drop oldest/EOL), bump the copyright year, and rely on the package's test suite passing under 3.15 before committing.
rule: When enabling a new Python implementation tree/overlay-wide, extend the PYTHON_COMPAT brace range and drop phased-out implementations (old CPython, stale pypy3_*) in the same one-line edit, verified by running the tests on the new implementation.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}, dropping pypy3_11 in the same edit; same idiom as the yapsy record.
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT extension python3_{12..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other content.

While enabling py3.15, the pytest run was still subject to autoloading of all system-installed pytest plugins, which is a common source of spurious test failures on a brand-new Python where unrelated plugins are broken.
root_cause: distutils_enable_tests pytest historically let pytest autoload every installed plugin; the newer EPYTEST_PLUGINS mechanism disables autoloading and only loads plugins the ebuild declares, but must be opted into.
fix_pattern: Add EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (empty array = test suite needs no pytest plugins) on the line BEFORE distutils_enable_tests pytest; the eclass then runs pytest with plugin autoloading disabled, insulating tests from whatever is installed on the host.
rule: When touching a distutils-r1 ebuild that uses distutils_enable_tests pytest, declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS (empty if none are needed) before the enable call so pytest runs without plugin autoloading.

problem: One-line PYTHON_COMPAT extension to python3_{12..15} (drops 3.11); no other content.
PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}, dropping pypy3_11; no other content.
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT extension to python3_{12..15}; no other content.
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT extension to python3_{12..15}; no other content.
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT extension to python3_{12..15}; no other content.
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT extension to python3_{12..15}; no other content.
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT extension to python3_{12..15}; no other content.
Ebuild still used the deprecated DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit backend name and ran pytest with plugin autoloading while being enabled for py3.15.
root_cause: The PEP517 backend value 'flit' (full flit package) is being retired in favor of 'flit-core', which is the actual build backend and a much lighter BDEPEND; opportunistic cleanups like this ride along on PYTHON_COMPAT rollout commits.
fix_pattern: In the same commit: DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit -> flit-core, PYTHON_COMPAT extended to python3_{12..15}, and EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() added before distutils_enable_tests pytest.
rule: When touching a distutils-r1 ebuild for any reason, migrate DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit to flit-core (matching upstream's build-backend = flit_core.buildapi) and declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS while you are there.
