problem: One-line PYTHON_COMPAT extension to python3_{12..15} (package uses DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=uv-build); no other content.
PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} plus DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 flit -> flit-core rename; same idiom as the sphinxcontrib-jquery record.
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT extension to python3_{12..15}; no other content.
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT extension to python3_{12..15}; no other content.

Upstream repository had moved from Holzhaus/sphinx-multiversion to the sphinx-contrib org, leaving HOMEPAGE, SRC_URI and the metadata.xml github remote-id pointing at the old location (works only via GitHub redirect, breaks version-checking tools).
root_cause: GitHub repo transfers keep old URLs alive through redirects, so stale references go unnoticed until a maintenance pass; automated version checkers (nvchecker-style, remote-id based tooling) query the recorded remote-id and miss releases under the new org.
fix_pattern: In one commit alongside the py3.15 enable: update HOMEPAGE and SRC_URI to the new org's URL, update <remote-id type="github"> in metadata.xml, and add EPYTEST_PLUGINS=(). Distfile name kept stable via '-> ${P}.gh.tar.gz' so the Manifest entry semantics stay clear.
rule: When you notice an upstream GitHub repo has been transferred, update HOMEPAGE, SRC_URI and the metadata/version-check remote-id to the new owner in the same commit instead of relying on redirects.

problem: One-line PYTHON_COMPAT extension to python3_{12..15}; no other content.
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT extension to python3_{12..15}; no other content.
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT extension to python3_{12..15}; no other content.
Ebuild carried a hand-rolled python_test() that manually exported PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 and a comma-joined PYTEST_PLUGINS list, plus duplicated pytest-plugin test deps in BDEPEND.
root_cause: Before distutils-r1/epytest grew declarative plugin control, controlling which pytest plugins load (needed because a pytest plugin package must load itself, and autoloaded plugins break tests under new Python versions) required manual env-var plumbing in python_test().
fix_pattern: Replace the custom python_test() with the declarative eclass API: EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( "${PN}" pytest-{httpbin,mock} ) names the plugins to load (the eclass also auto-adds their BDEPENDs, so the explicit test? deps were deleted), EPYTEST_PLUGIN_LOAD_VIA_ENV=1 loads them via PYTEST_PLUGINS env instead of -p args (required when the package under test is itself a plugin that must be importable before conftest), and EPYTEST_DESELECT moves to global scope.
rule: When an ebuild manually sets PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD/PYTEST_PLUGINS in python_test(), migrate to EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( ... ) (+ EPYTEST_PLUGIN_LOAD_VIA_ENV=1 for pytest-plugin packages) and drop the now eclass-managed plugin deps from BDEPEND.
snippet:
EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( "${PN}" pytest-{httpbin,mock} )
EPYTEST_PLUGIN_LOAD_VIA_ENV=1
distutils_enable_tests pytest

EPYTEST_DESELECT=(
	# Internet
	tests/test_blocking_network.py::test_block_network_with_allowed_hosts
)

problem: Test suite ran with pytest plugin autoloading enabled, so any pytest plugin installed on the system could inject itself into the test run and cause spurious failures (a common breakage source when enabling a new Python version).
root_cause: distutils_enable_tests pytest historically allowed autoloading of all installed pytest plugins; test results depended on the build host's installed package set instead of declared deps.
fix_pattern: While flipping PYTHON_COMPAT to python3_{12..15}, add EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest. An empty array tells the eclass to set PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD, running tests with no plugins at all for deterministic results.
rule: For any distutils-r1 package whose tests need no pytest plugins, declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest so the environment's installed plugins cannot affect the test run.

problem: A pytest-plugin package tested itself with plugin autoloading, so under a freshly enabled Python the plugin might not self-load correctly or foreign plugins could interfere.
root_cause: Packages that ARE pytest plugins must explicitly load themselves once autoloading is disabled, and loading must happen via the PYTEST_PLUGINS environment variable (not -p) so the plugin is active before conftest processing.
fix_pattern: Set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( "${PN}" ) plus EPYTEST_PLUGIN_LOAD_VIA_ENV=1 before distutils_enable_tests pytest; the eclass resolves ${PN} to the just-built plugin.
rule: When packaging a pytest plugin, declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( "${PN}" ) and EPYTEST_PLUGIN_LOAD_VIA_ENV=1 so the plugin under test loads itself deterministically with autoloading disabled.

problem: Ebuild used the legacy DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit backend name (and stale PYTHON_COMPAT incl. pypy3_11).
root_cause: distutils-r1 renamed the backend value for flit_core.buildapi from 'flit' to 'flit-core' to match the actual dev-python/flit-core dependency; the old spelling is deprecated.
fix_pattern: During the routine py3.15 compat flip, also change DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit to DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit-core and prune dropped implementations from PYTHON_COMPAT.
rule: When touching a distutils-r1 ebuild, modernize deprecated DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 values (flit->flit-core, poetry->poetry-core) in the same commit.

problem: Ebuild declared DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry, pulling the deprecated backend alias instead of the lightweight poetry-core build backend.
root_cause: distutils-r1 renamed the backend value to 'poetry-core' (matching dev-python/poetry-core, the actual PEP517 backend); 'poetry' is the deprecated alias.
fix_pattern: Alongside the PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}, switch DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry to poetry-core.
rule: Use the -core backend names (poetry-core, flit-core) in DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517; treat the short aliases as deprecated and fix them opportunistically when editing an ebuild.

problem: PYTHON_COMPAT still listed pypy3_11 and a python3_11 floor that the python eclasses were phasing out, while lacking the new python3_15 target.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT rots in both directions: new interpreters must be added after testing, and implementations dropped from the eclass support set must be removed, or the ebuild breaks when the eclass removes them.
fix_pattern: One-line hygiene edit during the enablement sweep: PYTHON_COMPAT=( pypy3_11 python3_{11..14} ) becomes PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} ) - add the new target and prune dying impls in the same touch.
rule: When adding a newly supported CPython target to PYTHON_COMPAT, simultaneously drop implementations scheduled for eclass removal (old floor, stale pypy) instead of leaving them for a second commit.

problem: Ebuild still carried a python_prepare_all sed stripping an obsolete 'nose>=1.0' requirement from setup.py, and tests ran with pytest plugin autoloading.
root_cause: Legacy source-mangling hacks outlive their purpose (nose deps are irrelevant under PEP517 pytest runs) and nobody re-audits them until the ebuild is touched again.
fix_pattern: During the py3.15 flip, delete the stale sed from python_prepare_all and add EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest for deterministic plugin-free tests.
rule: Whenever you touch an old ebuild, re-verify each sed/patch hack against the current source and delete the ones that no longer serve a purpose.
snippet:
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=()
 distutils_enable_tests pytest
 
 python_prepare_all() {
-	sed -e "/'nose>=1.0'/d" -i setup.py || die

problem: Package (a cargo-crates distutils ebuild) was capped at python3_14 after python3_15 became a supported eclass target.
root_cause: New CPython targets land in the python eclasses after ebuilds are written; each package must be test-verified and its PYTHON_COMPAT extended, done tree-wide as a mass sweep of small single-purpose commits.
fix_pattern: One-line PYTHON_COMPAT extension python3_{12..14} -> python3_{12..15} after the package's tests pass under the new interpreter; even Rust-extension (CRATES/DISTUTILS_EXT=1) packages need only this flip when the native parts already build.
rule: When a new CPython version becomes a valid eclass target, sweep the overlay: test each python package under it and extend PYTHON_COMPAT in one small commit per package ('pkg: Enable py3.XX').

problem: Routine py3.15 enablement: PYTHON_COMPAT 11..14 -> 12..15, no other change.
Routine py3.15 enablement plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() addition (same idiom as rply d0452a54).
Routine py3.15 enablement with pypy3_11 drop and EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() addition (same idioms as rapidfuzz/rply).
Routine py3.15 enablement, pypy3_11 dropped, no other change.
Routine py3.15 enablement: PYTHON_COMPAT 11..14 -> 12..15, no other change.
Routine py3.15 enablement: PYTHON_COMPAT 12..14 -> 12..15 on a DISTUTILS_EXT package, no other change.
Routine py3.15 enablement: PYTHON_COMPAT 11..14 -> 12..15, no other change.
Routine py3.15 enablement: PYTHON_COMPAT 12..14 -> 12..15, no other change.
Routine py3.15 enablement: PYTHON_COMPAT 11..14 -> 12..15, no other change.
Routine py3.15 enablement: PYTHON_COMPAT 11..14 -> 12..15, no other change.
Routine py3.15 enablement: PYTHON_COMPAT 11..14 -> 12..15, no other change.
Routine py3.15 enablement: PYTHON_COMPAT 11..14 -> 12..15, no other change.
Routine py3.15 enablement: PYTHON_COMPAT 12..14 -> 12..15, no other change.
Routine py3.15 enablement: PYTHON_COMPAT 11..14 -> 12..15 (legacy DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit left untouched here).
Routine py3.15 enablement: PYTHON_COMPAT 11..14 -> 12..15, no other change.
Routine py3.15 enablement: PYTHON_COMPAT 12..14 -> 12..15 on a DISTUTILS_EXT package, no other change.
Routine py3.15 enablement: PYTHON_COMPAT 11..14 -> 12..15, no other change.

Ebuild carried a manual BDEPEND block for a pytest plugin (test? ( dev-python/pytest-mock )) while newer distutils-r1/EPYTEST machinery expects plugins to be declared, not hand-listed, and pytest plugin autoloading is being disabled tree-wide.
root_cause: distutils-r1 grew the EPYTEST_PLUGINS API: setting it (even empty) makes the eclass disable pytest plugin autoloading and load only the named plugins with -p, and it auto-generates the test BDEPEND for them; hand-written plugin deps are now redundant and leave autoloading on.
fix_pattern: Delete the manual BDEPEND test? block and set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-mock ) before distutils_enable_tests pytest; the eclass adds the dependency and loads the plugin explicitly. Packages needing no plugins set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() to turn autoloading off entirely (seen in plyvel, pluginbase, mando, piexif in the same series).
rule: When touching a distutils-r1 ebuild that uses distutils_enable_tests pytest, declare its pytest plugins via EPYTEST_PLUGINS (empty array if none) instead of manual test? BDEPEND entries, so plugin autoloading is disabled and deps are eclass-generated.

problem: Ollama binaries triggered stripping QA problems (bug 978763): the Go binary was built pre-stripped, so portage's own strip/splitdebug handling breaks and debug symbols are unobtainable.
root_cause: Upstream's cmake/local.cmake hardcodes Go linker flags '-s -w' (strip symbol table and DWARF) in OLLAMA_GO_LDFLAGS, taking stripping out of the package manager's hands.
fix_pattern: Add a FILESDIR patch that removes '-s -w' from the Go ldflags in the build system, keeping only the -X version injection flags, and append it to PATCHES; portage then performs (or skips) stripping per FEATURES.
rule: When a Go (or any) upstream build passes -s -w / -Wl,-s style strip flags, patch them out of the build system so portage controls stripping and splitdebug works.

problem: Daemon package installed only the binary: no service user, no OpenRC/systemd units, no log directory, so users had to hand-roll service setup (bug 978721).
root_cause: Initial packaging of a server-style program omitted the standard Gentoo daemon plumbing (acct-* deps, init scripts, unit file, state dirs).
fix_pattern: Revbump adding: acct-group/ollama + acct-user/ollama to DEPEND; an OpenRC init using supervise-daemon with command_user="ollama:ollama" plus a confd exposing output_log/error_log; a systemd unit with User=/Group= and SupplementaryGroups=render video for GPU device access (/dev/dri/renderD*, /dev/kfd); newinitd/newconfd + systemd_dounit in src_install; keepdir /var/log/ollama with fperms 750 and fowners ollama:ollama in pkg_preinst; first-install einfo hints gated on [[ -z ${REPLACING_VERSIONS} ]].
rule: When packaging a daemon, ship acct-user/acct-group deps, both OpenRC (supervise-daemon) and systemd units, GPU access via SupplementaryGroups=render video if it touches the GPU, and create owned log/state dirs with keepdir+fowners in the ebuild rather than leaving it to the user.
snippet:
newinitd "${FILESDIR}/ollama.init" "${PN}"
newconfd "${FILESDIR}/ollama.confd" "${PN}"
systemd_dounit "${FILESDIR}/ollama.service"
# unit: SupplementaryGroups=render video
pkg_preinst() {
	keepdir /var/log/ollama
	fperms 750 /var/log/ollama
	fowners "${PN}:${PN}" /var/log/ollama
}

problem: New major release of a library dependency (dev-db/lmdb-1.0.0) breaks current postfix releases at build/runtime.
root_cause: Upstream postfix has not yet adapted to lmdb 1.0.0 API/ABI changes; the existing dependency only had a lower bound (>=dev-db/lmdb-0.9.11:=).
fix_pattern: Flip the dependency from a lower bound to an upper bound while keeping the slot operator: lmdb? ( <dev-db/lmdb-1.0.0:= ), applied to all affected ebuilds including the _pre snapshot; no revbump needed since installed systems are unaffected until lmdb upgrades.
rule: When a dependency's new major version breaks your package and no fix exists yet, cap it with an upper-bounded dep (<cat/pkg-X.Y:=) in every affected ebuild instead of waiting for breakage reports.

problem: Ebuild depended on dev-python/psycopg:0 while the code needs the psycopg2 API; after psycopg was re-slotted (psycopg3 in :0, legacy psycopg2 in :2) the dep pulled the wrong provider.
root_cause: Slot 0 of a re-slotted package silently changed meaning when upstream's new major version took over the default slot; consumers pinned to :0 out of habit rather than to the API level they use.
fix_pattern: Change the dependency to the slot matching the API actually imported: postgres? ( dev-python/psycopg:2[${PYTHON_USEDEP}] ), applied across all live ebuild versions in one commit.
rule: When a dependency package splits major versions into slots, pin the slot whose API your package actually uses (check its imports), and audit every ebuild version, not just the newest.

problem: ggml lacked the Vulkan GPU backend, so consumers (llama.cpp/ollama stacks) could not use GPU acceleration via the system ggml (bug 978725).
root_cause: Optional CMake backend (-DGGML_VULKAN) was never wired to a USE flag or its toolchain deps.
fix_pattern: Revbump (-r1, dependency change) adding IUSE=vulkan with deps split correctly by role: RDEPEND media-libs/vulkan-loader (runtime linkage), DEPEND dev-util/vulkan-headers (compile-time only), BDEPEND media-libs/shaderc (glslc shader compiler runs at build time), plus -DGGML_VULKAN=$(usex vulkan) in mycmakeargs.
rule: When exposing a GPU backend USE flag, split the deps by role - loader in RDEPEND, headers in DEPEND, shader compiler in BDEPEND - and revbump because installed-dep metadata changed.
snippet:
IUSE="... vulkan"
RDEPEND="vulkan? ( media-libs/vulkan-loader )"
DEPEND="${RDEPEND}
	vulkan? ( dev-util/vulkan-headers )"
BDEPEND="vulkan? ( media-libs/shaderc )"
# src_configure: -DGGML_VULKAN=$(usex vulkan)

problem: dist-kernel had no usable default kconfig on niche arches (alpha, arm, loong, m68k, ppc, sparc), forcing REQUIRED_USE savedconfig or empty .config there and blocking keywording.
root_cause: Gentoo's default configs come from Fedora's kernel configs, and Fedora does not ship configs for those architectures.
fix_pattern: Pull Debian's kernel-team kconfig fragments as a second config source: pin a salsa.debian.org commit in a DEBIAN_COMMIT variable, add its archive tarball to SRC_URI, then per-arch copy debian/config/config as the base .config and append arch fragments (e.g. sparc64/config.sparc64 + config.sparc64-smp) to the merge_configs array; drop arm/sparc from the savedconfig REQUIRED_USE and extend KEYWORDS.
rule: When your primary upstream config source lacks an architecture, borrow another distro's maintained config fragments pinned by commit SHA in SRC_URI and merge them via the eclass merge_configs mechanism instead of forcing savedconfig.

problem: rust-bin 1.95.0 had no binaries for niche arches (mips*, sparc64, ppc64-musl, riscv64-musl) because upstream Rust does not distribute them; the ebuild block for Gentoo-built bootstrap tarballs was disabled with 'if false'.
root_cause: Gentoo self-builds those toolchains (projg2/rust-bootstrap GitHub releases) and they lag the release; the ebuild ships with the extra-URI block commented out until the artifacts exist.
fix_pattern: Once the bootstrap binaries were published, flip the guard from 'if false; then' to a version check and add the tarballs to Manifest plus ~mips to KEYWORDS; per-arch SRC_URI entries use USE conditionals like elibc_musl? and big-endian? to pick the right tuple, and unavailable variants (ppc64le-musl) are simply removed until built.
rule: For prebuilt toolchains with self-hosted niche-arch artifacts, keep the extra SRC_URI block behind a trivially toggleable guard (if false / version test) so keywording can proceed the moment the binaries are uploaded, and express libc/endian variants with elibc_musl?/big-endian? USE conditionals.
snippet:
-if false; then
+if [[ ${PV} != *9999* && ${PV} != *beta* ]] ; then
	SRC_URI+=" sparc? ( ${GENTOO_BIN_BASEURI}/rust-${PVR}-sparc64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.xz ) "
	SRC_URI+=" ppc64? ( elibc_musl? (
		big-endian? ( .../rust-${PVR}-powerpc64-unknown-linux-musl.tar.xz )
	) )"

problem: Live (9999) ebuild's patch preventing installation of the bundled Qt-Advanced-Docking-System stopped applying after upstream reorganized the bundled lib's CMakeLists.
root_cause: Upstream bundles a third-party library whose default CMake install() rules would install headers/libs/cmake configs system-wide, colliding with the standalone package; the anti-install patch is version-coupled to the bundled CMake file and rots as upstream moves.
fix_pattern: Regenerate the patch against the new source (new filesdir patch named for the new version, 1.7.4): force the bundled lib to build STATIC with ADS_STATIC defined and comment out all install(FILES/TARGETS/EXPORT) rules; swap the PATCHES entry in the live ebuild while old ebuilds keep the old patch.
rule: When a package bundles a third-party lib, build it static and strip its install() rules via a filesdir patch to avoid file collisions, and expect to refresh that patch on live-ebuild syncs, versioning the patch filename to the release it matches.
snippet:
-if(BUILD_STATIC)
+#if(BUILD_STATIC)
     add_library(${library_name} STATIC ${ads_SRCS} ${ads_HEADERS})
     target_compile_definitions( ${library_name} PUBLIC ADS_STATIC)
-else() ... endif()
# plus: comment out every install(...) block

problem: A test deselection applied only to python3.14 also needed to apply to the newly enabled python3.15, and listing each version separately in the case statement does not scale.
root_cause: Version-specific EPYTEST_DESELECT entries keyed on exact ${EPYTHON} strings must be re-touched on every new interpreter enablement.
fix_pattern: Use a shell glob in the case pattern matching a range of versions: python3.1[45]*) so the deselect covers 3.14 and 3.15 in one branch.
rule: When a test skip applies to a range of Python versions, match ${EPYTHON} with a case glob (e.g. python3.1[45]*) instead of enumerating exact versions, so new-target bumps need fewer edits.

problem: Routine new-interpreter enablement: PYTHON_COMPAT capped at python3_14 blocks the py3.15 rollout.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT is a static list that must be bumped per package once upstream/tests are known to work on the new interpreter.
fix_pattern: Shift the brace range forward, dropping the oldest target at the same time (python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}); companion commits in the series also drop unstable pypy3_11 and set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() to disable pytest plugin autoloading while at it; update the copyright year.
rule: When enabling a new CPython target, bump the whole PYTHON_COMPAT brace range (adding the new, dropping the EOL-bound oldest) rather than only appending, and take the opportunity to declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS.

problem: Mirror of the gentoo-kernel Debian-kconfig change for vanilla-kernel; same Debian LTS config sourcing for niche arches (see 5070e63a lesson).
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT bump (python3_{12..15}) plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=(); pattern captured in plyvel/ffmpeg-python lessons.
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT bump also dropping pypy3_11; no package-specific insight.
One-line py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT range extension; nothing generalizable beyond the series pattern.
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT bump dropping pypy3_11.
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT bump plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=().
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT bump dropping pypy3_11 on a DISTUTILS_EXT package.
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT bump dropping pypy3_11.
One-line py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT range extension.
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT bump plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=().
One-line py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT range extension.
One-line py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT range extension (ebuild also shows PYPI_VERIFY_REPO usage but unchanged here).
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT bump dropping pypy3_11.
One-line py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT range extension.
Package not yet enabled for a newly-released CPython (3.15); representative of the batch-wide PYTHON_COMPAT window bump.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT brace range still ends at the previous interpreter; Gentoo rolls the supported window forward (add 3.15, drop 3.11) once the package is verified to work.
fix_pattern: Edit the brace range in place: PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{11..14} ) -> PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} ). No revision bump: extending/shrinking PYTHON_COMPAT changes nothing for already-installed files of other implementations, so Gentoo policy allows it without -rN. Old EOL impls (and often pypy3) are dropped in the same edit.
rule: When enabling a new Python target, adjust the PYTHON_COMPAT brace range in place without a revbump, drop EOL implementations in the same edit, and only do it after the test suite passes on the new interpreter.

problem: Ebuild carried a hand-written python_test() whose only job was to disable pytest plugin autoloading.
root_cause: Before distutils-r1 grew EPYTEST_PLUGINS, disabling pytest plugin autoloading required a custom python_test with 'local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1'; the eclass now handles this declaratively.
fix_pattern: Delete the custom python_test() and instead declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (empty array = load no plugins, autoload disabled) BEFORE the distutils_enable_tests pytest call. Non-empty EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( foo ) loads only the named plugins (see pkgcheck using EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pkgcore )).
rule: When a distutils-r1 ebuild only overrides python_test to set PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD, replace it with EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (or a list of the needed plugins) placed before distutils_enable_tests pytest.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}, no other change.
Routine py3.15 enablement; also dropped pypy3_11 and added EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (idiom covered under flask-login lesson).
Routine py3.15 enablement plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (idiom covered under flask-login lesson).
Routine py3.15 enablement, pypy3_11 dropped.
Routine py3.15 enablement of a scikit-build-core C++ extension; one-line PYTHON_COMPAT change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}.
Version bump 0.21.0 blocked by test failure on musl (bug 976960): a French-locale name-collation test mis-sorts accented names.
root_cause: The testsuite skips tests whose locale is not installed, but musl with musl-locales provides only a PARTIAL fr_FR locale, so the skip check passes while collation behaves differently from glibc, making the assertion fail.
fix_pattern: Ship a FILESDIR patch that deletes the locale-collation test entirely (with an explanatory header citing the bug) rather than trying to make libc collation match; add it via PATCHES=(). Secondary idiom in the same ebuild: strip flags from $MAKE before Sphinx runs it via subprocess: local safe_MAKE="${MAKE%% *}"; emake MAKE="${safe_MAKE}" -j1 -C doc/ html.
rule: When a test depends on locale collation, expect it to fail on musl even though the locale nominally exists (musl-locales are partial) - patch the test out instead of chasing libc sorting behavior.
snippet:
PATCHES=( "${FILESDIR}/khard-0.21.0-musl-testfix.patch" )
# patch deletes test_sort_order_for_accentuated_names(),
# which needs full fr_FR collation absent on musl-locales

problem: Trivial QA: removed redundant empty IUSE="" assignments (pkgcheck EmptyGlobalAssignment-style warning).
Routine py3.15 enablement; commit notes package has no tests so correctness is unverified.
C++ extension fails to compile against Python 3.15: PyWeakref_GetObject is no longer declared in the CPython headers.
root_cause: CPython 3.15 removed the deprecated PyWeakref_GetObject() declaration from the public headers while the symbol remains in the stable ABI, so old code compiles on <=3.14 but breaks on 3.15 at compile time only.
fix_pattern: Add a FILESDIR patch that re-declares the symbol behind a version guard instead of rewriting the code; patch was taken directly from Fedora rawhide dist-git (src.fedoraproject.org/rpms/python-pyqt6). #if PY_VERSION_HEX >= 0x030f0000 extern "C" PyObject* PyWeakref_GetObject(PyObject *ref); #endif
rule: When a C/C++ extension stops compiling on a new CPython because a deprecated C-API declaration was removed from headers but kept in the stable ABI, forward-declare it under a PY_VERSION_HEX guard - and check Fedora rawhide dist-git first for a ready-made patch.

problem: pyqt6-sip PYTHON_COMPAT had to be bumped together with pyqt6 even though the change itself is one line.
root_cause: pyqt6 imports the sip module at runtime; if the runtime-support package and the bindings advertise different PYTHON_COMPAT sets, dependency resolution for reverse dependencies produces confusing USE_PYTHON conflicts. The ebuild carries the comment 'keep compat in sync with pyqt6 or else it confuses some revdeps'.
fix_pattern: Bump PYTHON_COMPAT of the tightly-coupled runtime module (pyqt6-sip, pyqt-builder) in lockstep with the main bindings package, in adjacent commits.
rule: For split binding stacks (bindings + their generated-runtime/sip module), keep PYTHON_COMPAT identical across all members and bump them together, otherwise revdep dependency resolution breaks.
snippet:
# keep compat in sync with pyqt6 or else it confuses some revdeps
PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} )

problem: Routine py3.15 enablement of the PyQt build-system package, kept in sync with the pyqt6 stack.
Intermittent test failure (bug 978553): test_conf_parser_save.sh reads test_conf_parser.save, a file generated by a different test script.
root_cause: The automake test harness does not guarantee test execution order (and may parallelize), so a test consuming another test's output artifact races and fails depending on scheduling.
fix_pattern: Patch tests/Makefile.am to remove the dependent test from the test list and append its assertion to the end of the producing test script, so producer and consumer always run in one script in order.
rule: When a testsuite fails intermittently and one test reads files written by another, merge the consumer's check into the producer's script (or otherwise serialize them) - never rely on harness execution order in automake/ctest.

problem: Build failure with nvidia-cuda-toolkit-12.9 (bug 977044): the CUDA code is compiled by Clang, and the installed Clang did not support that CUDA release.
root_cause: Unbounded dep 'cuda? ( dev-util/nvidia-cuda-toolkit )' plus unversioned clang let users combine a CUDA toolkit newer than what their Clang front-end knows; Clang only partially supports newer CUDA versions (see PARTIALLY_SUPPORTED in clang's Basic/Cuda.h).
fix_pattern: Bound both sides so the minimum Clang supports the maximum toolkit: cuda? ( >=dev-util/nvidia-cuda-toolkit-12 <dev-util/nvidia-cuda-toolkit-12.10 ) in DEPEND and cuda? ( >=llvm-core/clang-22[llvm_targets_NVPTX] ) in BDEPEND, with an ebuild comment documenting where the support matrix lives. No clang slot operator needed because the newest installed clang is first in PATH.
rule: When compiling CUDA with Clang, pin an upper bound on nvidia-cuda-toolkit that the minimum required Clang version supports (consult Clang's Basic/Cuda.h), and raise both bounds together on bumps.

problem: dbus-python C extension broken on Python 3.15; upstream had switched to Py_TPFLAGS_MANAGED_WEAKREF for py3.12+ but the conversion is incomplete and does not work.
root_cause: Upstream merged a half-finished migration to managed weakrefs; newer CPython exposed the breakage. Upstream discussion confirmed the feature is incomplete.
fix_pattern: Take Fedora rawhide's 'Revert using Py_TPFLAGS_MANAGED_WEAKREF' patch verbatim (restoring the classic tp_weaklistoffset/weaklist member), ship it in a NEW revision (-r1) since the patch changes installed compiled code, and enable py3.15 in the same revision. Patch header cites both the upstream work item and the Fedora dist-git source.
rule: When upstream code breaks on a new Python due to a half-adopted C-API feature, reverting the feature (as Fedora does) is a legitimate fix - carry the revert as a patch with provenance URLs in its header, and revbump because installed binaries change.
snippet:
PATCHES=(
	"${FILESDIR}"/${PN}-1.4.0-py315.patch
)
# header: gitlab.freedesktop.org work_items/59 +
# src.fedoraproject.org rpms/dbus-python Revert-...patch

problem: Routine py3.15 enablement, pypy3_11 dropped.
Live -9999 ebuild PYTHON_COMPAT synced to match release ebuild changes ('sync live').
Build failure with USE="-wayland pipewire vulkan" (bug 977035): headers under CMAKE_BINARY_DIR/generated-src were only added to the include path inside the if(WAYLAND_FOUND) block.
root_cause: The cmake logic tied a generic include directory to the Wayland feature, and the pipewire capture backend genuinely requires Wayland support at runtime, neither of which the ebuild expressed.
fix_pattern: Two-part fix: (1) FILESDIR patch (already accepted upstream) moving the include_directories(SYSTEM ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/generated-src) call out of the WAYLAND_FOUND conditional; (2) encode the real feature constraint in the ebuild with REQUIRED_USE="pipewire? ( wayland )". Applied to both release and 9999 ebuilds.
rule: When a non-default USE combination fails to build, fix the build-system conditional with an upstreamable patch AND add a REQUIRED_USE clause for any genuine inter-feature dependency; always mirror such fixes into the -9999 ebuild.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}.
Routine py3.15 enablement, release and -9999 ebuilds updated in the same commit.
Test suite fails on Python 3.15: importlib.machinery SourceFileLoader.load_module() was removed, breaking a test that loads a helper module from an arbitrary path.
root_cause: Loader.load_module() had been deprecated for years and was finally removed in CPython 3.15; any code loading modules from non-package paths via SourceFileLoader(...).load_module() stops working.
fix_pattern: Backport the upstream commit as a FILESDIR patch (with issue + upstream commit URLs in the header): replace load_module() with the spec API - spec_from_loader + module_from_spec + loader.exec_module. Test-only patch, so no revbump needed; PYTHON_COMPAT bumped in the same commit.
rule: When code breaks on Python 3.15 with 'load_module' AttributeError, port it to importlib.util.spec_from_loader/module_from_spec/exec_module; patches touching only tests do not require a revision bump.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{12..14} -> {12..15} in release and 9999 ebuilds, no revbump needed for pure target additions.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to py3.15; copyright year refreshed in the same edit.
rust-bin 1.94.1 had no keywords or SRC_URI for mips/sparc/ppc64-musl/riscv-musl because Gentoo-built bootstrap tarballs for those targets did not exist yet at bump time.
root_cause: Upstream Rust does not ship binaries for niche arches; Gentoo builds its own (projg2/rust-bootstrap GitHub releases) which lag each version bump, so the niche-arch SRC_URI block cannot be enabled on day one.
fix_pattern: The ebuild keeps a permanent, separate SRC_URI+= block for the self-hosted niche-arch tarballs guarded by a condition that is stubbed to 'if false; then' at bump time. Once the tarballs are published, the guard is flipped back to '[[ ${PV} != *9999* && ${PV} != *beta* ]]', Manifest entries added, and ~mips ~sparc keywords appended. Variants that were never built (powerpc64le musl) are deleted from the block rather than left as dead URIs.
rule: When a binary package's per-arch artifacts arrive later than the main release, structure their SRC_URI as a separate guarded block you can stub with 'if false' at bump time and re-enable (plus keywords) once the artifacts exist; never ship SRC_URI entries pointing at not-yet-existing files.
snippet:
-if false; then
+if [[ ${PV} != *9999* && ${PV} != *beta* ]] ; then
 	# Keep this separate to allow easy commenting out if not yet built
 	SRC_URI+=" sparc? ( ${GENTOO_BIN_BASEURI}/rust-${PVR}-sparc64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.xz ) "
...
-KEYWORDS="amd64 arm arm64 ~loong ppc ppc64 ~riscv ~s390 x86"
+KEYWORDS="amd64 arm arm64 ~loong ~mips ppc ppc64 ~riscv ~s390 ~sparc x86"

problem: Same staged niche-arch enablement as 1.94.1 (guard flipped from 'if false' once self-built tarballs published); duplicate of the 27915830 lesson.
PYTHON_COMPAT bump to py3.15; lower bound simultaneously raised from python3_11 to python3_12 as old impl was retired.

Enabling py3.15 would have left the setuptools runtime dep wrong: it was wrapped in python_gen_cond_dep restricted to 3.12/3.13 only, so newer impls that still need setuptools (for the distutils import) would miss the dep.
root_cause: python_gen_cond_dep version lists are frozen snapshots; every PYTHON_COMPAT change can silently invalidate them because new impls are not in the listed set.
fix_pattern: While bumping PYTHON_COMPAT to python3_{12..15}, the conditional dep was replaced with an unconditional dev-python/setuptools[${PYTHON_USEDEP}] in RDEPEND since the package needs it on all supported impls now.
rule: Whenever you change PYTHON_COMPAT, re-audit every python_gen_cond_dep call in the ebuild: its hardcoded implementation list does not grow with COMPAT, so either extend the list or make the dep unconditional.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to py3.15, one-line change.
Routine py3.15 enablement; 9999 ebuild also aligned with release (dropped pypy3_11, raised lower bound).
networkx needed py3.15 enabled although upstream does not yet fully test/support 3.15, and its test suite has extras that only pass on fully supported interpreters.
root_cause: A new CPython can be package-usable before upstream declares it fully supported; gating everything on full support delays ecosystem bootstrap, but treating it as fully tested breaks the test phase.
fix_pattern: The ebuild separates the two sets: PYTHON_FULLY_TESTED holds the upstream-supported impls and PYTHON_COMPAT is that array plus the new interpreter, so distutils-r1 can relax test expectations for the not-fully-tested impl while still building for it.
rule: To enable a brand-new Python interpreter that upstream has not blessed yet, add it to PYTHON_COMPAT on top of a PYTHON_FULLY_TESTED array instead of pretending it is fully supported.

problem: Ebuild still listed the removed unversioned pypy3 target and old python3_11, blocking py3.15 enablement and keeping a dead target alive (bug 950991).
root_cause: Gentoo retired the unversioned pypy3 target (replaced by versioned pypy3_11, itself being phased out of many packages); PYTHON_COMPAT entries for dead targets linger until someone touches the ebuild.
fix_pattern: PYTHON_COMPAT rewritten to python3_{12..15} dropping pypy3 entirely; incidental style cleanups in the same commit (multi-line RDEPEND, ':;' no-op instead of 'return' in src_compile).
rule: When touching PYTHON_COMPAT for any reason, drop retired targets (unversioned pypy3, EOL CPython) at the same time instead of leaving them for a separate cleanup.

problem: Routine py3.15 enablement in a python-r1 multilib package; identical one-line change in release and 9999 ebuilds.
Routine py3.15 enablement (python-single-r1 used only for tooling).
Routine py3.15 enablement, release and 9999 kept in lockstep.
Routine py3.15 enablement plus copyright year refresh.
Routine py3.15 enablement across both slots (psycopg2 and psycopg3), lower bound raised to 3.12 in the old slot.
Routine py3.15 enablement; pypy3_11 target dropped in the same edit (ongoing pypy phase-out).
Routine py3.15 enablement; pypy3_11 dropped alongside.
Routine py3.15 enablement with lower bound raise to 3.12.
Routine py3.15 enablement, one-line change.
Test-only pytest-plugin dependency (hypothesis) was hand-written as a test? ( ... ) BDEPEND block, which is boilerplate and does not ensure the plugin is actually loaded in the isolated epytest run.
root_cause: distutils-r1/EPYTEST gained a first-class mechanism (EPYTEST_PLUGINS) for declaring pytest plugins: it generates the test deps automatically and controls plugin loading, superseding manual test? deps for plugins.
fix_pattern: Deleted the test? ( dev-python/hypothesis[${PYTHON_USEDEP}] ) block from BDEPEND and declared EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( hypothesis ) before distutils_enable_tests pytest; the eclass now adds the dep and loads the plugin.
rule: In dev-python ebuilds, declare pytest plugins needed by the test suite via EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( name... ) before distutils_enable_tests pytest instead of hand-writing test? BDEPEND entries for them.
snippet:
BDEPEND="
 	dev-python/hatch-fancy-pypi-readme[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]
 	dev-python/hatch-vcs[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]
-	test? (
-		dev-python/hypothesis[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]
-	)
 "
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( hypothesis )
 distutils_enable_tests pytest

problem: Copyright-year-only fixup after merging a PR that had sat unmerged across a year boundary; no packaging content.

Package was stuck on a 2016-era 1.8.0.1 release (gstreamer plugin ABI mismatch, bugs 885507/885417) because the original upstream went dormant; the new 1.24.0 comes from a maintained fork with a BSD-style Makefile that hardcodes cc and BSD install macros.
root_cause: Upstream relocation to a fork (BSDKaffee/gstreamer1-plugins-sndio) plus a BSD-origin Makefile that ignores CC from the environment unless exported and uses BSD_INSTALL_* macro variables for install modes.
fix_pattern: New ebuild points SRC_URI/HOMEPAGE at the fork with MY_P for the differing tarball name, uses tc-export CC in src_compile so the Makefile picks up the cross/toolchain compiler, and exports BSD_INSTALL_LIB='install -m 444' in src_install so the BSD install macro maps to GNU install.
rule: When packaging software with BSD-style Makefiles, tc-export CC (and friends) before the default compile and override the BSD_INSTALL_* variables with GNU install equivalents rather than patching the Makefile; when upstream is dead, re-point SRC_URI at the maintained fork and keep PN via MY_P.

problem: ggml build mishandled AVX-512 subfeature selection because the ebuild's cpu_flags_x86_* set was missing avx512bw, one of the SIMD options upstream's CMake dispatches on (bug 978738).
root_cause: SIMD-dispatch libraries (ggml/llama.cpp family) expose one CMake toggle per instruction-set extension; the ebuild mirrors these via an X86_CPU_FLAGS array, and any upstream-known flag missing from the array cannot be controlled by the user, leading to wrong-codepath builds or illegal instructions.
fix_pattern: Added avx512bw to the X86_CPU_FLAGS array (kept sorted) which is expanded into IUSE via prefix substitution; also demoted openmp from default-on (+openmp) to plain openmp.
rule: For packages whose build system takes per-SIMD-extension switches, keep the cpu_flags_x86_* IUSE array in one-to-one sync with the full upstream option list on every bump, and generate IUSE from the array rather than hand-listing flags.
snippet:
X86_CPU_FLAGS=(
	avx avx_vnni avx2 avx512bw avx512f avx512vbmi avx512_vnni
	bmi2 fma3 f16c sse4_2
)
CPU_FLAGS=( "${X86_CPU_FLAGS[@]/#/cpu_flags_x86_}" )
IUSE="${CPU_FLAGS[*]} openmp test"

problem: A single flaky test (testholidayregion) intermittently failed the whole src_test phase for arch testing and users with FEATURES=test (bug 948900).
root_cause: One nondeterministic upstream test; disabling all tests (RESTRICT=test) or patching CMakeLists would be disproportionate and unmaintainable.
fix_pattern: cmake.eclass supports a declarative CMAKE_SKIP_TESTS array whose entries become ctest exclusion regexes; the ebuild adds the flaky test name with a comment referencing the tracking bug, applied identically to every live ebuild version.
rule: To skip individual flaky or environment-dependent tests in cmake-based packages, declare CMAKE_SKIP_TESTS=( testname ) with a bug-reference comment instead of RESTRICT=test or CMakeLists patches.

problem: Same CMAKE_SKIP_TESTS flaky-test idiom as kholidays (bug 948899, karchivetest); duplicate of the 7ac29fa7 lesson.

Package could not enable a new Python target (py3.15) because its test suite failed against the new Sphinx 9 API, and test deps had been restricted to a subset of implementations via a PYTHON_TESTED workaround.
root_cause: Upstream test/runtime code used removed Sphinx internals (StringOutput/docwriter API); earlier, some test deps did not yet support all impls, so the ebuild used PYTHON_TESTED=( subset ) with python_gen_cond_dep to gate test deps per-impl.
fix_pattern: Backport the upstream compatibility PR as a FILESDIR patch (with the PR URL as a comment above the PATCHES entry), then collapse the PYTHON_TESTED indirection back to a plain PYTHON_COMPAT and plain [${PYTHON_USEDEP}] test deps once all deps support the full impl range. Also renamed DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit to flit-core.
rule: When test-only deps lag behind a new Python target, gate them with PYTHON_TESTED=( subset ) + python_gen_cond_dep; once the ecosystem catches up, remove the indirection and backport upstream compat PRs (cited by URL) to enable the new target.
snippet:
PYTHON_TESTED=( python3_{12..14} )
PYTHON_COMPAT=( "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}" python3_15 )
BDEPEND="test? ( $(python_gen_cond_dep '
  dev-python/whoosh[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]
' "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}") )"
# later, collapse back to:
PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} )

problem: Enabling py3.15 on an old, dead-upstream package with a pytest suite that could be perturbed by system-installed pytest plugins.
root_cause: distutils-r1 now disables pytest plugin autoloading when EPYTEST_PLUGINS is declared; suites that need no plugins should say so explicitly, otherwise random installed plugins (or missing ones) can break tests on new Python versions.
fix_pattern: Set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest to run the suite with plugin autoloading disabled and zero plugin deps; drop stale sed-based setup.py fixes that no longer apply.
rule: When a pytest suite needs no pytest plugins, declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest so tests run isolated from whatever plugins happen to be installed.

problem: DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit is a deprecated/renamed backend value; ebuild also needed the new Python target enabled.
root_cause: The distutils-r1 eclass renamed the 'flit' backend key to 'flit-core' to match the actual pyproject.toml build-backend (flit_core.buildapi).
fix_pattern: Rename DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit to DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit-core while touching the ebuild for the PYTHON_COMPAT bump.
rule: Set DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 to match the exact build-backend named in pyproject.toml; when an eclass renames a backend key (flit -> flit-core), migrate opportunistically during compat bumps.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to py3.15 plus flit->flit-core backend rename; also silently drops python3_11/pypy3_11 from the compat list.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to py3.15 plus flit->flit-core backend rename, no other changes.
Routine py3.15 compat bump with flit->flit-core and EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() additions already covered by sibling commits.
Routine py3.15 compat bump identical in shape to sphinxcontrib-applehelp (flit-core rename, EPYTEST_PLUGINS=()).
Test suite failed with newer Sphinx (invalid index-markup syntax in test fixtures), blocking the py3.15 enablement (bug 970018).
root_cause: Sphinx tightened validation of '.. index::' directives; the package's static test-root fixtures used now-invalid 'keyword:' syntax.
fix_pattern: Cherry-pick just the fixture-fix hunk from the upstream PR as files/${P}-test.patch with the PR URL in a comment, add it to PATCHES, and do NOT revbump because only test files change (nothing installed is affected).
rule: When a stricter toolchain breaks only test fixtures, cherry-pick the upstream test fix as a FILESDIR patch with the PR URL cited, and skip the revision bump since installed content is unchanged.

problem: Package broke against newer Sphinx (8.2 removed MathDomain.has_equations usage pattern) and its tests used removed sphinx.testing.path API; enabling py3.15 required patching installed runtime code.
root_cause: Dead-ish upstream: runtime module used Sphinx APIs removed in 8.2/9, and conftest used sphinx.testing.path dropped upstream.
fix_pattern: Add two FILESDIR patches (one test-only, one touching the installed sphinxcontrib/jsmath module, each with upstream commit/PR URL comments) declared as a local PATCHES array inside src_prepare before calling distutils-r1_src_prepare, and RENAME the ebuild -r4 -> -r5 because installed runtime code changed. Contrast: the sibling htmlhelp commit patching only tests did not revbump.
rule: Revbump whenever a new patch changes files that get installed; patches that touch only tests/fixtures need no revbump - and a local PATCHES=( ... ) inside src_prepare followed by the eclass phase call is the idiom when you already override src_prepare.

problem: Routine py3.15 compat bump (flit-core rename, EPYTEST_PLUGINS=()) identical to sibling sphinxcontrib commits.
Routine py3.15 compat bump (flit-core rename, EPYTEST_PLUGINS=()) identical to sibling sphinxcontrib commits.
DESCRIPTION started with an article ('An assortment...') and metadata.xml lacked a usable upstream tracker reference.
root_cause: QA style: descriptions should not begin with 'A/An/The' (pkgcheck flags it); a bare bugs-to URL without product parameters and missing remote-id makes automated upstream tracking impossible.
fix_pattern: Strip the leading article from DESCRIPTION in all live ebuilds, point bugs-to at the product-specific enter_bug.cgi URL, and add a <remote-id type="kde-invent">group/repo</remote-id> entry.
rule: Keep DESCRIPTION article-free and give metadata.xml a machine-usable upstream identity (remote-id plus a product-scoped bugs-to URL) so tooling can map the package to its upstream.

problem: One more flaky/broken autotest (kdatetimeedittest) failing in the sandboxed build environment (bug 948896).
root_cause: Individual KDE autotests depend on environment quirks (timezones, locales, focus) that fail headless; the rest of the suite is fine.
fix_pattern: Append the test name to the ecm/cmake eclass CMAKE_SKIP_TESTS array, with the Gentoo bug number as a comment directly above each entry.
rule: When only specific cmake/ctest tests are broken, list them in CMAKE_SKIP_TESTS with a per-entry bug-number comment instead of restricting the whole suite.

problem: Every test in the suite was broken or required a running KDE/D-Bus session, and the ebuild was accumulating CMAKE_SKIP_TESTS entries one by one (bug 977471).
root_cause: The whole suite needs a live desktop environment unavailable in the build sandbox, so per-test skipping had degenerated into skipping everything.
fix_pattern: Delete the CMAKE_SKIP_TESTS array entirely and set RESTRICT="test" with a one-line reason comment citing the bug.
rule: Skip tests individually while most of the suite works, but once effectively all tests need an unavailable environment, switch to RESTRICT="test" with a documented reason - do not maintain an exhaustive skip list.
snippet:
# requires running kde environment, bug #977471
RESTRICT="test"

problem: make check failed (bug 978743), but inspection showed the 'tests' are only source-style lint checks (shellcheck, tabs, security-pattern greps), not functional tests of the built program.
root_cause: Upstream's test target is a developer aid auditing the source tree, so failures say nothing about the built package and can break on the packager's environment.
fix_pattern: Override src_test with a commented no-op ( : ) explaining that upstream tests only lint source code, rather than RESTRICT=test, so the rationale lives next to the code.
rule: If upstream's test target only lints sources rather than exercising the product, neutralize src_test with an explanatory no-op instead of chasing lint failures.

problem: Repository skeleton still recommended EAPI 8 after EAPI 9 became current, and gave no guidance about EAPI choice for Portage's own dependency chain.
root_cause: New EAPIs cannot be used by packages Portage must upgrade through, because an older installed Portage cannot source them.
fix_pattern: Bump skel.ebuild to EAPI=9 and add a comment: packages that are direct or indirect dependencies of Portage must not use an EAPI newer than the latest Portage release supports.
rule: For any package in Portage's upgrade path (its direct or indirect deps), cap EAPI at what the latest released Portage supports; everything else may use the newest EAPI.
snippet:
# If your package is a (direct or indirect) dependency of Portage, use the
# same EAPI as the latest Portage version.
EAPI=9

problem: One-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to py3.15, no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to py3.15 (also drops python3_11/pypy3_11), no other changes.
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to py3.15, no other changes.

Tests failed with the latest setuptools-scm (vcs_versioning split) blocking py3.15 (bug 976748), and the ebuild carried a duplicate manual BDEPEND for a pytest plugin already declared via EPYTEST_PLUGINS.
root_cause: A newer version of a test-only dependency changed behavior/module layout; separately, EPYTEST_PLUGINS entries already generate the corresponding test BDEPEND automatically, so the manual >=dev-python/pytest-subprocess-1.5 dep was redundant.
fix_pattern: Backport the upstream test-fix commit as files/${P}-test.patch citing the PR URL; drop the manual pytest-subprocess BDEPEND and rely on EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-subprocess ) to pull it in; convert a per-test _EPYTEST_DESELECT of an unpackagable test file into EPYTEST_IGNORE of the whole file. No revbump since only tests changed.
rule: List pytest plugins the suite needs in EPYTEST_PLUGINS and delete duplicated manual plugin BDEPENDs - distutils_enable_tests generates those deps; when a whole test file needs an unpackaged dep, use EPYTEST_IGNORE rather than deselecting individual tests.

problem: py3.15 compat bump that also drops pytest-asyncio/pytest-rerunfailures test deps no longer loaded now that pytest plugin autoloading is off by default in distutils-r1.
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to py3.15, no other changes.
External kernel modules were not reliably rebuilt/refreshed against modprep-built kernels, and Portage's depgraph resolution around dist-kernels was weaker without the virtual link.
root_cause: Without a PDEPEND on virtual/dist-kernel, packages that rebuild against the dist-kernel (via linux-mod-r1's dist-kernel machinery) have no graph edge tying them to this kernel provider.
fix_pattern: Add PDEPEND=">=virtual/dist-kernel-${PV}" to every live ebuild of the kernel package - PDEPEND (not RDEPEND) because it must not create an install-order cycle, only a post-install rebuild trigger.
rule: When a package provides something consumers must be rebuilt against (kernels vs out-of-tree modules), express the link as a versioned PDEPEND on the shared virtual so Portage schedules the rebuilds without dependency cycles.

problem: Build broke (bug 978744) because the package's Makefile-driven build internally invokes PEP517 tooling that was not declared as a dependency.
root_cause: Upstream switched its Python-part build to call 'python -m build' (dev-python/build) under the hood, but the ebuild does not use distutils-r1's PEP517 machinery, so nothing pulled the tool in.
fix_pattern: Add dev-python/build[${PYTHON_USEDEP}] to unconditional BDEPEND in both the release and 9999 ebuilds as an explicit workaround, with a note that a proper fix comes later.
rule: When a non-distutils ebuild wraps a build system that shells out to Python packaging tools (python -m build, pip, installer), declare those tools explicitly in BDEPEND - the eclass will not do it for you.

problem: One-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to py3.15 (drops pypy3_11/python3_11), no other changes.
Package still compiled against EOL log4j 1.x; needed to move to log4j 2's 1.2-compat API without dragging log4j into runtime deps
root_cause: log4j:1.2 is dead/vulnerable; dev-java/log4j-12-api provides the 1.2 API on log4j 2, and here it is only needed at compile time by the ant build
fix_pattern: New revision puts >=dev-java/log4j-12-api-2.25.2:0 in DEPEND only (not CP_DEPEND/RDEPEND) and symlinks the jar into the ant lib dir with java-pkg_jar-from --build-only in src_prepare; also rm's test sources that break javadoc generation on modern JDK since tests never run
rule: When a Java package only compiles against log4j 1.x APIs, satisfy it with dev-java/log4j-12-api as a build-only dep via DEPEND plus java-pkg_jar-from --build-only, keeping runtime CP_DEPEND minimal
snippet:
DEPEND=">=dev-java/log4j-12-api-2.25.2:0 ..."
src_prepare() {
	java-pkg_clean
	java-pkg-2_src_prepare
	java-pkg_jar-from --build-only --into lib/ log4j-12-api
	java-pkg_jar-from --into lib/ mchange-commons
}

problem: Revbump changed the library's classpath (log4j 1.x replaced by log4j 2 + shim); already-installed consumers built against the old classpath would break
root_cause: java-pkg records dependency jars in the installed package's classpath metadata; reverse deps compiled against the old jar set keep referencing jars that no longer exist
fix_pattern: Add !<version blockers against the reverse dependencies (c3p0, sleuthkit) in RDEPEND of the new revision so portage upgrades them in lockstep
rule: When a Java library revbump changes its provided classpath or dependency set, add !<consumer-old-version blockers in RDEPEND to force reverse deps to rebuild/upgrade together

problem: Version bump needed optional log4j support and hit two test breakages: a test importing junit-jupiter FieldSource not in packaged junit 5, and a module conflict when junit:5 is built with USE=vintage
root_cause: Optional logging backends are modeled as USE=log4j propagated through the commons-logging chain; the packaged junit 5 lags upstream features; vintage engine exports a conflicting 'junit' module
fix_pattern: IUSE=log4j with [log4j=] USE deps on commons-logging/beanutils/digester; in src_prepare, if use log4j, extend the classpath with java-pkg_getjars --build-only --with-dependencies; rm the single incompatible test source with a comment quoting the compile error; DEPEND on dev-java/junit:5[-vintage] with an explanatory comment
rule: Gate optional Java deps behind a USE flag propagated with [flag=] and inject their jars via java-pkg_getjars in src_prepare; delete individual incompatible test sources (with the error quoted in a comment) instead of disabling the whole suite
snippet:
src_prepare() {
	java-pkg-2_src_prepare
	if use log4j; then
		JAVA_GENTOO_CLASSPATH_EXTRA="$(java-pkg_getjars --build-only --with-dependencies \
			log4j-12-api,snakeyaml,jnacl,jakarta-activation-api-1)"
	fi
	rm src/test/java/.../IBANValidatorTest.java || die
}

problem: Running the packaged junit-4 tests executed abstract/helper classes (Abstract*, TestBean, TestRule*) as tests, which upstream's Maven build excludes
root_cause: java-pkg-simple's default test discovery runs everything matching test name patterns; upstream pom.xml has explicit surefire exclusions the ebuild must replicate
fix_pattern: In src_test, build JAVA_TEST_RUN_ONLY with a find over src/test/java mirroring the pom.xml excludes, then convert file paths to class names before calling java-pkg-simple_src_test
rule: When packaged Java tests fail because helper/abstract classes get executed, replicate the upstream surefire include/exclude list via find into JAVA_TEST_RUN_ONLY (cite the pom.xml lines in a comment)
snippet:
pushd src/test/java || die
	local JAVA_TEST_RUN_ONLY=$(find * \
		! -name "Abstract*.java" ! -name "TestBean.java" \
		! -name "TestRule.java" ! -name "TestRuleSet.java" \
		-name "*TestCase.java" -o -name "*Test.java")
	JAVA_TEST_RUN_ONLY="${JAVA_TEST_RUN_ONLY//.java}"
	JAVA_TEST_RUN_ONLY="${JAVA_TEST_RUN_ONLY//\//.}"
popd

problem: Test suite needs commons-collections *test* classes that no installed jar ships, fails on JDK 17+ with reflective-access errors, and has locale-dependent tests
root_cause: Upstream test code compiles against another project's test sources; JDK 17 strongly encapsulates java.base internals; LocaleConvertUtilsTestCase assumes en_US locale
fix_pattern: Fetch the second source tarball only under test? ( ... ) in SRC_URI; in src_test compile the foreign test sources into a helper jar (override JAVA_JAR_FILENAME/JAVA_SRC_DIR, call java-pkg-simple_src_compile, append :acc.jar to JAVA_GENTOO_CLASSPATH_EXTRA); add --add-opens flags via JAVA_TEST_EXTRA_ARGS when java-config -g PROVIDES_VERSION >= 17; export LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8; in src_install reset JAVA_JAR_FILENAME so the helper jar is not installed
rule: Pull test-only auxiliary sources with a test?-conditional SRC_URI and compile them into a classpath helper jar inside src_test; on JDK>=17 pass --add-opens through JAVA_TEST_EXTRA_ARGS for reflection-heavy suites
snippet:
local vm_version="$(java-config -g PROVIDES_VERSION)"
if ver_test "${vm_version}" -ge 17; then
	JAVA_TEST_EXTRA_ARGS+=( --add-opens=java.base/java.{io,lang,util,time,time.chrono}=ALL-UNNAMED )
fi

problem: Test-only preparation (eapply to a second tarball, copying test data, JVM version probing) lived in src_prepare behind 'use test' guards; variable order deviated from skel
root_cause: Doing test-only work in src_prepare requires explicit USE guards and runs logic before it is needed
fix_pattern: Move all test-only setup (eapply of the auxiliary tree, cp of test data, --add-opens probing, LC_ALL export) into src_test where USE=test gating is implicit; keep helper variable definitions (ACC=...) grouped after inherit and before DESCRIPTION per style
rule: Put test-only preparation in src_test rather than use-test-guarded src_prepare; the phase itself only runs with FEATURES=test so guards disappear
snippet:
src_test() {
	# locale needed for LocaleConvertUtilsTestCase
	export LC_ALL="en_US.UTF-8"
	pushd "../${ACC}-src" >> /dev/null || die
		eapply "${FILESDIR}/${ACC}-fixes.patch"
	popd
	cp -r ../"${ACC}"-src/data . || die "copy test data"
	...
}

problem: After a junit5.eclass bugfix (b1cc32e229), the foreign commons-collections test sources listed in JAVA_TEST_SRC_DIR started being executed as tests, regressing the package
root_cause: The ebuild relied on buggy eclass behavior: it listed another package's test sources in JAVA_TEST_SRC_DIR only to get them compiled, assuming their tests would never run
fix_pattern: Drop the foreign dir from JAVA_TEST_SRC_DIR; in src_test compile it into a separate acc.jar (JAVA_JAR_FILENAME=acc.jar; JAVA_SRC_DIR=<foreign>; java-pkg-simple_src_compile) and append the jar to JAVA_GENTOO_CLASSPATH_EXTRA; in src_install reset JAVA_JAR_FILENAME and JAVA_SRC_DIR so only the real jar and sources are installed
rule: Never list another package's test sources in JAVA_TEST_SRC_DIR just for compilation; build them into a helper jar on the test classpath, because eclass fixes will eventually run whatever you list
snippet:
src_test() {
	JAVA_JAR_FILENAME="acc.jar"
	JAVA_SRC_DIR=( ../"${ACC}"-src/src/test )
	java-pkg-simple_src_compile
	JAVA_GENTOO_CLASSPATH_EXTRA+=":acc.jar"
	junit5_src_test
}

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT widening to python3_15 while dropping the lagging pypy3_11 target; no code changes needed
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}, dropping pypy3_11 and raising the floor from 3.11 in one edit
Enabling python3_15 broke a dnssec test that asserted on the exact exception message text
root_cause: CPython 3.15 changed the wording of an error message that the test compared verbatim against a hardcoded string
fix_pattern: Backport the already-merged upstream fix as files/${P}-py315.patch with the upstream commit URL as the first line of the patch header, add it to PATCHES, and widen PYTHON_COMPAT in the same commit (also dropping pypy3_11, floor to 3.12)
rule: When a new Python minor breaks only tests via changed error-message wording, backport the upstream test fix as a FILESDIR patch headed by its upstream commit URL instead of deselecting the test

problem: One-line PYTHON_COMPAT widening python3_{12..14} to {12..15}; nothing else needed
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}, dropping pypy3_11 and the 3.11 floor
BDEPEND still mirrored ${RDEPEND} with a bug-referencing comment for a build script's pytz usage, although upstream had switched to stdlib zoneinfo long ago
root_cause: Dep-mirroring hacks (BDEPEND="${RDEPEND}") added for one historical reason are never revisited on version bumps, so they silently pin obsolete deps into the build env
fix_pattern: Verify against the upstream commit that removed the need (cited in the commit message), then delete both the ${RDEPEND} reference in BDEPEND and its justification comment
rule: On bumps, re-verify any BDEPEND=${RDEPEND} or similar dep-mirroring hack against current upstream and remove it together with its comment once obsolete, citing the upstream change
snippet:
-# RDEPEND in BDEPEND for import_cldr.py usage, bug #852158
 BDEPEND="
 	app-arch/unzip
-	${RDEPEND}

problem: A for-loop variable ('dir') in python_test was not declared local, leaking into the global shell environment of the ebuild
root_cause: bash loop variables are global unless declared local; in ebuild phase functions this can clobber other phases' or eclasses' state
fix_pattern: Add 'local dir' before the loop, in both the release and the 9999 ebuild
rule: Declare every loop and temporary variable local in ebuild/eclass functions; apply the same fix to the live ebuild so it propagates to future versions

problem: pkgcheck QA warning VariableOrderWrong: KEYWORDS declared before LICENSE/SLOT
root_cause: Ebuild deviated from the canonical skel.ebuild global-variable ordering (DESCRIPTION, HOMEPAGE, SRC_URI, S, LICENSE, SLOT, KEYWORDS, IUSE, deps)
fix_pattern: Reorder to LICENSE, SLOT, KEYWORDS and refresh the copyright year; no revbump needed since output metadata is unchanged
rule: Keep the skel.ebuild variable order in ebuilds; run pkgcheck scan after edits to catch VariableOrderWrong and fix it without a revbump

problem: Upstream CMake creates a backwards-compat lowercase .so symlink via install(CODE execute_process ...) in ${CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX}/${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}, which writes outside the DESTDIR staging dir (sandbox violation / file missing from image)
root_cause: install(CODE) scripts that hardcode CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX run at install time against the real prefix and are never rebased onto DESTDIR
fix_pattern: Patch CMakeLists.txt to create the symlink at build time with add_custom_command(TARGET ... POST_BUILD ... BYPRODUCTS) in the build dir, then install it with a regular install(FILES $<TARGET_FILE_DIR:tgt>/link.so DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}); also sed out the MSVC-only __cdecl attribute from public headers
rule: When upstream CMake uses install(CODE ...CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX...) for symlinks or post-install steps, patch it into a POST_BUILD custom command plus install(FILES) so DESTDIR staging is honored
snippet:
add_custom_command(TARGET CAPSImage POST_BUILD
    COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND} -E create_symlink libCAPSImage.so libcapsimage.so
    BYPRODUCTS libcapsimage.so
    WORKING_DIRECTORY $<TARGET_FILE_DIR:CAPSImage>)
install(FILES $<TARGET_FILE_DIR:CAPSImage>/libcapsimage.so
    DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR} COMPONENT Runtime)

problem: Comment-only commit documenting why Python binding tests are not wired up (upstream check-local merely relinks with -Wl,--no-undefined); package-specific
setuptools BDEPEND was wrapped in python_gen_cond_dep listing only python3_12, so newer impls (3.13/3.14) never pulled setuptools; enabling py3.15 made the conditional fully obsolete
root_cause: python_gen_cond_dep with a literal impl list does not extend when new implementations are added to PYTHON_COMPAT; setuptools is needed on every impl >= 3.12 since distutils was removed from the stdlib
fix_pattern: Raise PYTHON_COMPAT to python3_{12..15}, replace the python_gen_cond_dep block with an unconditional dev-python/setuptools[${PYTHON_USEDEP}], and revbump (r3 -> r4) because dependencies changed
rule: Impl-list conditionals like python_gen_cond_dep '...' python3_12 rot as PYTHON_COMPAT moves; audit them on every compat bump and flatten to an unconditional dep (with a revbump) once all supported impls need it

problem: Package does 'import selinux' but only depended on sys-libs/libselinux[python] without impl matching, so libselinux bindings could exist for a different Python than the one this package installs into
root_cause: A bare [python] USE dep only guarantees bindings for *some* implementation; importing a module requires bindings built for each implementation in this package's PYTHON_TARGETS
fix_pattern: Change the dep to >=sys-libs/libselinux-${PV}:=[python,${PYTHON_USEDEP}] and revbump all affected versions (3.10 -> -r1, 3.11 -> -r1) plus the 9999 ebuild
rule: Whenever a package imports another package's Python bindings, depend on it with [python,${PYTHON_USEDEP}] (both flags), never [python] alone, and revbump since the dependency string changed

problem: One-line PYTHON_COMPAT widening to python3_15 in release and 9999 ebuilds in lockstep
SELinux userland lacked ~riscv although its library deps were already keyworded, blocking the arch's SELinux stack
root_cause: Arch keywording proceeds dependency-first; consumers need the keyword forwarded once their deps have it, and KEYWORDS here lives inside the [[ ${PV} == 9999 ]] else-branch shared with the live ebuild
fix_pattern: Add ~riscv to KEYWORDS in both the release ebuild and the identical 9999 ebuild so the next version copied from the live template keeps the keyword; repeat across the whole package set (secilc, semodule-utils, selinux-python) in dependency order
rule: When forwarding a new arch keyword, walk the reverse-dep package set in dependency order and also update live/9999 ebuilds that carry a conditional KEYWORDS block, so future release copies inherit it

problem: Same ~riscv keyword forward as policycoreutils, applied to release and 9999 ebuilds; no new idiom beyond the set-wide pattern
Same ~riscv keyword forward across the SELinux package set; nothing beyond the pattern already captured
Same ~riscv keyword forward across the SELinux package set; nothing beyond the pattern already captured
Live-ebuild housekeeping: only the copyright year line updated to keep the 9999 template current before copying to a release
Live-ebuild housekeeping: only the copyright year line updated in mcstrans-9999; no functional change.
Live-ebuild housekeeping: copyright year bump only in semodule-utils-9999.
The -9999 live ebuild still advertised python3_11 in PYTHON_COMPAT after release ebuilds had dropped it, so the live build could be attempted against a Python the package set no longer supports.
root_cause: Live ebuilds are not touched by version bumps, so metadata like PYTHON_COMPAT silently drifts from the release ebuilds.
fix_pattern: Periodic sync pass over -9999 ebuilds: trim PYTHON_COMPAT to match the newest release ebuild (python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..14}); same pass covers copyright year.
rule: When you change PYTHON_COMPAT (or similar compat variables) in release ebuilds, apply the same change to the package's -9999 live ebuild in the same sweep.

problem: Same live-ebuild sync as libsemanage: PYTHON_COMPAT in -9999 trimmed from python3_{11..14} to python3_{12..14}.
Live-ebuild housekeeping: copyright year bump only in secilc-9999.

Two QA issues in exim 4.99.4: upstream's miscmods Makefile invoked bare `ld -r` for partial linking of the dkim module (breaks cross toolchains/LTO and trips the direct-ld QA check, bug 976375), and `dodoc -r doc/.` installed the whole doc directory including unwanted files (bug 976376).
root_cause: Upstream hardcoded `ld` instead of letting the compiler driver do the relocatable link; ebuild used a recursive dodoc of the entire doc dir instead of an explicit file list.
fix_pattern: Revbump to -r1 with a FILESDIR patch replacing `ld -r` by `$(CC) -r` in the Makefile (compiler driver picks the right cross ld and flags), and change docs install to enumerate wanted files: `dodoc "${S}"/doc/{ChangeLog,NewStuff,README,README.SIEVE}` plus `dodoc "${S}"/doc/*.txt`. Note the new revision also dropped back to ~arch keywords across the board, restarting stabilization.
rule: When a build system calls `ld -r` directly for partial/relocatable linking, patch it to use `$(CC) -r` so the cross-prefixed toolchain and LTO plugins are used; and install docs by explicit list, not `dodoc -r dir/.`.
snippet:
-	$(FE)ld -r -o dkim.o $(LDFLAGS_PARTIAL) \
+	$(FE)$(CC) -r -o dkim.o $(LDFLAGS_PARTIAL) \
 		dkim_tmp.o dkim_transport.o pdkim.o signing.o

problem: Package not yet enabled for the newly-added CPython 3.15 target, blocking users on the new interpreter.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT is an explicit opt-in list; every dev-python package must be individually verified and extended when a new impl lands in python-utils-r1.
fix_pattern: After the test suite passes on the new interpreter, extend the brace range in PYTHON_COMPAT in the existing ebuild (no revbump needed, metadata-only widening): python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}. Done in dependency order (test deps like pytest plugins first).
rule: When a new CPython version becomes available in the eclass, run the package's tests on it and, if green, widen the PYTHON_COMPAT brace range in place without a revbump; enable test-only dependencies before their consumers.

problem: Routine py3.15 enablement: one-line PYTHON_COMPAT widening, same pattern as zope-configuration.
Routine py3.15 enablement of a pytest plugin (test-dep first ordering), one-line PYTHON_COMPAT change.
Routine py3.15 enablement, one-line PYTHON_COMPAT change.
Routine py3.15 enablement, one-line PYTHON_COMPAT change.

Ebuild worked around a failing pycurl redirect-credentials test by sed-deleting the test case; the deletion hid a genuine test bug (str.replace hit every 'http://' occurrence, corrupting the redirect target URL) and asserted nothing.
root_cause: Quick sed-based test removal was a stopgap for an upstream test defect; once the fix was written and submitted upstream (PR tornadoweb/tornado#3678), keeping the deletion would diverge from upstream and lose test coverage.
fix_pattern: Replace the src_prepare sed hack with a proper backported patch in PATCHES=( ), carrying the upstream PR URL as a comment so the patch can be dropped when it merges. The patch fixes the test (replace(..., 1) to limit substitution) instead of deleting it, and documents a known libcurl regression it deliberately skips.
rule: When you sed-delete or skip a failing test, track the upstream issue; as soon as an upstream fix exists, swap the deletion for the backported patch referenced by its PR/commit URL so coverage returns and the patch has a clear removal condition.
snippet:
PATCHES=(
	# https://github.com/tornadoweb/tornado/pull/3678
	"${FILESDIR}/${P}-pycurl-7.47.0.patch"
)
# removed: sed -i -e '/credentials in URL/d' tornado/test/httpclient_test.py

problem: Routine py3.15 enablement, one-line PYTHON_COMPAT change (package also carries rust ext via CRATES, untouched).
New CPAN release 0.08 needed in-tree (bug 978648), but CPAN's two-digit float versioning does not match Gentoo's dotted PV.
root_cause: perl-module.eclass normalizes CPAN versions: upstream 0.08 becomes Gentoo PV 0.80.0, so the ebuild must declare the raw upstream version separately or SRC_URI breaks.
fix_pattern: Set DIST_VERSION to the literal CPAN version and DIST_AUTHOR to the CPAN uploader before inheriting perl-module; name the ebuild with the normalized PV. Bump the dependency floor (Object-Pad) to what the new version requires in the same commit.
rule: When bumping a dev-perl package, compute the Gentoo PV via CPAN version normalization and set DIST_VERSION to the untranslated upstream version so perl-module.eclass fetches the right tarball.
snippet:
DIST_AUTHOR=PEVANS
DIST_VERSION=0.08   # upstream CPAN version; ebuild PV is normalized 0.80.0
inherit perl-module
RDEPEND=">=dev-perl/Object-Pad-0.823.0"

problem: XS module failed to build against perl 5.44 (bug 978474): BDB.xs defines a local `static void atfork_child(void)` which suddenly became a redeclaration error.
root_cause: Perl 5.43.2+ added Perl_atfork_child() to its public API with an embed.h macro `atfork_child -> Perl_atfork_child`; the macro rewrites the package's private symbol into a conflicting non-static declaration.
fix_pattern: FILESDIR patch: `#undef atfork_child`, rename the local function to a prefixed name (bdb_atfork_child) and update its single call site; patch header cites the Gentoo bug and the rt.cpan.org upstream report. Added to the existing -r1 in place (no revbump) since it only unbreaks compilation and changes nothing for already-installed systems.
rule: When a new interpreter/library release introduces a macro or API name colliding with a package's private symbol, #undef the macro and rename the local symbol with a package prefix, and file/link the fix upstream; a compile-only fix needs no revbump.
snippet:
+/* Perl 5.43.2+ defines atfork_child as a macro expanding to
+ * Perl_atfork_child. Use a different name to avoid the collision. */
+#undef atfork_child
-static void atfork_child (void)
+static void bdb_atfork_child (void)
...
-X_THREAD_ATFORK (atfork_prepare, atfork_parent, atfork_child);
+X_THREAD_ATFORK (atfork_prepare, atfork_parent, bdb_atfork_child);

problem: Cross-compiling rust (mips-linux-gnu, bug 978022) broke: libssh2-sys passed -I/usr/include for the build host to the target compiler, picking up CBUILD's openssl multilib wrapper headers.
root_cause: Two issues: globally exported PKG_CONFIG_PATH/OPENSSL_* leaked into build-host (CBUILD) crate compilations too, and libz-sys's system-zlib probing injected host include paths into dependent -sys crates (same failure Google/Chromium hit).
fix_pattern: Scope the env vars to the target triple, which the pkg-config and openssl crates support natively: PKG_CONFIG_PATH_<triple> / PKG_CONFIG_LIBDIR_<triple> (triple with . and - mapped to _) and <TRIPLE>_OPENSSL_DIR/_INCLUDE_DIR/_LIB_DIR (uppercased), respecting any pre-set user values; plus export LIBZ_SYS_STATIC=1 so libz-sys builds its own zlib and skips host probing entirely.
rule: When cross-compiling anything built by cargo with -sys crates, never export global PKG_CONFIG_*/OPENSSL_* vars — use the per-target-triple variants (PKG_CONFIG_PATH_${triple}, ${TRIPLE}_OPENSSL_DIR) pointed at ESYSROOT, and set LIBZ_SYS_STATIC=1 to stop host zlib include leakage.
snippet:
pcvar="PKG_CONFIG_PATH_${CHOST//./_}"; pcvar="${pcvar//-/_}"
printf -v "${pcvar}" "${ESYSROOT}/usr/$(get_libdir)/pkgconfig"; export "${pcvar}"
osslvar="${CHOST}_OPENSSL_DIR"; osslvar="${osslvar^^}"; osslvar="${osslvar//-/_}"
printf -v "${osslvar}" "${ESYSROOT}/usr"; export "${osslvar}"
# https://github.com/rust-lang/libz-sys/blob/1.1.18/build.rs#L25
export LIBZ_SYS_STATIC=1

problem: A previously-stabilized gentoo-sources version needed to stop being offered as stable (superseded/regressed) without being removed from the tree.
root_cause: Deleting the ebuild would strand users and lose history; the lighter tool is dropping stable keywords so the version stays installable but only for ~arch users.
fix_pattern: One-line 'destabilize' commit: rewrite KEYWORDS turning every stable arch back into ~arch, leaving the ebuild otherwise intact.
rule: When a stable version turns out to be superseded or regressed but should remain available, destabilize it by prefixing all stable KEYWORDS with ~ instead of removing the ebuild.
snippet:
-KEYWORDS="~alpha amd64 arm arm64 ~hppa ~m68k ~mips ppc ppc64 ~riscv ~s390 ~sparc x86"
+KEYWORDS="~alpha ~amd64 ~arm ~arm64 ~hppa ~m68k ~mips ~ppc ~ppc64 ~riscv ~s390 ~sparc ~x86"

problem: Same destabilize pattern as 6.18.38: all stable KEYWORDS of 6.12.95 reverted to ~arch in one line.
Same destabilize pattern as 6.18.38: all stable KEYWORDS of 6.6.144 reverted to ~arch.
Routine kernel-2 eclass bump: new gentoo-sources 7.1.3 ebuild pointing at genpatches revision via K_GENPATCHES_VER=5; patchset content changes live in the genpatches tarballs, not the ebuild.

Upstream meson.build force-added hardening and LTO flags globally (-fstack-protector-strong, -fcf-protection=full, -mbranch-protection=standard, -flto=auto, -D_FORTIFY_SOURCE=3), overriding user CFLAGS and breaking builds/policies (bugs 975182, 975614).
root_cause: Build systems that unconditionally add_global_arguments() toolchain-policy flags fight the distro toolchain defaults and user flags; forced -flto in particular breaks static-archive consumers and non-LTO toolchains.
fix_pattern: Version-refreshed respect-flags patch stripping the forced flag blocks from meson.build while deliberately keeping upstream's semantic choices (init/clear behavior). Ebuild pairs it with dot-a eclass lto-guarantee-fat + strip-lto-bytecode for static archives, tc-ld-is-mold && tc-ld-force-bfd, and disables auto-detected doxygen via a meson native/cross file mapping the binary to a nonexistent name. Same patch synced into the -9999 ebuild replacing the two older patches it merges.
rule: When upstream's build system force-adds hardening/LTO/FORTIFY flags, carry a respect-flags patch that deletes those add_global_arguments blocks (refreshing it each bump), and let the toolchain/user flags govern; disable unwanted auto-detected tools via a meson native-file [binaries] override rather than a dependency.
snippet:
# kill forced flags in meson.build (respect-flags patch), plus:
local native_file="${T}"/meson.${CHOST}.${ABI}.ini.local
cat >> ${native_file} <<-EOF || die
[binaries]
doxygen='doxygen-falseified'
EOF
emesonargs+=( --native-file "${native_file}" )

problem: Package still inherited the legacy qmake-utils eclass after Gentoo consolidated Qt helper functions into qt-utils.eclass.
root_cause: Eclass consolidation/rename: qt-utils.eclass supersedes qmake-utils with a compatible API, so consumers must migrate before the old eclass is removed.
