fix_pattern: Pure inherit swap — `inherit cmake qmake-utils xdg` becomes `inherit cmake qt-utils xdg` with no other ebuild changes, because the helper function names used by the ebuild are provided by the successor eclass.
rule: When Gentoo deprecates an eclass in favor of a consolidated successor with a compatible API, migrate by swapping the inherit line and verifying every eclass function the ebuild calls exists in the new eclass.
snippet:
-inherit cmake qmake-utils xdg
+inherit cmake qt-utils xdg

problem: Same qmake-utils -> qt-utils inherit swap as qelectrotech, applied to wireshark-9999.
Same qmake-utils -> qt-utils inherit swap, applied to mkvtoolnix-9999.
Same qmake-utils -> qt-utils inherit swap, applied to freecad-9999.

Ebuild inherited qmake-utils only for Qt6 helper functions (qt6_get_bindir etc.) after those helpers were split out into the new qt-utils.eclass and qmake-utils' qt5_* functions became banned in EAPI 9.
root_cause: Gentoo split generic Qt query helpers out of qmake-utils.eclass into qt-utils.eclass (EAPI 8/9); qmake-utils is being phased down and its qt5_* functions are banned/deprecated going forward.
fix_pattern: Tree-wide mechanical migration: swap 'qmake-utils' for 'qt-utils' on the inherit line wherever the ebuild only uses the qt6_*/generic query helpers; no other change needed because the function names are unchanged.
rule: When the main tree splits helper functions into a successor eclass (qmake-utils -> qt-utils), migrate overlay ebuilds by swapping the inherit as long as they only call the functions that moved; do it before bumping to an EAPI where the old functions are banned.

problem: Same tree-wide qmake-utils -> qt-utils inherit swap as dev-python/pyside commit c8789159.
Same tree-wide qmake-utils -> qt-utils inherit swap as dev-python/pyside commit c8789159.
Same tree-wide qmake-utils -> qt-utils inherit swap as dev-python/pyside commit c8789159.
Same tree-wide qmake-utils -> qt-utils inherit swap as dev-python/pyside commit c8789159 (plus copyright year bump).
Same tree-wide qmake-utils -> qt-utils inherit swap as dev-python/pyside commit c8789159.

New Go+CMake hybrid package whose upstream build downloads sources at build time (Go modules; CMake ExternalProject git-clones llama.cpp), bundles ggml, and fails to compile with GCC 17 - all incompatible with Gentoo's network-sandboxed, unbundled builds.
root_cause: Upstream assumes online builds: go.mod fetches modules, cmake/local.cmake ExternalProject_Add clones llama.cpp from GitHub at a pinned tag, ggml is vendored, and llama.cpp's common.h relies on transitive <fstream> includes that GCC 17 no longer provides.
fix_pattern: inherit cmake go-module with a pre-vendored ${P}-deps.tar.xz hosted at github.com/gentoo-golang-dist; add the pinned llama.cpp tag tarball to SRC_URI; patch ExternalProject_Add GIT_REPOSITORY line to 'DOWNLOAD_COMMAND true', then after cmake_src_configure rm+symlink the unpacked tree into ${BUILD_DIR}/_deps/llama_cpp-src; unbundle ggml with -DLLAMA_USE_SYSTEM_GGML=ON, DEPEND on sci-ml/ggml, and patch the install-target lib list to drop ggml-base/ggml; add missing '#include <fstream>' for gcc17; patch cmake_minimum_required 3.5->3.10; use 'cd ..; eapply' in src_prepare for a patch targeting a WORKDIR sibling outside ${S}; rm -rf ${D}/var in src_install; RESTRICT="mirror test".
rule: When a CMake build fetches a sub-project at build time via ExternalProject/FetchContent, add the pinned tarball to SRC_URI, neutralize the download (DOWNLOAD_COMMAND true) and symlink the unpacked source into the _deps/<name>-src path the build expects after configure.
snippet:
src_configure() {
	local mycmakeargs=( -DLLAMA_USE_SYSTEM_GGML=ON )
	cmake_src_configure
	rm -rf "${BUILD_DIR}"/_deps/llama_cpp-src || die
	ln -s "${WORKDIR}"/llama.cpp-${LLAMA_CPP_tag} \
		"${BUILD_DIR}"/_deps/llama_cpp-src || die
}

problem: Package not yet enabled for the newly added python3_15 target; still carried de-targeted python3_11 and pypy3_11 in PYTHON_COMPAT.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT is an explicit opt-in list: every new CPython release requires per-package enablement after the test suite passes, and implementations dropped from the tree's targets should be removed at the same time.
fix_pattern: Single edit shifting the brace range: PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{11..14} pypy3_11 ) -> PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} ), i.e. add the new impl and drop old/EOL impls in one commit, after testing under 3.15 (representative of an 18-commit mgorny batch in this window).
rule: When a new CPython target lands, bump the PYTHON_COMPAT brace range and drop de-targeted implementations (old python3_N, stale pypy3) in the same edit, but only after running the test suite under the new interpreter.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} (same idiom as zope-schema a81f937f).
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; Rust-extension package but same one-line idiom as zope-schema a81f937f.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} (same idiom as zope-schema a81f937f).
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} (same idiom as zope-schema a81f937f).

py3.15 enablement carried legacy test boilerplate: a python_test() that set PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1, ran 'epytest -p trio', held a local EPYTEST_DESELECT array, and duplicated the pytest-trio dep in BDEPEND.
root_cause: The manual plugin-autoload-control idiom predates distutils-r1's declarative EPYTEST_* variables; distutils_enable_tests now generates the plugin test-deps and epytest consumes global EPYTEST_DESELECT, so the custom phase is pure duplication.
fix_pattern: Delete python_test() entirely: declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-trio ) (which both restricts plugin autoloading to the listed plugins and auto-adds them to test deps, so pytest-trio was dropped from BDEPEND) and move EPYTEST_DESELECT to a global array before distutils_enable_tests pytest.
rule: When a python_test() only disables plugin autoload, passes -p plugins, and deselects tests, replace it with global EPYTEST_PLUGINS and EPYTEST_DESELECT and remove the now-redundant plugin entries from BDEPEND.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} (same idiom as zope-schema a81f937f).
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} (same idiom as zope-schema a81f937f).
Flaky test suite handled by hand-rolled python_test() running 'epytest -p timeout -p rerunfailures --reruns=3' with pytest-timeout and pytest-rerunfailures listed manually in BDEPEND.
root_cause: distutils-r1 gained declarative EPYTEST_RERUNS and EPYTEST_PLUGINS which generate both the epytest flags and the test dependencies, making the manual phase and BDEPEND block redundant.
fix_pattern: Drop python_test() and the test? BDEPEND block; set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-timeout ) and EPYTEST_RERUNS=5 before distutils_enable_tests pytest - the eclass pulls in pytest-rerunfailures automatically when EPYTEST_RERUNS is set; keep the src_test() override that exports env (INPUTRC) since that is orthogonal.
rule: For flaky pytest suites, set EPYTEST_RERUNS=N globally instead of invoking epytest with -p rerunfailures --reruns; the eclass adds the pytest-rerunfailures dependency itself.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} (same idiom as zope-schema a81f937f).

Test run needed pytest plugin autoloading disabled (to avoid interference from installed plugins) but no plugins loaded; previously required a manual PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 in python_test().
root_cause: An explicitly empty EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() array tells distutils-r1 to run pytest with plugin autoloading disabled while declaring zero plugin dependencies - distinct from leaving the variable unset (autoload stays on).
fix_pattern: Declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest during the py3.15 bump; keep any remaining python_test() logic that does real work.
rule: To run pytest hermetically with no plugins, set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (empty but set) rather than exporting PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD manually.

problem: Package is itself a pytest plugin; testing it required a manual python_test() with PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 and 'epytest -p pytest_tornasync.plugin' because the -p argument needs the entry-point module path, not the package name.
root_cause: For self-testing pytest plugins the plugin's import name (pytest_tornasync.plugin) differs from the distribution name; the newer eclass variable EPYTEST_PLUGIN_LOAD_VIA_ENV loads plugins via the PYTEST_PLUGINS environment mechanism by entry point instead of -p module paths.
fix_pattern: Replace the custom phase with EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( "${PN}" ) plus EPYTEST_PLUGIN_LOAD_VIA_ENV=1 before distutils_enable_tests pytest.
rule: When packaging a pytest plugin that must load itself for its own test suite, set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( "${PN}" ) with EPYTEST_PLUGIN_LOAD_VIA_ENV=1 instead of hand-passing -p with the internal module path.

Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} (same idiom as zope-schema a81f937f).

Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} (same idiom as zope-schema a81f937f).
Enabling py3.15 on a poetry-built package while it still used the heavyweight 'poetry' PEP517 backend
root_cause: DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry pulls the full poetry tool as build dep; poetry-core is the actual PEP517 build backend and is all that is needed
fix_pattern: During the PYTHON_COMPAT bump, switch DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 from poetry to poetry-core in the same commit
rule: When touching a poetry-based python ebuild, set DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry-core (not poetry) unless the build genuinely needs full poetry

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15} plus copyright year, no other change
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} on a setuptools+CRATES ebuild, one-line change
Test suite could pick up stray pytest plugins from the system, risking failures on the newly enabled py3.15
root_cause: distutils_enable_tests pytest historically ran with all installed pytest plugins autoloaded; new EPYTEST_PLUGINS API lets the ebuild declare the exact plugin set
fix_pattern: Declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (empty: no plugins needed) immediately before distutils_enable_tests pytest so the eclass runs tests with plugin autoloading disabled
rule: When bumping PYTHON_COMPAT on a package using distutils_enable_tests pytest, add an explicit EPYTEST_PLUGINS array (empty if the suite needs none) to isolate tests from unrelated installed pytest plugins

problem: Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}
Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}, also dropping pypy3_11 from the target list
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}, dropping pypy3_11
Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}, dropping pypy3_11
Wanted to enable py3.15 testing but some test deps (blockbuster, time-machine) did not yet support python3_15, while another dep (llhttp) had gained support for all impls
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT of a package and of its dependencies move at different speeds; a blanket [${PYTHON_USEDEP}] dep on a not-yet-ported dep blocks enabling the new impl
fix_pattern: Gate the lagging deps with python_gen_cond_dep restricted to the impls they support, and simultaneously un-gate deps that now cover every impl (move them out of python_gen_cond_dep back to plain [${PYTHON_USEDEP}])
rule: When adding a new impl to PYTHON_COMPAT, wrap each dependency that does not yet support it in $(python_gen_cond_dep '...' 3.{12..14}) instead of waiting for the whole dep tree; revisit and un-gate once the deps catch up

problem: Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}
C extension segfaulted on py3.15: find_argv_from_env() dereferenced environ which can be NULL because Python 3.15 implements os.environ.clear() as clearenv()
root_cause: New CPython version changed runtime behavior (clearenv sets environ=NULL) that the extension's environ-walking code never handled; additionally upstream setup.py had a cmdclass override making extension build failures silently fall back to a noop pure-python stub
fix_pattern: Backport the exact upstream fix commit as a FILESDIR patch (comment carries the upstream commit URL), revbump to -r1, apply via a local PATCHES array in src_prepare, and sed out the setup.py cmdclass override so a failed extension build is fatal instead of producing a broken noop fallback; keep DISTUTILS_EXT=1 with an explaining comment
rule: When a new Python version breaks a C extension, backport the upstream fix as a FILESDIR patch referenced by its upstream commit URL and revbump; if the package has a silent no-extension fallback, strip that override so build failures are loud

problem: Package had been made installable on py3.15 before its test suite passed there; tests also failed randomly from upstream's over-tight 0.5s timeouts and fragile warning-count assertions
root_cause: Test-dep availability and suite health lag behind interpreter support, so COMPAT and 'tested' sets must be tracked separately; upstream test constants are tuned for their CI, not arbitrary hardware
fix_pattern: Maintain PYTHON_TESTED=( ... ) separately from PYTHON_COMPAT=( "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}" python3_15 ), gate test deps with $(python_gen_cond_dep ... "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}"), then fold the new impl into PYTHON_TESTED once tests pass; sed-relax the LONG_TIMEOUT constant, sed-delete fragile warning assertions, deselect flaky tests, and use EPYTEST_RERUNS for the rest
rule: To ship a package on a new Python impl before its tests are ready, split PYTHON_TESTED from PYTHON_COMPAT and condition test deps and test runs on PYTHON_TESTED; merge the impl back in once green
snippet:
PYTHON_TESTED=( python3_{12..15} )
PYTHON_COMPAT=( "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}" )
...
$(python_gen_cond_dep "..." "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}")
# in python_test:
local EPYTEST_RERUNS=10

problem: PYTHON_COMPAT bump to py3.15 plus sed-removal of one test broken by a known upstream bug, annotated with the upstream issue URL
Live -9999 emulator ebuild depended on media-libs/jg:1= but upstream git now builds against the Jolly Good API v2 header
root_cause: media-libs/jg is a header-only package (its src_compile is a no-op), so the := slot operator conveyed no ABI meaning; the real constraint is a minimum header/API version, and until 2.0.0 is released only jg-9999 satisfies it
fix_pattern: Replace the slot-operator dep with a plain minimum-version dep: media-libs/jg:1= -> >=media-libs/jg-2.0.0 in DEPEND (RDEPEND=${DEPEND} follows)
rule: Do not put := slot operators on header-only build deps; when a live ebuild starts requiring a newer API header, express it as a >= version bound (which a -9999 dep version also satisfies)

problem: Same jg:1= -> >=media-libs/jg-2.0.0 dep retarget as vecx-jg (aa30340a), applied across the -jg emulator family
Same jg:1= -> >=media-libs/jg-2.0.0 dep retarget as vecx-jg (aa30340a)

Same jg:1= -> >=media-libs/jg-2.0.0 dep retarget as vecx-jg (aa30340a), here inside a jgmodule? USE conditional
Same jg:1= -> >=media-libs/jg-2.0.0 dep retarget as vecx-jg (aa30340a)
Same jg:1= -> >=media-libs/jg-2.0.0 dep retarget as vecx-jg (aa30340a)
Same jg:1= -> >=media-libs/jg-2.0.0 dep retarget as vecx-jg (aa30340a)
Live ebuild dep updated from media-libs/jg:1= slot dep to >=media-libs/jg-2.0.0 after upstream API bump; package-specific dep sync with no broader idiom.
Raw-Makefile package repeated the same make variables (PREFIX, USE_EXTERNAL_*) in both src_compile and src_install, easy to let them drift when adding a new option (ENABLE_VULKAN).
root_cause: Plain-Makefile upstreams take feature switches as make variables; duplicating the list per phase means an added option must be edited in multiple places or install behaves differently from compile.
fix_pattern: Compute the shared option list once in src_configure and export it (export MY_MAKEOPTS="${makeopts[@]}"), then splice it into per-phase arrays that add only the phase-specific vars (CC=$(tc-getCC), PKG_CONFIG=$(tc-getPKG_CONFIG), LIBDIR with get_libdir for compile; DESTDIR/DOCDIR for install). New USE=vulkan wired as ENABLE_VULKAN=$(usex vulkan 1 0) plus DEPEND on vulkan-headers/vulkan-loader/libsdl3[vulkan] and BDEPEND on dev-util/glslang for shader compilation.
rule: For Makefile-only upstreams, build the feature/option make-variable list once (src_configure) and reuse it in every emake invocation; only toolchain and DESTDIR-type variables belong per-phase.

problem: jgrf had no keyboard input when run under kmsdrm because libsdl3 was built without udev support; the plain dep media-libs/libsdl3[opengl] did not guarantee the needed backend.
root_cause: A runtime library dependency was satisfied version-wise but built without a USE flag the consumer functionally requires; SDL3's kmsdrm input path needs udev.
fix_pattern: Tighten the dep to media-libs/libsdl3[opengl,udev] and revbump the release ebuild (jgrf-1.4.0 -> jgrf-1.4.0-r1) so users re-resolve the dependency; apply the same edit to the 9999 live ebuild without revbump. Reference the upstream issue URL in the commit message (Upstream-Issue: trailer).
rule: When a package malfunctions because a dependency lacks a USE-conditional feature, encode the requirement as a USE dep (cat/pkg[flag]) and revbump the release ebuild; live ebuilds get the same fix with no revision change.

problem: twisted was installable on python3.15 but its test suite was skipped there (python3_15 sat outside PYTHON_TESTED); doctest counting, SourceFileLoader.load_module removal, and reporter API changes broke tests on 3.15.
root_cause: New CPython versions change stdlib behavior (doctest output format/counts, importlib deprecations removed, unittest addSubTest expected) so test suites need upstream porting work before they pass.
fix_pattern: Backport the upstream py3.15 compatibility PR as files/twisted-26.4.0-py315.patch, add it to PATCHES with the upstream PR URL as a comment, then promote the interpreter: PYTHON_TESTED=( python3_{12..15} ); PYTHON_COMPAT=( "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}" ). Done in the existing -r1 (patch only affects tests, no revbump needed).
rule: To finish enabling a new Python target, backport the upstream compat patch into FILESDIR with the PR URL as a comment, then move the target from the compat-only list into PYTHON_TESTED.

problem: twisted broke against newer dev-python/pyopenssl which dropped deprecated X509Req APIs; the fix upstream had not been released yet.
root_cause: pyOpenSSL is deprecating its crypto layer in favor of pyca/cryptography; consumers using OpenSSL.crypto.X509Req fail once the dependency is upgraded.
fix_pattern: Backport the whole upstream migration PR (5-patch series squashed into files/twisted-26.4.0-pyopenssl.patch, 456 lines) that ports X509Req usage to cryptography's CertificateSigningRequestBuilder, add it to PATCHES with the PR URL comment, and revbump twisted-26.4.0 -> -r1 because runtime code changes.
rule: When a dependency removes a deprecated API before your package's next release, backport the upstream migration PR as a FILESDIR patch and revbump; do not pin the old dependency version unless no fix exists.

problem: Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT widening python3_{13..14} -> python3_{13..15} after tests pass on the new interpreter.
twisted needed to be installable on python3.15 (to unblock the dependency graph) before its test suite actually passed there.
root_cause: Enabling a new CPython target across a dep-heavy stack is chicken-and-egg: reverse deps need the package importable on the new interpreter long before upstream test-suite porting lands, but blindly enabling tests produces build failures.
fix_pattern: Use the PYTHON_TESTED helper-array idiom: keep the tested interpreters in PYTHON_TESTED and append the new untested one only to PYTHON_COMPAT, so python_test can skip (or the deps be conditionalized) on untested interpreters. Later commit moves the target into PYTHON_TESTED once patches land. Simultaneously prune EOL targets (python3_11, pypy3_11) in the same edit.
rule: To stage a new Python target, split PYTHON_TESTED from PYTHON_COMPAT: PYTHON_COMPAT=( "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}" python3_XX ) makes the package installable on the new interpreter while tests keep running only on vetted ones.
snippet:
-PYTHON_TESTED=( python3_{11..14} pypy3_11 )
-PYTHON_COMPAT=( "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}" )
+PYTHON_TESTED=( python3_{12..14} )
+PYTHON_COMPAT=( "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}" python3_15 )
# python_test() can then: has "${EPYTHON/./_}" "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}" || return

problem: Test phase used the manual local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 idiom and a python3.14-only deselect that also applies to 3.15.
root_cause: distutils-r1 grew a declarative EPYTEST_PLUGINS mechanism superseding the hand-rolled autoload-disable env var; per-version test deselects written as exact [[ ${EPYTHON} == python3.14 ]] matches silently stop applying on the next interpreter.
fix_pattern: Set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest to declare 'no plugins needed' (eclass then disables plugin autoloading itself) and drop the manual PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD line; widen the version match with a glob: [[ ${EPYTHON} == python3.1[45] ]].
rule: Replace manual PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 with EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( ... ) (empty array for none) set before distutils_enable_tests, and write interpreter-conditional deselects with globs so they carry to the next Python version.
snippet:
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=()
 distutils_enable_tests pytest

-	[[ ${EPYTHON} == python3.14 ]] && EPYTEST_DESELECT+=(
+	if [[ ${EPYTHON} == python3.1[45] ]]; then
		EPYTEST_DESELECT+=( tests/.../future_test.py::FutureExceptionTest )
+	fi
-	local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1

problem: One-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}, dropping python3_11 and pypy3_11 in the same edit.
PYTHON_COMPAT widened to python3_{12..15} plus cosmetic dep-block reformat; no new idiom.
PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() addition (same idiom as pyhamcrest commit 2860a755).
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}.
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}, dropping pypy3_11.
Ebuild still used the deprecated DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit backend name and listed pytest plugin test deps by hand while manually disabling plugin autoload with an env var and -p asyncio.
root_cause: distutils-r1 renamed the flit backend value to flit-core (flit_core is the actual PEP517 backend), and grew EPYTEST_PLUGINS which both injects the plugins' BDEPEND and loads exactly those plugins with autoload disabled, superseding hand-listed deps plus PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD/-p flags.
fix_pattern: Switch DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit to flit-core; declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( hypothesis pytest-asyncio ) before distutils_enable_tests pytest and delete the corresponding dev-python/hypothesis and dev-python/pytest-asyncio entries from BDEPEND test? block; drop the manual autoload-disable and -p asyncio from python_test.
rule: Declare needed pytest plugins in EPYTEST_PLUGINS (eclass adds their deps and loads them explicitly) instead of hand-listing plugin BDEPENDs plus PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD and -p flags; use flit-core, not flit, as the PEP517 backend name.
snippet:
-DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit
+DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit-core
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( hypothesis pytest-asyncio )
 distutils_enable_tests pytest
# BDEPEND: removed dev-python/hypothesis, dev-python/pytest-asyncio
# python_test: removed PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 and '-p asyncio'

problem: pip's build-environment isolation broke on Python 3.15: system site paths were no longer stripped because .pth files are not re-executed for already-known paths, so tests failed.
root_cause: CPython 3.15 changed site.addsitedir()/.pth semantics (no re-execution for known paths), so pip's trick of calling addsitedir on a populated sys.path and diffing the tail no longer discovers system paths.
fix_pattern: Backport the upstream fix (clear sys.path before addsitedir so the discovered set is complete, then subtract) as files/pip-26.1.2-py315.patch, referenced with both the PR URL and follow-up commit hash in a PATCHES comment; also PYTHON_COMPAT bump and flit -> flit-core backend rename.
rule: When a new CPython release changes site/.pth initialization semantics, expect breakage in anything that manipulates sys.path via site.addsitedir and backport upstream fixes citing PR URL plus follow-up commit in the patch comment.

problem: The old setuptools-79 (<80) version, kept only because pip's test suite needs it, lacked python3.15 support, blocking py3.15 test enablement in dependent packages.
root_cause: Some old versions are retained purely as test fixtures for other packages; they too must gain new-interpreter support or they block the whole py3.15 test-enablement chain.
fix_pattern: Enable python3_15 in the untested slot (PYTHON_COMPAT=( "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}" python3_15 python3_{14..15}t ) including freethreading variants), declare test-only pytest plugins via local EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-{home,subprocess,timeout} ) inside python_test, and neutralize strict warning filters with epytest -o filterwarnings=.
rule: When an old version is kept alive as another package's test dependency, still extend its PYTHON_COMPAT for new interpreters (untested is fine) so the dependent's test enablement is not blocked; freethreading targets are listed as python3_XXt alongside the regular ones.
snippet:
-PYTHON_TESTED=( python3_{11..14} pypy3_11 )
-PYTHON_COMPAT=( "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}" python3_{13,14}t )
+PYTHON_TESTED=( python3_{12..14} )
+PYTHON_COMPAT=( "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}" python3_15 python3_{14..15}t )
	local EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-{home,subprocess,timeout} )
	epytest -o filterwarnings= ...

problem: glibmm build failed without Perl/XML-Parser installed: the perl-based code generator runs unconditionally, but dev-lang/perl and dev-perl/XML-Parser sat inside the gtk-doc? conditional BDEPEND block (bugs 788895, 978688).
root_cause: Over multiple refactors the perl deps were moved under a USE conditional that does not match actual build-system behavior; the loss went unnoticed because most builders already had perl installed, so only clean environments failed.
fix_pattern: Move dev-lang/perl and dev-perl/XML-Parser out of the gtk-doc? ( ... ) group into the unconditional part of BDEPEND across all four live versions; commit message uses Fixes: trailers naming the two commits that dropped the deps.
rule: A BDEPEND may only be USE-conditional if the tool is provably invoked only under that USE; when a missing-tool build failure appears in a clean chroot, check the git history for a dep that migrated into a conditional block, and restore it unconditionally in every affected version.
snippet:
BDEPEND="
 	${PYTHON_DEPS}
+	dev-lang/perl
+	dev-perl/XML-Parser
 	virtual/pkgconfig
 	gtk-doc? (
 		app-text/doxygen[dot]
-		dev-lang/perl

problem: After python3.15 became testable, the ebuild still carried the PYTHON_TESTED split, python_gen_cond_dep-wrapped test deps, and a python_test full of local EPYTEST_* boilerplate.
root_cause: The staging scaffolding (PYTHON_TESTED subset, conditional deps, tested-interpreter guard in python_test) becomes dead weight once every PYTHON_COMPAT target passes tests.
fix_pattern: Collapse PYTHON_TESTED back into PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} ); unwrap the $(python_gen_cond_dep ...) test deps into plain [${PYTHON_USEDEP}] entries; hoist EPYTEST_PLUGINS/EPYTEST_RERUNS/EPYTEST_XDIST/EPYTEST_DESELECT to global scope and let distutils_enable_tests pytest generate IUSE=test and RESTRICT, deleting the custom python_test entirely (also drop hand-written IUSE=test/RESTRICT).
rule: Once all interpreters pass tests, remove the PYTHON_TESTED scaffolding: plain PYTHON_COMPAT, unconditional test deps, and global EPYTEST_* variables consumed by distutils_enable_tests instead of a custom python_test.
snippet:
-PYTHON_TESTED=( python3_{12..14} )
-PYTHON_COMPAT=( "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}" python3_15 )
+PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} )
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( anyio pytest-mock )
+EPYTEST_RERUNS=5
+EPYTEST_XDIST=1
+distutils_enable_tests pytest
+EPYTEST_DESELECT=( ... )  # global, no python_test needed

problem: websockets failed on python3.15 because int.from_bytes() now requires C-contiguous buffers and the masking code passes reversed memoryviews; also the PyPI sdist lacks the test suite.
root_cause: CPython 3.15 optimized int.from_bytes() to use the buffer protocol with PyBUF_SIMPLE, so non-contiguous memoryviews (e.g. data[::-1]) raise BufferError; separately, upstream ships tests only in the GitHub tag, not the PyPI sdist.
fix_pattern: Add websockets-16.0-r1.ebuild (keeping -r0) with the upstream PR backported as files/${P}-py315.patch and PYTHON_COMPAT widened to python3_{12..15}; SRC_URI points at the GitHub archive with a '# tests are missing pypi sdist, as of 16.0' comment and '-> ${P}.gh.tar.gz' rename.
rule: If the PyPI sdist omits tests, fetch the GitHub tag tarball (renamed ${P}.gh.tar.gz) and note why in a comment; ship interpreter-compat code patches in a revbump rather than editing the existing revision.
snippet:
# tests are missing pypi sdist, as of 16.0
SRC_URI="
	https://github.com/python-websockets/websockets/archive/${PV}.tar.gz
		-> ${P}.gh.tar.gz
"
PATCHES=(
	# https://github.com/python-websockets/websockets/pull/1706
	"${FILESDIR}/${P}-py315.patch"
)

problem: Live 9999 emacs ebuild synced with upstream: minimum versions added to app-emacs deps, new app-emacs/llama dep, and elisp-enable-tests load argument fixed to an explicit ./test/*.el path.

check_icmp/check_dhcp were installed suid-root unconditionally; users had no way to prefer file capabilities, and 3.0.1 also failed to build with modern GCC (-Werror=incompatible-pointer-types on sockaddr_un passed to connect()).
root_cause: Privileged network checkers need raw sockets; suid-root is a bigger attack surface than targeted capabilities. Separately, GCC 14+ makes incompatible-pointer-types a hard error, breaking old code that passes struct sockaddr_un* without casting to struct sockaddr*.
fix_pattern: inherit fcaps; add USE=filecaps (via the eclass) preferred with USE=suid as fallback. In src_install: fperms ug-s to strip suid first, then fcaps [-m u+s] cap_net_bind_service,cap_net_raw on each binary — the -m u+s flag makes fcaps leave the binary suid if setcap fails. Note the path gotcha: fperms wants /path relative to ED (leading slash), fcaps prepends EPREFIX only when the path does NOT start with a slash, so the same dir variable is used as /"${pd}"/... for fperms but "${pd}"/... for fcaps. Buildfix as FILESDIR patch adding the (struct sockaddr *) cast.
rule: For suid network-check binaries, prefer capabilities: strip suid with fperms then grant via the fcaps eclass, passing '-m u+s' when a suid fallback is wanted; remember fperms paths start with / (ED-relative) while fcaps paths must not.
snippet:
inherit fcaps
local pd="usr/$(get_libdir)/nagios/plugins"
if use filecaps; then
	local flags; use suid && flags="-m u+s"  # suid fallback if setcap fails
	fperms ug-s /"${pd}"/check_{dhcp,icmp}
	fcaps ${flags} cap_net_bind_service "${pd}"/check_dhcp
	fcaps ${flags} cap_net_bind_service,cap_net_raw "${pd}"/check_icmp
else
	use suid || fperms ug-s /"${pd}"/check_{dhcp,icmp}
fi

problem: nagios-plugins 2.5 bump: same suid check_icmp unsigned-short target-count overflow as monitoring-plugins (oss-security advisory) not yet fixed upstream; plugins whined about needing root once suid was replaced by filecaps; and eautoreconf broke gettext's po build.
root_cause: Security fix from the sibling project (monitoring-plugins) applied to diverged fork; np_warn_if_not_root() hardcodes the suid assumption; eautoreconf regenerates po/Makefile.in.in with @mkdir_p@ which modern autoconf no longer defines (only @MKDIR_P@).
fix_pattern: Backport the overflow guard as a FILESDIR patch (refuse add_target past USHRT_MAX) citing the openwall advisory in the patch header; sed-delete the np_warn_if_not_root() call lines in src_prepare since filecaps make root unnecessary; after eautoreconf, sed -e 's/@mkdir_p@/@MKDIR_P@/' po/Makefile.in.in. Same fcaps/suid USE pattern as monitoring-plugins.
rule: After eautoreconf on a gettext-using package, fix po/Makefile.in.in's stale @mkdir_p@ substitution to @MKDIR_P@ or install will fail; and when a security fix lands in a diverged sibling project, check whether your fork shares the bug and backport with the advisory URL.
snippet:
eautoreconf
	# eautoreconf replaces $(MKDIR_P) with $(mkdir_p) in
	# po/Makefile.in.in. As you might expect, this does not work.
	sed -i po/Makefile.in.in -e 's/@mkdir_p@/@MKDIR_P@/' || die
	# don't warn about needing root; filecaps cover it
	sed -e '/^[ \t]*np_warn_if_not_root();$/d' \
		-i plugins-root/check_{dhcp,icmp}.c || die

problem: One-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} on a maturin/Rust package.
PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; python_gen_cond_dep 'python*' wrapper around cffi dropped as redundant once no pypy target remains in COMPAT.
PYTHON_COMPAT bump including freethreading python3_{14..15}t targets and EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() replacing a manual PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD python_test (same idiom as pyhamcrest 2860a755).
New Python target (3.15) needed enabling while some test-only functionality is not yet validated on it
root_cause: Upstream/test deps trail new CPython releases, but the package itself runs fine, so blocking the whole COMPAT bump on tests is too conservative
fix_pattern: Keep a PYTHON_FULLY_TESTED array for impls where the full test suite runs, and define PYTHON_COMPAT as that array plus the new interpreter; test logic keys off the array. Old impls (3.11, pypy3_11) dropped in the same edit.
rule: When enabling a new Python target whose tests are not fully supported yet, split PYTHON_COMPAT into a TESTED array plus the new impl instead of holding back the bump.

problem: Package not installable on the newest CPython (3.15) after the interpreter was added to the tree
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT is an explicit allowlist; every package must be bumped per-interpreter after its deps support it
fix_pattern: One-line PYTHON_COMPAT edit: python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}. Gentoo's convention during these sweeps is to add the new impl and drop the oldest (and often pypy3) in the same edit, after verifying the dep tree already supports the new impl.
rule: When rolling PYTHON_COMPAT forward for a new interpreter, do it as a brace-range shift (add newest, drop oldest/pypy) once all RDEPEND/BDEPEND already carry the new target; no revbump is needed for COMPAT-only changes.

problem: Ebuild carried a hand-rolled python_test with PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD, explicit -p asyncio, an explicit pytest-asyncio BDEPEND, and a dead python_gen_cond_dep block for py3.10
root_cause: Written before distutils-r1 grew EPYTEST_PLUGINS; conditional dep for 3.10 became dead code once 3.10 left PYTHON_COMPAT
fix_pattern: Declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-asyncio ) before distutils_enable_tests pytest: the eclass then disables plugin autoloading, loads exactly the listed plugins, and generates the test BDEPEND for them (so the explicit pytest-asyncio BDEPEND is removed). EPYTEST_DESELECT moved to a global variable, deleting python_test entirely. Dead python_gen_cond_dep '...' 3.10 removed since 3.10 is no longer in COMPAT.
rule: Replace hand-rolled PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD/epytest -p X blocks with EPYTEST_PLUGINS set before distutils_enable_tests (it also generates the plugin BDEPEND), and delete python_gen_cond_dep blocks whose target versions are no longer in PYTHON_COMPAT.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump 3.12-14 to 3.12-15, no other change
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}, no other change
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump, dropping pypy3_11 and python3_11 while adding python3_15
Ebuild still carried scaffolding for optional trio (python_gen_cond_dep window, opt-trio patch, has_version test gating) after trio gained py3.15 support
root_cause: trio temporarily lacked the newest Python target, so its test dep had been version-gated and trio tests skipped via -k filtering; once trio caught up the scaffolding became dead weight
fix_pattern: Flatten the python_gen_cond_dep block back to an unconditional dev-python/trio BDEPEND, drop the FILESDIR opt-trio patch, and remove the has_version + '-k "not trio"' logic from python_test.
rule: Version-gated test deps (python_gen_cond_dep + has_version test skips) are temporary: once the dep supports every impl in PYTHON_COMPAT, revert to a plain dep and delete the gating in the same commit.

problem: python_test contained two has_version branches to cope with trio/pytest-trio possibly missing on some impls
root_cause: trio and pytest-trio lagged py3.15; once they caught up the conditional plugin loading and -m 'not trio' filtering were obsolete
fix_pattern: Made trio an unconditional test BDEPEND, added pytest-trio to the static EPYTEST_PLUGINS list, and deleted python_test entirely (25 lines removed).
rule: After a lagging test dep gains support for all PYTHON_COMPAT impls, collapse conditional EPYTEST_PLUGINS/has_version logic into the static EPYTEST_PLUGINS array and remove python_test if nothing else remains.

problem: A pytest-plugin package testing itself needed PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD plus a manual PYTEST_PLUGINS env export so nested pytest invocations in its test suite could still see the plugin
root_cause: epytest's -p flag only affects the outer pytest process; tests that spawn nested pytest runs lose plugins loaded via -p, so the plugin must be injected through the PYTEST_PLUGINS environment variable
fix_pattern: Use EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( "${PN}" hypothesis ) with EPYTEST_PLUGIN_LOAD_VIA_ENV=1: the eclass then loads plugins via the PYTEST_PLUGINS env var (inherited by subprocesses) instead of -p, and generates the hypothesis test dep, allowing removal of both the manual python_test and the explicit BDEPEND.
rule: For packages that are pytest plugins (or whose tests spawn nested pytest), set EPYTEST_PLUGINS including "${PN}" and EPYTEST_PLUGIN_LOAD_VIA_ENV=1 so plugins propagate to subprocess pytest runs.

problem: A py3.14-only EPYTEST_DESELECT (CPython bug workaround) lingered in python_test during the 3.15 bump
root_cause: Interpreter-specific test failures were correctly scoped with case ${EPYTHON}, which makes them easy to audit and drop once the affected impl ages out or the CPython bug is fixed
fix_pattern: Scope interpreter-specific deselects in a case ${EPYTHON} block with a comment linking the upstream CPython issue; delete the whole block during the next COMPAT rotation once obsolete.
rule: Gate interpreter-specific test deselects behind case ${EPYTHON} with an upstream-issue comment so they self-document and can be removed wholesale when PYTHON_COMPAT rotates.

problem: Explicit test? ( dev-python/pytest-asyncio ) BDEPEND duplicated what the eclass can derive
root_cause: EPYTEST_PLUGINS, when set before distutils_enable_tests, both loads the plugins with autoload disabled and adds their BDEPEND automatically, making manual plugin BDEPEND redundant
fix_pattern: Dropped the test? BDEPEND block and declared EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-asyncio ) above distutils_enable_tests pytest.
rule: Never list pytest plugins in BDEPEND manually when EPYTEST_PLUGINS can declare them; the eclass generates the dependency and controls autoloading in one place.

problem: Enabling py3.15 while test dep trio only supported up to 3.14, plus new flaky upstream tests
root_cause: Lagging test dep requires rolling the python_gen_cond_dep version window forward rather than blocking the bump; unrelated test regressions surfaced on rebuild
fix_pattern: Shifted the conditional-dep window from 3.{11..13} to 3.{12..14} (trio skipped on 3.15 via existing has_version '-k "not trio"' fallback), converted to EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( anyio ), and added commented EPYTEST_DESELECT entries labelled 'random regressions'.
rule: When a test dep lags the new interpreter, roll the python_gen_cond_dep version window forward and rely on a has_version guard to skip that dep's tests on the uncovered impl.
snippet:
$(python_gen_cond_dep '
	dev-python/trio[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]
' 3.{12..14})
...
if ! has_version "dev-python/trio[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]"; then
	args+=( -o filterwarnings= -k "not trio" )
fi

problem: With plugin autoloading disabled via EPYTEST_PLUGINS, an impl-dependent optional plugin (pytest-trio) would never load on impls where it is installed
root_cause: EPYTEST_PLUGINS is static, but a version-gated plugin dep is only present on some impls, so plugin loading must become conditional inside python_test
fix_pattern: Set the base EPYTEST_PLUGINS globally, then inside python_test conditionally extend it as a local: 'local EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( "${EPYTEST_PLUGINS[@]}" pytest-trio )' when has_version confirms the plugin, else pass -m 'not trio' -o addopts= to skip those tests.
rule: To load a pytest plugin only on impls where its version-gated dep exists, shadow EPYTEST_PLUGINS with an extended local array in python_test guarded by has_version.
snippet:
if has_version "dev-python/pytest-trio[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]"; then
	local EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( "${EPYTEST_PLUGINS[@]}" pytest-trio )
else
	opts+=( -m "not trio" -o addopts= )
fi

problem: Package works on py3.15 but its large test-dep tree does not yet, so a plain COMPAT bump would break USE=test dep resolution
root_cause: Test BDEPENDs unconditionally spanning PYTHON_COMPAT force every dep to support the new impl before the package can be bumped
fix_pattern: Define PYTHON_TESTED=( python3_{12..14} ) with PYTHON_COMPAT adding python3_15; wrap the entire test? BDEPEND (including dev-python/pytest itself and plugin deps, since distutils_enable_tests is dropped) in python_gen_cond_dep over "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}"; add IUSE=test RESTRICT="!test? ( test )" manually; in python_test, skip untested impls with einfo and set EPYTEST_* as locals.
rule: To enable a new Python target before its test deps are ready, gate all test BDEPEND with python_gen_cond_dep over a PYTHON_TESTED array and short-circuit python_test on impls outside it.
snippet:
PYTHON_TESTED=( python3_{12..14} )
PYTHON_COMPAT=( "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}" python3_15 )
...
python_test() {
	if ! has "${EPYTHON/./_}" "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}"; then
		einfo "Skipping tests on ${EPYTHON}"
		return
	fi
	...
}

problem: Two separate python_gen_cond_dep blocks (one nested inside an amd64? group) duplicated the same version window
root_cause: USE- and arch-conditional groups can be nested inside a single python_gen_cond_dep, so per-dep blocks with identical windows are unnecessary duplication
fix_pattern: Merged blockbuster, trio and the amd64?-conditional uvloop into one python_gen_cond_dep '...' 3.{11..14} block, putting amd64? ( ... ) inside the quoted dep string.
rule: Consolidate deps sharing the same lagging-impl window into one python_gen_cond_dep block; USE/arch conditionals nest fine inside its quoted argument.

problem: Test-restricted network-test package pulled its heavy test dep tree unconditionally and used the outdated flit PEP517 value while enabling py3.15
root_cause: PROPERTIES=test_network applied unconditionally, and test BDEPEND had no USE=test conditional path once distutils_enable_tests was dropped for the PYTHON_TESTED pattern; DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit is the legacy spelling of flit-core
fix_pattern: Made the property conditional with PROPERTIES="test? ( test_network )", added IUSE=test with RESTRICT=test kept, wrapped all test deps (including pytest and plugins) in python_gen_cond_dep over PYTHON_TESTED, renamed DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 flit -> flit-core, and made python_test skip untested impls.
rule: Make test-only PROPERTIES like test_network USE-conditional (PROPERTIES="test? ( test_network )") when adding IUSE=test, and prefer DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit-core over the legacy flit value.

problem: sphinx pinned dev-python/docutils with an upper bound, blocking newer docutils in the tree, and one test failed with the newer docutils
root_cause: Upstream pyproject.toml carries conservative upper-bound caps that Gentoo tracks a single tree-wide version against; a docutils behavior change (long-word splitting) broke a search-index test assertion
fix_pattern: Revbump to -r1; strip upper bounds generically in python_prepare_all with sed on pyproject.toml; backport the upstream test-fix commit as a FILESDIR patch (with upstream commit URL in a comment) instead of deselecting the test.
rule: To unpin an upper-bounded dep in a PEP517 package, sed the caps out of pyproject.toml in python_prepare_all and carry the upstream test-fix patch rather than deselecting broken tests; revbump since runtime dep range changes.

problem: Build failed with USE=-crypt: QThread type undefined in topichistoryview.cpp
root_cause: The QThread declaration was only reaching the TU transitively through QCA's QtCrypto headers, so disabling the optional QCA dependency (cmake_use_find_package crypt Qca-qt6) exposed the missing direct #include
fix_pattern: Cherry-picked the one-line upstream patch adding '#include <QThread>' into FILESDIR and referenced it via PATCHES=( "${FILESDIR}/${P}-missing-include.patch" ); no revbump since the package failed to build in that config anyway.
rule: When a package builds with an optional USE flag enabled but fails with it disabled, suspect headers transitively included via the optional dep and fix by adding the missing direct include (preferably as an upstream cherry-pick).

problem: COMPAT rotation including freethreaded variants: python3_{13..14}t narrowed to python3_14t alongside adding python3_15; t-variants are separate COMPAT entries rotated independently
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}, plus copyright year
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump 3.12-14 to 3.12-15, no other change
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump 3.12-14 to 3.12-15, no other change
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump 3.11-14 to 3.12-15, no other change
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump 3.11-14 to 3.12-15, no other change
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump 3.12-14 to 3.12-15, no other change
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window shift python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other change.
Test-only pytest plugin deps were hand-written as a test? BDEPEND block, verbose and prone to drift when plugins change; also pypy3_11 kept alive past its usefulness during py3.15 enablement.
root_cause: Before distutils-r1 grew EPYTEST_PLUGINS, each ebuild duplicated test? ( dev-python/pytest-foo[${PYTHON_USEDEP}] ) blocks; the eclass now generates those deps and controls pytest plugin autoloading from a single array.
fix_pattern: Delete the test? BDEPEND block and declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-httpbin ) before distutils_enable_tests pytest; the eclass adds the [test] deps and runs pytest with only the listed plugins autoloaded. An explicit empty EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() means 'no plugins needed' and disables plugin autoloading for test isolation.
rule: When a distutils-r1 package needs pytest plugins for tests, declare them in EPYTEST_PLUGINS (empty array if none) before distutils_enable_tests pytest instead of hand-writing test? BDEPEND blocks.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; nothing else changed.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump; pypy3_11 dropped while widening to python3_{12..15}.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{10..14} -> python3_{12..15} plus copyright year.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}.
Same EPYTEST_PLUGINS migration as requests-futures (test? BDEPEND pytest-mock block replaced by EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-mock )) done during the py3.15 bump; idiom already captured under 9563170b.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{12..14} -> python3_{12..15}.
PYTHON_COMPAT bump plus explicit EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() added even though no plugins are needed, to disable pytest plugin autoloading for test isolation; semantics captured under 9563170b.
PYTHON_COMPAT bump plus explicit empty EPYTEST_PLUGINS=(); same idiom as 9563170b.
New privoxy 4.2.0 dropped mbedtls-2.x support upstream and building against mbedtls-3.x was broken, so the previous default TLS backend could not be used for the bump.
root_cause: Upstream moved to a new major version of an optional TLS backend before the ebuild ecosystem (and upstream's own code) could build against it, breaking the old backend choice.
fix_pattern: Keep both backend flags but flip the IUSE default to the working one (mbedtls +openssl), enforce exclusivity and threading with REQUIRED_USE='ssl? ( ^^ ( mbedtls openssl ) threads )', pin the new slot with net-libs/mbedtls:3= in DEPEND, and default configure to '--without-mbedtls --without-openssl' unless USE=ssl selects one.
rule: When an upstream bump breaks one of several optional backends, flip the IUSE default to a working backend and keep the broken one selectable behind ^^ REQUIRED_USE with a slotted := dep, instead of deleting the flag.
snippet:
IUSE="... mbedtls +openssl ..."
REQUIRED_USE="ssl? ( ^^ ( mbedtls openssl ) threads )"
DEPEND="ssl? (
	mbedtls? ( net-libs/mbedtls:3= )
	openssl? ( dev-libs/openssl:= )
)"
local myconf="--without-mbedtls --without-openssl"
use ssl && myconf="$(use_with mbedtls) $(use_with openssl)"

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{12..14} -> python3_{12..15}.
Package uses maintainer-generated dist tarballs (upstream git fork publishes no release archives), and the generation procedure lived only in the maintainer's head/shell history.
root_cause: When SRC_URI points at a self-hosted tarball built from a git tag, anyone else bumping the package cannot reproduce the distfile unless the recipe is recorded.
fix_pattern: Commit a files/make-dist-archive.sh next to the ebuild that clones the exact tag, strips .git, runs autogen/configure/make dist, GPG-signs the tarball, and uploads it to the dev space (kup putraw ... /pub/dev/<dev>@gentoo.org/), taking the version as its only argument.
rule: When you self-host distfiles generated from upstream git, check the exact archive-generation script into files/ so the tarball is reproducible and auditable by other maintainers.
snippet:
git clone --depth 1 --branch "${TAG}" <upstream-url>
cd gnupg && rm -rf .git
bash autogen.sh && ./configure --enable-maintainer-mode
make dist
gpg --detach-sign "${TAG}".tar.bz2
kup putraw "${TAG}".tar.bz2{,.sig} /pub/dev/<dev>@gentoo.org/freepg/

problem: maturin lacked free-threaded (nogil) CPython targets, and simply adding them made one test fail because a pyo3 test case is incompatible with free-threaded CPython.
root_cause: Free-threaded CPython builds are separate PYTHON_COMPAT targets (python3_XYt) and some upstream tests assume the GIL exists.
fix_pattern: Add the t-variants alongside regular targets in PYTHON_COMPAT: python3_{12..15} python3_{14..15}t, and in python_test() gate extra test skips on the interpreter suffix: [[ ${EPYTHON} == *t ]] && CARGO_SKIP_TESTS+=( develop::develop_pip_cases::case_02_pyo3_mixed ).
rule: When enabling free-threaded CPython, add python3_XYt targets to PYTHON_COMPAT and conditionally deselect GIL-dependent tests with an [[ ${EPYTHON} == *t ]] guard rather than skipping them for all implementations.
snippet:
PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} python3_{14..15}t )
...
if [[ ${EPYTHON} == *t ]]; then
	CARGO_SKIP_TESTS+=(
		# incompatible with free-threaded CPython
		develop::develop_pip_cases::case_02_pyo3_mixed
	)
fi

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT shift to python3_{12..15} python3_{14..15}t, dropping pypy3_11; t-range syntax already captured under 63026cdf.

sys-devel/rust-std ebuilds (python-any-r1 build-time python consumer) had stale PYTHON_COMPAT (still python3_11, missing python3_14), drifting from the matching dev-lang/rust versions that build the same sources.
root_cause: rust-std is a stripped-down twin of dev-lang/rust for crossdev; when dev-lang/rust updates its PYTHON_COMPAT per version, the twin package must be updated in lockstep or it blocks depgraphs when old python targets are removed.
fix_pattern: Per-version sync: each rust-std-X.Y ebuild got the same PYTHON_COMPAT window as the corresponding dev-lang/rust-X.Y (e.g. 1.74-1.79 -> python3_{12..12}, 1.80-1.86 -> python3_{12..13}, 1.87+ -> python3_{12..14}); since it is python-any-r1 (build-time only), no revbump was needed.
rule: When one package is a build-variant twin of another (rust-std vs rust), propagate PYTHON_COMPAT and similar toolchain-env changes version-by-version to the twin whenever the primary changes.

problem: Building cross_llvm-${target}/rust-std failed at link time with 'ld.lld: error: unable to find library -lgcc_s'.
root_cause: Pure-LLVM cross toolchains have no libgcc/libgcc_s; rust's bootstrap defaults to linking the unwinder from libgcc_s unless told to build/use LLVM libunwind.
fix_pattern: Add IUSE=llvm-libunwind and emit llvm-libunwind = "in-tree" in the [target.*] section of bootstrap.toml via usex, so rustbuild compiles its bundled LLVM libunwind instead of expecting libgcc_s.
rule: When a Rust (or other) build fails with -lgcc_s not found on an LLVM-runtime toolchain, switch the unwinder to LLVM libunwind (for rustbuild: llvm-libunwind = "in-tree" in bootstrap.toml, behind a llvm-libunwind USE flag).
snippet:
IUSE="debug llvm-libunwind"
...
cat <<- EOF >> "${S}"/bootstrap.toml
	llvm-libunwind = "$(usex llvm-libunwind in-tree no)"
EOF

problem: Building rust-std for a musl CTARGET panicked in bootstrap sanity checks: 'when targeting MUSL either the rust.musl-root option or the target.$TARGET.musl-root option must be specified in bootstrap.toml'.
root_cause: Rust's bootstrap requires an explicit musl sysroot path (musl-root) for musl targets; the ebuild only set crt-static=false and never pointed bootstrap at the crossdev sysroot.
fix_pattern: Under use elibc_musl, append both crt-static = false and musl-root = "/usr/${CTARGET}/usr" to the target section of bootstrap.toml — /usr/${CTARGET}/usr being the standard crossdev sysroot prefix.
rule: When rustbuild targets musl, set target.<triple>.musl-root to the cross sysroot (/usr/${CTARGET}/usr under crossdev) in bootstrap.toml alongside crt-static = false.
snippet:
if use elibc_musl; then
	cat <<- _EOF_ >> "${S}"/bootstrap.toml
		crt-static = false
		musl-root = "/usr/${CTARGET}/usr"
	_EOF_
fi

problem: Emerging cross_llvm-${target}/rust-std died in pkg_pretend with 'rust-std should only be used for cross' even though it was a legitimate cross build.
root_cause: The ebuild hand-rolled its own 'cross magic' (export CTARGET from CATEGORY, is_cross() comparing CHOST/CTARGET) that only recognized the cross-* category prefix, not the newer cross_llvm-* categories used by LLVM crossdev toolchains.
fix_pattern: Delete the ~15-line hand-rolled CTARGET/is_cross block, inherit crossdev.eclass, and replace the check with is_crosspkg; the eclass derives CTARGET from both cross-* and cross_llvm-* categories.
rule: Never hand-roll cross-category/CTARGET detection in ebuilds; inherit crossdev and use its helpers (is_crosspkg, target_is_not_host) so both cross- and cross_llvm- toolchain categories work.

problem: Cross-compiling bash in a clang-only environment failed with 'make[1]: gcc: No such file or directory' while building build-host tools (mkbuiltins etc.).
root_cause: Autoconf packages that build native helper tools use CC_FOR_BUILD for the build-host compiler and default it to gcc; on clang-only (or cross_llvm) hosts gcc does not exist, and the ebuild only exported CFLAGS_FOR_BUILD, not CC_FOR_BUILD.
fix_pattern: In src_configure, alongside the existing CFLAGS_FOR_BUILD export, export CC_FOR_BUILD="$(tc-getBUILD_CC)" when tc-is-cross-compiler, so the build-host compiler is taken from the profile/toolchain instead of a hardcoded gcc fallback.
rule: When a cross build fails invoking bare 'gcc' for helper tools, export CC_FOR_BUILD="$(tc-getBUILD_CC)" (plus CFLAGS_FOR_BUILD) under tc-is-cross-compiler instead of relying on the configure default.
snippet:
if tc-is-cross-compiler; then
	export CC_FOR_BUILD="$(tc-getBUILD_CC)"
	export CFLAGS_FOR_BUILD="${BUILD_CFLAGS} -std=gnu17"
fi

problem: Same clang cross-compile failure as bash ('gcc: No such file or directory') because configure's CC_FOR_BUILD defaulted to gcc.
root_cause: libassuan builds native helper tools with CC_FOR_BUILD; clang-only cross environments have no gcc.
fix_pattern: Variant of the bash fix: instead of exporting, pass CC_FOR_BUILD="$(tc-getBUILD_CC)" as an econf argument in the myeconfargs array (works because configure accepts VAR=value arguments).
rule: CC_FOR_BUILD="$(tc-getBUILD_CC)" can be handed to econf directly as a VAR=value argument when you already build a myeconfargs array — same effect as exporting it.

problem: Cross-compiling perl with clang segfaulted while running the freshly built miniperl (make ... lib/Config_git.pl: Segmentation fault).
root_cause: Perl's source relies on type-punning that is undefined under strict aliasing; gcc builds historically got -fno-strict-aliasing via hints, but the HOSTCFLAGS path used for the build-host miniperl under clang lacked it, and clang's optimizer miscompiled it.
fix_pattern: In the cross branch of src_configure, after setting HOSTCC/HOSTCFLAGS from tc-getBUILD_CC/CFLAGS_FOR_BUILD, append -fno-strict-aliasing to HOSTCFLAGS when tc-is-clang.
rule: When a build-host helper binary (miniperl-style bootstrap tool) segfaults only under clang cross builds, suspect strict-aliasing UB and append -fno-strict-aliasing to the host-tool CFLAGS (HOSTCFLAGS/CFLAGS_FOR_BUILD).
snippet:
# bug #977768
if tc-is-clang; then
	export HOSTCFLAGS="${HOSTCFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
fi

problem: Major bump of a meson C++ package with an optional Rust component (yaml config support): Rust deps must not be unconditional, cargo eclass phases are automagic, LTO breaks mixed C++/Rust linking, and 9999 needs a crates tarball that tracks git.
root_cause: cargo.eclass by default forces rust deps and defines src_compile/src_test; a partly-Rust package needs the Rust toolchain only behind its USE flag, and mixed C++/Rust objects cannot be reliably LTO-linked with different toolchains (bug #963128).
fix_pattern: Set RUST_OPTIONAL=1 and put ${RUST_DEPEND} inside 'yaml? ( ... )' in BDEPEND; call rust_pkg_setup only under 'use yaml'; explicitly define src_compile as 'cargo_gen_config; cargo_env meson_src_compile' and src_test as meson_src_test to override the automagic cargo phases; filter-lto with an ewarn when 'use yaml && tc-is-lto'; for 9999, name the self-hosted crates tarball with the first 8 sha256 chars of the crates bundle (CRATES_HASH) so it invalidates when regenerated.
rule: For a non-Rust build system with an optional Rust component, set RUST_OPTIONAL=1, gate ${RUST_DEPEND} and rust_pkg_setup behind the USE flag, wrap the real build with cargo_gen_config + cargo_env <buildsys>_src_compile, and filter-lto for mixed C++/Rust links.

problem: New codespell version needed two sanitizations: pyproject.toml addopts pulled in unwanted pytest options/plugins, and setuptools installed stray top-level files (tools/gen_OX.sh via SOURCES.txt) into site-packages.
root_cause: Upstream pyproject addopts assume dev-environment plugins (e.g. coverage), and stale egg-info SOURCES.txt entries make setuptools ship non-package files.
fix_pattern: In src_prepare after distutils-r1_src_prepare, sed -e '/addopts/d' -i pyproject.toml to drop the forced pytest options, and sed the offending path out of codespell.egg-info/SOURCES.txt to keep stray top-level files out of site-packages.
rule: When upstream pyproject.toml forces pytest addopts or egg-info SOURCES.txt lists stray top-level files, sed them out in src_prepare rather than adding the plugin deps or shipping the junk.
snippet:
src_prepare() {
	distutils-r1_src_prepare
	# Remove forced pytest options
	sed -e '/addopts/d' -i pyproject.toml || die
	# Do not install stray top-level files in site-packages
	sed -e 's#tools/gen_OX.sh##g' -i codespell.egg-info/SOURCES.txt || die
}

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{12..14} -> {12..15}; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump {11..14} -> {12..15} (3.11 dropped from eclass at same time).

setuptools BDEPEND was wrapped in python_gen_cond_dep restricted to the literal target python3_12, so on 3.13/3.14 the dependency was silently never pulled in even though those interpreters also need it (distutils removed).
root_cause: python_gen_cond_dep with an explicit target list does not track PYTHON_COMPAT growth: when new interpreters are added the condition silently excludes them, and when old ones are dropped the condition may become dead. The original condition was written to exclude py3.11 but effectively pinned to one version.
fix_pattern: Once every interpreter in PYTHON_COMPAT needs the dep, drop the conditional target list entirely so the dependency is unconditional: $(python_gen_cond_dep 'dev-python/setuptools[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]') with no version argument.
rule: Whenever you change PYTHON_COMPAT, audit every python_gen_cond_dep call in the ebuild: extend or remove its explicit version list, because stale lists silently drop dependencies on new interpreters.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump {11..14} -> {12..15}.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump {11..14} -> {12..15}.

python_test conditionally deselected tests with 'has_version dev-python/pykeepass', but the optional dep can be installed without the python target currently being tested (EPYTHON), so the test ran anyway and failed with ImportError on that interpreter.
root_cause: has_version on a bare python package atom only checks that the package is installed at all, not that it was built for the interpreter the test phase is currently running under; during multi-impl testing each EPYTHON needs the dep built with its own python_targets flag.
fix_pattern: Build a USE-dep string from EPYTHON and query has_version with it, so the skip logic matches per-interpreter availability.
rule: In python_test, when gating test deselections on optional dev-python/* packages, always query has_version with [python_targets_${EPYTHON/./_}(-)] instead of the bare atom, otherwise skips misfire on interpreters the dep was not built for.
snippet:
local usedep="python_targets_${EPYTHON/./_}(-)"
if ! has_version "dev-python/pykeepass[${usedep}]"; then
	EPYTEST_DESELECT+=( tests/imports/test_parse.py::TestParse::test_import_keepass )
fi

problem: Installed pkg-config files pointed at libdir=${exec_prefix}/lib, wrong on lib64/multilib systems, so consumers linking via pkg-config failed to find the libraries (bug 978404).
root_cause: The hand-rolled make-based buildsystem hardcoded the .pc libdir/includedir template instead of using its own HEADERINST_DIR/LIBINST_DIR install variables that already honor the configured libdir.
fix_pattern: Patch the buildsystem template to reuse the variables it already computes for installation, add the patch to PATCHES, and revbump (-r1) because installed file content changes.
rule: When a package with a custom (non-autotools/meson/cmake) buildsystem installs .pc files, verify the libdir in them matches the real install libdir (lib64), and fix by substituting the buildsystem's own install-dir variables rather than sed-ing paths in the ebuild.

problem: Enabling py3.15 required backporting an upstream fix: a C extension crash (error path did 'goto error' after PyErr_SetString on a partially built object) plus test fixes because pytest 9.1 rejects bare generators/itertools.product in @pytest.mark.parametrize.
root_cause: New CPython versions expose latent refcount/error-path bugs in C extensions, and pytest >= 9.1 requires parametrize argvalues to be a Collection, not an arbitrary iterable.
fix_pattern: Pull the exact upstream commit as a FILESDIR patch (with the upstream commit URL as the first line of the patch), create a new -rN ebuild (the fix changes installed C code, so a revision is warranted) with PYTHON_COMPAT extended, PATCHES=( "${FILESDIR}"/${PN}-1.0.0-py315.patch ), EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() and EPYTEST_XDIST=1.
rule: When enabling a new Python version breaks a C-extension package, backport the specific upstream fix commit as a patch and revbump; the patch file should start with the upstream commit URL for provenance.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump; pypy3_11 and 3.11 dropped, 3.15 added.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump; pypy3_11 and 3.11 dropped, 3.15 added.
Tests failed after dev-python/packaging was upgraded to 26.0 (bug 976744): the test-suite's toy resolver iterated specifiers per-requirement in a way packaging 26.0 no longer tolerates.
root_cause: Test-only code depended on old semantics of a third-party library (packaging SpecifierSet); the package's installed code was fine, only the functional tests broke.
fix_pattern: Backport the upstream test-only fix commit as a FILESDIR patch and add it to PATCHES without a revbump, since only test files change and the installed artifacts are identical.
rule: When a dependency upgrade breaks only a package's test suite, backport the upstream test fix as a patch and skip the revbump - revisions are only needed when installed files change.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump {11..14}+pypy3_11 -> {12..15}.
test_queue_full failed because cheroot 11 (a dependency) changed behavior to return HTTP 503 on connection-queue overflow.
root_cause: Behavioral change in a lower-layer dependency made an assertion in the consumer's test suite obsolete; upstream acknowledged it and marked the test xfail pending a real fix.
fix_pattern: Instead of a local EPYTEST_DESELECT, backport the upstream commit that adds @pytest.mark.xfail(reason='...issue URL') and reference the upstream issue in a comment above the PATCHES entry, preserving traceability for later removal.
rule: Prefer backporting an upstream xfail/skip commit (with issue URL) over ad-hoc local deselection when upstream has already triaged the failure - it documents why and when the workaround can be dropped.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump {11..14}+pypy3_11 -> {12..15}.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump {11..14}+pypy3_11 -> {12..15}.
Bumping PYTHON_COMPAT to 3.15 without touching the legacy-cgi conditional dep would have silently skipped dev-python/legacy-cgi on 3.15, breaking tests (cgi module removed from stdlib since 3.13).
root_cause: python_gen_cond_dep with an explicit version range '3.{13..14}' does not automatically cover newly added interpreters, and the stdlib-removal shim (legacy-cgi) is needed on every version >= 3.13.
fix_pattern: Extend the python_gen_cond_dep version list in the same commit as the PYTHON_COMPAT bump: ' 3.{13..14}' -> ' 3.{13..15}'.
rule: A PYTHON_COMPAT bump must grep the ebuild for python_gen_cond_dep and extend every open-ended version range (e.g. stdlib-removal shims like legacy-cgi/standard-imghdr) to include the new interpreter.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump {11..14}+pypy3_11 -> {12..15}.
Tests failed on newer Pythons/deps (bug 969982) because the released sdist predates fixes upstream already merged (escape() behavior changes, dead network test).
root_cause: Upstream fixed the test suite on master (commit 'drop support for py3.9/3.10') but never cut a release, so the packaged version needed the fix carried as a patch.
fix_pattern: Backport the upstream master commit as a FILESDIR patch, create -r1 with PYTHON_COMPAT bumped, add EPYTEST_DESELECT for the remaining network-dependent test with an explanatory comment, and modernise (EPYTEST_PLUGINS=(), optfeature for the soft dep).
rule: When tests fail on a new Python and the last release is old, check upstream master first - if the fix is already committed, backport that commit verbatim instead of writing your own patch.
snippet:
EPYTEST_DESELECT=(
	# needs network
	tests/test_pyquery.py::TestWebScrappingEncoding::test_get
)
EPYTEST_PLUGINS=()
distutils_enable_tests pytest

problem: One test failed only because a pkg_resources DeprecationWarning was escalated to an error under py3.15.
root_cause: Test suites running with warnings-as-errors turn harmless DeprecationWarnings from transitive deps (setuptools/pkg_resources) into failures on new Python versions.
fix_pattern: Deselect the single affected test via EPYTEST_DESELECT with a one-line comment stating the warning, rather than patching or restricting all tests.
rule: When a test fails solely on a DeprecationWarning raised by a dependency, deselect just that test with a comment naming the warning; do not RESTRICT=test or carry a patch.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump applied to both live ebuild versions in one commit.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump {12..14} -> {12..15}.

Test suite broke with pytest >= 9.0 (bug 976747): custom conftest collection hooks used the removed py.path API, and pytest 9.1 additionally rejected non-Collection iterables and duplicate IDs in parametrize.
root_cause: pytest 9.0 removed the legacy py.path hook arguments (pytest_collect_file(path, parent) and File.from_parent(fspath=...)) in favor of pathlib (file_path / path=), and pytest 9.1 requires parametrize argvalues to be a Collection with unique IDs under --strict.
fix_pattern: Two backported upstream PR patches: (1) port conftest hooks to pathlib signatures - pytest_collect_file(file_path, parent), file_path.suffix, from_parent(parent, path=file_path); (2) wrap generator/itertools.product parametrize sources in list() and deduplicate parametrized values. No revbump: only tests/conftest change.
rule: When a package's tests break with pytest 9+, look for two known migrations: py.path hook args -> pathlib (pytest_collect_file(file_path), from_parent(path=)), and non-Collection parametrize argvalues -> wrap in list(); upstream PRs usually exist to backport.
snippet:
-def pytest_collect_file(path, parent):
+def pytest_collect_file(file_path, parent):
-    return TreeConstructionFile.from_parent(parent, fspath=path)
+    return TreeConstructionFile.from_parent(parent, path=file_path)
-@pytest.mark.parametrize("data, encoding", param_encoding())
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("data, encoding", list(param_encoding()))

problem: Package works at runtime on 3.13-3.15 but its test suite crashes there (upstream issue 78), so naively enabling py3.15 would either block the bump or ship untested breakage flags.
root_cause: Tests exercising CPython internals (ctypes struct layouts) lag behind interpreter releases; usability of the module and testability diverge per interpreter.
fix_pattern: Declare a PYTHON_TESTED subset array, build PYTHON_COMPAT from it plus the untestable versions, condition the pytest BDEPEND with python_gen_cond_dep "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}", and short-circuit python_test with einfo+return for untested EPYTHONs.
rule: When a package runs on more Python versions than its test suite supports, use the PYTHON_TESTED array pattern to enable all versions while running tests only where they pass, instead of holding back PYTHON_COMPAT.
snippet:
PYTHON_TESTED=( python3_12 )
PYTHON_COMPAT=( "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}" python3_{13..15} )
BDEPEND="test? ( $(python_gen_cond_dep 'dev-python/pytest[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]' "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}") )"
python_test() {
	if ! has "${EPYTHON/./_}" "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}"; then
		einfo "Skipping tests on ${EPYTHON}, as they are broken"
		return
	fi
	epytest
}

problem: Doctests failed against >=libmemcached-1.0.18 (bug 978301), and the C extension linked against zlib without any zlib dependency declared; an obsolete local patch was still being carried.
root_cause: libmemcached behavior change (key verification off by default, different exception raised) invalidated doctest expectations; the missing dep was a latent QA issue (linked-but-undeclared library) surfaced during the rework.
fix_pattern: New -r3 revision: backport upstream PR (doctest fix + use-after-free fix in the C module), add 'virtual/zlib:=' to DEPEND (slot operator because it links against it), drop the stale patch, bump PYTHON_COMPAT; stable keywords reset to ~arch since installed code changed.
rule: When touching an old C-extension ebuild, verify every library the extension links against is in DEPEND with := where slotted (virtual/zlib:=), and drop patches that no longer apply to reality; behavior-changing patches require a revbump and re-stabilization.

problem: memcapable test binary failed against >=net-misc/memcached-1.6.40 because the daemon now answers DELETE errors with NOT_FOUND instead of ERROR/CLIENT_ERROR.
root_cause: Protocol/behavior change in the companion daemon: client-side test code hardcoded the acceptable set of error strings, and the daemon added a new legitimate response in 1.6.40.
fix_pattern: Backport the upstream one-line compat commit (accept NOT_FOUND too) as a FILESDIR patch with the upstream commit URL header, and revbump to -r1 because the installed memcapable binary changes.
rule: When a server package your library talks to changes its wire responses, backport the upstream compat commit into the client and revbump - even a test-utility binary shipped in the image counts as an installed-file change.

problem: Ebuild lagged behind the current Python implementation window (no py3.15, still carried py3.11 and pypy3_11).
root_cause: Gentoo rolls PYTHON_COMPAT as a window: when a new CPython is enabled, the oldest impl and stale pypy targets are dropped in the same edit rather than accumulating.
fix_pattern: Replace the whole brace range instead of appending: python3_{11..14} pypy3_11 -> python3_{12..15}; bump the copyright year; while touching python_test, give the die message context with the active interpreter.
rule: When enabling a new Python target, roll the entire PYTHON_COMPAT window (drop the oldest impl and dead pypy targets in the same commit) and include ${EPYTHON} in test die messages so multi-impl failures are attributable.
snippet:
-PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{11..14} pypy3_11 )
+PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} )
...
-	"${EPYTHON}" -m unittest zc.lockfile.tests -v || die
+	"${EPYTHON}" -m unittest zc.lockfile.tests -v || die "Tests failed with ${EPYTHON}"

problem: VirtualBox GUI hard-required X even on Wayland-only Qt systems; building it with USE flags mismatched against qtbase produced 'very buggy' binaries (bugs 949857, 973280, 974207).
root_cause: The app queries Qt native interfaces (QX11Application/QWaylandApplication) whose availability is baked into qtbase at its compile time via QT_CONFIG(xcb)/QT_CONFIG(wayland); the consumer must be compiled with exactly the same feature set as the installed Qt, so an independent [X?,wayland?] dep is not enough.
fix_pattern: Add IUSE='X wayland' and depend with the forced-match use-dep operator dev-qt/qtbase:6[X=,wayland=,widgets]; guard upstream X11/Wayland code paths in a files/ patch with #if QT_CONFIG(xcb) / #if QT_CONFIG(wayland); for components that genuinely still need X (opengl, sdl) skip REQUIRED_USE and instead depend on media-libs/libglvnd[X] and media-libs/libsdl2[X,video]; emit pkg_pretend einfo notices for flag combos that have no effect (X without gui).
rule: When a package must be compiled with the same optional features as a library it links (Qt xcb/wayland being the classic case), use the [flag=] use-dependency to force flag equality instead of [flag?], and prefer dependency-side [X] atoms over REQUIRED_USE when a feature simply implies an X-enabled dep.
snippet:
IUSE="X ... +gui ... wayland"
gui? (
	dev-qt/qtbase:6[X=,wayland=,widgets]
)
opengl? ( media-libs/libglvnd[X] )
sdl? ( media-libs/libsdl2[X,video] )
# patch: #if QT_CONFIG(xcb) ... #endif around QX11Application uses

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window roll to python3_{12..15}; no generalizable content beyond the pattern already recorded.
The old, widely-keyworded setuptools-scm 9.2.2 lacked py3.15 support, blocking the py3.15 dep tree on arches (~loong) where the newest version was not yet keyworded.
root_cause: Arch keywording lags: the newest ebuild version may not be keyworded everywhere, so enabling a new Python impl only on the latest version leaves lagging arches unable to resolve the dependency graph.
fix_pattern: Backport the PYTHON_COMPAT enablement to the older, better-keyworded ebuild version too ('enable py3.15* for old ... Needed because of lagging ~loong'); note the freethreading targets are rolled in the same way (python3_{14..15}t).
rule: When enabling a new Python implementation for a package that core tooling depends on, also enable it on older ebuild versions that still carry keywords the newest version lacks, otherwise lagging arches break.

problem: pytest test runs loaded every pytest plugin installed on the system, making test results depend on unrelated installed packages and prone to random breakage.
root_cause: pytest autoloads all installed plugins by default; without an explicit plugin list, a test environment is not hermetic and any newly installed plugin can break or alter the suite.
fix_pattern: Declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (empty array) before distutils_enable_tests pytest; the eclass then disables plugin autoloading and loads only the listed plugins (none here) plus declared test deps.
rule: For pytest-based python ebuilds, set EPYTEST_PLUGINS to exactly the plugins the suite needs (empty array if none) before distutils_enable_tests pytest, so tests run with plugin autoloading disabled and are isolated from whatever else is installed.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window roll to python3_{12..15}; pattern already recorded.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window roll to python3_{12..15}; pattern already recorded.
Routine py3.15 window roll plus the EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() isolation idiom already recorded under requests-toolbelt.
Routine py3.15 window roll plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=(); patterns already recorded.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window roll to python3_{12..15}; pattern already recorded.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window roll to python3_{12..15}; pattern already recorded.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window roll to python3_{12..15}; pattern already recorded.
FEATURES=test builds of pycurl failed because the test suite now imports websockets, which was not declared as a dependency (bug 977641).
root_cause: Upstream added a websocket test harness; test-only imports are invisible to normal dep generation, so the ebuild's test? BDEPEND block silently went stale on version bump.
fix_pattern: Add the missing module under the test? conditional in BDEPEND with PYTHON_USEDEP and the version floor upstream requires: >=dev-python/websockets-12[${PYTHON_USEDEP}].
rule: When a bumped package's test suite fails on a missing import, add that module to BDEPEND inside test? ( ... ) with [${PYTHON_USEDEP}] and an upstream-matching version floor, rather than skipping the tests.

problem: Routine py3.15 window roll, applied to both the release and the -9999 live ebuild in one commit; pattern already recorded.
Routine py3.15 window roll plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=(); patterns already recorded.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window roll to python3_{12..15}; pattern already recorded.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window roll to python3_{12..15}; pattern already recorded.
Routine py3.15 window roll (plus reordering DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 above PYTHON_COMPAT for house style); pattern already recorded.
Routine py3.15 window roll applied to both release and -9999 ebuilds; pattern already recorded.
Routine one-token PYTHON_COMPAT extension to python3_{12..15}; pattern already recorded.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window roll to python3_{12..15}; pattern already recorded.
After dropping pypy3_11 from PYTHON_COMPAT, the ebuilds still carried a pypy3.11-specific EPYTEST_DESELECT case branch that could never trigger.
root_cause: Implementation-conditional test deselects (case ${EPYTHON} in ...) are easy to orphan when the corresponding implementation is removed from PYTHON_COMPAT.
fix_pattern: Remove the impl from PYTHON_COMPAT and delete its case ${EPYTHON} branch (and its EPYTEST_DESELECT entries) in the same commit; collapse the case statement entirely if no branches remain.
