rule: When dropping an implementation from PYTHON_COMPAT, grep the ebuild for that impl's name in EPYTHON case branches and deselect lists and remove the dead branches in the same commit.
snippet:
-	case ${EPYTHON} in
-		pypy3.11)
-			EPYTEST_DESELECT+=( tests/test_inspect.py::... )
-			;;
-	esac

problem: Enabling py3.15 broke rich's test suite: py3.15 renamed print()'s variadic parameter *args to *objects, so tests asserting exact rendered signatures failed, and the upstream fix PR was unmerged and incomplete.
root_cause: Test suites that snapshot interpreter-version-dependent output (builtin signatures, repr rendering) break on every new CPython; upstream fixes often trail the interpreter release.
fix_pattern: Two-part idiom: (1) ship the unmerged upstream PR as files/rich-15.0.0-py315.patch with the PR URL as the first line of the patch for provenance; (2) deselect the still-failing tests only for the new interpreter via case ${EPYTHON} in python3.15*) EPYTEST_DESELECT+=( ... );; with a comment citing the PR, keeping other impls fully tested.
rule: When a new Python version breaks version-rendering tests, backport the upstream fix PR as a files/ patch headed by its URL and scope any remaining deselects to that interpreter with a case ${EPYTHON} branch, never with a global EPYTEST_DESELECT.
snippet:
PATCHES=( "${FILESDIR}/${P}-py315.patch" )  # PR URL on line 1 of patch
case ${EPYTHON} in
	python3.15*)
		EPYTEST_DESELECT+=(
			# https://github.com/Textualize/rich/pull/4082 isn't complete
			tests/test_inspect.py::test_inspect_builtin_function_only_python311
		)
		;;
esac

problem: Routine removal of pypy3_11 from PYTHON_COMPAT in two ebuilds; window-roll pattern already recorded.
Routine one-token PYTHON_COMPAT extension to python3_{12..15}; pattern already recorded.
Package not installable on newly-released CPython 3.15; ebuild still listed python3_{11..14}.
root_cause: Annual CPython release cycle: new target python3_15 added to the tree while the oldest impl python3.11 is being phased out; PYTHON_COMPAT must track the supported window.
fix_pattern: Slide the whole PYTHON_COMPAT window instead of only appending the new impl: python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}, dropping the oldest impl (and often pypy3_11) in the same commit. No revbump is done for PYTHON_COMPAT changes. Simultaneously set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests to run the suite with pytest plugin autoloading disabled (the new distutils-r1 default idiom during impl bumps).
rule: When enabling a new Python target, slide the PYTHON_COMPAT range (add newest, drop oldest EOL-bound impl) in one commit without a revbump, and declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS explicitly (empty array if the suite needs no plugins).

problem: Routine py3.15 enablement: PYTHON_COMPAT window slide 11..14 -> 12..15 plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=(); same mass-migration pattern as publicsuffix2.
Routine py3.15 enablement: window slide plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=(); no new idiom beyond the batch pattern.
Routine py3.15 enablement: one-line PYTHON_COMPAT window slide 11..14 -> 12..15.
Routine py3.15 enablement: one-line PYTHON_COMPAT window slide.
Routine py3.15 enablement: window slide plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=().
Routine py3.15 enablement: window slide plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=().
Routine py3.15 enablement; also dropped pypy3_11 from PYTHON_COMPAT as part of the same window slide (pypy target being phased out alongside python3.11).
Routine py3.15 enablement: one-line PYTHON_COMPAT window slide.

Ebuild carried a custom python_test() that manually exported PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1, kept a local EPYTEST_IGNORE, and ran 'epytest -p pytest_mock', plus a hand-written test? BDEPEND on pytest-mock.
root_cause: Before distutils-r1 grew EPYTEST_PLUGINS, controlling which pytest plugins load required boilerplate: manual autoload-disable env var, explicit -p flags, and manually keeping BDEPEND in sync with the plugins actually used.
fix_pattern: Migrate to the declarative eclass mechanism: set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-mock ) before distutils_enable_tests pytest. The eclass then disables plugin autoloading, passes the -p flags, AND generates the test BDEPEND for the listed plugins, so the manual BDEPEND entry and the entire python_test() override are deleted; EPYTEST_IGNORE moves to global scope where the default test phase picks it up.
rule: When a python ebuild has a python_test() that only sets PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD / passes -p plugin flags, replace it with a global EPYTEST_PLUGINS array before distutils_enable_tests and drop the now-redundant plugin BDEPENDs and the python_test override.
snippet:
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-mock )
 distutils_enable_tests pytest
-python_test() {
-	local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1
-	local EPYTEST_IGNORE=( tests/conftest.py )
-	epytest -p pytest_mock
-}
+EPYTEST_IGNORE=( tests/conftest.py )

problem: Routine py3.15 enablement: one-line PYTHON_COMPAT extension 12..14 -> 12..15.
Ebuild carried a hand-written BDEPEND='test? ( dev-python/pytest-asyncio[...] )' block for a pytest plugin needed by the test suite.
root_cause: distutils_enable_tests only generated the pytest dep itself; plugin deps had to be listed manually in BDEPEND, which drifts and does not control plugin autoloading.
fix_pattern: Delete the manual test? BDEPEND block and declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-asyncio ) before distutils_enable_tests pytest; the eclass generates the test dependency and loads exactly that plugin with autoloading disabled.
rule: When a python ebuild's only test BDEPENDs are pytest plugins, replace the BDEPEND block with an EPYTEST_PLUGINS array so the eclass owns both the dependency and the plugin loading.

problem: Routine py3.15 enablement: one-line PYTHON_COMPAT window slide.
Routine py3.15 enablement: window slide (also drops pypy3_11) plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=().
Routine py3.15 enablement: one-line PYTHON_COMPAT extension.
Routine py3.15 enablement: window slide (drops pypy3_11) plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=().
Routine py3.15 enablement: one-line PYTHON_COMPAT extension.
Test suite errored during pytest collection when optional dev-python/pillow was absent, even though the pillow-dependent test file was listed in EPYTEST_DESELECT.
root_cause: EPYTEST_DESELECT only skips selected test items after collection; pytest still imports the file during collection, so a module whose import fails (missing optional dependency) blows up regardless of deselection. Skipping import-time failures needs EPYTEST_IGNORE, which excludes the path from collection entirely.
fix_pattern: In the has_version-conditional block inside python_test(), move the whole test file from EPYTEST_DESELECT+= to EPYTEST_IGNORE+=. Commit message states the rule directly: 'We want _IGNORE for import failures.'
rule: Use EPYTEST_IGNORE for test files that fail at import/collection time (missing optional deps), and reserve EPYTEST_DESELECT for individual tests that collect fine but fail at runtime; deselecting an entire file is usually a sign IGNORE was meant.
snippet:
if ! has_version "dev-python/pillow[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]"; then
-	EPYTEST_DESELECT+=(
+	EPYTEST_IGNORE+=(
		tests/test_image_regression.py
	)
fi

problem: betamax's own test suite needs the betamax pytest plugin loaded, but the new EPYTEST_PLUGINS regime disables pytest plugin autoloading by default.
root_cause: When a package under test IS a pytest plugin, disabling autoloading also disables the just-built package's plugin entry points, breaking its own tests.
fix_pattern: Declare the package itself in the plugin list: EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( "${PN}" ) before distutils_enable_tests pytest. The eclass loads the in-build-tree plugin without adding a self-dependency.
rule: When packaging a pytest plugin, list the package's own name in EPYTEST_PLUGINS so its entry points load during its test suite despite autoload being disabled.

problem: Ebuild used DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit while the actual PEP517 build backend is flit_core.buildapi; the eclass value was renamed/split.
root_cause: distutils-r1 distinguishes the lightweight flit-core backend from the full flit tool; the legacy 'flit' value is being migrated to 'flit-core' so the BDEPEND matches the real backend (dev-python/flit-core) instead of pulling more than needed.
fix_pattern: Rename the variable value during a routine touch of the ebuild: DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit -> DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit-core (done together with the PYTHON_COMPAT slide, no revbump since generated deps are equivalent at install time).
rule: Set DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 to the exact backend named in pyproject.toml's build-backend (flit_core.buildapi => flit-core), and sweep deprecated eclass values opportunistically when already editing the ebuild.

problem: Routine py3.15 enablement: one-line PYTHON_COMPAT extension 12..14 -> 12..15.
Routine py3.15 enablement: one-line PYTHON_COMPAT extension.
Same PYTHON_TESTED-into-PYTHON_COMPAT promotion pattern as dev-python/pytest 508a2a50 (see that record); py3.15 moved from compat-only to tested set.

pytest had to be installable on python3.15 weeks before its test suite (and the plugin ecosystem it depends on) could actually run there, then later needed tests re-enabled without restructuring the ebuild.
root_cause: Core test-stack packages are at the bottom of the new-interpreter bootstrap: hundreds of packages need pytest on py3.15 before pytest's own test deps exist for py3.15, so compat and tested-ness must be decoupled.
fix_pattern: Keep two arrays: PYTHON_TESTED=( ...impls where the suite runs... ) and PYTHON_COMPAT=( "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}" python3_15 ... ), with python_test skipping impls not in PYTHON_TESTED. Enabling testing later is then a two-token edit moving the impl into PYTHON_TESTED; freethreading variants (python3_{14..15}t) stay compat-only until tested.
rule: For packages deep in the test-dependency bootstrap chain, split PYTHON_TESTED from PYTHON_COMPAT so a new interpreter can be enabled for install immediately and its tests switched on later with a one-line promotion.
snippet:
-PYTHON_TESTED=( python3_{11..14} pypy3_11 )
-PYTHON_COMPAT=( "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}" python3_15 python3_{13..15}t )
+PYTHON_TESTED=( python3_{12..15} )
+PYTHON_COMPAT=( "${PYTHON_TESTED[@]}" python3_{14..15}t )

problem: Routine py3.15 enablement: one-line PYTHON_COMPAT extension.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{12..14} -> {12..15}; no other change.
Enabling py3.15 also required declaring which pytest plugins the test suite needs, since distutils-r1 now disables pytest plugin autoloading by default.
root_cause: distutils-r1's pytest integration runs with plugin autoloading disabled; a package that is itself a pytest plugin must load itself explicitly or its own tests fail. Old pypy3_11 also dropped as no longer supported.
fix_pattern: Set EPYTEST_PLUGINS before distutils_enable_tests. For a package that is itself a pytest plugin, list its own PN; for a package needing no plugins, set an empty array to confirm the no-autoload default is intended.
rule: When using distutils_enable_tests pytest in a modern tree, always declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS explicitly (=( "${PN}" ) for pytest-plugin packages, =() when none are needed).

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to py3.15, dropping python3_11/pypy3_11.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{12..14} -> {12..15}.
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT bump plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() declaration (no plugins needed); pattern covered by pytest-httpbin lesson in this batch.
Enabling py3.15 exposed test failures with upcoming pytest-9.1, and the ebuild still used the heavy 'flit' PEP517 backend value.
root_cause: Upstream flask tests are incompatible with pytest >= 9.1 (upstream issue pallets/flask#6071); flit-only packages should build with the lighter flit-core backend rather than pulling full dev-python/flit.
fix_pattern: Changed DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit to flit-core, and added an upper-bounded test-only dependency with a comment carrying the upstream issue URL so the pin is auditable and removable later.
rule: When a new test-runner release breaks a package's tests, pin an upper bound in the test? () BDEPEND with a comment linking the upstream issue; and prefer DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit-core over flit for flit-built pure-python packages.

problem: Package failed to build under parallel make (bug #978431).
root_cause: Upstream Makefiles have missing inter-target dependencies, so parallel builds race and fail nondeterministically.
fix_pattern: Override src_compile to run emake -j1 with a comment citing the bug, instead of trying to fix the racy Makefiles; applied to all live ebuild versions.
rule: When a build fails intermittently only at high -j and the Makefile race is not worth patching, force serial build with 'emake -j1' in src_compile and leave a '# bug #NNNN' comment.

problem: Enabling py3.15 was not a PYTHON_COMPAT one-liner: tests failed on 'bad payload' cases because Python 3.15 changed base64.urlsafe_b64decode() to no longer require padding by default, so malformed input stopped raising and BadData was never triggered.
root_cause: New CPython versions can silently change stdlib semantics (here base64 gained a padded= kwarg with a new default), breaking library error-handling paths that relied on the old behavior.
fix_pattern: Wrote a version-conditional source patch (sys.version_info >= (3,15) -> urlsafe_b64decode(string, padded=True)), submitted it upstream, shipped it from FILESDIR with the upstream PR URL as a comment next to PATCHES, and did a revbump (-r1) rather than editing in place since installed behavior changes.
rule: When enabling a new Python target makes tests fail, suspect stdlib behavior changes; fix with a sys.version_info-guarded patch carried in FILESDIR, link the upstream PR in a comment, and revbump.

problem: New upstream (ovh-ttyrec) hardcodes -O2/-U_FORTIFY_SOURCE/-D_FORTIFY_SOURCE=2 and probes/injects warning flags in its homegrown configure, clobbering user CFLAGS/LDFLAGS; package also had no tests.
root_cause: Homegrown non-autoconf configure scripts often append their own optimization/hardening flags and -L/usr/local paths, violating Gentoo flag-respect QA.
fix_pattern: FILESDIR patch strips the hardcoded flags and the compiler-option-probing loop from Makefile.in/configure so only user flags apply; tc-export CC before econf; feature toggles passed as env vars using usev; added a src_test that actually records and replays a session as a smoke test.
rule: When upstream's hand-rolled configure filters or injects CFLAGS, patch the flag mangling out rather than fighting it with append-flags, and add a minimal src_test exercising the built binaries when upstream ships no test suite.

problem: Test suite failed on systems whose grep lacks PCRE support because tests use 'grep -P' (bug #978080).
root_cause: GNU grep's -P is an optional feature gated by the pcre USE flag on sys-apps/grep; the ebuild never expressed that test-time requirement.
fix_pattern: Added IUSE=test with RESTRICT="!test? ( test )" and a test-conditional BDEPEND on sys-apps/grep[pcre].
rule: When a test suite shells out to tools with optional features (grep -P, GNU sed extensions, etc.), add an explicit test? ( pkg[useflag] ) BDEPEND instead of assuming host defaults.
snippet:
IUSE="test"
RESTRICT="!test? ( test )"
BDEPEND="
	virtual/pkgconfig
	test? ( sys-apps/grep[pcre] )
"

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to py3.15.
Runtime ICE negotiation failures in asterisk caused by pjproject declaring ICE failed as soon as its own connectivity checks completed without a valid pair.
root_cause: Upstream bug fixed in a not-yet-released PR; waiting for the next release would leave stable users broken.
fix_pattern: Backported the full upstream PR as a FILESDIR patch named with the PR number (pjproject-2.17-r1-ice-early-terminate-pr5031.patch), put the PR URL as the first line inside the patch file, and revbumped to -r1 so installed consumers rebuild.
rule: For runtime bugs already fixed in an unmerged/unreleased upstream PR, backport the PR as a files/ patch whose name and header carry the PR reference, and revbump so binary/installed users pick it up.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to py3.15.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to py3.15.
An LTS branch needed a dozen backported CVE/build fixes; the ebuild's PATCHES list and files/ directory had grown unwieldy (12 patches, and files/ has size limits in ::gentoo).
root_cause: Long-lived LTS branches accumulate backports faster than upstream releases, and carrying each as a FILESDIR patch bloats the tree and the ebuild.
fix_pattern: Rolled all patches into a dated patch tarball (clamav-0.103-patches-20260702.tar.xz) hosted on developer space, added it as a second SRC_URI entry, replaced the whole PATCHES array with an eapply of the unpacked patch dir in src_prepare before default, and revbumped (-r4 -> -r5) since installed code changes (CVE fixes always warrant a revbump).
rule: When a package accumulates many backport patches, move them into a versioned/dated patch tarball fetched via SRC_URI and eapply the directory in src_prepare; always revbump for security backports.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to py3.15 (pypy3_11 kept).
Needed to enable both regular py3.15 and the free-threaded (nogil) python3_15t variant for a C-extension package.
root_cause: Free-threaded CPython builds are separate PYTHON_COMPAT targets with a 't' suffix; extensions that support them must list both variants.
fix_pattern: Used brace expansion with an optional t suffix to cover GIL and free-threaded interpreters in one token: python3_{13..15}{,t}; ebuild also documents (existing comment) staying on DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=no with meson because meson-python drops the installed .pc file (bug #966038).
rule: When a C-extension package supports free-threaded CPython, declare both variants compactly with python3_{X..Y}{,t} in PYTHON_COMPAT.

problem: Routine version bump; the interesting soname/subslot and header-location handling predates this commit.
Loop variables (dep, dimg, uiimg) in src_unpack/src_install were not declared local, leaking into the global shell environment of the ebuild.
root_cause: Bash for-loop variables are global by default; in ebuilds they can leak across phase functions or collide with eclass/global variables, causing hard-to-trace bugs.
fix_pattern: Added 'local dep' / 'local dimg uiimg' declarations immediately before the for loops inside the phase functions.
rule: Always declare loop and scratch variables 'local' inside ebuild phase functions; this is checkable with shellcheck-style linting.
snippet:
src_unpack() {
	...
	local dep
	for dep in *.{tar.gz,zip,png,svg}; do
		...
	done
}

problem: Unconditional RDEPEND on >=sys-apps/openrc-0.15 broke users running netifrc under systemd (openrc+systemd not coinstallable with certain USE, bug #693270).
root_cause: The package works with either init system, but the dependency modeled only the common OpenRC case, forcing openrc into every depgraph.
fix_pattern: Added IUSE=systemd and split the init dependency: systemd? ( sys-apps/systemd ) !systemd? ( >=sys-apps/openrc-0.15 ), applied to all live ebuilds plus 9999, with a revbump on released versions since RDEPEND changed.
rule: When a package runs under multiple init systems, gate the init-system dependency behind USE=systemd with a !systemd? OpenRC branch instead of hard-depending on one; revbump because RDEPEND changes affect installed packages.
snippet:
IUSE="+dhcp systemd"
RDEPEND="
	sys-apps/gentoo-functions
	systemd? ( sys-apps/systemd )
	!systemd? ( >=sys-apps/openrc-0.15 )
"

problem: Puppet picked OpenRC as the service provider even on systemd-booted Gentoo boxes, so service resources acted on the wrong init system.
root_cause: Upstream's OpenRC provider was defaultfor os.name=gentoo without confining itself to systems actually booted with OpenRC/sysvinit.
fix_pattern: Small FILESDIR patch adding a confine on /proc/1/comm containing 'init' (i.e. not systemd) to the provider; shipped as a straight-to-stable revbump (-r1 keeps existing stable KEYWORDS) while deliberately keeping the old revision around as a fallback.
rule: Detect the running init system via /proc/1/comm rather than distro identity; when pushing a low-risk fix straight to stable, add a new revision with stable keywords but keep the previous revision temporarily as a rollback path.

problem: Upstream signing keys fetched from a single source were stale/incomplete for verification (bug #978419).
root_cause: sec-keys.eclass can aggregate each key from multiple sources (manual, ubuntu keyserver, gentoo keyserver); listing only 'manual' made the keyring fragile.
fix_pattern: Extended SEC_KEYS_VALIDPGPKEYS entries from 'fpr:name:manual' to 'fpr:name:manual,ubuntu' / 'manual,gentoo' so the eclass adds keyserver-sourced DISTs, and changed SRC_URI= to SRC_URI+= because the eclass now pre-populates SRC_URI from those sources.
rule: In sec-keys ebuilds, list multiple sources per fingerprint (manual,ubuntu,gentoo) for resilience, and remember to append (SRC_URI+=) rather than overwrite once eclass-generated URIs exist.

problem: Destabilize: stable KEYWORDS flipped back to ~arch on a regressed kernel release instead of removing the ebuild; niche tree-policy action.
Meson project with vendored Rust subprojects must fetch crates through Portage (network-sandboxed builds) even though it is not a cargo-built package.
root_cause: Meson wrap dependencies expect tarballs in subprojects/packagecache, while Gentoo delivers crates as ${DISTDIR}/*.crate; cargo/llvm/rust eclasses are needed only conditionally.
fix_pattern: Declare CRATES + inherit cargo with CARGO_OPTIONAL=1/RUST_OPTIONAL=1 (and LLVM_OPTIONAL=1), add ${CARGO_CRATE_URIS} to SRC_URI, symlink each crate from DISTDIR into ${S}/subprojects/packagecache/<name>.tar.gz in src_unpack, and call rust_pkg_setup/llvm-r2_pkg_setup only under the USE conditions that need them.
rule: For meson projects with Rust wrap subprojects, reuse the cargo eclass only for crate fetching (CARGO_OPTIONAL=1) and populate subprojects/packagecache from DISTDIR by symlink in src_unpack.
snippet:
pushd "${DISTDIR}" >/dev/null || die
mkdir -p "${S}"/subprojects/packagecache || die
local i
for i in *.crate; do
	ln -s "${PWD}/${i}" "${S}/subprojects/packagecache/${i/.crate/}.tar.gz" || die
done
popd >/dev/null || die

problem: Dropped an IUSE flag (modplug) after upstream removed the feature; reminder to prune dead USE flags on bumps, but a one-line package-specific change.
Users installing games hit ugly package blockers because reverse deps required sdl-mixer[mod] / [modplug] which were off by default
root_cause: Library USE defaults did not match what most consumers demand via their dependency strings, forcing per-user USE churn and blocker resolution
fix_pattern: Turn the flags on by default in IUSE (+mod +modplug); when two alternative providers exist (modplug vs libxmp), default to the one with more consumers/reverse deps
rule: When many reverse deps require a USE flag on a common library and users hit blockers, make that flag default-on (+flag) and pick the implementation variant with the most consumers
snippet:
-IUSE="... mikmod mod modplug mp3 ..."
+IUSE="... mikmod +mod +modplug mp3 ..."

problem: Same default-USE blocker fix as sdl-mixer (190c22a) applied to the SDL2 variant; duplicate lesson
Packaging an upstream that only has moving git snapshots plus a huge separate data repository, both needed for one install
root_cause: No tagged release; code and data live in two GitHub repos that must be fetched reproducibly and built as two CMake trees
fix_pattern: Use a _pre/_p PV with pinned COMMIT_ID/COMMIT_ID_DATA vars in SRC_URI (archive/${COMMIT_ID}.tar.gz -> ${P}.tar.gz), set S to the commit-named dir, and configure/compile/install the second tree by overriding CMAKE_USE_DIR and BUILD_DIR per call; also -DCMAKE_DISABLE_FIND_PACKAGE_LuaJIT=ON plus -DLUA_VERSION=$(lua_get_version) to stop automagic LuaJIT pickup
rule: For untagged upstream snapshots pin exact commit hashes in SRC_URI variables, and drive a second CMake source tree by prefixing cmake_src_* calls with CMAKE_USE_DIR/BUILD_DIR overrides instead of hand-rolling a second build
snippet:
COMMIT_ID="329e03ae..."
SRC_URI="https://github.com/Org/Proj/archive/${COMMIT_ID}.tar.gz -> ${P}.tar.gz"
S="${WORKDIR}/Proj-${COMMIT_ID}"
# second tree, each phase:
CMAKE_USE_DIR="${CMAKE_USE_DIR}/content" BUILD_DIR="${BUILD_DIR}/content" cmake_src_configure

problem: Bundled C extension fails to build against modern NumPy: NPY_IN_ARRAY / NPY_ALIGNED / NPY_ENSURECOPY undeclared
root_cause: NumPy removed the pre-1.7 deprecated macro names; upstream C sources still use the old spellings
fix_pattern: sed-rename the deprecated macros to their NPY_ARRAY_* successors in src_prepare before distutils-r1_src_prepare; also note PYPI_NO_NORMALIZE=1 and S pointing into a monorepo 'package' subdir for GitHub tag tarballs
rule: When a Python C extension fails with undeclared NPY_* macros, sed-rename NPY_X to NPY_ARRAY_X (IN_ARRAY, ALIGNED, ENSURECOPY, ...) in src_prepare instead of carrying a patch

problem: Routine security version bump of the large Mozilla template ebuild (new patchset tarball, new source tarball); no new generalizable idiom in the delta
Bash completions installed as <command>.bash so bash-completion never loads them (completions must be named exactly after the command)
root_cause: Upstream Makefile convention appends a .bash suffix, clashing with the bash-completion lookup convention used by Gentoo
fix_pattern: sed the install rule in GNUmakefile to strip the .bash suffix, move the tweak into src_prepare after default, and revbump (-r1) because installed file names change
rule: When upstream installs bash completions with an extension, rename them to bare command names (patch the Makefile or use newbashcomp) and revbump since installed files change

problem: Build fails with CMake 4: cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 2.4) is below the removed <3.5 compatibility floor, and extension-less source names break under CMP0115
root_cause: CMake 4 removed compatibility with cmake_minimum_required < 3.5, and CMP0115 requires source files to be listed with explicit extensions
fix_pattern: Ship a small patch bumping CMAKE_MINIMUM_REQUIRED to 3.20 and rewriting add_library source lists with explicit .c extensions
rule: When a package dies under CMake 4 with 'Compatibility with CMake < 3.5 has been removed', patch cmake_minimum_required up (>=3.5, commonly 3.20) and add explicit file extensions to any extension-less source lists
snippet:
-CMAKE_MINIMUM_REQUIRED(VERSION 2.4)
+CMAKE_MINIMUM_REQUIRED(VERSION 3.20)
-add_library(cuefile-shared SHARED cd cdtext cue_parse ...)
+add_library(cuefile-shared SHARED cd.c cdtext.c cue_parse.c ...)

problem: clang-22 hard-errors on chained comparison 'if (0 < i <= cd->ntrack)' which does not behave like the mathematical expression
root_cause: C evaluates (0 < i) first to 0/1 then compares that to the bound, so the old code was a latent logic bug that new clang now rejects
fix_pattern: Rewrite as explicit conjunction ((0 < i) && (i <= n)); commit message notes the revbump is required because the patch fixes runtime behaviour, not just the build
rule: When clang errors on a chained comparison X < Y <= Z, split it into (X < Y) && (Y <= Z); since that changes runtime results, revbump rather than editing in place
snippet:
-	if (0 < i <= cd->ntrack)
+	if ((0 < i) && (i <= cd->ntrack))

problem: Upstream bundles a full copy of libfmt in libs/fmt, shadowing the system library
root_cause: Upstream vendored fmt for convenience with no configure switch to use the system copy
fix_pattern: Carry the upstream-pending PR patch adding --with-system-fmt (PKG_CHECK_MODULES([FMT], [fmt >= 11.0.0]) + AM_CONDITIONAL), switch includes from "fmt/..." to <fmt/...>, rm -r the bundled dir in src_prepare, eautoreconf, econf --with-system-fmt, and add >=dev-libs/libfmt-11.0.0:= to DEPEND
rule: To unbundle a vendored library, upstream (or patch in) a --with-system-X configure option, physically delete the bundled copy in src_prepare so it cannot be picked up, and depend on the system lib with a := slot operator

problem: Upstream changed the library ABI and soname between releases; consumers linked with := would not have rebuilt if the subslot stayed constant
root_cause: Subslot did not encode the soname, so ABI breaks were invisible to the dependency resolver
fix_pattern: Set SLOT="0/$(ver_cut 1-2)" so the subslot tracks the upstream major.minor that drives the soname; := consumers (sidplayfp etc.) then rebuild automatically on every ABI change
rule: Encode the shared-library soname (or the version components that determine it) in the subslot, and update the subslot scheme whenever upstream changes how the soname is derived

problem: OpenRC initscript never ran checkconfig on restart, so a broken config could take the running daemon down
root_cause: Inverted test in stop_pre: [ "${RC_CMD}" != "restart" ] instead of = — the check was skipped exactly in the restart case it was written for
fix_pattern: Fix the RC_CMD comparison so config validation happens in stop_pre only during restart, and revbump by renaming the ebuild to -r1 even though only files/ changed, because the installed initscript changed
rule: Validate config in stop_pre gated on RC_CMD = restart so a bad config aborts the restart before stopping the service, and always revbump when anything installed from files/ changes

problem: Same inverted RC_CMD restart check as knot-resolver (ff49537); duplicate lesson
git's meson build invokes cargo from a script, so cargo ran without Gentoo's toolchain configuration (wrong target/flags, no vendored-source config)
root_cause: Ebuild only inherited the rust eclass (RUST_OPTIONAL=1) which provides a rustc but none of the cargo environment (CARGO_HOME config, RUSTFLAGS, cross target) that the cargo eclass sets up
fix_pattern: Switch to 'CARGO_OPTIONAL=1; inherit cargo' (cargo eclass pulls in rust itself), call 'use rust && cargo_gen_config' at the end of src_unpack, and wrap the build-system call as 'cargo_env meson_src_compile' when USE=rust
rule: When a non-cargo build system (meson/make/cmake) shells out to cargo, inherit the cargo eclass with CARGO_OPTIONAL=1, run cargo_gen_config after unpack, and run the compile phase under cargo_env

problem: Upstream CMake gzip-compresses man pages itself, installs Debian changelogs/lintian files, and automagically rebuilds docs with pandoc/LaTeX if found
root_cause: Upstream build targets Debian packaging conventions; distro-side compression and doc policies conflict (portage compresses docs itself, pandoc dep is automagic and huge)
fix_pattern: Carry three small patches: install uncompressed .1 man pages, delete the Debian packaging block, and rip out the pandoc/LaTeX doc pipeline in favor of the shipped HTML docs; use docompress -x on the doc dir and plocale_find_changes/plocale_for_each_disabled_locale to prune locales
rule: Patch out upstream install rules that pre-compress man pages or perform automagic doc regeneration (pandoc/LaTeX); let portage handle compression and use docompress -x only for files that must stay uncompressed

problem: Routine prebuilt-binary version bump (new ebuild copy + Manifest); no generalizable content
Routine prebuilt-binary version bump on the ESR branch; no generalizable content
Package tests fail because setuptools is not present in the test environment
root_cause: Tests build/install a test package needing setuptools; since modern Python/pip no longer implicitly provide setuptools everywhere, it must be an explicit dependency
fix_pattern: Add dev-python/setuptools[${PYTHON_USEDEP}] inside the test? ( ) BDEPEND group, with a comment explaining why
rule: Never assume setuptools exists in a test env: if tests import pkg_resources/setuptools or pip-install a source package, declare setuptools as an explicit test dep

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT extension to python3_15 after tests pass; standard one-liner
Test suite invokes 'pip install' which, under build isolation, tries to download setuptools from the network inside the sandbox
root_cause: pip's default build isolation creates an ephemeral env and fetches build deps from PyPI, which is forbidden at build time
fix_pattern: Patch the test's pip invocation to add --no-build-isolation so it uses the already-installed build backends; also uses PYPI_VERIFY_REPO=<github repo> with .provenance Manifest entries for pypi attestation verification
rule: When tests shell out to pip, force --no-build-isolation (and pre-install the build backend as a test dep) so no network access happens in the sandbox

problem: With USE=acoustid the CMake configure phase violates the sandbox by touching /dev (bug #855281)
root_cause: The chromaprint/ffmpeg feature probe accesses device nodes at configure time, which the portage sandbox denies
fix_pattern: Conditionally addpredict /dev in src_configure only when the offending USE flag is enabled, instead of disabling the feature or the sandbox globally
rule: For configure/build-time sandbox violations on paths like /dev, add a narrowly-scoped 'use flag && addpredict <path>' workaround with a bug reference rather than RESTRICT or dropping the feature
snippet:
src_configure() {
	...
	if use acoustid; then
		addpredict /dev # bug #855281
	fi
	cmake_src_configure
}

problem: Routine security bump (new version copied from previous ebuild, keywords reset from stable to ~arch); no new idiom in the delta
systemd's fiber bootstrap needs ucontext functions which musl does not provide, breaking the build/runtime on musl profiles
root_cause: musl deliberately omits the deprecated makecontext/swapcontext family; a separate library provides them
fix_pattern: Add sys-libs/libucontext to the existing elibc_musl? ( ) dependency group in COMMON_DEPEND, applied to both the release and 9999 ebuilds
rule: When code uses ucontext.h (makecontext/swapcontext/getcontext) and fails on musl, add an elibc_musl? ( sys-libs/libucontext ) dependency instead of patching the feature out

problem: The 9999 live ebuild had drifted from the release ebuilds (renamed USE flags modplug->libopenmpt, shaders folded into vulkan deps, stale REQUIRED_USE)
root_cause: Live ebuilds are not bumped, so USE/dependency refactors done on release ebuilds silently rot in -9999 until someone syncs it
fix_pattern: 'sync live' commit: replay the release ebuild's IUSE/RDEPEND/mycmakeargs changes onto the 9999 ebuild, folding a dependent flag (shaders) into its parent (vulkan? ( vulkan-loader shaderc )) to shrink REQUIRED_USE
rule: After refactoring USE flags or deps in a release ebuild, diff it against the -9999 ebuild and sync, since future bumps are usually copied from live

problem: Dropped old samba versions had to be restored via git revert (drop was premature while newer versions still had issues); revert cleanly restores ebuilds and Manifest DIST lines
make-based install broke on Gentoo Prefix: files landed in doubled or un-prefixed paths
root_cause: DESTDIR was set to ${ED} (which already contains EPREFIX) while PREFIX also contained ${EPREFIX}, double-applying the offset; meanwhile LIBPATH/INCPATH lacked EPREFIX entirely
fix_pattern: Pass DESTDIR="${D}" (never ED) and put ${EPREFIX} into every absolute path variable handed to make (PREFIX, LIBPATH, INCPATH)
rule: With raw Makefile installs, use DESTDIR=${D} and embed ${EPREFIX} in each path variable; combining ED with EPREFIX-bearing paths double-prefixes and breaks Prefix installs
snippet:
emake ... \
	DESTDIR="${D}" \
	PREFIX="${EPREFIX}/usr" \
	LIBPATH="${EPREFIX}/usr/$(get_libdir)" \
	INCPATH="${EPREFIX}/usr/include" install

problem: Routine straight bump: ebuild unchanged, just git-renamed 21.12.2 -> 21.12.3 plus Manifest update for a security release
Security release 22.10.1 (bug 978372) needed in tree while 22.10.0 was still the version users had installed.
root_cause: Security fixes must reach stable users; removing the prior version before the new one is tested/stabilized would break the upgrade path, and the security bug must stay open until stabilization+cleanup.
fix_pattern: Add the patched version as a NEW ebuild (copy of previous, no changes needed) alongside the old one, and reference the security bug with 'Bug:' in the commit message rather than 'Closes:' so the tracker stays open through stabilization. The ebuild also shows durable idioms: SLOT="0/${PV%%.*}" to subslot on the major version, and pkg_postinst comparing ver_cut 1 of REPLACING_VERSIONS to only print the upgrade-doc elog on major-version crossings.
rule: For a security patch release, add the new version alongside the existing one and tag the commit 'Bug: <url>' (not Closes:); only drop old versions after the fixed version is stable.
snippet:
net-misc/asterisk: add 22.10.1

Bug: https://bugs.gentoo.org/978372  # kept open for stabilization
# ebuild: SLOT="0/${PV%%.*}"  # subslot = major version

problem: Patch-release security bump 23.4.0->23.4.1 done as a pure ebuild rename (100% similarity) plus Manifest update; ~arch-only branch so old version dropped in the same commit, unlike the 22.x stable-track branch where old was kept.
Ebuild declared DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry, pulling the full poetry tool (huge dep graph) as a build dep when only the build backend is needed.
root_cause: poetry and flit split their PEP517 build backends into tiny standalone packages (poetry-core, flit_core); the full tools are only needed for developer workflows, never for building a wheel. Gentoo's distutils-r1 accepts the -core backends directly.
fix_pattern: Change DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry to poetry-core (and flit to flit-core, done in warlock/tinyhtml5/tinycss2 in the same batch). Works whenever pyproject.toml's build-backend is poetry.core.masonry.api / flit_core.buildapi, which is almost always the case.
rule: When a Python package uses poetry or flit, set DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 to poetry-core/flit-core rather than the full tool to minimize BDEPEND.

problem: C-extension package with free-threaded CPython support only listed python3_14t; new 3.15 needed both the regular and the freethreading target extended.
root_cause: Freethreading (t) implementations are separate PYTHON_COMPAT targets from the regular ones; a plain range bump misses them, silently dropping free-threaded support for the new version.
fix_pattern: Extend both lists in one edit using brace ranges: python3_{12..15} python3_{14..15}t. Only list t variants for packages verified to build/test under free-threaded CPython (typically C-extension packages that declare support).
rule: When bumping PYTHON_COMPAT on a package that lists freethreading (t) targets, extend the t range in the same edit, and use brace ranges for both.

problem: Upstream GitHub repo moved from a personal account (niccokunzmann) to an org (pycalendar); HOMEPAGE, SRC_URI and metadata.xml remote-id pointed at the stale location.
root_cause: GitHub redirects moved repos so fetching keeps working silently (Manifest unchanged - same tarball bytes), but stale URLs break once the old name is re-registered, and tooling (nvchecker, remote-id) tracks the wrong repo.
fix_pattern: During the next routine edit (here a py3.15 bump), rewrite HOMEPAGE and SRC_URI to the new org and update metadata.xml remote-id; no Manifest change needed since GitHub archive bytes are identical through the redirect. Same commit also converted a pytest-plugin test dep to EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-click ).
rule: When a GitHub upstream redirects to a new owner, update HOMEPAGE/SRC_URI/remote-id to the canonical location at the next touch of the ebuild - do not rely on the redirect.

problem: PYTHON_COMPAT listed pypy3_11 and python3_11 which are being retired, and lacked new python3_15.
root_cause: Python implementation lifecycle: new CPython minors get enabled tree-wide after per-package test runs, while EOL/retired impls (py3.11, pypy3_11) are dropped from PYTHON_COMPAT at the same time to shrink the support matrix.
fix_pattern: Single-line edit combining add-new and drop-old via a brace range; no revbump - changing PYTHON_COMPAT does not alter installed files for existing impls, so no -rN is needed. The batch (23 packages in one afternoon) shows this is done mechanically after running the test suite under the new impl.
rule: Enable a new Python target by extending the PYTHON_COMPAT brace range and drop retired impls in the same edit, after tests pass under the new interpreter; no revision bump is required.

problem: Enabling py3.15 required more than the PYTHON_COMPAT line: a python_gen_cond_dep range for legacy-cgi and an EPYTHON version glob deselecting known-bad tests both encoded '3.14 is the newest' and would silently not apply to 3.15.
root_cause: Version-conditional logic elsewhere in an ebuild (conditional deps for removed stdlib modules like cgi, per-impl test deselections) hardcodes impl ranges; bumping only PYTHON_COMPAT leaves the new impl missing a runtime dep and running tests known to fail upstream.
fix_pattern: Grep the ebuild for every version-conditional site when extending PYTHON_COMPAT: extend python_gen_cond_dep '...' 3.{13..14} to 3.{13..15}, and widen the EPYTHON glob in python_test from python3.14* to python3.1[45]* so the EPYTEST_DESELECT for the upstream-tracked failure (linked issue in comment) still applies.
rule: When adding a new Python impl to PYTHON_COMPAT, audit and extend every python_gen_cond_dep range and EPYTHON version test in the ebuild in the same commit.
snippet:
-	' 3.{13..14})
+	' 3.{13..15})
...
-	if [[ ${EPYTHON} == python3.14* ]] ; then
+	if [[ ${EPYTHON} == python3.1[45]* ]] ; then
 		EPYTEST_DESELECT+=( # https://github.com/Pylons/webob/issues/479
 			tests/test_in_wsgiref.py::test_interrupted_request )

problem: Test phase let pytest autoload every plugin installed on the system, making test results non-deterministic and prone to breakage when some unrelated plugin does not yet support the new Python version.
root_cause: pytest autoloads all installed entry-point plugins by default; distutils-r1's newer EPYTEST_PLUGINS mechanism disables autoloading and loads only the declared list (empty array = no plugins).
fix_pattern: Add EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest for packages whose tests need no plugins; done alongside the DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit->flit-core swap. Applied across the batch (spake2, seedir, sqlalchemy-rqlite, webob).
rule: Declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS (empty if none) before distutils_enable_tests pytest so tests run without plugin autoloading and stay reproducible across interpreter bumps.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT brace-range extension python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; kept DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry (not switched to poetry-core here).
PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} dropping pypy3_11, plus flit->flit-core backend swap (idiom recorded under yaspin 9829f29f).
PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} dropping pypy3_11, plus flit->flit-core backend swap (idiom recorded under yaspin 9829f29f).
Test-only pytest plugins (flaky, pytest-timeout) were hand-written as a BDEPEND test? block, duplicating what the eclass can derive, and pytest still autoloaded all installed plugins.
root_cause: distutils-r1 grew EPYTEST_PLUGINS: declaring plugin names generates the test? BDEPEND automatically and runs pytest with autoloading disabled, loading exactly the listed plugins - the manual BDEPEND block is now redundant and less strict.
fix_pattern: Delete the BDEPEND test? block listing pytest plugins and set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( flaky pytest-timeout ) before distutils_enable_tests pytest; the eclass adds the deps and -p flags. Same migration in qtsass ( flaky ) and x-wr-timezone ( pytest-click ) in this batch.
rule: Replace manual 'test? ( dev-python/<pytest-plugin> )' BDEPEND blocks with EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( <names> ) declared before distutils_enable_tests pytest.
snippet:
-BDEPEND="
-	test? (
-		dev-python/flaky[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]
-		dev-python/pytest-timeout[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]
-	)
-"
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( flaky pytest-timeout )
 distutils_enable_tests pytest

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} on a pdm-backend package; no other changes.
Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{12..14} -> python3_{12..15} on a DISTUTILS_EXT=1 package.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() autoload opt-out (idiom recorded under warlock ed880911).
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() autoload opt-out (idiom recorded under warlock ed880911).
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump dropping pypy3_11/py3.11, adding py3.15, plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (idiom recorded under warlock ed880911).
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump plus BDEPEND->EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( flaky ) migration (idiom recorded under three-merge 76d8ac54).
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump dropping pypy3_11, adding py3.15, on a virtualx-tested package; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump dropping pypy3_11, adding py3.15; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump dropping pypy3_11, adding py3.15; no other changes.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}; DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit left unconverted here.
Package not yet marked compatible with newly-keyworded Python 3.15.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT was a static range (pypy3_11 python3_{11..14}) written before py3.15 existed; distutils-r1 refuses targets not listed.
fix_pattern: One-line PYTHON_COMPAT edit that simultaneously adds the new slot and drops end-of-life interpreters (python3_11 and stale pypy3_11): PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} ). No revbump: compat expansion changes no installed files for existing users; representative of ~18 identical commits in this batch by mgorny.
rule: When a new CPython slot lands, extend the PYTHON_COMPAT brace range and drop EOL CPython/pypy targets in the same edit after test-building with the new interpreter; a compat-only change needs no revision bump.

problem: While enabling py3.15, ebuild lacked explicit pytest plugin control, so tests run with whatever pytest plugins happen to be installed system-wide.
root_cause: distutils_enable_tests pytest without EPYTEST_PLUGINS lets pytest autoload all installed plugins, causing nondeterministic test failures; distutils-r1 now supports declaring the exact plugin set.
fix_pattern: Set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (empty array) immediately before distutils_enable_tests pytest when the suite needs no pytest plugins; distutils-r1 then disables plugin autoloading. Same idiom added in pykakasi, pyformance, mkautodoc, markdown-include in this batch.
rule: When a pytest suite needs no plugins, declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest so unrelated installed plugins cannot break or alter the tests.

problem: Upstream GitHub account was renamed (tomchristie -> lovelydinosaur), leaving SRC_URI, HOMEPAGE and metadata.xml remote-id pointing at the old name.
root_cause: GitHub account renames redirect old URLs only until the old name is reclaimed, and version-check tooling (nvchecker, pkgcheck remote-id) follows the recorded account, so stale references rot silently.
fix_pattern: Update all three places in one commit: SRC_URI download URL, HOMEPAGE, and the <remote-id type="github"> in metadata.xml; keep the -> ${P}.gh.tar.gz rename so the distfile name is unchanged (no Manifest churn beyond re-fetch).
rule: When an upstream GitHub repo or account is renamed, fix SRC_URI, HOMEPAGE and metadata.xml remote-id together in the same commit; do not rely on GitHub's redirect.

problem: XS module fails to build against perl-5.44 (bug 978368) although it built fine for years.
root_cause: The new ExtUtils::ParseXS shipped with perl-5.44 is stricter and turns a long-latent typo in the .xs file ('PROTOTYPES: DISABLES' instead of 'DISABLE') into a hard error.
fix_pattern: Trivial FILESDIR patch fixing the XS keyword typo, added via PATCHES=(), plus a straight revbump copy of the ebuild (-r3 -> -r4) so installed systems rebuild.
rule: When an old XS-based perl package breaks after a perl upgrade, suspect newly-strict ExtUtils::ParseXS parsing (keyword typos, malformed declarations) and fix with a one-line source patch plus revbump rather than pinning old perl.

problem: Gtk2 perl bindings fail to build with perl-5.44 (bug 978277).
root_cause: perl-5.44's new ExtUtils::ParseXS dropped/changed the XS length(param) pseudo-parameter feature used in the XSUB signature 'gint length(initial_chars)', so the generated C no longer compiles.
fix_pattern: Rewrite the affected XSUB to take a plain SV* and derive buffer+length manually with SvPVutf8() and g_utf8_strlen(), shipped as a FILESDIR patch with a revbump; ebuild also keeps append-cflags -std=gnu17 for the archived (dead) upstream's pre-C23 code.
rule: When perl-5.44's ExtUtils::ParseXS breaks XS code using the length() auto-length feature, patch the XSUB to accept SV* and extract the string and length explicitly via SvPV/SvPVutf8.

problem: Workaround that downgrades _FORTIFY_SOURCE=3 to =2 via 'append-cppflags -U_FORTIFY_SOURCE -D_FORTIFY_SOURCE=2' did not take effect under Clang (bug 971773).
root_cause: Gentoo's Clang driver automatically injects _FORTIFY_SOURCE itself; unlike GCC, a plain -U_FORTIFY_SOURCE on the command line does not stop that automatic injection, so the forced =2 still ended up fighting the driver default.
fix_pattern: Add the Gentoo toolchain escape-hatch macro -D_GENTOO_NO_FORTIFY_SOURCE alongside the -U/-D pair; that macro is the supported way to tell Gentoo's patched Clang not to auto-add _FORTIFY_SOURCE, after which the explicit -D_FORTIFY_SOURCE=2 wins. filter-flags first removes any user-supplied -D_FORTIFY_SOURCE=3.
rule: When an ebuild must force a specific _FORTIFY_SOURCE level on Gentoo, also define _GENTOO_NO_FORTIFY_SOURCE so Clang's automatic fortify injection is disabled; -U_FORTIFY_SOURCE alone only works for GCC.

problem: Same Clang _FORTIFY_SOURCE workaround fix as systemd-utils f982c023, applied across five systemd ebuilds including 9999 (see that record for the pattern).

Test suite failed: a dconf-backed test needs a D-Bus session bus (absent under virtx, and dbus[-X] provides no session for the X test env), and another test chokes parsing non-POSIX locale dirs like es_419 (bug 978295).
root_cause: GSettings/dconf tests require a running session bus that neither the ebuild env nor virtx provides; the locale test wrongly validates every locale dir/alias on the host, so results depend on installed locales.
fix_pattern: Three-part idiom: (1) add gnome-base/dconf and sys-apps/dbus to BDEPEND under test? and introduce IUSE=test with RESTRICT="!test? ( test )"; (2) wrap the runner as 'virtx dbus-run-session "${EPYTHON}" -m unittest discover -v -p Test*.py || die' so a private session bus exists inside the virtual X session; (3) disable the environment-sensitive test by sed-renaming the method (s/test_x(self)/_&/) with a bug-reference comment.
rule: When tests touch GSettings/dconf or otherwise need D-Bus, run them under dbus-run-session (composable with virtx) and add dbus/dconf to test BDEPEND instead of relying on the host session bus; neuter host-environment-dependent tests by renaming the test method with sed.
snippet:
BDEPEND="... test? ( gnome-base/dconf sys-apps/dbus )"
sed -e "s/test_assertIsLanguageCode_live(self)/_&/" -i tests/TestCommon.py || die
virtx dbus-run-session "${EPYTHON}" -m unittest discover -v -p Test*.py || die

problem: patool's rpm tests fail only on systems where app-arch/rpm was built without the sequoia USE flag, because signed-rpm handling differs (bug 977415).
root_cause: Test expectations depend not just on which dependency is installed but on how it was built (USE=sequoia changes rpm's signature verification backend), which the test suite cannot know about.
fix_pattern: Probe the installed dependency's USE configuration at test time with has_version including a USE-dep, and conditionally extend EPYTEST_DESELECT with the affected test IDs plus a bug-number comment, before calling epytest; applied identically to all live ebuild versions.
rule: When tests pass or fail depending on a USE flag of an installed dependency, gate EPYTEST_DESELECT entries on 'has_version cat/pkg[flag]' (or [-flag]) inside python_test instead of skipping the tests unconditionally.
snippet:
if has_version app-arch/rpm[-sequoia]; then
	EPYTEST_DESELECT+=(
		# bug #977415
		"tests/archives/test_rpm.py::TestRpm::test_rpm_file"
		"tests/archives/test_rpm.py::TestRpm::test_rpm"
	)
fi
epytest

problem: Users wanted WebP thumbnails but the capability comes from a runtime-pluggable gdk-pixbuf loader, not from anything rox-filer links against.
root_cause: gdk-pixbuf discovers image loaders at runtime, so a hard RDEPEND on the webp loader would force it on everyone while the package builds and runs fine without it.
fix_pattern: inherit optfeature and advertise the plugin in pkg_postinst via 'optfeature "WebP image thumbnails" gui-libs/gdk-pixbuf-loader-webp'; done as an in-place edit of -r1 without revbump since no installed files change (only an elog message).
rule: When an optional capability is provided by a runtime plugin package (gdk-pixbuf loaders, gstreamer plugins, etc.), suggest it with optfeature in pkg_postinst instead of adding a dependency, and skip the revbump because installed content is unchanged.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}, dropping pypy3_11/py3.11; pattern covered by dde8b3f2.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; pattern covered by dde8b3f2.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; pattern covered by dde8b3f2.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; pattern covered by dde8b3f2.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=(); patterns covered by dde8b3f2 and dfb74b29.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=(); patterns covered by dde8b3f2 and dfb74b29.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; pattern covered by dde8b3f2.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; pattern covered by dde8b3f2.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump in release and 9999 ebuilds; pattern covered by dde8b3f2.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; pattern covered by dde8b3f2.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; pattern covered by dde8b3f2.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump dropping pypy3_11; pattern covered by dde8b3f2.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump dropping pypy3_11; pattern covered by dde8b3f2.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; pattern covered by dde8b3f2.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=(); patterns covered by dde8b3f2 and dfb74b29.
Test suite fills the build partition (users hit ENOSPC mid-src_test) with no advance warning; bug 978323.
root_cause: GNU tar's test suite creates ~10G of scratch tarballs; the ebuild never declared a disk requirement, and packages without an explicit test USE flag can't gate the requirement on tests actually running.
fix_pattern: Inherit check-reqs, add a test USE flag with RESTRICT="!test? ( test )" (needed so the requirement is conditional), and call check-reqs_pkg_setup with a local CHECKREQS_DISK_BUILD from a helper invoked in BOTH pkg_pretend and pkg_setup (pretend for --pretend visibility, setup for the real run). Keep the bug URL as a comment next to the magic size.
rule: When a package's tests (or build) need large scratch space, gate a check-reqs disk requirement on the relevant USE flag and wire it into both pkg_pretend and pkg_setup via one shared helper.

problem: Initial CHECKREQS_DISK_BUILD=10G for the test suite was still too low; users kept hitting ENOSPC (same bug 978323), fixed same day by bumping to 11G.
root_cause: check-reqs sizes are empirical estimates; actual peak usage varies with filesystem/config and the first guess was measured too tight.
fix_pattern: Simply raise the constant (10G -> 11G) in place; no revbump needed since the change only affects build-time checking, not installed files.
rule: Treat CHECKREQS_* values as empirical with headroom: when users still run out of space, bump the constant in place (no revbump required for pretend/setup-only changes) and keep the bug reference comment.

problem: New major giflib 6.1.3 added; upstream test suite silences every command with make's @ prefix, so any test failure in the build log is undiagnosable, and the makefile builds docs unconditionally.
root_cause: Upstream's hand-rolled makefile test harness hides all commands/output behind @ and pipes through a TAP viewer; raw make-based (non-out-of-source) build needs per-ABI source copies for multilib.
fix_pattern: Ship a FILESDIR patch that strips the @ silencing from tests/makefile (keeping --quiet where removal breaks results) so failures are visible in logs; run tests with emake -j1 check because the harness is not parallel-safe; sed out the unconditional '$(MAKE) -C doc' from Makefile; use multilib-minimal + multilib_copy_sources for the in-tree make build; bump subslot (SLOT="0/7") for the new soname; pass toolchain via emake AR=$(tc-getAR) CC=$(tc-getCC) CFLAGS/LDFLAGS since the raw makefile ignores the environment.
rule: When packaging a raw-Makefile project, patch @-silenced test rules to be verbose so CI logs show why tests fail, force -j1 if the harness is order-dependent, and encode soname changes in the subslot.

problem: test_compressed_writer fails on systems where zlib is provided by zlib-ng[compat], blocking the py3.15 enablement test run.
root_cause: The test asserts bit-exact compressed output, but zlib-ng produces different (still valid) deflate streams than reference zlib, so byte-comparison of compression results is implementation-dependent.
fix_pattern: In python_test, conditionally deselect the test only when the alternative zlib implementation is installed, using has_version on the provider package, so the test still runs on reference zlib.
rule: When a test compares compressed bytes exactly, guard an EPYTEST_DESELECT on has_version of the alternative compression provider (e.g. sys-libs/zlib-ng[compat]) instead of deselecting unconditionally.

problem: Package not yet marked compatible with newly-added Python 3.15 target.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT is an explicit allowlist; each new CPython target requires a per-package edit after verifying tests pass.
fix_pattern: Extend the brace range to the new impl and simultaneously drop the oldest (EOL-bound) impl, applying the same edit to ALL live ebuild versions of the package in one commit (both 0.2.23 and 0.2.25 here); no revbump because impl-list changes rebuild via USE deps.
rule: When enabling a new Python target, bump the whole brace range (python3_{12..15}) rather than appending, drop EOL impls (3.11, stale pypy3_11) in the same edit, and touch every live ebuild of the package.

problem: C-extension package needed py3.15 plus its freethreading (nogil) variant enabled.
root_cause: Freethreading CPython builds are separate PYTHON_COMPAT targets (python3_Nt) that must be listed independently of the regular impls, and matter most for packages with C extensions (DISTUTILS_EXT=1).
fix_pattern: Use a second brace range for the t-variants and advance both ranges together; drop stale pypy3_11 at the same time.
rule: For DISTUTILS_EXT packages tracking freethreading support, keep a parallel python3_{N..M}t range in PYTHON_COMPAT and advance it in lockstep with the regular range.

problem: While enabling py3.15, the test phase could pull in arbitrary system-installed pytest plugins via autoloading, making test results environment-dependent.
root_cause: pytest autoloads any installed plugin; without an explicit plugin list, tests may fail or pass differently depending on what else is on the machine.
fix_pattern: Set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (empty array) before distutils_enable_tests pytest to run with plugin autoloading disabled and no extra plugin deps; list needed plugins in the array when the suite requires them (same idiom applied in jaraco-envs, housekeeping, gitdb, furl in this wave).
rule: For pytest-based dev-python packages, declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS explicitly (empty if none needed) before distutils_enable_tests to make tests hermetic against pytest plugin autoloading.

problem: Ebuild declared DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry, a deprecated/renamed backend value, noticed during the py3.15 bump.
root_cause: The actual PEP517 build backend is poetry-core (dev-python/poetry-core), not the full poetry tool; distutils-r1 renamed the value to match.
fix_pattern: Change DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry to poetry-core during a routine touch of the ebuild.
rule: Use the PEP517 backend name that matches the real build-backend package (poetry-core, not poetry); sweep such renames opportunistically when touching an ebuild for a compat bump.

problem: After raising minimum Python to 3.12, the ebuild still carried a python_gen_cond_dep block adding importlib-resources for python3.11 only.
root_cause: importlib-resources is a stdlib backport needed only on <=3.11; once 3.11 leaves PYTHON_COMPAT the conditional dependency is dead code.
fix_pattern: Delete the python_gen_cond_dep '...' 3.11 block together with the PYTHON_COMPAT range bump.
rule: When dropping an old Python impl from PYTHON_COMPAT, grep the ebuild for python_gen_cond_dep blocks keyed to that impl (backports like importlib-resources/-metadata, exceptiongroup, tomli) and remove them.

Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT range bump, one-line change.

pytest autoloads all installed plugins during ebuild tests, causing nondeterministic failures and hidden deps; ebuild had no declaration of which plugins the test suite needs
root_cause: distutils-r1/epytest historically let pytest plugin autoloading run; the new eclass API expects ebuilds to declare needed plugins explicitly via EPYTEST_PLUGINS
fix_pattern: During the PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}, add EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest to state that no pytest plugins are required (eclass then disables plugin autoloading)
rule: When touching a dev-python ebuild that uses distutils_enable_tests pytest, declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS (empty array if the suite needs no plugins) so pytest runs with plugin autoloading disabled

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}, dropping pypy3/freethreading 3.13t variants; no generalizable content beyond the batch pattern

A pytest-plugin package (flaky) must test itself: some tests need the plugin loaded, others must run without it; old ebuild used 'epytest -p no:flaky' hacks and redundantly listed dev-python/pytest in BDEPEND
root_cause: With the EPYTEST_PLUGINS eclass mechanism, plugin autoloading is disabled globally, so per-test-run plugin selection should be expressed by re-declaring EPYTEST_PLUGINS locally instead of -p no:NAME disabling; distutils_enable_tests already adds the pytest BDEPEND
fix_pattern: Set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() globally, drop dev-python/pytest from BDEPEND, and inside python_test run the plugin-free tests first, then 'local EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( flaky )' to opt the plugin back in for the runs that need it
rule: When different parts of a test suite need different pytest plugin sets, scope 'local EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( ... )' inside python_test between epytest calls instead of using 'epytest -p no:plugin'
snippet:
EPYTEST_PLUGINS=()
distutils_enable_tests pytest

python_test() {
	# this one needs to be run without the plugin
	epytest test/test_pytest/test_flaky_pytest_plugin.py || die
	local EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( flaky )
	epytest -k 'example and not options' --doctest-modules test/test_pytest/ || die
}

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}, dropping pypy3_11; nothing generalizable
Same batch pattern as funcy: PYTHON_COMPAT bump plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() added before distutils_enable_tests pytest
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{12..14} to python3_{12..15}; nothing generalizable
Same batch pattern: PYTHON_COMPAT bump plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() declaration

python_test manually exported PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 and PYTEST_PLUGINS=pytest_mock.plugin, and pytest-mock was hand-listed in test BDEPEND; ebuild also used the deprecated 'poetry' PEP517 backend
root_cause: The raw env-var approach predates the EPYTEST_PLUGINS eclass API, which both loads the named plugins with autoloading disabled and auto-generates their test BDEPEND; DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry pulls full poetry instead of the lightweight poetry-core backend
fix_pattern: Replace the two env exports with EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-mock ) before distutils_enable_tests, delete pytest-mock from BDEPEND (eclass adds it), and switch DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry to poetry-core
rule: Replace manual PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD/PYTEST_PLUGINS exports and hand-written plugin BDEPEND with EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( plugin... ), and prefer DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry-core over poetry for building
snippet:
-DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry
+DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry-core
...
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-mock )
 distutils_enable_tests pytest
 python_test() {
-	local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1
-	local -x PYTEST_PLUGINS=pytest_mock.plugin
	local -x COLUMNS=80
	epytest
 }

problem: After dropping pypy3_11 from PYTHON_COMPAT, the ebuild still carried a pypy-only EPYTEST_DESELECT case block that could never trigger
root_cause: Implementation-specific test workarounds (case ${EPYTHON} in pypy3*) become dead code once that implementation leaves PYTHON_COMPAT
fix_pattern: In the same commit that removes an implementation from PYTHON_COMPAT, delete its EPYTEST_DESELECT/case-${EPYTHON} workarounds from python_test
rule: When removing a Python implementation from PYTHON_COMPAT, grep the ebuild for ${EPYTHON}/pypy conditionals and drop the now-dead workarounds in the same change

problem: One-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; nothing generalizable
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump dropping pypy3_11; nothing generalizable

Ebuild overrode src_test and called 'distutils-r1_src_test --geoblocked' expecting the flag to reach pytest; phase functions do not forward arguments, so the custom pytest option was silently dropped and geoblocked tests ran anyway
root_cause: distutils-r1_src_test takes no arguments (it just loops python_foreach_impl python_test); custom pytest options must be passed at the epytest call site inside python_test
fix_pattern: Replace the src_test override with a python_test() that calls epytest --geoblocked directly
rule: To pass extra pytest options in a distutils-r1 ebuild, define python_test() calling 'epytest <opts>'; never append arguments to a phase-function call like distutils-r1_src_test

problem: Date-versioned keyring bump done as pure ebuild rename; distfile hash unchanged (same key content, new snapshot date). Routine sec-keys maintenance
Routine date-versioned keyring bump via ebuild rename; DIST name intentionally differs from ${P} (shared per-developer keyring file). No generalizable content
SRC_URI used ${PN} inside the GitHub path (github.com/richgel999/${PN}/...), making the URL non-greppable and fragile if PN ever diverges from the upstream repo name
root_cause: Gentoo QA preference: variables in SRC_URI hide the real upstream location; the repo/org name is upstream's identifier, not the Gentoo package name, so substituting ${PN} couples them incorrectly
fix_pattern: Spell the upstream repo (and org) name literally in SRC_URI, keeping ${PV}/${P} only for the version parts; same commit family also fixed constructed org names like ${PN}-devs (highfive) and added the GitHub repo to HOMEPAGE (sysstat)
rule: Write the upstream project/org name as plain text in SRC_URI and HOMEPAGE, using variables only for version components

problem: Same plaintext-PN-in-SRC_URI QA polish as dev-libs/miniz, plus adding the GitHub repo to HOMEPAGE and alphabetizing IUSE; covered by the miniz lesson
Upstream Makefile.in ignored user CPPFLAGS and force-appended '-O2 -fno-tree-slp-vectorize' plus autoconf @DEFS@ handling was broken, so user CFLAGS/CPPFLAGS were not respected (QA flags violation)
root_cause: Hand-rolled Makefile.in only guards CFLAGS with ifndef and hardcodes optimization flags; @DEFS@/@CPPFLAGS@ were never wired into the compile line
fix_pattern: Ship a files/ patch making the Makefile respect the environment: guard CPPFLAGS with ifndef, append @DEFS@ to CPPFLAGS, drop the hardcoded -O2/-fno-tree-slp-vectorize, and append $(CPPFLAGS) to CFLAGS; in the ebuild use tc-export AR, --disable-stripping (let portage strip) and --disable-compress-manpg, and remove the installed COPYING from docdir
rule: When an upstream Makefile hardcodes optimization flags or drops CPPFLAGS/@DEFS@, patch it to append user flags instead of overriding them, and pass --disable-stripping so portage controls stripping

problem: Constructed GitHub org name '${PN}-devs' in SRC_URI replaced with literal 'highfive-devs', plus dependency sorting; covered by the miniz plaintext-PN lesson
PYTHON_COMPAT listed the freethreading target only for one version (python3_14t) and would silently miss it on the 3.15 bump
root_cause: Freethreading (no-GIL) CPython builds are separate PYTHON_COMPAT targets with a 't' suffix and support brace ranges just like regular targets; extension modules that support them must list them explicitly
fix_pattern: Bump both the regular and freethreading ranges together using the brace-range 't' syntax
rule: For packages that support freethreading CPython, bump python3_{N..M}t in lockstep with python3_{N..M} during PYTHON_COMPAT updates

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump incl. freethreading range; same pattern as zstandard
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump; nothing generalizable
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump; nothing generalizable
PYTHON_COMPAT bump applied to both the release and the -9999 live ebuild in one commit; routine (minor reminder to keep live ebuilds in sync)
Same batch pattern: PYTHON_COMPAT bump plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() declaration
One-line PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; nothing generalizable
Package not installable on new Python 3.15 target; PYTHON_COMPAT window stale (still carrying py3.11 and pypy3_11).
root_cause: New CPython release added to PYTHON_TARGETS requires each dev-python package to opt in via PYTHON_COMPAT; Gentoo rotates the window (add newest, drop oldest + stale pypy) in one edit.
fix_pattern: In-place edit of the existing ebuild, no revbump: PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{11..14} pypy3_11 ) -> PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} ), bump copyright year. Drive-by cleanup replaced 'touch "${S}/.tox"' with the shell builtin '> "${S}/.tox" || die' to avoid an external command for creating an empty file. Enabling/dropping Python implementations never gets a revision bump because it changes USE-conditional metadata, not installed files for existing configurations.
rule: When enabling a new Python implementation, rotate the whole PYTHON_COMPAT window in-place (add newest, drop EOL/oldest and stale pypy) without a revbump, after confirming the test suite passes on the new target.
snippet:
-PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{11..14} pypy3_11 )
+PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} )
...
-	touch "${S}/.tox" || die
+	> "${S}/.tox" || die

problem: pytest test run pulls in every system-installed pytest plugin via autoload, causing nondeterministic/breaking test behavior on new Python targets.
root_cause: distutils_enable_tests pytest by default lets pytest autoload all installed plugins; the modern distutils-r1/pytest eclass mechanism wants an explicit plugin list so BDEPEND and autoload are controlled.
fix_pattern: Declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (empty array = no plugins needed, disables plugin autoload and adds no plugin BDEPENDs) on the line before distutils_enable_tests pytest. Non-empty arrays list needed plugins by name (e.g. EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-timeout )).
rule: When touching a distutils-r1 ebuild that uses distutils_enable_tests pytest, set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( ... ) explicitly (empty if no plugins are required) instead of relying on plugin autoloading.

problem: cffi dependency wrapped in python_gen_cond_dep restricted to 'python*', and python_test manually exported PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 - both now dead weight.
root_cause: The conditional dep existed only because PyPy bundles cffi (dep must not apply to pypy3 targets); once pypy3_11 is dropped from PYTHON_COMPAT the condition is always true. PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 is the legacy manual idiom superseded by the eclass-level EPYTEST_PLUGINS variable.
fix_pattern: When dropping pypy from PYTHON_COMPAT, collapse $(python_gen_cond_dep 'dev-python/cffi[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]' 'python*') to a plain dev-python/cffi[${PYTHON_USEDEP}] dep. Replace 'local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1' inside python_test with EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest.
rule: After removing pypy3 from PYTHON_COMPAT, simplify any python_gen_cond_dep blocks conditioned on 'python*' into unconditional deps; and migrate manual PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD exports to EPYTEST_PLUGINS.
snippet:
-	$(python_gen_cond_dep '
-		dev-python/cffi[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]
-	' 'python*')
+	dev-python/cffi[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]
...
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=()
 distutils_enable_tests pytest
 python_test() {
-	local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1
 	epytest

problem: Ebuild fetched an autogenerated GitHub tarball, requiring MY_P renaming, an S override, and 'export SETUPTOOLS_SCM_PRETEND_VERSION=${PV}' because the git archive lacks setuptools-scm version metadata.
root_cause: GitHub autogenerated archives contain no .git metadata and no PKG-INFO, so setuptools-scm cannot determine the version; the PyPI sdist has the version baked in and a predictable layout.
fix_pattern: Switch SRC_URI to the PyPI sdist by inheriting pypi.eclass: drop the explicit SRC_URI/MY_P/S lines and the SETUPTOOLS_SCM_PRETEND_VERSION export; pypi.eclass supplies SRC_URI and the default S matches the sdist directory. Distfile name changes (python-varlink-31.0.0.gh.tar.gz -> varlink-31.0.0.tar.gz) so no revbump is needed despite the hash change.
rule: For setuptools-scm projects, prefer the PyPI sdist (inherit pypi) over GitHub autogenerated tarballs to eliminate SETUPTOOLS_SCM_PRETEND_VERSION, MY_P, and S overrides.

problem: Ebuild used the legacy DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry backend value.
root_cause: distutils-r1 renamed/deprecated the 'poetry' value in favor of 'poetry-core', matching the actual build backend package (dev-python/poetry-core), so the old alias must be migrated when touching the ebuild.
fix_pattern: Change DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry to DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry-core while doing the PYTHON_COMPAT rotation; also added EPYTEST_PLUGINS=().
rule: When editing a poetry-based Python ebuild, use DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry-core (the backend package name), not the legacy 'poetry' alias.

problem: cffi runtime dep wrapped in python_gen_cond_dep '... ' 'python*' became unconditional once pypy3_11 was dropped from PYTHON_COMPAT.
root_cause: The 'python*' condition existed solely to skip the cffi dep on PyPy (which bundles cffi); with no pypy targets left the wrapper is redundant.
fix_pattern: Collapse the python_gen_cond_dep wrapper to a plain slotted dep: >=dev-python/cffi-1.1.0:=[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]. Same pattern as xkbcommon in the same run.
rule: Dropping pypy from PYTHON_COMPAT should trigger a sweep for python_gen_cond_dep blocks conditioned on 'python*' that can be flattened.

problem: python_test manually set 'local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1', the legacy way to stop pytest plugin autoloading.
root_cause: distutils-r1/eclass grew the EPYTEST_PLUGINS mechanism which both disables autoload and declares plugin BDEPENDs; the manual env export is superseded.
fix_pattern: Add EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest and delete the PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD line from python_test, keeping any custom epytest arguments (-o 'python_files=test*.py').
rule: Replace manual PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD exports with the declarative EPYTEST_PLUGINS array whenever an ebuild is touched.
