pypi.eclass provenance verification broke after a mass sed replaced the upstream repo URL (iterative/shtab -> tqdm/shtab) everywhere in the ebuild, including PYPI_VERIFY_REPO.
root_cause: PYPI_VERIFY_REPO must match the source repo recorded in the PyPI provenance attestation of the ALREADY-PUBLISHED sdist. Upstream moved the GitHub repo after the 1.8.0 release, so the attestation still names the old org; blindly sed-ing the URL across the ebuild changed a verification anchor, not just cosmetic metadata.
fix_pattern: Revert only the PYPI_VERIFY_REPO line back to the repo that actually published the packaged release (iterative/shtab), while keeping HOMEPAGE at the new location (tqdm/shtab). The two variables are allowed to disagree until a new release is published from the new repo.
rule: When upstream renames or moves its repo, update HOMEPAGE freely but keep provenance/verification variables (PYPI_VERIFY_REPO and similar pinned-source values) pointing at the repo that built the currently-packaged release; never apply a repo-URL sed to lines that anchor artifact verification.

problem: Test dependencies for pytest plugins (hypothesis) were hand-written as BDEPEND="test? ( ... )", and pytest ran with all installed plugins autoloaded, which breaks unpredictably when a new Python target is enabled.
root_cause: distutils-r1/EPYTEST gained a declarative mechanism: EPYTEST_PLUGINS lists exactly the pytest plugins the suite needs; the eclass generates the test? BDEPEND for them and runs pytest with plugin autoloading disabled, loading only the listed plugins. Manual BDEPEND duplicates this and leaves autoloading on.
fix_pattern: Delete the manual test? BDEPEND for the plugin package and set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( hypothesis ) (package names without the dev-python/ prefix) BEFORE the distutils_enable_tests pytest call, since that function consumes the variable.
rule: In distutils-r1 ebuilds, declare pytest-plugin test deps via EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( plugin... ) placed above distutils_enable_tests pytest instead of hand-writing test? BDEPEND, so autoloading is disabled and only the declared plugins load.
snippet:
-BDEPEND="
-	test? ( dev-python/hypothesis[${PYTHON_USEDEP}] )
-"
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( hypothesis )
 EPYTEST_XDIST=1
 distutils_enable_tests pytest

problem: Test suite ran pytest with autoloading of whatever pytest plugins happen to be installed, causing spurious failures on new Python targets; ebuild also carried stale pypy3/pypy3_11 tokens.
root_cause: Without EPYTEST_PLUGINS, the eclass cannot know the suite needs no plugins, so it cannot disable plugin autoloading; unrelated system-installed plugins then run against the suite.
fix_pattern: Set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (empty array) immediately before distutils_enable_tests pytest to assert 'this suite needs no pytest plugins', making the eclass run pytest with autoloading disabled; simultaneously normalize PYTHON_COMPAT to python3_{12..15}, dropping pypy targets.
rule: When touching a distutils-r1 ebuild whose tests need no pytest plugins, add EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() above distutils_enable_tests pytest to pin the plugin set to empty and disable autoloading.

problem: Package lacked support for the newly-added Python 3.15 target.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT was frozen at python3_{11..14}; new interpreter targets are opt-in per ebuild after the maintainer test-runs the suite.
fix_pattern: Slide the whole compat window rather than only appending: python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}, retiring the oldest target in the same commit that enables the newest, and bump the copyright year.
rule: When enabling a new CPython target in ::gentoo-style ebuilds, move the PYTHON_COMPAT brace-range window forward (add newest, drop the impl being phased out) in one pass after running the test suite under the new target.

problem: After dropping pypy3_11 from PYTHON_COMPAT, the ebuild still wrapped dev-python/cffi in python_gen_cond_dep '...' 'python*', i.e. a conditional that now matched every remaining implementation.
root_cause: python_gen_cond_dep with the 'python*' pattern exists only to exclude pypy (which bundles cffi); once no pypy target remains in PYTHON_COMPAT the conditional is dead weight.
fix_pattern: Unwrap the dependency to a plain dev-python/cffi[${PYTHON_USEDEP}] entry when removing the last pypy target.
rule: When removing pypy from PYTHON_COMPAT, audit python_gen_cond_dep blocks whose pattern is 'python*' (or pypy-only) and flatten or delete them since the condition is now constant.

problem: Ebuild used the legacy DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit backend name.
root_cause: The PEP517 build backend actually used is flit_core; distutils-r1 renamed the setting value from 'flit' to 'flit-core' to match the real backend package, deprecating the old spelling.
fix_pattern: Change DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit to DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit-core during a routine touch (here bundled with the py3.15 enablement and EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() addition).
rule: When editing a distutils-r1 ebuild, migrate deprecated DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 values (flit -> flit-core) to the current backend names to stay ahead of eclass removal of the alias.

problem: Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT window bump plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() addition; idioms covered by other records in this batch.

Routine py3.15 bump plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=(); idioms covered elsewhere in this batch.
Routine py3.15 bump plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=(); idioms covered elsewhere in this batch.
Package lacked support for the newest CPython target (python3_15) while still carrying the oldest one (python3_11).
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT is a fixed window that must be manually rolled forward when python-utils-r1 gains a new target; leaving old targets in delays tree-wide removal of the old interpreter.
fix_pattern: Shift the whole PYTHON_COMPAT window instead of appending: python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}, dropping the oldest CPython and stale pypy3_11 in the same edit; bump the copyright year; commit WITHOUT a revbump (PYTHON_COMPAT changes are not revbump-worthy in Gentoo python policy). Representative of ~20 identical commits in this wave.
rule: When a new CPython target lands in the eclass, roll the PYTHON_COMPAT brace range forward (add newest, drop oldest/pypy being retired) after confirming tests pass, and do not revbump for it.

problem: Enabling a new Python target risks spurious test failures from unrelated pytest plugins installed on the system being autoloaded into the test run.
root_cause: pytest autoloads every installed plugin by default; on a freshly-enabled interpreter, half-ported third-party plugins crash or alter test behavior even though the package itself is fine.
fix_pattern: Declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest. The distutils-r1/epytest machinery then runs pytest with plugin autoloading disabled (PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD), loading only the plugins explicitly listed (none here). mgorny added this line to nearly every package while enabling py3.15.
rule: When enabling tests (or a new Python target) for a package whose test suite needs no pytest plugins, set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() so autoloaded system plugins cannot break the run.

problem: Test-only pytest-plugin dependency was declared as a manual test? conditional DEPEND block, which neither disables plugin autoloading nor is recognized by the epytest machinery.
root_cause: Older idiom predates EPYTEST_PLUGINS; manual DEPEND duplicates what the eclass can generate and leaves pytest autoload enabled, so untested plugins leak into the run on new interpreters.
fix_pattern: Replace the manual test? ( dev-python/pytest-mock[...] ) DEPEND block with EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-mock ) before distutils_enable_tests pytest; the eclass both adds the test BDEPEND and passes only that plugin with autoload disabled.
rule: When a python package needs specific pytest plugins for tests, list them in EPYTEST_PLUGINS instead of hand-writing test-conditional dependencies.

problem: Upstream repository moved to a different GitHub org (iterative/shtab -> tqdm/shtab), leaving HOMEPAGE, metadata.xml remote-id, and PYPI_VERIFY_REPO pointing at the redirect.
root_cause: GitHub org transfers keep old URLs working via redirects, so stale references go unnoticed until verification tooling (pypi provenance check via PYPI_VERIFY_REPO) or the redirect breaks.
fix_pattern: While touching the ebuild for the py3.15 bump, update all three places in one commit: PYPI_VERIFY_REPO, HOMEPAGE, and the <remote-id type="github"> in metadata.xml.
rule: When you notice an upstream repo has moved orgs, fix HOMEPAGE, metadata.xml remote-id, and any verify/nvchecker URLs together in the same commit rather than relying on redirects.

problem: Ebuild used the legacy DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit backend value.
root_cause: distutils-r1 split the flit value: flit-core is the correct backend for packages that only need the lightweight flit_core build backend; the plain flit value is being retired/repurposed.
fix_pattern: Change DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit to flit-core during a routine touch; no revbump needed since the generated BDEPEND (dev-python/flit-core) is effectively the same closure.
rule: When a package's pyproject build-backend is flit_core.buildapi, set DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit-core, not flit.

problem: Freethreaded CPython targets in PYTHON_COMPAT were stale (python3_{13,14}t) when adding py3.15.
root_cause: Freethreaded (t) targets form a second, independent window in PYTHON_COMPAT that must be rolled forward together with the regular one; 3.13t was being retired as 3.15t arrived.
fix_pattern: Roll both windows in one edit: PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} python3_{14..15}t ). Note the sibling commit roman-numerals only kept python3_14t, showing the t-window is advanced per-package based on what actually passes.
rule: When rolling PYTHON_COMPAT forward, remember freethreaded tNN targets are a separate window: drop retired tNN and add the new one only if the package tests pass on it.

Mechanical PYTHON_COMPAT window shift (drops pypy3_11); duplicate of the pattern captured at 545def9e.
Ebuild only allowed python3_11..3_14; needed py3.15 support as part of tree-wide new-interpreter rollout, and pytest could pick up random installed plugins during tests.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT is a static range that must be advanced for each new CPython; distutils-r1 pytest runs autoload every installed pytest plugin unless the ebuild opts out, which makes test results depend on the build host.
fix_pattern: Slide the PYTHON_COMPAT brace range forward at both ends in one edit (add newest, drop oldest no-longer-targeted impl) and declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest so plugin autoloading is disabled and only listed plugins load. No revbump: PYTHON_COMPAT changes are done in place per Gentoo python policy. Copyright year bumped in same edit.
rule: When enabling a new CPython target, change PYTHON_COMPAT as a sliding window (python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}) in place without revbump, and take the opportunity to pin test plugins with EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (or a list of actually-needed plugins) before distutils_enable_tests pytest.

problem: Rust/PyO3 extension (DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=maturin) cannot be built for python3.15 because the vendored PyO3 crate version predates 3.15 and refuses to compile against an unsupported interpreter.
root_cause: PyO3 hard-fails at build time when the target CPython is newer than the maximum version the crate release knows about; for abi3 (stable-ABI) builds this check is overly strict since the ABI is forward-compatible.
fix_pattern: Export PYO3_USE_ABI3_FORWARD_COMPATIBILITY=1 in the ebuild global scope so PyO3 accepts the newer interpreter for abi3 builds, instead of waiting for a new upstream release that bumps vendored pyo3 crates. Also dropped pypy3_11 from PYTHON_COMPAT in the same sweep.
rule: When a PyO3/maturin package fails to build on a new CPython with 'the configured Python interpreter version is newer than PyO3's maximum supported version', export PYO3_USE_ABI3_FORWARD_COMPATIBILITY=1 in the ebuild rather than pinning to older Python.

problem: Ebuild carried a 24-line manual python_test() with PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 and a PyPy-only EPYTEST_DESELECT list; needed py3.15 and cleanup.
root_cause: The deselect block existed solely for pypy3 (re vs regex module repr mismatches); once pypy3_11 is dropped from PYTHON_COMPAT the entire conditional and the hand-rolled python_test wrapper become dead code, and the modern eclass variable EPYTEST_PLUGINS subsumes the manual PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD export.
fix_pattern: Drop pypy3_11 from PYTHON_COMPAT, delete the whole python_test() function including its case-on-EPYTHON deselects, and replace the manual 'local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1' idiom with declarative EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest.
rule: When dropping a Python implementation from PYTHON_COMPAT, also delete every workaround conditional on it (EPYTEST_DESELECT case blocks, patches), and migrate manual PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD python_test wrappers to the declarative EPYTEST_PLUGINS variable.
snippet:
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=()
 distutils_enable_tests pytest
-python_test() {
-	local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1
-	case ${EPYTHON} in pypy3*) EPYTEST_DESELECT+=( ... ) ;; esac
-	epytest
-}

problem: Test suite broke on newer pytest/py3.15 because upstream pyproject.toml carries invalid pytest ini-option syntax (quoted string values for python_files/norecursedirs).
root_cause: Upstream wrote TOML-quoted values that pytest's ini parser mis-handles; newer pytest is stricter, turning a latent config bug into a test failure.
fix_pattern: sed-delete the offending config lines from pyproject.toml in src_prepare instead of carrying a patch file, letting pytest fall back to defaults; combined with EPYTEST_PLUGINS=().
rule: When upstream test-runner config in pyproject.toml has broken or over-strict-parsed syntax, strip just the offending keys with sed in src_prepare (|| die) rather than deselecting tests or pinning the test runner.
snippet:
src_prepare() {
	# fix broken pytest config syntax
	sed -e '/python_files = "/d' \
		-e '/norecursedirs = "/d' \
		-i pyproject.toml || die
	distutils-r1_src_prepare
}

py3.15 enablement also had to add DISTUTILS_EXT=1: the package builds a compiled (cffi) extension but the ebuild was treated as pure-Python by distutils-r1.
root_cause: distutils-r1 optimizes pure-Python packages (build flags not exported, wheel handling differs); a package that compiles C extensions must declare it so CFLAGS/LDFLAGS are respected and QA checks apply.
fix_pattern: Set DISTUTILS_EXT=1 above the inherit line while doing the PYTHON_COMPAT bump; also shifted PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{11..14} ) to python3_{12..15}.
rule: When a dev-python package compiles any C/C++ extension (setuptools ext, cffi, cython), set DISTUTILS_EXT=1 before inheriting distutils-r1.

problem: Ebuild declared DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry although the actual PEP 517 build backend is poetry-core; fixed during the py3.15 bump.
root_cause: pyproject.toml build-backend is poetry.core.masonry (provided by dev-python/poetry-core); the eclass now distinguishes the correct 'poetry-core' value from the legacy 'poetry' alias, which pulls the wrong/legacy dependency.
fix_pattern: Change DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry to poetry-core; in the same commit adopt the modern pytest-plugin test idiom (EPYTEST_PLUGINS + EPYTEST_PLUGIN_LOAD_VIA_ENV) and drop pypy3_11.
rule: Set DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 to the exact build backend named in pyproject.toml's [build-system]; for poetry projects that is poetry-core, not poetry.

problem: Standard mass py3.15 enablement showing the canonical PYTHON_COMPAT window shift.
root_cause: New CPython target (3.15) added tree-wide; simultaneously the oldest target (3.11) and stale pypy3_11 are dropped rather than letting the list grow unboundedly.
fix_pattern: Single-line change: PYTHON_COMPAT=( pypy3_11 python3_{11..14} ) -> ( python3_{12..15} ). Test suite must pass under the new interpreter before committing; no revbump needed since installed systems are unaffected until they re-emerge.
rule: When enabling a new Python target, shift the whole brace-range window (add newest, drop oldest/EOL-track and unused pypy variants) instead of only appending, and do it without a revbump.

problem: Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT window shift, same pattern as pytest-repeat.
py3.15 COMPAT shift plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS/EPYTEST_PLUGIN_LOAD_VIA_ENV addition; idiom covered by pytest-import-check record.

Testing a pytest-plugin package needed a hand-written python_test() exporting PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 and PYTEST_PLUGINS=<entry module> so the not-yet-installed plugin under test gets loaded.
root_cause: distutils-r1/epytest now disables pytest plugin autoloading by default; a plugin package's own test suite needs its plugin force-loaded via environment because the entry point is not registered at test time. The old boilerplate duplicated eclass logic and hardcoded the internal module path.
fix_pattern: Replace the manual python_test() with the eclass variables: EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( "${PN}" ) declares which installed plugin packages tests need, and EPYTEST_PLUGIN_LOAD_VIA_ENV=1 makes epytest load them via -p/PYTEST_PLUGINS env instead of entry-point autoload (needed when the plugin under test is only in BUILD_DIR). Related variant seen tree-wide: EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() for packages whose tests need no plugins at all.
rule: For pytest-plugin packages, drop custom PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD python_test() boilerplate and set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( "${PN}" ) plus EPYTEST_PLUGIN_LOAD_VIA_ENV=1 before distutils_enable_tests pytest.
snippet:
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( "${PN}" )
+EPYTEST_PLUGIN_LOAD_VIA_ENV=1
 EPYTEST_XDIST=1
 distutils_enable_tests pytest
-python_test() {
-	local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1
-	local -x PYTEST_PLUGINS=pytest_import_check.plugin
-	epytest
-}

Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT window shift.

py3.15 shift plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS idiom; covered by pytest-import-check record.
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT window shift.
New 1.5.1 ebuild (EAPI=9) must ship the upstream config template without clobbering /etc and without portage gzip-compressing the template users are told to copy.
root_cause: Upstream make install puts rsnapshot.conf.default into sysconfdir; installing under /etc risks CONFIG_PROTECT noise for a file that is only a template, and files under /usr/share/doc get compressed by default which breaks 'cp the template' instructions.
fix_pattern: Redirect the template into docs by overriding sysconfdir at install time (emake install sysconfdir="${EPREFIX}/usr/share/doc/${PF}"), exclude it from compression with docompress -x, patch the program's hardcoded /etc/ template path in src_prepare, and point users at it via pkg_postinst elog.
rule: When upstream installs a config template into /etc, divert it to /usr/share/doc/${PF} via a make-variable override and docompress -x it, then elog the copy instructions.

problem: PyTorch/caffe2 failed to compile for ROCm target gfx1100 (bug 978229): LLVM backend error when CUDA_KERNEL_ASSERT follows a 64-bit getelementptr in Loss.cu.
root_cause: LLVM insertSimulatedTrap bug (fixed upstream in llvm-project PR 174774 but not in released LLVM-22): abort()-based asserts emit llvm.trap(), which makes FinalizeISel skip blocks containing S_ADD_U64_PSEUDO, leaving it unexpanded on some gfx targets.
fix_pattern: Carry a files/ patch that, under #ifdef USE_ROCM, redefines the assert macro to call __assert_fail(expr, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__) instead of CUDA_KERNEL_ASSERT, preserving assertion semantics without emitting llvm.trap(). Patch header documents the LLVM PR and Gentoo bug. No revbump: the change only fixes a build failure, so no installed system has a wrong build.
rule: When a GPU/arch-specific build failure traces to a known compiler bug, add a minimal source-level workaround patch documenting the upstream compiler fix link, and skip the revbump since only failing builds are affected.
snippet:
#ifdef USE_ROCM
#define CHECK_INDEX_IN_CLASS(INDEX, N_CLASSES) \
  if (C10_UNLIKELY(!(INDEX < N_CLASSES))) {    \
    __assert_fail(#INDEX " < " #N_CLASSES,     \
        __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__); }
#else
/* original CUDA_KERNEL_ASSERT */
#endif

problem: py3.15 enablement also had to declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (empty) for a non-plugin package.
root_cause: With pytest plugin autoloading disabled by default in distutils-r1, every package using epytest must state which plugins its tests require; an explicit empty array documents 'none' and prevents the eclass fallback/autoload behavior.
fix_pattern: Add EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() immediately before distutils_enable_tests pytest for test suites with no pytest-plugin dependencies; use EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( name... ) when specific plugins are needed.
rule: Declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS explicitly in every distutils-r1 ebuild with pytest tests: an empty array for plugin-free suites, or the plugin package names the suite actually uses.

problem: Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT window shift.
Ebuild fetched the GitHub tag tarball and needed export SETUPTOOLS_SCM_PRETEND_VERSION=${PV} to build, because setuptools-scm cannot derive a version without git metadata.
root_cause: GitHub autogenerated archives contain no .git directory and no PKG-INFO, so setuptools-scm-versioned projects fail or misversion; the PyPI sdist ships PKG-INFO with the version baked in, needing no workaround.
fix_pattern: Replace the GitHub SRC_URI with 'inherit pypi' (keeping the release-only branch of the 9999 conditional), drop the .gh.tar.gz Manifest entry and the SETUPTOOLS_SCM_PRETEND_VERSION export; done together with the py3.15 COMPAT shift and EPYTEST_PLUGINS=().
rule: For setuptools-scm/hatch-vcs projects, prefer the PyPI sdist over GitHub tag tarballs so version detection works without SETUPTOOLS_SCM_PRETEND_VERSION hacks.

problem: py3.15 shift plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() in both release and 9999 ebuilds; idiom covered by pyte record.
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT window shift.
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT window shift.
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT window shift (package already had DISTUTILS_EXT=1).
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT window shift, drops pypy3_11.
Package not installable with newly-added Python 3.15 target; oldest target (3.11) still carried.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT lists an explicit interpreter range that must be manually slid forward each time Gentoo adds a new CPython target and retires the oldest one.
fix_pattern: Single-line window slide: PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{11..14} ) -> PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} ). New target added and retiring target dropped in the same edit, with NO revision bump: PYTHON_TARGETS-driven rebuilds handle re-emerging, so revbumping PYTHON_COMPAT changes is unnecessary churn.
rule: When a new CPython target lands, slide PYTHON_COMPAT (add new, drop the target slated for removal) in-place without a revbump; only revbump if installed files change for existing targets.

problem: Test dependency on a pytest plugin (hypothesis) was declared as a hand-written BDEPEND test? block, and pytest plugin autoloading was uncontrolled during the py3.15 enablement sweep.
root_cause: Before the EPYTEST_PLUGINS API in distutils-r1/python-utils, each ebuild manually wrote BDEPEND="test? ( dev-python/foo[${PYTHON_USEDEP}] )" for pytest plugins, and plugin autoloading pulled in whatever plugins happened to be installed, causing nondeterministic test runs.
fix_pattern: Replace the manual BDEPEND test block with EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( hypothesis ) set BEFORE distutils_enable_tests pytest. The eclass then generates the test dependency automatically and runs pytest with PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1, loading only the listed plugins (-p) explicitly.
rule: When an ebuild's tests need pytest plugins, declare them via EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( name... ) before distutils_enable_tests pytest instead of hand-writing test? BDEPEND, so plugin deps and autoload-disabling are handled by the eclass.

problem: Test suite ran with pytest plugin autoloading enabled, so unrelated installed plugins could break or slow the test phase.
root_cause: Without an explicit EPYTEST_PLUGINS declaration, the eclass historically left PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD unset and pytest loaded every plugin present on the system.
fix_pattern: Add EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (empty array) before distutils_enable_tests pytest to explicitly state 'no plugins needed'; the eclass then disables plugin autoloading for a hermetic test run.
rule: For pytest-based ebuilds whose tests need no plugins, set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest to opt into autoload-disabled, deterministic test runs.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT slide to python3_{12..15}, also dropping retired pypy3_11 target; same sweep pattern as pyscard.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT slide to python3_{12..15} dropping pypy3_11; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT slide to python3_{12..15} dropping pypy3_11; no other change.
Ebuild used DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit, a deprecated backend alias, and ran tests with plugin autoloading.
root_cause: distutils-r1 renamed the flit backend value: the actual PEP 517 build backend is flit_core.buildapi provided by dev-python/flit-core, so the eclass value 'flit' was superseded by the more accurate 'flit-core'.
fix_pattern: Change DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit to DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit-core during a routine touch (here the py3.15 sweep), plus add EPYTEST_PLUGINS=().
rule: When touching an ebuild that sets DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit, rename the value to flit-core to match the current eclass-supported backend name.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT slide to python3_{12..15}; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT slide to python3_{12..15} dropping pypy3_11; no other change.
PYTHON_COMPAT slide had to preserve the freethreaded python3_14t target, which is not covered by the brace range.
root_cause: Freethreaded (no-GIL) CPython targets like python3_14t are separate PYTHON_COMPAT entries; python3_{12..15} expands only to the GIL builds, so a naive range rewrite would silently drop freethreading support.
fix_pattern: Keep the t-suffixed target listed explicitly next to the slid range: PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} python3_14t ). Any automated compat-slide tooling must treat python3_XYt entries as independent tokens.
rule: When sliding PYTHON_COMPAT ranges, preserve freethreaded python3_XYt entries as separate list items — they are not part of the brace expansion.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT slide to python3_{12..15} dropping pypy3_11; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT slide plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() addition; same idioms as pyscard/pyrqlite.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT slide to python3_{12..15}; no other change.

Routine PYTHON_COMPAT slide plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() addition; same idioms as pyscard/pyrqlite.

Routine PYTHON_COMPAT slide to python3_{12..15}; no other change.

Ebuild carried a 20-line python_test() whose only purpose was pypy3-specific EPYTEST_DESELECT entries and a manual PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1, kept alive after the pypy3 target it served was being dropped.
root_cause: Implementation-conditional test workarounds (case ${EPYTHON} in pypy3*) ...) outlive the PYTHON_COMPAT entry they were written for; nobody garbage-collects them when the interpreter is removed, leaving dead code and a redundant custom python_test.
fix_pattern: When dropping pypy3_11 from PYTHON_COMPAT, delete the entire custom python_test() including its EPYTEST_DESELECT case-block, and replace the manual 'local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1; epytest' with the declarative EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest.
rule: When removing an interpreter from PYTHON_COMPAT, grep the ebuild for ${EPYTHON}/impl-conditional blocks that only serve that interpreter and delete them; a python_test() that only sets PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD is superseded by EPYTEST_PLUGINS=().
snippet:
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=()
 distutils_enable_tests pytest
-python_test() {
-	case ${EPYTHON} in pypy3*) EPYTEST_DESELECT+=( ... );; esac
-	local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1
-	epytest
-}

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT slide to python3_{12..15} dropping pypy3_11; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT slide to python3_{12..15} dropping pypy3_11; PYTHON_REQ_USE untouched.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT slide to python3_{12..15} dropping pypy3_11; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT slide to python3_{12..15}; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT slide plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() addition; same idioms as pyscard/pyrqlite.
Package not installable/testable on newly-added python3_15 target; PYTHON_COMPAT stuck at python3_{11..14} and pytest ran with plugin autoloading, pulling in undeclared plugins from the build host.
root_cause: New CPython target added to the tree-wide supported set while python3.11 (and pypy3_11) were dropped from it; additionally, distutils-r1 pytest runs without an explicit EPYTEST_PLUGINS declaration autoload whatever plugins happen to be installed, hiding missing test deps and causing host-dependent failures.
fix_pattern: Mass py3.15 enablement idiom: rewrite PYTHON_COMPAT from python3_{11..14} (plus any pypy3_11) to python3_{12..15} in one edit, and add EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() immediately before distutils_enable_tests pytest. Explicitly setting EPYTEST_PLUGINS (even empty) makes python-utils-r1 set PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1, so tests only see plugins you declare; non-empty values also auto-add the plugin BDEPENDs and -p load args.
rule: When enabling a new CPython target, bump both ends of the PYTHON_COMPAT brace range to the current supported window (dropping EOL targets in the same commit), and set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (or the actual plugin list) before distutils_enable_tests pytest so plugin autoloading is disabled and test deps are explicit.

Ebuild carried a hand-written python_test() whose only purpose was to export PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 before calling epytest; this boilerplate was duplicated across live and release ebuilds.
root_cause: Before EPYTEST_PLUGINS existed in python-utils-r1, disabling pytest plugin autoloading required manually overriding python_test and exporting the env var; the eclass now handles this declaratively (explicit EPYTEST_PLUGINS, or EAPI 9+, disables autoloading automatically).
fix_pattern: Delete the custom python_test() { local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1; epytest; } override and instead declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest, letting the eclass-generated python_test handle autoload disabling (and -p args / BDEPENDs for any listed plugins).
rule: When an ebuild overrides python_test only to set PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 and call epytest, replace the whole override with EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( needed plugins... ) (empty array if none) before distutils_enable_tests pytest.

problem: Ebuild declared DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry, a value that no longer names a real build-backend package.
root_cause: distutils-r1 keeps 'poetry' only as a legacy compatibility alias that it silently remaps to poetry-core (the actual PEP517 backend dev-python/poetry-core); the alias is slated for removal and hides which backend dep is really used.
fix_pattern: Rename the value to the canonical backend during a routine touch of the ebuild: DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry-core. The eclass then adds dev-python/poetry-core as BDEPEND and selects poetry.core.masonry.api directly instead of via the alias case.
rule: Use the canonical PEP517 backend name (poetry-core, flit-core, flit-scm, jupyter-packaging) in DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 rather than legacy aliases (poetry, flit, flit_scm, jupyter), fixing aliases opportunistically when editing an ebuild.

problem: Routine mass py3.15 enablement: PYTHON_COMPAT bump plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (idiom covered by pycdio lesson in this batch).
Routine mass py3.15 enablement: PYTHON_COMPAT bump plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (idiom covered by pycdio lesson in this batch).

Ebuild still carried legacy setuptools namespace-package workarounds: an RDEPEND blocker !dev-python/namespace-paste and a python_compile override deleting *.pth files from BUILD_DIR.
root_cause: Old setuptools-style namespace packages installed shared *.pth loader files that collided between packages sharing a namespace, requiring split namespace-* packages, blockers, and .pth-deletion hacks; modern releases use PEP 420 native namespace packages, and once the namespace-* package is gone from the tree the workarounds are dead weight.
fix_pattern: During the PYTHON_COMPAT bump, delete the !dev-python/namespace-paste blocker (whole RDEPEND) and the python_compile override that ran find "${BUILD_DIR}" -name '*.pth' -delete, reverting to plain eclass phases.
rule: When touching a dev-python ebuild, treat !dev-python/namespace-* blockers and *.pth-deleting phase overrides as removable legacy once the package uses PEP 420 native namespaces and the namespace-* package no longer exists.

problem: Routine mass py3.15 enablement: PYTHON_COMPAT python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no content beyond the batch pattern.
Package not installable/testable on new CPython 3.15; test phase silently pulls in whatever pytest plugins happen to be installed.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT listed the old interpreter window (pypy3_11 python3_{11..14}); pre-EPYTEST_PLUGINS ebuilds let pytest autoload all system plugins, causing nondeterministic test failures on new interpreters.
fix_pattern: Shift the whole PYTHON_COMPAT window instead of appending: python3_{11..14}+pypy3_11 -> python3_{12..15} (oldest target and pypy dropped in the same commit). Declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest so the eclass disables pytest plugin autoloading (test suite needs no plugins).
rule: When enabling a new CPython target in a distutils-r1 ebuild, shift the PYTHON_COMPAT range (drop the oldest / stale pypy targets) rather than just appending, and declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS (empty array if none) before distutils_enable_tests to make plugin loading deterministic.

problem: Test-only pytest-plugin dependencies were maintained as a hand-written BDEPEND test? block, easy to get out of sync with what the test suite actually loads.
root_cause: Before the EPYTEST_PLUGINS eclass API, each ebuild duplicated plugin deps in BDEPEND and pytest autoloaded any installed plugin, so declared deps and loaded plugins could diverge.
fix_pattern: Replace the manual BDEPEND="test? ( dev-python/hypothesis[...] dev-python/pytest-mock[...] )" block with EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( hypothesis pytest-mock ) set before distutils_enable_tests pytest; distutils-r1 then generates the test deps and runs pytest with only those plugins enabled (autoload disabled).
rule: When a distutils-r1 ebuild lists pytest plugins in a test? BDEPEND block, migrate them to EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( plugin... ) and delete the manual block; the eclass generates the dependencies and pins exactly those plugins at test time.
snippet:
-BDEPEND="
-	test? (
-		dev-python/hypothesis[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]
-		dev-python/pytest-mock[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]
-	)
-"
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( hypothesis pytest-mock )
 distutils_enable_tests pytest

problem: python_test() manually exported PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1, and freethreading targets were listed as python3_{13,14}t alongside the old window.
root_cause: Manual plugin-autoload suppression predates the EPYTEST_PLUGINS eclass mechanism; freethreading (t) targets need the same window-shift discipline as regular ones.
fix_pattern: Set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() at the top and remove 'local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1' from python_test(); the eclass now handles it. Freethreading targets shifted python3_{13,14}t -> python3_14t in the same PYTHON_COMPAT edit.
rule: When an ebuild sets PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD manually in python_test, replace it with a top-level EPYTEST_PLUGINS declaration; and treat freethreading python3_*t targets with the same add-new/drop-old window policy as regular targets.
snippet:
-PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{11..14} python3_{13,14}t pypy3_11 )
+PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} python3_14t )
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=()
 distutils_enable_tests pytest
...
-	local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1

problem: Ebuild still used the deprecated DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit backend name.
root_cause: distutils-r1 renamed the backend value: pure flit was split so 'flit-core' names the actual lightweight build backend dependency; the old 'flit' value pulls the wrong (heavier/deprecated) dep.
fix_pattern: Rename DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit to DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit-core during the routine compat bump; no other change needed since upstream pyproject.toml already uses flit_core.buildapi.
rule: When touching a distutils-r1 ebuild that declares DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit, rename it to flit-core to match the current eclass backend naming.

problem: GitHub URLs pointed at the old upstream org (eclipse/paho.mqtt.python) after upstream moved to eclipse-paho, so remote-id, HOMEPAGE and SRC_URI referenced a redirect.
root_cause: Upstream renamed its GitHub organization; GitHub redirects mask the breakage until the redirect target is reused or removed, and version-check tooling keyed on remote-id watches the wrong repo.
fix_pattern: In the same maintenance commit, update metadata.xml <remote-id type="github">, HOMEPAGE, and SRC_URI to the new org (eclipse-paho/paho.mqtt.python); keep the -> ${MY_P}.gh.tar.gz rename so the distfile name is unchanged and no re-fetch churn occurs.
rule: When upstream moves GitHub orgs, update remote-id, HOMEPAGE, and SRC_URI together instead of relying on GitHub redirects, keeping the arrow-renamed distfile name stable.

problem: Ebuild referenced the pre-rename GitHub org (jcrist/msgspec) and a stale personal homepage; SRC_URI fetched via redirect.
root_cause: Upstream project moved from a personal account to its own GitHub org (msgspec/msgspec); package also uses GitHub tarballs instead of PyPI sdist because the sdist ships no tests.
fix_pattern: Update remote-id, HOMEPAGE and SRC_URI to the new org in one commit; keep the '# No tests in sdist' comment documenting why a .gh.tar.gz GitHub archive is used instead of pypi.eclass sdist.
rule: When a PyPI sdist omits the test suite, fetch the GitHub tag tarball (-> ${P}.gh.tar.gz) and leave a comment stating why; when upstream changes GitHub owner, fix remote-id/HOMEPAGE/SRC_URI together.
snippet:
-<remote-id type="github">jcrist/msgspec</remote-id>
+<remote-id type="github">msgspec/msgspec</remote-id>
 # No tests in sdist
+SRC_URI="
+	https://github.com/msgspec/msgspec/archive/refs/tags/${PV}.tar.gz
+		-> ${P}.gh.tar.gz
+"

Package not installable on newly-added CPython 3.15 target; PYTHON_COMPAT still pinned at python3_{11..14}.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT is a static per-ebuild list; when the python-r1 eclass gains a new interpreter target every ebuild must opt in explicitly, and the oldest target ages out of the supported window at the same time.
fix_pattern: Shift the brace range instead of appending: PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{11..14} ) -> PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} ). The new target is added and the oldest (3.11, leaving the support window) dropped in one edit; copyright year bumped since the file was touched. No revbump needed because installed-file content for existing impls is unchanged.
rule: When a new CPython target lands in the eclass, enable it by sliding the python3_{A..B} brace range forward (add newest, drop the target leaving support) after confirming the test suite passes on the new interpreter; a PYTHON_COMPAT-only change needs no revision bump.

problem: Same PYTHON_COMPAT range shift python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no new content beyond the matrix-common lesson.
Same PYTHON_COMPAT range shift to python3_{12..15}; duplicate of the sweep pattern.
PYTHON_COMPAT range shift on a maturin/cargo (rust extension) package; identical one-line idiom, rust side untouched.
Enabling a new Python target risks test failures from unrelated system-installed pytest plugins being autoloaded into the package's test run (plugins often lag new interpreter support).
root_cause: distutils_enable_tests pytest historically ran pytest with plugin autoloading on, so every pytest plugin installed for that interpreter injects itself into the suite; on a brand-new interpreter many plugins are broken or unported, causing false test failures unrelated to the package.
fix_pattern: Set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before calling distutils_enable_tests pytest. The empty array tells the eclass to disable pytest plugin autoloading (PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD) and load only explicitly listed plugins; the package's own tests need none. Also dropped pypy3_11 while shifting PYTHON_COMPAT to python3_{12..15}.
rule: When enabling tests via distutils_enable_tests pytest, declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS with exactly the plugins the suite needs (empty array if none) so system pytest plugins cannot break the test phase, especially on newly-enabled interpreters.

problem: Same PYTHON_COMPAT range shift to python3_{12..15}; duplicate of the sweep pattern.
PYTHON_COMPAT shift that also drops pypy3_11; duplicate of the sweep + pypy-drop pattern.
PYTHON_COMPAT shift that also drops pypy3_11; duplicate of the sweep pattern.
PYTHON_COMPAT shift plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() added on a DISTUTILS_EXT=1 package; same hermetic-pytest idiom as looseversion.
Same PYTHON_COMPAT range shift to python3_{12..15}; duplicate of the sweep pattern.
PYTHON_COMPAT append-only bump python3_{12..14} -> python3_{12..15} (lower bound already at 3.12); duplicate of the sweep pattern.
Same PYTHON_COMPAT range shift to python3_{12..15}; duplicate of the sweep pattern.
PYTHON_COMPAT used the compact freethreading form python3_{13..14}{,t}, which cannot express 3.15 support because the freethreaded (t) variant does not exist for every CPython version in the range.
root_cause: The {,t} brace suffix multiplies every version in the range by both GIL and freethreaded variants, but Gentoo only ships freethreaded targets for the versions where the eclass defines them (3.14+ here, not 3.13), so extending the range forces splitting normal and t targets into separate expansions.
fix_pattern: Split the declaration into one range for regular interpreters and a separate range for the freethreaded variants: PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{13..15} python3_{14..15}t ).
rule: When a package supports freethreaded CPython, list the t targets as their own brace range covering only the versions that actually have a freethreaded target, instead of gluing {,t} onto a range where some versions lack it.

problem: Enabling py3.15 while the ebuild still advertised pypy3_11, an interpreter target being phased out; keeping it would block or falsify the compat claim.
root_cause: pypy3 targets track a fixed CPython feature level (pypy3_11 ~ 3.11) and age out on the python team's schedule independent of the CPython range; carrying them through a compat sweep without re-testing leaves stale claims.
fix_pattern: Drop pypy3_11 from PYTHON_COMPAT in the same edit that shifts the CPython range: PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{11..14} pypy3_11 ) -> ( python3_{12..15} ). Done uniformly across the sweep (mock, parameterized, kiwisolver, kconfiglib, littleutils, looseversion same day).
rule: When shifting PYTHON_COMPAT for a new CPython, re-evaluate any pypy3_* entries and drop ones the python team is retiring rather than mechanically preserving them.

Same PYTHON_COMPAT range shift to python3_{12..15}; duplicate of the sweep pattern.
PYTHON_COMPAT shift dropping pypy3_11 on a C++-extension (DISTUTILS_EXT=1) package; duplicate of the sweep + pypy-drop pattern.
Same PYTHON_COMPAT range shift to python3_{12..15}; duplicate of the sweep pattern.
PYTHON_COMPAT shift dropping pypy3_11 (PYTHON_REQ_USE=ncurses untouched); duplicate of the sweep pattern.
PYTHON_COMPAT shift on a C-extension package; a '# TODO: freethreading' comment marks that t targets are deliberately deferred rather than forgotten.
Same PYTHON_COMPAT range shift on a DISTUTILS_EXT=1 package; duplicate of the sweep pattern.
Same PYTHON_COMPAT range shift to python3_{12..15}; duplicate of the sweep pattern.
PYTHON_COMPAT shift dropping pypy3_11; duplicate of the sweep + pypy-drop pattern.
PYTHON_COMPAT shift dropping pypy3_11 plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest; same hermetic-pytest idiom as looseversion.

Package not installable/testable on newly-released CPython 3.15; PYTHON_COMPAT capped at python3_14.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT is an explicit whitelist; every new CPython minor release requires touching each ebuild. Gentoo handles this as a mass sweep that simultaneously drops the oldest still-listed target.
fix_pattern: Shift the PYTHON_COMPAT brace-range window rather than only appending: python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}. No revbump is done for PYTHON_COMPAT changes (they only affect new installs / re-emerges under the new target). Copyright year is refreshed in the same edit. Change is committed one-package-per-commit with subject '<pkg>: Enable py3.15' only after tests pass under the new interpreter.
rule: When enabling a new CPython target, shift the whole PYTHON_COMPAT window (add newest, drop oldest no-longer-needed) in one edit, run the test suite under the new interpreter, and do not revbump.

problem: Ebuild carried a manual 'test? ( dev-python/hypothesis[${PYTHON_USEDEP}] )' BDEPEND block plus relied on pytest plugin autoloading, which the distutils-r1/pytest tooling now disables by default.
root_cause: distutils-r1 gained the EPYTEST_PLUGINS mechanism: naming plugins in the array both generates the test dependency and explicitly loads the plugin under PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD, making hand-written test? deps redundant and autoload-dependent tests fragile.
fix_pattern: Delete the manual test? BDEPEND for the pytest plugin and declare it via EPYTEST_PLUGINS before distutils_enable_tests; the eclass adds the dep and -p loads it.
rule: When a python package's tests need pytest plugins (hypothesis, pytest-mock, ...), declare them in EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( ... ) before distutils_enable_tests pytest instead of writing test? ( ... ) BDEPEND blocks.
snippet:
BDEPEND="
 	dev-python/setuptools-scm[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]
-	test? (
-		dev-python/hypothesis[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]
-	)
 "
 
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( hypothesis )
 distutils_enable_tests pytest

problem: Rust/maturin python extension (PyO3-based) cannot be enabled for a brand-new CPython version: the bundled PyO3 crate refuses to build against an interpreter newer than it officially supports.
root_cause: PyO3 hard-fails at build time on unknown CPython versions unless told to use the abi3 stable ABI in forward-compatibility mode; upstream crate bumps lag CPython releases.
fix_pattern: Export PYO3_USE_ABI3_FORWARD_COMPATIBILITY=1 in the ebuild global scope so the PyO3 build treats the new interpreter as abi3-compatible, allowing py3.15 in PYTHON_COMPAT without waiting for a PyO3 bump. Done alongside the normal PYTHON_COMPAT window shift; test suite (epytest + cargo_src_test) still validates the result.
rule: When a PyO3/maturin package fails to build on a new CPython with a 'version not supported' error from pyo3-build-config, export PYO3_USE_ABI3_FORWARD_COMPATIBILITY=1 instead of pinning to older Python.

problem: Tests needed a src_prepare sed to strip '--cov ... --cov-report' from setup.cfg addopts, a has_version-conditional deselect, and a manual PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD export -- all brittle boilerplate.
root_cause: Upstream pytest config injects coverage addopts (pulling in pytest-cov) and some tests break with newer dep APIs; the old workarounds predate eclass support for plugin control and inline option overrides.
fix_pattern: Replace the setup.cfg sed with 'epytest -o addopts=' (command-line override of the ini option, no source patching); replace the has_version conditional with an unconditional EPYTEST_DESELECT entry carrying the upstream issue URL; replace manual PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD with EPYTEST_PLUGINS=().
rule: When upstream pytest config forces coverage or other unwanted addopts, neutralize them at invocation with 'epytest -o addopts=' rather than sed-ing setup.cfg/pyproject.toml in src_prepare.

problem: python_test carried a manual 'local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1' export that is now redundant boilerplate.
root_cause: Once EPYTEST_PLUGINS is declared (even empty), the distutils-r1/pytest eclass machinery itself disables pytest plugin autoloading, so the hand-rolled export duplicates eclass behavior.
fix_pattern: Add 'EPYTEST_PLUGINS=()' above distutils_enable_tests pytest and delete the manual PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD line from python_test.
rule: When migrating an ebuild to EPYTEST_PLUGINS, remove any manual PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD exports -- declaring the array (empty or not) already handles autoload suppression.
snippet:
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=()
 distutils_enable_tests pytest
 ...
 	rm -rf immutables || die
-	local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1
 	epytest

problem: Package lists freethreading (no-GIL) CPython targets that also need updating when the support window moves.
root_cause: Freethreading targets are separate PYTHON_COMPAT entries (python3_XYt) with their own version window; forgetting them leaves stale/no-longer-provided targets listed.
fix_pattern: Shift the t-variant window in the same edit as the main window: python3_{11..14} python3_{13..14}t pypy3_11 -> python3_{12..15} python3_{14..15}t (pypy dropped in the same sweep when unsupported).
rule: When shifting the PYTHON_COMPAT window, shift any python3_*t freethreading entries and re-evaluate pypy3 entries in the same commit.
