Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT window shift; no package-specific content.
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT window shift; no package-specific content.
Package not yet enabled for newly-added python3_15 target; oldest target py3.11 and pypy3_11 still listed
root_cause: New CPython interpreter added to the tree; PYTHON_COMPAT is a manually curated window that must be moved per package after testing
fix_pattern: Representative commit of a tree-wide sweep: shift the brace range in one edit, adding the new target and dropping the oldest CPython plus pypy3_11 at the same time, after running the test suite under the new interpreter
rule: When a new CPython target lands, bump PYTHON_COMPAT as a sliding window (python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}) in one edit rather than only appending the new target, and only after tests pass under it

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window shift to python3_{12..15}, dropping pypy3_11 (same sweep pattern)
Routine py3.15 window shift; also adds EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests (pattern documented under freetype-py e9eb1c2)
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window shift to python3_{12..15} for a DISTUTILS_EXT=1 package
Routine py3.15 window shift plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() addition (same sweep pattern)
Ebuild used deprecated DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit value and carried a pypy3*-only EPYTEST_DESELECT block after pypy3_11 was being dropped from PYTHON_COMPAT
root_cause: PEP517 backend values track the actual build-backend package (flit_core -> value flit-core); interpreter-specific test workarounds become dead code once that interpreter leaves PYTHON_COMPAT
fix_pattern: In the same target-bump commit: rename DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit to flit-core and delete the entire python_test() with its case ${EPYTHON} in pypy3*) EPYTEST_DESELECT block, falling back to plain distutils_enable_tests pytest
rule: When dropping an interpreter from PYTHON_COMPAT, grep the ebuild for EPYTHON case blocks and interpreter-conditional deselects and remove the now-dead ones; keep DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 matching the real backend name (flit-core, not flit)
snippet:
-DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit
+DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit-core
# and drop dead block:
-case ${EPYTHON} in
-  pypy3*) EPYTEST_DESELECT+=( ... ) ;;
-esac

problem: Custom python_test() exporting CI=1 just to suppress hypothesis health checks; hypothesis dependency implicit via autoloaded plugins
root_cause: Before EPYTEST_PLUGINS existed, packages hacked around pytest plugin autoloading and hypothesis health checks with ad-hoc env vars in python_test
fix_pattern: Replace the whole custom python_test() with a declarative EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( hypothesis ) line above distutils_enable_tests pytest; the eclass loads only the named plugins and applies its standard hypothesis handling
rule: When a python_test() exists only to tweak pytest plugin behavior (CI=1, PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD), replace it with EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( <needed plugins> ) and let distutils-r1 manage the run
snippet:
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( hypothesis )
 distutils_enable_tests pytest
-python_test() {
-	# suppresses hypothesis health checks
-	local -x CI=1
-	epytest
-}

problem: Routine py3.15 window shift plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() addition (same sweep pattern)
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window shift to python3_{12..15} (same sweep pattern)
Routine py3.15 window shift plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() addition (same sweep pattern)
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window shift to python3_{12..15} (same sweep pattern)
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window shift for a Cython extension package (same sweep pattern)
HOMEPAGE and metadata.xml github remote-id still pointed at the old personal repo (agoose77/...) after upstream moved to the jupyterlab org
root_cause: Upstream repos migrate between GitHub accounts; stale remote-id silently breaks version-check tooling and points users at an archived fork
fix_pattern: Piggyback the URL refresh on a routine maintenance commit: update HOMEPAGE and the <remote-id type="github"> entry in metadata.xml together so they stay consistent
rule: When touching an ebuild for routine maintenance, verify the GitHub org/repo still matches upstream and update HOMEPAGE and metadata.xml remote-id in the same commit (also fix any nvchecker/watch config keyed on the old repo)

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window shift to python3_{12..15} (same sweep pattern)
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window shift to python3_{12..15} (same sweep pattern)
PYTHON_COMPAT mixed freethreading targets python3_{13..14}t with the regular range; the freethreaded list was not moved when the main window shifted
root_cause: Freethreaded (nogil) CPython builds are separate PYTHON_COMPAT targets with a t suffix and their own supported window, which must be curated in tandem with the regular targets
fix_pattern: Trim the freethreading list to the currently supported freethreaded target while shifting the main range: python3_{13..14}t python3_{11..14} became python3_14t python3_{12..15}
rule: If an ebuild lists python3_*t freethreading targets, re-evaluate that list every time you shift the main PYTHON_COMPAT window; the t targets do not follow the brace range automatically

problem: py3.15 got enabled on the old gast-0.6.0 ebuild as well as 0.7.0 during a scripted sweep; a follow-up commit had to revert 0.6.0 back to python3_{12..14}
root_cause: Mechanical PYTHON_COMPAT edits applied per package directory hit every ebuild version, but only the latest version was actually tested (or intended) for the new interpreter; older releases may lack upstream support
fix_pattern: Follow-up revert commit narrowing only the old version: gast-0.6.0.ebuild python3_{12..15} -> python3_{12..14}, leaving 0.7.0 with py3.15
rule: When scripting PYTHON_COMPAT bumps, restrict the edit to the specific ebuild version you tested (usually the newest); older ebuilds keeping the old target set is correct, and an accidental enable warrants an immediate revert commit, not a shrug

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window shift to python3_{12..15} (same sweep pattern)
Routine window shift that also dropped stale pypy3 and pypy3_11 targets and py3.10/3.11 in one edit
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window shift to python3_{12..15} (same sweep pattern)
Sweep commit that enabled py3.15 on both gast-0.6.0 and 0.7.0; the 0.6.0 half was accidental and reverted in 958a764 (lesson recorded there)
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window shift to python3_{12..15} (same sweep pattern)
One doctest (ftfy/formatting.py monospaced_width) fails under the new interpreter/wcwidth combo, blocking the py3.15 enable
root_cause: A single upstream-known test failure should not hold back enabling a new Python target for the whole package
fix_pattern: Enable the target anyway and add an EPYTEST_DESELECT entry for the failing doctest, with a comment linking the upstream issue so the deselect can be dropped when fixed upstream
rule: When one known-broken test blocks a new Python target, deselect it via EPYTEST_DESELECT with an upstream-issue URL comment instead of skipping the target or the whole test suite

problem: python_test() manually exported PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 to keep unrelated installed pytest plugins from breaking the test run
root_cause: distutils-r1 gained the declarative EPYTEST_PLUGINS mechanism: naming the plugins tests need (empty array = none) makes the eclass disable autoloading and load only those, superseding the per-ebuild env-var hack
fix_pattern: Set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest and delete the manual PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 line from python_test(); keep python_test only if it still selects a test path
rule: In distutils-r1 ebuilds, declare pytest plugin needs with EPYTEST_PLUGINS (empty array when none) instead of exporting PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 in python_test

problem: Routine append of python3_15 to an already py3.12-based range (same sweep pattern)
Package not installable on newly-added Python 3.15 target; PYTHON_COMPAT also still carried EOL-bound targets (python3_11, pypy3_11).
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT is a static list; each new CPython target requires an explicit edit, and Gentoo periodically drops old/pypy targets from the supported set.
fix_pattern: Shift the whole PYTHON_COMPAT window in one edit: add the new target AND drop targets leaving the tree (py3.11, pypy3_11), rather than only appending; bump the copyright year at the same time. Done tree-wide as 'Enable py3.15' sweep after testing.
rule: When enabling a new Python target, rewrite PYTHON_COMPAT as a contiguous window (e.g. python3_{12..15}) that also drops targets being retired, instead of appending to a stale list.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window shift to python3_{12..15}, dropping pypy3_11; no other change.
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT bump, applied to both the release and the -9999 live ebuild in the same commit.
Ebuild fetched a GitHub tag tarball (${P}.gh.tar.gz) and had to export SETUPTOOLS_SCM_PRETEND_VERSION because the archive lacks VCS metadata for setuptools-scm.
root_cause: GitHub archives carry no version metadata, forcing PRETEND_VERSION hacks, and provide no cryptographic link to the released PyPI artifact; the pypi.eclass now supports PyPI provenance attestations that verify the sdist against the source repo.
fix_pattern: During the py3.15 bump, drop the custom SRC_URI and the SETUPTOOLS_SCM_PRETEND_VERSION export, inherit pypi, and set PYPI_VERIFY_REPO=<upstream github URL>; the Manifest then gains a ${P}.tar.gz.provenance entry that verifies sdist provenance. The sdist's PKG-INFO makes the PRETEND_VERSION hack unnecessary.
rule: When a python package is on PyPI with provenance attestations, prefer the pypi sdist with PYPI_VERIFY_REPO over GitHub archives — it removes SETUPTOOLS_SCM_PRETEND_VERSION hacks and adds supply-chain verification.
snippet:
DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=hatchling
PYPI_VERIFY_REPO=https://github.com/gmr/flatdict
PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} )
inherit distutils-r1 pypi
# (drop SRC_URI override and SETUPTOOLS_SCM_PRETEND_VERSION export)

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window shift to python3_{12..15}, dropping pypy3_11.
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT bump with copyright year update.
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT bump, dropping pypy3_11.
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT bump in both release and live ebuild.
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT bump; package already used PYPI_VERIFY_REPO.
Test suite could pick up arbitrary pytest plugins installed on the system, making test results nondeterministic across machines.
root_cause: pytest autoloads every installed plugin by default; distutils-r1 added EPYTEST_PLUGINS to control autoloading, and ebuilds must declare which plugins the tests actually need.
fix_pattern: Declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (empty array = no plugins needed) before distutils_enable_tests pytest, so the eclass disables plugin autoloading and runs the suite with a clean pytest.
rule: For distutils-r1 packages using pytest, always set EPYTEST_PLUGINS explicitly — an empty array when tests need no plugins — so plugin autoloading is disabled and tests are reproducible.

problem: Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT bump, dropping pypy3_11.
Tests fail because pyproject.toml carries a broken/pinned pytest 'addopts' line (already fixed upstream but not released); package also gained freethreading targets python3_{14..15}t.
root_cause: Upstream pytest configuration in pyproject.toml injects addopts (e.g. coverage or plugin flags) that break in a clean, plugin-less test environment.
fix_pattern: In src_prepare, sed-delete the addopts line from pyproject.toml, with a comment linking the upstream commit that fixes it, then call distutils-r1_src_prepare; do not add the plugin dependencies just to satisfy addopts.
rule: When upstream pyproject.toml addopts break the test run, strip the addopts line in src_prepare (citing the upstream fix) instead of pulling in coverage/plugin deps.

problem: Routine py3.15 bump that also adds EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest (same idiom as faust-cchardet in this batch).
Routine one-line py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT extension.
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT window shift.
Routine py3.15 extension; lower bound stays python3_13 because the package backports a module removed from stdlib in 3.13.
New advanced-branch release 11.15.1 broke a test: pnmtojpeg-scans compared program output against a stored .ok expectation file, and a change to pm_message() altered the message line-wrapping.
root_cause: Upstream test harness stores literal expected-output (.ok) files; any cosmetic change in a program's diagnostics makes the comparison fail even though behavior is correct. Also, upstream only tarballs its 'super stable' flavor, so the maintainer self-hosts advanced-branch tarballs.
fix_pattern: Ship a tiny patch against the expected-output file itself (test/pnmtojpeg-scans.ok) updating it to the new message format, rather than skipping the test. Package parallel upstream branches with subslots (SLOT="0/stable" vs SLOT="0/advanced") and fetch the untarballed branch from a dev-hosted distfile (https://distfiles.gentoo.org/pub/dev/<dev>/...). Conditional test-list pruning via sed on test/Test-Order for USE-disabled features and musl-broken locale tests.
rule: When a version bump changes diagnostic output and tests diff against stored .ok files, patch the expectation files to match instead of disabling the tests; use subslots to co-package divergent upstream branches.
snippet:
PATCHES=( "${FILESDIR}"/netpbm-11.15.1-fix-test-pm_message.patch )
# patch edits test/pnmtojpeg-scans.ok to the new pm_message wrapping
SLOT="0/advanced"  # stable branch lives in a parallel ebuild as 0/stable

problem: Routine one-line py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT extension.
Package advertised python3_11/3_12 support, but its dependency dev-python/pycurl no longer works with Python 3.12, leaving uninstallable PYTHON_COMPAT combinations.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT must be the intersection of the package's own support and every dependency's PYTHON_COMPAT; when a dep drops a target, revdeps silently become broken for that target.
fix_pattern: Shrink the revdep's PYTHON_COMPAT to match the dependency (python3_{11..14} -> python3_{13..14}) in a dedicated 'Remove py<3.13' commit explaining which dep forced it.
rule: When a dependency drops a Python implementation, immediately trim that implementation from all reverse dependencies' PYTHON_COMPAT rather than waiting for resolver failures.
snippet:
-PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{11..14} )
+PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{13..14} )  # pycurl no longer supports 3.12

problem: Routine one-line py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT extension.
Stale HOMEPAGE URL updated to upstream's current page; metadata-only fix.

Static libraries (.a) installed by the package are broken for consumers when built with LTO: they contain compiler-version-specific GIMPLE bytecode instead of machine code, so linking from a different toolchain fails.
root_cause: With -flto, object files archived into .a hold LTO IR only; installed static archives must contain real code (fat objects) and have the bytecode stripped to be usable by arbitrary consumers.
fix_pattern: Migrate to the dot-a.eclass: inherit dot-a; call 'use static-libs && lto-guarantee-fat' in (multilib_)src_configure (forces -ffat-lto-objects), and call 'strip-lto-bytecode' in the install phase so shipped .a files contain only machine code.
rule: Any ebuild that installs static libraries should inherit dot-a, call lto-guarantee-fat before configure when static-libs is enabled, and strip-lto-bytecode after install, so LTO-built .a files stay consumable.

problem: Users needed a Qt6-capable development snapshot (bug 972728 etc.) but upstream had no tagged release; the ebuild must build a specific git commit reproducibly and still report a sane version.
root_cause: Untagged development snapshots have no release tarball and the build system derives its version from git metadata that GitHub archives lack.
fix_pattern: Pin the commit: version the ebuild as ${PN}-2.8.0_preYYYYMMDD, set GIT_HASH=<full sha>, SRC_URI=github archive/${GIT_HASH}.tar.gz -> ${P}.tar.gz, S=${WORKDIR}/${PN}-${GIT_HASH}, and document the commit/author/date in a header comment. Write the version stamp in src_prepare ('echo ${PV} > .version') so the build does not need git. Unbundle vendored libs by rm -r src/thirdparty/zxcvbn, and apply a compat patch conditionally with has_version '<dev-libs/zxcvbn-c-2.6'. Disable build-system ccache autodetection (-DWITH_CCACHE=OFF) since Gentoo manages ccache via FEATURES.
rule: To package an untagged upstream snapshot, use a _preYYYYMMDD version fetching the pinned commit archive with S set to ${PN}-${GIT_HASH}, and stamp the version into the source tree so nothing invokes git at build time.
snippet:
GIT_HASH="ce28e2afb7a59ab5c4b64512b7119eec4a9f2008"
SRC_URI="https://github.com/keepassxreboot/keepassxc/archive/${GIT_HASH}.tar.gz -> ${P}.tar.gz"
S="${WORKDIR}/${PN}-${GIT_HASH}"
src_prepare() {
	echo "${PV}" > .version || die
	rm -r ./src/thirdparty/zxcvbn || die  # unbundle, bug 958062
	cmake_src_prepare
}

problem: Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT window shift with copyright year update.
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT window shift with copyright year update.
tar-1.35 fails to build against >=sys-apps/acl-2.4.0: conflicting declarations for acl_get_file_at/acl_set_file_at/acl_delete_def_file_at.
root_cause: libacl 2.4.0 added its own acl_*_at() prototypes to <acl/libacl.h>/acl headers; tar had defined private static wrappers with the same acl_-prefixed names, so the new library headers now collide with tar's internal symbols (a library owns its prefix namespace).
fix_pattern: Add a new revision tar-1.35-r1 carrying a backported upstream patch (files/tar-1.35-acl-2.4.0.patch, from the bug-tar mailing list) that renames the internal wrappers to tar_acl_*_at, added via PATCHES=( "${FILESDIR}"/${PN}-1.35-acl-2.4.0.patch ). The old -r0 ebuild is left in place; the fix ships as a revbump so binary hosts rebuild. The consumer fix references the same bug (978280) as the library version bumps, keeping the whole transition traceable.
rule: When a library bump introduces new public symbols that collide with a consumer's identically-prefixed internal functions, backport/write a rename patch (prefix the consumer's wrappers with its own name, e.g. tar_acl_*) and ship it as a revbump filed under the same bug as the library bump.
snippet:
PATCHES=(
	"${FILESDIR}"/${PN}-1.35-acl-2.4.0.patch
)
# patch: -static acl_t acl_get_file_at (...)
#        +static acl_t tar_acl_get_file_at (...)

problem: New major-ish release acl-2.4.0 needed packaging; upstream now provides GPG-signed tarballs and no longer links against libattr.
root_cause: Upstream changes across a version bump: release signing became available (sec-keys/openpgp-keys-acl), and the libattr runtime dependency was dropped upstream, so the old RDEPEND was stale.
fix_pattern: Retrofit verify-sig in the non-live branch: set VERIFY_SIG_OPENPGP_KEY_PATH, inherit verify-sig, add the .sig to SRC_URI inside a verify-sig? ( ) block, and BDEPEND on the sec-keys package. Because the else-branch assigns BDEPEND, later unconditional additions use BDEPEND+=. Simultaneously delete the obsolete RDEPEND on >=sys-apps/attr-2.4.47-r1[${MULTILIB_USEDEP}]. The 9999 live ebuild is updated in the same commit so it stays a copy of the newest release ebuild.
rule: On a version bump, re-audit dependencies against upstream's current link/requirements (drop what upstream dropped), and when upstream starts signing releases add verify-sig (key path + .sig in SRC_URI under verify-sig? + sec-keys BDEPEND); keep the 9999 ebuild synced in the same commit.
snippet:
VERIFY_SIG_OPENPGP_KEY_PATH="/usr/share/openpgp-keys/acl.asc"
inherit verify-sig
SRC_URI="
	mirror://nongnu/${PN}/${P}.tar.xz
	verify-sig? ( mirror://nongnu/${PN}/${P}.tar.xz.sig )
"
BDEPEND="verify-sig? ( sec-keys/openpgp-keys-acl )"
BDEPEND+=" nls? ( sys-devel/gettext )"

problem: attr-2.6.0 bump: needed verify-sig support, and the carried musl-1.2.5 patch had to be dealt with.
root_cause: attr and acl share the same upstream maintainer/signing key, so no new sec-keys package is needed; the attr-2.5.2-r1-musl-1.2.5.patch was merged upstream, making the carried patch obsolete in 2.6.0/9999.
fix_pattern: Reuse the sibling project's key: VERIFY_SIG_OPENPGP_KEY_PATH=/usr/share/openpgp-keys/acl.asc with BDEPEND on sec-keys/openpgp-keys-acl even though the package is attr. Drop the upstreamed musl patch from PATCHES on the bump. Ebuild also shows the LTO+static-libs idiom: inherit dot-a, 'use static-libs && lto-guarantee-fat' in configure and strip-lto-bytecode in multilib_src_install_all so installed .a files contain real objects, not GIMP bytecode.
rule: At every version bump, check whether carried FILESDIR patches were merged upstream and drop them; when a related project family shares one signing key, point VERIFY_SIG_OPENPGP_KEY_PATH at the existing sec-keys package instead of creating a duplicate.

problem: Enable python3.15 on a C-extension package (DISTUTILS_EXT=1).
root_cause: Routine Gentoo python-target rollout: new interpreter added to the eclass-supported set while the oldest target and stale PyPy version are retired.
fix_pattern: PYTHON_COMPAT is treated as a sliding window, not append-only: python3_{11..14} pypy3_11 becomes python3_{12..15} in one edit — the floor is raised and dead pypy3_11 dropped at the same time the new target is added. Enabling implies the maintainer verified build/tests on 3.15 (for DISTUTILS_EXT packages, that the extension compiles).
rule: When enabling a new CPython target, slide the whole PYTHON_COMPAT window (raise the floor to the oldest still-supported target and drop retired pypy entries) instead of only appending the new version.

problem: py3.15 enablement also had to modernize the build backend and test setup: DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit and implicit pytest plugin autoloading.
root_cause: distutils-r1 gained a distinct flit-core backend value (pulling only dev-python/flit-core instead of full flit), and EPYTEST_PLUGINS was introduced so the eclass disables pytest plugin autoloading unless plugins are declared; old ebuilds predate both.
fix_pattern: Change DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit to flit-core (package only needs the build backend, not the flit tool) and set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest so tests run with PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD and no plugin deps.
rule: When touching an old dev-python ebuild, switch DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit to flit-core if only the backend is used, and declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS (empty array when tests need no plugins) to opt into deterministic pytest runs.

problem: py3.15 enablement on a package that also supports freethreaded CPython targets.
root_cause: Freethreaded (no-GIL) CPython builds are separate PYTHON_COMPAT targets with a 't' suffix and must be bumped in lockstep with the regular targets.
fix_pattern: PYTHON_COMPAT lists the freethreading targets as their own brace range next to the normal ones: python3_{11..14} python3_{13,14}t became python3_{12..15} python3_{14..15}t. Note the comment '# PYTHON_COMPAT used only for testing' — for wheel-only/ensurepip packages the targets gate tests, not the installed payload.
rule: If a package is enabled for freethreaded CPython, bump the python3_XYt targets together with the regular python3_XY window on every target rollout.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window bump to python3_{12..15}; no generalizable content beyond the sliding-window idiom already recorded.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window bump (drop pypy3_11, add 3.15) on a standalone-PEP517 package; nothing new.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window bump to python3_{12..15}; no other change.
Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT append of python3_15; no other change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window bump plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (idiom already recorded under entrypoints).
Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT append of python3_15.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window bump plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (idiom already recorded).
Routine target bump combining already-recorded idioms: flit->flit-core, freethreading t-targets slide, EPYTEST_PLUGINS=().
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window bump on a hatchling package.
Enabling py3.15 required adding an explicit test dependency on dev-python/pkg-resources or the test suite fails to import pkg_resources.
root_cause: setuptools >= 81 stopped shipping pkg_resources; Gentoo split it out as dev-python/pkg-resources, so anything importing pkg_resources (here, only the tests) must depend on it explicitly instead of getting it for free via setuptools.
fix_pattern: Add BDEPEND="test? ( dev-python/pkg-resources[${PYTHON_USEDEP}] )" alongside the PYTHON_COMPAT bump; runtime code that imports pkg_resources would need it in RDEPEND instead.
rule: If a package (or its test suite) imports pkg_resources, depend on dev-python/pkg-resources explicitly — modern setuptools no longer provides it.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window bump on both the meta (pkg-resources-0) and split-from-setuptools (81.0.0) ebuilds.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window bump; flit backend left as-is.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window bump; PYTHON_REQ_USE="xml(+)" unchanged.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window bump.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window bump plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (idiom already recorded).
Ebuild carried a hand-written python_test() whose only purpose was exporting PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 before epytest.
root_cause: distutils-r1/python-utils-r1 gained native support for controlling pytest plugin autoloading via the EPYTEST_PLUGINS variable, making the manual override boilerplate.
fix_pattern: Delete the python_test() override entirely and declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest; the eclass now sets PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD and injects only the listed plugins as test deps. Same commit also migrated flit -> flit-core.
rule: Replace python_test() bodies that only set PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 with a declarative EPYTEST_PLUGINS array (empty when no plugins are needed) and let the eclass generate python_test.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window bump.
Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT append of python3_15 on a meson-python extension package.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window bump plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (idiom already recorded).
Package not installable on the newest CPython (3.15) while still advertising support for a target being retired (3.11).
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT is a static list; each new interpreter release requires opting every package in, and Gentoo policy pairs adding the new target with dropping the oldest one in the same commit.
fix_pattern: Slide the brace range in PYTHON_COMPAT one step in a single commit: python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}; bump the copyright end-year to the current year in the same hunk. No revbump needed since it does not change installed files for existing targets.
rule: When enabling a new CPython target, slide the whole PYTHON_COMPAT brace range (add newest, drop the oldest deprecated target) in one commit rather than only appending, and update the copyright year.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT range slide python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}; same idiom as cwcwidth commit.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT range slide plus copyright year bump; no new idiom.
PYTHON_COMPAT still listed pypy3_11, a PyPy target being retired, alongside the CPython range needing the 3.15 bump.
root_cause: PyPy targets are versioned like CPython ones (pypy3_11) and go EOL on their own schedule; leaving stale PyPy targets in PYTHON_COMPAT blocks tree-wide removal of the old interpreter.
fix_pattern: Drop the stale pypy3_11 entry in the same commit that slides the CPython range: PYTHON_COMPAT=( pypy3_11 python3_{11..14} ) -> ( python3_{12..15} ). PyPy support is only re-added when a matching new pypy target exists and the package is a PyPy dependency.
rule: When bumping PYTHON_COMPAT for a new CPython, also remove versioned PyPy targets that correspond to retired interpreters instead of leaving them behind.

problem: Test suites of simple packages break or slow down when unrelated globally-installed pytest plugins autoload, especially on a brand-new CPython where many plugins are not yet ported.
root_cause: pytest autoloads every installed plugin via entry points; on a new target (py3.15) plugins that are not yet compatible get imported anyway and crash collection, even though the package needs none of them.
fix_pattern: Set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (empty array) before calling distutils_enable_tests pytest. The distutils-r1/python-utils machinery then runs pytest with plugin autoloading disabled and only the listed plugins (here: none) as test deps. Must be set BEFORE distutils_enable_tests since that call consumes it to generate BDEPEND.
rule: When a python package's tests need no pytest plugins, declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() above distutils_enable_tests pytest to disable plugin autoloading and keep new-interpreter enablement from being blocked by unported plugins.

problem: Same bulk pattern: PYTHON_COMPAT slide, drop pypy3_11, add EPYTEST_PLUGINS=(); idioms already captured on crashtest/crc32c commits.
Same bulk pattern: PYTHON_COMPAT slide, drop pypy3_11, add EPYTEST_PLUGINS=(); no new idiom.
Same bulk pattern: PYTHON_COMPAT slide plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=(); no new idiom.
Freethreading (no-GIL) targets python3_{13,14}t lagged behind the regular CPython range when 3.15 was enabled.
root_cause: Freethreading builds are separate PYTHON_COMPAT targets with a t suffix and must be maintained in lockstep with the regular targets they mirror; forgetting them silently drops no-GIL support.
fix_pattern: Slide the t-suffixed range together with the main range in one commit: python3_{13,14}t -> python3_{14..15}t next to python3_{12..15}. Also added EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest.
rule: When a package lists freethreading targets (python3_Nt), slide that range in the same commit as the regular CPython range so no-GIL support tracks the same versions.

problem: Same bulk pattern: PYTHON_COMPAT slide plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=(); no new idiom.
Routine one-line PYTHON_COMPAT range slide; no new idiom.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT slide with pypy3_11 removal; no new idiom.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT slide with pypy3_11 removal; no new idiom.
Ebuild carried a hand-rolled 'local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1' inside python_test, and used the deprecated DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit backend value.
root_cause: Before EPYTEST_PLUGINS existed, disabling pytest plugin autoloading required exporting PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD manually per-ebuild; and 'flit' as PEP517 value pulls the full dev-python/flit tool when upstream's build-backend is flit_core.buildapi, which only needs the lightweight dev-python/flit-core.
fix_pattern: Replace the manual env export in python_test with a global EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() declared before distutils_enable_tests pytest (the eclass now handles autoload disabling), and change DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit to flit-core so BDEPEND matches the actual build backend. Custom python_test body keeps only its real customization (epytest --doctest-modules).
rule: Migrate ad-hoc PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 lines in python_test to the eclass-level EPYTEST_PLUGINS variable, and use DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit-core (not flit) when pyproject.toml declares flit_core.buildapi.
snippet:
-DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit
+DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit-core
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=()
 distutils_enable_tests pytest
 python_test() {
-	local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1
 	epytest --doctest-modules
 }

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT slide with pypy3_11 removal; no new idiom.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT slide with pypy3_11 removal; no new idiom.
python_test invoked pytest indirectly through script(1) to fake a TTY and still carried a manual PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 export.
root_cause: Same pre-EPYTEST_PLUGINS era workaround as colour; the eclass-level variable now sets up the plugin-autoload environment even when python_test runs pytest through a wrapper command instead of epytest.
fix_pattern: Add EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest and delete the manual 'local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1' from the custom python_test, keeping only the package-specific part (script -eqc "${EPYTHON} -m pytest" to provide a TTY).
rule: The EPYTEST_PLUGINS migration applies even to ebuilds with fully custom python_test bodies: declare it globally and drop the per-phase env export.
snippet:
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=()
 distutils_enable_tests pytest
 python_test() {
-	local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1
 	script -eqc "${EPYTHON} -m pytest -vv -s" /dev/null

problem: Routine slide of both regular and freethreading ranges (python3_{13,14}t -> python3_{14..15}t); idiom already captured on configupdater commit.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT slide plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=(); no new idiom.
Enabling py3.15 made pytest collection fail with an ImportError on pkg_resources coming from files under the docs/ directory.
root_cause: pytest collects any matching files in the source tree, including doc scripts/examples; those imported pkg_resources, which is no longer available on the new interpreter (setuptools dropped it), so collection itself crashed even though the real test suite was fine.
fix_pattern: Exclude the offending directory from collection with EPYTEST_IGNORE=( docs ) and a short comment explaining why, instead of patching the docs or restricting tests. Combined with EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() in the same commit.
rule: When a new Python version breaks pytest collection in non-test directories (docs/, examples/), add the directory to EPYTEST_IGNORE with a comment rather than disabling the whole test suite.
snippet:
+EPYTEST_PLUGINS=()
 distutils_enable_tests pytest
+
+# trigger pkg_resources ImportError
+EPYTEST_IGNORE=( docs )

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT slide with pypy3_11 removal; no new idiom.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT slide plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=(); no new idiom.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT slide plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=(); no new idiom.
DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 was set to the deprecated 'flit' value although the package only needs the flit_core build backend.
root_cause: 'flit' as the PEP517 value adds the full dev-python/flit tool to BDEPEND; packages whose pyproject.toml build-backend is flit_core.buildapi only need dev-python/flit-core, so 'flit' over-declares deps and is being phased out.
fix_pattern: Change DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit to flit-core during a routine touch of the ebuild (here the py3.15 enablement), alongside the PYTHON_COMPAT slide and EPYTEST_PLUGINS=().
rule: Use DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit-core whenever upstream's build-backend is flit_core.buildapi; reserve 'flit' for the rare package that genuinely builds with the flit CLI.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT slide with pypy3_11 removal; no new idiom.
Ebuild lacked py3.15 support and its pytest run relied on implicit plugin autoloading.
root_cause: New CPython target added tree-wide; distutils-r1's epytest now wants plugins declared explicitly, and python3_11/pypy3_11 are being dropped in the same wave.
fix_pattern: Shift the PYTHON_COMPAT window to python3_{12..15} (dropping python3_11 and pypy3_11 in the same commit) and declare EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest so no pytest plugins are autoloaded.
rule: When enabling a new CPython target, shift the whole PYTHON_COMPAT window (drop EOL CPython and pypy impls in the same commit) and set EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() explicitly when the test suite needs no pytest plugins.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump python3_{11..14} -> python3_{12..15}; no new idiom beyond the wave pattern.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; one-line change, no new idiom.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} dropping pypy3_11; no new idiom.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; no new idiom.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() addition; same idiom as the chameleon/astor records.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; no new idiom.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; no new idiom.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; no new idiom.
Ebuild still used the deprecated DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry value.
root_cause: distutils-r1 renamed the backend value to poetry-core to match the actual build-backend package (dev-python/poetry-core); the bare 'poetry' name is deprecated.
fix_pattern: Replace DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry with DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry-core while doing the routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump.
rule: Use DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=poetry-core (never 'poetry') for poetry-backed packages, and fold such eclass-value modernizations into routine bumps.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; no new idiom.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; no new idiom.
HOMEPAGE and metadata.xml github remote-id pointed at a moved upstream repo (aio-libs/async-timeout was transferred to aio-libs-abandoned).
root_cause: Upstream transferred the repository to an 'abandoned' GitHub org; a stale remote-id misleads users and breaks version-check tooling keyed on the repo path.
fix_pattern: Update HOMEPAGE and the <remote-id type="github"> entry in metadata.xml to the new org while doing the routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump.
rule: During any bump, verify the upstream repo URL still resolves to the canonical location and update HOMEPAGE plus metadata remote-id when the project has moved or been archived.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; no new idiom.
A custom python_test() existed only to set 'local -x PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1' and a local EPYTEST_IGNORE, pure boilerplate around epytest.
root_cause: distutils-r1/epytest gained first-class support: EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() disables pytest plugin autoloading declaratively and EPYTEST_IGNORE works as a global array, so the phase override is redundant.
fix_pattern: Delete python_test() entirely; declare global EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest and a global EPYTEST_IGNORE=( ... ) array.
rule: When python_test() only sets PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD/EPYTEST_* locals and calls epytest, replace it with global EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() and EPYTEST_IGNORE/EPYTEST_DESELECT arrays.

Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15}; no new idiom.

platformio enforces its own pip-style version pins at runtime (starlette <0.53, uvicorn <0.41, click <8.4), so it breaks against distro-current starlette 1.x, newer uvicorn, and click 8.4/8.5 even though the code could work with them.
root_cause: Upstream vendors a runtime dependency checker (platformio/dependencies.py) with hard upper bounds, and two real API breaks existed (starlette on_startup removed in favor of lifespan; click's private launch helper) that needed actual code changes.
fix_pattern: Revbump -r2: backport the two upstream compat commits as FILESDIR patches (starlette lifespan context manager, click.launch), sed the remaining pins in dependencies.py inside python_prepare_all, and widen the ebuild RDEPEND ranges to match (<starlette-2, <uvicorn-1, <click-9).
rule: When a Python app enforces its own runtime dependency version pins, patch or sed the in-source pin list to match the ranges the ebuild actually depends on, backporting upstream compat commits for genuine API breaks, and revbump.

problem: New bleachbit release phones home for update checks by default, and its unittest suite fails in sandboxed builds: network-dependent tests, a hardcoded 'strings' binary name, /proc permission assumptions, host-locale and host-filesystem dependence, and missing $HOME files.
root_cause: Upstream defaults and tests assume an online desktop system with unprefixed GNU binutils on PATH; Gentoo builds run network-sandboxed, possibly with cross/prefixed toolchains and arbitrary user locales/filesystems.
fix_pattern: Patch OPTION_DEFAULTS check_online_updates to False (distro policy); in python_prepare_all under 'use test' rm whole network-only test files and disable individual flaky methods by underscore-renaming them with sed "s/test_name(self)/_&/"; sed the literal 'strings' invocation to "$(tc-getSTRINGS)"; run the suite under virtx with LC_ALL=C.UTF-8.
rule: For desktop apps, patch out phone-home defaults and neutralize environment-dependent tests by rm-ing network test files and underscore-renaming flaky test methods (sed 's/test_x(self)/_&/'), replacing hardcoded toolchain binary names with tc-get* equivalents.
snippet:
sed -e "s:'strings':'$(tc-getSTRINGS)':g" -i tests/TestWipePath.py || die
rm tests/Test{Chaff,GuiChaff,Network,Update}.py || die
sed -e "s/test_notify(self)/_&/" -i tests/TestGUI.py || die

problem: Upstream publishes detached PGP signatures but the ebuild fetched the tarball unauthenticated.
root_cause: verify-sig support was never wired up; note the signatures live on a different host (sourceforge detached_signatures dir) than the tarball itself (download.bleachbit.org).
fix_pattern: Set VERIFY_SIG_OPENPGP_KEY_PATH before inherit, add verify-sig to inherit, add a USE-conditional .sig entry to SRC_URI (it may point at a different mirror than the tarball), and add verify-sig? ( sec-keys/openpgp-keys-<pn> ) to BDEPEND.
rule: When upstream ships detached signatures, add verify-sig support: key-path variable + inherit verify-sig + verify-sig? ( url.sig ) in SRC_URI + the sec-keys package in BDEPEND; the .sig URL need not share the tarball's host.
snippet:
VERIFY_SIG_OPENPGP_KEY_PATH=/usr/share/openpgp-keys/${PN}.asc
inherit verify-sig
SRC_URI="
	https://download.bleachbit.org/${P}.tar.bz2
	verify-sig? ( https://download.sourceforge.net/project/${PN}/${PN}/${PV}/detached_signatures/${P}.tar.bz2.sig )
"
BDEPEND="verify-sig? ( sec-keys/openpgp-keys-bleachbit )"

problem: Test suite failed on hosts whose system locale uses accented names or uncommon codes (bug 977413): the language-code validation regex and locale-derived expectations broke under real-world locales.
root_cause: Tests validated locale codes with a regex assuming @modifier before .codeset (wrong POSIX order, e.g. be_BY.utf8@latin fails) and derived expectations from the builder's live locale environment instead of a fixed one.
fix_pattern: Backport the upstream test fixes as a FILESDIR patch (regex reordered to codeset-before-modifier, skip reserved qaa-qtz alias range) and export LC_ALL=C.UTF-8 in python_test so tests run under a known-valid hardcoded locale.
rule: When a test suite behaves differently depending on the build host's locale, pin LC_ALL=C.UTF-8 in the test phase and backport the upstream test fix rather than deleting the tests.

problem: cmake test suite loaded the system-installed mlt plugins/data instead of the just-built ones, so tests failed on first install or tested the wrong code (bug 978139); an xml test also ran with USE=-xml.
root_cause: The library discovers its plugin/data directories via env vars (MLT_REPOSITORY, MLT_DATA, ...) that default to the install prefix, and the test binaries resolve libmlt via the default linker path; nothing pointed them at BUILD_DIR.
fix_pattern: Add src_test() that exports the package's own runtime-lookup env vars into the build tree output dir and prepends BUILD_DIR paths to LD_LIBRARY_PATH and PATH (mirroring upstream's setenv script), then call cmake_src_test; skip USE-conditional tests via the CMAKE_SKIP_TESTS array.
rule: When a package's tests can load the installed copy instead of the just-built one, replicate upstream's test env-setup in src_test (local -x vars into BUILD_DIR plus LD_LIBRARY_PATH/PATH), and use CMAKE_SKIP_TESTS to drop tests for disabled USE flags.
snippet:
src_test() {
	local -x MLT_REPOSITORY="${BUILD_DIR}/out/lib/mlt"
	local -x MLT_DATA="${BUILD_DIR}/out/share/mlt"
	local -x LD_LIBRARY_PATH="${BUILD_DIR}/out/lib:${LD_LIBRARY_PATH}"
	local -x PATH="${BUILD_DIR}/out/bin:${PATH}"
	local CMAKE_SKIP_TESTS=()
	use !xml && CMAKE_SKIP_TESTS+=( QtTest:xml )
	cmake_src_test
}

problem: Removal of an obsolete src_prepare sed (assertEquals->assertEqual test fixup annotated with the upstream PR URL) once no longer needed; routine workaround-cleanup with no generalizable content beyond 'annotate seds with upstream links so they can be dropped later'.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT rotation to python3_{12..15} (dropping py3.11/pypy3_11) during the mass py3.15 enablement pass; no unique content.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT rotation to python3_{12..15} for py3.15 enablement; no unique content.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT rotation to python3_{12..15} in release and 9999 ebuilds; no unique content.
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT rotation plus adding EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() (idiom covered by the annotated-types record in this batch).
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT rotation plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=(); no unique content.

Ebuild carried a hand-written 'test? ( dev-python/pytest-mock )' BDEPEND block and a custom python_test with pypy-only EPYTEST_DESELECT, making the mass py3.15/PYTHON_COMPAT rotation noisier than needed.
root_cause: Before distutils-r1 grew EPYTEST_PLUGINS, pytest plugin deps had to be spelled out manually in BDEPEND, and pytest ran with whatever plugins were installed (nondeterministic); dropping pypy3_11 from PYTHON_COMPAT also obsoleted the pypy-specific deselects.
fix_pattern: Replace the manual test BDEPEND with the declarative EPYTEST_PLUGINS array before distutils_enable_tests pytest: EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-mock ) generates the test deps and runs pytest with only the listed plugins enabled; EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() means 'no plugins', isolating tests from stray installed plugins. Delete impl-specific python_test/EPYTEST_DESELECT blocks when the impl leaves PYTHON_COMPAT.
rule: In distutils-r1 ebuilds, declare pytest plugin needs via EPYTEST_PLUGINS (empty array for none) instead of hand-rolled test? BDEPEND, and prune impl-conditional test deselects whenever that impl is dropped from PYTHON_COMPAT.
snippet:
PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} )
...
EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( pytest-mock )
distutils_enable_tests pytest

Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT rotation plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=(); no unique content.
Routine py3.15 rotation with cosmetic modernization (dual GitHub+PyPI HOMEPAGE, EPYTEST_PLUGINS=()); no unique content.
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT rotation plus EPYTEST_PLUGINS=(); no unique content.
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT rotation; no unique content.
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT rotation; no unique content.
Test suite ran serially, slowing the py3.15 enablement pass for a package whose tests are xdist-safe.
root_cause: distutils-r1 runs pytest without xdist unless the ebuild opts in, since parallelism breaks some suites.
fix_pattern: Set EPYTEST_XDIST=1 next to EPYTEST_PLUGINS before distutils_enable_tests pytest; the eclass then adds pytest-xdist to test deps and runs epytest in parallel.
rule: When a Python package's test suite is known parallel-safe, opt in with EPYTEST_XDIST=1 rather than leaving long suites serial (and never enable it blindly for suites with shared state).

problem: Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT rotation; no unique content.
Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT rotation; no unique content.
freecad failed to build against qt 6.11 (bug 977409): its CMake helper copied Qt${N}${COMPONENT}_LIBRARIES/_INCLUDE_DIRS variables that newer Qt CMake config files no longer populate.
root_cause: Qt's CMake packages moved fully to imported targets (Qt6::Component); legacy old-style result variables disappear in newer minor releases, breaking consumers that alias them.
fix_pattern: Backport the upstream PR as a FILESDIR patch whose header lines carry the upstream PR URL and the Gentoo bug URL; the patch prefers the imported target when it exists (set libs to Qt${N}::${COMPONENT}, read INTERFACE_INCLUDE_DIRECTORIES via get_target_property) and falls back to the old variables otherwise.
rule: When a dependency's minor update (e.g. a new Qt release) breaks a consumer's configure because legacy CMake result variables vanished, look for the already-merged upstream fix, ship it as a patch headed by the PR and bug URLs, and prefer imported targets over old-style variables.

problem: New freecad release moved AddonManager into a git submodule, so the GitHub release tarball ships an empty src/Mod/AddonManager directory and the build lacks that component; 9999 only needed EGIT_SUBMODULES.
root_cause: GitHub autogenerated tarballs never include submodule contents; only git-r3 checkouts can fetch them via EGIT_SUBMODULES.
fix_pattern: Pin the submodule revision in a variable, fetch it as a second SRC_URI commit tarball, then in src_prepare rmdir the empty placeholder directory (rmdir, so it dies loudly if upstream starts shipping content) and mv the unpacked tree into place; live ebuild instead sets EGIT_SUBMODULES. The bump also raised the dep floor to the ondselsolver snapshot the new code needs and replaced a hardcoded '(gentoo)' version tag with the branding eclass's BRANDING_OS_NAME.
rule: When upstream converts a source subtree to a git submodule, add a commit-pinned GitHub tarball to SRC_URI and swap it into the empty directory during src_prepare for release ebuilds, keeping EGIT_SUBMODULES for the 9999 ebuild.
snippet:
ADDON_MANAGER_COMMIT="937b6877239dc78ef59eeefe8099e5f14243eda1"
SRC_URI+=" https://github.com/FreeCAD/AddonManager/archive/${ADDON_MANAGER_COMMIT}.tar.gz -> AddonManager.tar.gz"
# src_prepare:
rmdir "${S}/src/Mod/AddonManager" || die
mv "${WORKDIR}"/AddonManager-${ADDON_MANAGER_COMMIT} "${S}"/src/Mod/AddonManager || die

problem: Installation failed when multiple PYTHON_TARGETS were enabled (bug 978173): the mv/rm/newinitd fixups in python_install ran once per Python implementation, so the second run found the files already moved/removed and died.
root_cause: distutils-r1 runs python_install once per impl (and potentially in parallel); one-shot ${ED} filesystem fixups are not idempotent, so they belong in the once-only phase.
fix_pattern: Move the ${ED} layout fixups from python_install into python_install_all, which distutils-r1 runs exactly once after all impls are installed; keep the call to distutils-r1_python_install_all.
rule: In distutils-r1 ebuilds, never put non-idempotent ${ED} file moves/removals in python_install; do them in python_install_all so they execute once regardless of how many Python impls are enabled.

problem: kubo failed to build on systems with an older dev-lang/go (bug 974260): go.mod demands a newer toolchain but nothing in the ebuild expressed that.
root_cause: The go-module eclass does not translate the go/toolchain directives in go.mod into a dependency, so portage happily builds with any installed Go and the compile aborts with a 'requires go >= X.Y' error.
fix_pattern: Add an explicit BDEPEND on the minimum Go toolchain matching each version's go.mod (>=dev-lang/go-1.25.0 for 0.40.1, >=dev-lang/go-1.26.2 for 0.41.0).
rule: For every go-module bump, read the go/toolchain line in go.mod and mirror it as BDEPEND ">=dev-lang/go-<ver>" since the eclass will not enforce it for you.

problem: Routine py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT rotation; no unique content.
Routine one-character py3.15 PYTHON_COMPAT extension; no unique content.

New apscheduler version's test suite tries to talk to external servers (mongodb, redis, rethinkdb, zookeeper, etcd) and has timing-flaky scheduler tests, which would fail in the network-sandboxed ebuild environment.
root_cause: Upstream CI provisions live datastore services; the fixtures only importorskip the client library, so with the library installed the tests attempt real connections, and scheduler timing tests are inherently flaky.
fix_pattern: Patch each external-server fixture to 'pytest.skip("requires external server")' at the fixture level (one line per fixture kills every dependent test, cleaner than long EPYTEST_DESELECT lists), keep EPYTEST_DESELECT for stragglers, set EPYTEST_RERUNS=10 for flaky timing tests, and declare needed plugins via EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( anyio pytest-timeout ).
rule: For suites needing live services, inject pytest.skip into the shared fixtures via a patch instead of deselecting dozens of tests individually, and use EPYTEST_RERUNS for timing-flaky tests rather than deleting them.
snippet:
@pytest.fixture
 def redisjobstore():
+    pytest.skip("requires external server")
     redis = pytest.importorskip(...)
# ebuild:
EPYTEST_RERUNS=10
EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( anyio pytest-timeout )

problem: Bumping gdbus-codegen (a Python tool carved out of the glib tarball) needed a ~19KB patch against glib 2.88.2, too big for FILESDIR, and the package must build only a subdirectory of a much larger upstream tarball.
root_cause: Devmanual caps FILESDIR patches at ~20KB, and gdbus-codegen has no standalone upstream release; it lives inside glib's tarball with meson-only build glue.
fix_pattern: Host the oversized patch as an .xz distfile on dev space and append it to SRC_URI (SRC_URI+=" https://distfiles.gentoo.org/pub/dev/sam%40gentoo.org/.../glib-2.88.2-const-whoops.patch.xz"), eapply it from ${WORKDIR}; set S to the subdirectory inside the big tarball (S=${WORKDIR}/glib-${PV}/gio/gdbus-2.0/codegen), substitute the @VERSION@/@PYTHON@ meson templates with sed, ship a local setup.py, and skip tests with an einfo note that the parent package (dev-libs/glib) tests this code.
rule: When a patch exceeds the FILESDIR size limit, distribute it as a compressed SRC_URI distfile and eapply it from WORKDIR; when packaging a subproject of a bigger tarball, point S at the subdirectory and reproduce the tiny build glue the parent build system would have generated.
snippet:
SRC_URI+=" https://distfiles.gentoo.org/pub/dev/sam%40gentoo.org/dev-libs/glib/glib-2.88.2-const-whoops.patch.xz"
S="${WORKDIR}/glib-${PV}/gio/gdbus-2.0/codegen"
# in python_prepare_all:
cd "${WORKDIR}"/glib-${PV} || die
eapply "${WORKDIR}/glib-2.88.2-const-whoops.patch"

problem: Routine weekly gcc snapshot addition (new patchset tarball PATCH_VER=9, toolchain.eclass boilerplate); no generalizable content beyond toolchain workflow.

New glibmm version miscompiled by trunk GCC due to invalid G_GNUC_CONST attributes; the fix patch modifies m4 code-generator templates whose output is shipped pregenerated in the tarball, so patching the templates alone changes nothing at build time.
root_cause: Release tarballs ship files generated from tools/m4 templates; a patch to the generator only takes effect if the build regenerates those files, which only happens in maintainer mode. Separately, the new release added a build option (mmgir) that pulls new deps if auto-detected.
fix_pattern: Apply the upstream MR as a FILESDIR patch AND pass -Dmaintainer-mode=true in meson args (with an XXX comment to drop it once the fix is in a release) so the patched generators re-emit the affected headers. Also explicitly pass -Dbuild-mmgir=false to pin the new optional feature off instead of relying on its default.
rule: When a backported patch touches code generators or templates whose output is shipped pregenerated in the tarball, enable the build system's maintainer/regeneration mode so the patch actually takes effect, and explicitly disable any newly introduced build options that could pull extra dependencies.
snippet:
local emesonargs=(
	-Dbuild-mmgir=false
	# XXX: Drop this once MR 77 is in a release
	-Dmaintainer-mode=true
)

problem: Upstream removed a feature (SMB_LM NTLM helper) but the ebuild still had configure bits for it, and users upgrading with USE='ldap samba' got no hint that they must migrate to a different helper.
root_cause: Feature removal upstream left stale configure logic in the ebuild, and version upgrades crossing a feature-removal boundary silently drop functionality.
fix_pattern: Drop the stale ntlm_modules configure logic, then inherit eapi9-ver and use ver_replacing -lt <first-version-without-feature> in pkg_postinst, combined with the relevant USE flags, to print an ewarn only when upgrading from an affected older version.
rule: When a version bump removes user-visible functionality, clean out the dead configure logic and add a pkg_postinst warning gated by 'ver_replacing -lt <boundary>' (eapi9-ver) plus the relevant USE flags so only actually-affected upgraders see it.
snippet:
inherit eapi9-ver
pkg_postinst() {
	if ver_replacing -lt 7.6 && use samba && use ldap ; then
		ewarn "SMB_LM support is now gone from >= Squid 7.6. Please use ntlm_auth instead"
		ewarn "from net-fs/samba."
	fi
}

problem: Test suite failed on systems where coreutils' 'true' is a multicall binary (rust-coreutils, busybox/Alpine): the Makefile did 'cp $(TRUE) $@' to create a stamp executable, and a multicall 'true' copied under another name refuses to run.
root_cause: Upstream test harness assumed GNU coreutils' standalone /bin/true can be copied and executed under any filename; multicall binaries dispatch on argv[0] and break this.
fix_pattern: Backport the upstream PR as a FILESDIR patch replacing 'cp $(TRUE) $@' with 'touch $@ && $(CHMOD) +x $@' (an empty executable file exits 0 when run by sh, or serves as a plain stamp), and add it to PATCHES.
rule: When tests fail on musl/rust-coreutils/busybox systems because a Makefile copies the 'true' binary as a stamp/dummy executable, patch it to create an empty executable file (touch + chmod +x) instead.

problem: New kernel branch (7.1) entered the tree but all out-of-tree driver ebuilds still declared MODULES_KERNEL_MAX=7.0, so linux-mod-r1 would warn/refuse against the new kernels even though most driver versions build fine.
root_cause: linux-mod-r1's MODULES_KERNEL_MAX is a per-ebuild compatibility ceiling that must be manually re-verified and raised whenever a new kernel branch appears; nothing bumps it automatically.
fix_pattern: Build-test each non-legacy driver version against the new kernel branch and bump MODULES_KERNEL_MAX only in the ebuilds known to work, leaving broken versions (580.159.03-r1) at the old ceiling.
rule: When a new kernel branch is added, re-test every out-of-tree kernel-module ebuild against it and raise MODULES_KERNEL_MAX per-version only where the build is confirmed, ideally before the new gentoo-kernel is keyworded.

problem: New version failed to build with clang (comparison operator used by list sort not const-qualified) and the release tarball shipped stale build artifacts (build/ dir, .qmake.stash, generated Makefile) that pollute an out-of-tree qmake build.
root_cause: Clang enforces that member operator< called on const objects (e.g. by sort()) must be const; upstream tarball was rolled from a dirty working directory including qmake cache and object files.
fix_pattern: Add a small FILESDIR patch marking 'bool operator < (...) const' (noting 'not reported upstream, no bugtracker' in the patch header), and in src_prepare remove the shipped artifacts: 'rm -r build .qmake.stash Makefile || die' before running eqmake6.
rule: When an upstream tarball ships prebuilt objects or build-system cache files (.qmake.stash, Makefile, build/), delete them in src_prepare so the ebuild's own configure step controls the build; and const-qualify comparison operators to fix clang build failures.

problem: One LIT test (mlir-pdll-lsp-server/view-output.test) failed unpredictably regardless of environment; keeping it in per-configuration skip lists was unreliable.
root_cause: Genuinely nondeterministic test with no understood trigger; conditional LIT skip lists only cover the enumerated configurations.
fix_pattern: Delete the flaky test file unconditionally in src_prepare ('rm test/... || die' with a comment) and drop it from the conditional skip list in multilib_src_test.
rule: When a test is flaky in an environment-independent way, remove the test file outright in src_prepare with an explanatory comment instead of maintaining fragile conditional skip lists.

problem: Same lesson as llvm commit 0544891c: LLVM 23.x build now references utils/docs, so LLVM_COMPONENTS sparse-checkout list needed utils/docs added.
LLVM 23.x live builds broke because the build system started referencing utils/docs, a path not included in the eclass-driven sparse checkout of the monorepo.
root_cause: llvm.org.eclass only fetches the subdirectories listed in LLVM_COMPONENTS; when upstream's CMake starts referencing a new monorepo path, the checkout is missing files and configure/build fails.
fix_pattern: Add the newly referenced path to the LLVM_COMPONENTS array (and test-only paths to LLVM_TEST_COMPONENTS) before llvm.org_set_globals.
rule: For packages built from a partial/sparse checkout of a monorepo, treat 'file not found' errors after a bump as a signal to extend the components/paths list rather than to patch the build system.
snippet:
-LLVM_COMPONENTS=( llvm cmake libc third-party )
+LLVM_COMPONENTS=( llvm cmake libc third-party utils/docs )

problem: New upstream release started shipping detached PGP signatures; the bump was used to add supply-chain verification which the old ebuilds lacked.
root_cause: Without verify-sig wiring, tarball authenticity rests solely on the Manifest hashes recorded at bump time.
fix_pattern: Standard verify-sig triple: set VERIFY_SIG_OPENPGP_KEY_PATH before inherit, add the .asc to SRC_URI guarded by 'verify-sig?', and BDEPEND on a date-pinned sec-keys/openpgp-keys-* package. Also note QA_CONFIG_IMPL_DECL_SKIP+=( ioctlsocket ) for a known configure implicit-declaration false positive.
rule: When upstream publishes detached .asc signatures, wire up verify-sig at the next bump: key path variable + verify-sig?-guarded .asc in SRC_URI + BDEPEND on the matching (minimum-version-pinned) openpgp-keys package.
snippet:
VERIFY_SIG_OPENPGP_KEY_PATH=/usr/share/openpgp-keys/nlnetlabs.asc
inherit verify-sig
SRC_URI="
	https://.../${P}.tar.gz
	verify-sig? ( https://.../${P}.tar.gz.asc )
"
BDEPEND="verify-sig? ( >=sec-keys/openpgp-keys-nlnetlabs-20260101 )"

problem: Ebuild installed zsh completions with raw 'insinto /usr/share/zsh/site-functions; doins', duplicating a path the shell-completion.eclass now owns.
root_cause: Legacy idiom predating shell-completion.eclass; hardcoded paths drift if the canonical completion dir changes and bypass eclass QA.
fix_pattern: inherit shell-completion and replace the insinto/doins pair with a single dozshcomp call (analogues: dobashcomp/newbashcomp, dofishcomp).
rule: When installing shell completion files, use shell-completion.eclass helpers (dozshcomp/dofishcomp) instead of hardcoding insinto paths.

problem: New snapshot ebuild failed to unpack into the expected ${WORKDIR}/${P}: the GitHub archive's top-level directory is named after the repository's historical name (zsh-completion-${PV}), not the Gentoo package name.
root_cause: GitHub /archive/ tarballs unpack to <reponame>-<ref>; when the repo name differs from PN (or the tag content dictates another prefix), the default S=${WORKDIR}/${P} is wrong.
fix_pattern: Set S explicitly inside the non-live (else) branch next to SRC_URI: S="${WORKDIR}"/zsh-completion-${PV}; keep the live branch using git-r3's default S.
rule: For GitHub archive SRC_URIs, always verify the tarball's top-level directory name and set S when it differs from ${P}; in live/release dual ebuilds put the S assignment in the release branch only.

problem: Straight version bump: new ebuild is a copy of 3.11.1 with all stable keywords reset to ~arch; no other change.
Straight version bump: identical to 1.8.1.1 ebuild except stable keywords dropped back to ~arch.
src_compile ran ant twice ('eant' then 'use doc && eant javadoc'), doing a conditional second build-tool invocation for an optional target.
root_cause: Conditional targets were expressed as separate command invocations instead of conditionally appended arguments.
fix_pattern: Collapse into one invocation using usev, which expands to its second argument when the USE flag is set and to nothing otherwise: 'LC_ALL=C eant deploy $(usev doc javadoc)'.
rule: Use $(usev flag value) to conditionally append targets/arguments to a single build command instead of running the tool again under 'use flag &&'.

problem: Same usev consolidation as commit 5278d041, applied to the 10.1.x ebuild.
Same usev consolidation as commit 5278d041, applied to the 11.0.x ebuild.

Chromium build hung because gn got stuck resolving self-referential symlinks shipped by the pinned older nodejs version; fixed by bumping the ebuild's NODE_VER pin to 24.16.0-r1 (bug 977622). Package-specific pinned-tool maintenance.
Copyright header year update only (required after editing old ebuilds in a prior commit).
Copyright header year update only.
Copyright header year update only.

25 ebuilds depended on dev-db/postgresql[server], but USE=server only exists in postgresql via a Gentoo patch and is absent in 9999 (where the server is unconditionally built), making the dep unresolvable against flag-less versions.
root_cause: A USE-conditional dependency on a flag that some versions of the target package do not declare fails dependency resolution; PMS provides USE-default syntax (+)/(-) to state what to assume when the flag is missing.
fix_pattern: Change POSTGRES_USEDEP="server" to POSTGRES_USEDEP="server(+)" (composable with other flags: "server(+),threads(+)"), meaning: if the version lacks the flag, treat it as enabled.
rule: When depending on a USE flag that may not exist in every version of the dependency (distro-added flag, flag removed in newer/live versions), append (+) or (-) to declare the assumed default rather than leaving the bare flag name.

problem: Ebuild used postgres eclass's ${POSTGRES_DEP} but never set REQUIRED_USE, so emerge could proceed with no postgres_targets_* flag enabled and fail later at build time (bug 978223).
root_cause: postgres/postgres-multi eclasses export paired variables: POSTGRES_DEP for dependencies and POSTGRES_REQ_USE to force at least one compatible postgres_targets flag; consumers must wire up both.
fix_pattern: Add REQUIRED_USE="${POSTGRES_REQ_USE}" alongside DEPEND="${POSTGRES_DEP}".
rule: When an eclass exports both a dependency-string variable and a matching REQUIRED_USE variable (POSTGRES_DEP/POSTGRES_REQ_USE, PYTHON_DEPS/PYTHON_REQUIRED_USE), always set both - the dep string alone does not enforce target selection.

problem: Same POSTGRES_REQ_USE wiring fix as pygresql commit d35fa110 (bug 978223).
Same POSTGRES_REQ_USE wiring fix as pygresql commit d35fa110 (bug 978223), plus SLOT quoting style.
postgres-multi consumers could be emerged with no postgres_targets_* USE flag enabled, producing broken/empty builds (tracker bug 978223)
root_cause: The postgres/postgres-multi eclasses only provide the POSTGRES_REQ_USE variable; they do not export REQUIRED_USE (nor *DEPEND) themselves, so ebuilds that forget the assignment silently allow an empty target set
fix_pattern: Assign REQUIRED_USE="${POSTGRES_REQ_USE}" in the ebuild; also move `inherit postgres-multi` ABOVE the assignment because the variable is only defined after inherit (repmgr's inherit was after KEYWORDS and had to be relocated)
rule: When an eclass documents helper variables like FOO_REQ_USE or FOO_DEP instead of exporting the metadata itself, every consumer ebuild must assign them explicitly, after the inherit line

problem: Missing REQUIRED_USE="${POSTGRES_REQ_USE}" in postgres eclass consumer; same fix as 2ddb4b0 (bug 978223)
Missing REQUIRED_USE="${POSTGRES_REQ_USE}" across 5 ebuilds; same fix as 2ddb4b0 (bug 978223)
Missing REQUIRED_USE="${POSTGRES_REQ_USE}"; same fix as 2ddb4b0 (bug 978223)
Missing REQUIRED_USE="${POSTGRES_REQ_USE}"; same fix as 2ddb4b0 (bug 978223)
Missing REQUIRED_USE="${POSTGRES_REQ_USE}" across 8 ebuilds, plus removal of empty IUSE="" and unquoted SLOT=0; same fix as 2ddb4b0 (bug 978223)
Besides missing REQUIRED_USE, the ebuild hand-wrote src_compile/src_install phases that byte-for-byte duplicated what postgres-multi already exports, plus obsolete `|| die` after emake
root_cause: Phases copy-pasted from old templates; postgres-multi EXPORT_FUNCTIONS already provides src_compile (postgres-multi_foreach emake) and src_install, and in EAPI>=4 emake dies on failure by itself
fix_pattern: Delete src_compile and src_install entirely so the eclass-exported defaults run; keep only the src_prepare override that chains `default` then postgres-multi_src_prepare; add REQUIRED_USE="${POSTGRES_REQ_USE}"
rule: When a phase function in an ebuild merely re-implements the inherited eclass default (including `emake || die` noise), delete it and let EXPORT_FUNCTIONS do the work

problem: Live (9999) python-tests ebuilds referenced stale python-gentoo-patches tarballs; same PATCHSET-sync idiom as 493ebe3
Live 9999 ebuilds pinned old PATCHSET tarballs (e.g. python-gentoo-patches-3.13.3) after newer patchsets shipped, so git builds got outdated distro patches and the Manifest carried dead DIST entries
root_cause: Live ebuilds cannot auto-track versioned support tarballs; a pinned PATCHSET variable rots unless synced whenever a new patchset is released with a release bump
fix_pattern: On every patchset release, update PATCHSET="python-gentoo-patches-X.Y.Z[_pN]" in each 9999 ebuild and prune the superseded DIST lines from the Manifest in the same commit
rule: When a live ebuild fetches a versioned auxiliary tarball (patchset, test data), bump that pin as part of every corresponding release bump and drop the old Manifest entries

problem: Packaging the freethreading (no-GIL) CPython build alongside regular CPython without a separate package or USE-conditional rebuild
root_cause: Both variants come from the same upstream tarball but must be co-installable and independently keyworded/slotted; USE=freethreading on one ebuild cannot give two parallel installs
fix_pattern: Ship the variant as a fake-version ebuild in the same package: PV prefixed with 0. (python-0.3.14.6_p1), then REAL_PV=${PV#0.} for SRC_URI/S, PYVER="$(ver_cut 2-3)t" and SLOT="${PYVER}" so the 3.14t slot coexists with 3.14; sigstore verification via VERIFY_SIG_METHOD=sigstore + VERIFY_SIG_CERT_IDENTITY/OIDC_ISSUER
rule: To ship a co-installable build variant of the same upstream release inside one package, encode the variant in a version prefix (0.X.Y.Z) plus a distinct SLOT, and derive the real upstream version with ${PV#0.}

problem: Distro patchset updated without a new upstream release: how to version an ebuild whose only delta is the Gentoo patchset
root_cause: Portage needs a higher version to trigger the update, but the upstream tarball is unchanged
fix_pattern: Add a _pN suffix ebuild (3.14.6_p1); SRC_URI strips it for the upstream tarball via MY_P="Python-${PV%_p*}" while PATCHSET="python-gentoo-patches-${PV}" carries the full _pN version, so only the patchset tarball is new
rule: For patchset-only updates, bump with a _pN version whose SRC_URI derives the unchanged upstream tarball with ${PV%_p*} and the patch tarball with the full ${PV}

problem: postgres-multi extension ebuild had no ${POSTGRES_DEP} in *DEPEND, no REQUIRED_USE, and src_install ran plain `default`, so the extension was not installed for each PG slot (bug 976778)
root_cause: The postgres-multi eclass deliberately does not set *DEPEND/REQUIRED_USE (only provides POSTGRES_DEP/POSTGRES_REQ_USE variables), and a plain `default` install ignores the per-slot foreach machinery, silently mis-installing
fix_pattern: Set RDEPEND="${POSTGRES_DEP}", DEPEND="${RDEPEND}", REQUIRED_USE="${POSTGRES_REQ_USE}"; replace src_install body with einstalldocs + postgres-multi_src_install; revbump (-r1) because installed files change
rule: When overriding a phase in an eclass-driven multi-build package, call the eclass phase implementation (eclass_src_install), never `default`, and revbump when the installed image changes
snippet:
REQUIRED_USE="${POSTGRES_REQ_USE}"
RDEPEND="${POSTGRES_DEP}"
DEPEND="${RDEPEND}"

src_install() {
	einstalldocs
	postgres-multi_src_install
}

problem: Same *DEPEND/REQUIRED_USE/src_install fix as c21d552, plus src_compile `default` replaced with postgres-multi_src_compile
Same postgres-multi *DEPEND/REQUIRED_USE/src_install fix as c21d552 (bug 976778 series)
Same postgres-multi *DEPEND/REQUIRED_USE/src_install fix as c21d552
Same postgres-multi *DEPEND/REQUIRED_USE/src_install fix as c21d552
Same postgres-multi *DEPEND/REQUIRED_USE/src_install fix as c21d552

Bug 977512 reported ardour breakage tied to mbedtls; first response added net-libs/mbedtls:0 to RDEPEND with a revbump (9.2.0 -> 9.2.0-r1) and to the live ebuild — a dep that was reverted hours later as not actually direct

net-libs/mbedtls:0 sat in ardour's RDEPEND (and was even freshly added the same day) although ardour's own binaries do not link mbedtls — the mbedtls-slot breakage users saw came through net-libs/libwebsockets
root_cause: A library visible in the process/linkage of a package via a middle dependency was mistaken for a direct dependency; the direct consumer (libwebsockets:=) already carries the mbedtls dep and its := subslot triggers the rebuilds
fix_pattern: Drop net-libs/mbedtls:0 from all ebuilds; the hours-old, never-shipped 9.2.0-r1 was renamed back to 9.2.0 (revbump reverted), while 9.7.0 which had shipped the bogus dep was revbumped to -r1 to propagate the metadata change
rule: Before adding a library dep from a runtime-breakage report, confirm the package's own ELF objects list it in NEEDED (scanelf -n); transitive breakage is fixed by the := slot operator on the direct consumer, not by copying the dep — and only revert a revbump if it never reached users

problem: Upstream git split the moodbar feature into extra CMake switches (ENABLE_GSTFASTSPECTRUM, ENABLE_WAVEFORM); the live ebuild had to wire both to the existing moodbar USE flag to keep the feature deterministic and avoid automagic detection
Vendored-crate update raised the minimum supported rustc, but the ebuild still declared the old RUST_MIN_VER, letting users with an older rust attempt a doomed compile
root_cause: cargo.eclass/rust.eclass only enforce the rustc floor declared via RUST_MIN_VER; crate MSRV bumps (rust-version in Cargo.toml) are invisible unless the maintainer mirrors them
fix_pattern: Bump RUST_MIN_VER (1.83.0 -> 1.85.0) above the inherit so the eclass selects/depends on a new enough rust slot; no revbump needed when the ebuild had not shipped a release with the wrong floor being exercised
rule: When bumping a Rust/cargo package or refreshing CRATES, re-check the highest rust-version among workspace and vendored crates and raise RUST_MIN_VER to match

problem: libsass imports pkg_resources at runtime but nothing guaranteed it was installed, so `import sass` could fail with ModuleNotFoundError: pkg_resources (bug 978189)
root_cause: setuptools removed/deprecated bundling pkg_resources and Gentoo split it into dev-python/pkg-resources, so packages that still `import pkg_resources` no longer get it implicitly via the build-time setuptools dep
fix_pattern: Add dev-python/pkg-resources[${PYTHON_USEDEP}] to RDEPEND and revbump (-r1) so installed systems pull the new runtime dep
rule: If a Python package imports pkg_resources at runtime, RDEPEND on dev-python/pkg-resources[${PYTHON_USEDEP}] explicitly (never rely on setuptools), and revbump when adding it

problem: Routine live-ebuild sync with the new 11.15.0 release ebuild: add the new test-expectation patch and drop a stale LC_ALL=C prefix from src_test
New netpbm advanced-branch release: upstream publishes no tarballs for its svn 'advanced' branch, and a pm_message() output change broke an expected-output test
root_cause: Upstream only tarballs its 'super stable' flavor; the stable and advanced svn branches must be self-snapshotted; upstream's message-formatting change made test/pnmtojpeg-scans.ok mismatch actual output
fix_pattern: Maintainer rolls the tarball and hosts it at a stable dev-space URL (SRC_URI="https://distfiles.gentoo.org/pub/dev/ceamac@gentoo.org/${CATEGORY}/${PN}/${P}.tar.xz"); parallel branches are distinguished by subslot (SLOT="0/stable" vs "0/advanced", advanced never stabilized, strategy documented in an ebuild comment); the cosmetic test failure is fixed by patching the .ok expectation file instead of RESTRICTing tests
rule: When upstream ships no tarball for the branch you package, self-host a reproducible snapshot at a permanent URL and encode the branch policy in SLOT subslots; when only a test's expected output is stale, patch the expectation file rather than disabling the test

problem: Version bump carrying two hygiene fixes: dev-cpp/eigen:3 pin replaced with eigen:= slot operator, and builds against catch2-3.8 failed in tests for a missing interface header
root_cause: A literal :3 slot pin does not request rebuilds or track a future slot move the way := does; catch2 3.8 stopped transitively including catch_interfaces_capture.hpp so code using the result-capture interface no longer compiled
fix_pattern: Use dev-cpp/eigen:= in the new revision; add a FILESDIR patch that inserts the now-required explicit #include <catch2/interfaces/catch_interfaces_capture.hpp>, and apply the same patch to the 9999 ebuild so it does not regress
rule: When a header-splitting update of a dependency (catch2/boost style) breaks a consumer, patch in the missing explicit #include rather than pinning the old dependency, and mirror release-ebuild patches into the live ebuild

problem: Package failed to link only when built with USE=test: undefined references in a unit-test binary (bug 977901).
root_cause: Upstream CMake test target was missing two source files in EXTRA_SOURCES, so symbols used by the test were never compiled into it; only distros running the test suite hit it.
fix_pattern: Backport the upstream commit as a FILESDIR patch (keeping the upstream commit header inside the patch for provenance), append it to PATCHES=(), and revbump the ebuild (-r1 -> -r2) even though only the test build changes.
rule: When a build failure appears only under USE=test, patch the upstream test build files via FILESDIR and revbump; keep the upstream commit message in the patch header so future bumps know when to drop it.

problem: Temporary Gentoo-infra workaround URL (gitweb-cdn-origin.gentoo.org) reverted to canonical gitweb.gentoo.org in SRC_URI once no longer needed; no revbump since the distfile is identical.
A routine version-bump commit accidentally contains a stray Manifest hunk adding a privoxy-4.2.0 DIST entry to net-proxy/privoxy/Manifest, an unrelated package.
root_cause: Developer had another bump staged in the working tree and committed with a path filter that was too wide (or 'git commit -a'), so an unrelated Manifest change rode along.
fix_pattern: Nothing in-commit; the lesson is procedural. Before committing a bump, check 'git diff --cached --stat' and confirm only your package's directory is touched; repoman/pkgcheck will not always flag a valid stray Manifest entry.
rule: Before committing an ebuild bump, verify the diffstat touches only the intended package directory - stray Manifest entries from other in-progress work commit cleanly and silently.
snippet: git diff --cached --stat -- . | grep -v '^ net-vpn/tinc/' # should be empty

problem: New CPython (3.15) not yet enabled for a working package, blocking dependency-tree-wide interpreter rollout.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT is an explicit opt-in list; every dev-python package must be individually tested and extended when a new interpreter is keyworded.
fix_pattern: After running the test suite under the new interpreter, widen the brace range in PYTHON_COMPAT; no revbump needed because installed files for existing impls are unchanged and the change only adds a new impl.
rule: Enabling a new Python implementation is a one-line PYTHON_COMPAT range widening with no revbump, but only after the test suite passes under that interpreter.

problem: Ebuild still depended on dev-qt/qtwayland:6, which Gentoo folded into dev-qt/qtbase as a USE flag; dependency no longer expresses the real requirement.
root_cause: Gentoo merged the qtwayland module into qtbase behind USE=wayland (Qt 6.10-era packaging change), so separate dev-qt/qtwayland deps became stale.
