fix_pattern: Revbump (-r1 -> -r2), drop the dev-qt/qtwayland line and add 'wayland' to the qtbase USE-dep list; the new revision resets stable keywords to ~arch because the dependency graph changed and needs re-testing.
rule: When a Qt module is absorbed into qtbase as a USE flag, replace 'dev-qt/qtwayland' style deps with 'dev-qt/qtbase:6[wayland]' in a new revision (and expect keywords to drop back to ~arch).

problem: Same qtwayland -> qtbase[wayland] dependency migration as x11-misc/screengrab (6fa68eb4), applied across the LXQt suite via revbumps.
Build failed for users with an older system Go toolchain (bug 978101): go.mod of podman 6.0.0 requires a newer Go than the oldest keyworded dev-lang/go.
root_cause: Go modules declare a minimum toolchain in go.mod ('go'/'toolchain' directive); a bare 'dev-lang/go' or go-module eclass default BDEPEND does not enforce it, so users on older Go get compile errors.
fix_pattern: Add an explicit versioned toolchain bound to BDEPEND matching the go.mod requirement: '>=dev-lang/go-1.25.6'. Applied in place without revbump since it only tightens build-time resolution.
rule: For Go packages, mirror the go.mod minimum toolchain version as '>=dev-lang/go-X.Y.Z' in BDEPEND whenever it exceeds the oldest available dev-lang/go.

problem: Runtime breakage after dev-qt/qtbase upgrades (bug 975689): OBS uses Qt private APIs, and installed binaries were not scheduled for rebuild when qtbase changed.
root_cause: Qt private headers have no ABI stability across even patch releases; without a := subslot operator on dev-qt/qtbase, Portage never rebuilds the consumer when qtbase's subslot changes.
fix_pattern: Change 'dev-qt/qtbase:6[...]' to 'dev-qt/qtbase:6=[...]' so subslot rebuilds trigger; same commit also pins 'vlc? ( <media-video/vlc-4.0:= )' to fence off an incompatible future major.
rule: Any package that uses Qt private headers must depend on dev-qt/qtbase:6= (subslot operator) so it is rebuilt on every qtbase release.

problem: Routine lockstep bump of LLVM_PATCHSET tarball rev (16.0.6-r6 -> -r7) plus PYTHON_COMPAT trim; same pattern as llvm-core/llvm (87c413c4).
Routine lockstep LLVM_PATCHSET rev bump across two versions; same pattern as llvm-core/llvm (87c413c4).
Routine lockstep LLVM_PATCHSET rev bump (18.1.8-r7 -> -r8); same pattern as llvm-core/llvm (87c413c4).
Routine lockstep LLVM_PATCHSET rev bump (16.0.6-r6 -> -r7); same pattern as llvm-core/llvm (87c413c4).
Routine lockstep LLVM_PATCHSET rev bump across three clang versions; same pattern as llvm-core/llvm (87c413c4).
Fixes must reach ~10 split LLVM packages across up to 7 live versions without editing dozens of patch files in-tree or revbumping everything.
root_cause: LLVM is split into many packages built from one llvm-project tarball; Gentoo centralizes distro patches in a versioned 'llvm-gentoo-patchset-<ver>-rN.tar.xz' distfile selected by the LLVM_PATCHSET variable consumed by llvm.org.eclass.
fix_pattern: Publish a new patchset tarball revision, then in each consumer ebuild bump only 'LLVM_PATCHSET=${PV}-rN' and re-Manifest; build-fix-only patch changes (e.g. new-toolchain compat) are applied in place without ebuild revbumps. PYTHON_COMPAT floors are trimmed in the same sync pass.
rule: For package families sharing one upstream source, keep distro patches in a versioned side tarball referenced by a single variable so a fix is one variable bump per ebuild instead of N patch files.
snippet:
LLVM_COMPONENTS=( compiler-rt cmake llvm/cmake )
LLVM_PATCHSET=${PV}-r7
llvm.org_set_globals

problem: Sanitizer runtimes failed to build against new sys-kernel/linux-headers-7.0 (bug 977690) for every live version 16-21.
root_cause: compiler-rt sanitizers parse kernel UAPI headers directly; a headers-package major update changed structures and broke compilation of all older LLVM releases at once.
fix_pattern: Backport the upstream header-compat fix into the shared llvm-gentoo-patchset tarball and bump LLVM_PATCHSET in every live version's ebuild in one commit - six versions fixed without any source-tarball or revision change.
rule: When a new linux-headers (or glibc) release breaks a package, the fix must be backported to ALL live versions in the tree, not just the newest - users build old stable versions against new headers.

problem: New Emacs tree-sitter mode package needed; shows the current canonical elisp packaging skeleton.
root_cause: Emacs site-lisp packages need a Gentoo site-init file, autoloads generation, an Emacs version floor, and (for ts-modes) a dependency on the grammar library.
fix_pattern: EAPI=9 + 'inherit elisp'; set NEED_EMACS="29.1", SITEFILE="50${PN}-gentoo.el" with a matching files/ stub that add-to-list's the load path and loads autoloads; RDEPEND on dev-libs/tree-sitter-<lang> and mirror it into BDEPEND (byte-compilation loads the grammar); rename GitHub tarball with '-> ${P}.gh.tar.gz'.
rule: For an Emacs mode package: inherit elisp, set NEED_EMACS/SITEFILE, ship a 50<pkg>-gentoo.el site file, call elisp-make-autoload-file in src_compile, and depend on the tree-sitter grammar in both RDEPEND and BDEPEND.

problem: Routine kernel-2 eclass bump: new ebuild with K_GENPATCHES_VER incremented to pull the genpatches tarball carrying the vdso2c fix; no reusable idiom beyond the standard workflow.
Python-plus-Rust package (inherit cargo distutils-r1 pypi) failed at build time (bug 978092): cargo could not find vendored crates / tried to work without the offline registry config.
root_cause: Both cargo.eclass and pypi.eclass export src_unpack; with 'inherit cargo distutils-r1 pypi' the last eclass (pypi) wins, so cargo's src_unpack - which runs cargo_gen_config to point cargo at the vendored crates and enable offline mode - was silently never executed.
fix_pattern: Define src_unpack explicitly in the ebuild and call both eclasses' pieces: pypi_src_unpack for the sdist, then cargo_gen_config (crates tarball is unpacked normally as part of ${A}).
rule: When two inherited eclasses both export the same phase (classic case: cargo + pypi src_unpack), define that phase explicitly in the ebuild and invoke each eclass's function yourself - the inherit order otherwise silently drops one.

problem: pkg_setup checked required Emacs build features one at a time and died on the first missing one, forcing users to fix-and-retry repeatedly.
root_cause: Loop died inside the first failed iteration instead of collecting all failures.
fix_pattern: Probe each feature by evaluating an elisp predicate via ${EMACS} ${EMACSFLAGS} --eval "(princ ...)", accumulate missing feature names into one variable, and emit a single eerror/die listing every missing USE flag.
rule: In pkg_setup/pkg_pretend precondition checks, accumulate all failures and die once with the full list, so the user fixes everything in one pass; query Emacs capabilities with ${EMACS} --eval '(princ PREDICATE)'.
snippet:
for (( i=0; i<${#feat[@]}; i+=2 )); do
	[[ $(${EMACS} ${EMACSFLAGS} --eval "(princ ${feat[i+1]})") = t ]] \
		|| missing+="${missing:+ }${feat[i]}"
done
[[ -n ${missing} ]] && die "Emacs misses: ${missing}"

problem: After the passlib->libpass pkgmove, PYPI_PN=libpass became identical to PN and was dropped as redundant; pypi.eclass defaults PYPI_PN to PN.
Package needed renaming (dev-python/passlib -> dev-python/libpass) because the tree now ships the libpass fork; installed-package databases and every reverse dependency reference the old name.
root_cause: Gentoo package identity lives in the category/name path; renaming without a pkgmove entry breaks installed systems (VDB) and revdep atoms.
fix_pattern: Single atomic commit: git mv the package directory and rename ebuilds; add 'move dev-python/passlib dev-python/libpass' to profiles/updates/<Q>-<YYYY>; update every reverse dependency's atoms in the same commit; keep PYPI_PN pointing at the real PyPI name; update package.deprecated and comments.
rule: A package rename is: profiles/updates 'move old new' entry + directory rename + all revdep atom updates in one atomic commit - never rename without the updates entry or Portage cannot migrate installed systems.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT widening to python3_15 (same one-line pattern as dev-python/xxhash aa302d29), notable only in that it applies equally to maturin/Rust-ext packages.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT widening to python3_15; same pattern as dev-python/xxhash (aa302d29).
PyTables stopped building/working against >=dev-libs/c-blosc2-3.1 after the system c-blosc2 was upgraded past a compatibility break.
root_cause: c-blosc2 3.x changed defaults/API (BLOSC2_MAX_DIM handling); the release tarball's setup.py predates the fix, and Gentoo builds against the system c-blosc2 (unbundled via PYTABLES_NO_EMBEDDED_LIBS/USE_PKGCONFIG).
fix_pattern: Cherry-pick the upstream fix commit into files/ as ${P}-blosc2-3.1.patch, cite the upstream commit URL in a comment above the PATCHES entry, and add a new revision (-r1) since installed extension modules change; the ebuild keeps stripping bundled c-blosc ('rm -r c-blosc/{blosc,internal-complibs}') to force system libs.
rule: When a system dependency's major upgrade breaks a consumer that bundles its own copy, unbundle (build against system libs), backport the upstream compat commit with its URL recorded, and revbump.

problem: Upstream (torproject) signs a sha256sum file instead of the tarball itself, so the stock verify-sig SRC_URI+default flow cannot authenticate the distfile.
root_cause: verify-sig.eclass defaults assume a detached signature of the artifact; some upstreams only publish signed checksum files.
fix_pattern: Fetch tarball + .sha256sum + .sha256sum.asc in SRC_URI under verify-sig?; in src_unpack, first verify the detached signature of the checksum file with verify-sig_verify_detached, then validate the tarball against it with verify-sig_verify_unsigned_checksums, then run default.
rule: When upstream signs checksum files rather than tarballs, chain verify-sig_verify_detached on the checksum file with verify-sig_verify_unsigned_checksums on the artifact inside src_unpack.

problem: Routine live-ebuild sync: EAPI 8->9, cosmetic quoting, drop .git suffix from EGIT_REPO_URI.
Routine live-ebuild sync: EAPI 8->9 only, no other change.
Snapshot ebuilds copied from the 9999 template need a pinned upstream commit; a bare COMMIT="" placeholder in the shared template means every snapshot copy must be hand-edited.
root_cause: The live 9999 ebuild doubles as the template for -pYYYYMMDD snapshot releases, and the non-live branch referenced ${COMMIT} that was left empty in the template.
fix_pattern: Gate the pinned hash on the exact snapshot PV inside the shared else-branch so the identical file text works both as 9999 and as the snapshot ebuild: [[ "${PV}" == *p20260303 ]] && COMMIT=e5f22ad408740ec517a436ec19b74ce1398e61bc
rule: When a live ebuild is the template for commit-snapshot ebuilds, set the pinned COMMIT via a PV-conditional in the shared text so copying 9999 to the snapshot name needs no edits.
snippet:
if [[ "${PV}" == *9999* ]] ; then
	inherit git-r3
	EGIT_REPO_URI="https://github.com/greghendershott/${PN}"
else
	[[ "${PV}" == *p20260303 ]] && COMMIT=e5f22ad408740ec517a436ec19b74ce1398e61bc
	SRC_URI="https://github.com/greghendershott/${PN}/archive/${COMMIT}.tar.gz -> ${P}.snapshot.gh.tar.gz"
fi

problem: Upstream release tarball lacks its git submodules (brotli, libspdm, mbedtls, mipi-sys-t, openssl, libfdt), so the build cannot proceed from the plain archive.
root_cause: GitHub release tarballs never include submodule contents; edk2 vendors six of them at pinned commits.
fix_pattern: Pin each submodule SHA in a top-of-ebuild variable, fetch each as a GitHub commit tarball in SRC_URI (arch-conditional where only some arches need it), then in src_prepare replace each empty submodule directory with a symlink into ${WORKDIR} via a small helper. Per-arch extra distfiles use a @ARCH@ placeholder URI expanded with ${VAR//@ARCH@/amd64}.
rule: When a release tarball is missing submodules, add per-submodule SHA-pinned tarballs to SRC_URI and rmdir+symlink them into the source tree in src_prepare instead of using the live git fetch.

problem: Upstream CMake downloads its build framework (OBCMake) from the network at configure time via FetchContent GIT_REPOSITORY, which fails in the network-sandboxed ebuild environment.
root_cause: FetchContent_Declare with GIT_REPOSITORY/GIT_TAG performs a git clone during src_configure; portage forbids network access after src_unpack.
fix_pattern: Fetch the FetchContent dependency as a separate SRC_URI tarball, move it into the source tree in src_unpack, patch FetchContent_Declare to take SOURCE_DIR from a cache variable, and pass that variable via mycmakeargs. Same patch also fixes bad install destinations (cmake config files to ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}/cmake, headers to CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR).
rule: When a CMake project uses FetchContent_Declare with GIT_REPOSITORY, add that dependency as a pinned SRC_URI tarball and patch the declaration to a local SOURCE_DIR handed in through a -D cache variable.
snippet:
FetchContent_Declare(OBCMake
-    GIT_REPOSITORY "https://github.com/oblivioncth/OBCMake"
-    GIT_TAG ${git_ref}
+    SOURCE_DIR "${OB_CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}"
 )
# ebuild: src_unpack moves tarball to ${S}/.obcmake, then
#   -DOB_CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR="${S}/.obcmake"

problem: USE=test build fails because the test suite links against qttest but Qt was not declared as a test dependency (bug 978043).
root_cause: BUILD_TESTS_WITH_QT6=ON means the tests always use qttest regardless of the package's optional Qt integration, so tests need Qt even when the qt-related modules are disabled.
fix_pattern: Restructure deps so RDEPEND is primary and DEPEND="${RDEPEND} test? ( dev-qt/qtbase:6 )"; add a comment on the cmake switch explaining it only selects qt5-vs-qt6 for the always-Qt tests.
rule: When a package's test suite hard-requires a framework independent of the package's USE flags, add it as test?-conditional DEPEND and document why next to the build-system switch.
snippet:
DEPEND="${RDEPEND}
	test? ( dev-qt/qtbase:6 )
"
# in src_configure:
-DBUILD_TESTS_WITH_QT6=ON # tests use qttest; switch only picks qt5 or qt6

problem: Package needs Emacs built with dynamic-module and sqlite support; without them users get obscure failures instead of a clear message (bug 977615).
root_cause: USE-deps on app-editors/emacs express the requirement for depgraph resolution, but a manually-built or stale Emacs can still lack the features at build time.
fix_pattern: Declare the features as USE-deps (emacs[dynamic-loading,sqlite]) AND verify them in pkg_setup by evaluating elisp feature predicates against the actual ${EMACS}, dying with an actionable error naming the USE flag to enable. Native module built with MBED_FLAGS=$($(tc-getPKG_CONFIG) --cflags --libs mbedcrypto-3) and installed via elisp-modules-install.
rule: Express interpreter/editor feature requirements both as USE-dependencies and as a pkg_setup runtime probe that fails with an actionable message.
snippet:
local i feat=(
	dynamic-loading "(fboundp 'module-load)"
	sqlite "(sqlite-available-p)"
)
for (( i=0; i<${#feat[@]}; i+=2 )); do
	if [[ $(${EMACS} ${EMACSFLAGS} --eval "(princ ${feat[i+1]})") != t ]]; then
		eerror "Emerge app-editors/emacs with USE=\"${feat[i]}\"."
		die "Missing ${feat[i]} support"
	fi
done

problem: The generated libR.pc pkg-config file embeds the build's LDFLAGS, leaking the builder's link flags into every downstream consumer of libR.
root_cause: src/unix/Makefile.in substitutes both $(MAIN_LDFLAGS) and $(LIBR_LDFLAGS) into the @others field of libR.pc.in when generating the installed pkg-config file.
fix_pattern: Carry a small patch that drops the LDFLAGS variable from the .pc substitution so only intrinsic flags remain. Bump-maintained patch renamed per version (R-4.6.1-no-LDFLAGS-in-libR-pkg-config.patch). Ebuild also shows: ac_cv_path_PDFTEX= exports to defeat automagic TeX detection when USE=-doc, and docompress -x for docs the program opens at runtime.
rule: Installed .pc and foo-config files must not contain the build's LDFLAGS/CFLAGS; patch the file generation to substitute only flags intrinsic to linking the library.

problem: A blanket <dev-cpp/doctest-2.5 upper bound had been applied to all rspamd versions, but 4.1.1 and 9999 actually build fine with new doctest.
root_cause: The initial pin (c3eb96c0) was applied to every version for speed; upstream had already fixed the const-correctness issue in newer releases.
fix_pattern: Follow-up commit relaxes the bound back to plain dev-cpp/doctest only in the fixed versions' ebuilds, leaving the pin in place for old versions; commit message uses Fixes: <sha> to link the correction to the original commit.
rule: Scope dependency upper bounds to exactly the package versions that are broken, and lift them version-by-version as upstream fixes are confirmed.

problem: emerge failed because src_prepare copies ${FILESDIR}/gentoo.config-1.0.4 which was never committed to the package's files/ directory (bug 978075).
root_cause: The new ebuild copied the cross-compile helper idiom from dev-libs/openssl (cp "${FILESDIR}"/gentoo.config-1.0.4 gentoo.config) but the helper file itself was not copied over; dynamic FILESDIR references inside phase functions escape the static QA checks that catch missing PATCHES entries.
fix_pattern: Commit the referenced helper file into net-analyzer/sslscan/files/. Detection: grep every ebuild for ${FILESDIR}/ references (not just the PATCHES array) and stat each resolved path; or simply test-build the ebuild before pushing.
rule: Every ${FILESDIR}/x reference, including ones inside phase-function commands, must have a matching committed file; when borrowing an idiom from another package, bring its files/ assets along.
snippet:
# check idea:
grep -oP '\$\{FILESDIR\}/[^" ]+' *.ebuild | while read -r f; do
  [[ -e files/${f#*FILESDIR\}/} ]] || echo "MISSING: $f"
done

problem: Build fails against newer dev-cpp/doctest: doctest.h:1277 'error: passing const rspamd::mime::basic_mime_string<char> as this argument discards qualifiers' (bug 973396), found on the binhost.
root_cause: A new doctest major release is stricter about const-correctness in its stringification loop, exposing latent const-incorrect container usage in rspamd's headers; doctest is header-only so the breakage hits at package compile time via DEPEND.
fix_pattern: Add a versioned upper bound in DEPEND immediately (<dev-cpp/doctest-2.5) across affected ebuilds, quoting the exact compiler error in the commit message so the pin's reason is auditable later, and reference the tracking bug.
rule: When a new version of a header-only dependency breaks compilation, add an upper-bound pin right away with the error pasted in the commit message, then relax it per-version once upstream fixes are confirmed.

problem: A sed call in src_prepare had no error handling, so a failure (e.g. Makefile disappears/renames in a future bump) would be silently ignored.
root_cause: Newly-added ebuild missed the mandatory || die on a non-eclass command.
fix_pattern: Append || die: sed -i 's/ -D_FORTIFY_SOURCE=2/ /' Makefile || die. Note sed still exits 0 when the pattern simply does not match, so for load-bearing edits also verify the effect (grep afterwards).
rule: Every failable command in an ebuild phase (sed, cp, mv, ln, rm, touch) must end with || die; greppable and enforceable by a lint script.

problem: sslscan must be linked against a static OpenSSL built with weak/legacy ciphers enabled so it can test them; the system OpenSSL cannot be used (bugs 977230, 895546).
root_cause: The tool's purpose (probing insecure TLS configurations) requires cipher support that distro OpenSSL intentionally disables.
fix_pattern: Pin a specific openssl release tarball in SRC_URI next to the package tarball; ln -s it into the source dir and touch .openssl_is_fresh; reuse dev-libs/openssl's gentoo.config helper for cross-compile target detection (unset APPS SCRIPTS CROSS_COMPILE; tc-export CC AR RANLIB RC); scrub hardcoded -O/-march/-fomit-frame-pointer from the generated Makefile CFLAGS and splice in user CFLAGS/LDFLAGS; backport small upstream fixes (SIGPIPE ignore, missing newline) as FILESDIR patches headed by the upstream commit URL.
rule: When a package needs a specially-configured static copy of a library, pin that library's release tarball in SRC_URI and borrow the reference ebuild's cross-compile and flag-sanitizing machinery rather than reinventing it.
snippet:
OPENSSL_RELEASE_TAG="openssl-3.6.3"
SRC_URI="https://github.com/rbsec/sslscan/archive/${PV}.tar.gz -> ${P}.tar.gz
	https://github.com/openssl/openssl/releases/download/${OPENSSL_RELEASE_TAG}/${OPENSSL_RELEASE_TAG}.tar.gz"
src_prepare() {
	ln -s ../${OPENSSL_RELEASE_TAG} openssl || die
	touch .openssl_is_fresh || die
	...
}

problem: Routine live-ebuild maintenance: upstream dropped lxd-to-incus, so the build loop, dosbin list and QA_PREBUILT entries were pruned in lockstep, plus a Go BDEPEND floor bump.
cmake fails on systems without doxygen because ebuild-passed -DENABLE_DOXYGEN/-DENABLE_MANUAL variables are reported as unused (bug 977904).
root_cause: docs/CMakeLists.txt executes return() when find_package(Doxygen) fails, so the option()/variable evaluation below it never runs, tripping cmake.eclass's fatal check for manually-specified-but-unused variables.
fix_pattern: Patch out the early return() so the remaining variables are still evaluated (components stay disabled anyway), add the patch to PATCHES, and rename the ebuild to -r1 since build behavior changes for existing installs.
rule: When cmake dies with 'manually-specified variables were not used' only on hosts missing an optional tool, look for an early return() in a sub-CMakeLists guarding on that tool and patch it out; revbump the fix.
snippet:
find_package(Doxygen)
 if (NOT Doxygen_FOUND)
   message(STATUS "Doxygen not found - skipping ...")
-  return()
 endif()

problem: New version uses Qt private headers (rhi/qrhi.h via Qt::GuiPrivate) whose ABI changes every Qt minor release, and its tests need an X-less platform plus a real UTF-8 locale.
root_cause: Qt private APIs have no compatibility guarantee, so a package built against qtbase 6.x breaks under 6.y without rebuild; QCollator::setNumericMode is unsupported under the POSIX/C locale so the file-path test fails in sandboxed builds.
fix_pattern: Use a subslot-operator dep with an explanatory comment: dev-qt/qtbase:6=[...] preceded by '# slot op: Uses Qt::GuiPrivate for rhi/qrhi.h'. In src_test export QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen and probe locale -a for en_US.utf8, exporting LC_ALL if present or adding the locale-dependent test to CMAKE_SKIP_TESTS otherwise.
rule: When an app consumes Qt private headers, depend on dev-qt/qtbase:6= (slot operator) with a comment naming the private header, so it is rebuilt on every Qt minor bump.
snippet:
# slot op: Uses Qt::GuiPrivate for rhi/qrhi.h
dev-qt/qtbase:6=[concurrent,dbus,gui,...]

src_test() {
	local -x QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen
	if locale -a | grep -iq "en_US.utf8"; then
		export LC_ALL="en_US.utf8"
	else
		local CMAKE_SKIP_TESTS=( pqt_scriptsfilespaths )
	fi
	cmake_src_test -j1
}

problem: Build fails because $CC is empty inside shell subcommands spawned by make (bug 977581).
root_cause: Passing CC="$(tc-getCC)" as an emake argument sets a make variable, but the Makefile invokes the compiler through subshell constructs that read CC from the process environment, where it was never exported.
fix_pattern: Replace the per-invocation make-variable overrides with tc-export AR CC before emake; tc-export both sets and exports the toolchain variables so subshells see them.
rule: If a build system reads toolchain variables from the environment (in $(shell ...) or helper scripts) rather than as make variables, use tc-export CC AR ... instead of emake CC=... AR=...

problem: USE=bindist license installation broke after upstream moved license texts into a LICENCES/ directory: the dodoc glob LICEN[CS]E.* no longer matched (bug 978064).
root_cause: Upstream commit 1221848f2376 reorganized the license files; a glob-based install in the ebuild silently depends on the old top-level layout, and bindist redistribution legally requires those notices to be installed.
fix_pattern: Switch to recursive directory install matching the new layout: use bindist && dodoc -r WHENCE LICENSES, applied to both the dated release and the live ebuild.
rule: After every bump of a package that installs files by glob, re-verify the globs against the new tarball layout, especially license/notice files whose installation is a bindist compliance requirement.
snippet:
-	use bindist && dodoc WHENCE LICEN[CS]E.*
+	use bindist && dodoc -r WHENCE LICENSES

problem: Bundled lodepng misdetects restrict support on in-development GCC releases like 17.0.
root_cause: The feature gate '(__GNUC__ >= 3) && (__GNUC_MINOR__ >= 1)' ANDs the major and minor checks independently, so any GCC X.0 (minor 0) fails the minor test even though X >= 3; development snapshots of new GCC majors always carry minor 0.
fix_pattern: Patch the check to accept any major above the threshold and only compare the minor at the boundary major, keep it as a FILESDIR patch added to PATCHES.
rule: Compiler version gates of the form (MAJOR >= m && MINOR >= n) break on newer majors with minor 0; rewrite as MAJOR > m || (MAJOR == m && MINOR >= n) — greppable as '__GNUC__.*&&.*__GNUC_MINOR__'.

problem: Enabling python3.15 on a pytest-using package can fail from autoloaded pytest plugins that are not yet ported to the new target.
root_cause: distutils_enable_tests pytest historically autoloaded all installed pytest plugins into the test run; on a freshly-enabled Python target many plugins are still broken or missing.
fix_pattern: Alongside the PYTHON_COMPAT bump, declare the exact plugin set before distutils_enable_tests — an empty array EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() means the tests need no plugins, so autoloading is disabled and the run is hermetic.
rule: When bumping PYTHON_COMPAT to a new target, set EPYTEST_PLUGINS explicitly (empty if none are needed) so pytest plugin autoloading cannot break the test phase.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump adding python3_15, one-line change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump adding python3_15, one-line change.

Tracking five separate submodule tarball hashes (SPIRV/Vulkan headers, dxbc-spirv, libdisplay-info) per dxvk release was fragile and tedious, with a 20-line src_prepare shuffle of rmdir/mv/ln to place them.
root_cause: Upstream vendors dependencies as git submodules and per-submodule pinned SRC_URI tarballs must all be re-resolved on every bump; a curated EGIT_SUBMODULES list also breaks whenever upstream adds/renames a submodule.
fix_pattern: For the live ebuild, drop EGIT_SUBMODULES entirely (fetch all submodules; slightly more data but never breaks). For releases, switch to a single maintainer-repacked tarball including submodules, hosted on the dev's distfiles space with a comment 'tarball is same as upstream except for including git submodules'.
rule: When an upstream needs several git submodules, prefer one repacked all-in-one tarball (and full submodule fetch for live) over per-submodule hash-pinned SRC_URIs that must be updated on every bump.
snippet:
# tarball is same as upstream except for including git submodules
SRC_URI="https://distfiles.gentoo.org/pub/dev/ionen@gentoo.org/${P}.tar.xz"

problem: ffmpeg-compat's slotted old-branch ebuilds drift behind the main ffmpeg package's patchlevels (bug 978041), needing periodic re-sync.
root_cause: Compat package carries the same upstream branches at older majors; without shared ebuild text every ffmpeg fix must be duplicated by hand.
fix_pattern: The ebuild text is written to be byte-identical between media-video/ffmpeg and ffmpeg-compat: all differences (SLOT, doc handling, S=WORKDIR path) key off a PN test, so syncing is a pure file copy plus dropping obsolete versions and Manifest entries.
rule: For pkg/pkg-compat pairs, parameterize a single ebuild text on [[ ${PN} == *-compat ]] so keeping them in sync is a verbatim copy instead of a manual merge.
snippet:
[[ ${PN} == *-compat ]] && FFMPEG_UNSLOTTED= || FFMPEG_UNSLOTTED=1
...
[[ ${FFMPEG_UNSLOTTED} ]] && : 0 || : "$(ver_cut 1)"
SLOT="${_}/${FFMPEG_SUBSLOT}"
S=${WORKDIR}/ffmpeg-${PV} # avoid ${P} for ffmpeg-compat

problem: kde-cli-tools 6.7.1 regained hard requirements on Qt6/KF6 modules that Gentoo's split packaging intentionally makes optional, breaking users (bug 978033).
root_cause: The version bump dropped the downstream-only FILESDIR patch (unrequire-kf-qt-modules) that earlier versions carried; downstream packaging patches never get merged upstream, so dropping them on a bump silently reverts the distro behavior.
fix_pattern: Re-add the patch to PATCHES with a comment marking it as a permanent downstream change: PATCHES=( "${FILESDIR}/${PN}-6.2.4-unrequire-kf-qt-modules.patch" ) # downstream split. Commit message 'Still unrequire...' signals it must be carried forward.
rule: During a version bump, diff the old ebuild's PATCHES against the new one: carry downstream-only patches forever (mark them with a comment) and drop backports only after confirming the fix is in the new release.

problem: A _pre snapshot ebuild carried stable keywords (amd64, arm64, ppc64...) that it should not have had; commit 'destabilizes' it back to ~arch.
root_cause: New prerelease ebuilds are usually created by copying the previous (stable) version's ebuild, silently inheriting its stable KEYWORDS.
fix_pattern: Edit KEYWORDS in the snapshot ebuild, prefixing every stable arch with ~ (keeping explicit -sparc masks as-is): KEYWORDS="~amd64 ~arm ~arm64 ~loong ~ppc ~ppc64 ~riscv -sparc ~x86".
rule: When copying a stable ebuild to create a _pre/_rc/_alpha/_beta or snapshot version, always reset KEYWORDS to testing (~arch); audit for prerelease versions with stable keywords.

problem: Routine security version bump of a C++ package with vendored Rust crates; CRATES list regenerated per version, two release branches maintained in parallel.
Routine security bump adding patch releases on two upstream branches (5.0.x and 5.1.x); no new packaging technique.

Shell-based auditing tool misdetected Gentoo: os-release ID parsing failed on single-quoted values, nftables built into the kernel (not a module) was reported missing, and kernels in /boot/EFI/Gentoo/ were not found.
root_cause: Upstream tool assumed other distros' conventions (double-quoted os-release, nftables as loadable module, vmlinuz directly in /boot).
fix_pattern: Carry the not-yet-merged upstream PR as a files/ patch with the PR URL recorded in the commit/patch header, add it via PATCHES=(), and revbump to -r1 because installed file content changes for the same version.
rule: When a distro-compatibility fix exists as an open upstream PR, ship the PR's patch in files/ with provenance noted and revbump the ebuild; drop it once upstream releases it.

problem: Plain version bump adding 2.0.7 by copying the previous ebuild; nothing generalizable.

Package used the llvm-r2 eclass (LLVM_COMPAT + get_llvm_prefix) even though each version hard-pins exactly one LLVM slot equal to its own SLOT, causing problems selecting the LLVM install (bug 977919).
root_cause: llvm-r2's slot-selection machinery is overkill and can pick the wrong prefix when a package only ever supports a single LLVM major version tied to its SLOT.
fix_pattern: Drop 'inherit llvm-r2' and LLVM_COMPAT; point the build directly at the slotted LLVM: pass -DLLVM_ROOT="${ESYSROOT}/usr/lib/llvm/${SLOT}" for dependency lookup (ESYSROOT = cross-correct) and -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX="${EPREFIX}/usr/lib/llvm/${SLOT}" for install.
rule: When a package supports exactly one LLVM slot (SLOT == LLVM major), skip the llvm-r2 eclass and inline LLVM_ROOT/CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX against the slotted /usr/lib/llvm/${SLOT} path, using ESYSROOT for lookups and EPREFIX for install.

problem: RDEPEND allowed '|| ( net-firewall/iptables net-firewall/nftables[json] )' but incus actually requires nftables with JSON support; users satisfying the || with iptables got a broken runtime (bug 978032).
root_cause: Any-of (||) runtime dependencies express 'either works', but upstream in fact needed one specific backend/feature; the || silently let the wrong alternative satisfy the dep.
fix_pattern: Replace the || group with the single hard dependency net-firewall/nftables[json], revbump the release ebuild to -r1 so the corrected dep propagates to installed systems, and apply the same change to the 9999 live ebuild.
rule: When a runtime works only with one specific provider/feature, replace any-of || dependency groups with the concrete atom (including USE deps) and revbump; always mirror dependency fixes into the live ebuild.

problem: Plain version bump; new ebuild is a copy with existing verify-sig/QA_EXECSTACK idioms, no new technique.
Compile-time feature toggles keyed on has_version '<sys-kernel/linux-headers-X' misbehaved under cross-compilation.
root_cause: Bare has_version queries ROOT (the installed runtime root, -r default); build-time decisions about headers/libraries the compiler will see must query the DEPEND domain (ESYSROOT), which differs when cross-compiling.
fix_pattern: Add -d to every has_version call that gates compile-time options, so the check runs against ESYSROOT where build-time deps (linux-headers) are actually installed.
rule: Any has_version/best_version used in src_* to decide compile-time features against headers or libraries must use -d (ESYSROOT); use -b only for build-host tools; bare calls are only correct for runtime-root questions.

problem: Package appeared to build fine against new libplanarity-5, but bug 977995 revealed configure had silently fallen back to a bundled planarity-4.x copy, so the 'port' was never actually tested against the system library.
root_cause: Build system ships bundled copies of its C dependencies and falls back to them without failing when the external-library check does not pass, masking incompatibilities.
fix_pattern: Revbump to -r2: pin the dep to >=sci-mathematics/planarity-5:=, add a files/ patch porting the code and the m4 check to the new API, run eautoreconf, and 'belt and suspenders' rm -r the bundled extern/ copies in src_prepare so any future silent fallback becomes a hard build failure.
rule: When a build system can silently fall back to bundled third-party libraries, delete the bundled directories in src_prepare (rm -r ... || die) so misdetection fails loudly, and pin the minimum version of the external dep your patches target.
snippet:
src_prepare() {
	# belt and suspenders
	rm -r extern/bliss-0.73 \
		extern/edge-addition-planarity-suite-Version_4.0.0.0 \
		|| die
	default
	eautoreconf
}

problem: Routine snapshot bump of linux-firmware; ebuild copied unchanged.
Upstream silently replaced the already-published release tarball in place (to fix a test-script shebang/line endings), so the Manifest hash no longer matched what GitHub served.
root_cause: Upstream re-rolled a release artifact under the same filename; Gentoo distfiles must be immutable, and just updating the Manifest hash would break users/mirrors that have the old file.
fix_pattern: Rename the fetched distfile with a local revision suffix via SRC_URI arrow (-> ${P}-r2.tar.gz) so the new tarball gets a distinct DIST entry, revbump the ebuild, and drop the now-unneeded src_test workaround the new tarball fixed.
rule: When upstream replaces a release tarball in place, never just refresh the Manifest hash: re-fetch under a renamed distfile using 'SRC_URI=... -> ${P}-rN.tar.gz' and revbump the ebuild.

problem: mesa's opencl dependencies still referenced llvm-core/libclc after the package moved to the llvm-runtimes/ category, in three separate places (llvm_gen_dep block, plain RDEPEND, and CLC_DEPSTRING for BDEPEND).
root_cause: A package category move (pkgmove) upstream is not automatically reflected in consumers' dependency strings; each occurrence must be chased manually and the same atom often appears in multiple dep variables of one ebuild.
fix_pattern: grep the ebuild(s) for the old category atom and rewrite every occurrence (llvm-core/libclc -> llvm-runtimes/libclc), preserving version pins and USE deps like [spirv(-)].
rule: After a package is moved between categories, grep your whole overlay for the old category/pn atom and update every dependency occurrence; do not assume one dep variable per ebuild.

problem: Same libclc category-move dependency fix as mesa (aa59ab61): llvm-core/libclc -> llvm-runtimes/libclc in the llvm_gen_dep block.
Plain version bump to 26.1.3 (new ebuild copy); nothing generalizable in the commit itself.
The existing CMake-4 compatibility patch bumped cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.0) to 3.5, which still caused problems (bug 964517) and diverged from what upstream adopted.
root_cause: A bare cmake_minimum_required bump changes policy behavior for all CMake versions; the version-range form is the proper fix because it keeps old-version compatibility while declaring policies up to a newer CMake.
fix_pattern: Update the patch to the range syntax cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.0...3.10), matching the fix upstream merged, and add a 'Source: <upstream commit URL>' header line to the patch; reference both bugs in the ebuild comment. No revbump needed since build output is unchanged.
rule: For CMake 4 'compatibility with versions < 3.5 removed' failures, patch cmake_minimum_required to the range form (e.g. 3.5...3.10 or whatever upstream chose) instead of a bare bump, and record patch provenance with a Source: header.

problem: Version bump of a Go package whose Makefile embeds the release commit hash; a stale hash would misreport the version, and obsolete build options needed dropping.
root_cause: runc's build embeds COMMIT= into the binary for 'runc --version'; the pinned RUNC_COMMIT variable in the ebuild must be refreshed from the release tag on every bump.
fix_pattern: Keep a commented RUNC_COMMIT=<sha> variable at the top of the ebuild ('update on bump, look for commit ID on release tag') and pass it via emake COMMIT="${RUNC_COMMIT}"; on bump, resolve the tag to its commit and update the variable, and prune emake options upstream removed.
rule: For Go/Make builds that embed a VCS commit for --version output, pin the release tag's commit in an ebuild variable with an 'update on bump' comment and pass it to the build; refresh it every bump.
snippet:
# update on bump, look for commit ID on release tag.
RUNC_COMMIT=bb14dabeb7185bb72c8c86735d090dcb20f36587
...
myemakeargs=( BUILDTAGS="${options[*]}" COMMIT="${RUNC_COMMIT}" )

problem: pkgcheck's RDEPEND still listed dev-python/chardet although upstream had switched to charset-normalizer, so the real runtime dependency was missing.
root_cause: Upstream swapped a Python dependency between releases and the ebuild's hand-maintained RDEPEND was not re-checked against upstream metadata (pyproject/setup deps) at bump time.
fix_pattern: Correct the atom (dev-python/chardet -> dev-python/charset-normalizer[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]), revbump the release ebuild to -r1 so installed systems pull the new dep, and apply the same change to the 9999 ebuild without revbump.
rule: On every Python package bump, diff the ebuild's *DEPEND against upstream's declared dependencies; pure runtime-dependency corrections require a revbump on release ebuilds (but not on live ones).

problem: Dropped IUSE=capi and its optional net-libs/libcapi runtime dep across all versions because libcapi is being removed from the tree (bug 977907); same cleanup theme as wine-staging 3b60bf39.
net-libs/libcapi was being last-rited (bug 977907), so wine's capi USE flag would soon reference a nonexistent dependency.
root_cause: A tree-cleaned dependency package forces all consumers to remove the optional feature; leaving configure autodetection could silently pick up a stale system copy.
fix_pattern: In every affected version and the 9999 ebuild: remove capi from IUSE, delete the capi? ( net-libs/libcapi:= ) dep, replace $(use_with capi) with a hard --without-capi annotated with the bug number, and delete the flag's <flag> entry from metadata.xml; no revbump for a feature that defaults off and is going away.
rule: When a dependency is last-rited, strip the USE flag from all consumer ebuilds and pin the feature off explicitly in configure (--without-foo #bugno) instead of leaving autodetection, and clean metadata.xml in the same commit.

problem: Same IUSE=capi removal as wine-staging (3b60bf39) applied to the wine-vanilla variant.
eza unconditionally depended on dev-libs/openssl although openssl-sys is only pulled in through the git feature (via libgit2), forcing an unneeded dep on USE=-git builds (bug 977175).
root_cause: For Rust packages, system-library deps enter through -sys crates that are often only activated by specific cargo features; declaring them unconditionally overstates DEPEND.
fix_pattern: Move dev-libs/openssl inside the git? ( ) conditional next to libgit2; since the change was done as a new -r1 ebuild it re-entered ~arch keywords for re-stabilization.
rule: For cargo packages, gate each system-library dependency on the USE flag that enables the cargo feature pulling its -sys crate (check the feature graph in Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock), rather than depending unconditionally.

problem: Build failed to detect python correctly on systems without sys-apps/which: configure located python-config by calling 'which' (bug 977972).
root_cause: Autoconf script shells out to 'which python*-config'; 'which' is no longer guaranteed present on Gentoo, and heuristic lookup may also find the wrong python slot's config script.
fix_pattern: Export PYTHON_CONFIG from the python eclass helper before econf so configure's own lookup (and its 'which' call) is bypassed entirely: use python && export PYTHON_CONFIG="$(python_get_PYTHON_CONFIG)".
rule: When a configure script probes for python-config (especially via which), pre-seed it by exporting PYTHON_CONFIG=$(python_get_PYTHON_CONFIG) in src_configure; more generally, export cache/override variables instead of patching probe logic.

problem: Existing release needed enabling for a new CPython (3.15) while retiring old implementations.
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT is a whitelist; new interpreter versions must be added per-package after tests pass, and EOL impls (python3_11, pypy3_11) are dropped opportunistically in the same edit.
fix_pattern: Edit PYTHON_COMPAT in place from '( pypy3_11 python3_{11..14} )' to '( python3_{12..15} )' with no revbump: widening/narrowing PYTHON_COMPAT is not a revbump event because installed content for existing users is unchanged until they re-emerge for the new target.
rule: Enable a new Python implementation by editing PYTHON_COMPAT in the existing ebuild (after verifying tests) without a revision bump, and drop EOL implementations in the same change.

problem: Same PYTHON_COMPAT widening to py3.15 as python-snappy (4c536a97); routine no-revbump edit.
acct-user ebuild manually appended DEPEND+=/RDEPEND+= on its acct-group after calling acct-user_add_deps, duplicating what the eclass now does
root_cause: acct-user.eclass was updated so acct-user_add_deps automatically generates dependencies on every group listed in ACCT_USER_GROUPS; the hand-written appends predate that and became dead boilerplate
fix_pattern: Delete the manual 'DEPEND+=" acct-group/foo "' and 'RDEPEND+=" acct-group/foo "' lines; keep only ACCT_USER_GROUPS=( foo ) followed by acct-user_add_deps, which emits the group deps itself
rule: When an eclass grows automatic dependency generation (acct-user_add_deps from ACCT_USER_GROUPS), drop any manual DEPEND/RDEPEND appends that duplicate it instead of carrying them forward
snippet:
ACCT_USER_GROUPS=( vdradmin )
acct-user_add_deps
# removed:
# DEPEND+=" acct-group/vdradmin "
# RDEPEND+=" acct-group/vdradmin "

problem: Same acct-user cleanup as 5e00a46f: dropped redundant manual acct-group DEPEND/RDEPEND appends now handled by acct-user_add_deps
Same acct-user cleanup as 5e00a46f: dropped redundant manual acct-group/maradns DEPEND/RDEPEND appends now handled by the eclass
Variant of 5e00a46f: dropped explicit RDEPEND+=" acct-user/root" user dependency now generated by acct-user.eclass
Same acct-user cleanup as 5e00a46f: dropped redundant manual acct-group/qmail DEPEND/RDEPEND appends now handled by the eclass

Package broke when a newer version of its dependency (data-default-class-0.2) entered the tree: an upstream package split made this compat shim incompatible, but the unbounded ':=' dep still forced rebuilds against the new version
root_cause: RDEPEND had no upper bound even though upstream (Hackage) itself declares a <0.2 constraint, so slot-operator rebuilds pulled in the incompatible dep version
fix_pattern: Add an upper bound matching the constraint upstream already publishes, keep the slot operator, and leave a comment naming the breaking version and the issue link; done without revbump because the package was already fully broken (working-but-suboptimal siblings would need a revbump for the same dep change)
rule: When a dependency's new version breaks a package and upstream metadata already declares a version cap, mirror that cap as an upper bound in the ebuild dep with an explanatory comment; dep changes on an already-broken ebuild need no revbump, but on a working installed package they do
snippet:
# data-default-class-0.2 requires data-default-0.8 which then breaks this.
# https://github.com/gentoo-haskell/gentoo-haskell/issues/1783
RDEPEND="<dev-haskell/data-default-class-0.2:=[profile?]
	>=dev-lang/ghc-7.4.1:=
"

problem: Documentation silently stopped being installed after a previous commit added shell completions
root_cause: Defining python_install_all() in a distutils-r1 ebuild replaces the default phase implementation, and the default is what calls einstalldocs; the override only installed completions, so docs were dropped
fix_pattern: Call einstalldocs explicitly as the first line of the overridden python_install_all(); same applies to any src_install/*_install_all override in eclasses whose default phase installs docs
rule: Whenever you override python_install_all (or any phase whose eclass default runs einstalldocs), call einstalldocs inside the override or documentation is silently lost

problem: Package not yet available for the newly added CPython 3.15 target
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT capped at python3_14 from before the new interpreter was added to the tree
fix_pattern: Extend the brace range in PYTHON_COMPAT after test-running under the new interpreter; sibling commits in the same batch show the companion hygiene of dropping EOL/unsupported impls (python3_11, pypy3_11) in the same one-line edit, e.g. 'pypy3_11 python3_{11..14}' -> 'python3_{12..15}' (cftime, cramjam, numexpr, textdistance); no revbump needed since the change only widens availability
rule: When a new CPython target lands, bump the PYTHON_COMPAT brace range after testing, and use the same edit to drop implementations that left the supported set; PYTHON_COMPAT-only changes do not need a revbump

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_15 (rust/maturin-style package, no other changes); see 35464ffe for the pattern
Routine py3.15 enablement that also dropped pypy3_11 and python3_11 in the same PYTHON_COMPAT edit; see 35464ffe
Routine py3.15 enablement, dropped pypy3_11/python3_11 in same edit; see 35464ffe
py3.15 enablement that also added EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() before distutils_enable_tests pytest to disable pytest plugin autoloading; see 5d7ad2f6 for the full EPYTEST_PLUGINS lesson
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_15; see 35464ffe
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_15; see 35464ffe
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_15 with python3_11 dropped; see 35464ffe
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_15; see 35464ffe
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_15 with python3_11 dropped; see 35464ffe
Routine py3.15 enablement, dropped pypy3_11/python3_11 in same edit; see 35464ffe
py3.15 enablement that also added EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() to disable pytest plugin autoloading; see 5d7ad2f6
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_15 (maturin/rust package); see 35464ffe
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_15; see 35464ffe
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_15; see 35464ffe
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_15; see 35464ffe

Test suite pulled pytest plugins (hypothesis) via manual BDEPEND while relying on pytest's implicit plugin autoloading, which is fragile when new Python targets or unrelated installed plugins change behavior
root_cause: Older distutils-r1 idiom listed pytest-plugin packages by hand in test? () BDEPEND; the eclass now supports EPYTEST_PLUGINS, which both generates those BDEPENDs and runs pytest with plugin autoloading disabled, loading only the named plugins
fix_pattern: Move plugin packages out of BDEPEND into EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( hypothesis ) declared before distutils_enable_tests pytest; sibling commits (dictdiffer 158c22b8, opt-einsum 06620f24) show EPYTEST_PLUGINS=() for suites needing no plugins, so nothing autoloads; non-plugin test deps (numpy) stay in BDEPEND
rule: In distutils-r1 ebuilds, declare pytest plugins via EPYTEST_PLUGINS (empty array if none) before distutils_enable_tests pytest instead of hand-written BDEPEND entries, so plugin autoloading is disabled and only declared plugins run
snippet:
BDEPEND="
	test? (
		dev-python/numpy[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]
	)
"
EPYTEST_PLUGINS=( hypothesis )
distutils_enable_tests pytest

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_15; see 35464ffe
Enabling py3.15 exposed a doctest that fails when the optional scipy dependency is absent for the new impl.
root_cause: Test suite exercises optional-dependency code paths; scipy is not (yet) available for every impl in PYTHON_COMPAT, so tests fail on impls where it is missing.
fix_pattern: Add a python_test() that builds EPYTEST_DESELECT conditionally with has_version on the optional dep, then calls epytest; bump PYTHON_COMPAT range in the same commit.
rule: When tests depend on an optional package that may be missing for some Python impls, deselect those tests conditionally via has_version inside python_test instead of adding the dep or restricting tests.
snippet:
python_test() {
	local EPYTEST_DESELECT=()
	if ! has_version "dev-python/scipy[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]"; then
		EPYTEST_DESELECT+=( doc/user_guide.rst::user_guide.rst )
	fi
	epytest
}

problem: llvm-r1 eclass use forced LLVM_SLOT USE flags that needed constant flag-masking because recent libclc releases only ever support one LLVM version.
root_cause: llvm-r1 multi-slot machinery (LLVM_COMPAT + llvm_gen_dep + llvm-r1_pkg_setup) is overhead when exactly one LLVM slot works; the flags become pure noise.
fix_pattern: Drop llvm-r1 inherit, LLVM_COMPAT and llvm-r1_pkg_setup; depend directly on llvm-core/clang:${LLVM_MAJOR} and pass -DLLVM_ROOT="${ESYSROOT}/usr/lib/llvm/${LLVM_MAJOR}" in mycmakeargs since the eclass no longer sets up PATH/prefix.
rule: When a package genuinely supports only one LLVM slot per release, replace llvm-r1 USE-flag plumbing with a direct slotted clang/llvm dep plus an explicit -DLLVM_ROOT pointing into ESYSROOT.

problem: Live ffmpeg ebuild drifted from upstream: library SONAMEs bumped, a header dep minimum rose, and some ./configure switches (libnpp, omx) were removed upstream.
root_cause: Upstream major-version churn changes ABI (SONAME) and the configure option set; the ebuild encodes both (subslot, --disable flags).
fix_pattern: Update the SONAME-derived subslot variable (FFMPEG_SUBSLOT=61.63.63), raise the min version of the header-only dep, delete --disable flags for options upstream removed, and add --disable for new autodetected libs; note not-yet-packaged deps in a bug instead of blocking.
rule: When bumping a library package whose ABI changed, re-derive the subslot from the new SONAMEs and re-audit every configure switch against upstream's current configure help, dropping removed ones and explicitly disabling new autodetections.

problem: Package still listed python3_11 while the tree was retiring the py3.11 target.
root_cause: Treewide removal of an old Python implementation requires every PYTHON_COMPAT to raise its lower bound before the target is dropped from profiles.
fix_pattern: Shrink the brace range: PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{11..14} ) -> python3_{12..14} ). No revbump needed since installed behavior for remaining impls is unchanged.
rule: When a Python implementation is scheduled for removal, bump the lower bound of the PYTHON_COMPAT brace range in place, without a revbump.

problem: Routine py3.11 retirement: PYTHON_COMPAT lower bound raised, same pattern as media-video/vcsi in this batch.
Routine py3.11 retirement: PYTHON_COMPAT lower bound raised, same pattern as media-video/vcsi in this batch.
Same RUST_MAX_VER pin as the firefox 140.12.0 commit in this batch (Mozilla ESR shares the toolchain constraint).
Firefox ESR builds broke or risked breaking when portage selected a Rust newer than the LLVM versions the ebuild supports (LLVM_COMPAT=( 20 21 )).
root_cause: With RUST_NEEDS_LLVM=1 the rust toolchain links against LLVM; a rust built on an LLVM outside LLVM_COMPAT produces mixed-LLVM breakage, and rust.eclass picks the newest installed rust unless capped.
fix_pattern: Set RUST_MAX_VER alongside RUST_MIN_VER to the newest Rust release built against a supported LLVM slot, and document that the max tracks LLVM_COMPAT.
rule: In any ebuild with RUST_NEEDS_LLVM=1 and a pinned LLVM_COMPAT (Firefox/Thunderbird forks especially), cap RUST_MAX_VER at the last Rust version matching the supported LLVM slots whenever a newer Rust lands in the tree.
snippet:
LLVM_COMPAT=( 20 21 )
RUST_NEEDS_LLVM=1
# Minimum version required to build.
# Max version supported by LLVM_COMPAT.
RUST_MIN_VER=1.82.0
RUST_MAX_VER=1.94.1

problem: Shell-completion generation added earlier silently required dev-python/shtab at build time; build failed where it was absent, and ppc had no shtab keyword.
root_cause: Upstream conditionally imports shtab (--print-completion only exists if importable); the ebuild ran that code in python_compile_all without declaring the dep, so success depended on what happened to be installed.
fix_pattern: Add BDEPEND="dev-python/shtab[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]" and drop ~ppc from KEYWORDS because the new dep is not keyworded there.
rule: When a build step invokes the package's own CLI feature that conditionally imports an optional module, declare that module in BDEPEND, and dekeyword any arch where the new dep lacks keywords.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT widening to python3_15 after testing; one-line brace-range bump.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT widening to python3_15; one-line brace-range bump.
Dekeyworded ~loong ~sparc across all versions because a reverse dependency (tox) needs consistent keyword coverage down its dep chain; routine keyword hygiene.
Cross-compilation looked up libsepol/libselinux libraries in ${ROOT}usr/$(get_libdir), which is wrong for cross builds (bug 638286).
root_cause: ROOT is where the package gets installed at runtime-merge time, not where build-time link inputs live; for cross builds compile/link inputs of DEPEND live in ESYSROOT.
fix_pattern: Replace ${ROOT:-/} with ${ESYSROOT}/ in the sed that injects lib_dirs into setup.py, in both release and live ebuilds.
rule: Any path an ebuild feeds to the compiler or linker (include dirs, lib dirs) must be prefixed with ESYSROOT (or BROOT for build-tool assets), never ROOT; ROOT is only for pkg_* phase runtime paths.

problem: A gui USE flag pulled heavy PyQt6/pygraphviz deps that only gate pure-Python runtime imports, inflating the flag matrix for no build-time difference.
root_cause: For pure-Python optional features nothing changes at build time, so a USE flag only forces rebuilds and dep-graph churn; optfeature conveys the same information at install time.
fix_pattern: Drop IUSE=gui and its RDEPEND block; add pkg_postinst with optfeature naming the runtime deps (dev-python/pyqt6[gui,widgets], dev-python/pygraphviz); keep the GUI deps only under test? in BDEPEND where the test suite needs them.
rule: When a USE flag only toggles runtime-import Python dependencies without changing what gets built, replace it with optfeature in pkg_postinst.

problem: systemd-261 failed to build with USE=bpf when the BPF objects were compiled with GCC's BPF backend (-Dbpf_compiler=gcc): static asserts on a hand-mirrored .bss struct layout fired.
root_cause: bpftool gen skeleton emits the bss struct in the physical order the compiler placed globals; clang preserves declaration order but gcc reorders .bss, so upstream's mirror-struct offsets (and a hardcoded offset-0 store) were wrong under gcc.
fix_pattern: Backport the upstream PR as files/261-gcc-bpf.patch (deriving the field offset from the generated skeleton via offsetof instead of a mirror struct) and append it to PATCHES in the existing -r1 ebuild without a further revbump, since only broken (unbuildable) configurations are affected.
rule: A patch that only fixes a build failure can be added to an existing revision without revbumping, because no one can have a broken install of it; keep upstream bug/PR URLs at the top of the patch file.

problem: New tor release must be verified although upstream signs a sha256sum sidecar file, not the tarball itself.
root_cause: Some upstreams (tor) publish tarball + signed checksum file; plain verify-sig usage expects a detached signature on the artifact.
fix_pattern: Fetch .sha256sum and .sha256sum.asc under verify-sig? in SRC_URI, then in src_unpack verify the signature on the checksum file with verify-sig_verify_detached and verify the tarball against it with verify-sig_verify_unsigned_checksums; also gate KEYWORDS on non-prerelease PV patterns.
rule: When upstream signs a checksum file instead of the tarball, chain verify-sig_verify_detached on the checksum file with verify-sig_verify_unsigned_checksums for the tarball inside src_unpack.
snippet:
if use verify-sig; then
	cd "${DISTDIR}" || die
	verify-sig_verify_detached ${MY_PF}.tar.gz.sha256sum{,.asc}
	verify-sig_verify_unsigned_checksums \
		${MY_PF}.tar.gz.sha256sum sha256 ${MY_PF}.tar.gz
	cd "${WORKDIR}" || die
fi

problem: Custom src_prepare() { default; cabal-mksetup; } silently skipped the haskell-cabal eclass phase, so updated cabal revisions were never applied (bug 968626).
root_cause: Defining a phase function in the ebuild replaces the eclass-exported phase entirely; calling `default` runs the EAPI default, not haskell-cabal_src_prepare, and cabal-mksetup was redundant anyway since haskell-cabal_src_configure calls it.
fix_pattern: Delete the src_prepare override so the eclass phase runs, and revbump out of caution because the skipped phase does substantial work.
rule: Never override an eclass-exported phase with `default` plus extra steps; either call the eclass phase function explicitly (eclass_src_prepare) or drop the override, and revbump when restoring skipped eclass logic changes the installed result.

problem: Perl virtual pointed at =dev-lang/perl-5.42* while the module version is actually provided by perl 5.44; one-line provider version fix, perl-core maintenance routine.
Dual-life module version numbers in the perl 5.44.0-RC1 ebuild's src_remove_dual table had to be synced to the versions actually bundled; routine perl release-candidate bookkeeping.
Package shipped no shell completions although upstream can generate them via its own CLI (--print-completion), bug 976076.
root_cause: Completions are produced by executing the just-built tool, which is not directly runnable when cross-compiling; naive $(command --print-completion) breaks cross builds.
fix_pattern: Inherit shell-completion and sysroot eclasses; in python_compile_all run the tool through sysroot_try_run_prefixed with PYTHONPATH pointing at the source tree, generating bash and zsh completions, then install with newbashcomp/newzshcomp in python_install_all.
rule: To install completions that only the built program can emit, generate them in compile phase via sysroot_try_run_prefixed (cross-safe) and install them with the shell-completion eclass helpers.

problem: Dev-space distfile URL moved from https://dev.gentoo.org/~user/distfiles/ to https://distfiles.gentoo.org/pub/dev/user@gentoo.org/; infra migration of self-hosted tarballs.
Stable-branch KDE release needed several upstream bugfix cherry-picks plus a dependency correction (kscreenlocker had wrongly become conditional).
root_cause: Between upstream point releases, accumulated fixes are too many for files/ patches; and dep pruning had removed a library still required for a DBus interface.
fix_pattern: Package the cherry-picks as a versioned patchset tarball (${P}-patchset-1.tar.xz) hosted on dev space, referenced via a PATCHSET variable appended to SRC_URI and applied from ${WORKDIR}; revbump to -r2 and restore the unconditional dependency.
rule: When carrying multiple upstream cherry-picks, ship them as a versioned patchset tarball referenced by a PATCHSET variable and revbump; bump only the patchset suffix (-patchset-1, -2, ...) on later additions.

problem: Routine portage version bump to 3.0.81 closing a batch of bugs; new ebuild is a copy of the previous template, nothing new to generalize.
Live (9999) ebuild failed because autotools was inherited in the release branch of the PV conditional instead of the live branch that actually runs eautoreconf.
root_cause: Release tarballs ship pre-generated configure so they don't need autotools, while a git checkout does; the conditional inherit was attached to the wrong side of the [[ ${PV} == 9999 ]] branch.
fix_pattern: Move `autotools` into the 9999 branch: `inherit git-r3 autotools` there, and only `inherit verify-sig` in the release branch.
rule: Inherit autotools in the branch that runs eautoreconf (usually only the live/git branch); verify each conditional inherit matches where its functions are called.
snippet:
if [[ ${PV} == 9999 ]] ; then
	EGIT_REPO_URI="https://github.com/nghttp2/nghttp2.git"
	inherit git-r3 autotools
else
	inherit verify-sig
fi

problem: make_desktop_entry called with the entry name as a bare second positional argument, which is invalid under the EAPI-9 desktop.eclass API.
root_cause: EAPI 9 changed make_desktop_entry from positional arguments to option flags; the display name must now be passed with -n.
fix_pattern: Fix the call in place (no revbump needed for a metadata-generation fix in an unreleased-behavior migration): make_desktop_entry ${PN} -n Pengupop.
rule: When migrating an ebuild to EAPI 9, convert make_desktop_entry positional name/icon/category arguments to their option-flag forms (-n for name), and grep for all call sites.

problem: After bumping ebuilds to EAPI 9, old positional-argument make_desktop_entry calls are wrong: the name argument must now be passed with -n.
root_cause: desktop.eclass changed the make_desktop_entry API in EAPI 9 from positional args (exec name icon type fields) to option flags (-n name, -i icon, ...).
fix_pattern: Edit each call in place (no revbump needed, output identical): 'make_desktop_entry ${PN} "Atari 800 emulator"' becomes 'make_desktop_entry ${PN} -n "Atari 800 emulator"'.
rule: When migrating an ebuild to EAPI 9, convert every make_desktop_entry call to the new flag syntax (-n for name, -i for icon) instead of positional arguments.

problem: EAPI-9 ebuild still used positional make_desktop_entry arguments for both name and icon.
root_cause: Same EAPI 9 desktop.eclass API change: name and icon are now flags, not positional args.
fix_pattern: Convert both positional args to flags: name gets -n, icon path gets -i; fixed in place without revbump since generated .desktop content is unchanged.
rule: In EAPI 9, make_desktop_entry icon arguments must be passed via -i (and name via -n); grep for calls with more than one non-flag argument when migrating.

problem: Java package tests fail on modern JVMs (17+) due to module encapsulation, and full test suite needs many unpackaged deps.
root_cause: JPMS strong encapsulation blocks reflective access to java.base internals; some test deps (spring, hibernate, testkit) are not in the tree.
fix_pattern: In src_prepare, query the active JVM with java-config -g PROVIDES_VERSION and conditionally add --add-opens to JAVA_TEST_EXTRA_ARGS; RESTRICT=test with a comment block listing every missing package so future maintainers know why.
rule: When Java tests need reflective access on JDK 17+, gate --add-opens flags on ver_test of PROVIDES_VERSION instead of hardcoding them; when tests need unpackaged deps, RESTRICT=test and enumerate the missing packages in a comment.
snippet:
local vm_version="$(java-config -g PROVIDES_VERSION)"
if ver_test "${vm_version}" -ge 17; then
	JAVA_TEST_EXTRA_ARGS+=( --add-opens=java.base/java.{io,lang,math,util}=ALL-UNNAMED )
fi

problem: Freshly-bumped curl 8.20.0 busy-loops (high CPU) in event-based processing with the multi API.
root_cause: Upstream bug: wakeup socketpair events were consumed in curl_multi_poll/wait but not in curl_multi_socket event processing, causing busy loops.
fix_pattern: Copy ebuild to -r1 revbump, add the exact upstream commit as files/curl-8.20.0-cpu-use.patch with provenance URLs (Debian bug, curl-distros ML post, upstream commit URL) at the top of the patch, append to PATCHES.
rule: When upstream recommends a post-release fix for a regression, backport the upstream commit verbatim as a FILESDIR patch with source URLs in the patch header and revbump, rather than waiting for the next release.

problem: Routine pypi bump, but distfile authenticity needs verification and one test fails in sandbox.
root_cause: pypi.eclass supports sigstore/provenance attestation via PYPI_VERIFY_REPO; gpg-agent-dependent tests fail in the ebuild environment.
fix_pattern: Set PYPI_VERIFY_REPO=https://github.com/pypa/setuptools-scm before inherit pypi so the .provenance file is fetched and checked (shows up as extra DIST entries); deselect the gpg-signed-commit test with EPYTEST_DESELECT.
rule: For pypi packages whose upstream publishes attestations, set PYPI_VERIFY_REPO to the source repo to get provenance verification for free; deselect (not RESTRICT) individual sandbox-incompatible tests like gpg-agent ones.

problem: Package sits in the wrong category (llvm-core) for what it actually is (a runtime library), requiring a tree-wide move.
root_cause: Category semantics drifted; libclc is a runtime library so belongs in llvm-runtimes.
fix_pattern: Atomic pkgmove commit: git mv the package dir, add 'move llvm-core/libclc llvm-runtimes/libclc' to profiles/updates/<quarter> (e.g. 2Q-2026), update every reverse dependency's dep atoms (mesa, mesa_clc), update stabilization-groups file and profiles/arch masks referencing the old atom.
rule: A package move must update, in one commit: the package dir, profiles/updates/<quarter> move entry, all revdep atoms, and any profiles/ or metadata files (masks, stabilization groups) that name the old category/package.

problem: cmake was installing emacs-mode files into /usr/share/emacs that are supposed to be provided (byte-compiled, with sitefile) by the separate app-emacs/cmake-mode package; a stale unused SITEFILE variable lingered in the ebuild.
root_cause: Leftover from an earlier refactor that split the emacs mode into its own package but never removed the duplicate install from the main ebuild.
fix_pattern: Delete the files in src_install with rm -r "${ED}"/usr/share/emacs || die plus an explanatory comment, drop the dead SITEFILE var and files/50cmake-gentoo.el; deliberately no revbump, with the rationale (heavy build, frequent releases, no file collision) stated in the commit message.
rule: When content is split into a companion package, prune it from the original with an rm in src_install and remove leftover variables; skipping the revbump is acceptable only when there is no collision or runtime breakage, and the reason should be documented.
snippet:
# Part of app-emacs/cmake-mode instead
rm -r "${ED}"/usr/share/emacs || die

problem: Build fails with USE=-moodbar: a member variable (seekbar_mode_) and header include were wrapped in #if defined(HAVE_MOODBAR)||defined(HAVE_WAVEFORM) but used unconditionally elsewhere.
root_cause: Upstream only tested the all-features-on configuration; ifdef guards excluded a declaration that non-guarded code depends on.
fix_pattern: Backport the already-merged upstream PR as a FILESDIR patch (patch header carries the upstream PR URL), moving the declaration out of the ifdef; add to PATCHES=, no revbump since it only fixes a build failure.
rule: When a USE-flag-off combination fails to compile because upstream ifdef guards are too broad, backport or write a minimal patch un-guarding the shared declaration and cite the upstream PR URL in the patch header; build-failure-only fixes need no revbump.
snippet:
-#if defined(HAVE_MOODBAR) || defined(HAVE_WAVEFORM)
   SeekbarMode seekbar_mode_;
-
+#if defined(HAVE_MOODBAR) || defined(HAVE_WAVEFORM)

problem: With USE=-gtk the package still built/required GTK because the ebuild only passed -DENABLE_GTK=ON in the use-enabled branch and passed nothing in the else branch.
root_cause: Upstream flipped the CMake option default from OFF to ON; the ebuild had silently relied on the old upstream default instead of setting the option explicitly in both branches.
fix_pattern: Add the explicit negative to the else branch: mycmakeargs+=(-DENABLE_GTK=OFF -DINSTALL_DESKTOP=OFF).
rule: Never rely on an upstream CMake option default for the disabled case of a USE flag: always pass an explicit ON/OFF for both branches (or use $(cmake_use ...) helpers), because upstream defaults change between releases.
snippet:
if use gtk ; then
	mycmakeargs+=(-DGTK_BUILD_TYPE=GTK3 -DENABLE_GTK=ON)
else
	mycmakeargs+=(-DENABLE_GTK=OFF -DINSTALL_DESKTOP=OFF)
fi

problem: Package broke when LLVM 22 was allowed by LLVM_COMPAT before upstream supported it (bug 977946).
root_cause: New LLVM major slots landing in the tree get picked up by llvm-r2 ranges optimistically; upstream instrumentation passes lag behind LLVM API churn.
fix_pattern: Shrink the compat range in place: LLVM_COMPAT=( {15..22} ) -> ( {15..21} ); no revbump needed because it only narrows the acceptable build toolchain.
rule: When a new LLVM slot breaks a package using llvm-r2, drop that slot from LLVM_COMPAT immediately (in place, no revbump) and re-add it once upstream catches up.

problem: USE=doc build fails (bug 977903) because doxygen documentation generation invokes graphviz's dot which was not depended on.
root_cause: Doxyfile enables diagram generation (HAVE_DOT), making media-gfx/graphviz a hidden build-time requirement of the doc target.
fix_pattern: Add media-gfx/graphviz to the doc? ( ... ) group in BDEPEND, in place, no revbump (docs are build-time only).
rule: If a package's doxygen docs use diagrams/dot, put media-gfx/graphviz next to app-text/doxygen in BDEPEND's doc? group; check Doxyfile for HAVE_DOT when adding USE=doc.

problem: Runtime failure (bug 977914): the base/collections module directory added by upstream was never installed because src_install uses an explicit doins list.
root_cause: Explicit file/dir enumeration in src_install rots silently when upstream adds new data directories; nothing fails at build time.
fix_pattern: Add the missing dir to the doins -r list and revbump (-r1), since the installed image changes for existing users.
rule: When src_install enumerates data files explicitly, re-audit the list against upstream's install target on every bump, and revbump when adding files users are missing at runtime.
snippet: doins -r base/*.asy base/*.js base/*.sh base/*.ps base/collections base/shaders base/webgl

problem: Same missing base/collections install as c0d521d4, applied to the 3.12 ebuild as -r2; duplicate of that lesson.

Configure fails or guesses wrong when cross-compiling because dovecot's autoconf runtime checks (gmtime max, epoll, fd passing, mmap+write, posix_fallocate, va_copy) cannot execute target binaries on the build host.
root_cause: AC_TRY_RUN-style tests have no result under cross-compilation; configure either dies or picks pessimistic defaults.
fix_pattern: Under tc-is-cross-compiler, pre-seed the autoconf cache by exporting the package's i_cv_*/lib_cv_* variables with known-correct answers, deriving arch-dependent ones from toolchain-funcs helpers (tc-has-64bit-time_t selects 40 vs 31 bit gmtime max).
rule: When configure run-tests break cross-compilation, export the corresponding autoconf cache variables (found in the package's m4 files) in src_configure guarded by tc-is-cross-compiler, computing arch-dependent values with toolchain-funcs.
snippet:
if tc-is-cross-compiler; then
	local maxtime=31
	tc-has-64bit-time_t && maxtime=40
	export i_cv_gmtime_max_time_t=${maxtime}
	export i_cv_epoll_works=yes
	export i_cv_fd_passing=yes
	export lib_cv_va_copy=yes
fi

problem: Cross-compiling fails because the build runs freshly-built helper tools (uca9dump, comp_err, comp_sql...) that are target-arch binaries, and CMake CHECK_C_SOURCE_RUNS tests cannot execute.
root_cause: Upstream buildsystem assumes build == host: it compiles code-generation tools and immediately executes them during the build.
fix_pattern: Under tc-is-cross-compiler: cp -R the source to a _build_native tree, configure/build only the needed tools there with tc-env_build cmake_src_configure/cmake_build, sed the target tree's CMakeLists to invoke those tools by absolute path from the native runtime_output_directory, sed CHECK_C_SOURCE_RUNS to CHECK_C_SOURCE_COMPILES, and add the native tools' libs (zstd/openssl/zlib) to BDEPEND.
rule: When a buildsystem executes just-built tools during the build, do a second native configure+build of only those tools with tc-env_build and point the cross build at them by absolute path; deps of the native tools belong in BDEPEND.

problem: Cross-compiling: -I"${EPREFIX}"/usr/include/iodbc pointed at build-host headers instead of target headers, and acct-user/acct-group in BDEPEND meant fowner could not find the ldap user in the target root.
root_cause: EPREFIX resolves inside the build host's prefix, not the sysroot being compiled against; user/group lookups (fowner) and runtime ownership need the accounts present in the target ROOT, i.e. RDEPEND.
fix_pattern: Replace EPREFIX with ESYSROOT in append-cflags include paths; move acct-group/ldap and acct-user/ldap from BDEPEND to RDEPEND (keeping only verify-sig keys in BDEPEND).
rule: Header/library search paths added by the ebuild must be rooted at ESYSROOT (never EPREFIX) so cross builds see target headers, and acct-user/acct-group packages belong in RDEPEND because ownership is applied in the target root.

problem: Inverted test in OpenRC initscript stop_pre ([ "${RC_CMD}" != restart ] instead of =) meant checkconfig never ran on restart, defeating the safe-restart guard.
Routine in-place PYTHON_COMPAT widening to python3_15, no revbump needed for adding an impl.
Routine in-place PYTHON_COMPAT widening to python3_15 for a maturin/rust python package.
OpenRC reported kea as started even when it immediately failed, because start-stop-daemon --background returns success before the daemon finishes initializing.
root_cause: Backgrounded daemons that create their pidfile only after successful startup give s-s-d nothing to verify at fork time.
fix_pattern: New initscript revision (-r4) adds start_post() { ewaitfile "${STARTUP_TIMEOUT:-1}" "${pidfile}"; } so the service only reports started once the pidfile exists; checkconfig errors are shown by not discarding stderr; ebuild renamed to -r1 revbump because an installed file changed.
rule: For --background daemons that write their pidfile only after successful init, add ewaitfile on the pidfile in start_post to catch startup failures; any change to an installed initscript requires a new files/ revision and an ebuild revbump.

problem: Enabling the test suite for an ant-built Java package: tests try network access (bintray, update sites) and the build wants to resolve dependencies online.
root_cause: Upstream ant build resolves jars from the network and several JUnit tests contact remote repositories, which the sandbox forbids.
fix_pattern: Build with eant passing -Doffline=true -Dno.resolve=true and -Dgentoo.classpath=$(java-pkg_getjars ...); patch network-dependent tests with @Ignore annotations (one FILESDIR patch per test class, failure log quoted in patch header); rm test classes that are wholly network-bound; clean bundled jars with java-pkg_clean using a ! -path exception for ones tests need.
rule: For ant-based Java packages, force offline builds with -Doffline=true -Dno.resolve=true plus an explicit gentoo.classpath, and neutralize network tests by patching in @Ignore rather than restricting the whole suite.

problem: Test suite needs a ~3 GB test-data tarball and 2 GB of JVM heap, which should not burden non-test users.
root_cause: Upstream keeps test vectors in a separate huge repo; JVM default heap is too small for the crypto tests.
fix_pattern: Guard the big distfile with test? ( ... ) in SRC_URI and only unpack it in a custom src_unpack under use test; use check-reqs with CHECKREQS_MEMORY=2048M called from both pkg_pretend and pkg_setup only when USE=test; pass -Xmx${CHECKREQS_MEMORY} via JAVA_TEST_EXTRA_ARGS.
rule: Fetch and unpack large test-only distfiles conditionally via test? ( ... ) SRC_URI plus a use-test-guarded src_unpack, and declare test resource needs with check-reqs guarded on USE=test.

problem: Ancient Java package no longer builds with JDK 26 (bug 977086).
root_cause: Legacy source targets an old -source/-target level that newer javac releases dropped support for; upstream is dead so no fix will come.
fix_pattern: Replace the open-ended >=virtual/jdk-1.8:* build dep with an upper bound <virtual/jdk-26:* (with a comment citing the bug), revbump, and take the chance to bump EAPI 8->9.
rule: When a dead-upstream Java package cannot build under a new JDK, cap DEPEND with <virtual/jdk-N:* and a bug-reference comment instead of patching; keep RDEPEND on the old >=virtual/jre floor.
snippet:
# max jdk 25 for bug #977086
DEPEND="
	${CDEPEND}
	<virtual/jdk-26:*
	source? ( app-arch/zip )"

problem: Compilation fails on musl (bug 970934) due to #include <sys/cdefs.h> in a project header.
root_cause: sys/cdefs.h is a glibc internal header that musl does not ship; the include was unused anyway.
fix_pattern: Backport the upstream commit that simply deletes the include, add as FILESDIR patch to PATCHES; no revbump for a build-failure-only fix.
rule: A musl build failure on 'sys/cdefs.h: No such file or directory' is almost always fixed by deleting the include (it is a glibc-internal header); patch it out and upstream the removal.

problem: Build fails (bug 977166) when a newer slotted dev-lang/zig is installed: the BDEPEND zig atoms lacked a slot, so the dep was satisfied by a zig version different from the one zig.eclass selects.
root_cause: dev-lang/zig and zig-bin are slotted per minor version; an unslotted >= atom lets portage satisfy the dep with any newer slot while ZIG_SLOT pins the actual compiler used.
fix_pattern: Append :${ZIG_SLOT} to both the zig and zig-bin atoms inside the || ( ) BDEPEND group so the dep tree guarantees exactly the slot the eclass will use.
rule: With zig.eclass (or any slot-selecting eclass), always pin the toolchain BDEPEND atoms to the eclass slot variable (e.g. :${ZIG_SLOT}); an unslotted >= dep is a latent build failure once a newer slot appears.

problem: Zig-built package could pick an incompatible zig version because the || ( zig-bin zig ) BDEPEND atoms lacked a slot
