root_cause: dev-lang/zig is slotted per minor version; a bare >=dev-lang/zig-0.15.2 is satisfied by any newer slot whose API may not match what the build expects (ZIG_SLOT set elsewhere in the ebuild)
fix_pattern: Append :${ZIG_SLOT} to every zig and zig-bin atom so the dep resolves to the exact slot the ebuild builds with
rule: When an ebuild sets ZIG_SLOT (or depends on any slotted toolchain), every dep atom on that toolchain must carry the matching :slot, including inside || groups

problem: Live 9999 ebuild had drifted from the fixed release ebuild: stale filter-lto workaround and gtest in the wrong dep variable
root_cause: Fixes applied to release ebuilds are easy to forget in the -9999 ebuild; also gtest is a library linked into test binaries so it is a target (DEPEND) dependency, not a build-tool (BDEPEND) one
fix_pattern: Ported release-ebuild changes to 9999: dropped filter-lto (upstream fixed the LTO bug) and moved 'test? ( dev-cpp/gtest )' from BDEPEND into DEPEND alongside ${RDEPEND}
rule: After changing a release ebuild, diff it against the -9999 ebuild and sync; put linked test libraries like gtest in DEPEND, not BDEPEND, for cross-compile correctness

problem: Package failed to compile with gcc-17/clang-22: undeclared memcpy/strlen/std::string and missing fmt symbols
root_cause: Newer libstdc++/libc++ stopped transitively including <cstring>, <string>, <sstream>; fmt/core.h no longer pulls in full formatting API, so headers must include fmt/format.h themselves
fix_pattern: Backported the upstream include-hygiene fixes as a single FILESDIR patch whose header lists the Gentoo bug URL and the upstream PR URLs, then added it to PATCHES=( "${FILESDIR}"/${P}-fix_gcc17.patch )
rule: When a new GCC/Clang breaks a C++ package with 'X was not declared' errors, the fix is almost always adding missing standard includes (<cstring>, <string>, <sstream>) or fmt/format.h; backport upstream's commits as one patch headed with bug and PR URLs instead of pinning the old compiler

problem: src_test failed because one ctest case (pdal_io_copc_remote_reader_test) downloads a remote file, which the sandbox blocks
root_cause: Upstream test suite mixes network-dependent tests with local ones; Portage builds have no network access
fix_pattern: Added the failing test name to the existing ctest --exclude-regex alternation in myctestargs, with a comment noting the prefetch idea
rule: When a test needs network access, add it to a ctest --exclude-regex (or EPYTEST_DESELECT equivalent) instead of RESTRICTing the whole suite; keep the excluded names in one commented regex

problem: DEPEND contained ${REPEND} (typo for ${RDEPEND}), silently expanding to empty and dropping all runtime deps from DEPEND
root_cause: Bash expands undefined variables to the empty string with no error, so a misspelled variable reference in a dep string is invisible at source time
fix_pattern: Corrected ${REPEND} to ${RDEPEND}
rule: Misspelled variable expansions in ebuild dep strings fail silently; run pkgcheck scan (VariableScope/undefined-variable checks) or grep for non-standard ${...} names before committing

problem: New version's test suite shipped tests that fail in the sandbox (tests invoking 'stack', oversized output tests)
root_cause: Upstream tests assume the Haskell 'stack' tool and unconstrained environments that do not exist in an ebuild build
fix_pattern: In src_prepare after haskell-cabal_src_prepare: rm -v the unusable test files with || die, and cp fixed replacement test files kept in FILESDIR over the shipped ones
rule: When only specific data-driven test files are broken, delete or overwrite those files in src_prepare (rm/cp ... || die) instead of RESTRICTing the whole test suite

problem: Old ebuild carried a blanket RESTRICT=test ('test-suite fails to build') with the test deps commented out, leaving tests permanently disabled
root_cause: A temporary test breakage was hard-coded as RESTRICT=test and never revisited on later bumps
fix_pattern: On the bump, re-enabled the test suite: CABAL_FEATURES gained test-suite, IUSE=test with RESTRICT="!test? ( test )", and real test? ( ... ) deps replacing the commented block
rule: Treat unconditional RESTRICT=test as debt: on every version bump, retry the test suite and convert to IUSE=test + RESTRICT="!test? ( test )" with proper test deps once it builds
snippet:
IUSE="test"
RESTRICT="!test? ( test )"
DEPEND="${RDEPEND}
	test? ( dev-haskell/hspec
		>=dev-haskell/hspec-core-2.6.0
		dev-haskell/quickcheck )"

problem: Upstream's tests live in a standalone meson project under src/libaudcore/tests that the main meson build never configures, so plain meson_src_test finds nothing
root_cause: Test suite is a separate meson source tree, not a subdir target of the top-level build
fix_pattern: Configure and compile the test tree as a second meson build by overriding EMESON_SOURCE and BUILD_DIR around the eclass calls, then run meson_src_test with that BUILD_DIR
rule: For an out-of-tree test subproject, invoke meson_src_configure/compile/test a second time with EMESON_SOURCE pointing at the test source dir and a distinct BUILD_DIR
snippet:
if use test; then
	EMESON_SOURCE="${S}"/src/libaudcore/tests \
	BUILD_DIR="${WORKDIR}"/${P}-tests-build \
	meson_src_configure
fi
# src_test:
BUILD_DIR="${WORKDIR}"/${P}-tests-build meson_src_test

problem: ZNC module source ships no build system, so the ebuild must provide one that matches the installed znc's module ABI
root_cause: Out-of-tree ZNC modules are normally built ad hoc with znc-buildmod; packaging needs a reproducible CMake build
fix_pattern: Generate CMakeLists.txt once with 'znc-buildmod -v', store it versioned in FILESDIR (CMakeLists-r1.txt, header noting the znc version it came from), cp it into ${S} in src_prepare, and depend on net-irc/znc:= for the ABI
rule: For plugin packages without a build system, vendor a generated build file in FILESDIR with a -rN suffix and a comment recording how/against which host-package version it was generated, and use a := dep on the host package

problem: Build failed against >=abseil-cpp-20260107.0 because that abseil requires C++20 while the project pins CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17
root_cause: Header-only/template-heavy deps like abseil impose their C++ standard on all consumers; the pinned lower standard now fails at compile time
fix_pattern: In src_prepare, gate a sed that raises CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17 to 20 behind has_version of the new abseil, so the ebuild still builds correctly against older abseil
rule: When a dependency raises the required C++ standard, bump the consumer's CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD; if both dep versions are in tree, gate the tweak with has_version instead of unconditionally patching
snippet:
if has_version ">=dev-cpp/abseil-cpp-20260107.0"; then
	# needs >=c++20, bug #976275
	sed -e 's/set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17)/set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 20)/' \
		-i CMakeLists.txt || die
fi

problem: Build broke with USE=exif disabled: GeoImageDock sources referencing exiv2 were compiled unconditionally even when GEOIMAGE=0
root_cause: Upstream listed the optional feature's source files in the global source list instead of appending them inside the feature's if() block
fix_pattern: Backported the upstream PR that removes the files from the unconditional list and does list(APPEND merkaartor_SRCS ...) inside if(GEOIMAGE); patch header carries bug URL and upstream PR URL; added to PATCHES with a '# bug #NNNN' comment
rule: A build failure that only appears with a USE flag disabled usually means optional-feature sources are compiled unconditionally; fix in the build system's option guard and always test both USE states
snippet:
if (GEOIMAGE)
     list(APPEND PKGCONFIG_REQUIRED_LIBS exiv2)
+    list(APPEND merkaartor_SRCS src/Docks/GeoImageDock.cpp src/Docks/GeoImageDock.h)
     add_definitions(-DGEOIMAGE=1)
 endif()

problem: vtk failed to build against gdal-3.13: GDAL APIs now return CSLConstList (const char* const*) which cannot convert to char**
root_cause: GDAL 3.13 const-ified the return types of GDALGetMetadata/GetCategoryNames
fix_pattern: Backported upstream's patch that wraps each call site in a GDAL_VERSION_MAJOR/MINOR #if choosing const char* const* vs char**, keeping compatibility with older gdal; patch header cites the upstream MR and commit
rule: When a dependency const-ifies API return types, backport the upstream version-guarded #if fix so one ebuild builds against both old and new dep versions, rather than adding a < version cap

problem: postgis failed to build against gdal-3.13 because it used the MIN macro that previously leaked from GDAL's cpl_port.h and is no longer exposed
root_cause: Code relied on a macro incidentally provided by a third-party dependency header instead of its own project's utilities
fix_pattern: Backported the one-hunk upstream commit replacing GDAL's MIN with PostgreSQL's own Min macro, added via PATCHES
rule: Never rely on macros/symbols that leak from a dependency's headers; when a dep bump removes them, backport the upstream switch to the project's own equivalents

problem: Bump had to keep working around the 'system executables owned by nonzero uid' QA notice for non-suid-root binaries, and show upgrade notes only when crossing an old version
root_cause: fcron's fcrontab/fcrondyn are deliberately setuid to the fcron user, tripping the QA check; symlinks would not hide it
fix_pattern: Install the real binaries under /usr/libexec and generate tiny /bin/sh exec wrappers installed as /usr/bin/fcrontab etc. (bug 925512); use eapi9-ver's ver_replacing -lt 3.2.1 in pkg_postinst to gate upgrade-only elog messages; new cmocka patch adds '#include <stdint.h>' before cmocka.h in tests
rule: To silence the nonzero-uid QA notice for intentionally non-root-suid tools, hide binaries in /usr/libexec behind shell wrappers (not symlinks); use eapi9-ver ver_replacing for version-conditional postinst messages; cmocka test sources must include stdint.h (and setjmp.h) before cmocka.h
snippet:
cat > "${file}_wrapper" <<-EOF
#!/bin/sh
exec "${EPREFIX}/usr/libexec/${file}" "\$@"
EOF
# postinst:
elif ver_replacing -lt "3.2.1"; then

problem: Live ebuild would break: it rm's a hard-coded list of unknown-license firmware files that upstream deleted from the repository
root_cause: The ebuild hard-codes upstream file lists (unknown_license blacklist, USE=unknown-license machinery); upstream removing those files makes 'rm ... || die' fail and leaves dead USE/LICENSE/RESTRICT/blocker logic
fix_pattern: Dropped the unknown-license USE flag, its LICENSE clause, RESTRICT bindist coupling, alsa-firmware blockers, and the whole filename array from the 99999999 ebuild, citing the upstream commit in the message
rule: When an ebuild embeds lists of upstream file names, audit them on every sync/bump; when upstream drops the files, remove the entire dependent USE/LICENSE/RESTRICT machinery, not just the list entries
snippet:
-IUSE="... +redistributable unknown-license"
+IUSE="... +redistributable"
-	unknown-license? ( bindist )"
-	local unknown_license=( ... )
-	use !unknown-license && rm -v "${unknown_license[@]}" || die

problem: Every ghc emerge died: the ebuild referenced ${FILESDIR}/ghc-9.4.5-musl-target.patch but the file was never committed
root_cause: The earlier commit added the PATCHES entry without 'git add'-ing the new file under files/; nothing at commit time verified FILESDIR references resolve
fix_pattern: Committed the missing patch file. Its content is itself a musl lesson: GHC's llvm-target m4 only special-cased *-musleabi* triplets, so plain *-musl* (x86_64/aarch64 musl) lost the vendor part; widening the case glob to *-musl* fixes LLVM target lookup
rule: Before pushing, verify every "${FILESDIR}"/... reference points to a committed file (pkgcheck scan reports this); and match musl triplets with *-musl*, not only *-musleabi*

problem: Test suite failed only on arm because an expected error-message string differs on that arch
root_cause: Upstream test compares exact diagnostic text that varies per architecture (upstream issue filed)
fix_pattern: Declared multilib_src_test with the cmake eclass's CMAKE_SKIP_TESTS array, using $(usev arm 'testname') so the skip applies only on arm, commented with the Gentoo bug and upstream issue; applied identically to release and 9999 ebuilds
rule: For arch-specific test failures, skip just that test with CMAKE_SKIP_TESTS + $(usev <arch> 'name') in (multilib_)src_test instead of restricting tests or dropping keywords

problem: Routine version bump of a small autotools library (SLOT=0/10 subslot unchanged, .la files deleted); no generalizable content beyond standard bump hygiene
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT extension python3_{12..13} -> {12..14}; trivial one-line bump
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT extension to python3_14; trivial one-line bump
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT extension to python3_14; trivial one-line bump
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT extension to python3_14; trivial one-line bump
Enabling py3.14 on an application whose plugin family and kernel dep were not yet py3.14-ready
root_cause: A new Python target can only be enabled once every RDEPEND in the chain supports it; spyder needed spyder-kernels fixed first (bug 973918 done via git snapshot), then the app, then each plugin, each under its own tracker sub-bug
fix_pattern: Bottom-up rollout in one push: snapshot spyder-kernels with the upstream py3.14 fixes, then flip PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..14} ) on spyder and each spyder-* plugin, one commit per package closing its own sub-bug
rule: Enable a new Python target across a package family bottom-up (libraries/kernels first, app, then plugins), one commit per package referencing its tracker bug, only after deps already list the new target

problem: Latest release lacked upstream py3.14 fixes, blocking the whole spyder stack from the new Python target
root_cause: Needed commits existed only on upstream's git master, not in a tagged release
fix_pattern: Packaged a GitHub commit snapshot versioned ${release}_pYYYYMMDD: pin COMMIT sha, fetch archive/${COMMIT}.tar.gz renamed to ${P}.gh.tar.gz, set S=${WORKDIR}/${PN}-${COMMIT}; also gates optional-dep tests with has_version-driven EPYTEST_IGNORE/EPYTEST_DESELECT and uses EPYTEST_PLUGINS
rule: When required fixes are unreleased, package a git snapshot as version_pYYYYMMDD with a pinned COMMIT sha and gh tarball instead of going -9999 or waiting; deselect tests for optional deps via has_version
snippet:
COMMIT="630121acceaec56ee20e3c24e8489d028b382f2c"
SRC_URI="https://github.com/spyder-ide/${PN}/archive/${COMMIT}.tar.gz -> ${P}.gh.tar.gz"
S=${WORKDIR}/${PN}-${COMMIT}
if ! has_version "dev-python/pandas[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]"; then
	EPYTEST_IGNORE+=( spyder_kernels/utils/tests/test_nsview.py )
fi

problem: Package-specific expansion of a hand-rolled src_test matrix to also run the *_int32 demo binaries; no generalizable idiom
USE=cuda build of a CMake package failed because nvcc picked the system default host compiler/linker, which CUDA does not support
root_cause: nvcc only supports specific GCC versions; without CUDAHOSTCXX/CUDAHOSTLD the CMake CUDA toolchain uses the active (too-new) compiler, and the sandbox blocks /dev/nvidia* device access during configure checks
fix_pattern: inherit cuda toolchain-funcs; call cuda_src_prepare in src_prepare; in src_configure call cuda_add_sandbox plus addpredict /dev/char/, pass the target architectures with a CUDAARCHS fallback, and export CUDAHOSTCXX=$(cuda_gccdir) and CUDAHOSTLD=$(tc-getCXX)
rule: When adding USE=cuda to a CMake ebuild, inherit the cuda eclass and explicitly set CUDAHOSTCXX=$(cuda_gccdir) / CUDAHOSTLD=$(tc-getCXX) and cuda_add_sandbox instead of letting nvcc use the default host toolchain
snippet:
if use cuda; then
    cuda_add_sandbox
    addpredict "/dev/char/"
    mycmakeargs+=( -DSUITESPARSE_CUDA_ARCHITECTURES="${CUDAARCHS:-all-major}" )
    local -x CUDAHOSTCXX="$(cuda_gccdir)"
    local -x CUDAHOSTLD="$(tc-getCXX)"
fi

problem: Same CUDA host-compiler/sandbox enablement idiom as sci-libs/spqr (faf5b846) applied to a sibling SuiteSparse package; no new pattern
Same CUDA host-compiler/sandbox enablement idiom as sci-libs/spqr (faf5b846) applied to suitesparseconfig; no new pattern
CMake FindBLAS linked against whatever BLAS it detected first, ignoring the system-chosen provider and 64-bit integer (index64) variant
root_cause: SuiteSparse's CMake needs BLA_VENDOR and SUITESPARSE_USE_64BIT_BLAS set explicitly to match the installed virtual/blas provider; autodetection is nondeterministic
fix_pattern: query the installed virtual/blas USE flags with has_version in src_configure and translate them into CMake cache options: index64 -> SUITESPARSE_USE_64BIT_BLAS=ON, flexiblas -> BLA_VENDOR=FlexiBLAS else Generic
rule: When a CMake package consumes BLAS/LAPACK, set BLA_VENDOR (and 64-bit index options) from has_version checks against virtual/blas USE flags instead of letting FindBLAS guess
snippet:
if has_version 'virtual/blas[index64]'; then
    mycmakeargs+=( -DSUITESPARSE_USE_64BIT_BLAS=ON )
fi
if has_version 'virtual/blas[flexiblas]'; then
    mycmakeargs+=( -DBLA_VENDOR=FlexiBLAS )
else
    mycmakeargs+=( -DBLA_VENDOR=Generic )
fi

problem: CMake ran an automagic CUDA toolchain lookup for a package with no cuda USE flag, making the build depend on whether nvcc happened to be installed
root_cause: SuiteSparse CMake autodetects CUDA (SUITESPARSE_USE_CUDA defaults to auto) unless explicitly disabled
fix_pattern: add -DSUITESPARSE_USE_CUDA=OFF to mycmakeargs so configure never probes the CUDA toolchain
rule: Force autodetected optional features OFF in mycmakeargs whenever the ebuild has no corresponding USE flag, so builds stay deterministic and free of automagic dependencies

problem: Same -DSUITESPARSE_USE_CUDA=OFF automagic-detection fix as umfpack (bef05752); no new pattern
Same -DSUITESPARSE_USE_CUDA=OFF automagic-detection fix as umfpack (bef05752); no new pattern
Same -DSUITESPARSE_USE_CUDA=OFF automagic-detection fix as umfpack (bef05752); no new pattern
Same -DSUITESPARSE_USE_CUDA=OFF automagic-detection fix as umfpack (bef05752); no new pattern
Same -DSUITESPARSE_USE_CUDA=OFF automagic-detection fix as umfpack (bef05752); no new pattern
Same -DSUITESPARSE_USE_CUDA=OFF automagic-detection fix as umfpack (bef05752); no new pattern
Daemon had use-after-free crashes (valgrind-confirmed) both in released code and in a large USE=poll feature patch carried by the ebuild
root_cause: Upstream C bug (c->fd written after call_close() freed the struct); the big optional patch is maintained out-of-tree and needed its own fix
fix_pattern: revbump to -r2; small upstream-PR fix carried in FILESDIR and listed in PATCHES; the large optional patch is a versioned distfile fetched via SRC_URI inside 'poll? ( ... )' and applied with 'use poll && eapply ${DISTDIR}/...'; the updated patch got a new -v2 filename so mirrors and Manifest track the new content
rule: Ship large or USE-conditional patches as versioned SRC_URI distfiles (rename the file on every content change, e.g. -v2) and eapply them from ${DISTDIR}; keep small fixes in FILESDIR; revbump whenever installed code changes

problem: pkg_postinst migration ewarn told users to move astdb.sqlite while the check and real file are astdb.sqlite3; message-only fix edited in place across all live ebuilds without revbump
soapyuhd stopped compiling against >=net-wireless/uhd-4.9.0.1 (and current Boost)
root_cause: uhd's public headers now use C++17 features (std::optional, std::is_convertible_v) while soapyuhd hardcoded CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 14; newer Boost also stopped transitively providing boost/lexical_cast.hpp
fix_pattern: revbump to -r1 with two FILESDIR patches (both submitted upstream): raise set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17) in CMakeLists.txt and add explicit #include <boost/lexical_cast.hpp> to the two sources using it; add the same PATCHES + src_prepare to the 9999 live ebuild so the fix persists
rule: When a dependency's public headers adopt a newer C++ standard, patch the consumer's CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD (and add now-missing transitive includes) rather than hacking CXXFLAGS, revbump, and mirror the patch into the live ebuild

problem: New library version bump; man pages failed to install with compression disabled because upstream CMake passed bare filenames instead of source-dir paths to UHD_INSTALL
root_cause: upstream CMakeLists only prefixed ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR} in the compressed-manpage branch; the uncompressed branch installed nonexistent relative paths
fix_pattern: carry a FILESDIR patch (linked to the upstream issue) that builds the full-path list before UHD_INSTALL; keep SLOT="0/$(ver_cut 1-3)" so the subslot changes every feature release and :=-consumers (like soapyuhd) get rebuilt
rule: Encode a library's ABI in a version-derived subslot (SLOT="0/$(ver_cut 1-3)") so consumers with := deps rebuild automatically on every bump, and carry install-path fixes as patches referencing the upstream issue

problem: darcs built with USE=-threaded (non-threaded GHC runtime) misbehaved for users (bug 868387)
root_cause: upstream effectively requires the threaded GHC RTS; exposing it as an optional USE flag allowed a known-broken configuration
fix_pattern: remove threaded from IUSE, replace $(cabal_flag threaded threaded) with unconditional --flag=threaded in haskell-cabal_src_configure, drop the flag description from metadata.xml, and update the hackport flags comment
rule: When a USE flag permits a configuration upstream considers broken, delete the flag and hard-enable the feature instead of leaving a default-on toggle

problem: darcs bump blocked because darcs.cabal only enabled the modern crypton-connection dependency stack under 'if impl(ghc >= 9.8)', mismatching the tree's packaged deps (bug 736842)
root_cause: .cabal compiler-version conditionals select dependency sets Gentoo does not package for older GHC
fix_pattern: FILESDIR patch flips the cabal conditional 'if impl(ghc >= 9.8)' to 'if true' so the crypton-connection code path is always used; test suite disabled with RESTRICT="test" plus a comment naming the deprecated deps that block it
rule: When a .cabal file gates dependency choices behind compiler-version conditionals that conflict with the packaged dep set, patch the conditional to force the supported branch instead of packaging legacy libraries

problem: GHC users_guide documentation build crashed at runtime with sphinx-9.1
root_cause: Sphinx 9 removed deprecated behavior: the bundled rtd-theme jinja layout passed the css object to pathto() instead of css.filename
fix_pattern: backport the upstream GitLab MR fix as a FILESDIR patch, eapply it in src_prepare, and deliberately scope it to the one version (ghc-9.8.4) that was actually tested before spreading it
rule: When a Sphinx (or other doc toolchain) major bump breaks a package's docs, backport the upstream docs fix as a patch scoped to tested versions instead of masking the doc USE flag or pinning old sphinx

problem: Package needed relaxed dependency bounds published as a Hackage metadata revision, without any new upstream tarball
root_cause: Hackage allows .cabal-only revisions; the ebuild pins which revision it builds against via CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION
fix_pattern: bump CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION (6 -> 11) in place; the eclass fetches the revised .cabal as its own DIST entry so only the Manifest changes alongside
rule: For Hackage packages, pick up revised dependency bounds by bumping CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION rather than patching the .cabal file or waiting for a new release

problem: Old hackport-generated ebuild carried manual MY_PN/MY_P/SRC_URI/S boilerplate for a Hackage package whose upstream name differs only in case
root_cause: older haskell-cabal eclass/hackport lacked CABAL_PN and CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION support
fix_pattern: regenerate with modern hackport: set CABAL_PN="Only" and CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION=2, delete MY_PN/MY_P, SRC_URI, S, and empty IUSE; eclass derives everything
rule: Replace hand-rolled MY_P/SRC_URI/S plumbing in Haskell ebuilds with CABAL_PN plus CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION and let haskell-cabal.eclass derive the rest

problem: Routine hackport-regenerated -r2 revbump adopting CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION=5 and refreshed dependency ranges; no new pattern beyond the CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION idiom
Upstream .cabal dependency upper bounds were too tight for the versions actually in the tree (base < 4.21, text-show < 3.11)
root_cause: Hackage packages pin conservative upper bounds that lag behind the GHC/library versions Gentoo ships
fix_pattern: declare CABAL_CHDEPS pairs (exact old constraint string, replacement) in the ebuild; the eclass rewrites the .cabal at build time, no patch file needed
rule: Relax over-tight .cabal dependency bounds declaratively with CABAL_CHDEPS old/new string pairs instead of shipping FILESDIR patches

problem: Package is bundled as a GHC core library for some compiler versions, so building/installing it separately there would conflict
root_cause: haddock-library ships inside certain GHC releases; the eclass must know for which GHC versions the package is already provided
fix_pattern: set CABAL_CORE_LIB_GHC_PV to the space-separated GHC versions that bundle the library, combined with CABAL_CHDEPS to lift stale test-dep bounds
rule: When a Haskell package is shipped as a GHC core library for specific compiler versions, list those versions in CABAL_CORE_LIB_GHC_PV so haskell-cabal.eclass skips a duplicate install

problem: doctest test suite failed when unrelated Haskell packages were installed on the system (bug 916851)
root_cause: a bare 'doctest [...]' runner uses the global package environment, so out-of-scope installed packages leak into scope and break compilation
fix_pattern: patch upstream to migrate to cabal-doctest: Build-Type: Custom with a custom-setup stanza, doctest.hs driven by Build_doctests pkgs plus -package-env=- / -hide-all-packages / -no-user-package-db; in the ebuild add cabal-doctest to GHC_BOOTSTRAP_PACKAGES when tests are enabled
rule: Haskell doctest suites invoking doctest directly are unreliable with stray system packages; migrate them to cabal-doctest and export GHC_BOOTSTRAP_PACKAGES+=( cabal-doctest ) under USE=test

problem: Hackage package unconditionally built and installed an executable with the generic name 'example', polluting /usr/bin and risking collisions
root_cause: upstream .cabal declares an example executable with no flag gating it and a non-namespaced binary name
fix_pattern: patch the .cabal to add a manual 'examples' flag (default false) with 'buildable: False' when unset; expose IUSE=examples via $(cabal_flag examples examples); rename the binary with CABAL_CHBINS=( 'example' "${PN}-example" ); revbump to -r1
rule: Gate upstream example executables behind a manual cabal flag mapped to IUSE=examples, and rename generic binary names with CABAL_CHBINS to avoid /usr/bin collisions

problem: Haskell package fails to configure because its .cabal file pins overly strict version bounds (including caret '^>=' constraints) on deps like base/containers that the tree has moved past
root_cause: Hackage packages routinely ship conservative upper bounds that reject newer, API-compatible dependency versions; patching the tarball for every bound change is heavy-weight
fix_pattern: Use the haskell-cabal eclass CABAL_CHDEPS array to rewrite constraint strings in the .cabal file at prepare time; each pair is 'exact old constraint text' 'new constraint text' and the eclass dies if the old text no longer matches (self-verifying across bumps)
rule: When a Haskell dep bound is the only blocker and the newer version is API-compatible, loosen it with CABAL_CHDEPS=( 'old' 'new' ) instead of a files/ patch; the exact-match requirement makes stale entries fail loudly on the next bump

problem: Upstream cabal file unconditionally builds an executable literally named 'example', which would install a generically-named binary into /usr/bin (collision- and QA-prone)
root_cause: Haskell packages often ship demo executables with no build flag to disable them, and cabal installs every buildable executable
fix_pattern: Two-part idiom: (1) patch the .cabal to add a manual 'examples' flag defaulting to False that sets 'buildable: False' on the example executable, exposed as IUSE=examples via cabal_flag; (2) use the haskell-cabal CABAL_CHBINS array to rename any installed binary to a namespaced name
rule: When an upstream build unconditionally produces a generically-named demo binary, gate it behind an off-by-default flag and rename it with a package prefix (CABAL_CHBINS or mv in src_install) to avoid /usr/bin collisions

problem: Build breaks with gcc-15 (default -std=gnu23): 'too many arguments to function malloc; expected 0, have 1' from K&R-style 'extern void* malloc();' declarations; also QA notice 'implicit function declarations in configure logs' from an m4 test program missing <stdlib.h>
root_cause: C23 removes K&R unprototyped function declarations, so legacy 'extern void *malloc();' re-declarations conflict with the builtin prototypes; old autoconf test snippets call libc functions without including their headers, which C99+ flags as implicit declarations
fix_pattern: Patch the hand-written extern declarations to full prototypes (or better, include <stdlib.h>) and add the missing #include to the m4/configure test source; both patches were taken from/sent upstream and referenced by URL in the patch header
rule: When gcc-15/C23 breaks a build on unprototyped extern declarations or configure logs show implicit-function-declaration QA notices, patch in real prototypes/headers at the exact declaration site rather than downgrading -std

problem: Test suite of a code-generating tool fails in src_test because the just-built binary cannot find its runtime data files (templates) which are not installed yet
root_cause: Cabal's generated Paths_<pkg> module resolves the datadir to the final install location, but it honors a '<pkgname>_datadir' environment variable override at runtime
fix_pattern: Export the Paths_* env override pointing at the source data directory when invoking tests, so the uninstalled binary self-locates its data
rule: When a freshly built Haskell tool fails tests because it looks for installed data files, set <pkgname>_datadir=${S}/data in the src_test environment instead of installing first or skipping tests

problem: Package needed newer Hackage metadata (loosened bounds) without any source change
root_cause: Hackage supports metadata-only '.cabal revisions'; the tarball stays identical while a -revN.cabal file with relaxed constraints is published separately
fix_pattern: Bump CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION=N in the ebuild; the haskell-cabal eclass then fetches ${P}-revN.cabal as an extra DIST entry and substitutes it over the in-tarball .cabal; only Manifest changes, no patch and (per Haskell-team practice) no ebuild revbump since the tarball is unchanged
rule: When upstream publishes a new Hackage cabal revision that fixes your dependency bounds, bump CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION and re-manifest instead of patching the .cabal

problem: New package for an old unmaintained Hackage release: it neither compiles with modern GHC (missing Semigroup instance required since the Semigroup-Monoid change) nor accepts current dep versions
root_cause: base 4.11 made Semigroup a superclass of Monoid, so code defining only 'mappend' fails; stale upper bounds reject current libs (SVGFonts API also changed)
fix_pattern: Combine three tools: CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION for revised metadata, CABAL_CHDEPS to drop dead upper bounds, and small files/ patches (with git-format headers) adding the Semigroup instance and porting to the new SVGFonts API
rule: When resurrecting an old Haskell package, expect the Semigroup-Monoid migration: move mappend's body to a new 'instance Semigroup X where (<>) = ...' and keep 'mempty' in the Monoid instance
snippet:
+instance Semigroup Arrows where
+  Arrows f <> Arrows g = Arrows (f . g)
 instance Monoid Arrows where
   mempty = Arrows id
-  Arrows f `mappend` Arrows g = Arrows (f . g)

problem: Package with a custom Setup.hs fails to build against Cabal 3.14: autogenPackageModulesDir now returns a SymbolicPath instead of FilePath, and the custom-setup depends block caps Cabal < 3.13
root_cause: Cabal 3.14 changed the Setup API (symbolic paths, CommonSetupFlags); packages with build-type Custom break even when the library code itself is fine
fix_pattern: Apply the upstream PR patch guarded by '#if MIN_VERSION_Cabal(3,14,0)' using getSymbolicPath, and raise the custom-setup Cabal upper bound; declare the build tool via GHC_BOOTSTRAP_PACKAGES=( gtk2hs-buildtools ) so the eclass makes it available to Setup.hs
rule: When a Haskell package with build-type Custom breaks on a new Cabal release, look for an upstream Cabal-compat PR and carry it as a MIN_VERSION_Cabal-guarded patch; tools needed by Setup.hs go in GHC_BOOTSTRAP_PACKAGES, not just BDEPEND
snippet:
+#if MIN_VERSION_Cabal(3,14,0)
+    targetDir  = getSymbolicPath . autogenPackageModulesDir $ lbi
+#else
     targetDir  = autogenPackageModulesDir lbi
+#endif
...
GHC_BOOTSTRAP_PACKAGES=( gtk2hs-buildtools )

problem: doctest test-suite is unreliable in a system package environment: doctest picks up whatever packages are installed globally, pulling in out-of-scope deps and failing
root_cause: Plain 'doctest [files]' invocations resolve modules against the ambient GHC package db, not the sandboxed build-deps of the package under test
fix_pattern: Patch upstream to the cabal-doctest framework: switch build-type to Custom, add cabal-doctest to custom-setup, and rewrite the driver to use the generated Build_doctests (flags/pkgs/module_sources), plus setLocaleEncoding utf8; loosen the doctest bound via CABAL_CHDEPS
rule: When a Haskell doctest suite fails due to system-installed packages leaking into scope, migrate it to cabal-doctest so doctest runs with exactly the configured package flags
snippet:
+import Build_doctests (flags, pkgs, module_sources)
 main :: IO ()
-main = doctest ["-fobject-code", "System/Random/MWC.hs"]
+main = do
+    setLocaleEncoding utf8
+    doctest $ flags ++ pkgs ++ module_sources

problem: Packaging a self-hosting lexer generator: docs are missing from the Hackage tarball, system-installed copies of alex/happy could leak into the build, and USE=static needs special cabal flags
root_cause: Hackage release tarballs omit the sphinx doc sources (only in git); cabal auto-detects installed alex/happy which would make the build depend on the previous installed version
fix_pattern: Add a doc?-conditional second SRC_URI pointing at a pinned GitHub commit tarball and copy its doc/ into ${S}; pass --with-alex=false --with-happy=false so configure cannot find system tools; for USE=static pass --disable-shared --enable-executable-static and depend on gmp/libffi[static-libs]
rule: When a build tool could accidentally use its own installed copy, explicitly disable detection with --with-<tool>=false (or equivalent) so the build is self-contained and reproducible

problem: Need to package one sub-component (GHC's hadrian build system) that only ships inside the huge ghc source tarball; stock build triggers two QA notices (unrecognized --with-cc configure option, compressed doc archives in docompress-ed dirs)
root_cause: Upstream does not publish hadrian separately, and its defaults pass flags/install artifacts that violate Gentoo QA
fix_pattern: Reuse the parent tarball in SRC_URI and point S into the subdirectory, overriding CABAL_FILE; carry small patches that delete the offending --with-cc configure flag and the doc-archive install rules; express toolchain constraints as USE-deps on ghc (threaded? requires ghc[-unregisterised])
rule: To package a sub-project that only exists inside a larger release tarball, keep the parent DIST and set S=${WORKDIR}/<parent>/<subdir> (plus any eclass file-location overrides) instead of re-rolling a tarball

problem: Cabal expresses a dependency as a union of incompatible ranges (text ^>=1.2 || ^>=2.0) which a single >=/< pair in RDEPEND cannot represent
root_cause: Portage range deps are single intervals; caret-union constraints allow disjoint windows
fix_pattern: Model the union with a || group of parenthesized range pairs plus a separate slot-operator dep for the rebuild trigger
rule: When a cabal constraint is a union of version windows, write it as '|| ( ( >=a <b ) ( >=c <d ) )' in *DEPEND and keep a bare 'pkg:=' alongside for subslot rebuilds

problem: Package's threaded/smp cabal flag must not be enabled on GHC builds that lack SMP support (unregisterised arches), and its test suite hangs when run single-threaded
root_cause: Hardcoding --flag=smp breaks on non-SMP GHC; upstream tests forced -with-rtsopts=-N
fix_pattern: Query the compiler capability at configure time with ghc-supports-smp from the ghc-package eclass and pass smp/-smp accordingly; fix test parallelism by backporting the upstream SpecHook patch
rule: Gate threaded/smp cabal flags on 'ghc-supports-smp' rather than hardcoding, so the same ebuild works on unregisterised architectures
snippet:
inherit ghc-package haskell-cabal
src_configure() {
	if ghc-supports-smp; then
		local smp_flag=smp
	else
		local smp_flag=-smp
	fi
	haskell-cabal_src_configure --flag="${smp_flag}"
}

problem: Packaging a GHC boot library creates a circular dependency (Cabal itself depends on mtl) and risks needlessly rebuilding what GHC already ships
root_cause: mtl is bundled with GHC as a core library; the normal haskell-cabal Cabal dependency would cycle
fix_pattern: Add 'nocabaldep' to CABAL_FEATURES to break the Cabal dep cycle, and list every GHC version bundling this exact library version in CABAL_CORE_LIB_GHC_PV so the eclass just registers the shipped copy instead of rebuilding; constrain ghc with both lower and upper bounds
rule: When packaging a GHC-bundled core library, use CABAL_FEATURES+=" nocabaldep" plus CABAL_CORE_LIB_GHC_PV="<ghc versions>" instead of a plain build
snippet:
CABAL_FEATURES+=" nocabaldep"  # break circular deps, gentoo-haskell#810
RDEPEND=">=dev-lang/ghc-9.6:= <dev-lang/ghc-9.13"
CABAL_CORE_LIB_GHC_PV="9.6.2 9.6.3 ... 9.12.3"

problem: Test dep tasty-lua <1.1 no longer exists in the tree; package fails to resolve test deps after the tree moved to tasty-lua 1.1+
root_cause: The .cabal bound and the ebuild DEPEND both pinned the removed version window
fix_pattern: Loosen the bound in two synchronized places: a CABAL_CHDEPS entry rewriting the .cabal constraint, and the matching <dev-haskell/tasty-lua-1.2 edit in DEPEND; done in place on the existing ebuild since only test deps changed
rule: Every CABAL_CHDEPS loosening must be mirrored in the ebuild's *DEPEND range, otherwise portage and cabal disagree about what is installable

problem: Package refused to build on older-but-supported GHC because its cabal 'base' lower bound was tighter than necessary
root_cause: The 'base' library version is fixed by the GHC release (base 4.15.1.0 = GHC 9.0.2, 4.16 = 9.2), so base bounds translate directly into a GHC version requirement
fix_pattern: Loosen the base lower bound via CABAL_CHDEPS and simultaneously lower the >=dev-lang/ghc dep to the matching release
rule: When adjusting a Haskell package's 'base' bounds, always update >=dev-lang/ghc-X in RDEPEND to the GHC version that ships that base (they are one-to-one)

problem: Test suite needs live network access (fails in sandboxed builds), but a blanket RESTRICT=test would also skip the valuable offline tests
root_cause: Upstream splits tests into 'spec' (network) and 'spec-nonet' suites; only the former is sandbox-hostile, and a couple of nonet redirect tests still bind real sockets
fix_pattern: Carry a versioned disable-net-tests patch that sets 'buildable: False' on the network test-suite in the .cabal and neutralizes individual flaky cases with 'when False $ it ...'; refresh the patch on each bump (0.7.10 -> 0.7.16 variant)
rule: When only part of a test suite needs network, patch that suite/test off (buildable: False / when False) instead of RESTRICT=test, so offline tests keep running under FEATURES=test
snippet:
test-suite spec
+  buildable: False
...
-    it "does strips header on redirect..." $ ...
+    when False $ it "does strips header on redirect..." $ ...

problem: Custom Gtk2HsSetup.hs breaks against Cabal 3.14 API changes (CommonSetupFlags, symbolic paths); fixed by carrying the upstream MIN_VERSION_Cabal-guarded compat PR, same pattern as dev-haskell/pango in this series
Routine backfill of an older tasty version (1.4.3-r1) with an explicit --flag=unix configure flag; no generalizable content beyond standard hackport output

Build fix: added the upstream 'missing Functor instance for AltList' commit as a files/ patch (cited by URL in the header) that had been omitted from the ebuild; done in place without revbump since the package failed to compile anyway

Tests broke because transformers-0.6 removed the deprecated ListT transformer; fixed by cherry-picking the upstream PR that drops the ListT test, with a revbump to -r1 and modernized eclass boilerplate
Revbump to pick up Hackage cabal revision 3 (CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION=3) with -bench flag; same metadata-revision idiom as dev-haskell/errors

Version bump using CABAL_CHDEPS to loosen *lower* bounds (tasty >=1.5 to >=1.4, QuickCheck >=2.15 to >=2.14) so the package accepts the older versions present in the tree; standard CHDEPS idiom applied in the downward direction
Bump to 1.1.1.2 with hackage revision plus backporting the same tasty-lua CHDEPS loosening to the old 1.0.4 ebuild; duplicate of the pandoc/resolv CHDEPS lesson
Revbump combining CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION=3 and CHDEPS loosening (nothunks, primitive caret-union); no new idiom beyond patterns already captured in this batch

Bump that also drops a redundant quickcheck lower bound already guaranteed by the tree minimum and simplifies a '|| ( bitvec[gmp] ghc[gmp] )' dep to plain ghc[gmp] (bug 916191); mild dependency-hygiene cleanup
Dependency used an ||-of-two-ranges construct for dev-haskell/text even though the two ranges are effectively contiguous, making the dep string noisy and slower for the resolver.
root_cause: Upstream .cabal files express disjoint version ranges (text ^>=1.2 || ^>=2.0) which get transliterated 1:1 into portage || groups, but the gap between the ranges (1.3..2.0) contains no versions that were ever packaged.
fix_pattern: Collapse '|| ( ( >=text-1.2.3.0 <text-1.3 ) ( >=text-2.0 <text-2.2 ) )' into the single range '>=dev-haskell/text-1.2.3.0 <dev-haskell/text-2.2'.
rule: When an ||-group of version ranges covers a span with no real packaged versions in the gaps, replace it with one continuous >=min <max range.

problem: Haskell package failed to build (bug 960337) because building/compiling the package's Setup.hs no longer worked with the current GHC/Cabal.
root_cause: haskell-cabal packages ship their own Setup.hs; for Simple build-type packages the shipped Setup can bit-rot, while the eclass provides cabal-mksetup to generate a canonical minimal one.
fix_pattern: Override src_prepare, call the eclass default first, then cabal-mksetup to overwrite Setup.hs with a generated trivial one.
rule: When a Simple-build-type Haskell package fails while compiling its Setup.hs, add a src_prepare that runs haskell-cabal_src_prepare followed by cabal-mksetup.

problem: Ebuild carried manual CABAL_CHDEPS edits of the .cabal file to relax stale upstream bounds, and used an old hand-written SRC_URI, drifting from the current Hackage metadata revision.
root_cause: Hackage publishes metadata-only 'revisions' of .cabal files (relaxed bounds) without a new tarball; haskell-cabal.eclass can fetch these directly instead of the ebuild sed-ing bounds itself.
fix_pattern: Set CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION=<N> before inheriting haskell-cabal, drop the manual SRC_URI (eclass composes tarball + revN.cabal URLs, Manifest gains a -revN.cabal DIST entry), and delete the CABAL_CHDEPS hack.
rule: To pick up relaxed dependency bounds on a Haskell package without a new upstream release, set CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION=N instead of patching the .cabal with CABAL_CHDEPS.
snippet:
CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION=5

CABAL_FEATURES="lib profile haddock hoogle hscolour"
inherit haskell-cabal
# SRC_URI removed - eclass fetches ${P}.tar.gz plus rev${N}.cabal

problem: systemd unit used DynamicUser=yes; logrotate's 'su user group' directive could not match the dynamically-allocated UID, so log rotation clashed with the service (bug 869872).
root_cause: DynamicUser allocates a transient UID at service start, so any external subsystem that must reference the service's user by name/UID (logrotate su, chown'd state dirs) breaks.
fix_pattern: Drop DynamicUser, depend on acct-user/ + acct-group/ packages, set User=/Group= to the static account, and keep unprivileged port binding via CapabilityBoundingSet + AmbientCapabilities CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE. Ship the changed unit as a new files/-r1 name and revbump.
rule: When a daemon's files are touched by logrotate or other out-of-service tooling, use a static acct-user/acct-group in the systemd unit instead of DynamicUser, adding AmbientCapabilities for any needed privileged ports.

problem: Doc build via distutils_enable_sphinx failed (bug 977866) because the ebuild listed dev-python/alabaster as the theme dependency while docs/conf.py actually uses the furo theme.
root_cause: distutils_enable_sphinx's extra arguments are the doc-build BDEPENDs; they must mirror html_theme/extensions in the package's docs/conf.py, which upstream changed.
fix_pattern: Replace the wrong theme dep in the distutils_enable_sphinx call with dev-python/furo; commit also trimmed PYTHON_COMPAT to python3_{12..14} on the older ebuild.
rule: When a USE=doc sphinx build fails on a missing theme or extension, read docs/conf.py (html_theme, extensions) and make the distutils_enable_sphinx arguments match exactly.

problem: Rotated logs began with large runs of NUL bytes; logrotate copytruncate raced with the daemon which kept writing through its old file offset (bug 763495).
root_cause: copytruncate truncates the file while the daemon's fd still points past the old end; the next write re-extends the file, filling the hole with NULs. Any daemon that does not reopen its logfile is affected.
fix_pattern: Replace copytruncate with a postrotate script that hard-restarts the service; ship the new logrotate config under a -r1 filename in FILESDIR (no extra revbump here only because the ebuild version was introduced in the same push).
rule: For daemons that hold their logfile open and lack a reopen/HUP mechanism, do not use logrotate copytruncate; restart (or signal) the daemon in postrotate, and rename changed FILESDIR configs with an -r1 suffix plus revbump.
snippet:
/var/log/dnscrypt-proxy/*.log {
	su dnscrypt-proxy dnscrypt-proxy
	missingok
	notifempty
	sharedscripts
	postrotate
		/etc/init.d/dnscrypt-proxy restart > /dev/null
	endscript
}

problem: Version bump of a vendored go-module package whose example config references bare relative filenames that break when the daemon runs from a different working directory or under Gentoo Prefix.
root_cause: Upstream's example-dnscrypt-proxy.toml uses relative paths (cache_file = 'public-resolvers.md' etc.); installed as /etc config these must be absolute, and Prefix installs need the EPREFIX prepended.
fix_pattern: Carry a versioned FILESDIR patch rewriting each config path to '@GENTOO_PORTAGE_EPREFIX@/etc/...' or '@GENTOO_PORTAGE_EPREFIX@/var/cache/...', inherit prefix, and run eprefixify on the config in src_install before newins; refresh/rename the patch at each bump and keep the 9999 ebuild in sync.
rule: When shipping an upstream example config containing relative file paths, patch them to @GENTOO_PORTAGE_EPREFIX@-prefixed absolute paths and eprefixify the file during src_install.
snippet:
inherit fcaps go-module prefix systemd
...
eprefixify example-dnscrypt-proxy.toml
insinto /etc/dnscrypt-proxy
newins example-dnscrypt-proxy.toml dnscrypt-proxy.toml

problem: Package was marked stable on amd64 while its required LLVM 22 dependency chain was still only ~arch, creating an unsatisfiable/inconsistent stable dep graph.
root_cause: Stabilization (or a stale KEYWORDS line copied into a new version) happened without checking that all runtime/build deps are themselves stable on that arch.
fix_pattern: De-stabilize by changing KEYWORDS 'amd64' back to '~amd64' in the affected ebuild version.
rule: Never keep a stable keyword on a version whose dependencies are not stable on the same arch; if it slips through, drop back to ~arch rather than force-stabilizing the deps.

problem: meson configure probed device nodes beyond /dev/dri for the video-backdrop feature, triggering portage sandbox violations that a narrow 'addpredict /dev/dri' no longer covered.
root_cause: Build-system feature detection (gstreamer/video) opens device files at configure time; the sandbox denies access, and pinning individual device paths is brittle as probes change.
fix_pattern: Widen the exception to 'addpredict /dev' but scope it under the USE flag that triggers the probing, keeping the sandbox tight for other configurations.
rule: When configure-time hardware probing causes sandbox violations, addpredict the smallest sufficient path but broaden to the parent directory if probes keep moving, and guard it with the relevant USE flag.

problem: eselect pinentry module lacked third-party pinentry implementations (bemenu, tqt, fuzzel) in SYMLINK_TARGETS; new module version adds them.
Emacs package carried an unnecessary RDEPEND on app-emacs/transient (bundled with modern Emacs) and a stale BYTECOMPFLAGS='-L ./elisp' override that broke byte-compilation; both removed in a revbump.
dash 'read' mishandled NUL bytes in input (bug 977650); fixed by backporting the upstream git patch into a numbered FILESDIR patch series with a revbump to -r2.

cmake's test suite kept growing new Fortran-dependent tests, and the ebuild's hand-maintained exclusion list (CMAKE_SKIP_TESTS names) went stale every release, failing src_test on systems without gfortran (bug 835014).
root_cause: Excluding tests one-by-one by name cannot keep up when upstream adds tests; but ctest tags Fortran tests with a label, enabling category-wide exclusion.
fix_pattern: Declare 'local myctestargs=( -LE "Fortran" )' in src_test before calling cmake_src_test; cmake.eclass passes myctestargs to ctest, and -LE excludes every test carrying that label, present and future.
rule: When skipping a whole class of ctest tests, prefer label-based exclusion (myctestargs=( -LE <label> )) over enumerating test names, so future upstream additions are covered automatically.

problem: Build failed with binutils 2.46: "'FALSE' undeclared" in code and in the configure-time disassembler probe, because binutils removed the legacy bfd TRUE/FALSE macros and bfd_boolean.
root_cause: binutils >=2.46 dropped TRUE/FALSE from public bfd headers; disassembler()'s second argument is now plain C bool. Both the sources and the m4 conftest used the removed macros.
fix_pattern: Backport upstream patch replacing TRUE/FALSE with integer literals 1/0 (valid for both old bfd_boolean and new bool, so no binutils version guard needed). Because the patch touches m4/tools.m4, the ebuild must 'inherit autotools' and run eautoreconf in src_prepare.
rule: Whenever a carried patch modifies configure.ac or m4/ macros, inherit autotools and add eautoreconf to src_prepare; and for binutils-2.46 TRUE/FALSE breakage, substitute 1/0 to stay compatible with both old and new headers.

problem: Runtime SIGFPE in llvmpipe with a GCC 17 snapshot: lp_native_vector_width was 0 because repeated calls to util_get_cpu_caps() were CSE'd away before the CPU caps were initialized.
root_cause: The function was marked ATTRIBUTE_CONST (__attribute__((const))) despite reading lazily-initialized internal state; the const attribute forbids reading objects that affect the return value between invocations, so newer GCC legitimately optimized calls out.
fix_pattern: Patch removes the bogus const attribute from the inline getter. Deliberately no revbump: the miscompile only manifests with an unreleased compiler snapshot and mesa is expensive to rebuild, so rebuilding installed systems buys nothing.
rule: Treat __attribute__((const/pure)) on functions with lazy-init or cached internal state as a latent miscompilation bug under newer compilers; and skip the revbump when a fix only matters for unreleased toolchains on an expensive-to-build package.
snippet:
-static inline ATTRIBUTE_CONST const struct util_cpu_caps_t *
+static inline const struct util_cpu_caps_t *
 util_get_cpu_caps(void)

problem: Two issues: build failure with GCC 17 (missing #include <sstream>), and PATCHES/user patches could not be applied because S pointed at the autotools subdir Project/GNU/Library which contains only the build system, not the sources.
root_cause: S was set to the nested build-system directory for convenience, but eapply/eapply_user run relative to S, so any patch touching real sources (under Source/) could never apply; GCC 17 stopped transitively providing <sstream>.
fix_pattern: Set S to the tarball's top-level source dir (WORKDIR/MediaInfoLib) so patches apply normally, then 'cd Project/GNU/Library || die' at the start of each phase (src_prepare/configure/compile/install) that needs the build dir; add the one-line missing-include patch.
rule: Never point S at a nested build-system directory; keep S at the source root so PATCHES and user patches work, and cd into the build subdir inside each phase instead.

problem: ffmpeg-9999 configure failed after upstream removed the libcelt option; fix was deleting the stale --disable-libcelt flag from the live ebuild (routine live-ebuild sync).

Bump needed to keep the system-oniguruma unbundling coherent: the bundled vendor/oniguruma sets the true minimum system dep version, and the bundled copy still wasted time in eautoreconf even when unused; test failures were also opaque in logs.
root_cause: Packages that vendor a lib but support system copies silently raise their minimum dep with each bump; leftover vendored trees still get autoreconf'd; automake test harness hides failure detail in test-suite.log.
fix_pattern: Keep the min dep in a named variable with a sync comment (ONIGURUMA_MINPV='>=dev-libs/oniguruma-6.9.10' # Keep in sync with vendor/), rm -rf the vendored dir plus sed it out of Makefile.am before eautoreconf, and wrap emake check in nonfatal so test-suite.log can be cat'ed before die.
rule: When unbundling a vendored dependency, encode the bundled copy's version as a commented MINPV variable at each bump, delete the vendored tree pre-autoreconf, and dump test-suite.log on test failure.

problem: Header-only library failed to build with clang 20+/22 (dependent template call needed explicit template args) and bundled rapidjson + doctest copies (bugs 946812, 792444).
root_cause: Bundled header-only libs go stale and duplicate system copies; the clang failure was upstream code relying on a template-argument-deduction leniency newer clang rejects.
fix_pattern: Backport the upstream PR patch adding explicit template arguments; unbundle by 'rm -r' of the bundled dirs then sed include paths to the system locations (cereal/external/rapidjson -> rapidjson, "doctest.h" -> <doctest/doctest.h>), adding dev-cpp/doctest under test? BDEPEND; revbump.
rule: To unbundle header-only libraries, delete the bundled directory, sed the include paths to system headers, and add the system package as a (test-conditional) dependency so any drift fails loudly.
snippet:
rm unittests/doctest.h || die
sed -e 's/\"doctest\.h\"/<doctest\/doctest.h>/g' \
	-i unittests/common.hpp || die
...
BDEPEND="test? ( dev-cpp/doctest )"

problem: New version's CMake project lives in a subdirectory (QXlsx/) of the tarball and upstream forgot to set a sane SOVERSION and misplaced a public header, risking broken ABI tracking and installs.
root_cause: Some upstreams keep CMakeLists.txt in a subproject dir; cmake.eclass builds from CMAKE_USE_DIR, not S, so no S hack is needed; SOVERSION errors upstream require sed/patch plus subslot to signal ABI.
fix_pattern: Keep S at the tarball root and set CMAKE_USE_DIR="${S}/QXlsx"; sed SOVERSION to a unique value per release, express it as SLOT="0/${PV}" subslot with := deps downstream, and carry the upstream-PR patch for the header install list.
rule: When a CMake project's CMakeLists.txt is in a subdirectory, set CMAKE_USE_DIR instead of moving S, and mirror unstable sonames into the subslot so consumers rebuild on bump.

problem: Routine version add of an out-of-tree kernel module package; standard linux-mod-r1 + verify-sig template (MODULES_MAKEARGS, modules_post_process, CONFIG_CHECK) copied forward unchanged.
Build broke against >=linux-headers-7.1 because the kernel dropped the AF_ROSE/HW_ROSE UAPI bits that net-tools' ROSE support compiled against.
root_cause: Kernel headers periodically remove dead protocol UAPI; userland packages with compile-time protocol toggles must disable those features once the headers are gone, matching upstream's own plan.
fix_pattern: Gate the feature on the installed headers version at configure time: set_opt HAVE_AFROSE has_version '<sys-kernel/linux-headers-7.1' (same for HAVE_HWROSE), so old-header systems keep the feature and new ones silently drop it; applied to both release and 9999 ebuilds.
rule: When newer linux-headers remove an API a package optionally uses, make the configure knob conditional on has_version '<sys-kernel/linux-headers-X.Y' instead of hard-disabling or pinning headers.
snippet:
set_opt HAVE_AFROSE has_version '<sys-kernel/linux-headers-7.1'
set_opt HAVE_HWROSE has_version '<sys-kernel/linux-headers-7.1'

problem: systemd 261 unconditionally pulled in acct-user/systemd-imds and built the IMDS component even for users who do not want the cloud Instance Metadata Service daemon.
root_cause: A newly-added upstream component with its own dedicated system user was wired in unconditionally at the previous bump instead of being made optional.
fix_pattern: Introduce an 'imds' USE flag: metadata.xml <flag> description, IUSE entry, REQUIRED_USE='imds? ( curl )' for its library needs, move acct-user/systemd-imds under 'imds? ( ... )' in RDEPEND, and pass $(meson_feature imds); revbump since the default install changes.
rule: When a package grows a new optional daemon requiring a dedicated acct-user, gate the user package, the meson feature, and any library requirements behind one USE flag with matching REQUIRED_USE, and revbump.
snippet:
IUSE="... imds ..."
REQUIRED_USE="imds? ( curl )"
RDEPEND="...
	imds? ( acct-user/systemd-imds )"
...
$(meson_feature imds)

problem: OpenRC init script could restart the daemon into a broken state (no config validation on restart/reload), redefined start/stop needlessly, set a dangerous pidfile default, and pulled 'need net' although it binds to loopback by default.
root_cause: Hand-rolled start()/stop() functions and unconditional dependencies accumulate errors; OpenRC's declarative command/command_args plus RC_CMD-aware hooks handle these cases correctly.
fix_pattern: Use declarative command/command_args with default start/stop; run checkconfig from start_pre when RC_CMD != restart and from stop_pre when RC_CMD = restart (so a broken config refuses to stop a working service); implement reload via start-stop-daemon --signal HUP; drop 'need net' and document adding rc_need="net" only for non-loopback binds.
rule: In OpenRC scripts, validate config in start_pre/stop_pre keyed on RC_CMD so 'restart' cannot kill a running service with a broken config, and avoid 'need net' for loopback-only daemons.

problem: Routine version add of openrc 0.63.2; ebuild copied forward unchanged (notable only as an example of eapi9-ver's ver_replacing for one-time migration logic in pkg_postinst).
systemd needed newer musl features; the old dep allowed a patched musl revision (1.2.5-r8) that is being retired
root_cause: musl-specific requirements were carried by a Gentoo revision of musl; once musl 1.2.6 ships the features, the dep should point at the clean upstream version
fix_pattern: Bump the minimum inside the elibc_musl conditional dependency block from the patched revision to the plain upstream release; live/9999 ebuild only, no revbump (dep relaxation on a live ebuild)
rule: When a package needs musl-only minimum features, express it as an elibc_musl? ( >=sys-libs/musl-X ) conditional and retire -rN patched-musl pins as soon as an upstream release covers them

problem: PDEPEND on virtual/dist-kernel used ${PATCH_PV} (a derived, truncated version variable) so the pulled-in virtual could lag behind the actual kernel version installed
root_cause: a helper variable meant for SRC_URI construction leaked into a version-locked dependency; virtual/dist-kernel is versioned to match the full package PV
fix_pattern: Restore the dep to >=virtual/dist-kernel-${PV} so the virtual is bumped in lockstep with every kernel release
rule: When a package must pull a version-matched virtual (dist-kernel pattern), always version the dep with ${PV}, never with an upstream-tarball helper variable like PATCH_PV

problem: Overriding pkg_postinst to print an ewarn silently dropped the linux-mod-r1 eclass postinst work (module database update/depmod handling), bug 977784
root_cause: defining pkg_postinst in the ebuild replaces the eclass-exported phase entirely; the author forgot to chain back to the eclass phase function
fix_pattern: Call the eclass phase function as the first line of the overridden phase, and revbump (-r1) because installed-system behavior changes
rule: Whenever you override a phase that an inherited eclass exports (pkg_postinst, src_install, ...), explicitly call eclassname_phasename inside it unless you intend to fully replace it; fixing a missed call requires a revbump

problem: New Go release: upstream Makefile passes -s to go link (pre-stripped binary triggers QA/loses portage-controlled stripping and splitdebug), plus upstream moved GitHub org zyedidia/micro -> micro-editor/micro
root_cause: Go projects habitually hardcode -ldflags "-s -w"; -s strips the symbol table before portage can manage stripping. Upstream repo transfers break SRC_URI and metadata remote-id
fix_pattern: Ship a small files/ patch removing -s from the Makefile ldflags (keep -w and GOVARS); update SRC_URI to the new org and fix <remote-id type="github"> in metadata.xml; vendored deps come from a gentoo-golang-dist vendor tarball added to SRC_URI
rule: When bumping a Go package whose build system passes -s in ldflags, patch -s out so portage handles stripping; when upstream changes GitHub org, update both SRC_URI and metadata.xml remote-id in the same bump
snippet:
-	CGO_ENABLED=$(CGO_ENABLED) go build -trimpath -ldflags "-s -w $(GOVARS) ..."
+	CGO_ENABLED=$(CGO_ENABLED) go build -trimpath -ldflags "-w $(GOVARS) ..."
SRC_URI+=" https://github.com/gentoo-golang-dist/${PN}/releases/download/v${PV}/${P}-vendor.tar.xz"

problem: ~loong keyword had to be dropped because a dependency (jupyter stack dep chain, bug 970751) cannot be keyworded on loong
root_cause: an arch loses support in one package deep in a dep chain; every reverse dependency carrying that keyword now has an unsatisfiable dep graph on that arch
fix_pattern: Remove the arch keyword from KEYWORDS in all affected ebuild versions across the whole revdep chain in one coordinated batch of commits, all referencing the same tracker bug
rule: When dekeywording an arch, sweep the entire reverse-dependency chain in the same batch (same Bug: reference) instead of leaving broken keyword graphs; a single-package dekeyword is almost never complete
snippet:
-KEYWORDS="amd64 arm arm64 ~loong ppc64 ~riscv x86"
+KEYWORDS="amd64 arm arm64 ppc64 ~riscv x86"

problem: Part of the same ~loong dekeyword batch (bug 970751) as jupyterlab-server; keyword removed from all live versions
Part of the same ~loong dekeyword batch (bug 970751); keyword removed from all live versions
Part of the same ~loong dekeyword batch (bug 970751); keyword removed
Package linking against clang/LLVM C++ libraries did not build with LLVM 21 due to API churn (getIdentifier removal, OpenCLKernelAttr rename, NestedNameSpecifier enum change)
root_cause: LLVM C++ API breaks every major release; LLVM_COMPAT upper bound gated the package to <=20 until code was fixed
fix_pattern: Add a new revision (-r1) that extends LLVM_COMPAT to the new slot and carries a files/ patch backported from the upstream PR, using #if LLVM_VERSION_MAJOR > 20 guards so one patch works across all slots in LLVM_COMPAT; old revision stays for users on stable LLVM
rule: To enable a new LLVM slot for a clang-libs consumer, backport the upstream compat patch with LLVM_VERSION_MAJOR preprocessor guards and widen LLVM_COMPAT in a revbump, keeping the unpatched revision behind it

problem: Rust package using llvm-r1 (libclang for bindgen) rejected newly-added LLVM slot 22
root_cause: LLVM_COMPAT is an explicit whitelist; new LLVM slots need every consumer to opt in even when nothing else changes, because bindgen uses libclang's stable C API
fix_pattern: Widen LLVM_COMPAT upper bound in place after a test build; no revbump needed since only accepted build-time deps are relaxed and the installed image is unchanged
rule: For cargo packages inheriting llvm-r1 for bindgen, extending LLVM_COMPAT to a new slot is usually a test-build-then-widen one-liner and does not warrant a revbump (dep relaxation, build-time only)

problem: Same LLVM_COMPAT widening to slot 22 as sequoia-sop ({17..20} -> {17..22})
Same LLVM_COMPAT widening to slot 22 ({16..20} -> {16..22})
Same LLVM_COMPAT widening to slot 22 ({18..20} -> {18..22})
3.1.0 bump: upstream setup.py imports pyproject_parser (a niche dep not worth packaging), and the package needs both the .NET and Python build systems driven in one ebuild
root_cause: upstream added a build-time-only dependency that stdlib tomllib can replace; mixed-runtime package needs dotnet-pkg (NUGETS, nuget_writeconfig) and distutils-r1 phases composed manually
fix_pattern: Patch setup.py to load pyproject.toml with stdlib tomllib instead of pyproject_parser (avoids introducing a new tree package); compose eclasses by explicitly calling both phase functions in pkg_setup/src_prepare/src_configure; pin nuget deps via NUGETS + SRC_URI+=" ${NUGET_URIS} " and guard disk usage with check-reqs
rule: When an upstream build script pulls a niche pure-parsing dependency, patch it to use the Python stdlib equivalent (tomllib for TOML) rather than packaging the dep; when mixing two build-system eclasses, explicitly chain each eclass's phase function in every overridden phase
snippet:
-from pyproject_parser import PyProject
+import tomllib
...
-        pyproject = PyProject.load("pyproject.toml")
+        with open("pyproject.toml", "rb") as f:
+            pyproject = tomllib.load(f)
src_configure() {
	dotnet-pkg_src_configure
	distutils-r1_src_configure
}

problem: Routine version bump to 2.5.12 carrying over existing runstatedir/glib-compile-resources seds and CONFIG_CHECK; nothing new introduced
RDEPEND listed bare dev-python/docutils without [${PYTHON_USEDEP}], so docutils could be installed only for Python interpreters the package's chosen single Python target cannot use
root_cause: in a python-single-r1 consumer (here a Ruby package shelling out to rst2html), every dev-python/* dep must be bound to the selected implementation; a bare dep gives no such guarantee
fix_pattern: Wrap the dep in $(python_gen_cond_dep '...[${PYTHON_USEDEP}]') and revbump (-r2) since the runtime dependency spec changed for installed copies
rule: Any dev-python/* dependency in an ebuild inheriting python-single-r1/python-r1 must carry [${PYTHON_USEDEP}] via python_gen_cond_dep (single) or directly (python-r1); fixing a missing one is a revbump

problem: Package blocked python 3.14 rollout; PYTHON_COMPAT still capped at 3.13 and included EOL-bound 3.10/3.11
root_cause: PYTHON_COMPAT is a static whitelist that must be moved forward with each interpreter cycle
fix_pattern: Shift the compat window rather than only appending: python3_{10..13} -> python3_{12..14}, dropping targets scheduled for removal in the same edit (and refresh the copyright year)
rule: When enabling a new Python target after testing, slide the PYTHON_COMPAT window forward — add the new target and drop soon-to-be-removed old ones in one commit; no revbump needed for compat widening

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window shift {11..13} -> {12..14} for py3.14 rollout
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT extension python3_13 -> python3_{13..14}
PYTHON_COMPAT {11..13} -> {12..15}; python-any-r1 build-time-only usage allows enabling even pre-release py3.15 early
Routine version bump to 5.10.2 closing a version-bump bug; no structural ebuild changes

While enabling py3.14, the ebuild still used DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit (full flit instead of flit_core), fetched a GitHub tarball needing MY_P/S juggling, and carried a stale blocker and duplicated BDEPEND
root_cause: packages needing only the flit_core backend should not pull full flit; pypi sdists remove the need for MY_P/S remapping of underscore project names
fix_pattern: Switch DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 from flit to flit-core, replace the GitHub SRC_URI with 'inherit pypi' (drops MY_P and S), and delete obsolete !dev-python/... blockers and redundant BDEPEND during the touch-up
rule: When touching an old python ebuild, downgrade DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit to flit-core if upstream only needs the backend, and prefer the pypi eclass over GitHub tarballs when the sdist is complete — it removes S/MY_P boilerplate
snippet:
-DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit
+DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit-core
-inherit distutils-r1
+inherit distutils-r1 pypi
(SRC_URI, MY_P and S= lines deleted)

problem: Migrating from a GitHub tarball to the pypi eclass for a project whose PyPI name keeps hyphens: default PEP 503 normalization would compute the wrong sdist filename
root_cause: the pypi eclass normalizes project names (hyphens to underscores) by default; older sdists were uploaded un-normalized
fix_pattern: Set PYPI_NO_NORMALIZE=1 before 'inherit pypi' so the original distribution filename is fetched; drop the GitHub SRC_URI
rule: When switching a package to the pypi eclass and the fetch 404s because the sdist name is not PEP 503-normalized, set PYPI_NO_NORMALIZE=1 instead of reverting to GitHub tarballs
snippet:
DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=setuptools
PYPI_NO_NORMALIZE=1
PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..14} )
inherit distutils-r1 pypi

problem: py3.14 enablement with test-suite housekeeping: EPYTEST_PLUGINS updated to declare pytest-xdist alongside pytest-mock, keyring added to test deps, stale EPYTEST_DESELECT entry removed after upstream fix
poetry RDEPENDed on poetry-plugin-export while the plugin depends on poetry, creating a circular dependency that complicates bootstrapping and py3.14 migration
root_cause: the plugin dep was kept out of inertia; upstream made poetry-plugin-export optional starting with poetry 2.0.0
fix_pattern: Drop the RDEPEND line entirely after confirming in the upstream changelog that the plugin is no longer required; users who want the functionality install the plugin themselves
rule: When a package and its plugin depend on each other, check whether upstream has made the plugin optional and break the cycle by removing the parent-to-plugin RDEPEND rather than hacking around it with use-conditionals

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT window shift python3_{11..13} -> python3_{12..14} to enable py3.14 (bug 973893); no other change needed.

While enabling py3.14 the maintainer also corrected DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=flit to flit-core; the wrong backend value pulls in the full dev-python/flit tool instead of the minimal flit_core backend the package actually declares.
root_cause: pyproject.toml declares build-backend flit_core.buildapi; the ebuild's DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 must name the matching Gentoo backend value (flit-core), not the superset tool (flit).
fix_pattern: Set DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 to the exact backend from pyproject.toml [build-system].build-backend: flit_core.buildapi -> flit-core, hatchling.build -> hatchling, setuptools.build_meta -> setuptools.
rule: When adding or touching a distutils-r1 ebuild, read pyproject.toml's build-backend and set DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517 to the matching minimal value; flit_core.buildapi means flit-core, not flit.

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..14} enabling py3.14 (bug 973870); one-line change.
scipy-1.18.0 needed >=dev-libs/boost-1.91.0, but that boost version was not yet keyworded on most arches, which would leave scipy with unsatisfiable dependencies there.
root_cause: Raising a minimum dependency version narrows the set of arches where the depgraph is satisfiable; keeping the old KEYWORDS line would produce NonsolvableDeps on every arch lacking the new boost.
fix_pattern: Bump the DEPEND bound and simultaneously drop KEYWORDS, keeping the old line as a comment with the reason so arch teams can re-add keywords once the dep is keyworded; in a conditional block use ':' as a no-op placeholder.
rule: When you raise a minimum dep version that is not keyworded on all of the package's arches, comment out KEYWORDS with a note ('due to X being not keyworded') instead of shipping unsatisfiable deps.
snippet:
if [[ ${PV} != *rc* ]]; then
		# due to dev-libs/boost being not keyworded
		# KEYWORDS="~amd64 ~arm ~arm64 ..."
		:
	fi
...
->=dev-libs/boost-1.89.0
+>=dev-libs/boost-1.91.0

problem: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} enabling py3.15; one-line change.
Routine PYTHON_COMPAT bump to python3_{12..15} enabling py3.15; one-line change.
telegram-desktop bundled the header-only TooManyCooks library; unbundling it also surfaced a missing qtshadertools build tool dep and cmark-gfm being consumed via a nonexistent find_package() config.
root_cause: Bundled third-party code hides transitive deps: a header-only library contributes no soname, so its own runtime deps (hwloc) must be declared with := on the consumer to trigger subslot rebuilds; upstream cmake_helpers only supported find_package(cmark-gfm) which no distro installs, while cmark-gfm does ship a pkg-config file; the build silently used the qsb binary from qtshadertools.
fix_pattern: Add the unbundled header-only lib to CDEPEND plus its runtime dep with subslot operator on the consumer (sys-apps/hwloc:= with an explanatory NOTE comment); patch the cmake to fall back to pkg_check_modules(IMPORTED_TARGET libcmark-gfm) and delete the find_package() line; add dev-qt/qtshadertools to BDEPEND for the qsb binary; keep the src_prepare sed that forces every pkg_check_modules() to REQUIRED so missing system libs fail loudly.
rule: When unbundling a header-only library, put its transitive shared-lib deps with := directly on the consumer (the header-only package cannot trigger rebuilds), and prefer patching upstream CMake to use pkg-config when no distro installs the find_package config files.
snippet:
+	dev-cpp/toomanycooks
+	sys-apps/hwloc:=
+# NOTE: sys-apps/hwloc ... needs to cause SLOT rebuilds here,
+#       as dev-cpp/toomanycooks is header-only
+    find_package(PkgConfig)
+    pkg_check_modules(DESKTOP_APP_CMARK_GFM IMPORTED_TARGET libcmark-gfm)
+    target_link_libraries(external_cmark_gfm INTERFACE PkgConfig::DESKTOP_APP_CMARK_GFM)

problem: The previous cmark-gfm patch installed Gentoo-only cmark-gfm-config.cmake files to make find_package() work; no other distro or upstream ships them, so consumers built against Gentoo would silently depend on nonportable files.
root_cause: Adding distro-unique CMake package config files creates an ecosystem fork: downstream projects start relying on find_package() succeeding only on Gentoo, and the patch must be carried forever.
fix_pattern: Trim the patch to only what upstream accepted (CMake 4 port, FindPython migration), drop the config-file installation, and instead patch the consumer (telegram-desktop) to use the pkg-config file that upstream already installs; document the reasoning in metadata.xml/PR discussion.
rule: Do not patch a library to install find_package() config files that neither upstream nor other distros ship; patch consumers to use the upstream-provided pkg-config file instead.

problem: Version bump of a heavily-bundling package (6.8.2 -> 6.9.3) required a full re-audit: new bundled deps appeared (cmark-gfm, MicroTeX, TooManyCooks), one patch became obsolete (fixed upstream), one keyword had to be dropped, and PYTHON_COMPAT updated.
root_cause: Packages that vendor many third-party libraries change their dependency surface on every release; the ebuild maintains an explicit keep-list of bundled dirs and greedily deletes the rest, so each bump must reconcile that list with the new tarball.
fix_pattern: On bump: diff the ThirdParty/ directory against the keep-list, decide bundle-vs-package for each newcomer (with a TODO comment), drop patches fixed upstream, add new system deps to CDEPEND, and keep the sed that forces pkg_check_modules to REQUIRED so any silently-missing system lib aborts configure.
rule: For packages with vendored dependencies, maintain an explicit keep-list of bundled dirs, rm everything else in src_prepare, and re-audit that list on every version bump.
snippet:
local keep=(
	GSL  # ...
	tgcalls  # Telegram-specific, no stable releases
	TooManyCooks  # TODO: consider packaging
)
for x in Telegram/ThirdParty/*; do
	has "${x##*/}" "${keep[@]}" || rm -r "${x}" || die
done

problem: Building Telegram Desktop 6.9.x failed because installed cmark-gfm lacked CMake config files and its exported cmake files landed in wrong destinations; tests also lost their Python interpreter under CMake >=3.27.
root_cause: Upstream cmark-gfm's CMake install() EXPORT destinations predate GNUInstallDirs conventions, ships no -config.cmake, and its tests use the FindPythonInterp module that CMake 3.27 removed; the ebuild also declared a frozen subslot 0/0.29.0 that would not change on library version bumps.
fix_pattern: Revbump with a patch (submitted upstream) fixing EXPORT destinations to ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}/cmake/<name>, generating config/config-version files via CMakePackageConfigHelpers, and porting find_package(PythonInterp 3) to find_package(Python 3 COMPONENTS Interpreter) with Python_EXECUTABLE; also change SLOT="0/0.29.0" to SLOT="0/${MY_PV}" so the subslot tracks the real version.
rule: When a consumer needs files the library does not install, revbump the library with an upstreamable patch rather than hacking the consumer; and derive subslots from the version variable (SLOT="0/${MY_PV}"), never hardcode them.
snippet:
-SLOT="0/0.29.0"
+SLOT="0/${MY_PV}"
# CMake >=3.27 port:
-find_package(PythonInterp 3 REQUIRED)
+find_package(Python 3 COMPONENTS Interpreter REQUIRED)
-${PYTHON_EXECUTABLE}
+${Python_EXECUTABLE}

problem: New package import from GURU (cmark-gfm needed by telegram-desktop); standard cmake ebuild with ver_rs to map Gentoo 0.29.0.13 to upstream 0.29.0.gfm.13 tag; no novel idiom beyond MY_PV="$(ver_rs 3 '.gfm.')".
LICENSE listed bundled-component licenses alphabetically (BSD Boost-1.0 CC0-1.0 GPL-3+ MIT) so the package's actual primary license GPL-3+ was buried mid-list.
root_cause: Gentoo convention is that the first entry in LICENSE should be the primary license of the package itself, with bundled/vendored component licenses after it; alphabetical sorting hides what the package really is.
fix_pattern: Reorder LICENSE to put the project's own license first and keep the per-component comment block above it explaining which component contributes which license.
rule: In LICENSE, list the package's primary license first and document per-component licenses in a comment; do not just sort alphabetically.
snippet:
