# mu: GPL-3+
# + tl: CC0-1.0
# + variant-lite: Boost-1.0
-LICENSE="BSD Boost-1.0 CC0-1.0 GPL-3+ MIT"
+LICENSE="GPL-3+ BSD Boost-1.0 CC0-1.0 MIT"

problem: ansifilter-2.22 failed to build with gcc-17 (bug 976605) due to a missing declaration that older libstdc++ headers provided transitively.
root_cause: Each new GCC/libstdc++ release prunes transitive includes from standard headers, so sources that used std::stringstream without including <sstream> stop compiling.
fix_pattern: Add a one-line missing-include patch in files/ (here: #include <sstream> in stylecolour.h), reference the Gentoo bug and the upstream MR in the patch header, and submit it upstream; no revbump needed since the package did not build at all.
rule: When a package breaks with a new GCC version, first suspect missing standard-library includes; fix with a minimal include patch carrying bug/upstream-MR URLs, and skip the revbump because previously-failing builds produce no installed copies to fix.

problem: godot's SCons build stopped honoring the AR variable (and never honored RANLIB), so builds used the host ar instead of the toolchain archiver (bug 977691), breaking cross/LLVM setups.
root_cause: Upstream removed AR from its SCons Options processing; SCons does not inherit AR/RANLIB from the environment unless detect.py explicitly copies them, and passing AR= on the scons command line was no longer processed.
fix_pattern: Patch platform detect.py to read AR and RANLIB from os.environ into the SCons env, and in the ebuild replace per-variable scons arguments with tc-export AR CC CXX RANLIB before calling escons so the exported environment variables are picked up.
rule: For SCons packages, do not trust command-line VAR=value to survive upstream refactors: export toolchain variables with tc-export and patch detect.py to import them from os.environ, then verify build.log shows the right commands.
snippet:
src_compile() {
	tc-export AR CC CXX RANLIB
	...
}
# patch platform/linuxbsd/detect.py:
+    env["AR"] = os.environ["AR"]
+    env["RANLIB"] = os.environ["RANLIB"]

problem: On alpha, glibc built only generic C mem*/str* routines even when CFLAGS carried -mcpu=ev6/ev67, leaving the tuned assembly unused.
root_cause: glibc selects sysdeps assembly by the host triplet machine prefix (sysdeps/alpha/preconfigure: machine=alpha/$machine), not by codegen flags; a bare alpha-*-* CHOST never reaches sysdeps/alpha/alphaev*.
fix_pattern: In setup_target_flags(), map $(get-flag mcpu) values to the most specific sysdeps directory that exists and rewrite the configure host via CTARGET_OPT (mirroring existing sparc handling), leaving generic build when -mcpu is unset.
rule: When a toolchain package selects arch-specific code paths from the configure triplet rather than CFLAGS, derive a more specific --host prefix from the user's -mcpu/-march via get-flag and CTARGET_OPT instead of expecting flags to matter.

problem: An EAPI-8 ebuild still called the removed eutils function epatch inside a USE-conditional branch (use poll && epatch ...), which only explodes at build time for users with that USE flag set.
root_cause: Dead eclass function calls hide inside conditional code paths that CI and most users never execute; epatch is gone in modern EAPIs and must be eapply.
fix_pattern: Replace epatch with eapply (which accepts files from DISTDIR just as well) and revbump so affected installs re-emerge with the working recipe.
rule: Audit USE-conditional branches for removed eclass functions (epatch, epause, etc.) when bumping EAPI, since routine testing with default USE will not exercise them; fix with eapply plus a revbump.

problem: A freshly-added gentoo-sources-6.12.94 ebuild was committed with stable keywords (amd64 arm x86 ...) copied from the previous version, stabilizing an untested release instantly.
root_cause: Copying the previous ebuild carries its stable KEYWORDS forward; new versions must always start testing (~arch) and go through the stabilization process.
fix_pattern: Edit KEYWORDS to all-~arch on the new version (no revbump needed, keyword changes never need one).
rule: After copying an ebuild for a version bump, always demote every stable keyword to ~arch before committing; a scripted 'ekeyword ~all' step in the bump pipeline prevents accidental early stabilization.
snippet:
-KEYWORDS="~alpha amd64 arm arm64 ~hppa ... ppc ppc64 ~riscv ~s390 ~sparc x86"
+KEYWORDS="~alpha ~amd64 ~arm ~arm64 ~hppa ... ~ppc ~ppc64 ~riscv ~s390 ~sparc ~x86"

problem: Routine 'sync live' commit keeping the -9999 ebuild aligned with release ebuilds (PYTHON_COMPAT window and min versions of snakeoil/pkgcore deps).
Routine 'sync live' commit updating the -9999 ebuild's PYTHON_COMPAT and snakeoil minimum to match the latest release ebuild.
zfs userland build failed for users because upstream forces -Werror in a FreeBSD-conditional part of Makefile.am, turning harmless new-compiler warnings into build errors (bug 904378).
root_cause: -Werror is hardcoded in the build system, so it cannot be stripped via CFLAGS filtering; every new GCC release generates new warnings that abort the build.
fix_pattern: sed the generated Makefile.in after eautoreconf to remove the -Werror occurrences, with a comment referencing the bug; done in both release and 9999 ebuilds.
rule: When upstream hardcodes -Werror in its build files, strip it in src_prepare with a targeted sed (after eautoreconf so it survives regeneration) rather than trying to filter it from user flags.
snippet:
eautoreconf
+	# strip forced -Werror, #904378
+	sed -i '/BUILD_FREEBSD_TRUE/s/-Werror //g' Makefile.in || die

problem: boost installed cobalt's headers but b2 silently skipped building libboost_cobalt, so consumers linking it failed at link time while the boost build itself succeeded (bug 977237).
root_cause: The global boost build pins -std=c++17, and cobalt's Jamfile declares a cxx20_hdr_concepts requirement; b2 treats an unmet requirement as 'silently skip this target', not as an error, so the missing library went unnoticed.
fix_pattern: Patch the component's Jamfile to drop the requires cxx20_hdr_concepts gate and inject per-component flags (<toolset>gcc:<cxxflags>"-std=c++20 -fcoroutines") into both build and test Jamfiles, then revbump; verify the .so actually appears in the image.
rule: When a multi-component build system can silently skip targets whose requirements are unmet, do not raise the global standard: inject per-component language flags and verify the expected libraries are really installed after a bump.

problem: lgi tests failed only under lua-5.1 because tests assert exact values like 0xffffffff and 0x80000000, which plain Lua 5.1 represents as doubles and cannot round-trip exactly (bug 836839).
root_cause: Lua 5.1 has no integer type; large unsigned constants lose precision in FP conversion, so the failures are an implementation limitation, not a packaging bug.
fix_pattern: Carry a small test patch gating the affected assertions on the runtime implementation (_VERSION == 'Lua 5.1' and not jit) instead of dropping lua5-1 from LUA_COMPAT or skipping whole test files; link the bug and the upstream issue in the patch header.
rule: For multi-implementation test failures caused by an old interpreter's numeric limits, patch the tests to skip only the unrepresentable assertions per-implementation rather than dropping the implementation or the whole test suite.
snippet:
+   -- skip large unsigned on Lua 5.1
+   if not (_VERSION == "Lua 5.1" and not jit) then
+      checkv(R.test_uint32(0xffffffff), 0xffffffff, 'number')
+   end

problem: exfatprogs' configure script broke when /bin/sh is a stricter POSIX shell because the script contains a bashism (upstream PR 372 pending).
root_cause: Autoconf configure scripts run under CONFIG_SHELL/sh; a bashism only manifests on systems where sh is dash or another non-bash shell.
fix_pattern: Force bash as the configure interpreter with CONFIG_SHELL pointing into BROOT, as a clearly-commented temporary workaround linked to the upstream PR, in both release and 9999 ebuilds.
rule: When a configure script contains bashisms not yet fixed upstream, override src_configure with CONFIG_SHELL="${BROOT}"/bin/bash econf and link the upstream fix so the workaround can be dropped later.

problem: exfatprogs configure uses PKG_CHECK_MODULES (for the new util-linux dep) but the ebuild did not declare virtual/pkgconfig, so builds fail on minimal systems without pkgconf installed.
root_cause: pkg-config is a build-host tool discovered at configure time; without a BDEPEND it is only present by chance, so the omission escapes testing on full systems.
fix_pattern: Add virtual/pkgconfig to BDEPEND (note BDEPEND+= appends when the variable is composed elsewhere).
rule: Whenever configure/CMake/meson files call pkg-config (PKG_CHECK_MODULES, pkg_check_modules, dependency()), declare BDEPEND=virtual/pkgconfig; grepping the build system for these macros is a scriptable pre-commit check.

problem: The live (9999) ebuild tracked upstream's default branch after development moved to a feature branch, and new code added deps needing musl handling.
root_cause: Upstream development for the next release happens on the exfat-next branch; new fts_* usage links against libc fts, which musl does not provide.
fix_pattern: Set EGIT_BRANCH in the 9999 conditional block and add DEPEND on sys-apps/util-linux plus the musl-conditional fts library.
rule: When code uses fts_open/fts_read, add elibc_musl? ( sys-libs/fts-standalone ) to deps since musl lacks fts; and pin live ebuilds to the actual development branch via EGIT_BRANCH.

problem: Routine linux-headers 7.1 version bump using kernel-2 eclass template (PATCH_VER conditional SRC_URI, musl patch carried forward); package-specific.
Adds a one-line comment linking wiki maintenance notes into the ebuild; documentation-only, no packaging technique.
Routine linux-headers 7.0 version bump; identical template to other versions, package-specific.
New GCC snapshot ebuild must keep using a shared patchset tarball even though some patches in it were merged upstream, causing eapply failures on the newer snapshot.
root_cause: Patchset tarballs are cut per release branch and reused across weekly snapshots; individual patches land upstream between snapshot dates so the tarball goes stale patch-by-patch.
fix_pattern: Maintain an upstreamed_patches array in src_prepare and rm those files from the unpacked patch directory before calling the eclass prepare, instead of respinning the whole patchset tarball.
rule: When sharing one patchset tarball across multiple snapshot/version ebuilds, delete individually-upstreamed patches via an explicit array in src_prepare rather than forking the tarball.

problem: USE=python built the pybind11 bindings but the resulting module was never actually importable: the .so was not installed into site-packages (and CMake could pick a random newest Python).
root_cause: Upstream CMake builds the extension but has no proper install rule for the python module, and FindPython defaults to the newest interpreter on the system rather than the one selected by python-single-r1.
fix_pattern: Pass -DPython_EXECUTABLE="${PYTHON}" in mycmakeargs to pin the interpreter, then hand-install the built extension in src_install with python_moduleinto/python_domodule, generating a tiny __init__.py wrapper so it imports as a package.
rule: When a CMake package builds python bindings without installing them, pin Python_EXECUTABLE to ${PYTHON} and install the built .so yourself with python_moduleinto + python_domodule from ${BUILD_DIR}.
snippet:
use python && mycmakeargs+=( -DPython_EXECUTABLE="${PYTHON}" )
# in src_install:
if use python; then
	echo "from .aftl import *" > "${BUILD_DIR}"/python/__init__.py || die
	python_moduleinto aftl
	python_domodule "${BUILD_DIR}"/python/{__init__.py,*.so}
fi

problem: Live ebuild installed the python extension into a bogus location (/usr/aftl.cpython-*.so) and the real module was never importable.
root_cause: Upstream CMake install rule drops the extension at an incorrect prefix path instead of site-packages; nobody had ever tested importing the module.
fix_pattern: Same manual install as the release ebuild (python_moduleinto/python_domodule) plus explicitly rm the mis-installed file from ${ED} so it does not ship: rm "${ED}"/usr/aftl.cpython-*.so || die. Keep the live ebuild in sync with the fixed release ebuild.
rule: After fixing an install bug in a release ebuild, port the same fix to the -9999 ebuild in the same series, and delete upstream's mis-installed artifacts from ${ED} with an || die so regressions are caught.

problem: Release tarballs were fetched without any upstream signature verification even though upstream publishes detached .sig files.
root_cause: verify-sig was never hooked up; the package also has a custom src_unpack (live/non-live split), so the eclass default unpack verification would not run automatically.
fix_pattern: Conditionally inherit verify-sig only in the non-live branch, add the .sig under a verify-sig? ( ) SRC_URI group, add BDEPEND on the sec-keys package and set VERIFY_SIG_OPENPGP_KEY_PATH, then call verify-sig_verify_detached manually inside the custom src_unpack before default.
rule: When adding verify-sig to an ebuild that overrides src_unpack, you must call verify-sig_verify_detached yourself in src_unpack; also guard the inherit/SRC_URI/BDEPEND so the live (9999) variant stays unaffected.
snippet:
inherit verify-sig  # non-live branch only
SRC_URI="... verify-sig? ( https://download.qemu.org/${MY_P}.tar.xz.sig )"
BDEPEND+=" verify-sig? ( sec-keys/openpgp-keys-mdroth )"
VERIFY_SIG_OPENPGP_KEY_PATH=/usr/share/openpgp-keys/mdroth.asc
# in custom src_unpack:
use verify-sig && verify-sig_verify_detached "${DISTDIR}"/${MY_P}.tar.xz{,.sig}

problem: Routine security bump to qemu 10.2.3; only notable act is resetting stable KEYWORDS (amd64 arm64 ppc64 x86) back to ~arch in the copied ebuild, which is standard bump hygiene.
Version bump to 10.0.10 where a FILESDIR patch listed in PATCHES no longer matched the new sources (optionrom -Wl,--no-error-rwx-segments patch).
root_cause: Carried patches drift: upstream code moves so a patch written against 7.2.16 stops applying, requiring a rebased copy for the new version while old ebuilds still need the old copy.
fix_pattern: Keep both patch generations in files/ (versioned filenames like ${PN}-7.2.16-... and ${PN}-10.2.2-...) and point each ebuild's PATCHES at the generation that applies; also reset stable keywords to ~arch in the new ebuild.
rule: When a carried FILESDIR patch stops applying on a bump, add a rebased copy under a new version-prefixed filename instead of editing the old one, so older ebuilds keep manifesting/applying cleanly.

problem: Security vulnerability (CVE-2026-46863) fixed upstream but no release available for the 8.0 LTS branch users were on.
root_cause: Upstream ships fixes in newer releases only; the stable branch ebuild needed the fix without waiting for/moving to a new upstream tarball.
fix_pattern: Cherry-pick the exact upstream commits into files/${P}-CVE-XXXX-XXXXX.patch (keeping full upstream commit headers and putting the Gentoo bug URL as the first line), append it to PATCHES, and copy the ebuild to -r1 (revbump) so users rebuild.
rule: To backport a security fix, put the upstream commits verbatim in a files/ patch named after the CVE with the bug URL at the top, add it to PATCHES, and revbump instead of touching the existing revision.

problem: Upstream (mysql.com) removes release tarballs from the primary download path once a newer version ships, breaking SRC_URI for anything but the newest ebuild; signatures are only served via a query-string URL with no usable filename.
root_cause: Vendors with rolling download pages move old artifacts to an archive host, and their GPG signature endpoint is a dynamic URL (?file=...&p=23) that portage cannot name sanely.
fix_pattern: List both the current download URL and the archive URL for the same DIST file in SRC_URI (portage tries each in order), and use the '-> filename' rename operator to give the query-string .asc URL a proper local name; slot per LTS branch with SLOT="$(ver_cut 1-2)".
rule: When an upstream moves old releases to an archive host, put both URLs in SRC_URI for the same distfile so old ebuilds keep fetching; use '->' to rename dynamic/query-string URLs to stable local filenames.

problem: Package failed on musl (gettext symbols not found by configure) and its test Makefile used non-portable 'echo -e/-ne', breaking with POSIX /bin/sh.
root_cause: On musl, gettext symbols live in libc but autoconf's gnugettext probe fails without a hint, and upstream assumed bash echo semantics in Makefile.am test recipes.
fix_pattern: Export the autoconf cache variable gt_cv_func_gnugettext1_libc=yes before econf when elibc_musl, patch 'echo -ne' to 'printf' in the test recipes, and depend on virtual/libiconv + virtual/libintl instead of sys-devel/gettext when only precompiled .gmo files are used.
rule: For musl-only configure misdetections, override the specific autoconf cache variable (gt_cv_*/ac_cv_*) via export before econf instead of patching configure; replace 'echo -e' with 'printf' for POSIX shell portability.

problem: Wine's bundled stripped-down PE ffmpeg (always built, independent of USE=ffmpeg) needs nasm on x86/amd64, otherwise the build falls back to a less optimized path or fails.
root_cause: A bundled component's assembler requirement is unrelated to the similarly-named USE flag that controls the *system* library, so the dep is easy to misattribute to USE=ffmpeg.
fix_pattern: Add the assembler as an arch-conditional BDEPEND (amd64? / x86?) unconditionally of the USE flag, with a commit message documenting why it is not tied to USE=ffmpeg; deliberately skip adding a USE flag for a marginal optimization toggle.
rule: When a bundled copy of a library needs a build tool regardless of the USE flag controlling the system copy, express it as an arch-conditional BDEPEND rather than hanging it off the USE flag.

problem: Same nasm-for-bundled-PE-ffmpeg arch-conditional BDEPEND as the wine-staging commit; duplicate application of that fix.
A single test (tunnel_next_upstream.t) fails under FEATURES=network-sandbox, blocking a security stablereq on test failures.
root_cause: The test needs real network access that portage's network-sandbox denies; upstream test is fine, the sandbox environment is the constraint.
fix_pattern: Delete the offending test file in src_prepare guarded by 'use test', with the bug number in a comment, rather than RESTRICTing all tests or patching the test.
rule: When only specific tests fail under network-sandbox, rm those test files conditionally in src_prepare (with bug reference) instead of adding RESTRICT=test for the whole suite.
snippet:
src_prepare() {
	nginx_src_prepare
	if use test ; then
		# Fails with network-sandbox (bug #976129)
		rm "${NGX_TESTS_S}"/tunnel_next_upstream.t || die
	fi
}

problem: Ebuild only supported up to py3.13 and did not verify PyPI artifact provenance.
root_cause: Routine PYTHON_COMPAT staleness; pypi.eclass gained provenance-attestation verification that needs the upstream repo declared to activate.
fix_pattern: Bump PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} ) after testing, and set PYPI_VERIFY_REPO=<upstream github repo> so the pypi eclass fetches and checks the .provenance file (new Manifest DIST entry appears for it).
rule: When bumping PYTHON_COMPAT on a pypi-eclass package, also consider setting PYPI_VERIFY_REPO to the upstream repository to enable sigstore provenance verification of the sdist.
snippet:
DISTUTILS_USE_PEP517=setuptools
PYPI_VERIFY_REPO=https://github.com/crossbario/txaio
PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{12..15} )
inherit distutils-r1 optfeature pypi

problem: Godot bundles ~20 third-party libraries; the scons builtin_* switches that select system copies are unreliable (some ignore whether the dep is used), risking silent vendoring; slotted mbedtls-3 is not found because its pkg-config file is mbedtls-3.pc not mbedtls.pc.
root_cause: Per-library 'builtin' toggles in upstream build systems are inconsistently honored, and Gentoo's slotted libraries can rename their .pc files, which upstream detect scripts hardcode.
fix_pattern: Physically rm -r the bundled thirdparty/<lib> directories for everything unbundled so any fallback fails loudly; sed the detect script to append the slot suffix to pkg-config names and to use $(tc-getPKG_CONFIG); symlink system headers (doctest) into thirdparty/ where the build insists on a local path.
rule: When unbundling with per-library builtin_* build switches, also delete the bundled source directories so a misfired switch causes a hard build error instead of silently using vendored code.
snippet:
local unbundle=( brotli freetype harfbuzz icu4c mbedtls pcre2 zlib zstd ... )
rm -r "${unbundle[@]/#/thirdparty/}" || die
# slotted mbedtls installs mbedtls-3.pc:
sed -E "/pkg-config/s/(mbedtls|mbedcrypto|mbedx509)/&-3/g" \
	-i platform/linuxbsd/detect.py || die

problem: Renaming the installed key file (openssl.org.asc -> openssl.asc) broke consumers whose VERIFY_SIG_OPENPGP_KEY_PATH still pointed at the old filename.
root_cause: Installed file paths under /usr/share/openpgp-keys are an interface consumed by other ebuilds; renaming without a compat path breaks them until every consumer is updated.
fix_pattern: Override src_install to call the eclass install then dosym the new file to the old filename, and revbump (-r1) so the fix propagates.
rule: When an installed file path that other packages reference changes, keep a compatibility symlink at the old path and revbump, instead of racing to update all consumers.

problem: Package byte-compilation failed because the elisp library now requires the app-emacs/compat library (and a newer minimum Emacs) which were not in the dependencies.
root_cause: Upstream started using compat-31 APIs; elisp dependencies are needed both at byte-compile time (BDEPEND) and at runtime (RDEPEND), and the minimum Emacs must be declared via NEED_EMACS before inheriting elisp.
fix_pattern: Set NEED_EMACS="28.1" before inherit elisp, put the elisp library dep in BDEPEND and fold it into RDEPEND via ${BDEPEND}; prune KEYWORDS to arches where the new dep is keyworded.
rule: For Emacs packages, declare elisp library deps in BDEPEND and reuse them in RDEPEND (byte-compile + runtime), set NEED_EMACS for the minimum version, and trim KEYWORDS to the dependency's keyword set.
snippet:
NEED_EMACS="28.1"
inherit elisp readme.gentoo-r1
BDEPEND="
	>=app-emacs/compat-31.0.0.1
"
RDEPEND="
	${BDEPEND}
	sci-visualization/gnuplot
"

problem: SRC_URI pointed at a gitlab.gentoo.org /-/archive/ on-the-fly generated tarball, which is not a stable distfile source (checksums/availability not guaranteed), breaking fetches/mirroring.
root_cause: Forge auto-generated archive endpoints regenerate tarballs on demand; compression or tar metadata can change, and the host is not a blessed mirror origin.
fix_pattern: Upload the exact tarball to the maintainer's dev.gentoo.org distfiles space and point SRC_URI at that stable URL (https://dev.gentoo.org/~dev/distfiles/mirrored/${P}.tar.bz2).
rule: Do not use forge auto-generated /-/archive/ URLs as SRC_URI for release tarballs; mirror the file at a stable location (dev space or release asset) so the checksum is permanent.

problem: Same fix as eselect-pwsh: replace gitlab.gentoo.org auto-generated archive SRC_URI with stable dev.gentoo.org mirrored distfile.
Removed a downstream sed hack that forced a useful soname after upstream fixed library versioning; reminder to annotate hacks with upstream issue links so they can be dropped when resolved.
Library consumers (neatvnc, wayvnc) did not rebuild automatically when aml's ABI changed across major versions, forcing consumers to hard-pin versions like =dev-libs/aml-0.3*.
root_cause: SLOT="0" with no subslot means := dependencies never trigger rebuilds on ABI breaks; the library bumps soname with its major version.
fix_pattern: Encode the ABI in a subslot derived from the version, SLOT="0/$(ver_cut 1)", in both release and live ebuilds, so consumers can use dev-libs/aml:= instead of version globs.
rule: When a shared library changes soname/ABI on major version bumps, set SLOT="0/$(ver_cut 1)" (or the soname) so :=-consumers get automatic rebuilds instead of hard version pins.

problem: Live (-9999) ebuild dependency bumped from =dev-libs/aml-0.3* to >=1.0.0 because upstream master moved on; routine live-ebuild dependency tracking.
Same live-ebuild aml dependency bump as neatvnc; routine tracking of upstream master requirements.
Routine metadata.xml maintainer takeover (replace <!-- maintainer-needed --> with maintainer block) plus ~ppc64 keyword add; no generalizable technique.
New version of a C library that uses BSD <sys/queue.h>, which glibc ships but musl does not, so the package fails to build on musl profiles.
root_cause: musl deliberately omits non-POSIX BSD headers like sys/queue.h; code including it compiles fine on glibc and breaks only on musl.
fix_pattern: Add a musl-conditional dependency on the standalone header package instead of patching the source: DEPEND="elibc_musl? ( sys-libs/queue-standalone )". Same idiom appears in lighttpd and many other tree packages. Also note the standard live/release split: [[ ${PV} == 9999 ]] guards git-r3 vs SRC_URI+KEYWORDS.
rule: When a package fails on musl due to missing <sys/queue.h> (or similar BSD headers), add elibc_musl? ( sys-libs/queue-standalone ) to DEPEND rather than patching the includes.

problem: qtwebengine fails to compile with gcc-16 on musl: PATH_MAX undeclared in chromium sandbox proc_util.cc and uint64_t undeclared in bundled GN.
root_cause: Newer libstdc++/gcc releases keep pruning transitive includes, and musl headers pull in less than glibc, so sources that never directly included <climits>/<cstdint> stop compiling only on the gcc+musl combination.
fix_pattern: Write a minimal patch that only adds the missing #include lines, name it descriptively (PN-VER-musl-missing-includes.patch), and consolidate multiple same-cause one-liner fixes into one patch file, replacing the earlier narrower patch in PATCHES for all affected versions including 9999 live ebuilds. Document in the patch header which toolchain combo needs it.
rule: When a package errors with 'PATH_MAX undeclared' or '(u)intNN_t was not declared' on new gcc or musl, add a tiny include-only patch (<climits> for PATH_MAX, <cstdint> for fixed-width ints) rather than anything invasive, and apply it across all live/release ebuilds sharing the source.

problem: Routine version bump: ebuild renamed 1.18.3 -> 1.18.4 with zero content changes, Manifest regenerated for the two (bin+oss) tarballs.
Bundled GN in qtwebengine fails to build with gcc-16+musl: missing <cstdint> in pool.h.
root_cause: GN upstream already fixed the missing include, but the fix was not yet in the bundled copy; gcc-16 no longer transitively provides cstdint via <string> on musl.
fix_pattern: Backport the exact upstream one-line fix as a FILESDIR patch and cite the upstream commit URL in the patch header so future bumps know when it can be dropped. Apply to the release ebuild and both 9999 branches in the same commit. Maintainer notes CI only tests clang+musl, so gcc+musl breakage surfaces only via user reports.
rule: When fixing bundled third-party code (GN, chromium, etc.), backport the upstream commit verbatim and record its URL in the patch header, so the patch is self-documenting and droppable at the next bump.
snippet:
Needed if using gcc-16 + musl which does not transitively include it.
https://gn.googlesource.com/gn/+/0fefaeb1b9f7...
+#include <cstdint>

problem: Live (9999) ebuild maintenance: bump pinned companion component WINE_MONO=11.1.0 -> 11.2.0; live ebuilds still need manual updates for pinned addon versions.
Same as wine-staging: WINE_MONO pin bumped in the 9999 ebuild; kept in sync across wine variants in back-to-back commits.

New upstream beta of a scientific package with a hand-written Makefile that hardcodes gcc/g++, ignores CFLAGS/CXXFLAGS/LDFLAGS, appends -g and -L/usr/local, uses GCC-only flags, relies on __int128 (unportable), has broken tests, and a large clang/libc++ compatibility patch too big for FILESDIR.
root_cause: Upstream build system predates distro conventions; __int128 is a GCC/clang extension not available everywhere; Gentoo policy forbids large patches in FILESDIR (git tree bloat).
fix_pattern: Layered approach: (1) Makefile patches making CC/CXX use ?= and routing $(CPPFLAGS) $(CXXFLAGS) $(LDFLAGS) into every compile/link rule; (2) reuse Debian's patches (int128 -> absl::int128 with dev-cpp/abseil-cpp:= dep) instead of rewriting; (3) host the oversized patch on dev.gentoo.org as an .xz distfile in SRC_URI and apply from ${WORKDIR}; (4) tc-export CC CXX in pkg_setup and append-cppflags -I"${EPREFIX}"/usr/include/cddlib for a nonstandard include dir; (5) filter-lto for an odr violation with a bug reference; (6) delete known-broken test dirs in src_prepare with die-guarded rm; (7) S=${WORKDIR}/gfan0.8beta for the nonstandard tarball layout.
rule: For packages with handwritten Makefiles, patch CC/CXX to ?= and inject CPPFLAGS/CXXFLAGS/LDFLAGS into the rules; ship patches larger than ~20KB as xz'd SRC_URI distfiles applied from ${WORKDIR}, and borrow Debian's portability patches before writing your own.

problem: ppsspp broken when built against ffmpeg-4 (bug 977474); dependency had no version bound, so portage happily built it against an incompatible ffmpeg.
root_cause: Unversioned media-video/ffmpeg:= dependency allowed older installed ffmpeg to satisfy the dep even though the package now requires the ffmpeg-5 API.
fix_pattern: Add a lower bound with slot operator (>=media-video/ffmpeg-5:=) and revbump by renaming the ebuild to -r1 so users on the old dep combination get a rebuild trigger. Single-line dep change, no other edits.
rule: When a bug reveals the package needs a minimum dependency version, add >=cat/pkg-VER:= and revbump (-rN) so already-installed users are re-emerged against the new constraint.

problem: Test invocation built emacs -l arguments via array substitution ${tests[@]/#/-l } with an embedded space, which only works unquoted via word splitting and breaks if any file path contains whitespace.
root_cause: Prefixing '-l ' (option + space) to each array element and expanding unquoted relies on shell word splitting to separate flag from value; quoting it would glue them into one word, so the code was forced to be unquoted and fragile.
fix_pattern: Switch to the --opt=value long-option form so each array element is exactly one argument and the expansion can be properly quoted: "${tests[@]/#/--load=}".
rule: When mass-prefixing an option onto a bash array of filenames, use the --option=value form inside a quoted "${arr[@]/#/--opt=}" expansion instead of an unquoted 'opt ' prefix that depends on word splitting.

problem: Package with a small C helper compiled it with the Makefile's hardcoded cc/gcc, ignoring the user's toolchain (breaks cross-compile, clang profiles; bug 977616).
root_cause: Upstream Makefile sets CC unconditionally; the ebuild's emake call did not override it.
fix_pattern: Pass the toolchain compiler explicitly as an emake argument: emake CC="$(tc-getCC)" (with toolchain-funcs inherited). Make-level command-line assignment beats the Makefile's own assignment, so no patch is needed.
rule: When a Makefile hardcodes its compiler, override it on the emake command line with CC="$(tc-getCC)" (and CXX="$(tc-getCXX)") instead of patching the Makefile.

problem: kernel-2 eclass version sanity check rejects the newly released kernel major version 7.1.0, blocking the ebuild.
root_cause: The kernel-2 eclass validates kernel versions against known ranges; a brand-new major series is not yet recognized.
fix_pattern: Set K_NO_VERSION_CHECK="True" in the ebuild before inheriting kernel-2 to bypass the check for the new series.
rule: When packaging a new kernel major version with the kernel-2 eclass fails its version check, set K_NO_VERSION_CHECK="True" as a temporary bypass.
snippet:
K_WANT_GENPATCHES="base extras"
K_GENPATCHES_VER="1"
K_NO_VERSION_CHECK="True"
inherit kernel-2

problem: Routine version add: 1.4.84 ebuild copied from previous version unchanged (upstream author submitted the bump); shows but does not introduce the verify-sig + elibc_musl queue-standalone idioms.
The vulkan USE flag produced a broken build/feature (bug 977492) and was not worth carrying.
root_cause: Upstream vulkan support was not in a shippable state; keeping a USE flag that yields a broken install harms users more than removing the option.
fix_pattern: Remove the flag from IUSE, delete its conditional deps, replace $(use_enable vulkan) with a hard --disable-vulkan in econf, and revbump to -r1 so users who had USE=vulkan get rebuilt without it. Old ebuild left in place, new -r1 added.
rule: When an optional feature is broken upstream, drop its USE flag and hard-disable the feature at configure time (with a revbump) instead of leaving a footgun flag.

problem: Build failure caused by upstream's own warning-hardening CMake toggle (FIX_WARNINGS) which injects warning/-Werror-style flags that break with newer compilers (bug 977487).
root_cause: Upstream enables aggressive warning options by default; new compiler releases add warnings, turning them into errors on user systems that upstream never tested.
fix_pattern: Turn the upstream toggle off in mycmakeargs with a bug-reference comment rather than patching sources: -DFIX_WARNINGS=OFF # bug #977487.
rule: When a build fails on warnings enabled by an upstream CMake option, disable that option in mycmakeargs (with a bug comment) instead of patching each warning.

problem: Package keyworded ~loong while its dependency kde-frameworks/kdnssd lost the loong keyword (re-keywording request timed out), leaving a broken dependency graph on that arch.
root_cause: Arch keywording must be consistent down the dependency tree; when an arch team cannot re-test a dep in time, everything depending on it must be dekeyworded too.
fix_pattern: Remove ~loong from KEYWORDS in every live version of the dependent package (both 26.04.1 stable-keyworded and 26.04.2 testing ebuilds), done as a sweep across all reverse dependencies in individual per-package commits.
rule: When a dependency loses an arch keyword, dekeyword all its reverse dependencies on that arch in all ebuild versions, or the tree fails dependency-resolution QA.

problem: Part of the ~loong dekeyword sweep after kde-frameworks/kdnssd re-keywording timeout; identical KEYWORDS-only change (see 62d6c6c6 for the pattern).
Part of the ~loong dekeyword sweep after kde-frameworks/kdnssd re-keywording timeout; identical KEYWORDS-only change.
Part of the ~loong dekeyword sweep after kde-frameworks/kdnssd re-keywording timeout; identical KEYWORDS-only change.
Part of the ~loong dekeyword sweep after kde-frameworks/kdnssd re-keywording timeout; identical KEYWORDS-only change.
Part of the ~loong dekeyword sweep after kde-frameworks/kdnssd re-keywording timeout; identical KEYWORDS-only change.
Part of the ~loong dekeyword sweep after kde-frameworks/kdnssd re-keywording timeout; identical KEYWORDS-only change.
Part of the ~loong dekeyword sweep after kde-frameworks/kdnssd re-keywording timeout; identical KEYWORDS-only change.
Part of the ~loong dekeyword sweep after kde-frameworks/kdnssd re-keywording timeout; identical KEYWORDS-only change.
Part of the ~loong dekeyword sweep after kde-frameworks/kdnssd re-keywording timeout; identical KEYWORDS-only change.

Package carried ~loong in KEYWORDS while its dependency kde-frameworks/kdnssd was not keyworded ~loong (arch-team re-keywording request timed out), leaving the deptree unsolvable on loong (pkgcheck DependencyMissingKeywords).
root_cause: A dependency lost its keyword (or never regained it after a bump/soname change) and the arch team did not act on the keywording bug within the timeout window; keeping the keyword on reverse dependencies would violate the invariant that every visible ebuild's deps are satisfiable on every keyworded arch.
fix_pattern: Mass-dekeyword the arch from ALL live versions of every reverse dependency, in one commit per package with the reason in the body ('kde-frameworks/kdnssd re-keywording timeout'). Note the edit is applied even inside stable-keyworded ebuilds, since ~arch entries there are independent testing keywords: KEYWORDS="amd64 arm64 ~loong ~riscv ~x86" -> KEYWORDS="amd64 arm64 ~riscv ~x86". Same batch touched 25 kde-apps games within minutes (scripted with ekeyword).
rule: When a dependency's keywording request times out or a dep drops an arch keyword, dekeyword that arch from every live version of all reverse dependencies rather than shipping ebuilds whose deps cannot be satisfied on that arch; overlay maintainers must likewise drop keywords their main-tree deps no longer carry.
snippet:
# scripted equivalent per affected ebuild:
ekeyword -loong ksirk-26.04.1.ebuild ksirk-26.04.2.ebuild
# result:
-KEYWORDS="amd64 arm64 ~loong ~riscv ~x86"
+KEYWORDS="amd64 arm64 ~riscv ~x86"
