QA warning during configure: '/bin/sh: line 2: -DHAVE_CONFIG_H: command not found' (bug 922298) — a flags string was being executed as a command.
root_cause: The autoconf configure script invokes $CPP, but the variable was unset in the build environment, so the shell line collapsed to just the flags and the first flag was executed as the command name.
fix_pattern: inherit toolchain-funcs and call tc-export CPP in (multilib_)src_configure so configure sees the cross/toolchain-correct preprocessor.
rule: When a configure log shows a bare compiler flag 'command not found', an empty toolchain variable ($CPP/$CC/$LD) is being invoked; inherit toolchain-funcs and tc-export the missing variable before econf.

problem: Prebuilt scanner driver had sloppy metadata: KEYWORDS lacked -*, depended on sys-apps/dbus and virtual/libudev it never links against, and shipped a GTK2 GUI binary that had earlier been 'fixed' by adding +x when it was deliberately not installed executable.
root_cause: Binary-only packages only work on the arch upstream ships, udev-rules-only consumers do not need the libudev linking virtual, and dbus was only used indirectly via avahi[dbus]; the non-executable binary was an implicit (undocumented) removal of a GTK2 dependency.
fix_pattern: Revbump: set KEYWORDS="-* ~amd64" for the prebuilt blob; replace virtual/libudev with virtual/udev when only udev rules are installed; drop the dbus dep and express it as avahi[dbus]; explicitly 'rm brscan_gnetconfig || die' with a comment instead of shipping a crippled file.
rule: For prebuilt packages, use KEYWORDS="-* <arches upstream ships>", depend only on what the binaries actually link or invoke (udev rules => virtual/udev, not virtual/libudev), and delete unwanted bundled binaries explicitly with a comment rather than leaving them non-executable.
snippet:
KEYWORDS="-* ~amd64"
RDEPEND="net-dns/avahi[dbus]
	virtual/udev"
src_install() {
	# Drop GTK2 GUI, in favour of cli brscan_cnetconfig
	rm brscan_gnetconfig || die
	...
}

problem: Portage QA warning that the installed binary does not record CFLAGS/uses unknown flags (bug 978974) on a pure-Rust package.
root_cause: Rust binaries are built by rustc with RUSTFLAGS, not CFLAGS, so Portage's CFLAGS-tracking QA check is a false positive for cargo packages.
fix_pattern: Set QA_FLAGS_IGNORED for the installed binary path.
rule: For cargo/Rust packages, silence the *FLAGS QA check on installed binaries with QA_FLAGS_IGNORED="usr/bin/${PN}" (list every installed ELF path).

problem: QA Notice: 'found go.mod file which specifies go 1.26.0' — ebuild's BDEPEND did not guarantee a new enough Go toolchain.
root_cause: Upstream raised the 'go' directive in go.mod on version bump, but the ebuild had no versioned dev-lang/go build dep, so builds on systems with older Go would fail.
fix_pattern: Add '>=dev-lang/go-<go.mod version>' to BDEPEND, exactly as the QA notice instructs.
rule: On every Go package bump, read the 'go X.Y.Z' directive in go.mod and mirror it as BDEPEND ">=dev-lang/go-X.Y.Z".

problem: Mixed Meson+cargo package emitted several QA warnings: missing RUST_MIN_VER, CFLAGS/prestripped QA on the Rust binary, a redundant src_unpack, and an obsolete gtk-doc-am build dep.
root_cause: Rust-in-GNOME packages need cargo-eclass metadata (RUST_MIN_VER before inherit), the Rust binary trips the CFLAGS and prestripped checks, src_unpack was pointlessly redefined when cargo is the only inherited eclass exporting that phase, and gtk-doc-am is autotools-only so it is wrong with Meson.
fix_pattern: Set RUST_MIN_VER="1.92" before 'inherit cargo'; set QA_FLAGS_IGNORED and (as stopgap) QA_PRESTRIPPED for the binary; delete the src_unpack wrapper that only called cargo_src_unpack; drop dev-build/gtk-doc-am from BDEPEND under Meson.
rule: For cargo-based packages: declare RUST_MIN_VER per the eclass QA warning, add QA_FLAGS_IGNORED for Rust ELFs, and do not redefine a phase just to call the single inherited eclass's implementation — eclass phase export already does that.
snippet:
RUST_MIN_VER="1.92"
inherit cargo gnome2 meson optfeature xdg

# Rust
QA_FLAGS_IGNORED="usr/bin/gnome-commander"
QA_PRESTRIPPED="usr/bin/gnome-commander"

problem: Removed a src_prepare that only called haskell-cabal_src_prepare + cabal-mksetup after haskell-cabal.eclass dropped the QA warning that had motivated it; housekeeping after eclass change, no new idiom.
Same cleanup as sop-core: dropped now-redundant cabal-mksetup call after the haskell-cabal.eclass QA warning was removed (bug 960337).
Newly added Python package (with ~170 vendored Rust crates) was reverted wholesale by the QA team.
root_cause: The package had multiple serious QA issues reported by both Portage and pkgcheck (bugs 977009, 977010), and neither author nor committer responded to the bugs for a week.
fix_pattern: QA lead reverted the original add commit entirely ('[QA] Revert "New package..."'), removing ebuild, Manifest and metadata.
rule: Before adding a new package (especially Python packages vendoring Rust crates), run pkgcheck scan and a full Portage build and fix every reported QA issue; in ::gentoo, unaddressed QA bugs plus maintainer silence lead to the package being reverted.

problem: Bump commits were reverted only because they depended on dev-python/zensical, which was itself reverted for QA issues; cascade revert restored docs.eclass (DOCS_BUILDER=mkdocs) usage. Process consequence, no new idiom.
Package gated man page generation behind IUSE="+man", which Gentoo QA policy PG0305 forbids.
root_cause: Policy: man pages must be built and installed unconditionally when the only cost is a trivial build-time dep (app-text/scdoc); a USE flag for them adds needless configuration surface.
fix_pattern: Drop 'man' from IUSE, make scdoc an unconditional BDEPEND, and force the build system option on: replace $(meson_feature man man-pages) with -Dman-pages=enabled (or unconditional 'docs' make targets).
rule: Never gate man pages behind a USE flag (QA PG0305): make the man page generator (scdoc etc.) an unconditional BDEPEND and hard-enable the corresponding build option.

problem: Same USE=man removal per QA PG0305 as gui-wm/sway 8525c426 (make-based variant: 'all docs' / 'install install_docs' made unconditional).
Same USE=man removal per QA PG0305 as gui-wm/sway 8525c426.
Same USE=man removal per QA PG0305 as gui-wm/sway 8525c426 (manual scdoc invocations made unconditional).
Same USE=man removal per QA PG0305 as gui-wm/sway 8525c426.
Same USE=man removal per QA PG0305 as gui-wm/sway 8525c426.
Same USE=man removal per QA PG0305 as gui-wm/sway 8525c426.
Same USE=man removal per QA PG0305 as gui-wm/sway 8525c426.
Same USE=man removal per QA PG0305 as gui-wm/sway 8525c426.
Same USE=man removal per QA PG0305 as gui-wm/sway 8525c426.
Same USE=man removal per QA PG0305 as gui-wm/sway 8525c426.
Portage QA warnings about 'implicit function declaration' during configure checks (bug 899928) for functions that legitimately do not exist on Linux.
root_cause: Configure probes for ioctlsocket (Windows-only) and sysctl (removed from Linux since kernel 5.5); with C23-strict compilers these probes trigger the implicit-function-declaration QA scanner, but the failures are intended negative results, not bugs.
fix_pattern: Declare the known-benign symbols in the QA_CONFIG_IMPL_DECL_SKIP array with comments explaining why each is expected to be missing.
rule: When the implicit-function-declaration QA notice fires on configure probes for symbols that genuinely cannot exist on the target (other-OS or removed-kernel APIs), whitelist those exact symbols via QA_CONFIG_IMPL_DECL_SKIP instead of patching configure.
snippet:
QA_CONFIG_IMPL_DECL_SKIP=(
	# only exists on Windows
	ioctlsocket
	# removed from Linux since 5.5
	sysctl
)

problem: CMake 4 QA warning: project declares cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.5), which CMake 4 no longer supports (bug 975078); also every l10n flag was optional though REQUIRED_USE demanded exactly one.
root_cause: CMake 4 removed compatibility with minimum-required versions < 3.10, so any upstream still declaring 3.5 breaks/warns; and '^^ ( l10n_* )' with no default flag forces every user to pick one manually.
fix_pattern: Revbump (-r1) carrying a FILESDIR patch that bumps cmake_minimum_required to 3.10, and default-enable English docs via bash substitution IUSE+=${LANG_USE/l10n_en/+l10n_en}.
rule: When a package warns/breaks under CMake 4, patch cmake_minimum_required up to >=3.10 and revbump; and when REQUIRED_USE demands exactly one flag from a set, ship a sensible default with +flag.

problem: Same cmake4 minimum-version patch + default +l10n_en fix as sibling commit e30ef77a, applied to the 9.0.9 branch.
pkgcheck ExcessiveLineLength warning caused by very long single-line CMake commands inside a src_prepare heredoc; fixed purely by wrapping the heredoc lines. Style-only, no behavior change.
Ebuild hygiene issues: test-only dependency (dev-cpp/catch) listed in RDEPEND, unsorted dependency blocks, phase functions defined out of execution order, and outdated :0= slot operators on openssl.
root_cause: test? ( ... ) in RDEPEND drags a build/test tool into every runtime install; unsorted deps and out-of-order phases hurt reviewability; dev-libs/openssl:0= is the legacy spelling of := for slot 0.
fix_pattern: Move 'test? ( dev-cpp/catch:= )' from RDEPEND into DEPEND, alphabetize each dependency block, reorder phase functions to match execution (src_configure, src_test, src_install, pkg_postinst), and use dev-libs/openssl:= instead of :0=.
rule: Test-only dependencies belong in DEPEND/BDEPEND under 'test? ( ... )', never in RDEPEND; keep dep blocks sorted and phase functions in execution order for reviewability.

problem: BDEPEND was seeded with "${RDEPEND}" pulling all runtime libs into build-host deps; deps/inherits unsorted; DESCRIPTION started with 'A ...'; libxcb dep used ':0=' instead of ':='.
root_cause: Copy-paste habit of BDEPEND="${RDEPEND} ..." is wrong: BDEPEND is for CBUILD-host tools only, and dragging runtime libs in breaks cross-compilation semantics and pointlessly widens the depgraph.
fix_pattern: Reduce BDEPEND to true host tools (virtual/pkgconfig, unpacker helpers); keep DEPEND="${RDEPEND}" for headers/libs; sort dep and inherit lists; strip 'A/An' article from DESCRIPTION; normalize slot operator to ':='.
rule: When writing BDEPEND, never include ${RDEPEND}; list only build-host tools there and let DEPEND=${RDEPEND} carry the libraries.

problem: Same hygiene pass as hxtools c2fd68a: trailing full stop in DESCRIPTION, RDEPEND included in BDEPEND, unsorted deps/inherits (duplicate of the hxtools lesson).
Two QA warnings after glibc-2.43: (1) 'QA Notice: implicit function declaration' for __assume in a configure check; (2) new const-qualification compiler warnings in pcre2grep.
root_cause: (1) __assume only exists in MS Visual C++, so the configure probe legitimately fails on Linux - the implicit-decl QA notice is a false positive. (2) glibc-2.43 implements C23 semantics where strchr/strstr/etc. return const-qualified pointers for const inputs, breaking code that assigned the result to non-const char*.
fix_pattern: Whitelist the platform-foreign symbol in QA_CONFIG_IMPL_DECL_SKIP with a comment citing the bug, and backport the upstream constness patch (change 'char *p = strchr(const_str, c)' to 'const char *p = ...').
rule: When the implicit-function-declaration QA notice fires on a symbol that only exists on another platform/compiler, add it to QA_CONFIG_IMPL_DECL_SKIP=( sym ) with an explanatory comment; when glibc-2.43/C23 const-return string functions break a build, patch the receiving variables to const.
snippet:
QA_CONFIG_IMPL_DECL_SKIP=(
	# Only exists in MS Visual C++ (bug #954363)
	__assume
)

problem: After a version bump, cargo.eclass QA flagged a mismatching Rust minimum version (RUST_MIN_VER=1.88.0 while upstream now requires 1.89.0).
root_cause: Upstream raised rust-version in Cargo.toml during the release; the ebuild variable was not synced during the bump.
fix_pattern: One-line change: set RUST_MIN_VER to the rust-version declared in the new release's Cargo.toml.
rule: On every cargo package bump, read rust-version from the new Cargo.toml and sync RUST_MIN_VER before committing.

problem: After bumping to 4.6.5 the build picked the wrong Lua implementation, a new unwanted GUI sidebar appeared, and a -Wunused-variable warning fired.
root_cause: Upstream 4.6.5 dropped the LUA_FIND_VERSIONS cmake variable and now auto-detects Lua from a hardcoded version search list, ignoring the lua-single eclass selection; the sidebar was a premature upstream feature with no preference toggle.
fix_pattern: sed the cmake module's version search list down to the eclass-selected implementation; carry small default-flipping patches for the unwanted feature plus a pkg_postinst einfo explaining the change and manual override; ship as -r1 revbump.
rule: When an upstream build system starts auto-detecting an interpreter version and ignores the knob you used to pass, patch/sed its search list to exactly the eclass-selected implementation (${ELUA}/${EPYTHON}).
snippet:
if use lua; then
	sed -i "s/set(LUA_VERSIONS5 5.5 5.4 5.3 5.2 5.1 5.0)/set(LUA_VERSIONS5 ${ELUA#lua})/g" \
		cmake/modules/FindLua.cmake || die
fi

problem: QA flagged mismatching RUST_MIN_VER after bump, and the tmpfiles.d entry created /var/log/vaultwarden.log while the service actually logs to /var/log/vaultwarden/vaultwarden.log.
root_cause: RUST_MIN_VER not synced with upstream's new toolchain requirement; tmpfiles config drifted from the service's real log path so the logfile was created at the wrong location with wrong ownership.
fix_pattern: Bump RUST_MIN_VER to match upstream; correct the 'f' line in the tmpfiles.d conf to the directory-nested path the daemon really writes.
rule: Installed tmpfiles.d/logrotate/service configs must be re-verified against the actual runtime paths on every bump, not just the ebuild itself; and keep RUST_MIN_VER synced with upstream Cargo.toml.

problem: The 9999 live ebuild still carried the QA_SONAME/QA_FLAGS_IGNORED suppressions and build-dir RPATH that had just been properly fixed in the release ebuild.
root_cause: Live and release ebuilds drift apart unless fixes are ported in the same commit series.
fix_pattern: Apply the identical fix (RUST_MIN_VER, patchelf --remove-rpath, newlib.so versioned install + symlinks, dev-util/patchelf BDEPEND) to quiche-9999.ebuild; only difference is the 9999 header include path (quiche/include).
rule: When you fix a versioned ebuild, port the same fix to the -9999 live ebuild in the same series so the next bump does not regress.

problem: Rust cdylib libquiche.so was installed unversioned with QA_SONAME/QA_FLAGS_IGNORED suppressions, carried an RPATH pointing into the bundled boringssl build directory, and bundled boringssl failed to configure with CMake 4 (cmake_minimum_required < 3.5).
root_cause: cargo does not set a SONAME or version cdylibs and bakes the build-tree RPATH of vendored C deps into the final .so; old vendored CMakeLists declare minimums CMake 4 rejects.
fix_pattern: Instead of suppressing QA: strip the bogus RPATH with patchelf, install the library under a real versioned name via newlib.so and create the .so.0/.so symlinks by hand, add dev-util/patchelf to BDEPEND, set RUST_MIN_VER, and patch every bundled cmake_minimum_required up to 3.10; revbump to -r1.
rule: For a Rust cdylib, do not paper over missing SONAME/bad RPATH with QA_* variables - patchelf --remove-rpath and install as libfoo.so.X.Y.Z with manual symlinks; and when a vendored dep says cmake_minimum_required(VERSION <3.5), patch it to >=3.10 for CMake 4.
snippet:
patchelf --remove-rpath "$(cargo_target_dir)"/libquiche.so || die
newlib.so "$(cargo_target_dir)"/libquiche.so libquiche.so.0.0.0 || die
ln -s libquiche.so.0.0.0 "${ED}"/usr/$(get_libdir)/libquiche.so.0 || die
ln -s libquiche.so.0 "${ED}"/usr/$(get_libdir)/libquiche.so || die

problem: Old (2010s-era) C++ package failed to build with modern toolchains: shell syntax error 'ax_cxx_fma =' in configure.ac, C++11 narrowing error in a double array initializer, and use of the 'register' keyword removed in C++17.
root_cause: Ancient codebase written before C++11/17 tightened rules; a stray space makes a shell variable assignment a command; int-typed expression narrowing into double aggregate; register is ill-formed since C++17.
fix_pattern: Three minimal one-hunk patches: remove the space in the configure.ac assignment, make the narrowing literal explicitly floating (4-mp::n_words -> 4.0-mp::n_words), delete the register keyword; each PATCHES entry annotated with its bug number.
rule: When an unmaintained C/C++ package breaks on a modern compiler, fix each diagnostic with a minimal targeted patch (drop register, fix narrowing, fix configure.ac shell syntax) instead of forcing an old -std= or appending -Wno flags.

problem: Cosmetic QA only: an over-long single inherit line was split into two 'inherit' lines across all ebuilds; no functional content.
Package sat on EAPI 7 with stale PYTHON_COMPAT and an obsolete xorg-server blocker, accumulating QA noise.
root_cause: Dormant leaf package never got a maintenance revbump, so eclass/EAPI/python-target drift piled up.
fix_pattern: Single -r9 revbump doing the whole refresh at once: EAPI=8, PYTHON_COMPAT extended to python3_14, dead blocker dropped, vala_setup called in src_prepare, python_optimize in src_install, .la files pruned.
rule: When touching a dormant package, do one combined maintenance revbump: raise EAPI, refresh PYTHON_COMPAT to current targets, and delete blockers/deps that no longer exist in the tree.

problem: QA Notice: 'Files built without respecting CFLAGS' for /usr/bin/wild, a cargo-built binary.
root_cause: cargo/rustc does not consume CFLAGS; C-flag markers are legitimately absent from pure-Rust binaries, so the QA check is a known false positive for Rust.
fix_pattern: Set QA_FLAGS_IGNORED for the installed binary path.
rule: For pure-Rust (or Go) binaries flagged with 'built without respecting CFLAGS/LDFLAGS', whitelist the exact installed path via QA_FLAGS_IGNORED rather than trying to inject CFLAGS.

problem: IUSE contained a 'man' USE flag gating man page generation/installation, which violates Gentoo QA policy.
root_cause: Man pages must always be installed; making them optional to save a few build-only deps is against policy since the cost is build-time only.
fix_pattern: Drop the flag: remove 'man' from IUSE, move the asciidoc/asciidoctor generators to unconditional BDEPEND (keeping the || ( ) alternatives), and keep only the has_version -b fallback logic in src_configure; revbump.
rule: Never gate man page (or basic doc) generation behind a USE flag - make the doc toolchain an unconditional BDEPEND and always install the man pages.

problem: pjproject bundled its own libyuv copy (QA notice / collision risk, bugs 955077+955129) and 2.15.x failed to build under C23 defaults.
root_cause: Upstream ships vendored libyuv used unless an external-yuv switch is given; C23 became the compiler default and old code broke (fixed by upstream 2.16).
fix_pattern: In the 2.16 ebuild: pass --with-external-yuv when USE=libyuv and add media-libs/libyuv:= (and libvpx:=) to RDEPEND so the system copy is linked; take the new upstream release for the C23 fix instead of patching.
rule: When upstream vendors a common library, look for its --with-external-*/--enable-system-* switch, wire it to a USE flag, and add the system lib with a := slot operator.
snippet:
# bug 955077 and bug 955129
use libyuv && myconf+=( --with-external-yuv )

problem: QA Notice: files built without respecting LDFLAGS for the sip/PyQt6 python extension.
root_cause: sip-build has no native handling of CFLAGS/CXXFLAGS/LDFLAGS from the environment, so user toolchain flags were silently dropped.
fix_pattern: Forward the flags into qmake variables on the sip-build command line via repeated --qmake-setting options; also bump PYTHON_COMPAT to python3_14 in the same revbump.
rule: For sip-build based Python bindings, pass toolchain flags explicitly as --qmake-setting 'QMAKE_CFLAGS/CXXFLAGS/LFLAGS += ...' because sip-build ignores the environment variables.

problem: cmake.eclass emitted CMake-compat QA warnings for bundled third-party sources that are not built as a cmake sub-project (false positive).
root_cause: cmake.eclass scans all CMakeLists.txt in the source tree for old cmake_minimum_required, including vendored code never entered by the actual build.
fix_pattern: Set CMAKE_QA_COMPAT_SKIP=1 before 'inherit cmake', with a comment citing the bug explaining the sources are bundled but unused by the cmake build.
rule: When cmake.eclass compat QA fires only on bundled sources that the build never configures, set CMAKE_QA_COMPAT_SKIP=1 above the inherit with an explanatory comment (contrast: if the code IS built, patch the minimums instead, as in quiche 13a7a9d).
snippet:
# Bundled sources without direct including as cmake sub-project, #957577
CMAKE_QA_COMPAT_SKIP=1

inherit cmake xdg

problem: Obsolete cmake/configure test files (dead TestBoostBug tree with ancient cmake_minimum_required) triggered the cmake compat QA warning.
root_cause: Upstream left behind files for a configure test that was removed long ago; QA scans still see their old CMakeLists.txt.
fix_pattern: Maintainer (who is also upstream) wrote a patch deleting the dead files, added it to PATCHES with an Upstream: PR link, fixing QA at the source instead of suppressing.
rule: When a QA warning comes from dead/unused files in the tarball, prefer a deletion patch submitted upstream over a QA-skip variable.

problem: QA warning 'IUSE=orc is missing while plugin supports it' is a false positive (only the a52dec plugin uses orc symbols); fix was purely an explanatory comment in the ebuild.
root_cause: Automated USE-flag detection saw orc symbols and assumed a missing flag; the maintainer determined it does not apply package-wide.
fix_pattern: Add a two-line comment in the ebuild quoting the exact QA message and why it is a false positive, so future maintainers do not re-investigate or 'fix' it.
rule: When you decide a QA warning is a false positive and there is no suppression mechanism, record the exact message and reasoning as a comment in the ebuild.
snippet:
# False positive as only a52dec uses symbols from orc.
# QA: IUSE=orc is missing while plugin supports it

problem: Trivial QA whitespace (double blank line) plus stale TODO comments in prebuilt Opera ebuilds; the useful content is the maintained CHROMIUM_VERSION pinning comment.
root_cause: Prebuilt Chromium-derivative browsers bundle a libffmpeg.so whose ABI corresponds to a specific Chromium major; the ebuild must pin the matching media-video/ffmpeg-chromium version.
fix_pattern: Keep a CHROMIUM_VERSION variable per ebuild; determine it with `strings libffmpeg.so | grep -F 'FFmpeg version'` on the bundled blob; only bump it when playback breaks, not on every browser release.
rule: For prebuilt Chromium-based browsers, discover the bundled ffmpeg's Chromium major via strings on libffmpeg.so and pin the system ffmpeg-chromium dependency to it; re-check only when it breaks.
snippet:
# Commit ref from `strings libffmpeg.so | grep -F "FFmpeg version"` matches this Chromium version
CHROMIUM_VERSION="142"

problem: Bundled self-contained remote-dev Xvfb component (plugins/remote-dev-server/selfcontained) shipped ELF binaries/libs failing soname/RPATH QA; ebuild also lacked support for the professional edition now shipped in the same tarball.
root_cause: Prebuilt vendor blobs carry private copies of Xvfb/xkbcomp and libs with no usable RPATH; their sonames cannot resolve against system libs.
fix_pattern: Gate the blob behind a USE flag: with USE=bundled-xvfb, patchelf --set-rpath '$ORIGIN/../lib' the binaries and '$ORIGIN' the libs so they resolve in-tree and add the real system deps (libpcre2, pam, audit) to RDEPEND; without it, delete the whole selfcontained dir and sed the launcher to export the env var that makes it use system libs (plus xorg-server[xvfb] dep). Combine with QA_PREBUILT="opt/${PN}/*" and RESTRICT="splitdebug".
rule: For an unresolved-soname/RPATH QA on a bundled component of a prebuilt app, either make its libs reachable via patchelf --set-rpath '$ORIGIN...' with matching RDEPEND, or remove the component and point the app at system equivalents - choose per USE flag when both are viable.
snippet:
if use bundled-xvfb; then
	patchelf --set-rpath '$ORIGIN/../lib' "${S}"/plugins/remote-dev-server/selfcontained/bin/{Xvfb,xkbcomp} || die
	patchelf --set-rpath '$ORIGIN' "${S}"/plugins/remote-dev-server/selfcontained/lib/lib*.so* || die
else
	rm -vr "${S}"/plugins/remote-dev-server/selfcontained || die
fi

problem: Implicit-function-declaration QA notice for GC_set_markers_count inside a configure check.
root_cause: The probed function exists only in unreleased dev-libs/boehm-gc (in non-threaded mode), so the configure check must fail today - expected behavior, not a bug.
fix_pattern: QA_CONFIG_IMPL_DECL_SKIP=( GC_set_markers_count ) with a comment explaining the dependency version situation and bug number; applied to both release and 9999 ebuilds.
rule: Configure probes for functions newer than any released version of the dependency are expected to fail - whitelist them in QA_CONFIG_IMPL_DECL_SKIP instead of patching the check out (same idiom as libpcre2 6ca648f).
snippet:
# Configure check, only recent (not yet released) dev-libs/boehm-gc carry this
# function in non-threaded mode, see #968001.
QA_CONFIG_IMPL_DECL_SKIP=( GC_set_markers_count )

problem: Two QA issues: SRC_URI was assigned before DESCRIPTION (variable-order QA), and REQUIRED_USE="|| ( X wayland )" was unsatisfiable with default USE because neither flag was enabled by default.
root_cause: A || REQUIRED_USE group needs at least one member enabled in the default configuration or every default build fails; metadata variable ordering is checked by pkgcheck.
fix_pattern: Move SRC_URI below DESCRIPTION and change IUSE="nls X wayland" to IUSE="nls +X wayland" so the || group is satisfied by default.
rule: Whenever you add REQUIRED_USE="|| ( a b )", enable one of the options by default in IUSE (+a).

problem: Upstream silently replaced an already-released tarball (same filename turbovnc-3.2.91.tar.gz, new content fixing a bundled zlib-ng cmake QA notice), which would cause Manifest checksum conflicts for users who fetched the old file.
root_cause: Re-rolled release artifacts under an unchanged filename collide with the existing DISTDIR/Manifest entry; Gentoo distfiles must be immutable per name.
fix_pattern: Bump the ebuild to ${PV}_p1 and rename the distfile with the SRC_URI arrow operator so the new content gets a unique local name, keeping the upstream URL pointing at the original filename; override S back to the un-suffixed directory.
rule: When upstream replaces a tarball in place, create a _pN revbump whose SRC_URI uses '-> ${P}.tar.gz' to force a distinctly named fresh download, and reset S with ${PV%_p*}.

problem: Three nginx CVEs needed fixing on the stable branch (1.30.x) as well as mainline; the bump must not re-hand-write hundreds of lines of module logic.
root_cause: nginx ships parallel stable/mainline release branches; Gentoo keeps both, so a CVE fix means adding a new version on each branch. The package's complexity lives in a dedicated nginx eclass, so a version add is only declarative arrays.
fix_pattern: Add nginx-1.30.4.ebuild (34 lines): declare NGINX_SUBSYSTEMS/NGINX_MODULES arrays, pin the external test suite with NGINX_TESTS_COMMIT=<sha>, list NGINX_MISC_FILES, inherit nginx, set ~arch KEYWORDS, keep only the local PATCHES. All build logic stays in the eclass.
rule: When a package tracks two upstream release branches, fix a CVE by adding the fixed version on BOTH branches in the same series of commits, and push repeated ebuild logic into an eclass so each security bump is a tiny declarative file.

problem: A test in the pinned nginx test suite fails under portage's network-sandbox, which would make every FEATURES=test build of the security bump fail.
root_cause: tunnel_next_upstream.t needs real network access, which the portage sandbox denies; upstream tests are not sandbox-aware.
fix_pattern: Delete the offending test file in src_prepare, guarded by USE=test, with a bug-reference comment and `|| die` so silent failure is impossible.
rule: When a specific upstream test needs network access, rm that test file in src_prepare under `use test` with a comment citing the bug, instead of restricting the whole test suite.
snippet:
src_prepare() {
	nginx_src_prepare
	if use test ; then
		# Fails with network-sandbox (bug #976129)
		rm "${NGX_TESTS_S}"/tunnel_next_upstream.t || die
	fi
}

problem: Newly added modsecurity-crs-3.3.10 landed with stable KEYWORDS="amd64 x86" and had to be de-stabilized back to ~arch two days later by a second developer.
root_cause: The 3.3.10 ebuild was copied from the stable 3.3.9 ebuild and the stable keywords were carried over instead of being reset to testing; new revisions/versions must always re-enter the tree as ~arch and go through normal stabilization.
fix_pattern: Follow-up commit changed KEYWORDS="amd64 x86" to KEYWORDS="~amd64 ~x86". The correct original workflow is `ekeyword ~all newpkg.ebuild` right after copying the old ebuild.
rule: When creating a new version by copying an existing stable ebuild, always downgrade KEYWORDS to ~arch (ekeyword ~all) before committing; stable keywords may only appear via explicit stabilization commits.

problem: Routine drop of superseded ~arch version 4.26.0 once 4.28.0 was in the tree; no generalizable content beyond normal old-version cleanup.
New CRS rule-set versions were added while the companion engine (www-apache/mod_security) got a security fix; old CRS ebuilds allowed engine versions that are now known-vulnerable.
root_cause: A data/rules package is only as safe as the engine it drives; the RDEPEND lower bound documented an obsolete minimum (>=mod_security-2.9.6).
fix_pattern: When adding the new CRS versions in the same PR that adds mod_security-2.9.14, raise the conditional runtime dependency floor to the security-fixed engine: RDEPEND="apache2? ( >=www-apache/mod_security-2.9.14 )". Also note MY_PV=${PV/_/-} to map Gentoo _rc style versions to upstream v4.x-rc tags.
rule: When a security fix lands in a package's companion/engine dependency, bump the version floor of that dependency in the dependent package's new ebuilds so users cannot mix fixed rules with a vulnerable engine.

problem: Upstream's autoconf lua detection (find_lua.m4) probes a long list of versioned pkg-config names (lua5.4, lua5.3, ...) and would link against whichever lua happens to be installed, ignoring the lua implementation selected by the lua-single eclass.
root_cause: Autotools-based upstreams hardcode versioned lua pkg-config name lists; Gentoo's lua-single/lua-utils eclasses instead expose the user-selected implementation as plain `lua` via a PKG_CONFIG_PATH wrapper, so any versioned probe bypasses the eclass choice.
fix_pattern: Ship a FILESDIR patch that replaces the LUA_PKGNAMES probe list with just "lua", then eautoreconf. Combined with LUA_COMPAT=( lua5-{1..3} ), inherit lua-single, REQUIRED_USE="lua? ( ${LUA_REQUIRED_USE} )" and `use lua && lua-single_pkg_setup`, the build now always uses the eclass-selected lua. Note also the pkg_setup pattern when mixing apache-module with lua-single: call _init_apache2 and _init_apache2_late manually because pkg_setup is overridden.
rule: When an autotools package autodetects a versioned lua (or similar slotted library) via a pkg-config name list, patch the probe to query only the unversioned name and let the lua-single eclass wrapper pick the implementation.
snippet:
-LUA_PKGNAMES="lua5.5 lua-5.5 ... lua5.1 lua-51 lua51 lua5 lua"
+LUA_PKGNAMES="lua "
# ebuild side:
LUA_COMPAT=( lua5-{1..3} )
inherit autotools apache-module lua-single
src_prepare() { default; eautoreconf; }

problem: Library security bump whose test suite downloads remote rule content, and whose configure script misbehaves under non-bash CONFIG_SHELL.
root_cause: Upstream regression tests config-secremoterules.json and operator-ipMatchFromFile.json fetch remote URLs (fails under network-sandbox); configure needs bash specifically.
fix_pattern: In src_prepare, rm the two network-dependent test-case files with `|| die` and a reason comment before `default`. Configure with CONFIG_SHELL="${BROOT}/bin/bash" econf. Keep SLOT="0/3" subslot so := consumers rebuild on ABI change, and delete libtool archives with find "${ED}" -name '*.la' -delete in src_install.
rule: When individual test fixtures need network access, delete exactly those fixture files in src_prepare with a comment; when configure is bash-only, prefix econf with CONFIG_SHELL="${BROOT}/bin/bash" instead of patching the script.
snippet:
src_prepare() {
	# fails tests, loads remote content
	rm test/test-cases/regression/config-secremoterules.json \
		test/test-cases/regression/operator-ipMatchFromFile.json || die
	default
}
CONFIG_SHELL="${BROOT}/bin/bash" econf "${myconf[@]}"

problem: Routine removal of the vulnerable 3.0.14-r2 after 3.0.15 was stabilized on all its arches (security bug 975681 cleanup); no new idiom.
The old stable branch 1.16.3 needed the same three security fixes already backported to 1.18.4, without duplicating 350+ lines of patch files.
root_cause: Multiple maintained release branches share the same vulnerable code, and the 1.18.4-named backport patches apply cleanly to 1.16.3 as well.
fix_pattern: Rename elixir-1.16.3.ebuild to elixir-1.16.3-r1.ebuild (revbump) and append the existing 1.18.4-named FILESDIR patches to PATCHES, reusing the same patch files across branches instead of copying them per version.
rule: When a FILESDIR security patch applies unchanged to several version branches, reference the one patch file (named after the version it was made for) from every branch's ebuild rather than duplicating it; only make a per-version copy when context differs.

problem: Same backport pattern as 46052a5 applied to the 1.17.3 branch (rename to -r1, add three shared 1.18.4-named patches).
Security fixes shipped only in elixir 1.20.1, but users on stable 1.18.4 (and older branches) would be forced into a major version jump to get them.
root_cause: Upstream fixes CVEs only in the newest release; Gentoo keeps several elixir branches keyworded, so each maintained branch stays vulnerable unless the fixes are backported in the ebuild tree.
fix_pattern: Extract the three upstream security commits as FILESDIR patches named after the branch (elixir-1.18.4-*.patch: cap strftime width, limit version-number length, validate archive extraction paths), add them to PATCHES, and revbump the ebuild to -r1 so installed systems actually rebuild. Repeat per maintained branch (1.16.3, 1.17.3, 1.19.5), reusing patch files where they apply.
rule: When upstream fixes a CVE only in its newest release, backport the specific fix commits as FILESDIR patches to every still-keyworded branch and revbump each ebuild, instead of dropping old branches or forcing a version jump.

problem: Same branch-backport pattern for 1.19.5; patches re-spun with 1.19.5 naming because context differs from the 1.18.4 copies.
Security bump 1.20.0->1.20.1 done as a pure git rename (100% similarity) plus Manifest update, dropping the vulnerable ~arch 1.20.0 in the same commit; routine.

Routine security bump to 3.11.5 by copying the previous ebuild unchanged; the interesting idioms in it (verify-sig with pinned OpenPGP key path, eapi9-ver ver_replacing upgrade warning) predate this commit.

CVE fix required packaging a new log4j-core, a Maven multi-module project, with java-pkg-simple which runs no Maven plugins - so annotation-processor output, META-INF/services files, and the multi-release jar layout are all missing by default.
root_cause: java-pkg-simple only javac's sources into one jar; Maven normally runs the PluginProcessor annotation processor, copies service-provider descriptors, and assembles a Multi-Release jar from log4j-core-java9.
fix_pattern: Three idioms: (1) hand-write the META-INF/services provider files in src_prepare (contents derived from `grep -nr '@ServiceProvider'`); (2) compile twice - first plain java-pkg-simple_src_compile, then again with JAVAC_ARGS pointing -processorpath/-processor at the just-built PluginProcessor; (3) rebuild the jar by pruning target/classes/META-INF/versions/9 to match upstream's java9.xml assembly, moving module-info.class, appending 'Multi-Release: true' to the manifest and re-running jar cfm. Also pin the sibling module with ~dev-java/log4j-api-${PV} in CP_DEPEND and use verify-sig against sec-keys/openpgp-keys-apache-logging.
rule: When packaging a Maven project with java-pkg-simple, reproduce Maven's side effects manually: generate service descriptors, run annotation processors via a second compile pass with JAVAC_ARGS, and pin same-project sibling jars with a ~ dependency on ${PV}.
snippet:
CP_DEPEND="... ~dev-java/log4j-api-${PV}:0 ..."
src_compile() {
	java-pkg-simple_src_compile
	JAVAC_ARGS=" -processorpath target/classes:$(java-pkg_getjars log4j-api) \
		-processor org.apache.logging.log4j.core.config.plugins.processor.PluginProcessor"
	java-pkg-simple_src_compile
}

problem: Routine drop of two old vulnerable/broken versions closing long-open bugs, plus a cosmetic move of RESTRICT below KEYWORDS for metadata-ordering style; no new idiom.
New hackage-security release has stale upper bounds in its .cabal file and a Cabal-syntax dependency that conflicts with modern GHC/Cabal, blocking the bump.
root_cause: Hackage packages carry tight version bounds that Hackage 'revisions' later relax; the tarball's original .cabal is outdated, and the Cabal-syntax flag logic adds a conflicting build-depends even when the flag is off.
fix_pattern: Use the haskell-cabal eclass knobs instead of sed hacks: CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION=3 fetches the revised .cabal (extra -revN.cabal DIST entry); CABAL_CHDEPS pairs rewrite remaining over-tight bounds in place ('old string' 'new string'); a small FILESDIR patch drops the conflicting Cabal-syntax build-depends; disable the flag explicitly in src_configure with --flag=-Cabal-syntax; RESTRICT="test" carries a reason comment; keep hackport's generator header comment for reproducibility.
rule: When a Haskell package fails only on .cabal version bounds, prefer CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION to pick up upstream's revised metadata and CABAL_CHDEPS to relax the rest, patching the .cabal only for structural dependency changes.
snippet:
CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION=3
CABAL_CHDEPS=(
	'base              >= 4.11     && < 4.21' 'base              >= 4.11'
	'template-haskell  >= 2.13     && < 2.23' 'template-haskell  >= 2.13'
)
RESTRICT="test" # Requires Cabal-3.8 or newer

problem: Security bump crossing a minor branch (4.98.2-r2 -> 4.99.4 for a CVE) initially looks like a rename, but the old patch stack and USE flags no longer match the new branch.
root_cause: Between 4.98 and 4.99 upstream merged one of Gentoo's patches (tidydb-crash), changed context under another (as-needed-ldflags needed a respin), and removed features entirely (radius auth, convert4r3/convert4r4 tools).
fix_pattern: In the rename commit: drop the upstreamed patch from src_prepare; replace exim-4.97-as-needed-ldflags.patch with a freshly respun exim-4.99.3-as-needed-ldflags.patch (new file named for the version it was rebased on); delete the radius USE flag, its freeradius-client dependency, its src_prepare sed and its src_configure Makefile block; trim removed tools from the dosbin loop.
rule: When a security bump crosses an upstream minor branch, audit the whole ebuild against upstream changes: drop upstreamed patches, respin-and-rename version-specific patches, and remove USE flags/installed tools for features upstream deleted.

problem: Routine drop of obsolete versions 3.3.7 and 4.20.0 after replacements stabilized; no generalizable content.
Routine ~arch version add of 4.26.0 (copy of 4.25.0 with MY_PV=${PV/_/-} tag mapping); no new idiom.

Upstream shipped an out-of-band security build named 'grafana_12.4.3+security-02_25720634919' - a version string containing '+' and a CI build id, which is not a valid Gentoo PV, so the tarball cannot be fetched from a PV-derived URL.
root_cause: Vendors of prebuilt binaries publish hotfix builds with non-PMS version syntax (semver build metadata, pipeline ids); Gentoo PV allows only [0-9.]+ with defined suffixes.
fix_pattern: Encode the hotfix as a legal PV (_p2 for 'security-02'), then reconstruct upstream's literal strings in REAL_PV/REAL_PVR variables used for SRC_URI, the per-arch tarball names, and S. Keep KEYWORDS="-* ~amd64 ~arm64" (only arches with binaries), RESTRICT="mirror", and QA_PREBUILT/QA_PRESTRIPPED for the blobs.
rule: When an upstream binary release version contains characters invalid in PV (+, build ids), map it to a legal PV suffix (_pN) and carry the exact upstream string in REAL_PV-style variables for SRC_URI and S, adding a comment explaining why plain PV cannot be used.

problem: Routine minor security bump to radvd 2.21 by copying the previous ebuild; existing idioms (export LEX=flex for reentrant lexer, readme.gentoo-r1) carried over unchanged.
Per-arch stabilization commit (x86) in the 3.0.15 security stabilization series; one-line KEYWORDS edit, same pattern as the arm64 commit recorded separately.
Per-arch stabilization commit (amd64) in the 3.0.15 security stabilization series; one-line KEYWORDS edit.
The security-fixed 3.0.15 had to replace vulnerable stable 3.0.14-r2, but stable keywords cannot be flipped wholesale without per-arch testing.
root_cause: Gentoo stabilization is per-architecture: each arch flips its own keyword after arch testing, tracked by a stabilization bug; only after all arches are done can the vulnerable version be dropped.
fix_pattern: One commit per arch, each a single KEYWORDS edit (~arm64 -> arm64, ~amd64 -> amd64, ~x86 -> x86) generated with ekeyword, subject formatted 'Stabilize 3.0.15 <arch>, #<bug>'. The vulnerable 3.0.14-r2 is dropped in a separate later commit referencing the same bug.
rule: For a security stabilization, land one keyword-flip commit per architecture referencing the stabilization bug in the subject, and drop the vulnerable stable version only after all arches have flipped.

problem: Routine arch-team stabilization: single KEYWORDS flip ~arm -> arm, no generalizable content beyond standard keywording workflow.
Stale metadata.xml USE flag description left behind after the last ebuild using the flag is gone.
root_cause: Old versions 3.0.13/3.0.14 had IUSE=pcre2; the new 3.0.15 made pcre2 unconditional, so once the old ebuilds are dropped the <flag name="pcre2"> entry in metadata.xml describes a flag no version declares.
fix_pattern: In the same commit that removes the last ebuilds carrying a USE flag, delete that flag's <flag> element from metadata.xml (and remove the DIST line from Manifest for dropped tarballs).
rule: When dropping old ebuild versions, prune metadata.xml <flag> entries (and Manifest DIST lines) that no remaining version references.

problem: Routine security version bump; new ebuild is a copy of the previous revision (pcre2 now unconditional via --with-pcre2, keywords reset to ~arch).
Routine security bump: 1.25.1 ebuild is byte-identical to 1.25.0, added at ~arch and stabilized separately via the security bug.
Security release needed to reach stable users quickly; a normal add-at-~arch plus 30-day stabilization cycle would leave stable users exposed.
root_cause: Upstream 2.6.20 is a patch-level security release; the ebuild is byte-identical to the already-stable 2.6.17-r2, so there is no packaging risk justifying a testing period.
fix_pattern: The new version was committed with the stable KEYWORDS copied unchanged from the previous stable ebuild (amd64 arm arm64 ppc ppc64 x86 stable, rest ~arch), i.e. straight-to-stable under the security-stabilization exception, with 'Bug:' referencing the security bug.
rule: When a security release changes nothing in the ebuild relative to the current stable version, it can inherit the stable keywords directly instead of resetting to ~arch; otherwise reset to ~arch and stabilize via a bug.
snippet: KEYWORDS="~alpha amd64 arm arm64 ~hppa ~loong ~mips ppc ppc64 ~riscv ~sparc x86"

problem: CVE-2026-46529 (argument injection via unquoted strings passed to ev_spawn) fixed upstream in git, but no upstream release tarball containing the fix exists yet.
root_cause: Upstream fixed the vulnerability in a single commit on their GitLab; waiting for a release would leave users exposed, so the fix must be carried downstream.
fix_pattern: Copy the ebuild to a new revision (evince-48.1-r1.ebuild), download the upstream fix commit as files/${PN}-${PV}-CVE-XXXX-NNNNN.patch, prepend provenance lines (Gentoo bug URL + upstream commit URL) to the patch header, and add it to PATCHES. The revbump forces a rebuild for everyone who already has 48.1 installed.
rule: When upstream fixes a CVE in git but has not released, revbump the ebuild and ship the upstream commit as a FILESDIR patch named ${PN}-${PV}-CVE-<id>.patch whose header carries the bug and upstream-commit URLs.

problem: Routine security version bump; new ebuild copied from previous version at ~arch, no packaging change.
Routine arch-team stabilization keyword flip (4.25.0 x86).
Routine arch-team stabilization keyword flip (3.3.9 x86); note both the legacy 3.3.x and current 4.x branches are stabilized in parallel for the same security bug.
Routine arch-team stabilization keyword flip (4.25.0 amd64).
Routine arch-team stabilization keyword flip (3.3.9 amd64).

Version bump broke a long-carried Gentoo patch: exim's hand-written Makefile places $(LFLAGS)/$(LDFLAGS) after the library list, so with -Wl,--as-needed libraries get discarded and linking fails; the old patch no longer applied to 4.99.3's Makefile-Base.
root_cause: Custom (non-autotools) build systems often put user LDFLAGS at the end of the link command; --as-needed only keeps libraries that are referenced by objects seen *before* them, so flags/libs ordering matters and every upstream Makefile refactor breaks the downstream respin.
fix_pattern: Re-spun the patch for the new version as files/exim-4.99.3-as-needed-ldflags.patch (versioned filename, Gentoo bug URLs kept in the header): move $(LDFLAGS) before the object files and demote the legacy $(LFLAGS) to the end, for every binary target, not just the main one. musl support kept via 'use elibc_musl && sed -removal of -lnsl + append-cflags -DNO_EXECINFO'. Commit message and elog document upstream feature removals (radius, eximon).
rule: When a custom Makefile fails to link under --as-needed, patch each link rule so user LDFLAGS precede the objects and all -l libraries come after the objects; re-spin and re-version the patch on every bump it no longer applies to.

problem: Four GStreamer security advisories (heap overflows/OOB in qtdemux) affected 1.26.11, which was in-tree but not yet stabilized.
root_cause: Upstream advisories (GStreamer SA-2026-00xx) were fixed in upstream merge requests but no fixed release existed; the tree version needed the fixes before its pending stabilization.
fix_pattern: Added one patch file per upstream advisory named ${P}-GStreamer-SA-YYYY-NNNN.patch, each headed by the Gentoo bug URL, the advisory URL and the upstream MR URL; added a PATCHES array (with '# bug #974286' comment) to the existing 1.26.11 ebuild WITHOUT a revbump, because 1.26.11 was still ~arch and was stabilized immediately afterwards with the patches included (bug 977162).
rule: Name security backport patches after the upstream advisory ID and head them with bug+advisory+MR URLs; a revbump can be skipped only when the patched version has never been stabilized and stabilization of the patched state follows immediately, otherwise revbump.

problem: Same advisory-backport pattern as gst-plugins-good in the same PR: per-advisory patch files added to the not-yet-stable 1.26.11 ebuild without revbump.
root_cause: Multiple GStreamer SAs spanned several split gst packages; each package gets its own commit with its own 'Bug:' trailers (two bugs here) inside one shared pull request.
fix_pattern: One commit per package even when fixing the same advisory set across split packages; multiple 'Bug:' trailers in the commit message when the patches cover several tracker bugs.
rule: When one upstream advisory set touches several split packages, commit per-package with per-package Bug: trailers so each package's changelog and bug references stay accurate.

problem: Third instance of the same GStreamer advisory-backport pattern (SA-named patches, provenance headers, patched in place pre-stabilization).
CVE-2026-6210 (bad-cast heap overflow in SVG marker/mask resolution) affected both in-tree versions, one of them stable.
root_cause: The same upstream codereview fix applies cleanly to both 6.10.3 and 6.11.0, so duplicating the patch per version would be waste; both versions are installed on user systems and need a rebuild.
fix_pattern: One patch file named after the oldest version it applies to (qtsvg-6.10.3-CVE-2026-6210.patch, header = Gentoo bug + upstream codereview URL) is shared by both ebuilds; each affected ebuild is git-renamed to -r1 (revbump) with the same PATCHES entry added. Upstream's regression test hunk is kept in the patch.
rule: When one CVE fix applies to several in-tree versions, ship a single FILESDIR patch named after the lowest applicable version and revbump every affected ebuild to reference it.

problem: Seven upstream security/correctness fixes (DNS OOB read, qsort UB and heap corruption, regex BRE backref, iconv gb18030, strptime tzname) needed backporting to musl 1.2.6, too many/too large to drop into FILESDIR.
root_cause: Gentoo policy discourages large or numerous patches in FILESDIR (mirrored git repo bloat); multi-commit backports need a distributable patchset artifact.
fix_pattern: Rolled the cherry-picked upstream commits into musl-1.2.6-patches.tar.xz hosted on distfiles.gentoo.org/pub/proj/musl/, added it to SRC_URI, pointed PATCHES at the unpacked directory (PATCHES entries may be directories, applied in sorted order), and revbumped to -r1. Commit message embeds the 'git shortlog' of the backported commits for provenance. verify-sig deliberately verifies only the upstream release tarball, not the Gentoo-generated extras.
rule: For multi-commit security backports, ship a patchset tarball via SRC_URI and reference the extracted directory in PATCHES instead of committing many files to FILESDIR; record the backported commit list in the commit message.

problem: CVE-2026-6245 (OOB read in PAM passkey responder) required a downstream patch; the stable-branch fix consists of two upstream commits (fix + follow-up simplification).
root_cause: Upstream fixed the CVE in two consecutive cherry-picked commits on the 2.9 branch; applying only the first would leave dead code and diverge from upstream's branch state.
fix_pattern: Concatenated both upstream format-patch commits into a single FILESDIR patch file (PATCH 1/2 + 2/2 with full upstream metadata and '(cherry picked from commit ...)' lines preserved), revbumped to -r1, and bundled an unrelated already-upstreamed dependency removal into the same revbump to avoid a second rebuild.
rule: Keep multi-commit upstream fixes together in one patch file with their format-patch metadata intact, and fold pending low-risk cleanups into a revbump that is already required for a security fix.

problem: Routine drop of superseded stable version 4.16.0 after 4.20.0 stabilized; Manifest DIST line removed together with the ebuild.
Routine bump on the legacy 3.3.x branch maintained in parallel with 4.x (same SLOT); ebuild adjusted for branch differences (no plugins dir, KNOWN_BUGS filename).
Routine bump 4.20.0 -> 4.25.0; only changes vs previous ebuild are copyright year and KEYWORDS reset from stable to ~arch pending stabilization.
New upstream branch bump (2.2.14-r1 -> 2.3.2) in which upstream removed classic PGP/S-MIME backends; the ebuild was moved by git rename, risking stale stable keywords and stale USE flags.
root_cause: Renaming a stable ebuild to a new version silently carries its stable KEYWORDS and IUSE forward; upstream dropped the pgp-classic/smime-classic backends so every reference to those flags had to go.
fix_pattern: In one commit: reset KEYWORDS to all-~arch on the renamed ebuild; remove the dropped flags from IUSE, REQUIRED_USE, RDEPEND, configure args (hardcode --disable-pgp --disable-smime) and metadata.xml; bump the per-PVR Gentoo patchset tarball (PATCHSET="gentoo-${PVR}/${PATCHREV}" hosted on dev space); and migrate the hardcoded bugs.gentoo.org text to the branding eclass variable BRANDING_OS_BUG_REPORT_URL.
rule: When bumping by renaming an existing ebuild, always reset KEYWORDS to ~arch and sweep IUSE/REQUIRED_USE/DEPEND/metadata.xml for USE flags the new upstream version no longer supports.
snippet:
-inherit autotools
+inherit autotools branding
-KEYWORDS="~alpha amd64 arm ... x86 ..."
+KEYWORDS="~alpha ~amd64 ~arm ... ~x86 ..."
-		$(use_enable pgp-classic pgp)
+		--disable-pgp

problem: Security fix (reject .install files escaping via '..' or absolute paths) applied as revbump + FILESDIR patch, but the patch file carries no provenance header and no versioned/CVE name.
root_cause: Minimal patch workflow: files/fix-dotinstall-escape.patch has no Gentoo bug URL, no upstream commit reference and no ${PN}/version prefix, making future audits and bumps harder (contrast with the evince/qtsvg/gst practice in the same period).
fix_pattern: Mechanics are still correct: git-rename ebuild to -r1 and add PATCHES=( "${FILESDIR}"/fix-dotinstall-escape.patch ); the missing provenance header is the anti-pattern to avoid.
rule: Even for small security patches, name the patch with package/version/CVE context and put the bug and upstream-commit URLs at the top of the file; a bare descriptive filename with no header is an audit dead end.
snippet: PATCHES=( "${FILESDIR}"/fix-dotinstall-escape.patch )  # works, but header lacks bug/upstream URLs

problem: Routine slotted toolchain bump (SLOT=PV) for cargo CVEs; commit message documents the CVE IDs with links and the upstream release blog, ebuild copied with dated rust-src SRC_URI path updated.

Security bump of dev-lang/rust that also had to fix bundled-library and dependency-declaration QA issues: cargo bundled its own sqlite, curl carried an unneeded := slot operator, and a prestripped rust-objcopy triggered QA warnings.
root_cause: Rust/cargo vendored *-sys crates (libsqlite3-sys) build bundled C libraries unless told to use pkg-config; slot operators on deps that are not ABI-sensitive cause pointless rebuilds (flagged by iwdevtools qa-vdb); upstream ships pre-stripped helper binaries.
fix_pattern: Unbundle via the -sys crate's env switch: export LIBSQLITE3_SYS_USE_PKG_CONFIG=1 (same family as RUSTONIG_SYSTEM_LIBONIG, LIBSSH2_SYS_USE_PKG_CONFIG) and add dev-db/sqlite:3 to DEPEND; change net-misc/curl:=[http2,ssl] to net-misc/curl[http2,ssl] per iwdevtools warning; append the new binary to QA_PRESTRIPPED; keep patches in proj/rust-patches.git tarball, never FILESDIR; migrate makeopts_jobs to get_makeopts_jobs.
rule: To unbundle C libraries from Rust builds, set the corresponding *-sys crate's USE_PKG_CONFIG/SYSTEM env var and add the system library to DEPEND; use iwdevtools to audit slot-operator correctness on bumps.
snippet:
# Avoid bundled copies of libraries
export RUSTONIG_SYSTEM_LIBONIG=1
export LIBSQLITE3_SYS_USE_PKG_CONFIG=1
DEPEND="
	dev-db/sqlite:3
	net-misc/curl[http2,ssl]
"
